description:
After countless battles in HacKClaD, an immense Hydra has broke into the last city of humanity, You play as "Witches", specialised combatants with the power of magical Foresight tasked with repelling the monster by breaking it apart, leaving behind precious magic shards ripped from its skin.

Through a series of rounds, players will draw and play cards from their unique character deck, utilising their effects to move, generate resources, and attack the Clad. However, before each round, the players see a list of the Clad's intentions, including how it will move and attack, allowing them to plan their actions to maximise their attack and avoid harm. Over time, players upgrade their decks by swapping their standard deck with "Enhanced Cards" of their choice, to modify and adapt their deck to the situation. As players deal damage, they receive the "Magic Shards" dropped from the Clad, and the player or team with the most at the end of the game wins.

HacKClaD.DeltA is a standalone expansion and introduces the new mechanic "Legion", the Clad will summon additional smaller enemies that can obstruct player movements or even perform further attacks, requiring players to destroy them. The new characters also come with "Crack Skills", special powerful abilities, at the cost of destroying an unknown amount of their scoring at the end of the game.
language_dependency: 4: Extensive use of text - massive conversion needed to be playable
artists: Masakazu Takizawa (たきざわ まさかず)

description:
Shady Guests is a tactical card game of influence, hidden moves, and perfect timing. Set at an ambassador’s party filled with suspicious guests and covert operatives, players lead rival spy networks competing to build the most influence before the event is exposed. On each turn, you play a guest or a spy into a shared room, either openly for an immediate effect or hidden for surprise, flexibility, and stronger reveals later.

As the party unfolds, tension rises through the shared Attention track. Every reveal brings new opportunities, but also pushes the gathering closer to its final revelation. Guests help shape the rooms, spies create shared disruptions and advantages, and timing becomes just as important as card strength.

With shared spaces, hidden information, shifting control, and constant player interaction, Shady Guests is a tense, medium-weight card game about bluffing, influence, and knowing exactly when to stay hidden — or step into the spotlight.

Carbono Zero is a competitive game for 2 to 4 players. During three rounds, each player will purchase cards to expand their city, thus reducing carbon emissions and increasing quality of life. The game ends after 3 rounds, and the player with the highest final score wins the match.

In Carbono Zero, players purchase cards to expand their cities, balancing credits, carbon, and quality of life. Throughout the game, they can evolve cities and industries to sustainable modes, manage credits to reduce carbon and maximize score. Additionally, players can fulfill objectives such as international treaties (Paris Agreement and Kyoto Protocol) and align their cities with the UN's SDGs, earning bonuses at the end of the match.

Passa a Letra... Guess the word... letter by letter! And eliminate your opponents... one by one! Choose a word with up to five letters and try to decipher your opponents' words before they discover yours. Passa a Letra is a game where logical reasoning and luck come together to entertain the whole family. By guessing letters one at a time and taking risky guesses at the right moment, you will be the great winner of this challenge!

complex is a card game for two would be real estate investors. Played over a series of rounds, each player uses their own deck of 15 Capital cards, players blind bid to purchase Duplexes, Triplexes, and Quadplexes across three suburbs.

The player who bids the highest acquires the available property, while the player who didn't bid the highest gets to move their Capital card to their Capital and use that card's one time ability (with ties being broken by the player with The Upper Hand).

At the end of a round, if player has more Properties than Capital cards, one of their Properties becomes distressed (losing its value).

The game ends when a player has no cards left to draw or there are no Properties left to bid on. Players gain points for their Properties (with complete sets in a suburb being worth additional points) and cards in their Capital.

-description from designer

A crazy, cute & surprisingly challenging strategy card game for two, based on the best-selling, Mensa award winning game "boop."

Flip a card, place a kitten – and “boop” all the cards next to it one space away. Find a way to boop three of your kittens into a row to graduate them into cats… then get three of your cats in a row to win.

But it isn’t that easy! You don’t know if you are drawing your kitten - or theirs. It might even be a super kitten, booping cards two spaces away, or a scaredy kitten who gets booped away anytime someone boops next to them. It’s like herding cats!
Fun new specialty cards make for a whole new strategic challenge.

-description from publisher

"Should I stop or continue?" Players can keep playing and risk losing everything they've collected, or stop at any time to collect the cards already turned over, as long as they form a set of 2, 3, or 4 cards. Dragon's Treasure is a memory game with unique twists, as matches aren't always simple pairs and certain cards' point values indicate how many cards must be found per turn, adding risk to the gameplay.

For example, when revealing a Chest card worth 4 points in the lower left corner, you must find 3 more chests to collect all 4 chests (at game's end, this converts to 4 cards or 16 points!). If you reveal a Doll card worth 2 points, you only need to find one more to collect the pair, like in traditional memory games (converting to 2 cards or 4 points). Players continue flipping cards as long as they match similar sets.

The game also includes 2 special cards that break the game's rhythm:
- Dragon card: When a Dragon is revealed, the player must end their turn and collect whatever they've won that round.
- Spider card: When a Spider is revealed, the round also ends but the player cannot collect anything they've won.

The winner is the player with the most cards or highest score.

Mestre da Mimica is guaranteed fun for everyone! Put your creativity into action! Use the card themes to unleash your imagination and act out your charade—jump, wave your arms, dance… just don't talk! You can even combine two or more themes to make the game more exciting! With over 1,700 words, fun is guaranteed!

In this incredible game, you'll find 4 different card categories: CHARACTERS, ACTIONS, PLACES, and THINGS, each with words worth different point values ranging from 1 to 4 points. Master of Charades by Copag develops social interaction, motor skills, and strategic thinking.

BeerCards is a collectible card game where the ultimate goal is to win the most cards, each representing a beer with a list of characteristics and specifics. Each round, players choose a card and a characteristic, depending on their strategy. The round is won by the card with the highest attribute. Participants use the cards to duel in two main ways: Standard Mode and Strategy Mode. In both forms, knowledge of beers is the key to victory.

BeerCards is a collectible card game where the ultimate goal is to win the most cards, each representing a beer with a list of characteristics and specifics. Each round, players choose a card and a characteristic, depending on their strategy. The round is won by the card with the highest attribute. Participants use the cards to duel in two main ways: Standard Mode and Strategy Mode. In both forms, knowledge of beers is the key to victory.

Duelo is a card-based argumentation game where players take on diverse and often unlikely characters and compete to persuade others who would best accomplish a given task. Each round, two players reveal their characters and face a shared challenge, while the remaining players act as judges. Within a time limit, each participant argues why their character is better suited for the action, with possible rebuttals before a final decision is made. The most convincing player wins the round and claims the action card. The first player to collect five action cards wins the game.

“What’s a puzzle, a game? There’s nothing the same. It’s weird in a wonderful way.
For more or for some, it’s a whole lot of fun, just open the box and play!
It’s Blorp! It’s Blorp! It’s strange, it’s thinky, it’s good.
It’s Blorp! It’s Blorp! If you haven’t tried it, you should!”

Blorp! is a tile placement game where players compete to build the biggest Blorps of three colors for the best score. Your biggest Blorp of each color will be worth 2 points for each tile and all other Blorps of that color are 1 point per tile. Grow them further with the use of Trumpets! But only completed Blorps, with no open ends, will earn you points at the end of the game.

For an extra dash of zany, try the speed rules for Blorp Blitz & race to complete your Blorp-o-Sphere first!

Do YOU love BLORP!??

The game Bíblia & Ação will put all your knowledge of God’s Word to the test. Discover whether you truly know the Bible as well as you think — and learn even more while having fun with friends and family!

Featuring 384 questions and answers covering the Pentateuch, the Prophets, Psalms, and everything in the Old Testament, as well as the life and work of Jesus Christ on Earth, the apostles and their mission, the letters of the Apostle Paul, Revelation, and the entire New Testament.

The game also includes questions about the history of God’s people, the history of the Bible itself, and challenges players to complete biblical verses, testing their knowledge in depth.

To win, a player must reach the end of the track first.

A dynamic and fun game that stimulates general knowledge and children's concentration. Through the clues on the cards, children locate the riddle's image on the board. Players must be quick to place their token on the image, as everyone has the same chance.

The game Pipas by Pais & Filhos is a fun option for children aged 4 and up. It includes 40 kite cards divided into 5 colors, with numbers from 1 to 8 that represent the points scored at the end of the game. This game is an excellent way to stimulate reasoning and strategy.

The 'Bet Your Brain' concept is based on betting on both your own and your opponents' knowledge. Or lack thereof! Therefore, everyone has a chance to win, regardless of how much prior knowledge you possess. Everyone is active in the game at all times, as bets must be made in each round - which also gives you the opportunity to win and participate even when it is not your own turn.

The family edition contains 2400 questions and tasks for all kinds of brains. Some of the questions are only for children, and in some cases they can score extra points if they can answer the adults' questions. Adults can also try to answer the children's questions - if they dare!

Since it is not only about being the smartest, but also about knowing yourself and your fellow and opposing players, 'Bet Your Brain' is a social game that can bring together family and friends across age, gender, education, etc.

In this competition, the winner is the first to reach the randomly selected point on the board, but to do so, you'll need great skill to overcome the constantly changing paths in this labyrinth. The goal of Labirinto – Turma da Mônica is to help children stimulate creativity, skill, memory, and logic to develop strategies and prevent opponents from escaping the labyrinth first. Fun is guaranteed for the whole family or with all your friends gathered together in this exciting board game.

Bet Your Brain - The Funny One is a new version of the popular quiz game. The reason for the great success of the Bet Your Brain series is probably that people don't sit around and fall into spells. Everyone has to bet on whether the player whose turn it is can complete the task in question.

This also means that the winner is not necessarily the smartest at answering the questions, but maybe just the best at figuring out the others!

In this version, the classic "who-what-where" questions have been replaced with:

- Pictures that you have to guess what represents

- Dilemmas where you have to guess what the others would do in some special situations

- Tasks where you have to mime, draw or write

- Challenges where you have to get as close as possible to a question such as how long Denmark's coastline is.

The objective of the game is to ensure that all comrades reach POPULAR AGRARIAN REFORM without leaving anyone behind and with the greatest number of seeds to sow the future. Throughout the game, players will face various obstacles that must be overcome collectively and cooperatively. To reach POPULAR AGRARIAN REFORM together, you and your comrades must gather at NATIONAL CONGRESSES to reflect, assess progress, and strengthen your principles and strategies. POPULAR AGRARIAN REFORM is a shared struggle, and therefore it is essential that everyone acts collectively, always paying attention to the needs of the group. By supporting one another, you and your comrades will have the help needed to reach POPULAR AGRARIAN REFORM together.

A game from Copag in partnership with Oggi

Each player receives one objective card and five item cards. On their turn, a player draws one card from the deck or the discard pile, then discards one card, always keeping five item cards in hand. Some action cards may appear during the game and can help (or hinder) players as they work toward their goals. Play continues in rounds until a player completes their objective card by collecting the five listed items before everyone else, winning the game.

Deep within the island lies a vast cavern, where sunlight barely touches the stone walls and silence fills the air. Here, plants struggle against shadows, pushing their way through cracks and reaching for the faintest glimmers of light.

In Cave Grounds, growth is constrained by rocky barriers and tight spaces. Careful planning is essential as you guide your stems through narrow passages, finding creative ways to bloom in the darkness.

-description from publisher's website

Across the island’s driest expanse, the sun scorches the earth and the wind carries only whispers of life. The ground is cracked and brittle, and water is scarce—but even here, resilience takes root.

In Desert Grounds, survival depends on careful planning. With limited energy and harsh conditions, every choice matters as you guide your plant to bloom against the odds, proving that life can flourish even in the sand.

-description from publisher's website

On the island, every day brings new wonders. In this expansion, you will draw 3 discovery cards from a set of 9. These discoveries will guide and reshape the way your flowers grow, offering new paths, powers, and possibilities. No two journeys of growth will ever feel the same.

Each set of discoveries creates a unique challenge, pushing you to adapt and explore new ways of growing your plant. With many possible combinations, every game feels fresh and full of new possibilities.
This expansion replaces the standard rules for growing flowers. Instead, you will use the abilities on three randomly chosen Discovery Cards to create blooms, fruits, and other special plant parts. Each card provides a unique path to scoring points.

-description from publisher's website

In the heart of the island lies a secluded, spring-fed lake. Here, the water is a perfect, clear pane, and your plant must live in two worlds at once. Its stems and leaves spread across the surface, while its roots dive into the mysterious depths below, drawing sustenance from the hidden currents.

In Floating Grounds, you will cultivate this delicate balance. Your growth mirrors the plant's nature: spanning across the water's surface to find new anchor points, while ensuring its hidden, submerged foundation remains strong. Every connection is a ripple that spreads across the lake.

-description from publisher's website

You have reached the island's barren heart—a windswept terrain of stone and shale. Life here is a stubborn contest against the unyielding rock. Plants cling to fissures and crannies, their roots digging deep into the slightest weaknesses in the stone, finding a way where there seems to be none.

In Rocky Grounds, you must conquer this harsh landscape. The vast, solid rock surfaces allow only the hardiest stems to traverse them; no delicate leaves can take root here. Your strategy must adapt, weaving a hardy network of stems across the stone to reach the isolated pockets of fertile soil where your plant can finally burst into vibrant bloom.

-description from publisher's website

Beneath the island’s surface lies a hidden network of roots and passages, where the soil is carved into winding tunnels. Life here grows in unexpected directions, slipping through gaps and finding light in the most unlikely places.

In Tunneled Grounds, stems can travel through pipes, entering on one side and emerging from another. These secret pathways open new routes for growth, challenging you to adapt and rethink how your plant spreads and blooms.

-description from publisher's website

"That Summer Week" is a solo, diceless campaign expansion where you follow a seven-day journal instead of rolling dice. Each day provides pre-set number groups (Forecast Groups) you must use strategically to grow your plant, turning the game into a careful planning puzzle.

The expansion mixes growth days with special rest days for interim scoring, adding a new rhythm. A challenging +3 energy bonus for rare six-number sequences tests your long-term strategy, creating a focused and meditative solo challenge.

-description from publisher's website

Nobilitea is a solo game of noble gossip and social standing played with a standard deck of cards.

It's time to spill some tea! You will visit members of the Court, determine their Interest, and choose combinations of Gossip from your hand that exactly match what they want to hear. When you succeed, the Gossip is shared and discarded. When you fail, it remains unshared. Manage your hand like a shedding puzzle as each new Noble raises the required total. Finish with as little unshared Gossip remaining as possible to earn the greatest Standing Increase.

An optional Advanced Play variant adds a Scandal that must be cleared by emptying your hand all at once.

In this diamonds placement game you have 3 Phases on 3 different boards to place diamonds to generate the most points. Each round you have roll four different colored dice to place a diamonds on the boards.

—description from the designer

Desire FOR Chances is a high-stakes survival card game that blends the tension of 'Russian Roulette' with magical chaos and strategic bluffing.

THE GOAL
Survive the deadly elixir tasting. The last player standing wins the game!

GAMEPLAY OVERWIEV
Each round begins with a roll of the dice to determine the ratio of "Death Elixirs" and "Empty Bottles" hidden face-down on the table. Players draw "Spell Cards" to manipulate the odds, peek at hidden dangers, or sabotage their rivals.

When it's your turn, you must trust your instincts and memory, and then make a critical choice;

Risk it on Yourself: If you believe a face-down card is an "Empty Bottle", reveal it on yourself. If you are right, you are safe. If you are wrong (Death Elixir), you lose 'Life Points'.

Target an Opponent: If you suspect a card is a "Death Elixir", throw it to a opponent! If you are right, they take damage.

KEY FEATURES

Spell Cards: Use cards like 'The Great Eye' to peek at elixirs, 'Handcuffs' to skip turns, or 'Acid Cauldron' to turn harmless bottles into lethal traps.

Dark Magic System: Unique passive abilities that trigger under specific conditions (like rolling dice to revive from the dead!), ensuring no two games are alike.

Be careful! With mechanics involving 'Push Your Luck' and 'Take That,' tables can turn in an instant.

—description from the publisher
language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups

description:
Dive into the Dungeon alone or with a friend in cooperations. Absorb dice attacks from monsters and reflect the dice back to deal damage to the monsters.

Play as Luke or Luce, the two barrier magic users that are found to be defective and cast out of the Welzner family. Fight your way through 3 dungeon levels and get different endings based on what character(s) you used.

Monsters will spam dice (attacks), your barrier magic are able to convert those dice into your own dice (mana) for storage. By combining adjacent dice, you deal damage back to the monsters.

Encounter different events, obtain unique Blessings (permanent upgrades) and Jewels (one-time-use items), and manage your dice (mana) properly to go through all the layers of the dungeon!

boardgame: Cards Against Humanity Explains the Joke
description:
Cards Against Humanity Explains the Joke is a new tariff-exempt informational product that explains every joke in regular Cards Against Humanity. It’s a 600-card reference guide, comprehensive authorial commentary, and historico-philosophical analysis that is very serious and definitely not a game.

Although, if you happen to have a copy of the rules for the original game laying around, there's nothing to stop you from playing with these cards instead!

—description from the publisher

A game that combines the timeless fun of puzzle arrangements, the strategic chaos of tiles placement, and the nerve-wracking precision of tower stacking, all on a spinning wheel of excitement!

Each turn, a player must choose one of their wooden tiles to balance inside the wheel, with the edge of their piece placed further in the rolling direction than previously played pieces. If the rolling wheel causes any pieces to fall, the player who just played must take them. A player with duplicate pieces can play them both to change the rolling direction, and anyone can stack identical pieces to try and keep from shifting the wheel's center of balance. The first player to place all of their pieces wins!

Convicted of murder Jens asks help from an independent team of investigators to review his trial and find the real culprit.

Crime scene investigation where players get to see court trial paper and evidence of the case. Going through them all they should be able to solve the case.

Shoot for the moon. Then, Conquer it.

The game of MegaDuel incorporates strategy and bold decision-making into a fantastical world of space-faring conquest.

Along your voyage, you're sure to encounter great adversaries, accomplish legendary feats, and earn notoriety across the cosmos.

There's no time to waste. Assemble your crew, batten down the hatches, and prepare to navigate your fleet through a world where space meets the sea.

In the end, only one Space Pirate will prevail.

—description from the designer

The Bats of Purple Valley live quietly in their cave. They generally dislike spending much energy. They prefer small negatives over large ones. They seem to live at a leisurly, unhurried pace. But on nights when the full moon rises, it's different... The great party begins and now, more and more companions gather... and the more, the merrier!

For the Moon is a ladder climbing game. The cards usually are negative, going weakest to strongest from -9 to -1. They must play a number of cards of the same value on the table, and the players must follow with the same number of cards with a higher value.

However, at any point, the players can place their moon coin on the invitation card to trigger the World of Positivity: the pile is cleared, that player draws one card of their choice from the discard and all players turn their cards around, and now the -1 become +1, -2 become +2, etc. This effectively reverses the card strengths, with the weakest -9 becoming the strongest +9.

The World of Positivity continues until everyone passes. Then the players turn their cards around again and they play with the negative values once more.

The first one to discard all their cards wins!

Nyanya is a game where players race to get rid of all their cards first. When placing a card, you can be honest about its number, or sometimes bluff to deceive other players!

But beware - if your bluff is exposed, a harsh penalty awaits. Use sharp observation and bold tactics to claim victory!

—description from the publisher

In this game, players create up to three rows of cards, and to play cards they must try to place them consecutively in one of the rows.

You can play them face up or face-down, and by doing so you can try to get rid of a card from your hand that isn't consecutive with the card next to it... but others may call your bluff on it!

Depending on the outcome of the call, the one who was in the right gives a card from their hand to the player who was in the wrong.

The 7s are special cards, and they have all players giving one of their cards to their neighbour.

The first one to shed all their cards wins! Once every player has been the dealer, the game ends.

—user summary

You are a little rabbit lost in the forest. Your home lies at the other side of the forest. Ask your way around to ther animals and if you and your siblings manage to all cross the forest before your opponent, you win!

Both players have 4 rabbits and are facing each other across a grid featuring various animals made up of horizontal 1x6 tiles. To play, the active player rolls 5 dice representing the various animals and chooses three of them. For each die, they may move a rabbit in a straight line as many spaces as they want as long as all spaces represent the type of animal on that die. They might also shift row's tile left to right by using a die which rolled the animal that is on its extremities.

The first player to have all 4 rabbits exit the forest wins!

A simple horse racing game from the early 1900s

Each player selects one of eight horses, then via a 'punch cards with holes' mechanic, a winner is selected at random along with a random price for winning

Likely a light gambling game introduced as a trade stimulator (hence the advertising of alcohol on the game pieces)

Senators of Rome is a political thriller negotiation game for 4–8 players, lasting approximately one to two hours. It is set in the Roman Republic, when annually appointed consuls and an expanding Senate governed a state under constant internal and external threat. Crises repeatedly tested the balance of power between ambitious senators. Success in confronting these events brought honor and influence; failure could destroy political careers—or destabilize the Republic itself. Players assume the roles of newly elevated senators seeking both to preserve Rome and to position themselves for ultimate authority.

The objective of the game is to accumulate the most Victory Points before the Republic collapses into Civil War. Each round, one player serves as consul and selects a team of senators to address a historical event threatening Rome. A structured negotiation phase allows players to bargain for inclusion, form alliances, trade political favors, and leverage their influence to shape the outcome. Players commit limited Political Capital to secure positions, support initiatives, bribe rivals, or organize assassinations that can alter the balance of power. Cooperation is often necessary to pass events, but ambition ensures that alliances remain temporary.

After each event, Victory Points are distributed based on the outcome. If the event succeeds, participating senators are rewarded; if it fails, abstaining senators instead benefit. With six possible end conditions, the Republic enters Civil War, which ends the game and triggers a final scoring. The senator with the most Victory Points is declared Emperor, claiming authority amid the ruins and restoring order under their rule.

—description from the publisher

You and your fellow adventurers have landed on an empty crumbling city floating high above the Earth. You must repair the portal, travel in time, all the while keeping the city afloat.

Will you survive long enough to uncover the secrets of the ancient city?

Forbidden Legacy hurls your team into a thrilling seven-episode quest where every choice counts. Work together through shifting moments in time, unlock abilities, reveal hidden components, and trigger lasting changes that reshape the game.

Like the classic Forbidden adventures, teamwork and daring moves are essential - but now the world evolves with you. New rules emerge, challenges escalate, and with each episode the city - and your path - transforms forever.

7 REPLAYABLE GAMES!
Seven linked episodes with choices that change the story each time you play.
UNBOX THE UNEXPECTED!
Open mystery boxes, hidden panels, and surprises as the story progresses.
COOPERATIVE FUN!
Team up to solve challenges and survive each episode together.
A STORY THAT REMEMBERS!
Your decisions carry over to the next game and then the next.
GRAPHIC-NOVEL GUIDED ADVENTURE!
The tale soars across the page in classic pulp fashion.
EASY TO LEARN GAMEPLAY!
Simple to start, with plenty of strategy as you go.

—description from the back of the box

boardgame: Taco Cat Goat Cheese Pizza: Valentine Edition
description:
Race against each other to SLAP a match between a card and spoken word!

Taco Cat Goat Cheese Pizza: Valentine Edition is filled to the brim with hand-slapping mayhem! As in Snap and Dobble, each player places a card from their hand face up into a community pile while saying taco/cat/goat/cheese/pizza in player sequence. When the card matches the mantra — boom! — everyone slaps their hand on the deck, with the last one to slap picking up the cards. Whoever rids themselves of cards first wins!

For extra fun, special action cards – the cupid-costumed moose, hat-tipping heart, and bear-hugging bear — force players to make certain gestures before racing to slap the deck.

—description from the publisher
family: Series: Taco Cat Goat Cheese Pizza

description:
SEWN is a 3 Phase trick-taking game with a farming arc:

Seed Phase: You play 6 tricks with no trump and no scoring. Each trick's played cards become face-up "seed" cards in four shared field piles (South, East, West, North). Then each player "plants" one hidden card into those same piles.

End of Summer Phase: Everyone plays their last card in a single special trick, highest rank wins, ties break by suit hierarchy. The winning card is set aside, its suit becomes trump for the Harvest.

Harvest Phase: Play 12 scoring tricks with trump active. Each trick won is 1 point. After 3 rounds, high score wins.

—description from the designer

OLOMOC: Seul is an alternative solo game mode for Olomòc that allows you to play games alone against the game environment and a game-controlled player, called the System.

You will thus have the pleasure of rediscovering the agendas from the rulebook as well as the Gargoyle agenda in a completely different way.

All the basic rules of Olomòc remain unchanged except for those specifically mentioned in this rulebook. 
Please therefore follow the rules adapted for SOLO mode.

Generation FPS takes root in your gaming memories.

It invites you to rediscover, on a tabletop, the charm of FPS games that have marked video game history: QUAKE, DOOM, UNREAL TOURNAMENT, in their multiplayer modes. 
Experience the game modes of these classics, enhanced with other match types of my own creation.

The game is fast-paced, simple, tactical, and fun.

The winning strategy will depend on your skills; the simple rules of the game avoid pitfalls and contentious issues.

You can pit 5 to 10 miniatures per side against each other in no fewer than 6 game modes, not counting the variants. The game is designed to be a fantastic toolbox, allowing everyone to add other modes of combat (at the end of the book, you'll find a space to record your creations).

Playable with 2 or more players, there's virtually no limit to the number of participants.

—description from the publisher

Collect as much cash as possible by betting on other players' success in various tasks and winning tasks with your own pair. You can earn a considerable amount of extra cash by participating in the Jackpot lotteries.

The traditional elements, as in previous titles in this series, remain: separate task variants for younger players, some timed tasks, and a division of tasks into skill and brain games.

The game's flavor is enhanced by the fact that all tasks are completed in pairs – which are drawn anew each turn. Additionally, there are lotteries where you can win extra cash. Perhaps fortune will be kind to you...

The game continues until the first pawn completes the entire game route!

Will you bet? The Jackpot – it provides great entertainment both with family and at a party with friends!

It's time for... a big bet!

The goal of the game is to skillfully place successive triangle-shaped tiles. Each placed tile must match its entire side with the side of a tile on the table so that all colors match on the adjacent tiles (red goes with red, blue goes with blue, etc.).

The game has two game modes:
1. "Colors Only" – in this mode, the player scores points for all colored triangles from "consistent" single-colored areas (composed of at least two triangles) created by placing their tile.
2. "Colors and Triangles" – in this mode, the player scores points as in the "Colors Only" mode, plus additional points for spotting each MAX triangle (with a side length of at least 2), i.e., one formed by placing multiple tiles.

Excellent observation skills and strategic thinking are required. The player with the most points at the end of the game wins.

The game: develops observation and strategic skills, teaches patience, and improves concentration.

Wizards Duel is a fast, tactical skirmish game for 1–2 players where small teams of powerful wizards clash in magical duels.
Build your wizard team from rival schools of magic, choose spells, and outmaneuver your opponent on a compact battlefield.
Every spell is a risk, every move matters, and magic itself is unstable.
Play head-to-head or face automated Fate Wizards in solo play — victory belongs to those who master positioning, timing, and raw arcane power.

—description from the designer

For generations, the Novarchs ruled the Domineum, and as they prospered and expanded, so too did the Great Houses of the galaxy to which they delegated their unmatched power. But within the royal House of Novarchon there was a sickness—a corruption of both body and mind—and a grim cult formed around the afflicted, foretelling a sinister salvation from beyond the void.
 But not all Novarchs accepted these dark divinations. In secret defiance, preparations were made in hushed tones and in lightless corners over untold cycles, and you now hold the torch as one such Novarch of the resistance. When the day of prophecy arrives, you’ll be forced to choose which Great Houses might rise and fight, and which will perish. You cannot save them all, but can you save enough to survive the Voidfall and found a New Domineum?

Voidfall: The Last Novarch is a narrative-driven campaign expansion set in the Voidfall universe. You’ll face tough decisions that shape the galaxy over the course of eight chapters with nearly 30 unique missions to choose from. Each mission has multiple endings, and every House has a multipart story culminating in over 100 epilogues reflecting the final state of your galaxy. Missions are tailor-made for specific Houses, and each features its own map, special rules, victory conditions, and bonus objectives, along with persistent rewards that open up new opportunities—if you can reach them. Failing a mission never means replaying it, but the consequences will leave their mark.

In addition to being fully resettable, the campaign encourages many playthroughs with branching pathways affecting both narrative and gameplay. Designed in parallel with Voidfall: Resurgence, this campaign can be played with or without expansion content, and allows both solo play and a 2-player cooperative mode using a campaign-specific House with drop-in/drop-out support.

—description from the publisher

An educational and entertaining game to discover the most iconic dragons from Catalonia’s traditional festivities
Nan Cucut, one of the most emblematic characters of our culture, gives his name to this 110‑card game that showcases the most representative festive beasts from towns across Catalonia.

It is a filler‑type game with an easy and very fun dynamic.

Form words or reach the required point values using cards featuring the capgrossos and gegants of different towns to feed the cucaferes or dragons you need. Win the game before the other players steal them from you by using their action cards.

The game is designed as a fast‑interaction filler aimed at fostering lexical skills, argumentation, and active listening among players, while also promoting the visibility of Catalan language, popular culture, and traditional characters within board games.

In Shijin, players face off in a duel between celestial spirits to determine who will become the divine protector of the newborn heir to the Emperor.
The four guardians—Seiryū, the Azure Dragon of the East; Byakko, the White Tiger of the West; Genbu, the Black Tortoise of the North; and Suzaku, the Vermilion Bird of the South—each can claim the role of the Imperial Prince’s tutelary guide.

This abstract strategy game unfolds on a 5×5 board. Each player places their Guardian miniature and four Seal tokens of their color onto the board. A player’s turn is quick and intuitive: move your Guardian or one of your Seals to an adjacent empty space, or perform a special move with a Seal if it is adjacent to your Guardian.
The first player who manages to place all four of their Seals adjacent to the opposing Guardian captures it and wins the game.

Don’t be fooled by the game’s elegant simplicity—every move hides deep strategic possibilities.
Each Guardian also possesses a unique special ability, enabling distinctive plays and strategies.

Shijin is a fast, accessible, yet deeply strategic game that will truly put players’ minds to the test.

—description from the publisher

The Architect's Deck is a clever little expansion for Craftsmen: Think and Write that kicks the dice right off the table! Inside, you’ll find 18 shiny new building cards to shake up your setup phase. No more leaving things to chance - you’ll be trusting your gut and plotting your master plan right from the get-go. Whether you're flying solo or squaring off with friends, this deck’s ready to build some serious fun!

-description from publisher's website

The Artisan Challenge is a twisty new way to play Craftsmen: Think and Write - and it’s not for the faint of heart! Think you’ve mastered the base game? Think again!

This variant cranks up the challenge. The core rules stay the same, but the order of actions each round gets shuffled around like a squirrel on espresso. Normally, you'd grab some coins, shop for resources, then get to work. But in Artisan Challenge, you might be building before you even have money, producing before you’ve stocked up, and scratching your head the whole way through.

Planning ahead is key - at least two rounds if you want to survive. Watch your rivals, stay sharp, and above all: good luck. You’re gonna need it!

-description from publisher's website

Craftsmen: Think and Write is the pencil-powered cousin of the original Craftsmen board game - reimagined to play with just paper, pencils, and plenty of brainpower!

We crafted Classic Way variant especially for fans of the classic box version. It keeps the same satisfying production chains, just without the bulky bits. So if you're craving that original Craftsmen vibe, this one's calling your name.

Go on - print it, play it, and let a little nostalgia float through the air... just like sawdust in the workshop.

-description from publisher's website

Harbour Master is a splashy new expansion for Craftsmen: Think and Write! This time, you're not sending your precious goods to town buildings - you’re shipping them off to mighty vessels at sea! Sounds like just a change of address? Think again! The harbour’s a bustling, competitive place, full of clever ways to score points - and just as many ways to fall behind if you're not paying attention. Keep a close eye on your rivals’ moves and outsmart them before they sail ahead!

Inside, you’ll find two different Harbour Sheets - but you’ll only use one per game. That means it’s basically two expansions in one neat little package. Anchors aweigh, strategist - your ship’s about to come in!

-description from publisher's website

Scenario Expansion is tailor-made for solo Craftsmen: Think and Write players who’ve sharpened their pencils and their wits one too many times and are hungry for a fresh challenge!

Inside, you’ll find two unique scenarios, each with its own twisty goals, tricky restrictions, and a splash of story to set the scene. Whether you're braving a supply shortage or chasing a legendary commission, every scenario feels like a whole new game. Pick your challenge, dive into the tale, and prove - once and for all—that you are the one and only Master Craftsman this town needs!

Provided components:
- Scenario: Hunger Calendar Sheet,
- Scenario: Hunger Craftsman Sheet,
- Scenario: War Calendar Sheet,
- Scenario: War Craftsman Sheet,
- rulebook

-description from publisher's website

Word has spread across the kingdom - this tiny town is hiring, and crafty folk of all kinds are rolling in, hungry for coin and ready to work! So why not put those eager hands to good use? After all, a little help never hurt anyone... right?

Worker's Caravan expansion brings 12 helpful worker cards to the table, each offering a boost to your bustling projects. But here’s the twist - you’re not the only one hiring! Your rivals will be grabbing help too, and sometimes those workers might end up doing more good for them than for you. Tricky bunch, aren’t they? Either way, this expansion adds a fun breeze of strategy to cool your overheated brain. Help is on the way - but who it really helps is up to you to find out!

-description from publisher's website

Flourishing times in the kingdom! With every card played, the kingdom’s display grows in power – but only your last two cards in hand will score at the end of the round.
Will you build up a character’s strength, or go for their victory points? And are the conditions met for a particularly lucrative scoring?

Bloom Kingdom is a clever card game with stunning artwork. On each turn, players simultaneously choose character cards from their hands and play them into a common display. Your goal is to manipulate the display in your favor and make certain character types more valuable than others. However, to make a character type more valuable, this usually means you have to add cards of that type to the display. But you also want to keep such cards as your final two in your hand, in order to score them at the end of the round.
This way, the display and the scoring potential grows over 3 rounds and whoever has the most points at the end of the third round wins.

—description from the publisher

The goal is simple: be the last one standing.

Each player must manage the Revolver tokens and their three Skill tokens. The Skill tokens represent both the player’s abilities and their remaining lives, which they must preserve to stay in the game. This game is not for the faint of heart...

On your turn, either shoot (the stack of tokens has 7 "clicks" and 1 "bang!") or activate one of your 3 skills (which will be turned face down).

Skills can range from passing the turn to another person and reordering the stack of shots to cancelling another skill or making someone shoot twice.

If you reveal a "click" when shooting, you will turn one of your skill tokens face up. If you reveal a "bang!", you will lose one of your face-up skill tokens.

A player will be eliminated if they reveal a "bang!" and all their tokens are face down or if they have no tokens left... until only one player remains.

Your head makes all the decisions, but your heart has the final say.

How much do you know about geography in Denmark? In this fun and challenging game, your knowledge will be tested in two different modes:

CARTOGRAPHER’S CORNER
In Cartographer’s Corner, you will put your geographical knowledge to the test by building a map in front of you round after round.

CONTINENTAL SHAKE UP
In Continental Shake Up, the group of players build a map in the center of the table as they play. But watch out! Only those cards that are correctly placed will remain on the table. Additionally, the riskiest placements are the ones that will give you the most points.

—user summary

Test your geography knowledge in a fun and challenging way! Place locations as accurately as you can in relation to what is already on the table. Turn over the card – and see if you were right!

Where Is That: Denmark is a game in the Where Is That? series.

—description from the back of the box (translated)

Test din geografividen på en sjov og udfordrende måde! Placer steder så præcist, du kan, i forhold til det, der allerede ligger på bordet. Vend kortet – og se, om du har ret!

Hvor er det? Danmark er et spil i serien Where is that?

—description from the back of the box

5 City Building Tiles are included in Suburbia: Collector’s Edition (Washington, Chicago, Los Angeles, New York City, and Orlando).

15 additional City Building Tiles were offered as backer exclusives during the original crowdfunding campaign, however, these tiles are now available to purchase through the publisher’s website:
• Knoxville (Sunsphere)
• Rome (Colosseum) 
• Barcelona (Sagrada Familia)
• Dubai (Burj Khalifa)
• Tokyo (Tokyo Skytree)
• Paris (Eiffel Tower)
• Toronto (CN Tower)
• Hong Kong (International Commerce Centre)
• Taipei (Taipei 101)
• London (Westminster Abbey)
• Philadelphia (Independence Hall)
• Las Vegas (The Mirage)
• Milan (Duomo Di Milano)
• Prague (St. Vitus Cathedral)
• Kuala Lumpur (Petronas Towers)

All City Building Tiles are C stack tiles. They are designed to provide a late game boost of some sort to their owner, usually in line with their iconic nature. The tiles are mixed in with the C tiles you’ll be using for the game prior to shuffling the C-stack and counting out tiles, so you won’t know for sure if any or all of these tiles will be appearing later in the game. While you can play with as many as you’d like, we’ve found it’s best to pick five of them that might be included, because that way all players will have an idea of what could be contained there, and can tailor their strategies towards those possibilities.

NOTE: This expansion is only contained in the Suburbia: Super Collector’s Edition (Kickstarter Edition). This expansion is NOT included in any of the standard versions of Suburbia: Collector’s Editions but may be purchased separately from the publisher’s website.

-description from publisher's website

boardgame: Fox TWO: Aces of the Sky
description:
Ace in the Sky brings Fox Two: Air Combat into new highs with more fighter jets and even more cards to strategize around.

This expansion adds three new aircrafts:
- F-18 Hornet,
- Harrier FA2,
- MIG-31 Foxhouond

Take command of your favorite aircraft and prove your skills in intense, simulated battles to become the king of the skies. Execute either offensive or defensive maneuvers each turn, carefully managing your energy to outmaneuver the enemy fighter. Lock on, launch missiles, and dominate the skies! Are you ready to claim air superiority? "-description from publisher's website"

After a series of bad omens, you seek refuge at a secluded cabin in the woods, hoping to turn your luck around. But as you settle in, strange things begin to happen. Can you shake off the bad vibes, or is something darker lurking in the shadows?

Friday Fright is an escape room style mystery game filled with puzzles that may test your perception, wordplay, and lateral thinking. English language fluency is required.

When you have solved a puzzle on the cards, you will enter the answer into the companion website (compatible with all smart phones and computers) and it will check whether you are correct. If you are, the website will tell you which card or cards to take next. The website also contains a graduated hint system, so if you get stuck you can get just a small hint about the current puzzle to get moving again without giving too much away.

This system is non-destructible: some groups may choose to write on cards, but this is not required. A group cannot re-play the game, but can pass it on to another group for their use.

Terra de Castells (Land of Castles) is a strategic board game of ambition set in the borderlands of medieval Catalonia, where players compete to conquer castles and secure their legacy across the territory of the Spanish March.

In Terra de Castells, two to four players assume the roles of rival lords striving to expand their domains by controlling fortifications, managing resources, and forging alliances. The game blends area control, tactical planning, dice combat, and resource management into a dynamic experience where every decision shapes the political and military balance of the region. Accessible rules make the game easy to approach, while layered strategic options reward long-term planning and clever negotiation. Players must carefully choose where to put a meeple (that represents the “castlà”: the administrator of a castle and its domains), when to fortify and prepare for the war, and how to outmaneuver their opponents to dominate the map.

Each round, players gather influence resources related to the feudal estates: the Crown, the Clergy, the Nobility, and the Peasantry. Each one will be useful to deploy “castlans” and control more strongholds, fight to conquer another’s castle, unfold the feudal power, ask for the favour of the Crown, or collect taxes from peasants and nobles who owe vassalage to their lords. Actions alternate around the table: claiming territories, connecting castles, securing zones, and challenging rival strongholds. Strategic timing is crucial — expanding too quickly may leave you vulnerable, while playing defensively could cost you key opportunities. To prevail, you must balance growth, defense, and influence, ensuring that your castles stand as enduring symbols of your power. The game will start in the XI century and will last until the end of XV century or when a faction achieves the victory condition (what happens first). Each century (round) will trigger historical events that affect the gameplay of all players.

Terra de Castells includes a board representing different regions, combat dice, resource tokens, faction cards and unique favour cards that give advantage in many situations and enrich the interactions between factions, and a setup mechanic that provides variable starting scenarios from game to game. With only one victory condition but multiple paths to achieve it and high replayability, each session tells a new story of rivalry, ambition, and medieval grandeur.

-description from designer

Batalha dos Bruxos is a Brazilian party game where fun, strategy, and magic meet! You and your friends will compete in an epic showdown to determine who is the most powerful wizard of all. Wield your wand and prove your worth! With fast-paced and engaging turn dynamics, the match is full of twists and turns, guaranteeing excitement from start to finish as you determine who is the most powerful wizard of all. Are you ready for battle?

A satirical, critical, and intelligent game featuring nearly 250 absurdities said by Bozo before and after becoming president, by his children (01, 02, and 03), by the guru-philosopher, and by his team of followers. The winner is whoever gets rid of all of them. To do so, you must answer the maximum number of questions correctly and know how to use the consequences at the right time. Do you remember when, where, and in what context the numerous nonsense statements were made?

In High Pressure, players compete to dive deeper than their rivals over four rounds.

Each round begins with players secretly selecting two Safe cards and one Hazard card from their hand. One card is placed face down into each depth row, building a shared grid of possible dive locations. Players always know where they placed their own cards, but must guess where others have placed hazards.

After the grid is built, all players simultaneously choose a column to dive in by secretly setting their die. The choices are revealed at the same time, and the selected cards at that depth are flipped.

If a player reveals a Safe card, their dive succeeds and they may either return to the surface to score points or continue deeper in the next phase. If they reveal a Hazard card, their submarine is forced back to the surface and they score nothing for that round.

The deeper a player dives successfully, the more points they earn:
• 10m = 1 point
• 20m = 2 points
• 30m = 3 points
(40m in Extended Mode = 4 points)

However, points are only scored if the player returns safely. If they hit a Hazard at any depth, they lose all potential points for that round.

After all dive phases are resolved, surviving players score based on the deepest level they reached. A new round then begins.

The game ends after four rounds. The player with the highest total score wins.

High Pressure is a simultaneous action selection game focused on prediction, risk management, and reading your opponents.

-description from designer

In Wróżki Zębuszki players take role of Tooth Fairies and compete against each other to collect as many teeth from other players, while protecting their own.

At the start of the game, each player takes 12 teeth, placing them on their board, as well as the deck matching the color of the Fairy they choose, plus 3 Position tokens.

During a turn, each player draws cards until they have 4, then selects two, playing them face down and assigning Position tokens to them in secret. Then, cards are revealed one by one, and their effects are resolved, until all cards are resolved. Used cards are discarded, and a new turn starts.

Once all cards in player decks have been drawn, the final turn begins, after which the winner is decided. Players score points for each healthy tooth they kept and for the teeth they managed to steal from other players, losing points for bad teeth and the lost ones.

Link is an intuitive game in which players will try to get rid of all their tiles before their opponents.

Each player must place their tile next to a tile on the table, as long as there is continuity of the images.

Children can play without strategy, simply trying to juxtapose the tiles. Even so, they need to pay attention and use spatial reasoning to identify where the tiles can be placed. When the player cannot discard, they will be forced to buy a new tile. Thus, the rounds continue until someone finishes with their tiles and wins the game. Younger children will play with LINK, searching for and discarding the right tiles. However, over time, they will realize that to win, it is necessary to have a strategy, analyzing their tiles, what is on the table, and the opponents' moves.

Although its existence is questionable due to lack of evidence, it is estimated that King Nebuchadnezzar II was responsible for its construction, marking the height of the neo-Babylonian empire between 605-562 BC. Among the 7 wonders of the ancient world, this is the only one that left no traces of its existence.

The ideas we have of its aesthetics come from ancient writings of Greek and Roman historians. They say that, to please his bride, Amitis, the king gathered his best architects to build a pyramid that would remind her of the mountainous regions of her homeland in Persia. This pyramid, composed of majestic pillars, waterfalls, fountains, statues, and trees of all kinds, would become known as the legendary HANGING GARDENS OF BABYLON.

GAME OBJECTIVE Be the player with the most victory points after completing the construction of the pyramid. You earn points by:

- Having constructions of the same type adjacent to each other. 
- Completing the objectives of your project card.
- Achieving the majorities of the public project cards.
family: Constructions: The Hanging Gardens of Babylon

description:
Welcome to the gridTHE LIGHTS ARE ABOUT TO GO OUT

...Engines fire.
...Headsets crackle.
...Monitors glow along the pit wall.
...Cars line up under the floodlights.

In modern Formula racing, the driver may sit in the cockpit…
but the race is won by the team.
By the strategist.
By the engineers.
By the one calling the shots.
That’s you.

In Race Pace – The New Formula, you step into the role of Team Manager, leading a modern Formula team through the relentless strategy battle behind every Grand Prix. This is not about reflexes. It’s about decisions made under pressure.

Every round, you’ll balance speed against survival.
Push too hard and your tyres fall away.
Harvest energy too long and rivals strike.
Deploy too early and you’re defenseless later.

Modern racing is no longer just about raw pace. It’s about energy, timing, and control.

But always keep one eye on Race Control. The weather can turn. Racing incidents can rewrite the race. Penalties can arrive without warning.

A single decision can define your weekend. Because in modern Formula racing, victory doesn’t always go to the fastest car — it goes to the team that controls the race pace.

				
				
					What Kind of Game Is This?Race Pace – The New Formula is an accessible, Formula 1–inspired, strategic racing game with meaningful decisions, clever timing, and tense player interaction.

The game is built around two central ideas:

You are the voice on the radio. You are the strategist behind the scenes. You are responsible for every call.

and

Your Race Pace determines everything — but your tyres decide if you can actually reach it.

In real Formula 1 racing, the driver may sit in the cockpit — but the race is run from the pit wall. Race Pace puts you in the role of a modern race team manager. From the pit wall you monitor data screens, track conditions, and rival strategies while guiding your driver and car through a full Grand Prix. Every round brings new messages from race control and evolving race situations, forcing you to decide whether to upgrade your car, coach your driver, manage tires, or respond to changing conditions. Your choices shape your race pace — and ultimately your shot at victory.

As Team Manager, you must master:

•    Tyre degradation — the true limiter of speed
•    Battery management — harvest vs deploy
•    Upgrades & development — long-term advantage
•    Race Control — penalties, interventions, and chaos
•    Driver growth — experience turns good drivers into great ones

Each race unfolds over multiple rounds in which players simultaneously plan their strategy, then reveal how fast their car can go that lap. Positions shift. Opportunities open. Pressure builds.

				
				
					Changes compared to the previous Race Pace editionsThis new edition reflects the latest era of Formula 1 regulations and technology, making it a standalone game rather than an expansion. Tyre management remains crucial, but now battery management and energy usage are just as important in determining performance over the race. Boosts, inspired by modern F1 push systems, play a more prominent tactical role, allowing teams to seize key moments on track—but only if timed perfectly. Together, these elements create a deeper and more contemporary racing experience that rewards both planning and adaptability.

A double-sided board introduces two distinct race environments: traditional daytime circuits and intense night races on tight street tracks. Each setting changes how tires behave, how energy is managed, and how valuable driver experience becomes.

A newly designed, intuitive pace-tracking system keeps the race flowing smoothly, reducing bookkeeping and letting players focus on strategy, timing, and high-stakes decisions.

To win, you must manage your team more effectively than your rivals and deliver the strongest overall race performance. Balance upgrades, driver development, tire and battery strategy, and perfectly timed boosts to achieve the fastest race pace. In Race Pace – The New Formula, only the team that best adapts to the modern rules of racing will take the checkered flag.

'Race Pace - the New Formula is coming to Gamefound end of 2026/begin of 2027. Click here to stay informed!

—description from the publisher

Expansion but also a stand alone playable set to the original game, Awkward Family Photos Greatest Hits. This expansion adds 50 new double sided image cards and 250 movie quote cards from 150 movies of the greatest decade, ever, totally. The new cards feature dialogue quotes in the movie categories of Drama, Comedy, Action/Adventure, Horror/Thriller and Family. Grab your Aquanet, Spandex, leg warmers and gag me with a spoon!

boardgame: Monster in My Pocket: Notte Senza Luna
description:
Goal of the game: Capture the greatest number of monsters generated by the volcano, become a powerful monster, and survive encounters with other monsters.

Players Place the monster cards on the table or put all the monsters in the center of the board in the space representing the mouth of the volcano. The cards have four different numerical values colored to correspond to: Strength (red), Intelligence (blue), Experience (green), and Agility (yellow).

There are four bags that have a matching "?" symbol and color for the four attributes. They are filled with all the tokens of the same color. Each player draws one token per color (without showing them to others) and places them behind their station. Each player then possesses values for Strength, Agility, Intelligence, and Experience that define the value of the player (as a monster).

The monsters come in 5 different colors: red, blue, green, yellow, and the rarer gold color. Each color defines a preferred characteristic of the individual monster; or rather, a red monster will prefer to fight with strength, a blue one will prefer to compare based on intelligence, and so on.

The players then roll 2 dice, one fora numerical movement and one for a color that shows the direction of movement.

The volcano also has four colored "gates" through which the monsters can exit. Monsters jump over each other on the path but cannot stay on the same space. Each player will try to move their monster toward a "meeting" space (those with eyes). When a player manages to stop a monster on a meeting space, they can face it or force one of the other players to face it.

Once a Meeting place is reached, a die roll determines the stat color to be fought with. Players may choose captured monsters to fight, or the player may fight using their stats.
The player who wins the final challenge is the winner!

Themes include aliens, propaganda, territory control, drone warfare and conspiracy theories.

Two players go head to head. One as the drones, one as the orbs. Each side has distinct advantages and weaknesses. Players take turns to use actions in order to advance across the board. Actions are decided by a slider for the orbs, and accrue over time for the drones.

As the turns pass you move through different phases of the game. inevitably the orbs get the mothership, which then makes its way toward the drones last stand, at which point the orbs win. The drones win condition is to move their pieces onto every orb portal before that happens.

Every promo window pattern from 2017 through 2025, and a brand new foil exclusive promo!

This Promo pack includes Window Pattern Cards:

     Balboa Bay / Vitraux
     Alpenglow / Komorebi
     Apollo / Godfather
     Fog of Love Crossover
     Intl. Tabletop Day
     Watch It Played
     We Our FLGS
     Galápagos Jogos
     Geekach Games
     CuseCon
     Gathering of Friends
     Board Game Geek
     The Dice Tower 2020
     Man vs. Meeple
     Man vs. Meeple Season 3
     Game Boy Geek
     Game Boy Geek 2021
     Game Boy Geek 2022
     Game Boy Geek 2023
     Game Boy Geek 2024
     Game Boy Geek 2025
     Tantrum House 2020
     Tantrum House Season 8
     Tantrum House Season 10
     Tantrum House Season 12
     2023 Gen Con Promo
     2024 Gen Con Promo
     2025 Gen Con Promo
     2020 Promo Pack Exclusive
     2022 Promo Pack Exclusive
     2025 Promo Pack Exclusive (foil)


These all match the styling of the Sagrada Remaster, but will still fit player boards from the original.

–description from the publisher

Six new foil promos to celebrate the launch of Sagrada Remastered.

Contains promos: PR33, PR38, PR39, PR40, PR41, and PR42

Party game designed to see how well you know your opponents.  Each person is given yes, no, and maybe cards.  After a question is read to the next player, all other players decide how they are going to respond.  Score based on how you match the respondent.
Winner is first player to reach 20 points.

Diceapult is a strategic dexterity board game. Command your monster army! Choose from goblins, wraiths, ogres, zombies, or shadow hunters—each with unique abilities that can turn the tide of battle.

Load your dice into the catapult and LAUNCH them into contested battle zones! Fight to capture territory and defeat your rivals in this thrilling mix of dexterity and strategy.

Can't aim straight? No problem! Play powerful Battle Spell cards to manipulate the dice and outmaneuver your opponents. Everyone has a path to victory!

Capture battle zones by accumulating six points of dice strength—but watch out! Enemy dice in your zone count against you. Claim walls from three zones to win!

—description from the designer

Evacuation is a 2-6 player game where you must get as many of your explorers out of a burning jungle as possible. To start choose one of the six available teams and place your units on the outskirts of the board. Next you will race to get your units to the helicopter pads at the center of the board.

As the game progresses more and more tiles will be burned so make sure you use your actions effectively! At various points you may not be able to save all your units and will be forced to choose who to save! Along your journey you will be faced with tribes, both friendly and hostile, rivers with piranhas lurking in the depths, and you may have to battle other players to gain access to the limited spaces. Every turn you will collect a card to help yourself or to hinder your opponents. Play them wisely to gain the largest advantage.

After every unit is either evacuated or killed, check to see who evacuated the most explorers, they are the winners!

boardgame: Wizard in Training: Dog on Holiday
description:
Wizard Dog On Holiday changes the look for the base map and adds a rewarding layer to your adventure. As you collect treats - available in three types - you’ll mark them off on the treats track, earning +5 points for each completed row. Gifts act as wild tokens, instantly substituting for any missing treat to help you complete rows faster. Strategic collection and clever gift use can turn a modest run into a high-scoring surprise.

—description from the publisher

Marketplace: Wizard In Training Expansion Cards adds a dynamic twist to the Wizard in Training universe with an all-new deck of purchasable upgrades. While exploring the magical realm, players can spend gems from their collection to buy powerful marketplace cards, each enhancing specific actions like spells, fetches, and dungeon runs. From enchanted blades to mystical books, each card offers unique bonuses and combos - but gems spent here won’t count toward your final score. With limited slots and first-come-first-serve mechanics, timing and resource management are key in this competitive, strategic addition to your magical journey.

—description from the publisher

Stack as many cards as possible and help the cat climb the tower until reaching its top!

The game basically has two types of cards: roofs and walls. Whoever knocks down the tower first loses, the other players count who has the fewest roof cards, the player with the fewest roof cards wins, if there is a tie the symbols on the cards must be counted, whoever has more wins. Whoever runs out of cards from their hand first also wins.

Connect, laugh, and predict your way to victory with Disagreeable! From contemplating philosophical questions to settling debates about social etiquette, Disagreeable challenges you to predict how many players agree or disagree with a wide range of statements. Disagreeing with friends and family has never been better.

Players can earn up to 2 points in each round. If you correctly predicted the number of players who agreed, you score a point. If you also correctly guessed someone who definitely agreed, that’s another point. The game is played over 7 rounds, and the player with the most points at the end wins!

Disagreeable Includes:


     150 Statement Cards
     16 Voting Cards
     A Rulebook
     8 Pencils
     A Scorepad
language_dependency: 2: Some necessary text - easily memorized or small crib sheet

description:
Poppi: Wizard In Training is a standalone, print-and-play roll-and-write map that reimagines the core game with a mischievous twist. You play as Poppi, a young merchant’s daughter accidentally teleported to the Training Ground after grabbing a magical coin. Guided (and mildly judged) by a sarcastic wizard dog, you'll flip cards to explore the realm, upgrade actions, collect treasures, and gather magical items - all while trying to prove you somehow belong. With clever combos, unique item interactions, and a fresh character arc, this map offers a playful and strategic solo adventure within the Wizard in Training universe.

—description from the publisher

Pursuits: Wizard In Training Expansion Cards adds personal objectives and tough trade-offs to the world of Wizard in Training. At the start of the game, each player draws three pursuit cards and must choose whether to gain their immediate resources (at a cost) or keep them as secret goals for bonus points at the end. Some goals reward exploration, others favor mastery of specific actions or collecting resources - and some are only achievable in multiplayer games. With every choice shaping your strategy, Pursuits adds a compelling layer of long-term planning to your magical journey.

—description from the publisher

Quests: Wizard In Training Expansion Cards introduces public objectives that add a competitive layer to your magical journey. At the start of each game, six quest cards are revealed, giving players shared goals to pursue - like collecting gems or progressing certain tracks. Be the first to complete a quest to earn a stronger reward, while others can still claim lesser bonuses later. In solo play, quests become benchmarks, with missed ones costing valuable points. Whether you’re outpacing rivals or racing the clock, Quests adds urgency, direction, and strategic variety to every game.

—description from the publisher

The biggest board gaming convention in the world is coming to your city, and local leaders want to develop a new city park to celebrate the event. They have solicited designs from the most prestigious firms in town to see who can plan the best park according to the demands of the local population. You, as a representative of one of these firms, must manage the limited budget of city hall and the restrictions of the proposed park site as you decide where to plant trees, lay paths, dig lakes, and build benches. The park planner that pleases the most people will win the bid and be honored at the opening ceremony of the convention.

This updated edition of Polyhedral Park Planner includes enhanced scoring rules, triple the number of cards in the original edition, and updated player boards.

—description from the designer

In this expansion, every student arrives with a unique personality and special abilities. From the Hacker bending the system to the Philanthropist handing out money (seriously, why?), each character will shake up your strategy and change your path to success. Will you adapt to university life, or will you end up like the Emo, surviving on a steady diet of sad faces? Pick your character and find out!

—description from the publisher

Origin Goal is a high-stakes tactical football (soccer) simulation board game that blends spatial strategy with dynamic risk management. Designed to bridge the gap between tabletop gaming and professional football thinking, the game challenges players to master the pitch through three core strategic pillars:

1. The Duel of Probabilities: Attack vs. Defense Dice
Every confrontation on the pitch is a calculated risk. Players must strategically choose between Attacking and Defending Dice sets, balancing aggressive pushes against defensive stability. This "Risk vs. Reward" mechanic ensures that every tackle and sprint is a mental battle before the dice are cast.

2. Tactical Adaptability: The Event Card System
Simulating the unpredictability of a real match, the Event Card system introduces dynamic shifts—tactical fouls, bursts of brilliance, and sudden momentum changes. Success depends on hand management and the ability to adapt your strategy on the fly to counter an opponent’s maneuver.

3. Mastery of Time and Space: The "Origin" Geometry
Played on a specialized hexagonal grid, Origin Goal is a game of spatial awareness. The unique "Origin" points (dark green hexagons) act as tactical anchors. A successful interception forces the opponent to reset their formation to these points, requiring players to constantly manage their positioning to either squeeze the space when defending or expand it for a final strike.

HATI is a cooperative real-time tower-building game for 2 to 6 players. Designed for children and families, players work together to construct a physical jungle tower and lift the elephant spirit Hati to the top before the sand timer runs out.

On each turn, a player plays one card and draws one card. Guardian cards allow players to place animal figures onto flowers; once enough matching figures gather, they must build a new level of the tower using those pieces. The animal figures vary in height and balance, shaping how stable the structure becomes as it grows.

Some action cards interact with the rotating jungle base beneath the tower. When Bichito cards are played, players must rotate the leaf board to align hidden insects beneath specific openings, adding a spatial and physical challenge to the construction. Other action cards can rotate turn order, flip or pause the timer, increasing the pressure as time passes.

The game unfolds across three progressive Rituals. Ritual 1 introduces the core system through increasing levels and optional missions. Ritual 2 adds Totems that impose persistent physical or structural constraints. Ritual 3 introduces mandatory structural patterns that must remain visible in the final tower.

If the tower collapses, players may rebuild as long as time remains. HATI creates a shared experience of coordination under pressure, encouraging perseverance, sustained attention, and learning to recover together when things fall apart.

-description from developer

What not to do in a crowded elevator? In this game you have 240 cards with fun performances that must be improvised. Players are divided into teams, and a mediator draws a scene and times the performance, using a whistle to signal when the improvisation ends. During a round, one team acts out the challenge while the mediator evaluates the scene and awards points if the performance is successful.

Challenges include crazy scenes, change it all, ask away, abc chat, and secret friend. The team that earns the most points after the rounds wins the game.

All players with pencil and paper in hand: a different question-and-answer game begins now! To win, besides answering questions correctly, you must help the other players by giving clues — drawing or acting in mime — and earn points if they get it right. Whoever advances furthest along the board's timeline wins. There are 400 questions about the history of Brazil, from the Discovery era to the present day.

boardgame: Polarizando: a disputa narrativa na politica brasileira
description:
Polarizando portrays the polarization phenomenon between social classes, left and right, order and subversion, that in recent years dominated the way politics is contested in Brazil.

Each player represents one of four narratives: Socialism (far left), Lulismo (center-left), Lavajatismo (center-right), and Bolsonarismo (far right), competing to achieve hegemony and popular support by game's end.

Each player deploys characters, militants, schemes, and battle cries unique to their narrative. Rally your militants, cancel opponents, organize a revolution or coup d'etat, and seize narrative hegemony in national politics.

AIŌN: AGES OF CONQUEST is a strategy and conquest game in which you control the destiny of a civilization throughout human history. You begin at the dawn of history and progress through several hundred years each turn, all the way to the 20th century.

You will choose new cultures to add to your civilization, granting you unique abilities, elite armies and fleets, and some of the greatest leaders in history.

Advance your technology and complete missions to outmaneuver rival civilizations by annexing their cities through war or religious conversion.

AIŌN is a game with virtually no element of chance; your choices will determine the triumph of your civilization!

AIŌN is a game in the CO³ (Conceive-Convert-Conquer) series initiated by DEFCON 1, which offers asymmetrical conquest games with modern and fluid mechanics, taking place in documented historical contexts that allow for alternate history.

The game plays in 5 turns maximum, each divided in phases: 

     Initialization phase
     Evolution phase
     Religion phase
     Production phase
     Breakthrough phase
     Campaign phase


How to Win:
to win you need to control all the cities marked with a star on your continent and some cities of your main opponent's continent.
With 4+ players you secretly select your main opponent.
You know who to target but you don't know who is targeting you.

boardgame: Mech vs. Mech: A tabletop mech skirmish game for people with short attention spans
description:
Grab a few battle mech miniatures, read 5 pages of rules, and play!

Mech vs. Mech is a simple battle mech game for your tabletop that allows you to customise your mech build, optimise its attacks and speed, while mitigating the strain of battle. 
As your mech levels up, you can add features or ground troops.

Mech vs. Mech is a complex game with a simple 2-action game loop.

It's another busy day at the Aster-3B Galactic Shipyard and that means you'll be racing your fellow mechanics to complete all available ship orders before they expire. Grab your materials and blueprints so you can start building some spaceships!

Build spaceships to earn cubits, otherwise known as "bits" and make sure you don't leave a job half completed or a lawsuit will dig into your paycheck. Once it's time to clock out, whoever collected the most cubits wins!

How To Play (Overview)
