On a player's turn, they draft dice from a common pool so that they gain six ship parts. The Dice Drafting mechanic in this game ensures each player gains six ship parts on their turn regardless of small or large dice rolls. This removes the "Dice Hate Me" issue of small rolls.

After gaining ship parts, the player either builds a single spaceship of their choice that they have the parts to build, train in a specific ship part installation method, or pass the turn to prepare to build a more important ship.

Whenever the dice need to be re-rolled, the Shipyard rotates on its axis and any ships that were not built fast enough will enter the scrapyard. Whichever players have the most ship materials matching each ship gains a lawsuit which are negative 2 Cubits (victory points) at the end of the game.

Another exciting feature of the game is a variable Dayshift Deck and variable Nightshift Deck that tracks the number of turns in the game as well as adding special abilities and events to make each game unique.

-description from designer

Gather 'round the fire and test your roasting skills in Swamp Mallows, the deliciously fun game of strategy, risk, and sweet rewards!

Each turn, players draw Heat Tokens from one of three bags. Will you play it safe with a light toast, or take a chance for the ultimate golden-brown perfection? But be careful too much heat, and your marshmallow will burn to a crisp!

Compete to satisfy hungry campers with perfectly roasted marshmallows. The player who feeds the hungriest of campers before the fire dies out wins!

Do you have what it takes to roast the perfect Marshmallow?

—description from the publisher

Get ready for the first episode of Marianne Bagelmaine's brand new cooking show!

Up to 6 contestants search their pantry's scattered supplies for the correct ingredients to make recipes that Marianne gathered through her tumultuous adventures!

Who will put the most ingredients in the central oven and manage to keep their calm during this Pantry Panic!?

Pantry Panic is a memory-driven ingredient game where players race to complete shared recipes by managing their own hidden pantry stacks. On your turn, you either draw two Ingredient Cards from your personal Unsorted Ingredient Stack and place them into any Pantry Nook on your mat (building your hidden layout), or you pick one Ingredient Card from a Pantry Nook. If the ingredient appears on the current Active Recipe Card(s), you add it to the recipe and may continue collecting from orthogonally adjacent nooks—so long as each ingredient remains valid and you don’t pick from the same nook twice in a row. If you draw an ingredient not listed, or run out of valid moves, your turn ends.

Play continues clockwise as players strategically remember ingredient locations and push their luck to complete recipes efficiently. Once five unique ingredients have been placed on an Active Recipe Card, the round ends and players score. Used Ingredient Cards are returned to their owners and reshuffled, the completed Recipe Card is removed, and a new Recipe Card becomes active. The game continues through the deck of Five Recipe Cards, and when none remain, the game ends.

-description from designer

Seidrleik is a Norse mythology-inspired chess variant where fate itself becomes a weapon. Drawing from the ancient magic of Seiðr — the power wielded by Odin and Freyja to reshape destiny — players guide pieces that momentarily become conduits for the gods' will. The game transforms the familiar chessboard into a battlefield where mortal strategy and divine intervention intertwine.

The flow of each turn is bound by ritual. A player must first move a piece without capture, making it a Seidr-Bearer. This piece then embarks on a journey dictated by an eight-sided die: rolling determines its path across the board, swapping places with any piece it encounters — friend or foe. The Bearer continues moving, roll after roll, until it can go no further, frozen at the board's edge. Only then does steel enter: the player must make a capture with a different piece, striking where the weaving of fate has created an opening.

Pawns walk a harsh path in Seidrleik. Those swapped onto the back rank vanish entirely — claimed by the gods. A pawn that bears Seidr remains a pawn throughout its journey, never promoting during the weaving. If it ends its path on the back rank, it too is lost. The only path to glory lies in the Call of Steel: a pawn standing on the seventh rank that captures an opponent's piece on the eighth promotes as in standard chess — a rare moment of mortal triumph amid divine chaos.

Victory is brutally simple: capture the opponent's king. There is no check, no checkmate, no special protection for royalty. The king is just another warrior in a golden helm, and the game ends when it falls. With no castling, no en passant, and draws only by mutual agreement, every match continues until blood is spilled — a true saga shaped by the threads the Norns spin.

—description from the designer

Aliens and Fish is the ultimate family game with lots of educational value, including spatial reasoning, image association and strategic thinking. The unique game play and silly images make this game memorable… easily a favorite for both kids and adults.

In this thinking game, players accumulate as many points as they can by carefully matching and stacking wooden picture bricks on a rotating game board grid. Each picture brick is printed with wacky characters and objects. The player with the most points win.

The game comes in a bright colored compact box, and includes instructions, 33 Picture Bricks, 40 Green Cubes, 40 Red Cubes, 1 Drawstring Bag, 1 Game Board Grid.

Furry Pawlitics is a light strategic card game for 2–4 players set in a busy veterinary clinic. Each player takes on the role of an ambitious vet doctor trying to manage patients, earn trust, and build the strongest reputation by the end of the day. As pets arrive and fill the clinic’s cubicles, players must navigate the subtle “politics” of placement, timing, and competition.

On their turn, players choose one main action: bring a pet into a cubicle, move or swap pets, attend a pet with their marker, fulfil a Treatment Goal, change a Goal, reserve a pet for later, or directly collect a pet by spending Trust Tokens. Goal Cards require specific patterns of pets to appear in the cubicles, and players must have their marker on at least one pet in that pattern to complete it. When fulfilled, those pets are added to the player’s collection and contribute to scoring.

The waiting room creates tension and resource management: pets in the front row are freely available, while accessing pets further back requires placing Trust Tokens on those ahead. Pets that accumulate too many waiting tokens leave the clinic, limiting opportunities. Each round is shaped by a “Clinic Hour” card that introduces small shifts, boosts, or setbacks as the workday progresses.

After 13 Clinic Hour cards are revealed, the clinic closes. Players score points from completed Treatment Goals and from two public End Game Scoring objectives revealed at setup. Pets in hand count as negative points. The player with the highest total score is declared the Best Doctor.

—description from the designer

Each moon brings a new rift in the sky, cracking the earth open and revealing crystals of rare power. It's time for the factions to wield their weapons and claim this treasure. 

From dawn to dusk, strategically deploy your creatures to collect 6 crystals before your rival. Each faction has its own deck and gameplay. Yield at the right moments to regain the upper hand and challenge your opponent to raise the stakes of the duel! 

Will you choose the might of Soul Blade, the agility of Moon Dragon, or the trickery of Black Thorn?

—description from the publisher

Each moon brings a new rift in the sky, cracking the earth open and revealing crystals of rare power. It's time for the factions to wield their weapons and claim this treasure.

From dawn to dusk, strategically deploy your creatures to collect 6 crystals before your rival. Each faction has its own deck and gameplay. Yield at the right moments to regain the upper hand and challenge your opponent to raise the stakes of the duel!

Will you choose the cunning of Death Whisper, the ferocity of Sea Raider, or the endurance of Fire Wind?

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Следствие: Дело не ждёт (Suspects: The Case Can't Wait)
description:
Следствие: Дело не ждёт ("Suspects: The Case Can't Wait") is the fourth collection of investigations featuring Claire Harper, which presents three mysterious cases in one box in classic format.

Claire Harper was born in 1910. She is an avid reader of Agatha Christie, a world traveller, and more British than 5 o’clock tea. Oxford University admitted her after implementing quotas for female students in 1927. She followed in her father’s footsteps and graduated with the key to unlocking her dreams: a degree in criminal law. Now Claire Harper investigates, observes, discovers, and thwarts. There’s no mystery that is too twisted for her brilliant mind, no case that’s too hard for her to crack. Claire Harper isn’t just a detective. She’s the very best.

In each case of Suspects, you will read Clue cards to progress through the plot like a book, discuss and reason amongst yourselves, and form hypotheses. Your goal is to score the most points when you answer the questions about the case. The "faster" you answer (i.e. having drawn and read as few Clue cards as possible), the more points you will score.

boardgame: Saumora Island of Secrets: A Campaign for Four Against Darkness
description:
Saumora is a large island with diverse terrain, from the towering mountains in the North to the lush jungle in the center, and the treacherous coastline to the East. In the South, the island is home to a variety of prehistoric creatures, including Thunder Lizards (dinosaurs).

6 missions (most of them playable multiple times), new vermin, minion, boss, weird and unique monsters, new secrets, new spells and new magic items.

—description from the publisher

El rey ha convocado una carrera en el reino por su cumpleaños. ¿El premio para el vencedor? Comer gratis en el mercado, eso sí, el último se lo comerá el dragón Manolo.
Fantasy Race es un juego de carreras asimétrico, de 3 a 6 jugadores, ambientado en un mundo de fantasía medieval en el que tomaremos el control de 1 de los personajes que conforman el juego, siendo el primero que cruce la línea de meta el vencedor.
Usa tus poderosas habilidades, armas y suerte con los dados para así poder alzarte con la victoria.

El objetivo es muy simple: ganar la carrera. Para ello deberás de cruzar la línea de meta y ser el que llegue más lejos. En caso contrario, si quedas el último, te comerá el dragón Manolo.

Cuando un jugador cruce la línea de meta, se acabará la ronda, haciendo que todos los jugadores tengan el mismo número de turnos.

El turno de un jugador consiste en: lanza los dados hasta que todos tengan un valor distinto, a continuación llevatelos a tu tablero para afectuar las acciones que les correspondan.

La gracia del juego reside en la asimetria de las habilidades de los personajes, además de cartas de evento que añadirán muchas más risas a cada partida.


The king has called for a race across the kingdom for his birthday. The prize for the winner? Free food at the market—but the last one to finish will be eaten by Manolo the dragon.
Fantasy Race is an asymmetrical racing game for 3 to 6 players, set in a medieval fantasy world. You'll take control of one of the game's characters, and the first one to cross the finish line will be the winner.
Use your powerful skills, weapons, and luck with the dice to claim victory.

The goal is very simple: win the race. To do this, you must cross the finish line and be the one who goes the farthest. Otherwise, if you come in last, Manolo the Dragon will eat you.

When a player crosses the finish line, the round ends, ensuring all players have the same number of turns.

A player's turn consists of: rolling the dice until they all have different values, then taking them to your board to perform the corresponding actions.

The fun of the game lies in the asymmetry of the characters' abilities, as well as event cards that will add even more laughs to each game.

-description from designer

boardgame: Twisted Axes: 36 Magic and Unusual Axes for Four Against Darkness
description:
This zine contains 36 axes for Four Against Darkness. The list features both mundane and magical axes, and is formatted as a d6 and d66 table to generate an axe as loot.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Twisted Daggers: 36 Magic and Unusual Daggers for Four Against Darkness
description:
This zine includes 36 magical and mundane daggers for Four Against Darkness. formatted as a single d66 table, allowing you to select a random dagger as part of loot.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Twisted Traps and Snares: 36 Wilderness Traps for Four Against Darkness
description:
This zine is a large, d66 table with 36 new traps for Four Against Darkness to be used in outdoor settings.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: The Beast Bulletin: A Collection of Monster Profiles – Volume 1
description:
The Beast Bulletin is an ongoing collection of monster profiles for Four Against Darkness. Each monster is presented with a brief description, a black and white illustration and game statistics to be used with adventures of all levels. The players are encouraged to collect and print out the profiles, arranging them in a 3-ring binder. Volume 1 of the Beast Bulletin contains: Ant People Warriors, Animated Limbs, Carrion Worms, Bilge Rats, Mind Screamer, Dark Lord of Xichtul, Black Manticore, Scissor Monster, Elephant Man, Greed Demon, Hobgoblin Blademasters, Mudmen, Boulder Beast, Deep Demons, Troll, Large Ghouls, Ghoul King/Queen, General Rules and Guidelines

—description from the publisher

boardgame: The Beast Bulletin: A Collection of Monster Profiles – Volume 2
description:
The Beast Bulletin Volume 2 has game statistics and information for the following creatures: Goblin Pirates, Vampire Bats, Deep Trolls, Werewolf, Homunculis, Zingram, Zombie Minotaur, Dragon Man, Deep Demon Lord, Chaos Lizardman, Orc Reavers, Troglodyte Lizardmen, Dark Elves Crossbowmen, Goblins, Eyeball Monsters, Zombies, Undead Pirate Captain and Deep Kobold Hunter. Each creature is on a separate letter-sized page, optimized for home printing and collecting in a 3-ring binder.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Play With Fire: The Card Game
description:
Play With Fire is a competitive elimination card game for 2 to 6 players (15-30 min) where the goal is to be the last survivor with at least one active Heart Card. The core gameplay centers on a dynamic, push-your-luck mana system used to fuel tactical spells and effects against opponents, featuring 110 hand-illustrated cards.

Each player chooses a Hero from those available by consulting the Matchmaking Table based on the number of participants. Each profile indicates how many and which Heart Cards (lives) are at the player's disposal at the start, which are placed face-up next to the hero. The battlefield is prepared with the Brazier (6 Fire Cards: 5 Blue Flames and 1 Red Flame, placed face-down and shuffled), the Magic Deck (Instant, Sorcery, and Equipment cards), and the Hearts Deck (remaining lives). All players start with zero cards in hand.

The game proceeds clockwise through three distinct phases. Phase 1: Ignition involves drawing a card from the Magic Deck, respecting the maximum limit of three cards in hand. Phase 2: Blaze (The Trial of Fire) is when the player challenges the Brazier by choosing a target, declaring their intention, and revealing a Fire Card. If Targeting Yourself: a Blue Flame grants +1 Mana and the player is forced to continue the Trial (unless using specific magic) by revealing another card, deciding whether to target themselves again or change the objective to attack an opponent; a Red Flame causes the loss of 1 Life, ending the Round immediately and requiring the Brazier to be reshuffled. If Targeting an Opponent: a Blue Flame causes the attack to fail, the target gains +1 Mana, and the player can still spend accumulated Mana to play cards or abilities before the turn ends; a Red Flame causes the target to lose 1 Life, ending the Round immediately and requiring the Brazier to be reshuffled. Phase 3: Embers concludes the turn as the fire subsides and any amount of Mana accumulated and not spent during the turn dissipates. It is important to note that Blue Flames revealed during turns remain face-up, increasing the risk for whoever challenges the brazier next, until the Red Flame is revealed.

Accumulated Mana is used to play Magic Cards or to activate the hero's specific ability by spending the indicated cost. Sorceries and Equipment can only be played during the Blaze phase of the player's turn, while Instants can be played at any time, during the player's turn or during opponents' turns. Each hero has a unique power that can be activated once per turn during the Blaze phase. By balancing the escalating risks of the Brazier with the strategic use of hand cards and hero abilities, players must outlast their rivals to secure victory in the trial.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: The Beast Bulletin: A Collection of Monster Profiles – Volume 3
description:
Beast Bulletin Volume 3 contains illustrated game profiles for Four Against Darkness for the following creatures: Rats, Stirges, Mutant Rats, Vampire Frogs, White Gargoyles, Skeletons, Cyclopean Thunderbeast, Scimitar Monster, Goblin Boneblade, Chaos Lizardman, Dimensional Invader, Hogman Brute, Mutant Toadoid, Sphynx of Nekhertas, Deranged Conjurer, and Lady of the Moths. Each profile is on a separate, letter-size page, perfect for printing at home and collecting in a 3-ring binder.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: The Beast Bulletin: A Collection of Monster Profiles – Volume 4
description:
This issue of the Beast Bulletin has a mix of new and classic monsters, including: Flying Reptiles, Rabid Rodents, Temple Bats, Serpent People Warriors of Ssiklis, Chaos Gremlins, Chaos Goblins, The Rotten Ones, Xichtul's Spawn, Giant Three-headed Chicken, Hag of Mount Larunn, Minotaur, Mummy, Chaos Halfling Champion, Chaos Centaur, Acolyte of Zuras and Grimgaur Automata.

—description from the publisher

NEIN  is a high-speed, family card game where players will all play cards to move a central token along a mountain path. Completing a lap on your turn will earn you points based on how far past the finish line you land.

Players are competing to be the first to score (and keep) EXACTLY nine points. Once a player has nine points on their score card, it is the job of the other players to force them to score again and send them backwards!

—description from the publisher

Imperial Germany laid down her last dreadnought in January 1915, and never completed her. But planning continued for new classes of battleships, battle cruisers, armored cruisers and scout cruisers. All understood that they could not possibly be built during the course of the ongoing First World War, but would be laid down afterwards.

Reflecting the lessons first of the battles of the Falklands, Coronel and Helgoland Bight, and eventually those of Jutland, the new German designs continued to emphasize protection. At the urging of sea officers, the new ships also now carried armament equal to that of the British, and would make much higher speeds as well.

Across the North Sea, the British Admiralty had no clear idea of what the Germans might be planning to build, but could state with certainty that they and other potential enemies would continue to improve their warships. New British dreadnoughts would carry ever-more-powerful armaments, including a new, secret 18-inch gun.

Jutland: Linienschiffe 1919 is an expansion for our Great War at Sea: Jutland game, studying these drawing-board battleships that would never actually be launched. It includes background essays, thirty-one new scenarios, and 80 new silky-smooth die-cut playing pieces (60 double-sized ship pieces and 20 square ones). It is not playable by itself; you’ll need our Jutland game and Risk Fleet book to play the scenarios. You can, of course, just read the essays and fondle the pieces without owning either. We won’t tell.

New pieces include three classes of German battleships designed but never built: a fast battleship with eight 15-inch guns, a battleship with ten 15-inch guns in five turrets, with three of them in a unique triple-stacked arrangement. And an even larger ship with 16.5-inch guns. There are two new classes of battle cruiser, one with 15-inch guns, one with 16.5-inch guns. And two variations on the fast armored cruiser proposed after the Battle of Jutland, one with ten 8.2-inch guns, and one with twenty (20) 5.9-inch guns. Plus, you add more scout cruisers and destroyers to your High Seas Fleet.

The British for their part receive their improved but slower Queen Elizabeth class, with ten 15-inch guns, and the massive ships proposed to carry eight 18-inch guns apiece, based on the Hood design but slower with battleship-scale protection. Plus their own fast armored cruiser.

Jutland: Linienschiffe 1919 is a new edition of our old Jutland: 1919 expansion.

—description from the publisher
family: Series: Great War at Sea (Avalanche Press)

description:
Common Sense is an asymmetrical trick-taking card driven wargame. One player will take control of the 13 colonies in their fight for independence while the other uses the might of the British crown to quash the rebellion. The game revolves around the Will to Fight track which tracks both sides' willingness to continue to fight the war. As it is an asymmetrical game, both sides have different problems they will have to manage to resist the decline of their Will to Fight. The colonies are primarily concerned with morale and their ability to believe that they could potentially win their independence. Losing control of colonies and failures of the Continental Congress to galvanize the colonies will have the heaviest impact on their conviction. The British on the other hand never had hearts and minds to begin with, as the war was never popular at home. Instead, their Will to Fight represents parliament’s willingness to continue to fund the war. Poor results relative to commitment of forces as well as losses of British Regulars can spell a speedy exit from the war for them. The game will end when both player’s Will to Fight markers converge on the track, or rarely at the end of 1783, with position on the track determining much of the game’s scoring. 
 Common Sense is played with 2 unique 36 card decks. Each card in a player’s deck is also unique, and represents a key personality, battle, event, or concept from the American Revolution. Cards are divided into 4 suits, which are Battle, Mobilize, Recruit, and Special. Since the game is asymmetrical, while Battle, Mobilize, and Recruit serve similar purposes for both players, their actual executions vary in some ways. Battles let you engage in fights with the enemy in the same space, Mobilize lets you move your armies between spaces, and Recruit lets you bolster your forces. The Special suit on the other hand varies radically and really showcases differences between the two sides. The Colonial Special suit governs training of militia into the Continental Army as well as all interactions with the French. The British Special suit on the other hand manages native led operations as well as the might of the British navy. Each card grants a certain number of actions of a specific type, as well as a historical themed event. Events can be one off effects, powerful action modifiers for the current turn, or remain in play for multiple turns granting powerful abilities or changing core rules.

—description from the designer

boardgame: Eagles in the Sky: Plan 1919 Expansion
description:
Plan 1919 assumes that World War I continued for another year, allowing both sides to put into widespread production aircraft that saw little or no action in the actual war.

The expansion includes modifications to the Engagements in the main game, plus a new campaign.

This expansion is not a complete game, it requires a copy of Eagles in the Sky.

—description from the designer

A Familiar Find: A charming, fast paced, strategic card game fun for the whole family.

The familiars are on an important mission for their spellcasters: gather rare ingredients from every corner of Exandria. But you’re not alone, competing familiars from rival summoners lurk nearby, setting traps, and attempting to sabotage your efforts!

In A Familiar Find, players collect ingredients from locations represented by piles of cards.

On their turn, a player takes all the cards from one pile and adds them to their collection. They then place three cards from their hand into any locations, distributing them among the piles. Two of the cards are placed face up and one is placed face down.

Over the course of the game, players attempt to set aside valuable cards for future turns while misleading opponents into taking piles filled with negative cards. Careful placement and timing are key to collecting the most valuable set of ingredients.

Can you collect the ingredients before your competitors? Or will their traps leave you tangled in a web of misfortune?

Fast-paced. Strategic. Fun for all ages.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Off With Their Heads!: Joker Promo
description: Two joker cards, illustrated as Tweedle Dee and Tweedle Dum, which play as either the highest black suited card, or lowest red suited card when revealed.

boardgame: Second World War at Sea: The Emperor's Sword
description:
In the years before the Second World War exploded, both the United States and the Empire of Japan expanded their fleets and air forces, planning to build even more new ships and planes. The outbreak of war scuttled many of these drawing-board vessels, but that hasn’t kept us from adding them to Second World War at Sea.

The Emperor's Sword is a massive expansion set for Second World War at Sea, taking place in the alternative history setting we’re calling The Long War. It features the ships and planes planned by the Japanese, pitting them against the United States Navy and the additional ships and planes of its own building plans.

You get 420 brand-new die-cut and silky-smooth pieces: 140 double-sized pieces for ships and 280 standard-sized ones for aircraft. Plus a big new operational map, extending the map from Midway Deluxe Edition all the way to the West Coast.

The Emperor’ Sword isn’t playable by itself; you'll need Midway Deluxe Edition.

—description from the publisher
family: Series: Second World War at Sea (Avalanche Press)

description:
In Hot Pot Hotshot, the hottest hands win the pot! In this deliciously fast-paced party game, players race to build the perfect hot pot by matching ingredients and slapping fast. Menu cards display tasty ingredient combinations worth points. Players flip ingredient cards, and when the revealed ingredients match a menu card—slap the pot! The fastest player is the hot pot hotshot!

—description from the publisher

Did you know that the Berlin Wall was 4 meters tall? And that giraffes have no vocal cords? Use all your knowledge to say what's true and false in the board game version of the fun game Certo ou Errado! There are 400 new challenges to guess if it's right or wrong!
This pack contains 24 cards: 22 Challengers, the Cheshire Whammy mod card (which allows you to sabotage an opponent's weapon), and Character Select weapon card (allowing you to switch contestants).

Based on the famous videos from the Nostalgia channel, the Nostalgia game features a series of cards with the most varied themes for all ages to try to guess the answer. YouTuber Felipe Castanhari from the Nostalgia channel challenges you to show all your knowledge about movies and series. Play with your friends and family.

In the heart of the Enchanted Sporewood, mushroom wizards sprout from ancient fungi once every hundred years to compete in the Grand Mycelium Games—a magical contest of wits, stealth, and spellcraft.

Each player guides a pair of wizards across the ever-shifting forest floor, secretly choosing cardinal directions to move, outmaneuver rivals, and collect powerful spellbooks. Every book added to your stack increases your magical prestige—but no collection is ever safe. Crafty opponents can pilfer your hard-earned tomes, and special powers earned along the way can open unexpected opportunities.

As the forest twists, paths change, and opportunities appear and vanish, players must balance timing, positioning, and a little daring to stay ahead.

When the spores settle, the wizard with the most valuable spellbook collection wins the Grand Mycelium Games!

—description from the publisher

Light & Night is board game played in a room without the light, or at least partially lighted. Game is played with a cards and with a cards back glowing in the dark.

It is educational game about European mythologies to help children fight against the fear of darkness.

Elements of the game are: cards, dices and sun shaped lamp positioned in the middle of the board, used to light up the game and to power up cards back with light.

Glades Emperor is a historical board game where players play as an outlaw in the swamps of southern Florida in the year 1910. The goal of the game is to establish the most robust covert economic empire without losing too much reputation with the local residents.

Each outlaw per game will develop a unique network of operations carried out through the workforce of a band of hired hands. These workers must deal with local residents, navigate the harsh Florida environment and the contend with the ambitions of rival outlaws.

After a series of rounds, the outlaw with the most valuable economy and strongest grip on the area wins. However, the outlaw that has most bad reputation among the locals is eliminated from the game, regardless of their wealth.

—description from the publisher
family: Theme: Wetlands / Swamps / Bogs / Fens / Marshland

boardgame: Corps of Discovery: Kickstarter Map Pack
description:
10 Maps that were given to Kickstarter backers as a bonus for supporting us on Kickstarter! If you didn't back us on Kickstarter, then here are 10 new maps!

6 for Fauna: 1011, 1012, 1013, 1014, 1015, 1016
4 for Flora: 2011, 2012, 2013, 2014

Note: These maps are included in the Deluxe and Ultimate editions.
Insecta Maps 1 has 10 new maps for Insecta (3015-3024)
Maldonado Maps 1 has 10 new maps for Maldonado (5015-5024)
Vameter Maps 1 has 10 new maps for Vameter (4015-4024)
Flora Maps 1 has 10 new maps for Flora (2025-2034)!
Fauna Maps 1 has 10 new maps for Fauna (1028-1037)!
Fog Maps 1 has 10 new maps for Fog (6015-6024)

boardgame: Corps of Discovery: Game Store Day 2025 Map Pack
description:
This map pack has 12 new maps!! During the month of November 2025, you could have asked your retailer for a free code to download this map pack.

Fauna (1040, 1041)
Flora (2037, 2038)
Insecta (3026, 3027)
Vameter (4025, 4026)
Maldonado (5025, 5026)
Fog (6025, 6026)

Build a powerful dungeon worthy of the Demon King's approval! A strategic tile-placement game using a bag-building system.

Bag Build Dungeons is a simultaneous, bag-building dungeon game where each player acts as a dungeon manager trying to impress the Demon King by constructing the strongest dungeon.

Each round has four phases: (1) draw five monster tiles from your bag, (2) gain bonuses present on the tiles drawn, (3) place one drawn monster onto your personal dungeon grid following adjacency and placement restrictions (or take a penalty if you cannot legally place) and (4) use the remaining drawn tiles to attract stronger monsters by paying specific tile combinations or substituting mana, then return unused tiles to your bag.

Over time your bag improves with stronger monsters, which both score more and fuel future draws. After 16 rounds, players score points from their monsters (in bag and placed), possibly score objective cards, and may lose points for penalties. The highest total points wins, with leftover mana as a tiebreaker.

Fated Blade: Heroes Arise (Expansion)

- Heroes Arise is the first expansion for the Fated Blade series. You will need a copy of the core book to make use of the expansion -

What is in the book?
Heroes Arise expands on the original book adding sixteen new Exalted Heroes, four new scenarios, a new army list of Eldritch Horrors and a new narrative campaign and lore!
The rules and format of the new book will be very familiar to those who have played Fated Blade, making it a seamless addition to your collection.
Heroes Arise promises new cunning war-tricks, new strategies and a whole host of fun new additions.
The expansion makes the game into the Backgammon of the wargaming world – but will the fates be your friend or your foe?

New Exalted Heroes
Sixteen new Exalted Heroes, from the brutal Werewolf to the sneaky Rogue, from the swift Wolfblade to the heavy Witch Knight, from the cunning Fatemancer to the arcane artillery of the Ringmancer.
Every new Exalted hero introduces new abilities and strategies. They are divided between three general 'classes' – Fighter heroes for damage output, Support heroes for enhancing troops and cursing enemies and Hybrid heroes who are a mix of the two.

The Eldritch Horrors Army List
A new army list which covers all of your favourite Eldritch Horrors – from Shelled Warriors and Deep Ones to Drowned Zombies and Corrupted Riders.
The Eldritch Horrors list favours close ranged skirmish shooting, fear-causing beasties and armoured hulks who wade through hordes of lesser foes. But it lacks long range shooting and the sheer versatility of land-based armies.

Four New Scenarios 
Sword and Sorcery – Send an elite warband to rescue a VIP before enemy reinforcements cut you off. This scenario favours a swift, mounted attacker charging into the fray.
Monster Slayer – The race is on to slay as any many mighty monsters as possible, taking their hides back to your paymaster. One slain monster respawns onto the board at the start of every turn, making this scenario a nail-biter from start to finish.
Enemy Under Siege – Launch a daring assault against a fortified defender. Whoever controls the fortress walls is the victor.
Mega Battle – Double up your warbands and unleash the horde against your opponent! The mega battle favours Support heroes as they have larger units to enchant or curse.

The Lore Section
The Lore section of the book covers the Myth Cycle of Eldnox – from the Age of the Trees, to the first Abyssal Host and the glorious days of Dawnshield, followed by the rise of the Guardians and their transformation into the Drakorian Empire.
The west-realm of Eratur is covered in more detail, expounding on the grip of Imperial Drakoria, the might of the Royal Army, the independent Warrior Guids and the Knightly Orders of the western realms.
Finally the lore section ends with a narrative campaign – The First Uprising. Follow the mighty Rul Tharyn as she gathers her Wyrm Slayers on an epic quest against the Imperial Drakoria, culminating in a final battle in the Drakorian Citadel.

The Bylands Awaken
Step into the Bylands, a realm teetering on the edge of destruction, where ancient rivalries, raw magic, and untamed warlords converge in a cataclysmic struggle for dominance. Warhost brings this world to life as a thrilling fantasy compendium to The Barons' War, offering a fresh set of rules, new factions, and an immersive narrative that will shape the future of this shattered land.

Key Features:

     Unleash Magic's Fury: Discover the unpredictable power of magic, woven into every aspect of warfare. Whether you're commanding mighty kingdoms or Orc tribes, magic is an omnipresent force on the battlefield, shaping strategy and changing the course of battle.
     Fierce New Factions: Lead unique forces into battle, from the noble Elves and resilient Dwarves to the savage Orcs and the haunting Dead. Each race brings its own abilities, traits, and strategic depth, ensuring no two battles are ever the same.
     Monstrous Behemoths: Prepare for the arrival of massive, awe-inspiring creatures that will challenge your tactics and threaten to trample everything in their path. These colossal foes require special rules to defeat, bringing a new level of excitement and complexity to your games.
     Campaign of Consequence: Immerse yourself in the Battle for the Bylands, a gripping campaign spread across three interconnected scenarios. Your decisions will directly influence the unfolding narrative, escalating from minor skirmishes to a climactic final battle where the fate of the Bylands hangs in the balance.
     Legendary Heroes and Villains: Encounter unforgettable characters like Gromgarr the Marauder, Prince Elarael, Morrok Manflayer, and Mhanadir. Their destinies are tied to the war, and their actions could shape the outcome of the conflict.
     Refined Tactical Play: Building on the core rules of The Barons' War, Warhost adds new layers of strategy and customisation, ensuring each race feels distinct. Special rules for new units, weapons, armour, and abilities deepen the experience while keeping gameplay streamlined and accessible.
     The Battle for the Bylands


The breach of a long-sealed gate has set in motion a war unlike any the Bylands have ever known. Elves and Orcs clash at the forefront, but their battles are only the beginning. Each faction has its own agenda, each with unique goals and motives that will bring them into conflict and force alliances to shift. Will your Warhost emerge victorious, or will the Bylands fall into ruin?

This supplement introduces exciting new units, abilities, and strategic options that will push your tactical acumen to the limit. Whether you seek to command armies or lead legendary heroes, Warhost offers an immersive and expansive fantasy wargaming experience that will challenge and captivate you.

The fate of the Bylands is in your hands.

Will you rise to the challenge?

Only those who combine well, place their ownership markers at the right time and keep track of everything can make the right decision and ultimately win “Biome Hikers”.

The goal of the game: By skillfully placing the property markers on landscapes at the right time and developing them, you
will ultimately score the most points.

Game Setup: All Biome Hikers tiles are shuffled and placed face down in the center of the table as a draw
pile. Each player receives 4 ownership markers of their color. The player who last hiked in a landscape goes first.

Weather extension:
- All weather tiles are shuffled face down and one is drawn at the start of the game. This is placed to the side and determines
the weather until a new one is drawn and placed on top of it.

The game ends when the draw pile is empty. Once everyone has placed their last tile, scoring begins.

—description from the designer

Sweet Revenge is an expansion for Dustlings that introduces a new faction – Her Highness Candy.
Her Sweetness commands the Crumbs and Gummies in a fight for survival in the Forgotten Corner.

Play against time and don’t let yourself get licked away.
Make sure to deploy the Gummies – your bodyguards.
Move the Crumbs wisely so the Creatures are forced to eat them.
Don’t assume Her Highness plays only defensively – look for an opportunity to eliminate all Creatures. It can be done!

The expansion adds support for a fourth player; however, when playing as Landrynka, the minimum player count is 3.

—description from the designer
family: Players: Games with expansions that change player count

description:
You are hungry bears, gathered by the river, paws raised, waiting for the perfect moment to strike.
River cards are revealed one by one: fish, eels, crayfish… and only the first bear to hit gets to eat! But choose your paw wisely, because it will decide how you score points.

In Bas Les Pattes, patience pays off, but wait too long and your meal will slip away.

Each bear only gets four strikes per game, so timing is everything if you want to feast and score big.

Frogs, clams, catfish… advanced cards bring new effects and surprises, keeping every game fresh and full of twists.

Join Grizzy and the Lemmings in this fast-paced game of speed and observation! Grizzy is desperate for a nap, but the Lemmings have decided to play with fireworks again! Quick—help Grizzy stop them with a well-placed frying pan before everything goes BOOM! Who will be fast enough to win the game?

How to Play
Roll and Strike: Roll the dice and be the first to slap the matching Lemming card to win it.

Claim Your Prize: Once you win a card, place it in front of you.

Keep Your Eyes Peeled: The card you're looking for might be around the draw pile or right in front of an opponent!

The Big Scramble: If the matching card isn't visible anywhere, be the first to slap the central draw pile!

Winning the Game: Once the draw pile is empty, the player with the most cards wins!

—description from the publisher

I Agree Hell's Terms leverages a traditional game to implement new mechanics, such as the "Soulless" health being generated based on the player's actual age, or for example, curses will play with factors external to the game itself, such as whether it's raining that day, or even the number of players in the game! And remember, the Devil always has the advantage... Will you be able to get your soul back?

—description from the publisher

Nuits Rouges is a criminal investigation game in augmented reality (AR) with a free companion App.

First, players have to place all game cards (AR markers) & character cards in their indicated locations: on a table, a wall, a piece of furniture. Using their smartphone or tablet, players scan each AR markers to display the room’s 3D objects.   This game needs a free iOS/Android application to be played. (It’s simply called Nuit Rouges). There are also character suspects cards and physical documents to find in an enveloppes.

It is like an escape game, that means players must resolve enigmas, but also a narrative co-operation game as players need to communicate information collected on objects and dialogues with suspects.

There are 2 different levels:

The teaser case: it is a 30min trial investigation case to understand the mechanics and how to play the game.

The Quantum Case: This is the main investigation. It lasts about 90 minutes.

—description from the publisher

Real poker is way too dependent on luck.
It gets much more skill based if you mark your cards or let some slide under the table or up your sleeve!
So trash your cards to keep on winning!
You don‘t even need to know how to play Poker to play Trash Poker!

Trash Poker is a competitive deck-destruction game inspired by the video game Balatro.

Everyone starts with a standard poker deck and competes to play the best hand. During the game you can trash cards from your deck which you do not want and add special cards into it.

Over the course of 8 poker hands you build and destroy your deck, upgrade your stats and trash a lot of cards.  You score Chips by winning poker hands and via having certain cards in your deck or trash at the end of the game.

Whoever has the most chips in the end, wins!

The game also contains 53 foil joker cards you can unlock for future games. These alter the play experience significantly!

—description from the designer

Descend once more into the unknown! The Second Delve expands your Rollgeon Quest experience with a brand-new dungeon sheet and four daring new heroes ready to face the darkness. Every decision carries greater risk-and even greater reward - for those bold enough to enter.

Joining your ranks are four new heroes, each bringing distinct talents and fresh strategies to your roll-and-write campaign. Mix and match them with your original heroes to create powerful synergies and uncover new paths to victory.

Whether you venture alone or alongside your fellow delvers, The Second Delve offers deeper challenges, new combinations, and the thrill of discovery that made Rollgeon Quest a true adventurer’s favorite.

-description from publisher's website

Not all heroes walk on two legs. In Paws and Perils, a new expansion for Rollgeon Quest, loyal and mischievous companions join the adventure! This pack introduces a brand-new dungeon sheet and nine unique pet cards, each bringing its own quirks and abilities to aid your party on their perilous quest.

Before each game, four pets are chosen to accompany your heroes, ensuring every delve unfolds in fresh and surprising ways. Some lend strength or cunning, others a touch of chaos - or simple charm. Among these brave beasts you’ll find a faithful dog, a steadfast tortoise, a sly lizard, a clever rat, and even an adventurous pig.

Whether bounding ahead or loyally trailing behind, these companions add warmth, humor, and unpredictable twists to your dungeon expeditions. Gather your courage - and your creatures - and prepare to face the dangers below together.

-description from publisher's website

A new chapter in the dungeon’s saga begins! Ledger of Legends expands Rollgeon Quest with four remarkable new heroes and a treacherous new dungeon sheet that will test even the most seasoned adventurers. But this time, the rules of glory have changed - how you score your loot and fight your battles follows new paths, challenging players to rethink their strategies and adapt to unfamiliar dangers.

The heroes of the Ledger bring bold abilities and inventive tactics, each rewriting the tale of how fortune and fame are earned in the depths. They can be freely mixed and matched with heroes from the base game and other expansions, opening the door to countless party combinations and new strategies to master.

Every decision counts, every battle matters, and treasure gleams differently in this fresh take on the Rollgeon Quest adventure. Face the unknown, rewrite your legend, and see if your name will be worthy of the Ledger.

-description from publisher's website

Two adventurers enter the dungeon - but only one will emerge victorious. Rivals of Rollgeon transforms Rollgeon Quest into a tense two-player contest played on a single, expanded dungeon sheet, where cunning and strategy matter as much as courage. Each player charts their path through the same labyrinth, racing for treasures, battling monsters, and scheming to outwit their opponent at every turn.

This competitive expansion also introduces four new hero cards that can be freely mixed and matched with heroes from the base game and other expansions, adding fresh tactics and unpredictable twists to every clash.

Face your rival in the depths, seize the glory, and prove who truly rules the dungeon.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Escape from Labyrinth: Trial of Shadows
description:
Ready to shake things up? This expansion for Escape from Labyrinth brings two fun-packed modules to spice up your adventure. Use one, use both - it’s your escape, your rules!

Trial of Shadows throws in 6 new challenge cards with extra goals and clever ways to score big. Prove your team’s got the brains and the guts to earn a spot on the mission to Crete.

Secret Passages swaps out the dice for cards, cranking up the tactics and giving your strategy a serious workout. Every move counts - think smart if you want to outwit the maze!

-description from publisher's website

Athens just leveled up your training ground - welcome to the biggest, twistiest maze yet! Grab your team and dive into this super-sized labyrinth, packed with even more traps, turns, and tight squeezes. Your mission? Guide every hero to safety without leaving a single one behind.

Bigger maze, bigger challenge - let’s see if you’ve got what it takes to escape!

-description from publisher's website

Add a seventh racer to the game with this Dirty Myrtle single character expansion! This add on includes her double sided character card, her screenprinted character pawn, her lap tracker token, and a few copies of her favorite Trash card: Extra Salty Fries.

boardgame: Escape from Labyrinth: Whispers of the Maze
description:
Whispers of the Maze isn’t here to play nice - it’s here to test your brainpower and stamina like never before! This twisty expansion adds sneaky new corridor types: dead ends that halt your heroes in their tracks and split corridors that can send them looping endlessly if you’re not careful.

Think it’s just a stroll through the labyrinth? Think again. One wrong move and your team could be wandering forever. Ready for the challenge? Let the maze mess with your mind!

-description from publisher's website

So you’ve mastered Escape from Labyrinth and guiding six heroes is a breeze? Time to crank up the chaos! Double the challenge, double the fun - now you’re in charge of 12 heroes at once! Think it’s impossible? Maybe. But give it a shot, and who knows - you just might earn a place among the legends of Greek heroism.

Ready to prove you're a true maze master? Let’s see what you’ve got!

-description from publisher's website

A Series of Interesting Choices is a game designed to help you practice your game design skills. It will guide you through the creative process of making a board game – from coming up with an initial idea, to writing down the rules, and finally testing it.

It has been used successfully at multiple universities in Sweden, as part of their game design programs.

In the first part of the game you will have to design an actual, working game, based on concepts found on a hand of cards dealt to you. Then you will play each others games, earning points if you manage to win in some of them. Finally, you must try to guess what cards the other players used for their games – and also hope that they will be able to figure out yours. The person who manages to do all these things in the best way will be the winner.

And if you don’t win? Hopefully you will have become a better game designer anyways!

—description from the designer
language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups

description:
This wild expansion packs in 9 brand-new dinosaur cards - each with trickier, twistier DNA to piece together! Think you're up for the challenge? You’ll need all your puzzle-solving smarts to bring these complex creatures back to life. The power’s in your hands: will you play it safe, or push the limits and unleash the most advanced beasts yet?

-description from publisher's website

This handy expansion brings 18 new cards to boost your game and lend a helping claw! Use them in your regular dino adventures, or break them out when playing with younger explorers or giving newbies a friendly head start. Whether you're leveling the playing field or just mixing things up, these cards add a fun twist to every game!

-description from publisher's website

Get ready to supercharge your science - this expansion adds a brand-new game sheet with extra space for DNA samples! That means even more dinosaurs to bring back to life. Tired of the classic game with just 12 dinos made from 5 DNA fragments each? Step up to the challenge with 14 beasts to resurrect, now needing six different DNA pieces to complete.

It’s tougher, twistier, and way more thrilling. Are you bold enough to dive into this advanced version of Dino Lab? Grab your dice, sharpen those pencils, and let’s see if you’ve got what it takes to conquer the ultimate dino challenge!

-description from publisher's website

Time to raise the stakes! This expansion cranks up the challenge with a brand-new game sheet full of tougher, trickier dinosaurs to bring back. If you’ve mastered the base game and you're itching for something a bit more brain-bending, this one’s for you. Get ready to stretch those grey cells and enjoy that satisfying spark as you crack even more complex DNA codes. Dino Lab just got a whole lot wilder—are you ready to test your genius?

-description from publisher's website

boardgame: The Beast Bulletin: A Collection of Monster Profiles – Volume 5
description:
This issue of the Beast Bulletin has a mixture of new and classic monsters, including some from unpublished books. In this issue you will find: Abyssal Brownies, Kobold Ghouls, Goblin Miners, Wormhounds, Wormspawn, Walking Dead, Orc Shooters, Bog Beasts, Chaos Minotaur, Bodak, Chaos Landwyrm, Orc Maceman, Demon Beast of Harukan, Demon of Kokras Tharn, Bog Minotroll, Ogre, Bog Naga and Hairy Goblins.

—description from the publisher

Heroes from all across time answer the call. The dungeons across time and space threaten the peace of the land and only the bravest of heroes can stand in the way of danger. The best huntress of the land uncovered, an old path to different worlds, after one really violent storm caused by the mad lich. Psionic, a human weapon, crosses the barrier of time, will he change the past to save the future? A curious reporter bit more than she can chew but will she find the truth at the end of the most dangerous caverns? Peaceful life of a village nun came to a close because of monsters swarming out of the old ruins.

DIE!geon: Hero Call is an expansion for Die!geon. It’s compatible with every other add-on for the base game. This expansion includes such heroes as Huntress Rose, Psionic Kayden, Reporter Patrice Collins as well as Priestess Caitlyn. Become one of the heroes and become a living legend.

—description from the publisher

New ways to upgrade your cocktails, earn more points and money, and unlock additional actions during the setup phase. And of course, some adorable little mini glasses to create your cocktails!

Contains:
1 Glasses board
4 Glasses Mini boards
20 Transparent Circle Tokens
4 Player Circle Tokens
16 Glass Cards
1 Collection Card
8 3D mini plastic cocktail glasses

DU-094 that is your serial number. You were grown in the lab as a security for the researchers in cryostasis on your ship. Your job requires you to take care of the spaceship and make sure that researchers will get in one piece onto the alien planet. During one of those routine check ups your autopilot overheated, pulling you forcefully out of the warp drive.

You’ve managed to avoid crashing into a planet but now there are still 5000 light years left for you to get to your destination and you need a new capacitor. Fortunately for you there is an abandoned research station not so far from you. What could go wrong?

DIE!geon: Infestation - Research Station is an expansion for DIE!geon. It’s compatible with every other add-on. This expansion includes Space Guard DU-094 hero as well as Abandoned Research Station dungeon infested by alien swarm. You will have to make your way through worker drones and soldier aliens to get to Alien Queen and come out alive to tell the tale. Sci-fi adventure of DIE!geon is near!

You’re detective Fisher. Back in the day you were the most ambitious cop in the whole city. After you finished your police school you were the youngest detective in your district. Years later new ambitious young cops took over, and you felt like you needed to bust a big crime family before you quit. You were on the trail of the notorious Morisson brothers. They had their hands in every major illegal business in the city and were avoiding justice for years. Your mole finally gave you enough evidence to put them in jail for additional three lives.

With an arrest warrant in hand, you’ve marched right into their hideout, you were here before but now something feels different. When you move through the corridors you sometimes notice moving shadows with a corner of your eye, but when you are looking around you can’t see anyone there. Finally the last place you had to check is the basement. Stairs seemed unnaturally long, but after a while you heard voices. Chanting in weird language that seems to pierce deep into your mind.

DIE!geon Veil of Madness is an expansion for DIE!geon. It’s compatible with every other add-on. This expansion includes Detective Fisher hero as well as Eldritch Mansion dungeon filled with cultists and mutants. You will have to make your way through mutated cultists to stop the Chained One, before he fully emerges into our world, and come out alive to tell the tale. Step into the Eldritch Horror adventure of DIE!geon.

-description from publisher's website

You’ve spent most of your life hunting monsters, there is no monster with a weakness you don’t know. You’ve traveled across the world searching for the source of evil that corrupts the land. Your name, Radcliff, is already praised by the people you’ve saved but you are still determined to finish the job.

You’ve traveled for so long that you forgot when your journey started. Finally after roaming the land, in search of the source of dangers, from the thick fog it finally appeared. The grand castle of the vampire lord filled with unliving horrors from the old stories. Prepare your stakes and silver bullets, save the land or become a part of the blood hungry legions.

DIE!geon: The Eternal Masquarade is an expansion for DIE!geon. It’s compatible with every other add-on. This expansion includes Monster Hunter Radcliff hero as well as Vampire Lord Castle dungeon filled with Ghouls and Vampire Spawn. You will have to make your way through undead legions to defeat the Vampire Lord and come out alive to tell the tale.

-description from publisher's website

Deep within the twisted woods lies the Goblin Bastion, a foul stronghold teeming with snarling marauders and mischief. The trail of the elusive Goblin King leads here - but before any would-be hero can confront him, they must survive the outer defenses. Goblin Marauders prowl the perimeter in vicious packs, while a notorious Goblin Raider, mounted on a snarling armored pig, strikes with brutal speed and chaos. Steel yourself - this is no ordinary dungeon. This is the beating heart of goblin-kind’s fury.

DIE!goen: Goblin Bastion is a expansion packed with an extra Dungeon Sheet to plunge into! Ready to cause some chaos in the world of DIE!geon? Now’s your chance. Jump into Goblin Bastion with any character you fancy—from a glorious Paladin to a Space Guard. It’s your story, your rules, and your goblins to smash. Get in there and show those sneaky green troublemakers who’s boss!

-description from publisher's website

Deep within the stone-choked mountains rises the Golem Stronghold, a fortress of unyielding granite shaped by forgotten hands. Here the Golem Lord broods, his heart of crystal pulsing with cold power, while his army of tireless stone sentinels waits in silence for a hero bold enough to challenge them. The path to his throne is no simple march - towering the Wall guards the outer halls, their eyes glowing with ancient fire, while a dreaded Defender, clad in shards of black glass, strikes with crushing strength and merciless precision. Steel your resolve - this is no mere dungeon. This is the relentless heartbeat of stone itself, and it will not break easily.

DIE!geon: Golem Stronghold is an expansion loaded with a brand-new Dungeon Sheet to conquer! Ready to test your mettle against an army of unyielding stone? Now’s your chance. Step into the Golem Stronghold with any character you choose - from a cunning Thief to a blazing Fire Mage. It’s your story, your rules, and your golems to crush. March inside and prove that even living mountains can fall!

-description from publisher's website

boardgame: The Beast Bulletin: A Collection of Monster Profiles – Volume 6
description:
In this issue of the Beast Bulletin, you will find game profiles for the following nasties: Salt revenant, Minotaur with Twin Clubs, Feathered Lizardbeast, Feathered Gargoyles, Firedrake, Fog Worm, Four-Headed Hydra, Fungus Champion, Goatman Warriors, Goblin Whipmaster, Lemures, Meat-Eating Moss, Salamandrine Men, Stone Colossus, Golem of Wood and Nails and Small Dragon.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: The Beast Bulletin: A Collection of Monster Profiles – Volume 7
description:
This issue of the Beast Bulletin features game stats, handmade illustrations and snippets of background information for the following beasts: Goblin Commander, Hobgoblin Maceman, Gorgoroth, Skeletal Rats, Living Statue, Horror Head, Icthullian Beast, Skeletal Gnomes, Purple Dragon, Ghoul Scouts, Wirricow, Loomshade the Nightmare Ink, Braintroll, Jackal Fruit, Barzaur, Demonic Harpy and Wild Ogre.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: The Beast Bulletin: A Collection of Monster Profiles – Volume 8
description:
Beast Bulletin- Volume 8 contains statistics for the following Four Against Darkness monsters: Three-headed Hydra, Baalgarg, Major Iron Eater, Bugman Champion, Crystal powered boulder beast, Orvork, Glort, Echo Siren, Cyclopean Ogre, Chaos Ogre, Executioner, Goblin Berserker, Goblin Bugmaster, Goblin Merchant, Goblin Nightblade, Goblin Siege Veteran and Mist Goblin.

—description from the publisher

Red Panda Surprise is a simple game where players want to player the highest sum of cards, but the lowest sum adds +10 and often wins the round. Winners discard their cards and everyone else puts their cards on the bottom of their deck. The first player to have three or fewer cards wins. There are also cards that let players swap for a card in someone else's hand. And there are unique secret cards advanced players can shuffle into their decks.

The game is quick and easy, you just play two of the 5 cards in your hand each round and then redraw from your own deck. While it might feel random who wins, paying attention to the cards played and the tactics other players gravitate towards helps seal a victory. Like, if others keep playing their lowest cards, your highest cards should beat them. If other keep playing their highest cards, your middle cards should win. The last twist is that players who exhaust their best cards early will struggle as others catch up and get rid of their middle cards before playing their best cards to end the game.

Red Panda Surprise is fast and adorable, but the cute art hides a cunning game.

Note: There are advanced rules for 1-3 players inspired by Pai Gow where you split your hand and play for asymmetric rewards.

—description from the designer

boardgame: The Beast Bulletin: A Collection of Monster Profiles – Volume 9
description:
The ninth issue of the Beast Bulletin features game stats, handmade illustrations and snippets of background information for the following beasts: Sandstorm Djinn; Clockwork Harrier; Mireborn Sludge; Venom Idol; Bloodshade Wolf; Grim Lantern Warden; Ashborne Vulture; Hivespawn Horror; Thunderhorn Rhino; Blight Pixie; Lamoran Skullspider; Ragpicker Fiend; Armored Mutant Guardian; Armored Death Messenger; Demon Gorilla; Crystal Scarab. All of these creatures are unpublished and appear for the first time.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: The Beast Bulletin: A Collection of Monster Profiles – Volume 10
description:
The new issue of the Beast Bulletin, the ultimate collection of monsters for Four Against Darkness, contains stats, hand-drawn illustrations and background information for the following foes: Frostbite Ape; Ember Wraith; Mirror Mimic; Ironwood Dryad; Sorrow Wisp; Hollow Oak Guardian; Starfallen Ooze; Three-Headed Zombie Giant; Bronze Scarab Swarm; Curklan Beast; Bugman Duelist; Catfolk Executioner; Energy Freak; Burrowing Worm; Candle Wax Golem; Iron-Bound Apostate; Drillworm; Land Siren; Hoary Ogre of the Caverns; Mushroom Men.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: The Beast Bulletin: A Collection of Monster Profiles – Volume 11
description:
The Beast Bulletin returns with a special issue entirely dedicated to 20 NEW minion types. In this issue: Ahtapot (cave-dwellers descended from squids); Animated Scarecrows; Bukozar (elite household undead guards); Cyclopean Lizardmen; Dark Gnomes; Demonic Goblins; Demon Fey; Dogfolk; Doruvak (four-eyed brutes); Dwarven Mercenaries; Goblin Archers; Goblin Knife Fighters; Headless Eunuchs (headless automatons); Hill Trolls; Elven Mercenaries; Jambard (feather-eared dying race); Kwezya (flexible eye-fungus humanoids); Mechanical Guardians; Mountain Orcs; Goblin Javelineers.

—description from the publisher

Inspired by the political realism of Machiavelli and the turbulent world of medieval and Renaissance church politics, Pontiff is a strategic game of influence, legitimacy, and calculated risk.

Players take the role of Renaissance Patrons seeking to dominate princely courts and ultimately control the Papal Conclave. Cards are placed face-down on Seats of Influence across eight Princely Courts, each seat defined by position, rank, and vocation. Placement is never neutral: the closer a card matches its seat, the stronger its influence when challenged during elections and revealed. Fortuna cards introduce instability — wars, famine, assassination, and witch hunts — disrupting carefully laid plans. No player is eliminated; power shifts until the Papacy is secured.

Court elections are triggered as hierarchies fill. Players challenge rivals, expose mismatches, and watch influence rise, collapse, or even change hands. Victors elect Princes, unlock Cardinal seats, and manoeuvre for position in the final Papal Election.

Bluffing in Pontiff lies not in hidden roles, but in strategic commitment: how boldly will you claim a seat knowing it may later be tested?

Through layered elections, hidden information, and shifting control of Influence dice, Pontiff creates a disciplined yet tense struggle for power in which legitimacy must be earned — or convincingly staged.

—description from the designer

Play bugs into your garden that flip, move, or discard your other cards, or play rocks and leaves to protect them!

Every card has a different bug or protection on the front and back. Draw a card, choose a side, and put it into your 4*4 grid. Do the card's action, often flipping other cards (but not retriggering those ones). Each bug has a different scoring condition! Most points after someone fills their garden wins!

—description from the publisher

Geopowr is a tile laying game where you build the power that your continent needs to thrive. The planet’s population is growing and needs energy. You must build the most power for your continent in order to win.

Players form an expanding grid of connected quadrants of different energy sources including solar, wind, hydroelectric, geothermal and fossil fuels. Laying power cubes on the grid allows you to stake claims and build power plants. You can choose to cooperate or compete to win big for the whole planet or just yourself.

You will be given Thermalina, Hydroelectrica, Wind Farmer, Oil Baron or Solar Goddess to help you achieve your mission. Assembling power cubes gives you an energy grid fed by power plants. Building these together will provide synergies to accelerate growth, and will allow you to scale energy production in power towers. Diversification of power is rewarded by the Well Digger and Green Queen.

The standard game version is for 1 to 3 players and takes about 25 minutes. Additional versions of this scalable board game are available for up to 4, 6, 8 or 10 players and include increasing numbers of energy sources, tiles, and power cubes.

—description from the publisher

7 new cards. Add a new card for each distillery to your game for even more fluctuation in the market!

These new cards allow you to affect future turns by placing a card from hand on a distillery, even if a player has already visited this round. Control your own future and the rate at which you sell your whiskey— or dilute the market and steer prices lower!

A clumsy Okapi, a quirky farm: flaws and imperfection finally have value... but will that be a good thing or a bad one?

Okapi Nine - a perfect imperfect card game: 9 coins to collect, your friends’ unexpected chaos undoes all your plans, just one card each per a fast-paced, no-mercy round.

Goal of the game: in each round, be the player who reaches 9 coins first.

The winner is the one who sends the Okapi as far as possible — that is, the player who collects the most coins to afford the priciest return ticket by the end of the game.

—description from the designer

In PAC-MAN: Craze Maze, each player builds their own maze over the course of the game, gradually shaping a network of paths filled with dots, power pellets, fruit, and ghosts.

On your turn, draw a tile and either replace one in your grid or reveal a hidden tile. As the maze evolves, you must plan carefully: at the end of the game, only one continuous path from PAC-MAN to a single exit will score.

Items along that path grant points, while ghosts create a risk–reward challenge. Place ghosts after a Power Pellet to score increasing bonuses — but any ghost without protection will cost you points. Multiple scoring combinations can be created along a path, rewarding careful timing and placement.

When all tiles have been revealed, players choose their best exit, trace one path, and total their score. The longest valid path earns a bonus. The player with the most points wins.

With simple turns, quick playtime, and tactical tile placement, PAC-MAN: Craze Maze is accessible for families while offering strategic depth for experienced players.

—description from the publisher

Quill Seeker is one of the 8 games in Dr. Finn's Book of Two-Player Strategy and Word Games. The game consists of one game sheet and requires no other components -- just a pencil for each player.

Quill Seeker is a five-round competitive word game in which players capture letters from a shared grid to build high-scoring words. At the start of the game, a randomly determined “starting letter” anchors the first move. On each turn, a player selects one of their available capture options and draws a rectangle around 4 to 8 adjacent letters in a single row or column. After the first turn, every capture must cross a previously captured set, creating an evolving web of intersecting letter paths across the board.

From the letters they capture, players spell a word using all but one of those letters, rearranging them as needed. Letter values determine the score for that round, and any star symbols may act as wild letters worth zero points. If a captured letter includes a paired object, the player marks that object toward end-game bonuses. The one unused letter from each turn is added to the player’s personal bonus word area, slowly building toward a potential five-letter word.

After five rounds, players total three sources of points: the five words they spelled during the game, their completed bonus word, and the number of different objects they collected. If the bonus letters form a five-letter word in the exact order written, the word’s value is doubled; otherwise, the letters may be rearranged for standard scoring. With spatial capture rules, limited options, and layered scoring decisions, Quill Seeker combines grid strategy and vocabulary skill in a focused, head-to-head contest.

-description from designer

Confusing Lands: Icebound Isles is a frosty standalone expansion to Confusing Lands that can be played on its own or combined with the original game for even bigger adventures.

Step into a snow covered world where icy winds sweep across floating islands and frozen terrain reshapes the sky itself. Every card and even the box are dusted in winter, introducing brand new Frozen Spaces adding a chilling strategic twists to the game.

In this clever card game, players create their own wacky floating landscapes in the sky! Discover towering snow capped mountains, trace icy rivers, and maybe even spot a brave little deer wandering through the frost.

Players take turns drafting and placing cards featuring unique symbols and scoring conditions. Once each player has played 8 cards, they tally points from a single shared scoring condition along with the scoring conditions within their own finished landscape. The player who crafted the most brilliantly confusing (and highest scoring) frozen world wins!

Play Icebound Isles as:

     A standalone solo or 2 player experience
     A longer solo or 2 player game when combined with Confusing Lands
     A 3–4 player game when merged with the original game.


The lands were already confusing… now they’re frozen.

—description from the designer

Necromancer’s Night is a head-to-head tactical grid battler where each player controls a fledgling necromancer (“Necro”) commanding skeletal units on a board. You issue limited Command actions each turn to move and attack with basic Bones or stronger classed Skulls, then upgrade Skulls by equipping Soul Cards to transform them into independent “Maria” units with enhanced stats, class synergies, and special abilities. Combat is spatial and range-based: units move according to class patterns, target enemies within attack range, and roll dice to deal damage. Destroying enemy units grants resources which can be used to cast powerful spells for various effects. The objective is to break through the enemy formation and reduce the opposing Necro’s health points to zero in order to win.

Say goodbye to black-and-white boredom. Color Flock brings a vibrant twist to your pasture! This cheerful expansion introduces six colorful breeds of sheep, each with their own quirky placement rules and neighbor preferences. Whether you’re lining up the fussy blue puffballs or keeping the fiery reds apart, every round adds a fresh layer of strategy and charm. Wrangling wool has never looked this good!

-description from publisher's website

More space, more sheep, more baa-rilliance! In Broadfield, Esmeralda’s pasture has grown - now there’s room for even larger flocks and trickier patterns. This expansion introduces an expanded pasture sheet, giving players more opportunities for clever combos, colorful clusters, and record-breaking herds. But beware: with more room comes more demands from your picky sheep! Can you master the big field without losing your wool?

-description from publisher's website

The fields just got trickier! In Fenced In, Esmeralda’s new pasture comes with built-in fences that block movement and split up space. You'll need to navigate tight corners, cozy pens, and awkward gaps as you place your sheep. With this new pasture sheet, clever planning is more important than ever. Can you keep your flock happy in these fenced-in fields - or will your strategy hit the rails?

-description from publisher's website

New goals, endless baa-ssibilities! Scoring Herds introduces 8 unique scoring cards that shake up how your flock earns points. Whether you’re chasing colorful clusters, isolated introverts, or perfectly penned pairs, each game now offers a fresh strategic twist. Combine different scoring cards for a new challenge every time - and discover just how many ways there are to impress Esmeralda with your herding genius!

-description from publisher's website

This expansion takes your rails to Africa! Lay down tracks and deliver goods across two brand-new map sheets:

South Africa – Begin in Pretoria or the bustling port of Cape Town, then stretch your network through the vast interior to reach cities like Durban and Johannesburg. Long hauls across open landscapes challenge you to balance efficiency with distance, making every delivery a test of endurance and planning.

Egypt – From Cairo, follow the mighty Nile as you connect to Alexandria, Luxor, and beyond. Here, geography is king: deserts limit your options while the river guides your routes. Can you keep goods flowing smoothly through this historic and strategic land?

No randomness, no dice - just the pure joy of planning rails and delivering cargo across Africa’s iconic landscapes!-description from publisher's website

This expansion takes your rails to the United States! Build your network and deliver goods across two brand-new map sheets:

California – Start in Sacramento or the busy hub of Los Angeles, then weave your way through the fertile Central Valley and along the Pacific coast to reach cities like San Francisco and San Diego. With mountains, coastline, and farmland to connect, California rewards clever routing and efficient deliveries.

Texas – From the state capital Austin, expand your rails toward the sprawling cities of Dallas, Houston, and San Antonio. Vast distances and wide-open plains make long hauls inevitable, but the promise of booming industries and heavy cargo makes every mile worth it.

No dice, no randomness - just pure strategic rail-building across America’s most iconic states!-description from publisher's website

This expansion brings your rails to Great Britain! Deliver goods and connect historic cities across two brand-new map sheets:

Scotland - Begin in Edinburgh or Glasgow and stretch your network through the rugged Highlands, linking smaller towns and ports along the way. Long routes across mountains and lochs demand foresight and efficiency - only the best planners will keep the cargo moving.

England - Start from the bustling capital London and extend your rails to major cities like Manchester, Birmingham, and Liverpool. With dense urban centers and busy industries, every connection counts, making England a map of tight networks and clever deliveries.

No dice, no randomness - just pure strategy as you draw your lines across Great Britain!-description from publisher's website

Set sail in Micro Hero: Ulysse, a solo card game blending drafting and poker combinations, inspired by The Odyssey. Across four increasingly challenging rounds, you must guide Ulysse back to Ithaca while facing a sea stirred by Poseidon’s wrath. By forming powerful constellations from the revealed cards, you attempt to outmatch the god of the seas while surviving legendary encounters such as the Cyclops, Circe, and the Sirens.

Each round builds a layered “Sea” tableau. As cards become visible, you select them to form a five-card hand. Your combination is then compared to Poseidon’s, which is formed from the visible Sea cards and his Wrath track. The difference between hands determines how his anger shifts: pushing too hard may turn him Furious and strengthen his position, while failure accelerates the rising danger. Ulysse has limited powers to manipulate the Sea or adjust his hand, but each intervention increases Poseidon’s Wrath.

The tension escalates over four rounds, with additional Trial cards introduced each round to disrupt your plans and alter the configuration of the Sea. At the end of each round, the Wrath gauge is reset, allowing you to save your progress and pause the game to resume later. Each round represents a distinct stage of the voyage while preserving a sense of increasing difficulty and mounting challenges.

Victory is achieved if you survive all four rounds and contain Poseidon’s anger before it spirals out of control. The game also includes a cooperative two-player variant in which players control Ulysse and Eurylochus, building a shared five-card hand together. Players may communicate freely under normal circumstances, but whenever Poseidon becomes Furious, all communication is immediately forbidden until he calms down again, creating a sudden and tense constraint at the most dangerous moments of the journey.

-description from designer

On to Berlin! Is an area impulse games that uses cards for activations and covers the entire European and North African theaters from 1939 to 1945.
