The redeveloped game, based on Rise and Fall of the Third Reich from Game Journal #70, is a fast playing game that allows players to control their faction’s forces and decide on their strategy to achieve victory.

Designed to be played in a single evening, the card purchase mechanic (tech tree) and game flow gives tremendous replay value that allows players to try different strategies for each game.

The rules have been rewritten to remove ambiguities and clear up potential issues for players. This version adds rules for maintaining the secrecy of cards in a player’s hand and implements previous optional rules to address potential game balance issues. Cubes have also been added to the components to easier track the political leaning of neutral countries.

Game Components
    1 Map 22" x 34"
    2 Counter sheets, 198 counters
    32 Cards
    35 wooden cubes
    1 16–page Rulebook
    2 Player Aid Cards
    8 dice

Complexity: Low/Medium
Solitaire: Low

-description from publisher

Welcome to the neon wildwoods - where synth-leaves shimmer under glitchy stars and the forest hums with electric heartbeats. Out here, nature runs on code and instinct is hardwired. And you? You're a Crittmancer: part engineer, part artist, all raw spark. You craft the untamed: cyber-bison with thunder in their hooves, boars armed with hydraulic tusks, and sleek deer whose antlers double as signal jammers. These aren’t just forest creatures - they’re biomechanical legends built for the brave. Whether for glory, grit, or the rush of creation, you're giving the woods a whole new kind of wild.

Heads up, Crittmancer - three fresh blueprints just landed in your folder, and they’re anything but a casual code stroll. These beasts aren’t predators, but they own the woods: the mighty bison, the unstoppable boar, and the proud, regal deer. Building them won’t be a cakewalk. Think more sparks, swearing, and late-night calibrations. But the payoff? Pure synth-soul satisfaction. So go on, unleash some wild dignity onto the neon streets. Because who says the concrete jungle can’t have antlers and attitude?

—description from the publisher

Welcome to the neon wildwoods - where chrome claws glint in the flicker of hacked moonlight, and the hum of circuits howls through the trees. Out here, survival isn’t just instinct - it’s art. And you? You’re a Crittmancer: part engineer, part artist, all wild spark. You conjure cyber-predators from the shadows: feral bears with reinforced limbs, sly foxes laced with stealth tech, and wolves whose howls sync with satellite arrays. Each one is a fusion of nature and machine, forged not just for clients or fame, but for the thrill of giving the forest its fangs back in full metal.

Jack in, Crittmancer! this expansion just dropped three fresh blueprints into your rig. Up next: a bear, a fox, and a wolf. Forest-born predators, now running on chrome, code, and a whole lotta bite. Your mission? Bring 'em to life: claws, teeth, attitude and all. Just... maybe don’t let them loose in the neon city after dark. The streets are already savage without a cyber-bear crashing through vending bots or a stealth-fox hacking streetcams for fun. You build 'em - you tame 'em. Or not. Your call, legend.

—description from the publisher

Welcome to the primal frontier of the neon wilds - where chrome tusks gleam beneath artificial suns, and the roar of circuitry rivals thunder. Out here, style isn’t just flair, it’s survival. And you? You’re a Crittmancer: part engineer, part artist, all instinct. You forge cyber-beasts: towering elephants, razor-maned lions, and armored rhinoceroses, each a masterpiece of metal and code. Whether built for clients, glory, or the sheer thrill of creation, your creatures prowl the digital savanna with power, purpose, and a spark of something almost alive.

This expansion uploads three wild new blueprints straight into your neural vault. Get ready to cyber-spawn the kings of the neon savanna: elephant, lion, and rhinoceros. Think your garage can handle that kind of chrome? Especially the elephant - yeah, good luck squeezing that trunked titan into a back-alley build bay. Or hey, it’s the cyberpunk era, why not craft a pint-sized, one-inch elephant with a fusion core heart and attitude to match? The schematics are loaded. The sparks are flying. It’s all in your cyber-hands now.

—description from the publisher

Welcome to the dark future - where chrome shines brighter than the sun, neon never sleeps, and data flows faster than you can blink. Style is survival, and bytes are bigger than bullets. And you? You’re a Crittmancer - part engineer, part artist, all genius. You craft cyber critters: mechanical beasts built either for clients, fame, or just the thrill of bending metal and code into something alive(ish).

Yo, Crittmancer - your archive just glitched itself a gift: two legendary blueprints thought lost to time (and terrible data backups). But guess what? You dug ‘em up from a dusty old floppy disk buried deep in some forgotten junkstack. Now it’s go-time. Fire up your rig and bring back the long-lost icons: the cyber-llama (all sass, no brakes) and the mecha-raccoon (trash-powered chaos in a chrome shell). Show the scene that while they were chasing trends, you were resurrecting relics. It’s time to flex forgotten tech and claim your crown as the true cyber-zoo master.

—description from the publisher

A hidden role-playing game for 2-3 players where each player controls a mafia group.

We'll move through a city that can be different each game.

On our turn, We can extort businesses to obtain resources, move hidden characters, or reveal a character's identity to use their unique ability.

Extort, shoot, steal, murder, betray, ambush, bribe, traffic alcohol... do whatever it takes to become the most powerful mafia at the end of the six turns.

—description from the publisher

Pegasus 1944 depicts the fights during the British and Canadian landings on Gold, Juno, and Sword beaches, from 6 to 12 June, 1944. The western flank is covered by the British 6th Airborne Division. The first seven days were crucial for Operation Overlord. The goal was to begin the liberation of all of Western Europe, but it was necessary to capture bridgeheads first. Meanwhile, the Germans quickly gathered their units and attempted to stop the Allies at all costs.

Each turn, swap your card with an opponent's to end up with the best card. Can you keep your cool and score the most points in this fun card game? You'll need a poker face to win this accessible and fast-paced card game!

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Iron Triangle: Search and Destroy Operations in the Vietnam War
description: Iron Triangle is an asymmetric lane battler set during the Vietnam War search and destroy operations from 1966 up to the Tet Offensive in 1968. Two players assume opposing roles: the Viet Cong, leveraging concealment, mobility, and disruption, and the United States, applying sustained pressure and attritional tactics to limit insurgent influence. Across three rounds, players commit action cards to search and destroy operations along three lanes, contesting control of three key regions in III Corps: War Zone C, War Zone D, and the infamous Iron Triangle.

Moonlight
Uncover secrets across 8 gripping episodes in an evolving noir crime saga—delivered in roll & write form. Step into the shadows of Midnight City to search for your missing partner and bring down the Mafia. Gather intel, evade the police, stake out perfect locations for your heists, and cover your tracks.

Your choices in early episodes shape what unfolds later, as new characters and mechanics are introduced along the way. Each episode is a self-contained adventure, but for the richest experience, we recommend playing from Episode 1 through Episode 8.

Episode 2:
Nic "Ghost" Fenn is thrust into a personal crisis when his girlfriend, Vera "Lucky" Lux, is abducted under circumstances eerily similar to Sly's partner's disappearance.

Moonlight

Gather intel, evade the police, stake out perfect locations for your heists, and cover your tracks.

Your choices in early episodes shape what unfolds later, as new characters and mechanics are introduced along the way. Each episode is a self-contained adventure, but for the richest experience, we recommend playing from Episode 1 through Episode 8.

Episode 3:
Sly and Ghost have intel on which businesses are mafia-owned fronts and are now targeting them exclusively, with some help from Deepwater. Meanwhile, Enzo “Three Tusk” Barone has been brought into town to try and clean up the mess that Victor and Big Louie have created. You’ve got other ideas: frame him for being involved in raiding Mafia properties to sow more discontent in their operations. As Enzo is an out-of-towner and Big Louie and Victor were local favorites, this shouldn’t be too hard.

-description from publisher's website

Moonlight

Step into the shadows of Midnight City to search for your missing partner and bring down the Mafia. Gather intel, evade the police, stake out perfect locations for your heists, and cover your tracks.

Your choices in early episodes shape what unfolds later, as new characters and mechanics are introduced along the way. Each episode is a self-contained adventure, but for the richest experience, we recommend playing from Episode 1 through Episode 8.

Episode 4:
With Enzo having failed in his goal of getting the situation under control, the Mafia is now out in force defending their turf in a more active way. The recent crime spree has the public of Midnight City in an uproar and there are calls for Deepwater to resign as Police Chief. The city is on the edge of a riot.

While Sly and Ghost press onward in their goals, they become more unsettled with the reach of the mafia’s influence, leading them to take a more direct approach in efforts to shut them down. Additionally, the latest disruptions have opened up some prize intel that leads them closer to The Brothers that Sly has been investigating for years ...

-description from publisher's website

Uncover secrets across 8 gripping episodes in an evolving noir crime saga - delivered in roll & write form. Step into the shadows of Midnight City to search for your missing partner and bring down the Mafia. Gather intel, evade the police, stake out perfect locations for your heists, and cover your tracks.

Your choices in early episodes shape what unfolds later, as new characters and mechanics are introduced along the way. Each episode is a self-contained adventure, but for the richest experience, we recommend playing from Episode 1 through Episode 8.

Episode 5:
Stuart “Whittles” Underfoot never planned on owing the mob more than he could count, but that’s where we find him - broke, hunted, and one false step away from vanishing for good. He’s skipping town nightly, trading hotels like poker hands, and pulling low-level heists just to breathe. Hot on his heels is Bruno “Bricks” Lupo, a blunt instrument in a cheap suit sent by Big Louie to collect - no matter the cost.

Whittles isn’t just running. He’s chasing something too.

Carmilla’s running her intel racket from within the casino, while, Whittles scrapes together leads the old-fashioned way: shoe leather and bad decisions. They’re both after the same prize, and neither plans to come in second.

Then there’s Vera. She’s trouble. The kind you want a second drink with. When their paths collide, it’s less rescue mission and more ticking time bomb. This is the story of how Whittles got in over his head - and what set the whole damn thing in motion.

—description from the publisher

Uncover secrets across 8 gripping episodes in an evolving noir crime saga - delivered in roll & write form. Step into the shadows of Midnight City to search for your missing partner and bring down the Mafia. Gather intel, evade the police, stake out perfect locations for your heists, and cover your tracks.

Your choices in early episodes shape what unfolds later, as new characters and mechanics are introduced along the way. Each episode is a self-contained adventure, but for the richest experience, we recommend playing from Episode 1 through Episode 8.

Episode 6:
Sly and Ghost are locked up tight on Paradise Island—a sunny resort destination on one side and prison-island on the other where Midnight City dumps their undesirables. With no cars, no easy exits, and only one way off: the boat that barely got you here. Vera "Lucky" Lux and Stuart "Whittles" Underfoot have made landfall with a plan: repair the escape vessel, gather supplies, and spring their friends loose without setting off every siren from the Port to the Highlands.

Dexter’s grandmother, Mabel "Blaze" Harrigan, operating in the background, helps line up the final approach - but it’s Vera and Whittles who do the dirty work. Heists, hustles, and a whole lot of guesswork stand between them and their biggest score ever: their companions freedom.

—description from the publisher

Uncover secrets across 8 gripping episodes in an evolving noir crime saga - delivered in roll & write form. Step into the shadows of Midnight City to search for your missing partner and bring down the Mafia. Gather intel, evade the police, stake out perfect locations for your heists, and cover your tracks.

Your choices in early episodes shape what unfolds later, as new characters and mechanics are introduced along the way. Each episode is a self-contained adventure, but for the richest experience, we recommend playing from Episode 1 through Episode 8.

Episode 7 – Regicide

—description from the publisher

Moonlight
Uncover secrets across 8 gripping episodes in an evolving noir crime saga - delivered in roll & write form. Step into the shadows of Midnight City to search for your missing partner and bring down the Mafia. Gather intel, evade the police, stake out perfect locations for your heists, and cover your tracks.

Your choices in early episodes shape what unfolds later, as new characters and mechanics are introduced along the way. Each episode is a self-contained adventure, but for the richest experience, we recommend playing from Episode 1 through Episode 8.

Episode 8 – Great Escape

-description from publisher's website

Road Trip: 10 Travel Games is a compact tabletop collection designed for travel and family play. The set combines ten classic logic and strategy games in a single reusable format suitable for road trips and small-group settings.

Each game is played on its own reusable board using dry-erase markers, allowing players to reset and replay without additional components. The collection includes grid-based strategy games, deduction games, and pattern-based challenges adapted for quick setup and portable use.

Players compete by outthinking opponents, completing objectives, or achieving winning alignments depending on the selected game. The modular structure allows different games to be played independently within the same physical system.

—description from the designer

boardgame: Zombicide: Mission C39 – Who Let The Dogs Out?
description:
Designed by players for the Kyoko mission contest, and later published by the original studio, C missions are supplementary missions released online for print and play.

C39 is a Hard difficulty exploration mission for the Prison outbreak and Zombie dogs.

Material needed: Toxic City Mall. 
Death is everywhere but life is invincible. Your team is entrenched in a mall, helping a woman to give birth. Today, you are heroes!

Designed by Daniel Winterhalter

boardgame: Junk World: The Magical Girl Promo Card
description:
A new challenger inspired by the Caster Chronicles character. She has two special abilities: secreting cards under her card to prevent damage, and giving weapons played a bonus attack.

This promo was an award for playing in the online Caster Chronicles/Junk World cross-over event "Campus Visit," which involved uploading photos of people playing the games to their respective Discord servers.

Designed by players for the Kyoko mission contest, and later published by the original studio, C missions are supplementary missions released online for print and play.

C40 is a Hard difficulty exploration mission for the Prison Outbreak
Death is everywhere but life is invincible. Your team is entrenched in a mall, helping a woman to give birth. Today, you are heroes!

Designed by Daniel Winterhalter

Rummy Colony is a family-friendly board/card game that blends traditional rummy mechanics with trivia and strategy.

Rummy Colony blends card play with multiple-choice trivia as players discover new and amazing facts about the world around them, and collect sets and runs of numbered cards to score points.

Each turn, one player will read a trivia question aloud along with a list of possible answers. The players must identify what they believe is the correct answer, voting in secret. Once all responses have been selected, players reveal their votes to score points whenever correct.

Player points are recorded with the cards placed in front of them, but these points are at risk of being stolen away by competitors until a colony is formed. To create a colony, connect one set of cards to another by building off of the last card in the set. For example, a run of cards from 3 through 7 can be protected by adding a perpendicular set of four 7s at the end. Or maybe you have just the right cards in hand to score a 7, 8, and 9. The choice and challenge is yours!

The base game supports multiple players and can be expanded with additional card packs to increase player count and variety.

C41 is a Hard difficulty exploration mission for the Toxic City Mall.

Designed by Daniel Winterhalter

Designed by Lachlan Abrahams

In Kniffel: Das Duell, the familiar basic rules of the classic dice game Kniffel remain unchanged, so you can start playing immediately. Using 5 dice, you try to achieve the various combinations. Always be alert to which areas will become important and steal valuable discs from your opponent. Who will win this exciting tug-of-war? The first player to secure a total of 5 discs or 3 adjacent discs on their side of the game board wins!

The Tower of Elmarsh is a roll-and-write open world adventure gamebook for 1 player, which sees you travel across the land with a party of rogues, smiths, adventurers, rangers, clerics and the ccasional Hawkguard. Help the good folk of Brighthelm, take on a variety of quests, make friends and allies along the way and turn your group of enthusiastic villagers into warriors. Discover the land’s many secrets and powers, and come home a hero. But don’t dally: time is short, and the threat of a moon strike is ever growing.

—description from the publisher

Players are dealt 10 cards with a Discard and Draw pile at the center. Players then take turns drawing and discarding their cards for the highest possible score each quarter. Play ends when the first player reaches 27 or more points in a quarter. Record your points on the scorepad. Tally the points at the end of four quarters to announce the winner!

boardgame: Dog Man Supa Buddies Card Game
description: Inspired by Dav Pilkey’s bestselling Dog Man books, the Dog Man Supa Buddies Card Game is a fast-paced and portable card game designed for kids ages 5 and up and their families. Players flip cards at the same time, racing to spot matching Supa Buddies, the Chief, or the SKUNK first to win the pile. The player who collects the entire stack of cards wins the game.

The catch me if you can game! Speedy Catch is a smart and fun game of speed and visual perception for the whole family! 1: Flip the top card. 2: continue flipping cards until two cards share a characteristic as indicated on the central card. 3: Collect the most cards to win the game!

boardgame: My Book Nook: Cozy Word Building Game
description:
Settle in with friends and build your dream reading nook, complete with shelves of adorable books and charming decorations.

My Book Nook is a cozy card game where players spell words and style their shelves to earn points.

Each round, you'll take turns crafting new words or drawing fresh cards to fill your nook with literary delight. When the last page turns, the player with the most impressive shelf wins!

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Crunch a Color: The Healthy Eating Game
description:
Created by a mom, Crunch a Color is the award-winning healthy eating game that makes mealtime fun! Kids earn points for eating a balanced and colorful plate of veggies, fruits, proteins and grains. Bonus points for trying new foods and good manners! Learning to eat healthy has never been so easy and rewarding.

By playing this healthy eating game, your kids will learn to choose nutritious foods for themselves and mealtime at the family table will be fun for everyone!

Get ready to flip, match, and crunch your way to victory in this fast-paced pickle-themed card game! Match the right pickles before your opponents and claim the title of Top Pickle!

Every card offers a clue, and every answer starts with a “P”. The first one to shout out the answer followed by the word Pickle (yes, you must say pickle following your answer!), wins the card! Example: Pinocchio PICKLE.

The GOLDEN RULE If you don't add "Pickle" to your answer, it's incorrect! No exceptions.

Note: The clue reader is also the judge. They decide who responded first and correctly! The quickest thinker snags the card!

Before there were expansions.
Before there were settings.
Before there were genres.

There was Chain Reaction.

Chain Reaction 2026 Complete is the definitive edition of the original Two Hour Wargames rules—the foundation on which NUTS!, 5150, Warrior Heroes, and every THW system was built. Powered by the legendary THW Reaction System, this game delivers fast, brutal, story-driven man-to-man combat where you act… and the world reacts immediately.

No turns. No artificial sequencing. No perfect control.

Just decisions, consequences, and survival.

This complete volume reflects years of refinement driven by the THW community and Patreon supporters—players who value games that work solo, co-op, or competitive without special cases, flowcharts, or scripted behavior. The rules reward instinct over optimization and create stories whether you win or lose.

Chain Reaction is:

     Era-agnostic – modern, WW2, pulp, sci-fi, fantasy
     Miniature-agnostic – use what you already own
     Solo-first by design – no AI decks or hidden rules
     Fast and lethal – games finish in under two hours


Characters aren’t chess pieces. They hesitate. They panic. They charge. They break. They become legends—or names scratched off a roster sheet. Survivors carry their reputation forward, building a living campaign shaped by what actually happened on the table.

This book exists because the THW community asked for it.
It endures because the system works.

Whether you’re a long-time supporter, a returning veteran, or discovering Two Hour Wargames for the first time, Chain Reaction 2026 Complete is the core expression of the THW philosophy:
Let the rules tell the story.
Let the dice decide.
Play the game your way.

INSIDE ME is a thoughtful card game about the emotions and inner states that live within us all. Players collect and manage emotions like Joy, Fear, Anger, and Shame, while also working with “happy hormones” such as Dopamine and Serotonin. The game turns everyday emotional experiences into playful strategy, inviting players to reflect on feelings, connections, and inner balance in a light, engaging way.

During the game, players divide cards into tempting offers and choose from them in turn, trying to build strong sets of emotions while limiting their opponents’ options. Special cards and cubes represent inner resources that can boost scoring but must be managed carefully. Timing, reading other players’ intentions, and making smart swaps all play a key role in shaping your emotional landscape.

At the end of the game, players score points for the emotion sets they control, along with bonuses unlocked by meeting special conditions. Extra points can be gained or lost depending on how well players manage their inner resources. The player with the highest total score wins, showing the best balance between strategy, emotional awareness, and adaptability.

-description from designer

Board game Рієлтор ("Realtor") — become a master of deals and the king of real estate!

Imagine: you have clients with completely different desires and requests. Someone is looking for a house by the lake, someone needs an apartment with a terrace, and someone dreams of a cozy family cottage. Your task is to find the perfect home for each of them, make the most profitable deals, and prove to everyone that you are the best realtor in town.

Realtor is a fast-paced, witty, and very lively card game about bargaining, intuition, and the ability to play on your opponents' predictions. Every move matters: whether to hide the right house, try to snatch a lucrative property, or get ahead of your opponent by literally one step.

The first to satisfy four clients and collect $4,000 will receive the prestigious title of “Most Successful Realtor of the Year.”

The categories are Jobs (yrken), Presents (Presenter), food (mat), Animals (djur), On Field Trip/Excurtion (på utflykt), and Home cozying (hemmamys)

Időfutár ("Time Runner") is a competitive card game in which a group of players work under conflicting goals centered around a mysterious drafting compass discovered near a school’s cleanup event. Players are divided into two factions: the seventh-grade students and one or more adversaries (agents and antagonists) seeking to seize the compass. Over the course of a card-laid board, players move their characters between locations, hide or transfer the compass, and use trick cards and false leads to misdirect opponents.

The game features asymmetric roles where one player controls the antagonists and the rest control student characters with unique abilities. The students’ objective is to conceal the compass successfully until the draw deck is exhausted, while the antagonists attempt to uncover it by searching locations or capturing students. Timing, deception, cooperation, and memory all play a part as players employ action and item cards to outmaneuver their opponents in this tactical, replayable card game.

In Hot Heads, you play as a reckless pilot in a hot-air balloon race. Try to reach incredible heights, but be careful not to fly too high. After all, high flyers catch a lot of wind and get lost in the storms raging high in the sky. Low flyers, however, score few points and won’t lead you to victory. If you can find the perfect balance, you might just emerge as the winner of the race.

During the game, on their turn, players draw one card and play one card. You may place this card either in front of yourself or in front of an opponent. This way you can help your own team rise, or sabotage an opponent. Cards with positive values make your balloons rise, while cards with negative values pull them down.

If a player draws a storm card, the storm begins. During a storm, all players must play cards from their hand into their own team. The number of cards that must be played depends on how many storm cards have appeared in the game. Each storm card means more cards must be played from your hand, causing your hand to shrink and your choices to become more limited. When the fourth storm card is drawn, players place all their remaining hand cards into their team, and the game ends.

When the game ends, players total the points for each type of hot-air balloon in their team (there are four types in total). For each balloon type, the balloon with the highest value catches too much wind and flies into the storm. Balloons that fly into the storm do not count toward the final score and are discarded. After removing the highest balloon of each type from the table, players add up the points of their remaining team members. The player with the most points wins the game!

—description from the publisher

Breed, raise, and protect your goats to build the most successful farm. The more lambs you nurture, the greater the chance they grow into the perfect prize goats.

But life on the farm isn’t peaceful. Rival farmers may steal your animals, and unexpected events — like the sudden closure of the barbed-wire factory — can disrupt your plans.

Play secret cards, chance cards, and event cards to outsmart your opponents and adapt to ever-changing conditions. Every well-fed goat earns you points. After 8 rounds, the farmer with the most points wins the game.

—description from the designer

Oh no! Naughty neighbour Josh has made stink bombs again. You have to quickly defuse them before they explode and stink up the whole neighbourhood with smells of garbage, skunk, and dirty socks!

Every player has to manipulate a set of dice held in one hand by using only the fingers of this hand. The goal is to build a tower on the bomb card with the dices so that the numbers match the sequence written on the card they have to defuse. The first player to complete his sequence has won.

Your world is dying. Soon, the last of its resources will be extracted from its husk, and it will no longer be able to support life. Now you turn your attention toward the stars. You’ll drain the life from your sun to propel your trains through the void, crack open the other planets in your system to exploit their precious minerals, and increase your company’s value to shareholders as all of you go extinct.

The first new train game from Amabel Holland in six years, Rayroads is an unusual approach to the genre that extends the logic of capitalism to its nihilistic conclusion.

Each round of Rayroads will see players take resources from their dying planet - making it less inhabitable - in order to take an action. You might plan a route for your space train - taking into account the orbits of planets - powered by the sun you're intentionally collapsing. You might build a moonbase to begin cracking open other planets - they're probably uninhabited, but you haven't really bothered to check. Or you'll tear open the fabric of space itself to make your travels more efficient - pity about what it does to the orbits of nearby planets.

The game ends when you've collapsed your sun, destroyed your home planet, or been destroyed by your capitalist-fascist eugenics doomsday cult. Players tally their profits, subtract their debits, and apply some multipliers to arrive at their final scores. The highest score wins, but as everyone is now dead, what does it matter?

Race to sew patterns faster than opponents.
How fast can you sew? 
After turning over one of the cards, the player immediately starts the race by threading the thread through the sewing board to match it to the picture! With a total of 20 challenge cards divided into four difficulty levels, players will have to do more than twist the strings into knots!

User description: 
Each player has a wooden board with a six by six grid of holes, and three shoelaces in different colours. The simple cards have patterns that do not overlap and use only two lace colours, the more complicated ones have patters with overlaps and three colours.

You can play a single round or multiple rounds, and it would be possible to play solo with a stopwatch.

—description from the publisher

At the dawn of a new era, humanity stands at a threshold. Across vast landscapes, small settlements begin to emerge, driven by a thirst for knowledge, a desire to flourish, and the ambition to leave an enduring legacy. Ahead lies an Inceptum - a great undertaking through which vision and resolve will shape the course of history.

INCEPTUM: Dawn of Civilizations is a medium heavy, non combat, engine building civilization strategy game for 2 to 4 players where asymmetry, timing, and long term planning create meaningful trade offs and lasting consequences.

Each player leads a civilization from the Ancient Age into the Medieval Age, striving to reach the Dawn of Modern Civilization. Along the way, players progress through branching research paths on the Technology Board, build and upgrade buildings on a personal City Board, expand their city by gaining more Population Tokens, trade for key resources, and construct exclusive Wonders that only one civilization can ever claim.

To advance into the Medieval Age, a civilization adopts a form of government by researching Republic or Monarchy. Reaching either option requires committing to different research routes, so the path taken shapes capabilities before the choice is even made. When a government is chosen, the player also selects a government benefit, and each benefit is exclusive. Once claimed, it is no longer available to other players, even if they choose the same form of government.

At the heart of the game is Per Turn Production, or PTP. Civilizations generate resources each round based on what they have built and researched, then reinvest those gains to increase future production. Research and construction strengthen long term income rather than providing immediate payouts, rewarding sequencing, foresight, and engine growth.

The game includes a dice based Bonus Production system that creates situational opportunities without making the game luck driven. At the start of a turn, two dice select a City Board slot. If a civilization has a structure there, it gains a small bonus just for that turn. The bonuses are intentionally modest, designed to reward adaptability by occasionally challenging even well laid plans, without undermining long term strategy.

No two games begin the same. Humble Beginnings alter early resources and openings, pushing civilizations down distinct paths from the first turns. Civilizational Traits deepen that asymmetry. Scientific civilizations accelerate research. Expansionist civilizations begin with greater population. Cultural civilizations build their first Wonder more efficiently. United civilizations can form population grids that boost production through city placement.

Wonders are singular achievements and some of the highest stakes races in the game. With 14 Wonders across the two ages, each one exclusive, timing matters as much as production. Many Wonders provide powerful effects and significant CP rewards that can reshape what remains possible for everyone else.

There is no combat or player elimination. Competition comes from timing, efficiency, exclusivity, and the pressure created when civilizations specialize and rivals respond. Players block opportunities, race for Wonders, and overtake through superior planning rather than force.

The game includes multiple ways to play, including the two Game Modes, Journey to Modern Civilization and For the Glory, with Trait driven victory conditions and a Prosperity path.

Civilization Points, or CPs, determine victory, earned through research, buildings, Wonders, and population milestones. Build an engine that compounds, commit to a path you cannot fully reverse, and define the age before your rivals do.

-description from publisher

You are the fearless General of a mighty ant army! Upon the discovery of a colossal, abandoned Anthill, a battle ensues as rivaling factions stake their claim to make this their new and glorious home.
The vicious race is on as you compel 40 ant soldiers to safety inside the Anthill. But natural disasters will strike without warning; mutiny and betrayal are ever-present threats and beware— an Anteater with an insatiable appetite roams the surface, seeking your troops as his tasty lunch!
Each turn, all generals will be given the exact same directives. So, it’s up to you to use your cunning to execute them most strategically. Take control of fortresses to assert your influence. As an astute general, you must mobilize your forces, seize key locations and urge your squads inward and upward to the center of the Anthill to lay claim to their new home.

—description from the designer
language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups

boardgame: The Mix: Custom Creation Card Pack
description: Adds 8 new cocktails, increasing variety and replayability.

Adds 2 technique cards, and 12 mocktails as decided by a survey during the Gamefound campaign. Final results:

Shirley Temple
Hawaian Ginger Ale
Chai Blossom
Tea Arnold Palmer
Dirty Spritz
Pomegranate Spritzer
Pineapple Coconut Water
Spicy Orange Shrub
Sparkling Tangawizi
Citrus Slushy
Virgin Cesar
Mango Lime Spritzer

boardgame: The Mix: Tiki Style Card Pack
description: Adds 12 new island-inspired cocktails and 2 technique cards, increasing variety and replayability.
Adds 12 new Signature Client cards, 2 technique cards, and 4 Glasses cards, increasing variety and replayability

Adds 12 new cocktail cards, 2 technique cards, and 4 Glasses cards, increasing variety and replayability. Recipes were curated with the help of Gamefound backers via a survey, and the final results were:

Blue Glacier (Iceland)
Machu Picchu (Peru)
Hugo Spritz (Italy)
Corn ’n’ Oil (Barbados)
Agua de Valencia (Spain)
Boulevardier (France)
Angelo Azzurro (Italy)
Caipirinha (Brazil)
Gin Basil Smash (Germany)
Singapore Sling (Singapore)
Poncha (Portugal)
Pimm’s Cup (summer cup) (United Kingdom)

Your Choice: Pets of War is a thoughtful card game inspired by real stories of animals who became symbols of hope during the war in Ukraine. Players build a small educational catalog of pets — some helped with fundraising, others became known for their incredible stories and support to people in hard times. You can’t include everyone, so each choice shapes whose story will be heard.

During the game, players divide cards into tempting offers and take turns choosing sets of pets to collect, trying to build meaningful collections while limiting their opponents’ options. Smart timing, reading other players’ intentions, and making tough choices are key as you decide which stories to preserve and which will be left untold.

At the end of the game, players score points for completed sets and special combinations of pets. The player with the most points wins, showing the best balance between strategy, values, and meaningful choices.

—description from the publisher

Want to make your friends hold their bellies in laughter? Or gasp in shock? Maybe even impress them with your wit? Santai Aja Lagi will do all that for you and more!
In this simple, impress-the-judge party game, you and your friends will compete to earn the approval of the ever-changing Judge.
The Judge will reveal and read a green prompt card, after which you and other players will attempt to curry the Judge's favor by filling in the blanks with your white answer cards. Psst! Make sure to tailor them to the Judge's sense of humor *wink wink*

After all players have submitted their answers, the Judge will read them out one by one, and pick their favorite. Whomever that card belongs to, earns the green card as a point!
Play continues in this manner until one player has 5 points, at which point they'll be crowned the winner!
With 100 green prompt cards and a whopping 500 white answer cards, this game is sure to be a mainstay in your circles while having you in stitches from laughing!

Do you have what it takes to win it all?

In Psycho: Visions of Fate you are a Psychopomp trying to shape reality to your liking.

The goal of the game is to use your Psychopomp's abilities, and those of your cards, to achieve 6 points of visions. Alternately, you may deal enough damage to your opponents win a game.

On your turn use your actions to attack locations, play cards, draw cards, gain energy, use card abilities, or to manipulate the board state in order to achieve a winning condition.

—description from the designer

Race to collect sets of attractions around the fair. Spend tickets to skip forward to the pile you want—or to steal one another player needs. At the end of each round, the player who holds the most of each attraction scores points.

—description from the publisher

Walkable City is a 1-4 player cooperative push-your-luck game about building public transit in a car-dependent city.

Players take on the roles of different modes of transit to build out a network for the denizens of the city to use. But any stoppage or interruption will erode your trust with the public, so players will need to work together to build out the city's infrastructure.

Players draw tiles from their individual player bags to build out their networks, but if players ask for too much their funding might get cut. Place your paths on the shared city board to build a transit network worthy of the city you call home!

—description from the designer

V1RUS: Roll & Write is a strategic city-building game set in a near-future world threatened by a rapidly spreading viral outbreak. Players take on the role of city planners working to develop safe, functional districts while the virus unpredictably spreads through different zones of the map. The theme focuses on balancing growth and safety, where every new building strengthens your city but also risks exposing it to infection.

Gameplay is played over several rounds, each divided into two phases. In the development phase, players simultaneously draw buildings on their personal maps, expanding their city by placing houses and towers according to placement rules. In the virus phase, one player rolls a die and all players mark the spread of the virus on matching numbered spaces, representing how quickly the outbreak moves through the city. Limited special abilities allow players to bend the rules, reroll the die, or protect key spaces, but using these powers comes at a cost in final scoring.

At the end of the game, players score points for how well they developed their city and balanced different building types. Bonus points can be gained for smart planning and unused abilities, while penalties are applied for buildings exposed to the virus. The player who manages their growth most efficiently while minimizing viral damage and maintaining a balanced city wins the game.

—description from the publisher

Designed by players for the Kyoko mission contest, and later published by the original studio, C missions are supplementary missions released online for print and play.

C46 is a Medium difficulty exploration mission for toxic city mall and Prison outbreak

Designed by Jason Mac Dougall

In Sneaky, with a little luck and good timing, you try to win the most valuable thieves for yourself. If you throw a thief into the center of the table, you take it, but not all of them are immediately safe.

Thieves with a value of 1 are considered safe and are placed face down. More valuable thieves, with values of 2 and 3, are placed face up in front of you. This is where the suspense begins, because only the top card of a stack is vulnerable and can be stolen by your opponents. In a two-player game, you even manage two face-up stacks.

Handcuffs play a special role: they help you protect thieves and earn you extra points at the end of the game. If you collect them wisely, you can secure a decisive advantage.

At the end, all the thief points collected are counted. In addition, you receive a bonus of 5 points if you have the most handcuffs. The player who is in the lead wins the game.

Sneaky combines simple rules with direct interaction, little mischief, and quick decisions—ideal for families, casual games, and anyone who enjoys short, exciting games.

Roll the dice wisely, secure your loot, and always stay one step ahead.

In this strategic duel for two players, your mission remains the same: maneuver your toy troops and cleverly use their effects to gain the upper hand over your opponent. To win the battle, you must capture the enemy headquarters or earn as many medals as possible.

Discover this brand new 9th battlefield, “La Croisette,” with the red and blue bases located at either end of the legendary promenade, one on a yacht and the other at the Palais des Festivals itself.

Adapt to the new effects and restrictions of this new board, including special bases placed on the water.

Who will achieve the best strategic advancement of their village across 5 ages and earn the most fame points? You start in the Middle Ages and hopefully make it all the way to the 20th century.

Who will achieve the best strategic advancement of their village across 5 ages and earn the most fame
points? You start in the Middle Ages and hopefully make it all the way to the 20th century.
Century.

Each player receives a player board/action board and a game board for their village development.

Additionally, a player board is used to store the acquired cards, and the central card board is placed in the middle. For each age, the cards are sorted, shuffled, and a face-down pile is created for each age. The first four cards are then revealed.
You start in the 1st age and have 25 inhabitants.

The game ends immediately as soon as someone reaches the 5th age and the target point on the progress bar. They receive an additional 5 points for this.

-description from designer

This bilingual (German/English) Content Pack 01 is the first mission expansion for the world of Under our Sun.

A total of 7 missions and 8 items/gear are included.
6 missions were written by us and will send you into the vastness of the wasteland with a wide variety of challenges.
The 7, however, is the winning mission from our Community Mission Challenge.

The new items include things like the condenser, which makes it possible to produce water, or the bear trap, which turns a map tile into a dangerous trap. There will also be a new weapon that allows you to inflict an injury on the target. 
It's definitely going to be exciting!

—description from the publisher

In Rotation Rumble you take over the role of a guild coach, that hires and commands mercenaries in the weekly rumble fights for money, cheesecake and glory. Chose wisely which fighters, challenges and inventory you combine to form the best possible pack of reckless rumblers.

Rotation Rumble is played with a pre-constructed deck of squared cards, which have 4 different actions on each side (in rare cases there are just 2) which can be activated by rotating them. This allows for endless combinations, possibilities and threats.

Cryptids & Contagion was originally a free add-on of the Rotation Rumble Base Set Kickstarter.

Oozefinger(black/green)
Coin Cough (green)
Foul Play (green)
Poxtrot (black/Green)
Ratastrophe (black)
Rat-atat-a (black)
Ratsputin (black)
Sick Sam (green) 
Sniffleton (black)
Snotter (green)
Venom Ring (green)

Mothman (blue/red)
Lab Coat (red)

Evil Science (red)

Mortimer (red)

In Rotation Rumble you take over the role of a guild coach, that hires and commands mercenaries in the weekly rumble fights for money, cheesecake and glory. Chose wisely which fighters, challenges and inventory you combine to form the best possible pack of reckless rumblers.

Rotation Rumble is played with a pre-constructed deck of squared cards, which have 4 different actions on each side (in rare cases there are just 2) which can be activated by rotating them. This allows for endless combinations, possibilities and threats.

The first player to 12 Victory points wins.

The Xtulhu Solo Deck can be played against all Rotation Rumble player decks.

boardgame: The Binding of Isaac: Four Souls – Warp Zone
description:
Cards for the game that feature characters and items sourced from other media and Twitch memes.

It was offered as a reward for the 2021 Kickstarter campaign, included in most pledges. It was not included in retail copies of the "Ultimate Collector's Edition", but could fit within.

RPSX is a 2-player abstract strategy game played on a 3x3 grid. Players take turns placing Rock, Paper, or Scissors chips onto the board. Chips can be stacked on top of existing chips, but only when the incoming chip beats the current top chip by rock-paper-scissors dominance (Rock beats Scissors, Scissors beats Paper, Paper beats Rock). Only the topmost chip in any cell counts toward board control and winning.

There are two ways to win. A Soft Win is achieved by getting three chips of the same type in a row (row, column, or diagonal). A Hard Win is achieved by getting Rock, Paper, and Scissors each in a row — one of each type. The Hard Win cannot be blocked or countered.

The advanced variant introduces on-board movement. Instead of placing a chip from their hand, a player may move a chip already on the board from one cell to another. Stacking rules apply on arrival. This mechanic is designed as a counterplay system: a player can use movement to change the top chip in a key cell, breaking an opponent's Soft Win line before it completes. Hard Wins remain unblockable in all variants.

Gigibugula is a fun family game, where you dive into a world of magic and try to be the first to brew a magical potion by skillfully tossing cards into the cauldron (the bottom part of the box).

The goal of the game is to collect all the ingredients from the recipe in the cauldron. When a player thinks all the recipe ingredients are in the cauldron, they must shout the magic word GIGIBUGULA, but they must do so only after successfully completing a throw.

You win by successfully cooking the magical potion or remaining the last one standing.

—description from the publisher

Players answer questions about soccer. The questions are divided into 4 disciplines (world championship, European championship, German Bundesliga, Premiere). Each correctly answered question counts as a "goal". 
If a player got a goal in each of the four disciplines, the other players choose a final question. 
The first player who answers the final question correctly wins the game.

boardgame: Galactic Cruise: Rescue Mission Promo Card
description:
Rettungsmission (Rescue mission) is a Promo card for the Galactic Cruise game. It is part of the magazine Spielbox 01/2026.

The card reads:
Rescue mission 
On a Day in Space, before receiving bonuses, you can pay 1 Fuel to add Any Guest from the supply to your spaceship, even without a free cabin.

Speculators is one of the 8 games in Dr. Finn's Book of Two-Player Strategy and Word Games. The game consists of one game sheet and requires no other components -- just a pencil for each player.

Speculators is a game about dividing opportunities and choosing wisely. Over six rounds, players take turns acting as the “Manager.” Each round, the Manager selects a box of 10 currency-related icons and divides it into two groups. The other player chooses one group, and both players gain the icons in their respective piles. Currency icons increase a player’s influence in one of four currencies (dollar, yen, pound, euro), point icons immediately score based on current influence levels, and arrow icons raise the market value of specific currencies—making those tracks more valuable at game end.

Players must carefully balance short-term gains with long-term strategy. Influence tracks and currency values can never exceed 10, so timing matters. The pencil icon determines who will be Manager next round, adding another layer of planning. At the start of the game, each player secretly selects two “bonus” currencies that will increase at the end of the game, potentially swinging the final score.

—description from the designer

In HACK.BAK - The Board Game, the group F.I.S.H (Frivolous Information Sealing Hackers) have encrypted your precious data. They've left you the tools you need to find the key, but they're spread across a network of servers, routers, and other devices. Your job is to find the commands, traverse the network, and unlock your Personal Data.

To play HACK.BAK - The Board Game, you’ll traverse a computer network looking for encrypted files and their keys. This network is built during play using various nodes and connectors.

Players will be scanning nodes and searching for new commands to find a path forward in order to be the first to decrypt their data.

The game can be played as a 6-stage introduction game that adds new elements at each step or, once familiar with the tiles, a single stage advanced game that uses all tiles immediately.

—description from the designer

This is a small, pocket-sized trivia game. It contains 50 cards with 1 question/exercise each. The backside of each card contains the answers. Most of the cards also show small illustrations.

The title translates to "Logical Thinking".

The object of the game is to capture the pig by landing on the pig's card. The player who accomplishes this wins.

The cards are laid in a circle, the pig is randomly placed on a card. The players must each choose a starting card at least 7 cards away from the pig. They place their marker on the selected card.

The players roll the dice and move their marker accordingly. If the player lands on the pig's card, they win. If they land on another card, they must look at the circle under the pig's card and move the pig to a card of the same colour.

The German original title is: Denkste sprach die Sau.

Player 1 places their pawn on an arrow in their respective color. Player 2 does the same. Player 2 then places a chip on the intersection of both arrows. 
Player 1 then moves their pawn and places a chip on the intersection. Then Player 2 moves their pawn, places a chip and so on.
If an intersection is already taken, the chip must be replaced with the new chip, even if it helps the opponent win the game.

The first player to have placed 3 chips in their color in a horizontal, vertical or diagonal line wins the game.

Between Wars, knights met in Tournaments to prove their skill.

Tournament is a classic playing card game for 2 (or 4) players where Knights (and Squires) play cards across nine Events of Speed, Accuracy, and Strength, each with its own test. Win the most Events to claim victory.

Tournament builds on the familiar card game War by adding meaningful player choice. Instead of relying solely on high card wins, players must adapt to shifting Event goals that reward speed, accuracy, and strength.

The result is a faster, more tactical experience with far less downtime. A full match plays out over three quick rounds of about a minute each, rather than the prolonged race to capture every card that can make traditional War drag on for 30 minutes or more.

A classic playing card game is needed to play.

Simple quiz game about animals. The host/hostess (probably Mum or Dad in this case) sets a time limit and the one with the most correct answers when time runs out, wins.

There are 50 cards with two questions per card and answers on the reverse. Questions aimed at kids so won't be too taxing.

The Games & More game “Tricky Tree” requires a lot of dexterity and a steady hand. The tree crown with the symbols is placed on the tree trunk. Now the first player rolls the dice and places the apple on the symbol shown on the dice. This continues until the tree falls over. Whoever causes the tree to fall loses the game.

Rondello is a light family roulette game for 2–4 players.
At the beginning of the game, each player receives a set of numbered pawns along with a special wildcard piece.

On their turn, players secretly select one of their remaining pawns and commit it to the current spin. The roulette wheel determines the winning number. Correct predictions allow the chosen piece to be placed into the corresponding scoring area, while unsuccessful attempts return to the player’s supply.

The winner is the first player to successfully place all of their pawns.

This is a three-dimensional abstract strategy game in which players take turns placing their stones on the structure in an effort to create a winning formation (a line or a square).

A player wins as soon as they complete the required formation — either a straight line or a square of their stones, as defined by the game’s rules.

Una Vaca al Volante is a family racing card game in which players compete to complete laps around a farm track while interfering with each other’s progress.

Each player begins with a hand of seven cards. On their turn, a player plays one card and then draws one from the deck. Movement cards advance the player 0.25, 0.50, 0.75, or 1 lap. Power cards affect opponents by forcing them to move backward, skip turns, copy movement, or gain temporary protection from effects.

Completed laps become locked and cannot be removed by other players. The first player to complete eight laps wins the game.

—description from the designer

Get ready for Fast Friends, the speed jinx guessing game where saying the same word is harder than you think.

Players race to match minds before the timer buzzes, hoping to shout the same phrase at the same time. Every round brings laughter, surprises, and friendly chaos as players lose Life Tokens and new pairs take their turn.

Simple to learn, fast to play, and endlessly replayable, Fast Friends is the ultimate family game for family game night, parties, and holidays.

Win as many treasures as possible by entering into conflicts and alliances, to compete for the control of lucrative islands. But you are a pirate, and so are "they". Alliances can change at any time...

Players have the same hand of numbered cards initially, and take turns placing one card on either side on an island. Alliances are possible, but only when a conflict takes place first, and different alliances can be formed over different islands. When all cards have been played, card values are summed on both sides of each island, and the strongest alliance on each island shares that island's treasure. The lion's share goes to the strongest pirate within the alliance. The winner is the pirate having gained the most treasure overall.

Note that special actions cards are available to play a few dirty tricks on opponents... May the trickiest pirate win!

-description from designer

Gagnez le plus de trésors possible grâce à des conflits ou des alliances, avec pour but de contrôler des îles pleines de richesses. Mais vous êtes un pirate, et "eux" aussi. Les alliances peuvent changer à tout moment...

Les joueurs commencent avec un main identique de cartes numérotées, et à leur tour placent une carte sur un des côtés opposés d'une île. Les alliances sont autorisées, mais seulement après qu'un conflit se manifeste, et les alliances peuvent être différentes sur chaque île. Une fois toutes les cartes jouées, les valeurs des cartes sont totalisées des deux côtés de chaque île, et l'alliance la plus forte sur chaque île se partage le trésor de cette île. La part du lion revient au pirate majoritaire dans l'alliance. Le vainqueur est le pirate ayant accumulé le plus de trésors.

A noter que des cartes spéciales sont disponibles pour faire quelques coups bas aux adversaires... Que le pirate le plus opportuniste gagne !

Bear Hunt is a tactical game of asymmetrical strategy, where one player controls a Bear and the other controls 3 Gladiators.

The Gladiators win by surrounding the Bear so it cannot move. The Bear wins by surviving 40 turns without being trapped.

I Think I Know Chinese is a light, cooperative party game for two or more players where everyone works together to guess Chinese words using Element Tiles.

This isn’t a language course. There are no tests, no pressure, and absolutely no homework. Instead, players learn naturally through exposure, repetition, and association.

By seeing words repeatedly, hearing them spoken aloud, and linking them to playful clues, your brain begins to recognize patterns on its own. Sounds become familiar. Characters stop looking mysterious. Meanings start to click.

Somehow, between the laughter, wild guesses, and questionable logic, words begin sticking in your memory through pure osmosis. Learning sneaks in while you’re busy having fun.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Squillo society: Non si può più dire niente – Revisionismo storico
description:
18+ Party game, heavy black humor.
You open your social media app and notice everyone is outraging about something. You need to guess what everyone is indignant at by looking at clues (social media comments), but beware of trolls, who won't give you relevant clues, they just want to see the world burn!

Third chapter of the series, the theme is historical revisionism

Link-15 is a strategic, run-building card game where players create colored numerical sequences, utilize game-changing wild cards, and race to build a “Link-15.” With a 96-card deck spanning six vibrant suits, each numbered 1 through 15 plus a matching “Link” (wild) card, every hand becomes a puzzle of timing, opportunity, and clever combinations. Players score by laying down runs of three or more same colored cards in order. Runs can be extended by anyone at the table, and every card you add earns you points — whether you’re building your own runs or “linking” to someone else’s. Link cards add a dynamic twist: Each one is a suit specific wild card that can be replaced, reused, or strategically locked into place for big points. The ultimate achievement is the Link 15. Having numbers 1 through 15 on the table at once, in any mix of colors, earns a massive 150 point bonus and can decide the game. With six suits, flexible wild cards, and open table play, Link-15 rewards smart hand management and encourages enjoyable interaction among family and friends. Easy to learn and endlessly replayable, Link-15 is ideal for families, casual players, and anyone who enjoys classical card strategy with a satisfying wild card twist.

—description from the publisher

In this semi-cooperative game players take on the roles of the main heroes from the novel and play out the plot of "Dracula". The Count has decided to come to Victorian London. Being a vampire, he has brought numerous crates of Transylvanian dirt with him in which he must rest. To defeat him, the players must track down and destroy all of these boxes, and then they must catch Dracula and put him to a final end.

The crates are initially together in a hidden crypt somewhere in London. Players will use the unique abilities of their characters to gather clues from Renfield in London, or perhaps journey to Transylvania to gather information there. Each location on the board will have cards on it, and players must manipulate those cards to meet a target number in order to successfully investigate a location. Each location on the board offers a unique action which can be augmented with items and privilege cards as the heroes work together towards their goal.

In the meantime, Dracula fights back by spreading location-blocking dread throughout the city and throwing obstacles at the heroes that they must fend off. The heroes can lose immediately if Dracula completes his plans or if the Resolve deck runs dry. But Dracula's most powerful weapon might be the corrupted and influenced players he has recruited to his side.

If the heroes can get to the final chapter, and have succeeded in sanctifying all of his resting places, they will pursue Dracula back to Transylvania. But will they have gathered enough resources by then to catch him? Will his minion help him escape?

—description from the designer

Completely original and unique gameplay! Easy to pick up, but hard to master. Capture an opponent's flag and return to your base without getting tagged. First to return with a flag wins. Simple, right? You need speed and dexterity with a pencil to maneuver around obstacles, monsters, traps, and collect powerups all while staying out of reach of your opponent!
Objective: Take an opponent's flag and return to your base without getting tagged to Capture it. First to Capture a Flag wins!

Setup: Players choose a Map together, and each chooses their Base on the Map and a pencil color to use.

Gameplay & Movement: 

     The first turn begins with a player making a Dot anywhere along their Base Line. The player then places the tip of their pencil on the Dot, and, using ONE finger on the opposite end, hold the pencil vertically in place.
     The player then allows the pencil tip to slide along the paper away the Base, drawing a Line. 
     The player then marks a large Dot anywhere along this new Unobstructed Line. The Dot signifies the end of the turn. 
     The next turn for this player starts on this new Dot.
     Players take turns moving in this way until one player crosses the Base Line of an opponent, at which time they have TAKEN the Flag. Play continues as they return to their own Base in the same way, thereby succeeding in the objective and Capturing  a Flag. 
         Players receive an automatic extra turn for taking an opponent’s Flag, which must be taken from the opponent’s Base Line.
    
     Players cannot go through nor under Obstacles, only around them. Play Lines are not Obstacles. 
     Players can Tag other players by drawing their Line through the opponent's current position Dot. If a player is Tagged they must immediately return to their Base, forfeiting any Flags they hold. The Tagger's Flag is automatically returned to its Base, Any Powerups the Tagged player holds are transferred to the Tagger.


—description from the designer

The purpose of fighter aircraft is the destruction of enemy aircraft. US Navy fighter Manual

Welcome to a different type of air combat game.  
A game of teamwork, planning, opportunity and luck. 
Each element rolls 6 performance dice to determine their opportunities for the next minute. 
The dice are modified by pilot skill and aircraft capabilities.

Are you in a Zero?  You probably want to get in close and mix it up.  
Are you in a Corsair?  A slashing attack strategy should work just fine. 
Movement is by area and maneuvers are abstracted so the game doesn't slow down.  
You need to think and act fast when you are moving over 300 mph!

A 4 v 4 game will play in under an hour.

Game includes stats for 62 models of fighters and 19 bombers from the US, UK, Germany, Japan, Italy and France. As a bonus 11 aircraft cards are included for late war US and German aircraft.

Crafting Crawler is a dungeon-building and exploration game where you generate the map as you forge your path, defeating the threats that lie within. 
Choose your hero,or a party of adventurers, to reach the deepest corners of this ever-shifting dungeon.Defeat the Ancient Dragon and claim the riches hidden behind its walls.

This variant expands play of the game, La Vendée-1793: Counterrevolution in the West, published in Against the Odd' 2019 Annual to portray the 1815 Vendée rebellion against Napoleon Bonaparte's return to power in France.
language_dependency: 4: Extensive use of text - massive conversion needed to be playable

description:
A dagger represents an option for a double move.

Stiletto is a game from the family of Gomoku, where players try to make a five in-a-row. The main difference is that the second player (traditionally White) starts with a dagger. This allows to compensate for Black's strong initial advantage. When players use the dagger, they pass it to the adversary.

Full rules and examples at the game's forum.

Stiletto, from the Italian, is a small, slender dagger with a long, narrow blade designed primarily for thrusting.

How well do you know your Christmas trivia? The Nutcracker's Christmas Quiz is a jolly card game full of festive questions to challenge your holiday knowledge!

Play solo or in teams and see who can answer the merriest (and trickiest!) Christmas trivia — from traditions to fun facts. It’s the perfect game to enjoy after a big holiday meal or by the cozy fire.

Ideal for family gatherings, parties, or anyone looking for some festive fun!"

Make your holiday merry and bright with this fun, festive Christmas quiz!

—description from the publisher

Colour the Chameleon is a pedagogic children's game, focusing on colour recognition. Players roll the die and move around the game board, trying to find the right pieces for their chameleon.

The first player to find all four pieces for their chameleon wins the game.

One player takes the red pieces, the other the black pieces. The pieces for each side consist of:

1 lion
2 snakes
2 tigers
2 elephants
2 bears
9 monkeys.

The pieces are placed on two opposite sides of the board. The trick is to free the captured lion by moving it to the center of the board with several moves and the help of the other pieces. The opposing pieces, in turn, prevent this with further moves. Each animal has its own movement type. The first player to get their lion to the center without being threatened by an enemy piece wins. Incidentally, if the center space is free, it can also be occupied by any other piece.

Alternatively the game can be played with the goal to trap the lion, like the king in chess.

The rocket is ready — but without the correct launch code, it will never leave the ground. In this cooperative, silent deduction game, players must assemble the exact sequence of command cards to initiate the countdown.

Each player holds a portion of the launch code, but communication is strictly forbidden. On your turn, choose one of your cards and place it face down into the shared command row, trying to deduce the correct order based on timing, intuition, and the limited information available.

Once all cards have been played, the sequence is revealed.

If the commands are in the correct order, the countdown begins and the team successfully launches the rocket.

If the sequence is incorrect, the launch fails — and the team must regroup and try again.

The game ends immediately after the reveal, resulting in either a collective victory or defeat. Success depends entirely on the group’s ability to read each other’s intentions and reconstruct the hidden code without a single word spoken.

-description from publisher

boardgame: Horrible Histories: From the Savage Stone Age to the Measly Middle Ages
description: Battle your way through years of Horrible History with two great card games in one box! The great Horrible Histories Stone Age Battle Pack contains 40 illustrated, jumbo-sized battle cards each featuring historical characters dating from the savage stoneage to the measly middle ages. For 2-4 players age 6 years and over.

boardgame: Horrible Histories From the Terrifying: Tudors to the Terrible 21st Century
description: Battle your way through years of Horrible History with two great card games in one box! The great Horrible Histories Tudors Battle Pack contains 40 illustrated, jumbo-sized battle cards each featuring historical characters dating from the terrifying Tudors through to the terrible 20th Century.
The game is a recreation of the Admiral Graf Spee's commerce raiding beginning in September 1939 and its efforts to evade the Royal Navy.

boardgame: Town Center: Designer's Attic Maps Volume 3
description:
Designer’s Attic Maps are Town Center expansions.

This is the volume 3.
Each volume will include 6 new maps with new twists and different lay outs. 
The variant is used by all the players. It is compatible with all the maps (except stated exceptions). Players can mix the cities and agree on each map they want to play.
You will visit:
- Cairo/ Forbidden City
- Berlin/Shanghai
- Venice/Lunar Base

Some maps are more difficult than others and like in reality, it is not fair all the time...

boardgame: Town Center: Designer's Attic Maps Volume 4
description:
Designer’s Attic Maps are Town Center expansions.

This is the volume 4.
Each volume will include 6 new maps with new twists and different lay outs. 
The variant is used by all the players. It is compatible with all the maps (except stated exceptions). Players can mix the cities and agree on each map they want to play.
You will visit:
- Athens/Atlantis
- Mexico City/Moscow
- St Petersburg/Vancouver

Some maps are more difficult than others and like in reality, it is not fair all the time...

boardgame: Adventure Time Card Wars: DarkLands Cooperative Expansion
description:
The DarkLands cooperative expansion requires two Adventure Time Card Wars decks to play. Players join forces against DarkLands Creatures and a powerful Overlord armed with devastating Curses. The Army deck contains 4 factions to mix and match to create sinister pairings. To win, defeat the Overlord before your castle is overrun or either you or your partner fall!

Contents include 4 DarkLands Overlords to defeat, 4 DarkLands Landscapes, and 91 cards (44 Creature, 40 Curse, 5 Barrier, 2 Difficulty Reminder), plus various tokens.

The DarkLands expansion was first released in a 2025 Kickstarter stretch goals box with other Card Wars cards. That version included 5 Overlords, 4 Landscapes, and 108 cards.

​👽 SECTOR 21:

Bio-density levels approaching critical. Technician 
7-G, initiate Purge Sequence immediately.

​Sector 21 is a tense, solo "push-your-luck" survival game played with a standard 54-card deck. As the last surviving technician on a derelict vessel, your only goal is to vent a mounting alien infestation into the vacuum of space before your life support systems fail entirely.

The Imperial Japanese Army deployed its tanks to defend the island of Saipan in June 1944, and again the Philippines where the 2nd Tank Division tried to stop the American advance. Japanese tanks were not very good, and the Imperial Army’s leaders made things worse by holding back their very best weapons for the defense of the home islands.

Pacific Armor is a Panzer Grenadier expansion, focused on Japanese tanks and their battles on Saipan and Luzon. You get the tanks that fought these battles, and the tanks that could have done so. Could the Japanese have done better if they’d sent their best tanks? Play it out for yourself!

You get 64 new pieces, all die-cut and silky-smooth. And 22 new scenarios of Pacific tank action. You’ll need Saipan 1944, Elsenborn Ridge, Leyte 1944, and Marianas 1944 to play all of the scenarios.

—description from the publisher

Kampen om Alingsås is a game about the Swedish city of alingsås. The mechanics is, roughly, a mix of Monopoly and Trivial Pursuit where most of the questions are about the city and its history. Players move around a track on the board and acquire properties and answer trivia questions about the featured city.

—description from the publisher

Dealing Dinos is a reaction game with a dinosaur theme. The deck consists of 60 circular cards with different dinosaurs and dino related images on them. All the cards are dealt out to the players who place them in a face down stack in front. The oldest player goes first and flips over their top card. If the card is an action card (has a dark border) each player performs the appropriate action. The slowest player picks up all the cards and places them underneath their stack. If a player performs an action at the wrong time they pick up all the cards and place them under there stack. The first player to get rid of all their cards wins.

Step into the animal world of social simulation designed for the sharpest strategists. Driven by an ambitious desire to thrive like humans, animals evolved to forge a civilized society through cooperation. In this animal society, the “King of the jungle” is no longer crowned by raw power, but through wealth! Emulating humanity’s way of life, their strategic sharpens. Gather your fellow furry friends, and plots amongst yourselves. In the end, only one can become the wealthiest new “King of the Jungle”.

In this simple mind game, players works in the ore mine reveals one of the three actions (Work, Guard or Steal) simultaneously and resolves according to the combination revealed. Players would either continue playing(work) or take their chances(Guard/Steal). Either way, precious ore with be distributed to the deserving players at the end of the season. Game continues until one player reaches target ore amount to become the new King. With Prisoner’s Dilemma at its core, each decision couldn’t be more exciting. Come see for yourself in “Dirty Work”.

-description from designer

A 'party game' iteration of on the old Pie Face game, apparently for grown-ups.  "Turn the handles to test your luck" and get smacked in the face by fluffy cream.

Players position their chin on the device, roll a die, and turn the handles as many times as the die requires. You either get a face full of cream, or points equal to the # of the die.  First to 30 points wins!

In the shadows of a desperate ritual, you can’t trust the person holding the holy water—or even the one standing right next to you.

The Possessed is a dark, high-stakes social deduction game for 6-10 players. A demon has inhabited one of you as a host, and the Clock of Doom—the Demon Track—is ticking.

As a team of Exorcists, you must navigate a two-stage nightmare. First, you must investigate to identify which of the nine ancient demons is active; get it wrong, and the demon grows stronger. Once identified, you must gather the precise ritual components to cast it out.

But here’s the twist: The Cultists are already among you. They know the demon, they know the ritual, and they are masters of sabotage—lying about research, discarding vital components, and sowing enough doubt to ensure the ritual fails. Meanwhile, Infiltrators know who the Cultists are but must stay hidden to guide the Good Team from the darkness.

It all culminates in The Exorcism, where the player with the most Trust performs the final act. If you’ve placed your faith in a secret Cultist or an overzealous Infiltrator, the ritual fails, and the world plunges into darkness.

Do you have the intuition to find the truth, or the silver tongue to unleash Hell?

The Possessed also includes a solo mode and a cooperative mode for 2–5 players.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Tag Team: The Fey Folk Promo
description:
Originally available as part of B Pack from the Dice Tower 2025 Gamefound campaign.

The Fey Folk

     Complexity 4 (out of 5)
     Support
     Elusive

From designated "home" cities, players travel the roads of Oregon to collect Region Cards, lay down City Cards and try to be the first to return back home.  Players may also visit Oregon sites such as Crater Lake, Oregon Caves, and Hell's Canyon.

boardgame: Fantasy Arcade: Un jeu de figurines de 1 à 4 joueurs
description:
Fantasy Arcade is a miniatures game for 1 to 4 players.

Across 5 scenarios, playable independently or as a campaign, you will face creatures controlled by the game itself, aiming to complete the scenarios in the most heroic way possible. 
You will control the flow of opponents entering the game, and the better you manage this, the higher your score will be. 
The game's simplicity, thanks to a unique and ingenious system, maintains the tactical interest of this type of game while reinventing the style with a more pronounced action sequence for even greater strategic depth. 
Managing your limited supply of power dice will offer enjoyable tactical choices.

The goal is to combine easy access with great depth of gameplay. But make no mistake, the primary objective will be to achieve the highest score!

Neopia Quest is a cooperative adventure board game where players take on the roles of their favourite Neopets, each joined by a loyal Petpet companion, to explore the vibrant world of Neopia.

Travel to iconic locations, interact with beloved characters, and embark on story-driven quests filled with challenges, teamwork, and classic Neopets charm.

Each of the ten unique quests offers a different objective, setup, and gameplay twist. You and your fellow Neopets must work together to succeed. Whether you’re gathering rare ingredients for the Soup Faerie’s grand dinner party or disabling Dr. Sloth’s baby ray gun to save Neopia itself, every quest delivers a fresh cooperative experience.
