At its heart, Neopia Quest combines route planning, pickup and deliver objectives, and resource management into a streamlined, accessible system that rewards communication, coordination, and clever decision making.

Adventure awaits across Neopia!

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Goblin Village Raid: A Procedural Adventure for Four Against Darkness
description:
With this mini-zine, you can generate raids into a goblin village for your Four Against Darkness games. Goblins have stolen something and your party must sneak (or charge!) into their village to bring back the loot. This mini-zine reprints the content of the Miniscreen adventure that was available only in print format directly from Ganesha Games. The content has been updated to the HCL system which scales dangers and foes to the party's level, so that it may be used for characters of all levels. It includes goblin-themed major foes, minor foes, loot and events.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Simple Battalion System: Module 08 – Korsun Pocket
description:
Simple Battalion System (SBS) is an operational wargame system for PNP.

This module contains a winter hex map in Ukraine 1944, two sheets of double-side counters and three scenarios.

Rules are simple with 12 pages only.

—description from the publisher

The Ogre Supplement for Battlecry ushers in a new era of epic warfare and dark mysticism. From the burning sands of the Great Desert rises the formidable Ogre faction, bringing with it unmatched strength, savage cunning, and mysterious rites of power.

This expansion redefines the Battlecry experience with sweeping updates: an enhanced core rules for tighter tactical play, redesigned equipment and spells, and unique faction rules that breathe fresh life into every army. Dive into rich new lore, discover expertly rebalanced gameplay, and master a bold, all-new game mode that will challenge battling players.

Whether you’re rallying your first troops or perfecting your battlefield strategy, The Ogre Supplement is a monumental addition that transforms how Battlecry: Fantasy Battles is played.

The next great age of warfare has begun—are you ready to lead it?

boardgame: Horse Racing Jam: Memory and Luck Showdown!
description:
"Jam it. Push it. Change it. Stop it!"
Inspired by Hong Kong's iconic horse racing and legendary traffic. This is a race like no other, tons of horses on the track. Shove your rivals off the track, flip the scoring rules, and suddenly call an end when the finish sprint! Gain score from others like a freerider. Use your memory, luck and sharp decision-making to outrun the chaos and claim victory.

—description from the publisher

2 Promocards from Essen ´25 from Spielezentrum Herne for Bier Pioniere:

- H2Ö Herne
(upper: 1 bulb, 1 rotation, instant draw 2 cards
lower: every other player must give you 1 card, if he cant helooses 1 VP)

- Cranger Kirmes
(side: action for lager
lower: every other player must give you 1 card, if he cant helooses 1 VP)

"Let us cross over the river, and rest under the shade of the trees" - Thomas "Stonewall" Jackson before his death

Mr. Lincoln's War is a historical game which captures the epic struggle of the American Civil War. From their first major battle at Bull Run in 1861, through the campaigns of Vicksburg, Gettysburg and Atlanta and until the final days at Appomattox in 1865, the armies of the Union and the Confederacy raged across America wreaking havoc and bloodshed on a scale never before or since witnessed in the New World. Reorganized into a single game, The Army of The Tennessee, which dealt with the war in the west, and The Army of the Potomac, which dealt with the war in Virginia, has been combined into one edition.

This box set is organized into two sub-games, “Army of the Tennessee,” which deals with the war in the western theater, and “Army of the Potomac,” which deals with the war in Virginia. Each has four short scenarios that recreate the maneuvers and battles of Shiloh, Gettysburg, Atlanta, and other monumental Civil War contests.

Campaign games enabling the players to fight the war in the West or East are provided with each sub-game. They may be combined to play the Mr. Lincoln’s War grand campaign that simulates the entire Civil War, on both fronts. The rules of the sub-games are identical, except for individual scenario rules, included to better recreate those campaigns. There are minor differences in victory and production rules between the campaign scenarios of the two games, but they are resolved when the sub-games are joined for the grand campaign.

Players may construct their armies as they see fit during campaign games by beginning with volunteer calls and later calling for a draft. Create navies to either smuggle goods through a blockade or close the Southern ports to trade. Can you hold off the Union or seize Richmond and bring about the end of America's bloodiest conflict?

Product Information:

Complexity: Medium   Solitaire: Medium
Time Scale: 1 Turn=1 Month
Map: 1 Hex=2.3 Miles
Unit Scale: One Strength Point=3000 Men
Players: 2-4
Playing Time: 2-6 Hours For Scenarios, 10+ Hours For Campaigns

—description from the publisher

Bear Necessities is a competitive tableau‑building game featuring simultaneous drafting with a reserve, in‑game scoring, and honeypots!

During each round, players draft two cards from their starting hand: one to add to their tableau and one to save for the next round, then pass the remaining cards to the player on their left.

The card you add to your tableau must be placed orthogonally adjacent to an already placed card, and it activates immediately, granting you victory points. It also triggers all of its adjacent neighbors, creating powerful combos and cascading effects.

The cards you receive from the other player are combined with the one you saved from the previous round, and you draft again until your hand is empty.

At that point, you replenish your hand and repeat the process until everyone has completed a 4‑column by 3‑row grid: after 12 rounds, the game ends and the player with the most points is the winner!

—description from the publisher

Something fell from the sky in Antarctica and was quickly captured.

The key to controlling the invader is finding their ship's navigation device, camouflaged among the base's equipment.

Can you find the device and take control of the ship?

If you're human, your objective is to discover the object chosen by the captured invader before time or your actions run out.

But if you're an invader, your objective is to prevent the humans from discovering the chosen object.

The future of humanity depends on what happens in these next few minutes. Are you up for the challenge of finding the device?

RUMMY STRATEGY MEET TRIVIA: Players discover amazing facts about the world while building their own rummy colonies. It's the perfect blend of trivia and rummy set collection — ideal board games for kids 8–12, teens, and adults alike.

+ 100 NEW CARDS: Discover New Questions from Your Favorite Topics. This game expansion includes a set of new cards with fun and fascinating questions about wild animals. Answer general knowledge questions, collect cards, and build powerful colonies! Easy to learn — a perfect board games for family night.

+ 1 PAWN for an EXTRA PLAYER: No More Friends on the Sidelines. This expansion includes 1 additional pawn and a set of four A-B-C answer cards, allowing you to add one EXTRA PLAYER to the game. Ignite curiosity and connection with an interactive family board game that brings everyone to the table. It's one of the best family games for kids and adults to enjoy together — meaningful fun for every generation.

THIS IS AN EXPANSION and NOT a stand-alone game! Make sure you also have the Rummy Colony Base Game. Discover available expansions and bring even more friends and family into the game. Great for fans of trivia games and educational games with strategy.

BET 賭博 • GUESS 猜詞 • WIN 贏大獎

ONE SHOT is a party game for 4–10 players combining word guessing with betting.

Each round begins with the Guesser drawing a word card and selecting a number, which reveals the secret word to all other players. The Predictors then write their hints on whiteboards and place them in the corresponding stalls on a first-come, first-served basis, along with the required coin bets. Alternatively, players may skip giving hints and instead place bets on the likely outcome of the round.

The Guesser reveals hints in order, earning one guess per board with decreasing coin rewards for correct answers. 
Correct bets pay out, incorrect ones feed the Jackpot.

Guessing correctly from the Jackpot stall splits the pot with that hint writer; a Burst rewards those who bet on failure with all wagered coins. After each player has been Guesser twice, the richest player wins.

An 18-card solo game played entirely in your hand!

Intrigue & lost souls lie deep behind enemy lines. You've been summoned to help with a series of daring operations, & you've answered the call determined to bring your expertise in these matters. Now the fate of these heroic missions rests in your hands.

You're the pilot of a spy plane, attempting to guide allies on the ground to obtain intel & rescue lost agents far from home. But beware, for danger lurks around every bend.

To play, simply perform a basic "round" over & over again until game's end.

Each round has 4 turns:

1st, you'll guide your secret CONTACT as they navigate their way through maps with varied terrain & icons.

2nd, you'll move your UNIT of Special OPS as they attempt to maneuver to desired positions.

3rd, the ASSASSIN (bad guy) will travel to areas according to the current logistics in play.

Finally, you'll SURVEY the land from your cockpit & address present weather conditions.

TO WIN, you'll have to complete 2 main stages: INTEL & AGENTS. Find secret intel by getting the correct ally to occupy a specific space within the maps. You must find 4 out of a possible 8 intel. Then, rescue 4 out of 8 lost agents in the field by acquiring pieces of each of their unique code symbols scattered throughout the land. After all this, you'll be tasked with getting your team to the chopper extraction point to become victorious.

You'll gain rewards &/or setbacks each time you find an intel or agent. You'll also risk damage to your pressure gauges, fuel loss, & the elimination of some of your Special OPS members depending on your movement, strategy, & tactics. If any of these aforementioned levels reach zero, you lose!

You'll also have access to a few emergency AIRSTRIKES to use on the Assassin if caught in a major jam.

Lastly, there are 7 optional expansion modes built into the final page of the rulebook (no need to purchase extra cards or components)! These can help ease you into the game (e.g., ROOKIE mode), provide harder challenges (MAYDAY), or just mix things up in new ways (THE DRUNKARD, PICK UP & DELIVER).

NOTES:

All cards are kept upright in a pile in your hand, with no need to hold them spread out awkwardly or turned horizontally.

There is no draw or discard pile. Easily play this on an airplane, couch, toilet, or wherever you like.

There's a handy "turn order" card that stays at the top of the deck. This helps you remember the decisions for each of the 4 turns. It also makes it easy to put the cards away & return to the game later to know exactly where you stand & where you left off!

You can also play this game with the cards laid out on a table if you like. Everything works the same way.

—description from the designer

A high-octane dexterity game inspired by classic fighting video games. Slam down wooden tiles, unleash combos, and outplay your opponent in real time.

In Blitz Fight, every match unfolds in two intense phases:

1) Real-Time Programming
At the same time as your opponent, you program your fighter’s actions with tiles on a shared lane of 10 to 12 slots. Chain moves by matching colors, line up attacks at the correct range, and build devastating combos under pressure. Fighters don’t physically move during this phase, even when movement tiles are played, so you must track both positions in your head while planning your next strike.

2) Resolution
When the programming stops, the action explodes. Both players resolve their moves step by step, revealing what really happens. Clean hits deal damage, while mistakes and mistimed tiles punish you instead. After all actions resolve, reset the tiles and jump straight into the next round. The fight continues until one fighter hits 0 health and gets KO’d.

Blitz Fight is fast, chaotic, and packed with hilarious moments, but victory belongs to skill. Every match sharpens your instincts as you learn to play faster, picture the battlefield, and execute clean combos under pressure.

The game is easy to learn and explosive to play. Whether it’s a quick duel or a full tournament with friends, Blitz Fight delivers nonstop adrenaline from the first tile to the final KO.

boardgame: It Walked With Thunder in Its Wake: An Adventure for Four Against Darkness
description:
A quick adventure where your party must hunt down a giant thunder lizard through a goblin-infested jungle. Old Scarred Eye is not so easy to hunt down, and there's a chance that the hunters will become the hunted...

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Four Against Beasts: Tribalpunk Solo Adventure Game
description:
Massive wars have shaken the Earth of the Four Places. Ykah, the occult science of altering reality with one’s thoughts, is being rediscovered. Beasts are countless, and hungry for flesh… Vicious uuzans experimented on the Beasts, creating aberrations called Kraazas. Half uuzan and half Beast, these creatures are unleashed on settlements. Beastlords command them, imprisoning and enslaving people. Adventurers called “itinerants” explore this world, employed by settlements as a last resort to do the dirty work no-one else would do. In Four Against Beasts, a single player runs a party of four savage Itinerants, fighting their way through a settlement infested by monsters and rife with danger. Walking in darkness but helped by heightened senses of smell and hearing, you will search for Clues to find the lair of the Kraaza Master and defeat it, and collect tools and weapons to aid you in your quest. Based on the popular Four Against Darkness rules, this book is a stand-alone, complete adventure game for solo play in a dark fantasy world that’s unlike anything you have played before.

–description from the publisher

CyberRoll is a cooperative cybersecurity strategy board game where you and your team take on the role of a company's security department.

Your goal is to survive three years of escalating cyber threats, manage resources, and keep morale up while hackers and office chaos try to knock you off course.

Years are divided into four quarters, and each quarter has 4 phases: Plan, Threat, Defend, Resolve. In Plan, you purchase controls, hire staff, and devise your strategy. In Threat, you roll to see what new threats will target your organization. In Defend, you see how your strategy does against the threats you draw. And in Resolve, you collect income and see if any strategies pay off.

—description from the publisher

A chill fog drifts across the sea. The crew whispers of curses and dark magic. It can’t possibly be true…but the strange sights you pass give you little comfort against the creeping dread.

Add haunting new marvels to discover, as well as a new ship (with new journey effects), with this 6-card expansion. Swap out marvels from the base game with new, cursed legends or add them in directly for an epic journey!

Cursed Seas 6-card expansion adds two optional components: a new ship with unique journey effects, and five marvels featuring distinctive costs and placement restrictions. Each can be used on its own with the base game or combined for greater variety. The expansion also includes a new Epic Mode, offering a longer journey and tougher scoring targets to conquer.

In Batatas, you can't let the hot potato stay in your hand!

Draw potatoes from the main pile until someone else draws a hot potato, then start a discard battle to prevent the potato from staying in your hand; otherwise, you're eliminated.

Potatoes is a game based on the hot potato game. It's a fast-paced game with easy-to-learn mechanics that can be played with friends or family.

Annulet is a 1-4 player strategy game set in the world of The Old King's Crown. A game which conjures the feeling of playing an ancient game, whether in a bustling tavern or stately hall, complete with tactile components made of wood, cloth and stone. All designed to transport you to a place in that fantastical land.

Players will compete for initiative each round, as they vie to draft cards from the river to create their kingdoms, whether they be lands, characters or annulets. 
Lands create valuable sets, characters alter the lands on which they are placed allowing for creative scoring opportunities, and finally, annulets provide precious stones which provide a currency for extra actions, as well as an end game bonus.

As players weigh scoring for crowns and gaining action economy enhancing stones, they'll also have access to a personal supply of fate cards, whose special abilities allow for shifts in momentum and control.

Meanwhile ever-present tale cards, which represent the storied history of the kingdom and its game, will alter the very rules for the whole table, meaning that no two matches of Annulet will play out the same.

-description from designer

Amigos do Veneno is a party game about honesty.

It consists of 3 decks: "Questions," "Target," and "Poison."

One player draws a question card, followed by the target card, which dictates who must answer.

All players can check their hands to see which poison cards can be used in the situation, potentially preventing players from answering the question or revealing more details about their lives.

The player who answers the most questions wins the game!

🛡🗡 THE RIBCAGE:

The world didn't end with a bang; it ended with a Casket.

​The Ribcage is a colossal, subterranean cathedral carved from the chest cavity of a dead god. It was built to contain the Four Blighted Kings, brothers who attempted to steal the god’s divinity and were instead fused into its rotting essence.

Now, the Ribcage is a "living" prison that breathes through the 9 Stone Pillars—the calcified vertebrae that hold the ceiling of the world aloft.

Video game adaptation here by ryeguy00:

https://ryeguy00.itch.io/the-ribcage

“Ninja Tricks” is a trick-taking game featuring a special “must-not-follow” rule: you may not play a card of the same suit as any suit that has already been played in the current trick. The goal of the game is to have the highest total score at the end of all rounds.

Cards that lose a trick become Scroll Cards, which you can use in future tricks. If you collect enough Scroll Cards before winning a trick, you can score a large number of points.

Be careful not to be disrupted by other players’ trump “10” cards. Since ties in number (strength) are broken in favor of the later-played card, the chance of being blocked is high. If you can slip past your opponents’ interference and make good use of your Scroll Cards, you’ll be one step closer to victory.

The rules are unusual, but very simple. Be sure to try this fresh twist on the classic trick-taking game!

Welcome to Bandiville, a city plagued by a wave of trafficking orchestrated by a gang of fearsome bandits!

Alongside Tarentule Poirot, Corbolumbo, and their sidekicks, investigate, search the neighborhoods, and work together to stop them before their accomplices' truck completes its tour of the city.

Each turn, roll the dice to choose your actions: movement, espionage, or arrests. But be careful! A wrong deduction can cause panic and muddy the waters, making the hunt even more difficult.

Between mounting tension, memory, and risk management, Enquêtes à Bandiville plunges you into a real race against time where every decision counts.

Will you be cunning enough to capture the six bandits before they escape?

—description from the publisher

In Catalonia, building castells (human towers) is more than just tradition — it’s an act of courage, trust, and coordination. In this game, you and your teammates form a human tower team performing in front of a packed square, just like in a real festival. Without speaking, in real time, and with full cooperation, you must prove you’re a well-synchronized team capable of building the most impressive towers.

Race all-time-greats or the 2018 roster of World Cup skiers in the Giant Slalom.  The system includes how to build a course and how to rate modern day skiers.  You can run a single race, or an entire Alpine ski season.

Each skier is rated on their starting ability, overall control, and general speed.  Each stage o the course is rated by which attribute is of primary importance.  At each stage, the player sets the ski mode: cautious, normal or charge.  Then they roll the dice to determine the time spent in the segment.

As you guide the skiers down the slope, you decide whether they should try to speed up, or reassert control (to avoid a fall).  Each run takes just a couple of minutes to resolve.  You can run an entire day’s worth of a ski event in just an hour.

Battles of Napoleon is an epic wargame series that simulates the most famous battles of Napoleon on a Tactical level sharing the same basic rules.

The series portrays the most important aspects of battles of the Napoleonic era with easy to learn and remember rules, intuitive mechanics and procedures. Players may focus on taking decisions and playing rather than consulting rules.

The system focuses on the “cat-and-mouse game” of coordination and timing of the various formations of the different arms without too much crunchy chrome.

Infantry was the predominant and most important element in every European army of the period. It usually formed between 60 and 90 per cent of an army in battle, and was both the cheapest and the most versatile type of troops. The foot soldiers had to endure most of the fighting and casualties. Depending on their task, they were either deployed in line (fire power), column (maneuver and assault) or square formation (protection against enemy cavalry).

Cavalry were screening the own army and provided the shock element on a Napoleonic battlefield. Due to their shock power they fixed enemy infantry and forced them to take shelter in square formation. If infantry failed to form square they were overrun. If infantry was fixed in semi-static squares they were very vulnerable to artillery fire.
 Artillery, the queen of the battlefield, was on the one hand having devastating effects on square and closely packed formations, on the other hand artillery alone was extremely vulnerable when getting charged by enemy units.

Generals are tasked with taking advantage of favorable terrain like the famous Duke of Wellington who mastered the reverse slope tactic.

By a simple game mechanic, players will never know the exact strength and power of an enemy unit. Units have a number of strength points that will gradually decrease while engaged with enemy units.

An innovative chit-based Command system, where players randomly draw activation chits determines which army may activate a formation, leads to highly interactive game play. Reaction follows action. We bring the chit-based Command system to a new level: In this games series, players decide which formation is activated when a chit is drawn. A formation may be activated several times per turn. But by each new activation it performs less effective.

During a game turn the players are under constant pressure to decide which formation to activate. By efficient timing of activation players are in the “driver’s seat” to dictate the pace of a battle.

Formations are given orders at the beginning of a game turn and are therefore limited by the actions they can perform on the battlefield. The more “aggressive” orders formations will receive, the more likely is the army will collapse due to fatigue. On the other hand, an army is more likely to gain the initiative on the battlefield by “aggressive” orders.

The scale of the series is appr. 150 meters per hex, infantry units are battalions, artillery is batteries and cavalry is represented as regiments.

Aspern-Essling 1809, depicting the encounter on the northern river bank of the Danube and the first defeat of the French Emperor. After the battle he said "I lost the battle against the river". The battle behind the battle is portrayed in a unique way no publisher ever did before. Special rules like the Austrian "Kolonnen" formation, ammunition shortage, the defense of special structures, the possible arrival of more French reinforcements add distinctive flavor of the battle.

—description from the publisher

In front of each player are two rows of three hidden cards that you must get rid of as quickly as possible. The rules are very simple. At the beginning, however, you don't know which cards you have in your hand.

Each turn, you can reveal one card. If the value or suit of the card matches one of the discard piles, you can place it there and continue to reveal cards. If the card does not match, it is placed face down and it is the next player's turn. The player who gets rid of all 6 cards wins a cup worth 3, 4, or 5 points.

You then receive 6 new cards. When all the cups have been distributed, the game is over.

Merl'inn: The Hilarious Tavern Management Competition

Every year, the grand Merl'inn competition is held in Cumbria.

Merl'inn is a strategic tavern-style party game that blends deckbuilding, development, and strong player interaction. Achieve your objectives, and annoy your neighbors.
Imagine a guild with unique powers: Mages, C'orcsairs, Drunken dwarves, and many others who have more than one trick up their sleeve.

Gameplay:

     Play cards
     Develop your tavern
     Buy furniture and improve your starting deck
     Collect resources from your adventurers:
         Dwarves generate gold to buy tables and furniture
         Elves generate mana to buy event cards from the market
         Orcs generate malice to activate actions on your guild board
    
     Complete challenges and quests

This time I have two great scenarios for Stars & Lasers, the first scenario is a great multi player scenario, it has been written for three players, and with the possibly of a fourth player turning up!

This is a small ship action where all the sides are trying to order / control a neutral merchant ship.

The second scenario is a real huge game which needs large numbers of ships and fighters and even space stations on the table.

This scenario is a large-scale attack on a safe and secure base of operations, they were not expecting it, they were not ready, how many will survive?

It’s time to get out those huge fleets!!

Stars & Lasers “Bio-Ships” is a supplement which contains all the information needed to use the bio-ships in your games of Stars & Lasers.

In this supplement you will find all the background information on these bio-ships and all the rules for using these ships in your games.

The supplement also includes all their weapons, systems and special rules.

You will also find all their ship datasheets ready for you to print out and use.

In this single player puzzle solitaire game, you take the role of an aspiring figure skater on the World stage, trying to claim their place in history while avoiding a disastrous slip up. Can you take home the Gold Medal with a flawless performance? Or will your nerves get the best of you?

—description from the publisher

In Drillers, players explore an abandoned mine using their mechs, trying to find and excavate anything useful or valuable left behind by a forgotten mining corporation. Each level of the mine brings new opportunities and new challenges—as fuel runs low and decisions become tighter. By collecting cards, players improve the drilling capabilities of their unique mech, unlock new actions, and push their operation further underground.

Mined minerals can be sold for profit also by use of drones, allowing players to refine their strategy and invest in better tools. Every decision affects everyone, often creating new opportunities for players.

Drillers is a game about planning ahead, tough decision-making, and knowing just how far you can afford to drill. Will you be the first to extend the horizons and uncover what the depths hide?

—description from the publisher
language_dependency: 2: Some necessary text - easily memorized or small crib sheet

description:
In Rosalie, players are aspiring rose breeders competing to build the most magnificent rose garden and earn renown as the finest nursery grower in town.

Each player begins with a small plot of land and a dream. Over the course of the game, players plant roses, crossbreed new varieties, cultivate their soil, and adorn their gardens with fountains and windmills. New and exotic roses are temperamental — neglect their care, and they will wither. To help tend the garden, players can recruit caretaker robots, powering them with electricity to keep their precious roses alive and thriving.

Workers are at the heart of the operation. When their hands are free, players direct them to carry out essential tasks: planting, breeding, tilling, and decorating. Every decision shapes the garden, gradually transforming a modest patch of earth into a source of pride.

Money is always available — the town is generous with its loans, and players can borrow freely whenever funds run short. But beware: at the end of each year, interest quietly accumulates. Debt begets debt, and what once felt like a lifeline can become a chain that strangles your ambitions. Managing finances wisely is just as important as cultivating beautiful roses.

Balance your budget, breed striking new varieties, and build the most captivating rose garden to claim victory.

—description from the publisher

Wyrmhunt is a cooperative card game for 1-4 players. Each player takes on the role of a member of the legendary Wyrmhunt Guild. Guiding their hero through perilous adventures. The gameplay is inspired by classic JRPGs of the 90s such as Lufia, Breath of Fire and Final Fantasy. In tactical, turn-based battles, players must work together to prepare of decisive boss fights within roguelike standalone adventures.
Roll twice as many dice as there are players and place them on the monster cards.

Gameplay: 
Place your hero on a monster card and remove one die from it. Ad that die to your personal dice pool. Place as many matching dice as possible on your hero grid to gain actions points. For each covered bonus symbol, you receive one resource. Use action points to unlock additional resources. Use your resources to deal damage or block monster attacks. Explore the worldmap to find and win against the boss monster. Discover new items and quests. Work together to prepare for decisive boss fights within roguelike standalone adventures or combine multiple adventures to a huge campaign.

-description from developer

boardgame: El Coso: Invasor C – carta expansión
description: Promo card for El Coso, that adds a new Invader, that is inmune.

A online-focused CC-BY-SA game about conquest in a balkanized USA where territories are fought for via board game combat. Players spin a (weighted) wheel when invading each other's territories and the board game the wheel lands on is what they must play. To win, either eliminate all players, own over 80% of the map, or agree to end the game as-is.

—description from the designer

Your princess has gotten lost and trapped in the enemy's castle.Your pals are gathering—go and get her back! This is a two-player game, and the first one to reach the opponent's castle wins.

How To Play?
1.Move a piece.
2.Move another piece, or take an action.
3.Win if you arrive at the opponent's castle.

The game features 17 types of animal pieces and 6 types of terrain. Each play can use a different setup, offering a unique experience every time.
language_dependency: 2: Some necessary text - easily memorized or small crib sheet

description:
This isn't a game of knowledge. This is a game of courage.
A question is asked. Everyone secretly chooses their side.
But to win, you don't choose the right one…
You must choose the one that is different from everyone else.

HOW TO PLAY?
1) A card is revealed.
2) 1 or 2 is chosen.
3) They are revealed simultaneously.
The least chosen option earns you points equal to the number of people who chose the most popular option.
If you are in the majority, you lose.

Promotional card for the Frag board game.

Play this card when you're fragged. Your body explodes, doing 3d damage to all characters within 2 squares. Anyone this eliminates is scored as a frag for you. Discard after use.

Daikoro: Elemental Dice Duel is a strategic two-player dice-drafting card game where you combine elemental forces to summon powerful Guardian Beasts and battle your opponent.

GAME OVERVIEW

Each player drafts an army of five creatures and a set of three dice. Every round, you'll roll your chosen die alongside a shared Environment Die, attempting to match the elemental combinations your creatures require. Successfully match the elements, and you summon that creature to attack your opponent or heal your allies.

Victory comes through clever drafting, probability management, and tactical combat. Eliminate all five of your opponent's creatures to win.

CORE MECHANICS

- Dice Drafting: Choose 3 dice from a pool of 8, each with different elemental distributions
- Creature Drafting: Snake draft 5 creatures from a roster of 10 unique Guardian Beasts
- Element Matching: Roll two dice (Environment + Your Choice) to create elemental combinations
- Strategic Summoning: Match your roll to a creature's element requirements to summon it
- Tactical Combat: Creatures have unique abilities, resistances, and attack values

THE FOUR ELEMENTS

Fire (Aggressive), Water (Sustaining), Earth (Durable), Wind (High-Impact)

Each creature requires a specific pair of elements to summon - some need the same element twice (Pure types like Fire/Fire), while others need two different elements (Dual types like Fire/Water).

WHAT MAKES IT SPECIAL

Elemental Beasts: Dice Duel was designed as a father-son collaboration project. The game balances accessibility (ages 7+, 30-40 minute play time) with strategic depth through its interlocking drafting, probability, and combat systems.

Extensive playtesting and balance iteration have produced a roster where every creature is viable - strong players will win most games, but the dice ensure underdogs always have a chance.

PLAYER COUNT: 2
PLAY TIME: 30-40 minutes
RECOMMENDED AGE: 7+
COMPLEXITY: Medium-Light

A game about calculated risks, elemental combinations, and mythical battles.

The objective of the game is to manage your 6-player team to conquer your opponents, make the playoffs, and claim the championship.

You try to improve your team with trades and drafts, but to make the playoffs you must cope with injuries and win more than your share of football games.

Pigskin, a game where player observation reigns supreme. You’ll be engrossed in making choices that elevate your team to greatness. But that’s not all—keep a watchful eye on your opponents’ lineups too!

—description from the publisher

Use furry friends to swap stables!

7 new epic adventurer cards to enhance your epic quest.

Fast paced dice and card based game of Boxing.
Roll the dice and cross reference the game cards for the teams and seasons you are playing to see the outcome.

The world’s first (and therefore the best) 100% crowdsourced party game of this or that created by the people, for the people! Red or Yellow is packed with wildly unpredictable prompts written by everyday legends like you.

Each round, flip a card and secretly choose the red answer or the yellow answer to the prompt. Then guess how the rest of the table will answer. Score points for matching minds, standing out, or boldly calling a unanimous pick! Get ready for epic debates, absurd logic, and mind-blowing groupthink moments!

boardgame: Victory by Any Means: Carrier Wars
description:
THE WAR FOR THE SPINWARD EXPANSE HAS BEGUN!

In the aftermath of the Great Hyperspace Collapse, the alien civilizations of the Old Empire slowly began rebuilding their empires. In the pursuit of this territorial expansion, many of them dispatched scout fleets and colonial expeditions into the SPINWARD EXPANSE, an uninhabited region of space known for its relatively low number of habitable worlds. A coalition of aliens known as the OUTWORLDS CONFEDERATION came to dominate the Expanse after the events of Human/Graal War and the Human Civil War, establishing itself along the spinward edge of Graal space.

Unbenownst to the Confederation, the aggressive KENTARI CLANS had themselves begun expanding their empire in the Spinward Expanse, and soon these two empires were set on a collision course that would inevitably lead to war between the two upstart powers. Control of the Spinward Expanse now hangs in the balance, and it’s up to you to decide who will become the masters of this sector of space!

CARRIER WARS is an expansion for VICTORY BY ANY MEANS that introduces advanced carrier warfare options for use in your campaigns, including ultralight fighters, new fighter special abilities, flak cannons, carrier strikes, fighter aces, auxiliary carriers, and more. Five new alien empires (the Outworlds Confederation, Kentari Clans, Altairian Free State, Procyon Commonwealth, and Ross Association) are included for use in your campaigns, along with full campaign scenarios that will allow you to experience the fight for the Spinward Expanse first hand.

—description from the designer

The expansion of the most award-winning Spanish card game of 2025. More cards, more characters, more betrayal. Includes a cloth bag.

With two-color monkey cards to make it easier to pair monkeys, as they are worth either one color or the other. With a new bonus card worth -8 points. With a new special card called “Despiojar” that allows you to steal a card from an opponent's hand. And with new character cards featuring crocodiles that allow you to steal any card you want from the pile of points belonging to the player of your choice.

These cards are added to the cards in the “Monkey King” deck and are played in the same way.

It also has a 1v1 game mode in case you only have the expansion.

—description from the publisher (translated)

Explore Point Reyes National Seashore and discover the plants, animals, and landmarks that define and distinguish this gem of the Northern California coast. Arrange and rearrange the cards to tell a story, plan an adventure, or create your own panoramic landscapes.
Arrange the 24 illustrated cards in any order — every card edge lines up to continue the scene. Mix, match, and discover how volcanoes, coastal clouds, monarchs, whales, lupine, poppies, and cypress all flow into endless Point Reyes stories.
This myriorama is inspired by walks, tides, and fog banks along Point Reyes. It celebrates threatened habitats, native plants, and the wildlife that defines this place.
Use it as an art object, a storytelling deck, an educational prompt, or a gift for people who love the California coast.
This is a storytelling game— one that isn’t won, but is played.

Fú: Feast & Flavour is an expansion for Fú: Festive Fortunes.
It adds 5 new snack stall tiles that are fully interchangeable with the base game, 4 new types of snacks, the "Rich Uncle" module, and 2 "Mystery Shoppers".

Cat Paper Scissors is a rock paper scissors engine builder, where as you lose games of rock paper scissors you gain paw tokens that allow you to buy cards with special abilities.

These cards then give you bonuses for winning or losing with certain symbols, adding an additional layer of strategy to the normal game of rock paper scissors. Be careful not to lose with rock, lest your opponent gain a bonus for winning with paper!

Cards and winning matches then give you cats, with the first to 7 cats winning the game. An additional fish mechanic also encourages you to lose to gain fish, powering up your cards.

—description from the designer

Prisoners Dilemma is a social strategy game inspired by the classic dilemma of trust and betrayal.

Players must navigate the prison, avoid the guards, and reach their disarm switches in an attempt to escape.

But this is where the real decision begins.

When a player reaches their switch, they must choose:

Disarm the alarm… or

Trigger a silent alarm

If everyone disarms the alarm, everyone escapes and wins.
If one player triggers the silent alarm, only that player escapes.
If two or more players trigger the silent alarm, the alarm is raised — and no one escapes.

Each game takes around 15–20 minutes, making it easy to play multiple rounds and see how trust (or betrayal) evolves between players.

Can you rely on your fellow inmates to cooperate… or will you betray them for your own freedom?

—description from the designer

Get ready to assemble armies, build bases, explore maps for resources and challenges, and face enemy forces and powerful monsters in Monstros & Tesouros - Campos de Batalha!

Set in the M&T universe but featuring independent mechanics from its predecessors, Campos de Batalha elevates the adventures of Thaeron's heroes to large-scale conflicts. Instead of leading just three characters through the world, you will choose a hero from one of three factions to muster armies of diverse troops.

In Exploration (cooperative) and Hegemony (competitive) modes, heroes will explore the Valley of the Five Cities in search of keys to a powerful portal—all while gathering resources, upgrading their bases and troops, and facing foes! Ultimately, to win the match, you must not only survive but also defeat Maldoblarkus, the leader of the demonic armies from the Burning Planes!

But that’s not all: in Combat and Tactical Scenarios (competitive and cooperative), place your armies on the modular battlefield and face your enemy's forces action-by-action. Move them across the terrain, using attacks, spells, and abilities to defeat enemy troops or seize their base! You can also play pre-set scenarios against the guardian monsters of the keys, or engage in castle sieges and other fortress invasions!

—description from the designer

The party card game of nuclear thievery.

Someone at the table has the holographic Nuke Codes card. But who?

Use cards in your hand to find out who and steal them for yourself.

You can start cold wars, commit espionage, execute a steal, force a trade, throw nuclear waste, and more.

The codes will switch hands multiple times, both out in the open for all to see and secretly where only 1 or 2 players will know.

So can you deduce the code movements and be the single player with possession of the codes by the time the game abruptly ends?

V2 version with 3 new card types for extra chaos!

—description from the publisher

Winter Den is a free semi-cooperative asymmetric survival game for exactly five players. Five animals (Bear, Fox, Raccoon, Rabbit, and Mole) share an underground burrow and must prepare for winter hibernation over seven days (rounds).

Each animal has unique survival requirements (food, warmth, water, shelter, and energy), different action costs, a fixed turn order, and a Strength Rank that determines resource-claiming priority.

Each round, every player takes one action: forage food, forage warmth, build or break a barricade, relocate resources, or rest. All information is open. There are no dice, no cards, and no hidden elements. Stronger animals claim contested resources first and can physically relocate weaker animals to different rooms. Barricades seal off tunnels, controlling access to water sources and creating sheltered areas required for survival.

After seven rounds, winter arrives and each animal is checked against all five survival requirements. Animals that fail any single check perish. An optional scoring system rewards survival (+5 points) and penalises hoarding: excess resources in a shelter earn bonus points if all five animals survive, but become penalties if any animal perishes. The highest score wins, with energy as a tiebreaker.

The game includes a hand-drawn game board, five animal player cards with individual action references and energy trackers, and a token sheet. It is a free print-and-play release.

—description from the designer

Burnout is a semi-cooperative real-time game for 2–5 players played in exactly three minutes. Players are a team delivering a project under a deadline, competing for an individual promotion while cooperatively trying to achieve at least a C grade on the group project.

A single opaque bag contains Green tokens (worth 3 points), Yellow tokens (worth 1 point), and Red tokens (stress), with the composition scaled to player count. On each turn, players choose one action: Work (draw a token from the bag), Rest (remove stress tokens), Delegate (move a stress token to another player), Support (remove stress from another player), or Overtime (draw twice). Play is sequential and clockwise, with the real-time timer creating constant pressure.

Accumulating 3–4 Red tokens makes a player Stressed, causing them to discard any Green tokens they draw and preventing them from using Support. At 5+ Red tokens, a player enters Burnout, immediately losing 3 tokens from their Work Area.

When the timer expires or the bag empties, individual scores are tallied and combined into a group grade. The highest individual scorer earns the Promotion, but only if the group achieves a C grade or higher. If the group fails, nobody is promoted regardless of individual performance.

—description from the designer

Nov42-May43 7 turns 40+ miles per hex.  26 units - 12 Axis and 14 Allied. Including Tigers!
Conventional I Go U Go game mechanics with combat results table including my asymmetric Disruption resilience rules.
Afrika Korps and 8th Army arrive on Turn 4 - Feb 43 - but it starts off helter skelter with Blade and Hart Force and Axis units debarking in Bizerte or Tunis.
If the Allies don't take and hold in supply Bizerte and Tunis, the Axis win.

—description from the designer
language_dependency: 2: Some necessary text - easily memorized or small crib sheet
family: Players: Wargames with Rules Supporting Only Two Players

description:
Set sail to a chain of islands overflowing with treasures and shells! In Moana: Gifts from the Ocean, you’ll collect gifts from the shore and carefully place them on your tapestry to create perfect sets. On your turn, choose a beach, take two gifts, and decide exactly where they belong: lining up three matching gifts scores immediately and keeps your island thriving!

—description from the publisher

Pack your bags and join Minnie and Mickey on a globetrotting journey filled with unforgettable experiences! Discover breathtaking destinations, collect magical memories, and compete to see who had the most exciting world tour.

Will you discover new places to enrich the Wishlist… or visit at just the right moment to claim the best experiences before your rivals?

—description from the publisher

With Orders in Turmoil expansion, six new Orders enter the rite. It includes 18 new Stones and 9 Emblem tokens. It introduces six new Orders
and two optional modules, bringing new strategic depth to the game.

Compete with other influential families in the city to achieve the most honorable titles through the skillful use of assistants and resources at your disposal. The player who succeeds in doing this best becomes the new "Obermufti" of Marrakesh.

Marrakesh is played over three rounds, with each round consisting of four turns. On each turn, players simultaneously and secretly choose three colored cylinders from behind their screen. Then, in turn order, they place matching colored assistants on their player board (these will be the actions they will perform later), then all cylinders are placed in the cube tower. The cube tower randomizes which cylinders are available this round, with some getting stuck and some from previous rounds coming out.

In turn order, players select a color and take 1-2 of the cylinders that have passed through the tower (or those that were stuck from a previous round but have now emerged) and place them on their player boards. These cylinders will enhance future actions taken in the same color in future rounds. Then, in turn order players will activate the regions where they have placed an assistant.

Actions allow players to gain wealth, which can be traded for influence in the city. Performing various actions also earns bonuses and enhances actions even more as the game proceeds. The player who scores the most points by the end of the game wins.

This Big Box Edition contains all Modules from all 3 expansions. Camels & Nomads, Gems & Experts and the new expansion Walls & Guards!

—description from the publisher
family: Players: Games with expansions that add solo play
Versions & Editions: Big Box Versions of Individual Games

description:
1st CEO is a high-interaction economic strategy game where every player takes on the role of a corporate CEO. Your goal is to build the most successful company while strategically investing in your rivals.

In this game, you can perform the following actions:

A. Purchase New Branches: Spend capital to expand your empire. Each branch introduces unique scoring rules and generates consistent revenue.

B. Acquire Shares: Purchase stocks in other players' companies. When those players collect income, they must distribute dividends to all shareholders accordingly.

C. Collect Revenue: Generate income based on your headquarters' performance. This total is then split proportionally among everyone who holds your company's stock.

At the end of the game, every share holds a different value. Success depends on your ability to buy into the right companies at the right time. Because every player brings a unique management style, their decisions will deeply influence their company's final market capitalization.

boardgame: King of Tokyo: Duel – Tokyo Bay
description:
The King of Tokyo Duel line is expanding! New Kaijus are emerging from Tokyo Bay to join the ultimate fight!

This standalone game comes with 6 new monsters and their asymmetrical powers. You can also play with monsters from KOT Duel and promo monsters such as Baby Gigazaur. The perfect Mix and Smash!

King of Tokyo Duel: Tokyo Bay offers a whole new experience with a brand-new board mechanics while maintaining the tension of tug of war!
Discover amazing power cards that add alternative victory conditions. You can even take advantage of your useless dice now! So no more excuses, even the unluckiest players can hope to become the King of Tokyo.

—description from the publisher

Your brother is in danger! His pendant has been found in the heart of the ancient forest, where ancient spirits and shamans have lived in harmony for generations.

Will you dare to venture into the woods to search for him? Be careful: there are many secrets lurking in the shadows

Fragments is a new line of narrative games with innovative mechanic and material consisting of jigsaw puzzle pieces.

Narrative will be the guiding thread of your adventure but it is through the use and manipulation of jigsaw pieces that the adventure will unfold.

The game can be played solo or in small groups of 2 to 4 players for optimal gameplay.

Fungataï is a 4 chapter campaign offering an experience around 7 hours long.

Ready to piece the story together?

—description from the publisher

Right before you leave your settlement in the Solara desert, you notice your companion is nowhere to be found. How will you adapt to this inhospitable environment to reunite with your friend

Could it be beginning of a greater story?

Fragments is a new line of narrative games with innovative mechanic and material consisting of jigsaw puzzle pieces.

The narrative will be the guiding thread of your adventure but it is through the use and manipulation of jigsaw pieces that the adventure will truly unfold.

Fragments Solara is a short standalone adventure consisting of a single chapter. This is the introductory box to this new universe. It lasts around 90 minutes.

—description from the publisher

As the rival kingdoms of the Warring States vied for dominance on the battlefield, a different struggle unfolded across the vast trade routes linking East and West. The Silk Road carried caravans laden with silk, jade, and grain through deserts and mountains, creating new opportunities for wealth and influence. Yet these routes were perilous: nomadic raiders threatened the land convoys, while pirates struck at ports along the eastern coast, demanding tribute or blood. To prosper in this age, rulers had to balance war and commerce, building prestige not only with armies and walls, but through mastery of exchange, trade, and the control of caravans.
The Silk Road offered fortune to those daring enough to command it - though few could escape its dangers.

Tianxia: Silk Road expansion introduces a new scoring opportunity at the end of each round. This scoring depends on the number of exchange actions they do, the scoring condition shown on the Caravan tiles, and the outcome of the Battles. There is also another threat the players must consider - the Pirates attacking the east coast.

—description from the publisher

The Zoo has opened four new enclosures, welcoming more animals into the park. Under the cover of darkness, these curious beings joined their friends and escaped! Help them return to the Zoo safely, but be careful – some of those new arrivals have more complicated ways of moving!

This expansion doesn’t alter any of the base game rules; it adds four new animals to choose from.

—description from the publisher

Quest & Request is a 1-5 player dungeon crawler arena game set in the chaotic and satirical kingdom of Remolith. The tone is dark fantasy with strong ironic and sarcastic elements that shape both narrative and characters.

The game is played on two fixed boards: an Adventure Board for exploration and objectives, and a Battle Arena Board for tactical combat. Scenario setup changes through tile placement and mission structure.

One player may take the role of Ciarman, controlling monsters and traps in a one-vs-many format. Alternatively, the game includes an automated algorithm that manages enemy behavior, allowing full solo or cooperative play without a dungeon master. While solo play is supported, the design emphasizes interaction between players.

The Adventure Book includes multiple missions and campaigns for more than 20 hours of structured gameplay. Campaigns feature progression, persistent elements, and scaling difficulty. Due to variable setup, scenario choices, enemy behavior, and player decisions, each campaign remains replayable.

In Quest & Request, the Raiders are anti-heroes driven by greed and ambition, while Ciarman is the "good" wizard attempting to restore order to a distorted kingdom.

—description from the designer

Tora Tora Tora! is a two-player Strategic level board-wargame that presents a holistic and dynamic view of the Pacific Front of World War II. Rather than focusing on individual historical battles, the game captures the broader struggle for control across the theater, emphasizing tempo, positioning, and resource management.

Players take command of either Japan or the Allies, each operating under a different economic logic. Japan pays to expand across areas of the map, while the Allies pay per unit regardless of distance. This fundamental asymmetry shapes every decision, forcing each side to approach movement, expansion, and confrontation in a distinct way.

Actions require limited resources, and battles involve hidden commitments before resolution, creating tension even before dice are rolled. Overextending can leave forces undersupplied and vulnerable, so players must constantly balance immediate gains against long-term sustainability. The game rewards careful pressure, territorial control, and forcing the opponent into inefficient responses.

The game ends after a fixed number of rounds, and victory is determined by strategic control of key areas and overall position on the map. Winning is less about a single decisive clash and more about managing tempo, preserving strength, and gradually exhausting the opponent’s options.

—description from the designer
language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups
family: Players: Wargames with Rules Supporting Only Two Players

description:
Sovereign: Shōgun is a competitive strategy game set in feudal Japan, where rival daimyos struggle for political dominance, military strength, and imperial legitimacy. Each player leads an asymmetric clan with unique advantages, competing to shape the future of the realm while a young heir sits at the center of shifting power.

On their turn, players select a single action using a River-style action track. Actions grow stronger the longer they remain unchosen, creating constant tension between acting early at lower strength or waiting for greater impact while risking that another player claims the opportunity first.

Core actions include Harvest to generate income from developed lands, Move to reposition armies, Develop to expand infrastructure and territories, Mastery to recruit and strengthen military forces, and Diplomacy to gain favor with temples and increase influence. Timing, positioning, and reading opponents are just as important as raw military strength.

The game ends when a player reaches the required threshold on either the Points or Glory track, or when a Harvest-related condition triggers the endgame. Victory belongs to the clan that best balances economic growth, military power, and political influence.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: It's (Almost) as if You are Playing a Board Game
description:
It's (Almost) as if You are Playing a Board Game (IAAIYAPABG - "I-Yap-a-Bag") is a game about playing board games for 1 or more players.

There are two levels to the game - first the gameplay itself, where players take turn making actions that affect the game state, following instructions on cards and managing an abstract central board featuring maps and spaces for tokens. And secondly, through the performance of playing a board game for spectators both real and imagined. The game offers opportunity for players to control how their play appears to passersby, to make it appear more complex and thinky, or more light and humorous.

The winner of the game can either be individual, or collective based on the combinations of card plays and resource collection.

IAAIYAPABG has a limited run of hand made physical copies, but can be printed and played at home with suitable materials (board, cubes, dice and tokens), following a freely available guide.

—description from the designer

boardgame: Warhammer Underworlds: Nexus of Power Rivals Deck
description:
Nexus of Power is a ready-to-play Rivals deck for Warhammer Underworlds. Any warband can use this deck just open, shuffle and play!

Nexus of Power is a Take and Hold playstyle deck. With it, your fighters become even more dangerous as they jealously guard their hard-won gains!

Nexus of Power contains
33 cards: 1 Plot Card, 12 Objectives, 10 Ploys and 10 Upgrades.

These cards can be used by any warband as a complete Rivals deck or as part of a custom-built deck.

You'll need the Core Game, Warhammer Underworlds: Embergard, to use this deck.

The Ossiarch agent Thanatek seeks ‘truebone’ – osseous matter imbued with unusual purity. To aid his search, the Emissarian has assembled an efficient band of corpse-reapers. The sword-wielding assassins Tukhar and Sekhmor eviscerate promising targets, while the Mornial caste retainers known as Xar-Tamok and Medeb-Ahk gather truebone-rich remains and soul-stuff in the caskets that protrude from their skeletal torsos.

This multipart plastic kit builds Thanatek's Tithe – a complete warband for use in games of Warhammer Underworlds. It comes with fighter cards and warscroll cards for the warband.

This set contains 5 plastic miniatures:

     1x Thanatek
     1x Tukhar
     1x Sekhmor
     1x Xar-Tamok
     1x Medeb-Ahk


This set also includes:

     5x double-sided fighter cards
     7x warscroll cards (in English, French, German, Spanish, Italian, Chinese, and Japanese)

Disbiotik is a competitive card game about building and managing your own gut microbiota. The goal is to finish with the healthiest microbiota.

On your turn, you play one card from your hand to build your microbiota, attack an opponent, or use antibiotics to remove infections. After resolving the card’s effect, you draw a new card from the open market to replenish your hand. Food and positive habit cards help you add microorganisms and increase their abundance and diversity, while negative impact cards allow you to infect opponents or disrupt their microbiota.

Balance is fragile. Pathogens reduce your health and often require antibiotics to remove them, which can also eliminate beneficial microorganisms if used carelessly. Choosing when to grow, when to attack, and when to cure is key to success. Special impact cards introduce hand manipulation, defensive reactions, and sudden shifts in momentum.

Once the draw deck and market are exhausted, the game enters its final phase. After a final round, players score health points for abundance and diversity, subtract penalties from pathogens, and add bonus points. The player with the highest health score wins.

—description from the designer

Royal Ransom is a finely-tuned bidding game in which the objective is to earn the highest score by winning the most "Royal" cards. The game utilizes two separate decks constructed from a standard deck of cards. The 40-card Main Deck consists of Aces and numbers 2-10, while the 10-card Royal Deck acts as the auctioned cards and contains Jacks, Queens, Kings, and a single Joker. Players are dealt a hand of 7 to 9 cards from the Main Deck depending on the player count, with the remainder set aside as the Bank.

Gameplay proceeds in rounds composed of a Bid phase and a Resolve phase. A Royal card is revealed, and players take turns bidding to win it using the cards in their hands. Bidding cards are valued at face value for numbers, 15 points for Aces, or 15 points if two cards of the exact same value are played together as a pair. Crucially, all cards played to the table are "spent" and discarded, regardless of who actually wins the auction. The highest bidder takes the Royal card; notably, winning a Jack lets you randomly "Hijack" a card from an opponent's hand. After the winner is decided, losing players can replenish their hands by either discarding one card to draw two from the Bank, or by sacrificing a previously won Royal for a larger draw.

If all players pass on an auction, a "rolling jackpot" takes place, keeping the un-bid Royal on the table to be won alongside the next revealed Royal. The game ends immediately after the final Royal card is won. Players calculate their final score by adding the points of their collected Royals—which range from 20 for a Jack up to 80 for the Joker—along with the total value of any cards remaining in their hand. The player with the highest overall score wins the game.

—description from the designer

In Microcosm, you enter the hidden world of microbial life, harnessing diverse microbes to recycle essential chemicals within a shared ecosystem. By deploying unique microbe cards, you transform resources, gain adaptive mutations, and carve out your ecological niche. Careful planning allows your microbes to grow and multiply, doubling their resources over time—but if a biome exceeds its carrying capacity, the entire cycle can collapse.

Players compete and collaborate to drive the carbon, nitrogen, iron, and sulfur cycles across different biomes. Over 12 rounds, players play microbe cards into biome tiles, where each microbe must be able to survive the biome’s environmental properties and access the necessary chemical resources to perform a metabolic reaction. When played, microbes convert chemical compounds (resources) from one form to another, beginning or extending a biochemical cycle with the goal of returning the chemical back to the form in the environment (for example, CO₂ → C → CO₂). Often playing cards relies on resources produced by other players’ microbes, creating a web of interdependence where timing and positioning are crucial.

Each turn, players take one primary action: play a microbe to metabolize available resources or discard a card for an alternate effect. Optional secondary actions—such as claiming objectives or using quorum sensing to gain an additional action—add strategic depth. At the end of each round, doubling time advances; when a microbe’s timer reaches zero, its resources replicate exponentially, accelerating both growth and the risk of ecological collapse, which will reduce scoring.

A cycle is completed when a series of microbial reactions converts a chemical back to its original form in the biome, rewarding all contributing players with energy points. After scoring, microbes may evolve by gaining mutations from biome or be archived for end-game points. With biomes shifting over time and competition intensifying, victory goes to the player who builds the most efficient, resilient, and adaptable metabolic engine (i.e. most energy/victory points).

—description from the designer

Whoever wants to reach the other side must get past the crocodile!

Somewhere in the African jungle live 5 monkeys and 4 crocodiles. These crocodiles are old and tired. They swim sleepily and sluggishly in the river that the monkeys must cross to reach “Fruit Island.” Recently, the red monkey managed to make it across, by stepping over the stones in the river and across the backs of the crocodiles, to reach Fruit Island. But now that he is sitting on the island surrounded by all those delicious fruits, that naughty monkey is trying, with the help of the crocodiles, to stop the four other little monkeys.

The four other monkeys are so hungry for the tasty fruit on the island that, despite the danger posed by the crocodiles, they are also trying to get to the other side.

Who will succeed in reaching Fruit Island and throwing the naughty red monkey into the water?

The magnets ensure that either the crocodile has its mouth open (it is asleep) or its mouth closed (it is awake, dangerous, and snapping at the monkey).

boardgame: Contrário: O jogo de Cartas dos Bois de Parintins
description:
Immerse yourself in the vibrant energy and centuries-old rivalry of the Amazon's largest folklore festival! In Contrário, players take on the enormous responsibility of commanding the performances at the Bumbódromo, defending their colors with strategy, cunning, and passion. Choose your side in this epic contest: the strength of Boi Caprichoso, black and sporting its imposing blue star on its forehead, or the tenacity of Boi Garantido, entirely white and guided by its vibrant red heart on its forehead.

The goal of the game is to orchestrate a flawless spectacle, managing to accumulate exactly 21 items in the arena during your performance. Through a fast and engaging mechanic, players simultaneously choose and reveal cards from their hands. These cards represent the elements of the festival, from numerical values ​​to build the score to powerful special items — such as The Shaman, The Lady of the Pavilion, or the Warrior Cunhã — that alter the rules in your favor.

However, the true essence of Contrário lies in the provocation and interaction with your opponent. The opposing fans are always on the lookout, ready to ruin your show. Players can use the back of their cards to point out "flaws" in their opponent's presentation, such as a disjointed choreography, fireworks that failed, or a float that doesn't move. These flaws are added to the opponent's list, adding unexpected points with the aim of making them exceed the limit of 21 items, blowing their score and guaranteeing victory for their side of the stands.

With dynamic matches of approximately 25 minutes and 85 cards full of tactics, Contrary is a fast-paced, immersive, and highly interactive game. Gather your team, cleverly manage your hand of cards, and get ready to shout louder in the arena!

—description from the publisher

Two new boxes of Newfoundlandia questions: One for Adults - One for children.

Newfoundlandia II Contains two additional boxes of questions that can be used as -

A TRAVEL GAME - Newfoundlandia II requires no board or playing pieces. It has its own rules and can be played without the original game. It is designed for use in your vehicle or other areas where setting up a board game would be difficult.

OR

SUPPLEMENTARY QUESTIONS - These new questions can be added to the original Newfoundlandia game. Newfoundlandia II can be stored neatly inside the original game. With the addition of the children's box Newfoundlandia now becomes a game for the whole family to enjoy.

Newfoundlandia II contains one box of questions for adults, one box of questions for children (ages 7-12), a travel scorepad and instructions for use as a travel game.

Rack-O Jr. is the first-ever junior version of the family-favorite game Rack-O! The goal is to be the first player to fill your rack with cards, from lowest to highest. Number cards are color-coded to match the colors of the rainbow (and colors on the racks). There are three levels of play and special cards, like the steal, discard and rainbow wilds, add to the fun.
family: Versions & Editions: Junior Versions of Grown-Up Games

description:
In Time Crimes, you are Detectives tasked with investigating and building a case against a serial murderer. Within the department, there is a new promotion coming up that you're competing for. At your disposal to aid you in this task is the new Time Stream technology, that will allow you to play with time and manipulate the evidence in your favor. Only time will tell who can build the most compelling case and win the coveted promotion.

Time Crimes is a competitive, must follow trick taking game, in which the tricks are the collection and manipulation of evidence from the crimes. Each of the four crimes represent a suit, with the most recent crime being the trump suit. It plays three players as an individuals or two teams of two.

After playing a card into the trick, players will each have the opportunity to either lock in their selected card, or take a Time Stream token and push the current card into the future, replacing it with a card from their hand. When the trick is finished, the winner of the trick will claim an evidence token and one of the cards from the trick other than their own.

On the next trick, any cards held in the Time Stream must be played on that player's turn (breaking the must follow rule if relevant.

The hand ends when one player plays their last card. At the end of the hand, the player or team who best balanced their gathered evidence tokens with their use of the Time Stream technology will score the most points.

—description from the publisher

You are attending a family style potluck dinner. Everyone will bring a dish to dinner, selected by your family members. Work to make an amazing meal and bring the perfect amount of food for the exact number of guests, without having any leftovers. So pass the potatoes and get preparing.

Family Style is a must follow trick taking game in which players will set their bid - estimated number of guests to feed - and leftover suit - personal trump - by passing cards around the table. Then take or avoid tricks to feed the guests you bid on, without having any leftovers. Score the most points to win the game.

At the beginning of each hand, players will pass three cards and get one of their original cards back to them as their Leftover card. The number on the left over card is their bid, while the suit is their personal trump suit (ex. If the lead player has a Dessert card in front of them, then any Dessert cards would trump the lead suit).

At the end of the hand, players score points for each trick they took, up to their bid, but no more. Extra points are awarded to players that make exactly enough food to feed everyone they planned on (their bid).

At the end of the game, the player with the most points wins.

—description from the publisher

Build patterning skills with this deliciously fun, cooperative game for early learners. Children will work together to complete the plate pattern and feed the hungry bunnies. Hurry up to solve the sequence before the mischievous mouse eats the food!

—description from the publisher

For dinosaur and pizza-loving kids–Pizzasaurus is the slap-tastic topping matching game. This original game for 2-4 players, ages 4+, includes 33 cards, a wooden die, and 3 shaped pizzasaurus tiles. To play, players roll the die and try to match the pizza toppings to collect cards. If a player flips a card that matches a pizzasaurus tile, all players race to be the first to slap the matching tile.

This fast-paced and humorous game also has added educational value highlighted on the back of the box, including matching skills, concentration, reaction time, and hand-eye coordination.

—description from the publisher

The world of Aurigae grows once again with an exciting new expansion: Les Montforts.

This expansion introduces 10 brand-new cards, adding fresh tactical options and unexpected twists to your chariot races. Whether you favor bold risk-taking or calculated strategy, these new cards will deepen your decisions and intensify every lap.

At the heart of this expansion is a unique, fully personalized chariot, designed exclusively for Les Montforts. This beautifully crafted chariot brings both visual distinction and strategic identity to the arena, allowing players to stand out on the track.

To enhance gameplay clarity and immersion, the expansion also includes a lap progression token, making it easier than ever to track race advancement throughout the game. No more confusion — every turn and every lap is clearly marked as the tension builds toward the finish line.

Les Montforts expands the legend of Aurigae, offering new depth, new strategies, and a renewed racing experience in the grandeur of ancient Rome.

Prepare your horses. The arena awaits.

boardgame: Green Team Wins: Movies and TV Promo Pack
description: Collection of 30 cards for use in the game. There are 10 different new cards in each of the three categories, all unique to this particular promo pack. All of the cards are related to a mix of movies and television.

In the bustling streets of ancient Jerusalem, two groups of devoted followers strive to guide Jesus as He spreads His teachings and healing miracles. Each group seeks to inspire new communities of faith, carefully leading Him through unvisited regions to bring hope and light. Returning to places He has already preached is seen as lost time and dampens the group’s spirit. The group that connects the most thriving communities first will achieve lasting glory, carrying Jesus’s message across the land.

Jesus is a strategic board game set in ancient Jerusalem, where 1-6 players guide the Jesus figure across a modular map to connect unvisited regions with team-colored markers, forming networks of faith. In competitive mode (2-6 players), black and white teams secretly plan moves via direction cards or a compass, navigating wind-prohibited directions, rocks, and morale penalties for failures like revisiting areas. Expansions include 12 disciple abilities, weather cards for special effects, and dummy/NPC rules for odd player counts. Cooperative mode pits humans against NPCs in story (15 sequential missions) or mission (5 random) campaigns. Victory comes from linking 4+ adjacent regions for points, with morale management key.

Standard Competitive Game
In this 2-6 player mode, split into black/white teams (alternating seats), assemble the Jerusalem board, place Jesus centrally, set morale (12-16), and position rocks per setup. Rounds cycle through: Wind Phase (prohibit one of 8 directions), Planning Phase (secretly select N/S/E/W/NW/NE/SW/SE moves, 1-2 steps via cards/compass), Action Phase (reveal clockwise; successes claim unvisited regions with markers, failures deduct morale for wind/edge/rock/revisits). Pass compass/wind token clockwise. Score 1 VP per 4+ orthogonally linked regions; first to 2 VP or foe's morale to 0 wins. Add disciples (unique abilities), weather (special rules), or dummy/NPC for balance. No private team talk.

Cooperative Game
For 1-3 humans (white) vs. black NPCs, add 1-3 NPCs to simulate full teams. No wind phase. Humans plan secretly (no comms); NPCs draw action cards (speed/level-based), redrawing on morale loss up to level. Resolve moves per standard rules. Story Mode: Clear 15 sequential missions (set morale/weather per card), win by  required VP or foe morale 0; fail/repeat on loss. Mission Mode: 5 random missions (level=mission #, escalating weather/NPC strength, human morale 6/NPC 12); win all for victory, fail any restarts from #1. Disciples chosen per mission; aim for 3 VP.

In his final manuscript, William Shakespeare recorded a secret list of extraordinary performers chosen for the greatest theatrical masterpiece in history. Now, he challenges his devoted students to uncover their identities. As time runs short, William will gradually reveal cryptic clues and hidden criteria, while the students must work together to deduce the three chosen characters before the final curtain falls.

Shakespeare is a deduction game where players uncover three hidden characters chosen by William Shakespeare for his legendary play. Each of 24 character cards features a male or female figure holding a book, knife, or goblet, dressed in red, yellow, blue, or green. Using 116 hint cards with conditions on these traits, players deduce secrets via cryptic clues. Modes include Standard Cooperative (2-9 players, team vs. time), Competitive (2-5, race to deduce opponent's secrets), Solo (optimize turns for grades A-E), plus Campaign (50 levels) and Mission (5 combo challenges). Components: books, scrolls, tokens, deduction boards. Perfect for logic lovers.

Standard Cooperative Game
In Standard Cooperative (2-9 players), one silent Master holds the book with three secret character numbers (1-8). Students get hint cards: plan silently (play/discard to fill Planning Zone with 3), discuss to pick 2 for evaluation. Master places one in GOOD REGION and one in BAD REGION, discard corresponding time token to answer the card in GOOD REGION. Build deductions over rounds using regions, boards, and tokens. Guess all three correctly to win together; wrong guess loses for all. When TIME run out, player have to guess. Add one of 50 level-cards (A-D types) for difficulty. Campaign: sequential levels; Mission: paired cards.

Standard Competitive Game
Standard Competitive (2-5 players) pits players against each other to fastest deduce the clockwise target's three secret numbers (1-8, overlapping neighbors). Setup: Each gets a book with three face-down tokens (one shared left/right). Rounds: Simultaneously add 3 time tokens to reserve, ask a question (draw 3 hints, play one affordable via red cost to target; they answer true/false, pay green/red), or guess (correct wins; wrong eliminates you).
