Undefeated answer for others. 2-player variant: each picks own secrets, one refuse chance. Use deduction boards/tokens to track traits. Adjust difficulty: 6-16 characters, vary starting time.

Solo Game
Setup mirrors cooperative with 8 scrolls/characters (1-8); shuffle 8 hints face-up in a row. Each turn: Select 3 consecutive hints. For each, identify exactly 3 matching characters (true to condition), place 1 time token on each. Consolidate multiples to 1 per character, move all to overlying scrolls. Remove 1 from lowest scroll; add 1 to TurnCount (left of row). Discard/replace all hints. Repeat until any scroll hits 3+ tokens. Score TurnCount: 0-4=E, 5=D, 6=C, 7=B, 8=A. Optimize efficient deductions to beat your grade; pure logic vs. self.

Say What Now?! is a fast-paced word party game where pronunciation beats spelling.

Each round, three vowel cards and one round objective are revealed. Players use the consonant cards in their hand along with the shared vowels to create a word that sounds real and matches the round goal. Before revealing their word, players announce their total score based on the card values used.

Players then reveal and say their word aloud - creative pronunciation is encouraged. If another player doubts it, they can shout “Say What Now?!” and the group decides whether the word is valid. If accepted, the score stands. If rejected, the player scores nothing for the round. Points are based on the value of the letter cards used, and the highest valid score that matches the goal wins the round card.

The first player to collect the required number of round cards wins. Optional Chaos cards allow players to disrupt each other, adding unpredictable twists to every round. No phones. No dictionaries. Just creativity, confidence, and chaos.

—description from the designer

The Story of Sir Diamant is the official solo mode of Tournament: Classic Playing Card Game. It uses the same core system, now played against AI opponents. All you need to play is a standard deck of playing cards and the printed game sheet.

Relive the rise of Sir Diamant (dee-ah-MAHN) as he climbed the tournament circuit. Across six chapters, you will replay the final tournaments that forged his legend. In each chapter, match Sir Diamant’s exact number of Event wins to faithfully recreate his performance.

Each time your result differs from his recorded victories, you discard 1 Plot Armor for each win you are off. If you cannot discard the required Plot Armor, the story ends in defeat. Use Plot Holes wisely to keep the story on track.

Successfully replay all six chapters of Sir Diamant’s journey to win the game.

—description from the designer

Finding reliable help isn’t always easy, even for a wizened wizard like yourself. In Familiar Spirits, you put your trust in your faithful animal companions; send them out to gather everything you'll need to build up a truly impressive potion pantry. But be careful... familiars have particular preferences about how they like the shelves organized, and it’s up to you to put away your potions in an arrangement that will keep them happy.

Your familiars come in three different sizes. Each turn, send one to an unoccupied location or bump out a smaller rival familiar from a location to activate it. In the wilderness they’ll forage the finest herbs, fungus, and crystals. At the alchemist they'll distill those ingredients into batches of arcane potions with unique powers. And in the village they’ll patronize various vendors offering helpful benefits, like acquiring rare oddities or hiring imps to help with the work. When the timing is right, spend a turn to rest and bring your team of familiars home.

Once your cabinets are filled with shelves of enchanted potions, the game ends. Will your display please your finicky familiars best, or will another wizard prove to be more adept at magical shelf-improvement? The wizard who has put together the most pleasing display of potions will win!

Familiar Spirits is the spellbinding new small-box game blending worker placement and tableau building, from the team behind Endeavor: Deep Sea and How to Save a World.

Tamashii: The Final Amendment is the sequel to Tamashii: Chronicle of Ascend. It is a standalone game that reimplements the same mechanisms and some core gameplay systems as the first game while adding new ones.

We thought we’d won. We thought the ultimate sacrifice was enough. But the rogue AI never died – it was only put to sleep for a while… Wake up, Tamashii! The fate of humanity lies in your hands again. It’s time to make the Final Amendment – and this time, make it right.

Join the fight against the rogue AI once more in Tamashii: The Final Amendment – the second installment of the adventure board game that immerses you in a cyberpunk dystopia unlike any other. Bring cyberpunk coding right to your table with Tamashii’s unique programming mechanic: arrange data into patterns and execute code to bring down your enemies or augment yourself. Jump between body shells to change the course of the game. Enter the adventure on your own terms with highly replayable semi-campaign scenarios that let you reshape the story and unlock new content as you go.

With deep narrative elements and innovative gameplay, Tamashii: The Final Amendment delivers an unforgettable cyberpunk experience where every move matters.

Some miniatures / body types of the characters can be used in Tamashii: Chronicle of Ascend at the same time.

-description from publisher

Two thousand years ago, the Roman Empire ruled the Mediterranean. Mighty Roman dynasties thrived through trade and architectural marvels. Now it’s your turn! Build your trade network, appease the ancient gods, and guide your people to prosperity.

Concordia is a highly acclaimed, peaceful strategy game of economic development. Rely on clever hand management and long‑term planning. Send out colonists, acquire new cards, and expand your trade empire to win.

Concordia: Special Edition revitalizes Mac Gerdts’s masterpiece, introducing a new, immersive, and historically grounded visual design with improved map readability. It also features stunning premium components and adds new content – a fresh map and two gameplay modules – to expand the Concordia experience.

The Special Edition will not be backwards compatible with previous Concordia content.
language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups

description:
Heroes, back off! Now calling all swindlers, commoners, outlaws, underworlders, ne'er-do-wells, desperadoes, ruffians, embezzlers, looters, and scallywags. Scoundrels, for short. We won’t make an acronym out of it. The plan is simple: we get in, we get the job done, we get out. Take everything that’s shiny, leave no witnesses, and have some bloody fun while you’re at it!

Scoundrels is a fully co‑operative, stealth‑based dungeon crawler set in the world of Evershade. Pick your favourites from a roster of asymmetrical, larger‑than‑life misfits. Combine tactical movement, inventory management, and push‑your‑luck mechanics. Run randomly generated fantasy heists or dive into a narrative‑driven campaign about the greatest heist of the millennium. Go stealthy or go brutal. Or do it all! I mean, who’s going to stop you, Scoundrel?

—description from the publisher

Humanity is basically gone. Earth is gone. Records are gone. No one here really knows what happened. We try to make sense from scattered files, records, and charts, but to no avail. The Intruders have won.
A group of us hides in a remote outpost. We desperately try to work things out, but it seems that the Intruder invasion will reach us soon. Scientists promise results and are relentlessly working on something, but they've been saying that for a long time. One more day is not going to make a difference. Well, we are going to die. 
But, maybe, just maybe, we can drag them to hell with us.

Nemesis: Legacy is a new installment of the Nemesis horror board game series. It is a standalone campaign game with legacy elements that tells the story of the last human survivors standing against the impossible odds of an Intruder terror. 
In a series of interconnected scenarios, players will face irreversible choices that will decide the fate of humanity. Will you take a risk and trust strangers in the moment of greatest need, or will your paranoia prevent you from cooperating? After all, no two humans share the same hopes and dreams.

The game is full of narrative twists, personal agendas, and character customization. The core gameplay rules are based on the bestselling Nemesis: Retaliation game by Adam Kwapiński. The campaign and legacy design is made by Rob Daviau and Jaime Barriga. This unique combination of talents results in the first legacy sci-fi survival horror game on the market.

Your Nemesis: Legacy experience won’t end after you finish the campaign. You will be able to restart the game with an included recharge pack, to let you replay the story in a different way. What’s more, you will be able to use Nemesis: Legacy components in Nemesis: Retaliation games: new playable characters, new enemy race, new missions, and boards will bring almost infinite new combinations to your Nemesis experience.

Chase cunning animals, recover stolen loot and outsmart your rivals in this fast-paced, twist-filled board game for 1-5 players.

Bag & Snatch! Ocean Turmoil is the second stand alone expansion for the Bag & Snatch system.

Bag & Snatch! is a fast-paced, strategic card game designed by David Spada and Nestore Mangone, lasting about 15 minutes, ideal for quick and fun games!

Players use cards to steal tokens from the table, divided into four groups of animals, each with abilities that can change the game, disrupt opponents and create new strategies.

Strategy is key! Points are awarded each turn based on the tokens collected: more tokens, more points! But if an animal's supply runs out, the others must return the corresponding tokens, losing points.

This pack contains the shark, turtle, octopus and crab, each with unique effects.

This game is compatible with other games in the Bag & Snatch system!

Love Me As a Worm is a fast-paced dice and card game for 3–6 players in which rival worms compete to attract all 10 Hearts from a single opponent and make them fall in love.

On your turn, you may play one card, then roll the Number Die and Heart Die together. The Number Die (1–3) determines how many Hearts are affected, while the Heart Die determines the action: Attract, Repel, Attract/Repel, or Skip. Choose one player and resolve the effect which attracts Hearts from them, or repels stolen Hearts back to their original owner. Players may reroll once, and Block cards can interrupt actions before they resolve, keeping everyone engaged even when it’s not their turn.

The game ends immediately when a player successfully attracts all 10 Hearts of one color from the same opponent during their turn. Hearts taken from multiple players don’t count — it must be a clean sweep to win their love.

The goal of the game is to find pairs of matching unicorn cards and discard them. Players take turns drawing cards from the draw pile or from a fellow player's hand. However, unlike the original game where you want to avoid the "Old Maid" card, in Einhorn gesucht!, the goal is to be the one holding the Unicorn Queen at the end of the game.

Positive Twist: There is no "loser" who gets stuck with a bad card; instead, there is a happy winner who finds the Queen.

A quick, engaging card game for 3 to 6 players that teaches memory skills and social interaction.

Step into the world of mythical beasts as a daring cryptozoologist! Collect rare creature parts, trade magical eggs, and craft legendary beings before your rivals do.

On your turn, take up to three actions: gather creature parts or eggs, place cards to build creatures, play potions to boost yourself or sabotage others, or trade eggs for powerful resources. Use a strategy to assemble creatures of matching rarity and region for maximum points.

The game ends when the first player completes four full creatures. Score points for your completed creatures, apply bonuses for rarity and region matches, and count leftover eggs.

The player with the highest total points wins, with ties broken by eggs and unused potions.

Fast-paced, strategic, and full of clever rivalry—will you become the ultimate cryptozoologist?

boardgame: Galactic Cruise: Split Interests Promo Pack
description:
This set of 3 Blueprint Tiles and 3 Cruise Tiles feature split icons that can be considered either of the types pictured.

It will also include a set of 3 Blueprints for the Advancements and Accommodations Expansion.

Race the clock as a team of processors in a digital device! In this real-time puzzle game, you have 10 minutes to complete as many tasks as possible. Your goal is to satisfy Task cards by calculating specific two-digit numbers on local and shared Storage Counters. You’ll calculate values using a hand of Action cards: Statements let you perform math operations to reach your target numbers whereas Accesses copy numbers between your local storage counters and the shared storage counters. Once you get the hang of things, add Danger Actions to the deck for a greater challenge! This included bonus pack introduces Bugs with inconvenient effects and Outages that trigger immediately to disrupt the entire team.

There are no turns in Kernel Panic. Everyone plays simultaneously, drawing from a shared action deck and computing as quickly as they can. Collaboration is all but mandatory: Many tasks are too complex for one processor to solve alone, requiring you to compute useful numbers for teammates to use or work together on shared requirements. Just be careful—most tasks have conflicting conditions, so helping one teammate with their Task might accidentally clobber another’s hard work.

The game ends the moment the 10-minute timer expires. Your team’s final score is based on the complexity of your completed Tasks and the number of Danger Actions successfully navigated. Every game is a race to optimize your performance and set a new high score!

— Description from the designer
language_dependency: 2: Some necessary text - easily memorized or small crib sheet

description:
Band together with a few trusted citizens to build the town of New Salem. You will be a Witch or a Puritan and certain Building types will score points for you. If there is too much Despair in the town by the end of the game, the Witch with the most points will win, otherwise the Puritan with the most points will win.

Buildings may score points but also may add Despair to the town. You will vote on which Buildings will be built using your Power and even Puritans will be forced to play Buildings with Despair to selfishly earn points for themselves. The way everyone votes will give you clues as to which team they are on so you will want to watch carefully.

When you are Constable, you will be able to trigger powerful Events that will gain information for you, aid your teammates, accuse the opposing team, or generally turn the tide in your favor.

It plays 3-8 players in about 30 minutes.

—description from the publisher

Toki & Bloki is a family card game for 2-5 players that plays in 10-20 minutes. Players collect small light sparks called Tokis and protect them from disruption while managing their hand of cards.

Each turn, a player draws a card and plays one card from their hand. Tokis are worth 1, 2, or 3 points and are placed in front of the player. However, they are vulnerable to Haki, a disruptive card that can remove unprotected Tokis from opponents.

Players can defend their Tokis by playing Bloki, which protects them from Haki. Gasi cards allow additional actions, creating timing decisions and small tactical combinations. Collected Tokis are secured in Walleta, where they remain safe until the end of the game.

The game ends when the draw pile is depleted. The player with the highest total value of secured Tokis wins.

Toki & Bloki blends light hand management, set collection, and interactive “take that” elements into a fast, family-friendly experience. With simple rules and meaningful choices, it creates moments of tension, protection, and joyful competition for both children and adults.

A solo survival sci-fi horror tabletop RPG about wandering an endless world, searching ruins and trying to stay alive long enough to find meaning.

Play as a solitary explorer in a complete, self-contained game of dice-driven hexcrawling, resource management, combat, and exploration through impossible architecture, radiation-scarred zones, and organic, machine-infested ruins, searching for relics from long-forgotten civilizations.

Inspired by Blame!, Death Stranding, Berserk, Stalker, Annihilation, and Mothership RPG.

—description from the publisher

Steamlands: The Rift Wars is a miniatures agnostic skirmish game built for Versus, Solo, Co-Op and Campaign play.

What Makes Steamlands Different?

Miniatures Agnostic
Play with the miniatures that you want or already have.

Alternating Activations
Stay engaged every moment. Players take turns activating one unit at a time, creating constant tactical tension.

Shared Momentum System (Ethos)
Push your units beyond their limits, but every Ethos spent powers your opponents.

Rift Storms
Beginning in Round 2, reality destabilizes. Random Rift Storm effects reshape the battlefield each round, forcing players to adapt on the fly.

Dynamic Objectives
Secure Supply Crates, extract recon data, hold the line, or break through enemy territory. Multiple scenario types keep battles fresh and unpredictable.

Campaign Play
Grow your Squad over multiple battles. Units gain experience, suffer lasting injuries, and develop into hardened veterans.

The Complete Core rules contains 10 playable scenarios, Deployment maps, Rift Storm system, Campaign rules, Solo and Co-Op guidelines, 3 Unique factions with your choice of multiple faction abilities.

Control to Catastrophe: Maximilian is a miniature wargaming rule set that puts you in command of the armies of the Renaissance. 
The rules cover everything you need to know about getting ready for play, the core rules covering all aspects of moving and combat, the effects of terrain, special rules that add histoical flavour to your armies, sample army lists and scenarios, and optional rules. CtC is not meant for tournament play but is written to be unambiguous and complete.

Here are some of the game’s features:

     Gridded playing surface – move your units quickly and precisely without the fuss of rulers and specialized maneuver tools.
     Regardless of the size of table or number of figures, fight battles not just skirmishes.
     Customizable scaling – choose the grid-size that you want and can accommodate using unit sizes that suit your budget and aesthetic preferences.
     Figure scale and basing conventions don’t matter. Fill the grid-box with models that you have and that evoke how you imagine the troops you are representing.
     The core rules are simple and open the way for fast play using D6s.
     Special rules for special units add character and tactical nuance to your armies.
     Sample Army Lists and 3 scenarios.
     Free Army Lists available on the Decisive Wargame Design website.
family: History: War of the League of Cambrai

boardgame: Battle Cry: Jackson Campaign Special Supplement
description: A 4-page special supplement issued originally at Origins 2000 and Gen Con 2000, focusing on the American Civil War era.

publishers: パニーニゲームズ（Paninee Games）

description:
The official BVB fan edition of STADT LAND VOLLPFOSTEN takes you to new heights! Find words that match the letter rolled on the dice and climb the levels. The first to reach the top wins!

—description from the publisher

Bei der offiziellen BVB-Fan-Edition von STADT LAND VOLLPFOSTEN geht’s hoch hinaus! Findet passende Begriffe zum gewürfelten Buchstaben und erklimmt so die Levels. Wer zuerst oben ist, gewinnt!

—description from the publisher (German)

Enter the world of Exandria, a land of infinite wonder and unexpected champions, and experience the Critical Role adventures through Codenames. Assemble a party of heroes from the first three Critical Role campaigns, embark on missions, and level up your characters to defeat the final boss.

Codenames: Critical Role Adventures is a unique take on Codenames inspired by the Critical Role universe. It’s a fully cooperative version of the game where one GM (game master) leads their party through perilous missions, each with its own twist and take on the classic Codenames rules and featuring a new set of themed words. Play the game in three separate campaigns, choose from seven Critical Role characters, and, by levelling them up, unlock new abilities and equip items to use during missions.

Space is a busy place! The hustle and bustle of the Omicron galaxy shows that the population is growing at an exponential pace. Commerce has exploded and the demand for mined materials is at an all time high. There is money to be made and you are the one to make it. If you don’t, your competitor most certainly will. Players race around the  galaxy collecting mined materials and delivering them to different corporations who are willing to pay top dollar.

Easy enough, right? Maybe! There is only way to out hustle the competitor, overload your truck and break the speed limit, which draws the ire of the Galaxy Guard. If they catch up to a player ship, they will unload your goods by half. There is the potential of a bribe contribution to the Galaxy Guard‘s favorite charity .... themselves.
 With each contract a trucker fulfills, you can earn straight money, or abilities (with a little money left over). As players gain abilities, their ships can do more per turn. Who will be the one who makes the  most money for the day.

Space Truckin’ is a two-player, pick up and deliver game where players will go around the picking  up and delivering goods as hot shot haulers. The players goal is to make as much money as they can. But competition is tough and the supply is ever changing. During the game your ship can only hold enough for two orders. As orders get fulfilled, a players bank account gets bigger and they can upgrade their ship for the remainder of the game.

Whoever reaches 20 coins at the end of the day, wins the game.

—description from the designer

boardgame: Codenames: Critical Role Adventures – Vox Machina Expansion Pack
description:
Add even more Critical Role to your Codenames and explore the adventures of Vox Machina!

Codenames: Critical Role Adventures – Vox Machina Expansion Pack features a set of 42 words themed after the first iconic Critical Role campaign, as well as 2 new items, and a new Vox Machina character that you can use in your Exandria Adventures—Keyleth.

Codenames Expansion Packs are bite-sized mini expansions that let you customize your Codenames experience with new thematic words or images. A copy of Codenames: Critical Role Adventures is required to play. The Expansion Pack is NOT compatible with the base Codenames.

ブラーマン Brahman[Bluffman] is a social deduction game based on the classic Werewolf.

In a world where Werewolves have won in every villiage there are no more villagers, and now the Werewolves are split into two major factions and vie for control over Moon Fragments in order to conduct either a Full Moon or New Moon Ritual and gain power to control the other faction or disassemble them from the inside.

Every turn, each player will be given a Faction they are part of and may choose to be Honest or Bluff in order to help their team win each round.

Players win by obtaining +10 Moon Fragments while playing Honestly, have another member of their team who is playing honestly have +10 Fragments, or have an honest player from the other faction or their own faction reach -10 Moon Fragments.

The Black Wolf wins whenever anyone reaches -10 Moon Fragments or they win alone by getting -10 Moon Fragments.

In Guardianes de la Huerta (Garden Guardians), you're part of a group of scarecrows learning to care for their crops throughout the seasons.

Each turn, you'll make two decisions: which crops to add to the garden and which areas to protect for the best harvest.

The key is planning: Where will your crops grow? Which areas do you need to protect now? And what's next?

Get your hands dirty and start planting!

Furry & Furious is a highly addictive, fast-paced arena brawler of intriguing dice-triggered actions, tactical card deck building and powerful, asymmetric animal clans with unique abilities.
It's an easy to learn 2-4 player combat game. Select from a variety of animal clans to play and master their unique abilities. Use your action dice to put your opponents in trouble with clever moves or invest them in mighty power-up cards. Push your luck, manipulate dice, and consider making sacrifices to achieve the ultimate goal: to become the “last clan standing”.

—description from the publisher

Rolling Fiefdoms is a roll-and-write game where you grow a small fief through clever dice pairing and careful planning. Each turn, the Regent rolls four dice and groups them into two pairs; players then choose where to build cottages, markets, and landmarks on their fief. When all plots are filled, assign labourers to activate buildings, score reputation, and see whose fief prospers most.

Rolling Fiefdoms also comes with 8 solo challenges that chronicle the rise of your fiefdom, from a humble settlement in untamed lands to a thriving center of learning and power. Each challenge may alter the setup, twist the rules, or even introduce new buildings as new pressures emerge and your responsibilities grow.

Winner of the BGG 2025 Roll & Write Game Design Contest.

—description from the designer

Volarak: Eel & F'fuoh
Varied | Unique Hero | Pirate & Monster | Unfathomable | Huge 11

Mobile Gunner Special Attack

Range 5. Attack 4. * Eel & F'fuoh may attack with Mobile Gunner Special Attack after attacking normally. You may target and attack non-adjacent figures with Mobile Gunner Special Attack, even while engaged.

Out of Control

If Eel & F'fuoh have 4 or more wounds at the start of their turn, they cannot use Mobile Gunner Special Attack and must attack all adjacent figures, even ones they are not engaged with.

Swim

Eel & F'fuoh do not have to stop their movement when entering a water space.

Stats
Life: 8

Move: 5

Range: 1

Attack: 5

Defense: 4

Points: 155

Eel & F'Fuoh
Goblins are practical. When a young goblin is old enough to hold tools, they are given a fitting name and a role to fulfill and set to work. Strong swimmer by age 9? Great. Your name is Eel and you’re a diver.

So, when Volarak summoned Eel to work in the harbors of Haukeland, Eel figured it was as good a job as any. Besides, reclamation diving for Volarak Enterprises sure beat diving for Dwarven scrap back home on Feylund. Sure, the waters were polluted and murky, but the worst thing you were liable to stumble across was an angry fish or some toxic waste, not a heinous forgotten trap from the 9th Age! Eel’s colleague Prawn found one of those. One minute he brushed up against some old sword, and the next his skeleton just ripped itself free from his body and started walking across the bottom of the bay to Mab knows where.

Plus, working for Volarak, if you reclaimed something that wasn’t on the mission brief, you could keep it. Well, that wasn’t officially part of the contract, but it also wasn’t not part of it, so it was fair game as far as Eel saw things. This let him claim pieces of scrap here and there for his tinkering, and Volarak rewarded self-improvement.

It’s also how he found F’fuoh. Deep in a steaming crevice, Eel found a smooth bronze box covered in strange markings—so of course he opened it. Inside staring back at him was a weird little green lump of eyes and tentacles. In that moment, as their psychic bond formed, Eel caught glimpses of what the creature had been and would become, saw galaxies stretch before him, and felt the creature’s contradictingly boundless senses of timelessness and of loss.

But goblins are practical. So, Eel also saw an opportunity. F’fuoh grew quickly. Within a week, they were bigger than Eel himself. After a month, Eel could ride them. A month after that, the pair left scrap diving behind for a life as privateers aboard The Equilibrium. Now, having ravaged the seas for over a year, the inseparable duo fights with unmatched coordination, Eel laughing along to quips only he can hear.

Eel & F'Fuoh is a Convention Exclusive figure. It will be obtainable in person at Gencon 2026, or on the Renegade Web Store during any major convention that Renegade attends, such as Gencon 2026, Adepticon 2027 and Gencon 2027.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Heroscape: Taelord the Kyrie Warrior Promo
description:
Taelord the Kyrie Warrior
Kyrie | Unique Hero | Gladiator & Warrior | Relentless | Medium 5

Onslaught Aura

Once per player turn, after another Gladiator figure you control or any figure you control within 4 clear sight spaces of Taelord the Kyrie Warrior rolls attack dice for a normal attack against an adjacent figure, it may add 1 skull to the roll.

Deadly Strike

When rolling attack dice with Taelord, each skull rolled counts for one additional skull.

Stealth Flying

Stats
Life: 5

Move: 5

Range: 1

Attack: 2

Defense: 4

Points: 120

TAELORD THE KYRIE WARRIOR
When Taelord first drew a blade, it was in the Ritual of Strength. A Boretooth Ratosk was let loose to face him in the coliseum of his ancestors, deep in the Kyrien Peaks, and when he plunged his blade into its side, he became a Kyrie warrior.

He drew his blade again when his daughter was born, and drew it across both of their palms so that their bond would be forged in blood. She did not scream, even then. Loviatäk was always strong.

He drew his blade when eighty-three Kyrie stood in the coliseum, hoping to become the right hand of the Valkyrie Utgar. When Taelord sheathed his blade two days later, only he remained standing.

He drew his blade when he led a legion of Utgar’s orcs to storm the village of Stechevan and slaughter the crimson-winged Kyrie who lived there. He cut down the Kyrie warrior Jalgard that day, although he barely remembers it. Just one more face, one more dying scream, one more vengeful child left behind.

He drew his blade the day the Marro witch Kee-Mo-Shi finally crawled out of his skull and back into a body of her own, and with it he shattered the amulet she had placed around his neck.

He drew his blade to meet Loviatäk’s axe when she led Utgar’s enemies against him and betrayed all Taelord had ever fought for. Although he would have preferred a warrior’s death that day, instead he was asked to become an advisor in her New Alliance.

Today, he draws his flaming blade, and the crowd roars. A blood-red dawn rises on Mitonsoul’s Arena, and warriors from Jandar’s and Utgar’s ranks have gathered to prove who is strong enough to survive in this new age. Some that draw their blades today draw them for the first time. And just like the Ritual of Strength taught him, he will teach them how to be strong.

Taelord the Kyrie Warrior will be available at select Gencon 2026 events and as an organized play promotional figure for Summer 2026. He will later be available on the Renegade Web Store.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Heroscape: Battle for Blackscar Battle Box
description:
Enter the shadow-scarred wilds of Blackscar, where assassins, undead royalty, airborne scouts, hardened lawmen, and disciplined samurai clash for control of Blackscar.

Heroscape: Battle for Blackscar introduces six all-new Heroes and a living fungal hazard that reshape the battlefield in bold, cinematic ways.

With dynamic powers, cross-faction synergies, and dramatic tactical moments, Battle for Blackscar offers everything players love about Heroscape—elevated with fresh mechanics and unforgettable characters.

This Battle Box includes everything two players need to jump into Heroscape, featuring 6 highly detailed miniatures, modular hex terrain, Shroudshroom pieces, dice, markers, and all the cards, rules, and scenarios needed for endless replayability. Expand your forces with any Heroscape expansion for even greater battles!

Contents

6 Highly Detailed Miniatures
6 Army Cards
8 D6 Combat Dice
1 20-sided Die
8 Order Markers
20 Wound Markers
1 Round Tracker
25 Hex Tile Terrain Pieces
2 Young Shroudshroom Destructible Objects
Rulebook
Scenario Guide
Features

Six Unique Heroes supporting the Umbral Covenant (Vydar/Reva) and Clockwork Combine (Einar/Volarak) alliances.
Shadow, undead, and lawman mechanics that interlock to offer deep synergy potential.
New terrain interaction through Shroudshrooms and Shadow tiles.
Reactive and interrupt-based abilities enabling high-tension decision-making.
Highly cinematic gameplay moments, including petrification, quickdraw duels, airborne scouting, and stealth assassinations.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Heroscape: Clockwork Combine Research Expedition Army Expansion
description:
The Clockwork Combine Research Expedition brings cutting-edge experimental weaponry, modular technology, and precision scouts to the battlefields of Valhalla.

This Army Expansion features Gimbal, a colossal inventor-dragon hybrid powered by detachable prototype systems, alongside the tactical Eisen Expedition—a three-figure scouting squad. Together with three powerful Treasure Glyphs, this pack delivers a toolbox of mobility, area control, and explosive offense that rewards clever activation order and battlefield awareness.

Contents

4 Highly Detailed Miniatures
2 Army Cards
3 Treasure Glyph cards
3 Treasure Glyphs
Features

1 Unique Hero and 1 Unique 3-Figure Squad
Gimbal – The Experimental Weapons Platform: the centerpiece of the expansion. Gimbal is a massive Volarak engineer whose body houses interchangeable prototype systems.
The Eisen Expedition is a Volarak-aligned, three-figure squad of expert explorers. Their abilities highlight speed, coordination, and tactical setup and excels at map control, objective pressure, and supporting Gimbal’s massive offensive footprint.
Three exclusive, technology-driven Treasure Glyphs that function as modular upgrades.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Heroscape: Crooked Crew of the Clockwork Combine Army Expansion
description:
The Crooked Crew of the Clockwork Combine brings a gritty blend of gunslinging justice, cursed steel, and experimental science to the battlefield. This Army Expansion introduces three unique Heroes–each with a distinct playstyle–and a deadly Treasure Glyph that rewards aggression at a price.

Designed for players who love synergy-driven tactics, high-risk maneuvers, and unpredictable battlefield moments, the Crooked Crew adds new tools to Einar-aligned armies and to mixed-faction builds across Valhalla.

Contents

3 Highly Detailed Miniatures
3 Army Cards
1 Treasure Glyph card
1 Treasure Glyph
Features

3 new Unique Armies and one Treasure Glyph to add to the Heroscape landscape
Guilty McCreech: A sharpshooter with a badge he probably shouldn’t have.
Takanaka Kaiga: Driven by torment and armed with the cursed Bloodcursed Blade.
Durnipia: Part mad scientist, part skyborn artificer.
Treasure Glyph included: The Bloodcursed Blade adds guaranteed damaged and unique tension to the game!

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Heroscape: Might of the New Alliance Army Expansion
description:
The Might of the New Alliance Army Expansion unleashes raw aggression and relentless momentum onto the battlefield. Featuring a Huge Marro Hivelord and a brutal raiding Unique Squad bound by blood and conquest, this expansion emphasizes forward pressure, escalating power, and punishing melee combat. Two powerful Glyphs further enhance speed and striking efficiency, ensuring the New Alliance advances with unstoppable force. 1 Unique Hero and 1 Unique 3-figure Unique Squad aligned with Utgar

4 Highly Detailed Miniatures

2 Glyphs

High-pressure melee gameplay built around momentum and attrition
Map-spanning mobility through Hive Tunnel and movement boosts
Snowball mechanics that reward aggressive positioning
2 Permanent Glyphs that enhance speed and combat consistency
Cinematic battlefield moments, from erupting hive strikes to last-stand raiders.

boardgame: Heroscape: Paragons of the New Alliance Army Expansion
description:
The Paragons of the New Alliance Army Expansion brings together three unlikely champions under the banner of Jandar, forging a powerful coalition built on sacrifice, coordination, and battlefield leadership. Featuring a holy warrior whose faith endures beyond death, a Kyrie commander who rewards disciplined order, and a precise artillery specialist, this expansion introduces layered synergy and positional play that strengthens both offensive and defensive strategies.

Contents

3 Highly Detailed Miniatures
2 Treasure Glyphs
3 Army Cards
2 Treasure Glyph Cards
Features

3 Unique Heroes aligned under Jandar with strong leadership and support roles. Jeanne d’Arc – Martyr of the Alliance, Concan the Kyrie Warrior – Commander of Balance and Frizzt Galagan – Precision Fire Support
2 Permanent Treasure Glyphs that reward tight formations and positional control: Spear of Gerda (Defense Aura) and Sword of Guether (Attack Aura)
Synergy-driven gameplay emphasizing Order Marker strategy and mixed-allegiance armies.
Defensive and offensive aura effects that create dynamic front lines.

boardgame: Heroscape: The Ancient Lands of Valhalla Terrain Expansion
description:
Deep beneath Valhalla, in the depths of the Myrkheim tunnels, lie the remains of a lost civilization. Metallic tiles imbued with strange runes line untold miles of labyrinthine tunnels and grand halls, centered around a mysterious and ancient site known as the Citadel. Though the Citadel has never seen sunlight, strange glowing mushrooms cast an eerie light. But there are other mushrooms as well… ones that seem to pull all light into themselves, obscuring those who venture near them from view.

Contents

23 Hex-tile Terrain Pieces
(2) 24-hex Ancient Tiles 
(3) 7-hex Ancient Tiles
(3) 3-hex Ancient Tiles
(5) 2-hex Ancient Tiles
(10) 1-hex Ancient Tiles
1 Adult Shroudshroom Destructible Object
1 Ancient Shroudshroom Destructible Object
2 Young Shroudshroom Destructible Objects
3 Cards
1 Setup Guide
Features

New line-of-sight-blocking terrain that actively shapes combat
Destructible objects with propagation mechanics, creating evolving battlefields
Shroudshrooms count as trees, enabling synergy with existing powers
Large variety of terrain tiles for flexible map construction
Ideal for scenario play, competitive maps, and narrative environments

Isamu
Undead | Unique Hero | Ninja | Relentless | Medium 5

Eternal Servitude

Isamu cannot be destroyed by wounds on this Army Card except during the end of a round.

Phantom Walk

Isamu can move through all figures and never takes leaving engagement attacks.

Stats
Life: 1

Move: 6

Range: 1

Attack: 4

Defense: 2

Points: 10

A damp smell, the cold touch of iron, the skittering of a rat. These are the sensations that register for Isamu as he stirs in the darkness. A quick shift of his limbs confirms his suspicion: He has been captured. A lifetime working his way up the ranks of the Twilight Clan has taught him to recognize the inside of a cell. He had once ruled the streets of the underwater metropolis of New York with strategic cruelty and swift retribution for any who dared cross his path. Who could have managed to capture this elusive ninja, Utgar’s favorite assassin?

His thoughts are interrupted by footsteps echoing from the passage beyond the dark cell. He relaxes his body and feigns unconsciousness. The sound of a key in a heavy lock is followed by a flood of torchlight.

“You can stop pretending, ninja. I know whether a mortal is sleeping.”

Isamu lifts his head and grins. “Good evening, Sonya.”

The Esenwein matriarch is flanked by two skeletal guards clad in red armor bearing her family’s crest. Her eyes are fixed on Isamu’s. Her hands trace the handle of a wooden dagger.

“Hawthorne wood, clever. But you should have known you couldn’t hide this from me.”

“I was following orders. If you’ve got a problem, bring it up with Utg—”

“Lord Utgar is dead, as you are well aware!” In an instant, Sonya is only a foot from Isamu’s face, her visage twisting to something truly horrifying.

“Well, how does his usurper feel about you imprisoning her best assassin?”

A smile curls Sonya’s lips.

“Who do you think told me where to find you?”

“I didn’t think your loyalty could be so easily bought.”

“Don’t speak to me of loyalty, ninja. My husband was many things. Strong, ruthless, cunning, but also arrogant, maybe too ambitious. Still…” Sonya pauses, looking at the dagger. “...I wouldn’t expect you to understand the loyalty it takes to hold a dynasty together for centuries.” A dark look passes over the vampire as she grips the dagger tighter. “However, I know how to teach you.”

A necrotic light fills Sonya’s eyes as a sinister laugh escapes her lips. Isamu looks at the vacant stares of the skeletons, and a sickening realization dawns on him as pain shoots between his ribs. A realization and, for the first time, fear.

How about some more heroes? With more heroes you can get a lot of variety out of your games. You can also play them in solo (either as or against them) and even play 3-4P team games.

Components:

     Dowdran Hero set
     Dowdran AI Sheet: for solo play, requires Imperator expansion.

How about some more heroes? With more heroes you can get a lot of variety out of your games. You can also play them in solo (either as or against them) and even play 3-4P team games.

Components:

     Thrasher Hero set
     Thrasher AI Sheet: for solo play, requires Imperator expansion.

Family Card Game for All Ages: 
Fast-paced and easy to learn: build crop sets and keep your harvest safe while goat and action cards shake up every turn. A laugh-out-loud, casual game full of surprises and one big slap moment that keeps everyone engaged. Perfect for family game nights and gifts for kids.

How to Play Screaming Billy: 
Grow your crop sets while goats freeze turns, help protect your harvest, or trigger chaotic action effects. Watch out! When Billy the goat starts screaming, slap fast, if you’re last to react, you lose 2 crops. Nonstop laughs and quick, surprising moments make every round memorable.

Quick Rounds & Easy Setup: 
Ages 7 and up, 2-4 players. Usually 10-15 minutes per game. Learn it in about 5 minutes. Great for family game nights anywhere. Travel-friendly and perfect to pack for vacations, camping, or trips.

Play Anywhere, Built for Family Fun: 
With 68 linen-finish cards and a portable box, Screaming Billy is perfect for backpacks and gifts for kids. Created by Kawaii Lab Games, a family business focused on screen-free fun and bonding. This crazy goat card game delivers loud laughs, surprise twists, and quick reflex moments for all ages. Will you spot Screaming Billy and slap first to save your harvest?

Focus, React, Win in Screaming Billy: 
Quick turns, chaotic action cards, and Billy’s surprise screams keep everyone alert. Reacting fast naturally sharpens focus, impulse control, and reaction speed. Unexpected twists keep the excitement light and the competition friendly, making every round engaging, mentally stimulating, and fun for kids, teens, and adults alike.

Family Card Game for All Ages: 
A twist on classic Tic Tac Toe (3 in a row) featuring cute Burger and Pizza characters. Don’t be fooled by the simple rules — it’s more strategic than it looks! Action Cards can change the game in a second, creating surprises that make every round unique. Strategy matters more than luck. Perfect as a gift for kids and fun for the whole family.

How to Play Tic Tac Twist: 
Play on an imaginary 3×3 grid. Place your cards to line up 3 in a row by matching food or color, guided by your Pattern Card. Block opponents by switching the food or color. Action Cards bring hilarious twists, turning the game around in seconds and keeping everyone on their toes.

Easy Setup & Light Strategy: 
Ages 8 and up, 2–4 players. Rounds last 10–15 minutes, and the game can be learned in about 5 minutes. Enjoy simple strategy that stays fun for kids, teens, and adults. Perfect for family game nights anywhere, and travel-friendly for vacations or camping, and makes a great gift for kids.

Play Anywhere, Built for Family Fun: 
Tic Tac Twist includes 72 high-quality cards, illustrated English rules (Spanish and French versions online), and a portable box ideal for backpacks or gifts. Outsmart your opponents and line up 3 in a row before they do, every game plays differently!

Focus, Plan, Win in Tic Tac Twist: 
Beyond laughs and fast-food rivalry, Tic Tac Twist keeps players alert as the grid changes and every card placement counts. Timing your Action Cards and adjusting moves on the fly sharpens focus, flexible thinking, and strategic planning in a fun, natural way. The light-strategy gameplay builds friendly competition and confidence, making each round engaging and mentally stimulating and perfect for kids gifts, teens, and adults alike.

boardgame: Heroscape: Valkyrie Trials Katana of Thorian Treasure Glyph Promo
description:
Beginning 2026 - Valkyrie Trials that players can compete and win prizes in.

A promotional prize of the Katana of Thorian Treasure Glyph game piece with rules summary Army Card can be won.

Unlike the figure promos, this glyph will not be purchasable as a separate item, but will be included in a future product.

Katana of Thorian
Every figure must be adjacent to this figure to attack it with a Normal Attack.

Each player chooses one of the 4 colors of cones and places them stacked on top of each other on the starting area (compass rose in the corner of the playing field).

At the beginning, each player may roll the die 3 times. If they roll a 6, they may place their first cone on the space of their color and may roll again, as after every 6. Depending on the number rolled, the player then moves their cone in the direction of the arrow (clockwise).

The game has 3 lanes, and the goal is to get your cones to the target (dice) in the middle as quickly as possible. However, since there are only 4 transitions to the next, more inward lane on each lane, this isn't so easy. Only those who land exactly on the intersection point can advance to the next inner lane on their next move. If they fail to do so, they must continue circling the lane until their cone comes to rest directly on one of the 4 intersection points. If the player manages to reach the new, more inward lane, they must continue moving clockwise along it. They must not immediately advance to the innermost lane. Only at the next intersection point can they move into the last lane, provided they again land exactly on the intersection point

If the cone lands on an occupied space, the player places their cone on top of their opponent's. The opponent only gets their captured cone back when the player who captured it reaches the finish line.

Inside the playing field are 4 dice. Each die has a specific number of pips. An arrow leads to each die from a point. The player may only move into the playing field of their color if they roll the number shown on their target die.

A cone can only be placed into the game when a 6 is rolled. So, the first player to have all 4 cones on their target die is the winner.

The Fall of Rome is free to download and introduces 7 new factions, including Late Romans, Picts, Goths, Britons and Huns. As the Roman empire starts to crumble, who will end up on top?

In your (perhaps digital) hands, you have the free Pillage supplement covering Late Antiquity. This “mini-supplement” has been created to push Pillage’s possibilities into a new era of gaming: Late Antiquity, and the end of the Western Roman Empire.

This mini-supplement includes 7 new factions, designed to work together as a coherent whole. However, there’s nothing stopping you from playing anachronistic games between armies from this supplement and the main rulebook – as long as you’re comfortable with the total dissolution of the time-stream!

Since this mini-supplement is free, it hasn’t undergone the same rigorous testing as the Pillage rulebook. It will therefore change over time as we use feedback from players to tweak costs and keep these new factions, abilities, and weapons properly balanced. This PDF has a date and version number you can check on the cover to see if you’re up-to-date.

This supplement may well become the core of a future full book-length supplement, complete with new factions like Vandals, Sassanids, and Lombards, new scenarios, and new game rules.

—description from the publisher
language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups

description:
In Arribada, players lay out turtles during a mass nesting event known as an arribada, “arrival by sea.” The goal of the game is to place turtles in groups (sequences or sets) to score points and to make sure as few of your turtles as possible are left stranded by the end of the game. Family means no turtle gets left behind!

Arribada is an approachable strategy game with surprising depth and layered interaction. It’s satisfying and easy to build sets and sequences of your turtles, but things get even more interesting when players begin to fight over the same turtle! Some turtles are better at scoring you quick points, while others are super effective for capturing opponent tiles or gaining bonus turns. When you capture an opponent turtle, it becomes a wild to help you build even more lucrative groups! Meanwhile, players are competing for presence and majorities across the four quadrants for massive end-game points. But be careful, any stranded turtles at game end will hurt your score!

Players take turns placing a turtle tile, claiming a neutral tile, or capturing an opponent tile. The game end is triggered when a player places out their last turtle shell.

—description from the publisher

In Seagrass, players take on the role of marine naturalists, exploring the many reefs and colonies of creatures that thrive among these expanding and diverse “rainforests of the sea.”

Seagrass is an approachable strategy game with surprising depth and layered interaction. On your turn, simply expand a colony of aquatic creatures or expand a reef. Your colonies score you more points the bigger they get, and they help you compete for majorities around reefs. Expanding reefs will increase their point value and help you replenish your hand of tiles, but expanding a reef will also make it a more juicy target for your opponents! Playing out multiple tiles of the same type will grant powerful turns, but spreading out your plays will allow you to score the same colonies more times! Decisions are a tricky strategic balance in this underwater ecosystem.

Seagrass is the next title in Bitewing Game’s Crown Jewel Selection. The game is an evolution of Trevor Benjamin and Brett J. Gilbert’s Maya, introducing many innovations by:


     Providing two game boards featuring tight interaction
     Introducing special creature tiles with powerful abilities
     Focusing the end-game trigger to be more exciting and impactful
     Hiding and balancing the scoring for a more tense competition
     Creating tough trade-offs with the new Discovery expansion
     Broadening the appeal and approachability with stunning aquatic illustrations by Alisha Giroux


—description from the publisher

In Museum Mixup, you and your companions have just landed the best summer gig at the National Institute of Artistry. But there’s the catch: the head curator has misplaced the event registry and all the artwork plaques! It’s up to you to save the day by renaming these iconic masterpieces... your way.

Each round, an Artwork Card is chosen and each player must use their collection of Word Magnets to create the perfect title for that piece of art. Each player reads their title outloud, and the best title is chosen by that rounds Curator. The role of Curator rotates players.

After each player has been the Curator twice, players count up their Artwork Cards and the player with the most wins. Simple as that, the museum is saved!

—description from the publisher
family: Theme: Fine Art and Art Museums

boardgame: Kingdom of Cutetopia: By Order of the King
description: Another standalone game in the Cutetopia universe, this is a trick-taking game about bounty hunters capturing their mark. A co-operative game of four players in two teams, each person is given a hand of five cards, with the top card of the draw deck being flipped over to determine trump. Then a card from the Baddie deck is placed in the center, and five rounds of tricks are played, the team that won the most gets the Baddie card. After this sequence is repeated four more times, the team with the highest value of Baddies wins the game.

boardgame: Kingdom of Cutetopia: Forest of Shadows – the Expansion
description: This ten-card expansion adds another column to the grid, adding four more forest cards, two more weapons and potions, the Emperor's Vase treasure, and the Cervitaur baddie.

After a series of bad omens, you seek refuge at a secluded cabin in the woods, hoping to turn your luck around. But as you settle in, strange things begin to happen. Can you shake off the bad vibes, or is something darker lurking in the shadows?

The Friday Fright, title #13 in the Holiday Hijinks line, is an escape room-style game in just 18 cards! As a group, you will solve a series of puzzles to unravel a mystery. Puzzles may test your perception, wordplay, and lateral thinking. English language fluency is required.

When you have solved a puzzle on the cards, you will enter the answer into the companion website (compatible with all smart phones and computers) and it will check whether you are correct. If you are, the website will tell you which card or cards to take next. The website also contains a graduated hint system, so if you get stuck you can get just a small hint about the current puzzle to get moving again without giving too much away.

This system is non-destructible: some groups may choose to write on cards, but this is not required. A group cannot re-play the game, but can pass it on to another group for their use. This entry in the series takes about an hour to solve.

Take to the Skies over the battlefields of WW1, and duel for Glory! Oh, and did we mention you're also animals? Yes, that's Camels flying Sopwiths vs Quokkas in Fokkers. They're Wild, and they're Aces!

In Wild Aces, players program their plane's manoeuvres each round by playing action cards, then execute them to outmanoeuvre opponents and score hits while avoiding damage.

All planes start in a row in the middle of the table: Allied planes on the left facing right, Axis on the right facing left, with the centre planes facing each other. Choose your side (supports 1v1, 1v2, or 2v2) and take your plane's deck of 10 action cards. Shuffle 4-6 condition cards (one per round) to determine the sortie length and round modifiers like Tailwind (5 actions instead of 4), Fog (lowest plane takes damage), or Clouds (highest planes are untargetable).

Each round, players deal 6 cards from their action deck. Starting with 2 cards in hand, each turn play one action card from the hand and then draw the next card from their deck.

Planes move on the tableau based on their actions—climbing to higher altitude levels, diving lower, moving forward or backward in the formation, or rotating to face different directions. When a Fire action is played, if any plane is directly in front (or behind for rear guns), that player draws a Strike card.

If they're lucky it may be a Glancing Strike, which takes immediate effect but won't be lasting.  Less lucky are Direct Strikes,  which permanently enter the action deck, clogging it with dead cards that do nothing when drawn and making them progressively less effective.

The sortie ends after all condition cards are resolved (4-6 rounds). Players count their total Strike cards (both Grazes held and Direct Strikes in their deck). In team play, the team with the fewest combined Strikes wins the sortie.

Additionally, the individual pilot with the fewest Strikes is crowned Ace Pilot—meaning you could win individual glory even if your team loses!

The different planes offer asymmetric abilities - Fokkers excel at manoeuvrability, Sopwith Camels are versatile all-rounders. Bombers sacrifice agility for bombing power and defensive rear guns, and the nippy Spad is great for a strafing run, just watch out for friendly fire!

—description from the designer

boardgame: TTMC?: Tu te mets combien ? – Science infuse: avec Science & Vie
description:
TTMC is a mix of party and trivia games. Self-assess your knowledge from 1 to 10 on a variety of scientific themes!

Be the first to reach the final card by answering the questions you chose. Each team will self-assess itself from 1 to 10 on a multitude of topics. Each grade has a more or less complex question: the higher the grade, the more difficult the question. If you answer it right, the team will go up the number of boxes corresponding to their self-rated score. For example, if you answer correctly question 6, you will advance 6 boxes.

Science infuse edition includes 4 innovative themes with 162 cards and more than 1 200 questions: body and spirit, progress, mother nature and exploration.

boardgame: TTMC: Tu te mets combien en Martinique ?
description:
TTMC?: Tu te mets combien ? is a mix of party and trivia games. Self-assess your knowledge from 1 to 10 on a variety of  themes, from classical to curious.

Be the first to reach the final card by answering the questions you chose. Each team will self-assess itself from 1 to 10 on a multitude of topics. Each grade has a more or less complex question: the higher the grade, the more difficult the question. If you answer it right, the team will go up the number of boxes corresponding to their self-rated score. Easy? Try a grade 8 about sunchoke.

It's funny, accessible and totally addictive!

So, how much do you know about mont Pelée? About holy towns? About historical Martinican monuments?

—description from the publisher

Mortal Kombat 1 enters the arena of deck-building games with deadly new game mechanics and familiar Kombatants.
The game takes the mechanics fans love from the Cerberus Deck-Building Game engine and reimagines and optimizes them for fighting games. Ratchet up the khaos with all your favorite Kombatants, Fatalities, and other memorable moves from the game.

Players choose a Kombatant, play cards to attack their foes and reduce their health to unleash Fatalities, but they'll be back next round, ready to continue the fight! The player with the highest VP wins the game.

—description from the publisher

Based on the movie of the series of the same name, it is a puzzle matching board game where you move spaces based on the spinner. Traverse familiar locations and interact with familiar characters to get to the end of the track, where the player with the most points is the winner.

Who knows the best methods to stay out of the way, or cause the right kind of trouble, or simply outwit your opponent? The player with the most "BLOCKS!" at the end of the game! Use your credits wisely and place your blocks carefully in this simple to learn, difficult to master blocks placing game.

Build the Shape Tower, then race your opponents to the Tower's top. Use strategy to bump your opponents at just the right time. Claim victory by playing your cards right.

Each game board can be oriented in 4 ways to create THOUSANDS of different towers in which to play on.

A standard deck of playing cards and five colored dice of the same color for each player. Each player receives a different color of dice. A game board for each player. The suits of the playing cards are divided by suit (diamonds, hearts, spades, and clubs). Each player receives one suit.

The game ends if one or all other players lost all their dice.

—description from the designer

The Cartaventura series expands with this new volume, entitled Exiles, which contains two stories written in partnership with the Human Rights League.

Follow the path of exile in the footsteps of Sahar and Moussa!

•••

La Gamme Cartaventura s'agrandit avec ce volet présenté sous le nom de Exils, qui contient 2 histoires rédigées en partenariat avec la Ligue des Droits de l’Homme.

Prenez la route de l'exil sur les traces de Sahar et Moussa !

This edition was created especially for adults. The game is about accurately answering unconventional questions about your acquaintances, family, and friends. How well do you know your friends and their Ego? During the game, you will learn many new things about one another — sometimes truly surprising ones.

The rules of this game for adults are childishly simple. Draw one of 770 intriguing questions and read it aloud. Answer it secretly by choosing one of the given options. The other players must then guess your choice. They place one or two tokens, depending on how confident they are. The winner is the player who collects the most tokens and proves to know their friends best.

The Ego challenge lies in giving an honest answer to a question about yourself and your views. This task may turn out to be more difficult than you think. Will you be honest, or will you mislead your opponents?

The year is 1320. The Grand Duchy of Lithuania and Ruthenia is on the rise, fighting with Teutonic Order on the west and defending from Golden Horde on the east. Pagans and christians live in peace. These lands are covered with dark forests and deep swamps, and only the gods know what is hiding here.

In Heroes of the Grand Duchy you step into a grim, low-fantasy medieval world where survival is not dictated by the roll of a dice, but by cold calculation, ruthless point and resource management, and fragile alliances.

You play as 1 of 6 asymmetric characters: from a catholic Witcher and a deserted Teutonic Knight to a local pagan Hunter and a adventurous Varangian. Your goal is to gain a certain number of Hero Points through hunting for the Grand Duke Gediminas, slaying mythical beasts from legends and beliefs and fending off raids on villages.

Key Features:

     Diceless core system: everything is calculable. Manage your AP, Health, and Morale with extreme precision.
     Player interaction: barter, oaths and manipulations with turn order.
     Deep asymmetry: each of the 6 characters has unique movement rules, starting stats, and personal advantages and disadvantages that drastically alter their playstyle.
     Historical dark fantasy setting: a meticulously crafted world blending the real history with local folklore.


Will you become a legend of the Grand Duchy or will your bones be swallowed by the marshes?

—description from the designer

An abstract chess-based game where the board belongs not only to White and Black. Here, there is a third force — the Heralds of Silence. They emerge from your moves, move as a swarm by the roll of a die, and spare no one. Neither kings, nor pawns.

HERALDS OF SILENCE – The Long Night Chess Variant
(Inspired by "30 Days of Night")

QUICK RULES

THE SETUP
Only kings (White on e1, Black on d8) and pawns (White on 2nd rank, Black on 7th).
No other pieces. Queens appear only through promotion.
You'll need: chessboard, kings + 8 pawns each, one d8, and 15–20 tokens for Heralds.

THE HERALDS OF SILENCE 
A neutral piece that appears when you make a move without capture:
Pawn move and Herald appears directly in front of the pawn.
King or Queen move and Herald appears on the square they left.
Heralds block the Queen's line of sight.

THE REAPING PHASE
After every move, if any Heralds are on board:
Roll d8 (1=↑, 2=↗, 3=→, 4=↘, 5=↓, 6=↙, 7=←, 8=↖).
All Heralds move one square in that direction, starting from the edge they're moving toward.
Collide with a piece? It's destroyed.
Collide with another Herald? The moving one survives.

KING'S TELEPORT
If you cannot move any pawn or queen at the start of your turn, your king may teleport to any empty square. A Herald appears where he left.
Bare kings move only by teleport — if you have no pawns or queen, your king simply teleports instead of walking.

WINNING
Kill the enemy king (check/checkmate don't exist).
Both kings die in Reaping Phase = draw.
30 moves without any capture = draw ("The night has ended").

Take on the role of a detective investigating the brutal murder of a young woman in the forests of Glastenbury. Use police files, forensic reports, photographs, witness statements, and maps of the area to conduct a thorough investigation and identify the killer.

Glastenbury Forest, Vermont. The police receive a disturbing phone call - a young woman reports that her sister, Anna, is missing. The police captain assembles a search team, and within hours they make a gruesome discovery deep in the woods. Anna's body is found hanging from a tree, battered and disfigured. What happened to her? This case is far too strange to be considered an ordinary crime. As the investigation unfolds, unsettling details emerge, and the eerie atmosphere of Glastenbury makes even the most obvious facts seem uncertain.

Uncover what truly happened that night. Why did Anna go into the forest? Who - or what - is responsible for her death? What dark secrets does Glastenbury Forest hide? Analyze police reports, witness testimonies, and physical evidence to piece together the full story. Play solo or with friends and experience the thrill of being a real detective in a case that will test your attention to detail and logical thinking.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Kingdom Death: Monster – Obligate Gallant Promo Cards
description:
This is a set of two promo cards that cam with the Griswaldan miniature, which itself is not part of the game.

Obligate Gallant Blade (Speed 2, Accuracy 4+, Strength 8): Combo Finish 2, Delusion - Sword
Zealous offense. At the end of the round, if you have not wounded the monster with this, set your insanity to 0.

Obligate Gallant Shield (Speed 1, Accuracy 6+, Strength 5): Delusion - Shield
Protector Knight. You are guardless. While another survivor is adjacent to you, they may ignore a hit when they are attacked.

Living glossary: Delusion - Sword/Shield: 
A gear special rule. This sword/shield does not truly exist and cannot be voluntarily placed into a gear grid. When a survivor gains a gear with Delusion - Sword/Shield, record it on their survivor record sheet. Only they may use this gear. While that survivor is insane, if they have a sword/shield in their gear grid, place this gear on top of it without archiving the sword/shield underneath. While that survivor is sane, remove this gear from atop the one it is covering. If this gear would be archived, archive any gear underneath it as well.

Rule for gaining the gear: 
When a survivor with Quixotic departs with a sword/shield in their gear grid while insane, they gain this gear. Limit once per campaign. (Refer to the Delusion - X glossary entry for details on how to place gear with this special rule into a gear grid).

boardgame: Kingdom Death: Monster – Heartleech Baselard Rene
description:
Heartleech Baselard - Rene is packaged in a graphic box that includes:

1 x Heartleech Baselard Rene hard plastic miniature
1 x Hard plastic Kingdom Death 30mm base and insert
1 x Ennui of Dominance King indomitable resource card
1 x Heartleech Baselard pattern card
2 x Heartleech Baselard gear cards
1 x Living Glossary card
1 x Install guide card
1 x Large matte art print

Her body was a flame. She could feel the last of her strength draining, unwilling muscles slackening. With a final, pitiful swipe the hulking mass collapsed with a sudden rush of metallic crashes. Aching in every fiber, she threw aside the heaps of majestic cloth until she lay breathless, clutching the dented scraps of the crown. With the last of her resolve she lowered its broken circle over her bloodied head. She felt nothing.

—description from the publisher

Rise of Eternals is a strategic board game set in the world of the Outer Lands, where you take on the role of a Hero competing to gain control over the fallen citadel of Lumen and dethrone its rulers, the Eternals.

Rise of Eternals features engine-building, resource management, strategic card play, and movement across the citadel of Lumen. A complex and engaging experience in an immersive and aesthetically pleasing setting.

Rise of Eternals is the concluding chapter of the Legends of Void saga. After the Fall of Lumen, the citadel has fallen — now you and your rivals race to claim its legacy before the Eternals reclaim it themselves.

Each player leads a Hero and a Faction, building an engine of cards, resources, and Specialists to outmaneuver opponents across the citadel's districts.

On your turn, you spend 5 Time Units across a mix of actions: move your Hero through the citadel's squares, gather resources by trading Life, play cards for powerful effects or discard them for quick gains, deploy Specialists to visit Buildings, plant Flags in key districts to secure influence and protect inhabitants, or challenge an Eternal or the Lumen Core to ascend in the Towers of Knowledge. The most costly action is Rest — it consumes your entire turn but lets you activate your engine, draw new cards, and restore your Specialists. Resting also triggers an Eternal to move, reshaping the citadel and driving the game toward its end.

Cards come in three forms: Survivors (characters with fulfillable Quests for bonus rewards), Buildings (reusable locations that reset after each Rest), and Rifts (one-time effects from the Inner Worlds).

The game ends when the Eternals have moved enough times to trigger the final reckoning — a moment defined by the specific pair of Eternals in play that game.

To win, players score points by climbing the two Towers of Knowledge tied to that game's Eternals (variable in each game) and the Lumen Tower (fix for each game). Collecting bonuses along the way and competing for the objectives revealed at each Tower's summit. The player with the highest combined score becomes the new ruler of Lumen.

With 25 Heroes, 21 Factions, and 12 Eternals generating a unique pair each game, no two playthroughs unfold the same way.

—description from the designer

description:
Zoom through space in the super-fast card game Warp 99!

On your turn, add 1 card to your own growing card row—it has to be a bigger number than the last one, and NO repeating colors!

Then blast as many cards as you can onto the 2 middle piles using cool rules like Same Color, Plus 1, or Total 10. Get Stuck? Change the rules! Match extra rules? Get bonus cards! Empty your hand? Get another turn!

Score big points by making long card rows and get 15 more points every time you use up your Personal Draw Deck. First to reach 99 points wins!

—description from the publisher

Rust & Research is a fast-paced card game that brings the profound transformation of Belval (Luxembourg) to life. From steel industry to university campus, players flip through time, encounter key historical and contemporary figures, and experience the site’s many changes through play.

Designed for 4 to 6 players and played over multiple rounds, the game combines competition with historical discovery, whether in the classroom, at home, or at a café.

—description from the publisher
publishers: Luxembourg Centre for Contemporary and Digital History

description:
The Noble Team Expansion features six Legendary Character miniatures made from high-detail MasterCraft™ Resin, and includes all their Character and Special Order Reference cards.  The team is comprised of:
> Carter-A259 (Noble One)
> Kat-B320 (Noble Two)

The expansion also includes a pair of UNSC Man Cannons to launch your Fireteam across the combat zone! These hand-cast scenery pieces feature in brand new scenarios contained within the included Noble Team Expansion booklet.

The Noble Team Expansion Booklet introduces the new double-mat game format to Halo: Flashpoint, complete with new rules and scenarios. These can be played by either combining two single Halo: Flashpoint boards or mats, or on the new double-size Reach neoprene gaming mat.
