Peacemaker Quest is a cooperative game for 2-4 players designed to strengthen conflict resolution skills, encourage communication and build empathy as players support one another along a journey from camp to their destination at the castle.

Players build a shared Conversation by playing Emotion Cards that match either the character or emotion to one of two discard piles. Each player has personal Challenge Cards representing situations their character is facing. When the right emotions or characters come together in the Conversation, a Challenge is resolved and flipped over to reveal a section of the path that advances the players along their journey.

Players win together when all Challenges are resolved and the path is complete. If the Emotion deck runs out, time has run out, and the group loses.

-description from designer

Death is just the beginning in the Cursed Seas.

52 Sails is a fast tactical skirmish game for two players using a standard deck of playing cards. 
Command a fleet of three ships, outmaneuver your opponent, and send them to the bottom. 
Your cards do double duty as both actions and measuring tools, making every hand a decision about what you're willing to spend.

The full rulebook includes six scenarios, Malachar's Cursed Artifacts that give every captain a unique playstyle, complete terrain rules, and everything you need for endless battles on the Cursed Seas.

How It Plays

     Your Deck Does Everything Cards are your actions, your movement templates, and your weapons. No rulers, no tokens, no extra components required.
     Choose Your Curse Each captain wields one of Malachar's cursed artifacts, giving you unique abilities your opponent has to play around.
     Every Action Is a Gamble When you attack or board an enemy ship, both players commit cards face-down in an escalating clash. Press your luck or hold back — but every card you spend now is a card you won't have later.


What You Need

     2 Players 
     25 to 45 Minutes 
     2 Standard Card Decks 
     10 Dice 
     3 Ship Miniatures per Player


Easy to transport.

boardgame: Flint Tactics: A Modular Historical Wargame
description:
Take command on the battlefields of the black powder era. Flint Tactics is a streamlined, fast-paced 18th and 19th-century historical system, designed for strategic depth without the tabletop bloat. Whether you are recreating the rigid linear warfare of the Seven Years' War, the grand batteries of the Napoleonic era, or the devastating rifled volleys of the American Civil War, this system puts command, morale, and maneuver at the forefront.

Core Features:

     Use the Miniatures You Own: Flint Tactics is completely basing-agnostic. Whether your collection features individually-based skirmish models or grand regimental multi-bases, the rules seamlessly accommodate your existing armies without rebasing a single miniature.
     Streamlined D6 Mechanics: Built around a simple 4-stat profile and an Alternating Unit Activation system, both players remain constantly engaged.
     The Psychology of War: Morale is just as lethal as musket fire. Units can become "Broken" under heavy fire or lost melees, requiring critical Break Tests to rally or face severe combat and movement penalties. Your General's presence on the front line is vital to holding the army together.
     Grand Campaigns System: Link your games into immersive 10-battle campaigns using three included historical tracks. Manage Requisition Points (RP) to supply your troops, deal with battlefield triage and injuries, and watch your surviving regiments earn Experience Points (XP) to unlock veteran training like "Sharpshooters" or "Hardened" discipline.
     Automata Solo Engine: Includes a robust, decision-tree-based Automata AI system that scales in difficulty, allowing you to play full pitched battles or entire campaigns completely solo against an unpredictable artificial enemy.
     The Flint Forge: Easily build your custom forces using the included web-based Flint Tactics Forge tool. Calculate points, equip your regiments, assign battlefield support, and generate printable, quick-reference unit cards.


What's Included:

     Core Rulebook : Streamlined rules for movement, terrain, combat resolution, and morale.
     Campaign Supplement : Rules for logistics, regimental progression, historical campaign tracks, and 3 unique tactical scenarios.
     Solo Automata Rules : The complete logic engine for running the AI opponent.
     Digital Tools : The HTML-based Flint Tactics Forge roster builder and the Automata Engine companion app.
     Universal Rosters : Ready-to-play JSON rosters for both multi-model and multi-based armies to jump right into the action.


—description from the publisher
family: History: War of the Austrian Succession

description:
Opetellaan Karuselli is an educational children’s board game designed to support early learning through playful interaction. The game is structured around a carousel theme, where players move around the board and complete simple tasks that reinforce basic cognitive skills such as recognition, matching, and early logical thinking.

Gameplay is straightforward and suitable for young children. Players take turns spinning or moving according to the game’s core mechanic and complete challenges based on visual cues, symbols, or simple instructions. The tasks are age-appropriate and designed to encourage participation, observation, and turn-taking.

The objective is typically to complete assigned tasks or reach a designated goal area first, depending on the specific rules included in the edition. The game emphasizes learning through repetition and positive reinforcement rather than competitive elimination, making it suitable for early childhood environments such as homes and kindergartens.

1st Band is a strategic card game where players compete to assemble the perfect musical lineup. Each round, players play cards representing different roles—Vocal, Guitar, Bass, Drums, or Keyboard. However, competition is fierce: if two players play the same role, only the musician with the higher value stays on stage, while the weaker performer is eliminated.

The game shifts into the Recruiting Phase once a full band (Vocal, Guitar, Bass, Drums + optional (Keyboard)) is formed. In a clever twist, the player who played the lowest-value card gets to pick first, choosing any musician from the stage to add to their personal collection. This creates a tense balance between playing high numbers to stay on the board and playing low numbers to secure the first pick of the best talent.

The game concludes when all cards have been played. Your final score is the total value of all musicians you’ve recruited, plus significant Set Bonuses: an extra 10 points for a standard 4-piece band or 20 points for a full 5-piece ensemble. Strategy lies in knowing when to fight for a spot and when to aim for the perfect set.

—description from the designer

In this monstrously fun dice game, players roll to steal buttons from the other players and take buttons from the center. But ... if anyone rolls 3 Fugglers, everyone races to grab Sasquoosh. The first player to get him gets the golden button hole, worth three points. Whoever has the most buttons when the center is empty wins.

Mïnomancer: Roll Them Bones! is a solitaire Roll & Write game chronicling your invasion into the domain of a rival necromancer.

Roll dice to raise your skeletal army, then place them in polyomino formations to search for keys, uncover hidden trapdoors, and bring the fight to your enemy.

in feudal Japan, power is not taken - it is earned. MEIYO is a strategic board game for 2-4 players in which rival Houses maneuver Samurai, summon Yokai spirits, and deploy Ninja across contested Provinces over four Seasons. For asymmetric Houses each bring a distinct playstyle and unique patron Yokai to the board, competing for control of a shifting, folklore-rich landscape.

Victory belongs to the most honorable House. Meiyo (Honor) accumulates across 12 rounds and is never spent, but earned through battles, governing productive Provinces, and controlling the Capital. But each path carries a temptation that corrupts it. Reach for supernatural power or shadowy subterfuge and your path to honor suffers. This tension between Giri (duty) and Ninjo (desire) lives within every decision you make.

MEIYO is a print-and-play game. 3 A-4 sheets for the board, 2 sheets for House Cards, Season Wheel, quick guide, 1 sheet for moveable tokens. The land remembers what you chose.

—description from the designer

boardgame: Відгадай локацію: Україна (Guess the location: Ukraine)
description:
Guess the Location of Ukraine is an educational party game that invites players to explore the beauty and diversity of Ukraine through stunning photographs. Each round presents an image taken somewhere in the country, challenging players to use their knowledge, intuition, and observation skills to determine where the photo was captured. Along the way, players also learn interesting facts about each featured location, making the experience both entertaining and informative.

The game is designed for 2 to 6 players. At the start, a large map of Ukraine is placed on the table, and a deck of location cards (each featuring a photo) is shuffled. In every round, one card is revealed face up (without showing the answer on the back). Players have one minute to study the image, then secretly place their colored marker on the map where they believe the photo was taken. Once all markers are placed, the correct location is revealed along with its coordinates and a short fact about the place.

Points are awarded based on accuracy: players who guess the exact spot receive 3 points, those who choose an adjacent space (horizontally, vertically, or diagonally) earn 2 points, and players within the next ring of spaces receive 1 point. The game continues until all cards are used or a predetermined number of rounds is played. The player with the highest score at the end wins.

Mano a Mano – THE FIGHT is a fast-paced, tactical two-player duel card game inspired by martial arts dynamics.
Each player selects an asymmetric fighter defined by Flow, Mind, and Structure, then secretly chooses cards representing attacks, counters, submissions, and tactical shifts. Cards are revealed simultaneously, creating tension, mind games, and momentum swings every round.
Victory depends on timing, resource management, and reading your opponent — not luck, but decisions under pressure.

—description from the designer

boardgame: Scrap Jacks: Salvage and Survival in the Big Empty
description:
You’re not here to clear the site.
You’re here to get paid—and get out.

Scrapjacks is a cooperative skirmish game about deep space salvage crews searching unstable wrecks, abandoned stations, and failed facilities until the risk outweighs the reward.

Pressure builds. Hostiles emerge. Hazards multiply. Mistakes compound. 
Every hull is a payday. But staying too long gets crews killed.

Clearing a site is never the objective.
Getting out on your own terms is.

_____________________________

Scrapjacks features:

     Crew-focused cooperative play
     Procedural escalation
     Hostile environments
     Extraction-centered objectives


Scrapjacks supports both one-off jobs and optional extended operations. Crews are never required to push deeper into a site, and each Level is treated as its own contracted operation.

This isn’t a game about heroics or conquest.
It’s about competent crews making hard calls in places that don’t forgive mistakes.

What You Need to Play

     Miniatures suitable for sci-fi skirmish play
     Modular interior terrain or tiles
     Dice and basic measuring tools


—description from the publisher

CHROMA is an abstract strategy game for two players on a hexagonal grid. Two teams — RGB (Red, Green, Blue) and CMY (Cyan, Magenta, Yellow) — compete using omplementary color capture.

Players take turns placing one of their three colors on empty hexes. A piece can capture an adjacent enemy if it is its complementary color: Red captures Cyan, Green captures Magenta, Blue captures Yellow. When a player forms a triangle with all three of their colors adjacent to each other, that area becomes permanent territory.

The game ends when the board is full or both players pass. The player with more territories wins. There are no dice, no cards, no hidden information — just pure strategy built on real color theory.

—description from the designer

Pizza Beats is a funny body percussion and rythm game for small and big groups. The players can either download the app which plays the beat or say "1, 2, 3, 4" to keep in time. 
All the pizza quarters are shuffled and 4 are put face up on the table. On each pizza piece, there are symbols which tell the players what they'd need to do. Eg. if there is salami on the pizza piece, the players have to clap 2 times. 
The youngest player starts. If he manages to go round 5 times, he wins a pizza token. The player who is the first one to have 8 pizza tokens, wins the game.

Object of the Game: Collect property cards, and try to complete sets. Use action cards to collect rent, steal properties, and more. Be sure to stash money in your personal Bank for when players go after you! The first player to have three different complete property sets wins!

What’s different about Monopoly Deal No Mercy?
You can do into debt.
When you owe another player money and can’t pay, give them a debt chip. On your next turn, must pay them back by giving them a card. That counts as one of the three cards you may play on your turn!
Nothing is safe.
From complete property sets to the cards in your hand, everything can be stolen in the blink of an eye.
Action Cards are more ruthless!
They got a mean makeover-charge double rent and even force someone to give away all the cards in their Bank!

Every player gets 5 cards and puts a mouth piece in his mouth. He tries to read one of his cards. If he manages to make the other players understand him, he can discard this card. The player who is the first one to have no more cards wins the game.

In this game, children learn how to protect nature and how to sensibly handle garbage.

On their turn, the player moves their meeple towards garbage in their respective player colour. If they reach garbage in their colour, they "pick it up" with their pawn. The meeple can only carry one piece of garbage at a time, so it has to return to the bin and put the garbage into the bin.

The first player who collects three pieces of garbage in their bin wins the game.

boardgame: NASTRACK HEAT: Stock Car Solo Racing
description:
Welcome to NASTRACK HEAT where stock car racing is taken to somewhere new and exciting bringing all the action-packed thrills of racing to your table, it’s close to watching it live. The raceway is a linear track where the cars change positions along the race lane. The game uses an innovative system between player cards and BOT action cards in a turn sequence, with use of a D20 die, in most cases, determining the success of slipstreams, overtakes, wrecks/cautions, defensive, spinouts, and more. The game comes with accurately portrayed raceway cards, which you choose prior to each race, which will contribute to the excitement of racing on actual tracks, Talladega for instance, modifying die rolls for slipstreams, overtakes, wrecks, and spinouts. The pilots in the game all have been assessed and attribute DRM modifiers applied to their cards for slipstreams, overtakes, wrecks, defensive, and pit crew. There are fourteen well known pilots covering different eras in NASCAR that constitute seven different teams, yes, you will control one team the best you can with how you play your cards, all about positioning and the points awarded at the end of the race. One Game of NASTRACK HEAT involves two races and the team with the most points at the end of the second race takes home the CHAMPIONSHIP.
Get Ready for Some of the Most Exciting Fast Action NASCAR Racing Yet!

—description from the designer

Looking for a Publisher.

Conflicto is a fast-paced, procedural miniatures wargame. 
Every army is unique: generated by a seed string on the Alessar Games web app, no two forces are ever the same. 
Command your units, maneuver your legions, clash across dangerous terrain, and shape a story that changes every battle! Creative freedom, endless tactical possibilities, and a new experience every time you play!

What’s in the Current Edition?

     Procedural armies: one seed, one army, total uniqueness!
     Expanded rulebook with clarified mechanics.
     Scenarios


1. What Makes It Unique?
Conflicto is a fast-paced miniatures game of tactical skirmishes and emergent storytelling. Every army is procedurally generated using a seed string: same string, same army; change the string, and a completely new force appears. Units evolve, armies adapt and no list is ever fixed. With flexible mechanics, Activity Tokens, Panic Tokens,  each match becomes a dynamic sandbox of strategy. True replayability comes from procedural generation, modular rules, and the limitless variety of armies!

2. What to Expect on the Table

     Procedural Army Generation: each seed produces a unique army with units, weapons, and traits. Check publisher page online.
     Flexible Action Token System: units perform actions using Activity Tokens (ATs). 
     Panic Tokens track morale and disorder, influencing unit behavior.
     Multiple Scenarios: from pitched battles and broken columns to sacred objectives and hazardous terrain.
     Solo or co-op play using NPC armies generated procedurally.


Whether you want fast, brutal clashes or long, strategic campaigns, Conflicto adapts to your table. Each battle is a narrative, each dice roll a chance to rewrite history.

3. What You Need to Know

     Model count: 5-20 models per player.
     Miniature scale: Any! It is miniature agnostic.
     Board size: 60x60 cm or larger.
     Game length: 45–75 minutes.
     Players: Any number; solo and co-op possible.
     Components required: Standard D6 dice, tokens or counters, measuring tool for inches/centimeters, miniatures for units, access to the Army Generator web app. Printable army list inside, you can write the generated ones on it!


4. Modularity & Expandability

     Fully miniature agnostic; use any fantasy, kitbashed, or custom miniatures.
     Ideal for hobbyists, storytellers, and tactical wargames fans. Casual gamers will love it!
     Army generator supported via Alessar Games Official Web App.
     Low entry cost, endless replay value.
     Content will increase in time!


About the Artworks – No AI involved. All illustrations are based on public domain sources, hand-modeled, or manually rendered. Alessar Games had previously explored AI-generated art, but all current visuals are 100% AI-free.

—description from the publisher

In Doxa (meaning “Glory” in ancient Greek), you take control of an ancient city-state and shape its destiny by influencing its fundamental pillars: agriculture, engineering, population, military, and economy.

Strategy and cunning are the keys to victory in Doxa!

The goal of the game is to defeat your opponent through economic, scientific, civil, or military victory. Each player has a board representing their city with various parameters: population, wealth, engineering, food, science, and military.

Each turn, you will improve your city by acquiring cards representing various buildings that will affect the parameters of your city or those of your opponent.

Each card has a mandatory “maintenance cost” and effects when played, so it's up to you to balance your deck management so that your city doesn't descend into chaos or your opponent's army invades it.

Observing your opponent's city and strategy is one of the keys to victory.

boardgame: Battle of the Bismarck Sea WD
description: Battle of the Bismarck Sea is a solitaire wargame that uses individual ships and flights/squadrons of aircraft.  The Player assumes the role of General George Kenney, Commander of the 5th U.S. Army Air Force, with the mission of intercepting the Japanese effort to reinforce its ground forces on the island of New Guinea.  The Player must utilize the limited resources available and then determine how best to apply them within specific time constraints.  The results of these efforts will be borne out in the effectiveness of air operations.

This latest expansion introduces a diverse array of new and hazardous locations in deep space where you can conduct your battles, adding a fresh layer of strategic depth to your games. These new environments are designed to push your tactical skills to the limit, offering both opportunities and challenges that can drastically alter the course of a battle.

Whether you’re playing solo or in a standard player vs. player match, these rules add a new dimension of unpredictability to your games. Solo players will especially appreciate the added challenge and immersion these environments bring, turning each mission into a thrilling survival experience.

To further enhance your battles, this supplement also includes two brand-new ships: a colossal mining vessel designed for extracting resources in the most dangerous of areas, and a specialized survey vessel built to explore and analyse these perilous regions. Both ships are featured in two exciting new scenarios, providing you with fresh content to enjoy and test your tactical mettle.

While Expanse of Perils is tailored for Stars & Lasers, the rules and scenarios are versatile enough to be adapted to any other spaceship combat game with just a few tweaks.

—description from the publisher

You are a commander in the midst of a Great Conflict! 
Muster your group of soldiers and lead them through wars from the Napoleonic to the post-WWII era.
 A fast, agnostic, minimalistic skirmish wargame where your squad's stats and equipment are generated directly from your personal data (name, birth date, and place). Featuring hidden orders, unique stat-based combat, and optional campaign systems, Patria is perfect for history buffs and players who love emergent, challenging list-building!

1. What Makes It Unique?
Patria is a minimalistic, totally agnostic skirmish wargame covering eras from the Napoleonic Wars to the post-WWII period. Player-Driven Roster: Soldier stats (Aim, Vigor, Courage), weapons, and inventory are uniquely generated from your personal name, surname, and birth date/month. Core gameplay uses hidden, simultaneous order assignment, making every turn tense and unpredictable.

2. What to Expect on the Table

     Personal Data Roster: Your stats and gear are uniquely tied to the player's identity.
     Hidden Orders: Secretly assign orders (e.g., Shoot, Run, Hide) for simultaneous resolution.
     Stat-Based Combat: Compare your soldier's Aim against the target's Vigor using a single, universal table.
     Stress & Morale: Use your Courage stat to pass crucial Stress Tests and manage the cruelties of war.
     Medals: Earn Medals by performing specific actions to spend on critical re-rolls.
     Optional Campaign System: Booty of War and Map Campaign rules allow for tracking progression, losses, and recruitment between games.


3. What You Need to Know

     Model count: 2 main soldiers + 1-3 Unknown Militants per player.
     Miniature scale: Any! It is miniature agnostic.
     Game length: Variable (scenario/player count dependent).
     Players: Any (Solo, Coop, or Free-for-All).
     Components required: D6 dice, measuring tool, character trackers, secret order tokens.


4. Modularity & Expandability

     Compatible with any scale of historical/generic miniatures.
     Includes an optional "Constructed List Format" for traditional point-budget list building.
     It includes new modules like ALIEN INVASION, HELLISH WEAPONS, ZOMBIE INVASION and MUCH MORE!
     Solo Play Support: Rules for Solo Play and NPC Patriots with algorithmic behavior.
     Supported by a Web app and social media.


About the Artworks - The rulebook illustrations are based on public domain images. Alessar Games had previously explored AI-generated art. All visual elements are now 100% AI-free.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Morgue Stars: Smuggle Till You Die ! Definitive edition
description:
If you’re reading this, it means the signal bounced off a hyperspatial communication buoy... Morgue Stars is a fast-paced, asymmetric procedurally generated sci-fi skirmish game of smugglers, chaos, and absurd gear combos. Artificial characters play with cards, Organics roll dice. Build hybrid crews, loot strange equipment, upgrade your ship, and dive into a fresh narrative after every battle.

From 1 vs 1 smugglers games to a full RPG-like Galaxy! And you can also blow up entire planets...

What’s New in the Definitive Edition (3rd)?

     Miniature agnostic: use any models you want.
     Over 140 pages with rewritten rules, improved layout, and a new preface.
     New item category: Relics, plus expanded Tools and equipment options.
     New smuggler type: Phantom, adding a chaotic rock-paper-scissors twist.
     Full Base and Ship Management system with rooms such as Med Bay, Pocket University, Cantina Bar, Orbital Weaponry, and more.
     RPG Mode included, fully compatible with skirmish play.
     New Action: Restrain, plus spaceship chase rules.
     Expanded scenarios and long-long-long-term campaigns. 


This is the most complete version ever: more content, clearer rules, and endless ways to create your own Morgue Stars experience. And also more lore!

1. What Makes It Unique?

     Asymmetric engine: Artificial smugglers use cards and Organic ones roll dice. Cyborgs mix both for unpredictable tactics.
     Demons! They use letters (check GMG review for this)
     Hybrid crews and freedom of design: customize characters with items, character fusions, pets, and weird gear. No classes, no limits.
     Roguelike loot generation.
     Scalable gameplay: quick one-shots or long campaigns with ship upgrades, bases, logistics, and evolving characters.
     Dual system: includes a fully compatible RPG Mode, so you can play skirmishes or run full pen-and-paper sessions with the same ruleset.


2. What to Expect on the Table

     Super tactical!
     Over 100,000 gear combinations. Tons of optional content.
     Violent or pacifist scenarios, factions, holidays, NPC chaos, and post-battle stories that reshape your crew every time.
     New Relics, the Phantom smuggler type, new facilities, Momentum Knights, The Cove and head-hunting missions.
     A full RPG Mode with one-shots and campaign material
     A Time Machine ready to use if your last mission was a DISASTER!
     Bigger, sharper, and completely customizable.


3. What You Need to Know

     Model count: 1–4 per player (up to 24 if you includes extra optional Contractors).
     Miniature scale: Any! It is miniature agnostic.
     Board size: 60x60 cm or larger.
     Game length: 45–75 minutes.
     Players: Any number; solo and co-op possible.
     Components required: Standard D6 dice, poker card deck, tokens or counters, no measuring tape needed (you can use it if you want btw)!


4. Modularity & Expandability

     Fully miniature agnostic: use any sci-fi or kitbashed miniatures
     Modular ship system for evolving campaigns
     Ideal for hobbyists, storytellers, and sci-fi fans
     Procedural generated, so you will always discover something new!
     Official support web app on the Alessar Games website: click and generate you starter squad! IT IS FREE!
     Low entry cost and extremely high replay value. HUGE, a true labour of love 


—description from the publisher

boardgame: Minutes to Midnight: The Board Game
description:
Minutes to Midnight: The Board Game is a negotiation game for two to six players. Each player takes on the role of an evil mega-corporation with a secret plan to destroy to world (and one back-up plan). Each player additionally starts with one billion dollars in seed money and six pawns to place in areas of vital influence: Technology, Agriculture, Weapon production, and more. Use your pawns to collect resources in these industries, or move them up through voting to the higher layers of power, eventually entering the secret Inner Circle and gaining access to forbidden technologies.

Once in the Inner Circle, you can activate and reveal your Master Plan to try to end the world! However, the other players get two turns to try and undo your plan.

Seems straightforward, but everyone else is trying to do the same thing, with different hidden plans. Rise quickly and unleash your plan first to end the world.

—description from the designer

boardgame: Transorient Express: Festival International des Jeux de Cannes Promo
description: A promo card given out at the Cannes International Game Festival featuring the festival venue itself. It is purple and can be any number. Playing it allows you to draw 1 Luggage card and adds 1 to your hand limit.

Coin! Qui Osera? ("Who whill dare?") is a card game where you try to get rid of the points in your hand and must assess whever or not to stop the round.

The deck has 6 suits of 14 cards: 10 numbered cards (worth their number in point value) and 4 "corner" cards: the 1-, 2- and 3-corners cards are each worth 12 points and the 4-corner card is worth 15 points. The corner cards are consecutive to the numbered cards, and as such the 1-corner card functions as an 11 when used in runs.

There are also -1 and -2 cards as well as a joker card which is worth 20 when still in hand at the end of the game.

All the player begin with a point capital of their choosing. Each round, the dealer chooses to deal a hand of 6 to 9 cards to every players. On their turn, players must either call "Coin!" to end the round or discard cards forming either sets of cards of the same value or runs of cards. Should they choose the latter, they must first draw a card from the deck without looking at it or the top card from the discard, but they wont add it to their hand until after they have discarded their card(s).

If they call "Coin!", the round stops. If the player calls "Coin!" has the least amount of points in their hand, all other players lose as many points from their capital as the value of the cards in their hands. Otherwise, the player who called "Coin!" loses as many points as the biggest hand among all players.

The game ends when one player loses all their capital. The player with the biggest capital wins!

There is an advanced mode where you can gain back capital with special combinations (discarding cards of a single color, calling "Coin!" without even discarding a single card, etc).

Bluff King Game Rules

1.Overview

Bluff King is a classic bluffing card game for 2-8 players. Players take turns playing cards face down and making claims about their rank. Other players may either call bluff or keep adding cards. Claims can be true or false: outplay everyone, empty your hand first, and become the Bluff King.

2. Components

- Deck setup: 1 or 2 standard 54-card decks (including Jokers), chosen in game options.

- Wild cards: Both Jokers are wild and can represent any rank from A to K.

3. Objective
Be the first player to get rid of all cards in hand and win as the Bluff King.

4. Setup

Shuffle and deal all cards as evenly as possible among all players. Randomly choose a starting player with the lead.

5. Turn Structure

Each round follows these phases:

1) Play and Claim
The player with the lead:
Plays any X cards face down to the Play Area.
Declares a claim in the form: "X x Y" (where Y is any rank from A to K).
The claimed rank does not need to match the real cards. Bluffing is the core of the game.

2) Challenge Phase
Starting from the next player in turn order, each player decides whether to call bluff.

No one calls bluff -> proceed to Add Cards Phase.
Someone calls bluff -> reveal the most recent played set and resolve:

Result    |   Who takes cards in Play Area    |   Next lead
Claim is false (bluff)    |   Claiming player    |   Challenger
Claim is true    |   Challenger    |   Claiming player

After challenge resolution, the new lead starts a new round.

3) Add Cards Phase

If no one calls bluff, starting from the next player in turn order, each player may:

- Add and claim: play X cards face down and claim "X x Y". Here Y must match the current round rank, while X can be any legal number. The adding player becomes the new claimant, and the game immediately returns to the Challenge Phase.
- Not adding cards: skip this opportunity.

4) Round Settle

If all eligible players choose not to add cards:
All cards in the Play Area are discarded.
The last player who played/adds-and-claims keeps the lead and starts the next round with a freely chosen new rank.

6. Win Condition

At the end of each challenge phase, the game checks victory:
The first player with 0 cards in hand wins immediately as the Bluff King.

All other players are ranked by remaining hand cards (fewer cards rank higher).

Important: even if a player appears to have emptied their hand, victory is only confirmed after challenge-phase resolution. If that last play is challenged and proven false, that player takes the Play Area cards and the game continues.

7. Strategy Tips

- Timing your truth and lies: choose carefully when to make honest claims and when to bluff.
- Pressure reading: watch each opponent's habits and challenge patterns.
- Risk scaling: as the Play Area grows, failed challenges become more expensive.
- Use Jokers wisely: wild cards can swing critical moments.
Track hand counts: keep watching each player's remaining cards, especially the lowest hand-count player, and pressure them with targeted challenges.

Explore an exciting chaos with 400 picture cards in this fun game! Each player gets a secret hiding place and 30 seconds to find gadgets that describe their place. Can your fellow players guess where you are? Everyone rummages through the cards at the same time and then the gadgets are passed around. If you get it right, you both get points! A fast-paced and clever game for 3-6 players, perfect for family and friends.

Y2Killed is a choose-your-own-adventure murder mystery RPG with multiple endings. The killer is determined based on the choices players make and the objectives they complete.

It's set in a world with jocks, goths, nerds, space witches, and maybe vampires. Oceanside High School may look normal, but it's secretly packed full of every supernatural, sci-fi, and fantasy troupe of the late 90's.

Andy Smith, the most popular kid in school, is throwing the first big party of the year and, like, everyone is going to be there. There will be secret notes to pass, public arguments to play out, and lots and lots of juicy gossip! Be there, or be an L-7 Square. Whatever, loser.

—description from the designer

This no shuffle card game gives you a chance to twist fate in your favor by utilizing your 9 Omens. Don't lose your HAND on Reality!

Each player will pick 1 "team" of 9 cards. This will be their hand. If they are forced to discard and cannot, they will lose.

Players can do one action per turn. Actions are, play a card or pull a card back from discard.

Players can then attack or twist their omens. Combat is like Yugioh and Twisting allows the change of position between attack and defense.

—description from the publisher

Lucky Saloon is a Wild-West card game where the goal is to be the last player alive.

Setup:

     Each player starts with life tokens and a few item cards.
     Shuffle the item deck and bullet deck.


On your turn:


     Draw cards.
     Play item cards (heal, steal, protect, etc.).
     Choose a player and shoot by drawing a bullet card.


If the bullet is real, the target loses life. If not, nothing happens.
When a player’s life reaches 0, they’re out. Last player alive wins.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: VITA: Born in 836 – Rise of the Vikings
description:
In VITA - Born in 832 - Rise of the Vikings players explore the life of characters in a historical period.

Born on the coast of Denmark: A youth marked by harsh winters and clan feuds leads to a life of exploration and conquest. Travel across the seas or rebuild your homeland. Answer the call of the ancient gods or establish a dynasty in a foreign land under a new one.

In this thematic strategy game, 2-4 players build up the biographies of their characters using events they obtain from a common "Tree of Life". The goal of the game is to gather the most happiness.

VITA is played throughout 3 Chapters:
In the YOUTH, characters are customized by their first life events and obtain an Ambition - a goal for their life.

In the ADULTHOOD, characters compete with each other over valuable events and resources like wealth and fame.

In the OLD AGE, previously gathered score cards are scored for everyone together - every player tries to gain more bonuses than the others.

—description from the designer

On your journey, play board games with the animal travelers staying at the same inn!

Living room or bedroom? Where would you like to play tonight? Step into the 5x5 grid of the simulated inn, move around, observe, and gather your companions to start your favorite game.

It tests not only your memory and judgment but also sometimes your teamwork. A flash of inspiration, plus a little luck, often holds the key to victory!

In a warm and relaxing atmosphere, a journey of encounters, choices, and playing board games together quietly unfolds.

•••

在旅途中，和住進同一間旅宿的動物旅人一起玩桌遊吧！

客廳or房間? 今晚想在那裡玩?走進模擬旅宿的 5x5 格場地，移動、觀察，找齊同伴一起打開你最想玩的那套遊戲。

不僅考驗記憶力與判斷力，有時也需要彼此合作。你的靈光一閃，再加上一點點運氣，往往就是勝負的關鍵！

在溫暖又放鬆的氛圍中，一段有關於相遇、選擇與一起玩桌遊的旅程，悄悄展開。

boardgame: Mega Empires: The Far East – North
description:
The Far East - North is an empire-building strategy board game for 3 to 5 players, set in East Asia. In this game, players lead their historic empire as they gain victory points by expanding their population, building cities, trading and developing their knowledge and culture. Notwithstanding its large scope, the game's core mechanics are straightforward and easy to learn. A key element in the game is player interaction as they position themselves on the map board and search for valuable commodities during interactive trading sessions.

Gameplay
Each player starts the game with a single population token on the board. As the game progresses, population will expand, spread over the board, fight some border conflicts and build cities. Cities bring resources and wealth, expressed in trade cards. The more cities players manage to build on the board, the more trade cards they receive. Trade cards are mainly valuable commodities, but sometimes turn out to be disastrous calamities.

Trade
Trading is the heart of the game, a social experience where all players interact with each other to optimize the value of their sets of commodities. In addition to the desired commodities, calamities are secretly traded as well, adding an additional layer to the trading dynamics. Once the trading phase has ended, players that end up with a calamity in their hand will suffer its effects.

Develop Civilization Advances
When the smoke of trading and resolving calamities has settled, players can decide how to spend the wealth gathered through trading. The game includes 45 different advances that can be developed, each of which provides benefits in the game to its holder. For example, Horsemanship enhances movement over land, Urban Planning facilitates city building, and Spiritual Guidance reduces the impact of calamities.

The 45 Civilization advances are divided into the categories Arts, Crafts, Science, Civics and Religion, each of which has specific powers. Each advance acquired by a player provides discounts to other advances that can be purchased later in the game, thereby creating a path of strategic decisions. The ultimate advance in the game, The Pinnacle Temple, is only in reach for players who manage both their trading and purchasing strategy very well.

Winning the Game
The players who have best developed their position on the board whilst avoiding making enemies and defended their empire against natural or civic calamities and stand the test of time, will eventually gather the most victory points and compete for wining the game.

New Components and Game Mechanics
Mega Empires: The Far East introduces new components and game mechanics compared to Mega Empires: The West (2019), Mega Empires: The East (2021):

    Rivers are printed features on the map that facilitate movement of tokens along them.
    Pirate ships are placed on the board at the start of the game. They block ship movement unless tribute is paid, but they can also serve as mercenaries for attack or defense, creating interesting new dynamics on the board.
    Walls can be placed on the board when acquiring The Great Wall, which prevent opponents from crossing boundaries between areas separated by a wall and thus provide excellent defense to your empire.
    Events are new effects triggered when moving the turn marker on the A.S.T. They generally apply to all players and introduce various balancing effects.


Combining with Mega Empires: The Far East - South
Mega Empires: The Far East - North can be combined with its counterpart Mega Empires: The Far East - South (2027) to scale up the game to as many as 10 players! Where North has a strong focus on the land masses of East Asia, South combines the continent, islands and abundant waters in South East Asia, which stimulates movement by ship. Both games were designed as balanced counterparts, albeit with unique characteristics.

—description from the designers

boardgame: Zombicide: Mission C47 – Coup de Grâce
description:
Designed by players for the Kyoko mission contest, and later published by the original studio, C missions are supplementary missions released online for print and play.

C47 is a Hard difficulty exploration mission for Toxic City Mall and Prison Outbreak.
Pressure takes its toll and can break the bravest men. A survivor is shooting everything in sight, put him out of his misery and rescue his own teammates from a desperate end!

Designed by Michael Baxter

A two-player strategy game played on a board reproduced from archeological finds in Pompeii. The object is simple: create a row of four pegs. On each turn one may place or move their pieces on the board. However, special pegs assigned to the outer ring can lock the positions of pegs on a spoke of the wheel-shaped board. It's a trickier challenge than one might think at first glance.

—description from the designer

Answer the call. Push Deeper, pay the price, outplay your Rivals, & claim the Crown, before the Island claims you.

Beyond the edge of every honest map lies Treasure Island.
An island of jungle and ruin. Of forgotten temples and buried gold. Of pirates who vanish,  or return as legends.

You are rival Captains chasing that dream. Each Voyage you sail closer. Each step inland tempts fate. Turn back early, sail away and keep your coins. Push deeper, and grow rich.

But beware. The seas grow harsher. Supplies grow costly. 
The Rising Tides spare no one. The Island remembers. 
It remembers greed, bravery, and cowardice.
Only one Pirate Captain will command the waves.
Only one will be crowned Pirate King.

Each Voyage is a round of pirate poker with shared danger.

The Island reveals five shared cards, one in each Island Zone (Shoreline, Jungle, Ruins, Cavern, and Treasure Chamber). 
These are the same as community cards in Standard Texas Hold’em Poker. Each Captain holds two hidden Crew Cards (your hole cards).

At key moments, all players simultaneously and secretly choose to either Push Deeper or Sail Away.

If you Push Deeper, you must pay the Rising Tides cost.
If you Sail Away, you preserve what you have and live to sail again.

If you reach the Showdown, you form the best five-card poker hand from seven total cards:  your two Crew Cards and the five Island Zone cards.

There are two chests.

The Treasure Chest is the main prize, won by the best hand.

The Scavenger Chest is the runner-up prize for the second-best hand, plus driftwood, salvage, and the spoils of caution.

Both matter. The Island makes sure of it.

—description from the designer

Shapes of the States is a STEAM-focused tabletop educational game designed for 1-6 players or students ages 12 and up. The game teaches players about U.S. geography with a unique right-brained approach to identification. The game further tests players knowledge of state size, population, and date established.

The 30-45 minutes of gameplay revolves around players selecting and identifying state cards by their shape (silhouette/outline) printed on the back of their chosen card. They place their card in a network of state cards on the tabletop that have already been played, but only score points if their card is placed adjacent to, or on top of, another state card with which it shares an actual border in the United States.

Players score points (1-12) based on which of the three scoring tokens was placed on that card: Size (square area, not including water), Population (2024 census reports) and Date Established (year, and sometimes month and day) that statehood was granted.To encourage new or young players, the game includes an element of luck with wildcards, some worth more than the state cards (like Alaska). After all cards have been drawn, the player with the most points wins.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Mega Empires: The Far East – South
description:
The Far East - South is an empire-building strategy board game for 3 to 5 players, set in East Asia. In this game, players lead their historic empire as they gain victory points by expanding their population, building cities, trading and developing their knowledge and culture. Notwithstanding its large scope, the game's core mechanics are straightforward and easy to learn. A key element in the game is player interaction as they position themselves on the map board and search for valuable commodities during interactive trading sessions.

Gameplay
Each player starts the game with a single population token on the board. As the game progresses, population will expand, spread over the board, fight some border conflicts and build cities. Cities bring resources and wealth, expressed in trade cards. The more cities players manage to build on the board, the more trade cards they receive. Trade cards are mainly valuable commodities, but sometimes turn out to be disastrous calamities.

Trade
Trading is the heart of the game, a social experience where all players interact with each other to optimize the value of their sets of commodities. In addition to the desired commodities, calamities are secretly traded as well, adding an additional layer to the trading dynamics. Once the trading phase has ended, players that end up with a calamity in their hand will suffer its effects.

Develop Civilization Advances
When the smoke of trading and resolving calamities has settled, players can decide how to spend the wealth gathered through trading. The game includes 45 different advances that can be developed, each of which provides benefits in the game to its holder. For example, Horsemanship enhances movement over land, Urban Planning facilitates city building, and Spiritual Guidance reduces the impact of calamities.

The 45 Civilization advances are divided into the categories Arts, Crafts, Science, Civics and Religion, each of which has specific powers. Each advance acquired by a player provides discounts to other advances that can be purchased later in the game, thereby creating a path of strategic decisions. The ultimate advance in the game, The Pinnacle Temple, is only in reach for players who manage both their trading and purchasing strategy very well.

Winning the Game
The players who have best developed their position on the board whilst avoiding making enemies and defended their empire against natural or civic calamities and stand the test of time, will eventually gather the most victory points and compete for wining the game.

New Components and Game Mechanics
Mega Empires: The Far East introduces new components and game mechanics compared to Mega Empires: The West (2019), Mega Empires: The East (2021):

    Rivers are printed features on the map that facilitate movement of tokens along them.
    Pirate ships are placed on the board at the start of the game. They block ship movement unless tribute is paid, but they can also serve as mercenaries for attack or defense, creating interesting new dynamics on the board.
    Walls can be placed on the board when acquiring The Great Wall, which prevent opponents from crossing boundaries between areas separated by a wall and thus provide excellent defense to your empire.
    Events are new effects triggered when moving the turn marker on the A.S.T. They generally apply to all players and introduce various balancing effects.


Combining with Mega Empires: The Far East - North
Mega Empires: The Far East - South can be combined with its counterpart Mega Empires: The Far East - North (2027) to scale up the game to as many as 10 players! Where South combines the continent, islands and abundant waters in South East Asia, North has a strong focus on the land masses of East Asia. Both games were designed as balanced counterparts, albeit with unique characteristics.

—description from the designers

Bonehead is a trick-taking game played using a normal set of 28 dominoes. It takes inspiration from traditional games like Schafkopf and Skat, but offers new twists on play.

Every domino is counts as its "lighter end": the side with the smaller number. For example, a 6-1 is worth 1 point, and a 3-0 is worth 0 points. There are therefore 56 points total in the deck of tiles. Players will bid how many points out of 56 they think they can capture. The player with the highest bid becomes the declarer, and gets to announce the game type and trumps.

The trumps announcement can either be "Doubles Only" in which only doubles are trumps, from 6-6 as the strongest down to 0-0 as the weakest; or a trump suit game, where the declarer will announce a trump suit (0 through 6). In the trump suit game, the highest trumps are still doubles, but are followed by all tiles of the announced suit.

Players play out the hand, trying to collect valuable tricks. At the end of the hand, players add up the points in their collected tiles. If the declarer got at least as many points as their bid, they score points equal to their bid. If they failed to do so, the defenders instead get points for the failed bid. Additionally, regardless of whether the declarer reached their bid, all defenders score any points in their won tricks individually.

—description from the designer

Our planet faces enormous economic, social, and environmental challenges.
The United Nations has called upon the world to achieve the Sustainable Development Goals — a global effort to end poverty, promote prosperity and well-being for all, and protect the planet by addressing climate change.

In [b]ODS: Jogo de Cartas[/b], each player takes on the role of a world leader who must make crucial policy decisions to help achieve the Sustainable Development Goals.

Players must acquire Decision Cards and attach them to corresponding Issue Cards in order to resolve them and earn progress points.

On their turn, a player may perform two actions chosen from the following:
a) Draw a card from the Decisions display, possibly optimizing their choice to gain extra cards.
b) Buy a card from the Issues display.

When an Issue has three attached Decision Cards, it is considered resolved and will be worth 5 points at the end of the game.

There are also Goal Cards, which may grant additional points during or at the end of the game.
language_dependency: 2: Some necessary text - easily memorized or small crib sheet

description:
In Sky 5, players push their luck as they build a tableau of colorful sky cards, trying to assemble one of each of the five colors to complete a Sky 5. Each turn presents a simple but tense decision: draw a card to grow your tableau, or roll the die to try to bank the cards you already have.

Drawing cards helps you work toward the perfect five-color collection, but it comes with risk. If you reveal a color you already have, the card may end up helping your opponents instead. Rolling the die offers a chance to secure your points, but only if the result does not match any value in your tableau. If it does, you bust and lose the cards you were hoping to score.

Quick to learn and full of nail-biting decisions, Sky 5 is a lively push-your-luck game where timing is everything. Build the right collection, roll at the right moment, and bank your points before the sky turns against you.

—description from the publisher
family: Organizations: Game Designers of North Carolina

description:
Council of Serpents is a manipulative tug-of-war game with hidden objectives. Three noble Houses are vying for power, but you are not one of them... You are a member of the Council of Serpents, and manipulate these Houses to achieve your own ambitions.

You scheme to orchestrate the triumph or collapse of one of these great Houses by manipulating their three attributes; the strength of their army, the amount of gold in their coffers and their popularity with the people. If one of these attributes ever reaches the top of its track, the game is immediately over, and that House triumphs. Whereas if an attribute reaches zero, the game is also immediately over, and that House collapses.

You achieve this by playing Influence cards to cause Conflicts, escalate Conflicts into Wars, prepare for these Wars by playing facedown Intrigue cards, make Peace and orchestrate support from powerful Guilds. After playing an Influence card, you enact the top card of the timetrack, often causing the Houses themselves to act. When the timetrack runs out of cards you collectively build it anew, shaping the plans of the Houses for the turns to come.

—description from the publisher

Apou! A Game of Cunning and Strategy!

Between nighttime battles, raging elements, and the sneaky maneuvers of rival families, you’ll need to sharpen your claws and fangs to claim your share of territory. A game of bidding, bluffing, and hand management…

Dive into a wild adventure where every card played can change the course of the game!

In a world inhabited by clever animals, families clash through tricks, duels, and unexpected surprises! Prepare to face storms, sudden maneuvers, and the twists brought by chaos cards.

Use your cards to disrupt your opponents, change your objectives during play, and find the best strategy to gain the upper hand. Your choices — bold or mischievous, might just lead you to victory!

Accessible from age 7 and up, Apou! promises laughter and playful mischief for the whole family.

So, are you ready to sharpen your claws and have fun conquering your share of territory with Apou!?

— description from the publisher

Alea Iacta Est
“The dice have been thrown."
Three says, 216 possible outcomes… will you achieve your goal?
216 recaptures the essence of classic dice games and combines it with a modern twist. You must meet the challenges presented by the Objective Cards, while managing strategic decisions
that makes each game unique, dynamic, and exciting.

—description from the designer

Alea Iacta Est
“los dados han sido lanzados”
Tres dados, 216 resultados posibles… ¿lograrás alcanzar tu objetivo?
216 recupera la esencia de los juegos clásicos de dados y la combina con un giro moderno. Deberás cumplir los desafíos que proponen las Cartas de Objetivo, mientras gestionas decisiones estratégica que hacen que cada partida sea única, dinámica y emocionante.

—description from the designer (Spanish)

In a group of thief mice, decide whether you’ll become the Leader or a Member and steal cheese together.
The amount of cheese you get depends on your role!

However, you won’t always get the role you want.
Fights may break out over who becomes the Leader — or you might end up being forced into the role instead…

And if you steal too much cheese, the Cat Police might arrest you!?

Who will play the smartest, avoid getting arrested, and collect the most cheese of all?

boardgame: Mrs. Brown's Boys: The Ultimate Feckin' Party Game!
description:
A hilarious party game featuring the voice of TV's number-one domestic goddess, Mrs. Brown.

A board game like no other, with acting, drawing, speaking and guessing games.

Contains audio timer featuring Mrs. Brown's dulcet tones that lets you know just how rubbish you really are!

Break tradition this Saturday; stay in with friends and play a great game... on second thoughts. . . nah... . . . feck off!

—description from the back the box

This store has changed.

Presenting the first large expansion set of Night Flowers, the 9.91 million yen Kickstarter Engine Build Game, featuring only 4 different races. With the introduction of new characters with special effects and new resources "Chaos", you can enjoy more dynamic games.

"Nightflowers" is a nightclub where girls of different races work. You are one of the store’s leaders.
The contraindications - taboos - imposed by the four newly hired daughters of different races, has turned the security of the shop completely desolate.
Choose a confrontation to restore the former elegance to the store, or will you take advantage of the confusion and become chaotic yourself?
The future of the store and the cast in your charge is left to your judgment ...

Product Features:
1. Presenting 4 different ethnic daughters with strong personality. Use special effects to create a unique cast.
2. Use the newly added resource "Chaos" to perform "Taboo Actions"! Experience the exhilaration of "doing whatever you want" by taking double actions, substituting resources, and more!
3. Counter players who violate taboos at will by "accusing" them!
Eliminate Chaos, restore dignity to your shop, and earn rewards!

Panda Royale Mayhem Expansion Set includes three new expansions that can be added in to the base game one at a time or all at once. The expansions in this set can also be mixed and matched with expansions with the original Expansion Set.

The Mischief Panda dice allow players with the highest roll to force an opponent to re-roll one of their dice.

The Cannon Panda dice are eight sided dice that always multiply by 10 but can only be used once.

The Mercenary Panda dice multiply by the round number but can be lost when they match the roll of the captain die.

There are 10 mischief panda dice, 10 cannon panda dice, 9 mercenary dice, and 1 mercenary captain die for a total of 30 dice in this expansion.

—description from the designer

Join the revolution with Crystals: The Card Game! Designed for friends and/or family; this card game is fun for adults, educational for kids, and promotes critical thinking for all players.

In each round, players draw from the coin cards deck and use coin values to buy crystal cards. There are also Hold cards in the coins deck that can help your cause. But beware! There are also Action cards in the coins deck that may help OR hurt your cause. Be the first to buy all 12 crystal types to win the round. The player with the lowest score at the end of game play wins!

Ideal for family game nights and friendly gatherings, this card game is suitable for ages 5+. Family card games for adults and kids also make great travel games and go-to games for game night and family time. It is also a fun gift for teens, adults, and kids who love card games, family card games, board games, and even dice games because of the fun and competitive similarities.

boardgame: The Worms Beneath: An Adventure for Four Against Darkness
description:
The Worms Beneath This is an adventure for Four Against Darkness — a dungeon crawl through a collapsed castle and a maze of tunnels, to discover the reason why Castle Marrath collapsed and the fate of the nobles who lived there. Includes 6 new magic items, 12 new spells (for druids and wizards), 18 new monsters. Suitable for a party of any level.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Twisted Doors: A Collection of Magic and Unusual Doors for Four Against Darkness
description:
Twisted Doors is a zine for Four Against Darkness with a complete system for random generation of the doors in a dungeon. From doors made from tied up branches to metal doors, doors laden with magic and mechanicals traps, and unusual magical doors that demand several types of tithes, doors take on a completely new light with this supplement.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Twisted Polearms: Magic and Unusual Polearms for Four Against Darkness
description:
Twisted Polearms is a generator with a huge d100 table with 100 different polearms (and a javelin), ranging from training weapons to spears designed for tunnel fighters, with a huge variety of magical polearms. For each weapon, there is a brief description, game stats and resale value. Suitable for all levels of play, the zine requires only the 4aD core rules to be used.

—description from the publisher

You are vampire bats and are happily kicking one bulb of garlic after the other into the old tower. Whoever gets rid of all their cards first wins the round and gets a super garlic bulb. The first player to get 3 super bulbs wins the game.
With a roll of the dice, collect treats for your dogs and gather cards. Whoever has the most cards at the end wins.

boardgame: KeyForge: Winds of Exchange – Archon Deck
description:
Winds of Exchange is the sixth KeyForge set and the first published by Ghost Galaxy! Winds of Exchange features hundreds of new cards, including the new Traders of Ekwidon faction and introduces a major new mechanic in the form of token creatures!

The product is comprised of one sealed pack containing one fully-playable 36-card KeyForge deck!

—description from the publisher

Akimbo is a drawless connection game for two players: Black and White. It is played on the intersections (points) of an initially empty square grid (board). The top and bottom edges of the board are colored black; the left and right edges are colored white.

A naked diagonal is a pair of like-colored, diagonally adjacent stones with no other like-colored stone adjacent to both. A crosscut is a 2×2 area with two interlocking naked diagonals of opposite colors.

Black plays first, then turns alternate. On your turn, place a stone of your color on an empty point. If this completes a crosscut, remove your other stone in the crosscut. There must never be more than one naked diagonal of each color on the board — not even momentarily before removing a stone.

You win if, at the end of your turn, there is a chain of orthogonally connected stones of your color touching the two opposite board edges of your color.

To make the game fair, White will have the option, on their first turn only, to swap sides with Black instead of making a regular move.

—description from the designer

boardgame: Mage Knight: Emergence – The Portal to Power
description:
Whether you’re discovering Mage Knight for the first time or adding to a long-standing collection, Mage Knight: Emergence delivers meaningful new adventures while preserving the core gameplay that made Mage Knight a modern classic.

Mage Knight: Emergence expands the world of Mage Knight with an all-new standalone release that is fully compatible with existing Mage Knight content. It’s the same deep, strategic experience fans love, now with new heroes, new challenges, and a streamlined path for new players to enter the game.


     Meet two powerful new Heroes. Darraz Firerock and Evinya Freegrove join the fight. Both are bound to the mysterious Council of the Void and walk very different paths. Darraz is a resilient, forceful hero built for players who favor strength and calculated power, while Evinya is a master of stealth and archery who rewards precision, mobility, and decisive strikes.



     Perfect for new AND experienced players. Mage Knight: Emergence - The Portal to Power has everything 2 players need to start a game, and expands to 3-4 players with other Mage Knight products. It features three guided scenarios that help ease new players into Mage Knight, along with 5 new full scenarios packed with strategy. 



     Enjoy expanded content for endless strategy. This new standalone game builds on the legacy of one of the most respected strategy games. It offers a true entry point for new players and meaningful new content for longtime fans — find new heroes, new challenges, and a streamlined path for new players to enter the game! 


—description from the publisher

Form as many flowers as possible by assembling tiles as you draw them during the game. Make sure your flowers attract pollinators, and take advantage of other insects that, while they do not pollinate, still influence your flowerbed.

In Petals, players build a blooming flowerbed by placing tiles as they are drawn throughout the game. By carefully assembling petals into complete flowers, players aim to attract valuable pollinators. However, not all insects are beneficial—some affect the garden in unexpected ways, creating tactical opportunities and constraints.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Carnivore: A cataclysmic dinosaur skirmish game
description:
The End came from the Sky.

A star of fire split the heavens and shattered the Earth.
Smoke blotted out of the Sun.
Forests withered, seas boiled, and the great herds thinned to shadows.

Now every day is hunger.

Where once the plains shook beneath countless preys, only scattered carcasses remain.
Predators who once ruled alone now clash fang-to-fang, claw-to-claw, over the same dying quarry.

There is no balance. No order.
Only the last war of scale and bone.

Eat First, Die Last

CARNIVORE is a cataclysmic dinosaur skirmish game where predators battle for survival in the shadow of extinction.
Built on a fast, visceral D10 system, it delivers combat that is tactically deep but accessible—easy to learn, brutal to master. Unlike other skirmish games, players compete over prey driven by instinct rules—objectives that move, react, and fight back. Designed with a commitment to paleo-accuracy, every miniature and mechanic reinforces the raw, prehistoric struggle. 
With high-quality miniatures and a boutique, art-driven rulebook, CARNIVORE is as immersive on the table as it is on the page.

Standard games of CARNIVORE are small tabletop skirmish games played head to head, with two players guiding packs of predatory dinosaurs competing to hunt the same herd of prey. Players can also play solo, as the herbivore Instinct system functions as intended whether prey are hunted by one pack or many.

An extinction-level meteor has collided with prehistoric Earth, filling the atmosphere with a dark shroud that blots out the sunlight and kills the plants herbivores depend on. Beneath the looming dust, carnivores are forced to clash fang and claw over the same dying quarry.

You will strike at rivals and prey alike, struggling to outlast extinction itself, using tooth, claw, and sheer mass to win the fight. A carcass feeds only the victor. Stake your claim. Ensure you die last.

Choose a predator and accept the way it hunts. Will you wield the crushing presence of Spinosaurus, rely on the relentless pursuit of Allosaurus, or flood the field with Velociraptors racing to feed first?

Gameplay:
A turn of Carnivore is divided into:

     Drive phase,
     Movement phase,
     Fight phase,
     Panic phase.


Each builds tension but everything culminates in the Fight.
Position matters.
Every attack has consequences.

—description from the publisher

Pairloopa, the clever, competitive card game that’s easy to learn and exciting to play.

Here's the challenge: Find 4 complete words using just the 8 cards in your hand.

Each card displays one-half of a compound word. Example: Find a SUN card and a FLOWER card to create 'SUNFLOWER'. 
It’s a race to find the perfect pairing before someone else does! 
The first player to lay down 4 correct word pairs wins the round. 
Now, here's the exciting part, the Out of Turn Trade Rule. Before the next player starts their turn—before they draw a card—any other player may ask that next player to trade for the card that was just discarded.

If you want it, be the first to say: “Can I trade for that?”
Only the first player to ask gets the chance to trade, so be quick!

The player whose turn it is answers yes or no.
If they say yes, the asking player takes the face-up card and trades it with one card from their hand to complete the trade and play continues.

If they say no, the trade doesn’t happen and play continues.
One important note: If an out-of-turn trade helps you complete all four word pairs, you must wait until your turn to win the round. If another player wins before your turn, they win the round, and all other players—including you—add up your points for that round.

Designed for 2 to 8 players, this competitive game takes about 20 to 40 minutes to play and is suitable for ages 7 and up.

—description from the designer

In the known regions of Monsterra, trainers bond with powerful elemental creatures called Mobamon. Only the greatest trainer in the world will earn the right to claim the legendary Apex Stone. Teams of five clash across an elemental hex based map, battling through jungles, fountains, and objective zones in a race to fill their Mobadex first.

Mobamon is a 1 to 6 player tactical board game that fuses MOBA style teamfight combat with monster drafting, capturing, and evolution. Players select a team of 5 Mons, equip Items, and play Action Cards each round to move, attack, and outmaneuver opponents across a modular hex board. Mons can be evolved at the Nexus Tower using Evostones, captured wild Mons grant powerful spell like abilities, and Talent cards provide passive edges that shape each trainer's playstyle.

Each game is played over 7 rounds of two phases: the Equip Phase, where players secretly slot Items onto their Mons, and the Action Phase, where players take turns activating Mons to move, attack, capture, and push toward victory. Combat is resolved through cards where attackers play Action Cards to strike and defenders play Block Cards to counter, with Items triggering off key moments like attacks, blocks, and knockouts.

The first team to claim 5 Mobadex Entries wins. Entries are earned by knocking out an enemy Mon or by evolving a Mon at the Nexus Tower using Evostones, so victory is as much about smart evolution timing as it is about aggressive combat.

—description from the publisher

The Dodgeball Game lets you, the coach, control professional dodgeball teams in exciting matches, whether playing solo or against a friend, using team sheets and two standard decks of poker cards.

Each team has its own squad of six players, each with their own attributes and abilities. As a coach, you decide which players will throw the ball and who their targets will be. On defense, you choose whether to block, dodge, or go for a risky catch. Every time your players perform an action, each coach also plays a card to give valuable bonuses to their players. Cards are placed face down and then revealed simultaneously, creating tension with every action.

The first team to eliminate all six players on the opposing team is declared the winner.

—description from the designer

Destructor-Series3000 was a success, albeit an evil one. The power of D-S3K seemed insurmountable, as it forced the country to surrender to the evil scientists demands. The bad guys actually won... at least temporarily. It wasn't long before they fought amongst themselves, though. Sharing power never works. Greed... power lust... All the scientists ended up hacking into the indestructible robot against each other and broke it! ... all their evil glory undone, all the power wasted. With the robot malfunctioning, the authorities quickly subdued the scientists. Now, it’s the people's turn to have some fun. The scientists will be tried for their crimes against humanity in the most inhumane way possible... in a match to the death! The country only needs one genius to advance its technology. Is it right? Who cares! I’m rooting for the robot. Commence the battle!

D-S3K is a cutthroat area-control action-based boardgame where players are competing for points. Each round, players will take turns after choosing actions in secret. After revealing, certain actions cause D-S3K to cause chaos in different ways. It's up to the players to safely navigate droids through the arena's exits to score points, activate teleporters and more droids, or of course, join in on the destruction.

—description from the designer

Kitticicles is a punishing cooperative exploration game where kittens (players) attempt to find a lost pair of mittens and make it back home before they freeze to death.

—description from the designer

Sisters of Sarenhag is an adrenaline-rushed potion brewing game where players attempt to brew the most potent potions, while sabotaging their opponents’ potions. The game takes place over three rounds with three phases each. The Brewing Phase, where players are adding ingredients and casting incantations into cauldron in real time; the Bottling Phase, where players try to sell their potions, without exploding, for money; and the Sweeping Phase, where players prepare for the following round by cleaning-up their ingredients. At the end of the third round, players will determine who is the best witch around!

—description from the designer

Lamort City is a turn-based game of survival. Players take on the role of a group of survivors trying to reclaim a city ravaged by a mad scientist who has unleashed his army of science projects upon the city. Only by building up your squad may the survivors overcome this unbearable force of monsters.

Featuring a customizable game area, multiple game variants, and over a hundred different cards to improve your survivors, Lamort City is designed for replayability.

—description from the designer

Diceocracy: Power to the Pips expansion introduces two new Candidates entering the dicey world of politics with charismatic Personality Tiles! The expansion also includes the new Clone Factory District with silver "Clone" Demographic Dice, presenting new opportunities for the players! New eventful Political Stunt & Advertising Cards also await, and full support for majestic 5-player games with the new licorice black player color!

—description from the publisher

At the beginning of summer, mosquitoes swarm around the pond.
Fortunately, this is a feast for many animals!

The swift dragonfly, the placid frog, the fluttering swallow, the clever perch or the greedy pipistrelle will help you catch them.
As for the cunning heron, it could well come and spoil everything!
In Mosquitos, players move the animals (pawns shared by all) using their cards to catch the mosquitoes.
Each player takes a set of cards, in equal numbers, and selects one to reveal at the same time. The chosen animals are played in order.
Heron (1), Dragonfly (2), Frog (3), Swallow (4), Perch (5) and Pipistrelle (6).

If several players have selected a card with the same number, the first to play the animal is the player with the Heron token, or the player closest to it in a clockwise direction.
Each animal card performs a different action that affects the turn, but also the other players.

The game ends at the end of the 10th day and the player who has collected the most mosquitoes (points) wins!

-Translated description from the publisher's website:

Prohibition Heist: 1929 is a competitive strategy game set in Prohibition-era Manhattan. Players control rival gangs competing for territorial dominance, income, and influence through coordinated heists and public stand-offs.

Each round, players secretly assign actions before resolving simultaneous confrontations across the city. Controlling districts generates income and strategic positioning, but success increases pressure from rivals and the law. Stand-offs force players to choose between cooperation and escalation, creating tension-driven moments that shape the balance of power.

The player who builds the strongest criminal network while managing risk and retaliation wins.

—description from the publisher

For more than a decade after the Spanish Civil War, anti-Francoist guerrilla groups composed of combatants and civilians that supported the extinct Second Spanish Republic, maintained a rural and armed resistance across the whole Spain. Designed with historical accuracy and accessible mechanics (card-driven game, blocks, point-to-point map), the game allows players to explore the different political, military and social dynamics that marked this struggle in post-war Spain.

Guerrilleros is an asymmetric and strategic game for two players that recreates the uneven conflict between the guerrillas and the forces of the dictatorship. Despite the actions of these maquis or 'guerrilleros' began with the Civil War in 1936, their relevance and number rose after its end in 1939, reaching its peak activity on the years between 1944 and 1952 - right where the game is set.

The maquis player cannot expect a military victory yet it can slowly but seriously harm the prestige of the regime in an attempt to win the game. The guerrilleros will need to invest in their mere survival, remaining hidden, releasing propaganda, and launching calculated and timely attacks to strategic regime positions. In turn, the regime player must try to locate, isolate and eliminate all 'agrupaciones guerrilleras', the main and most organised guerrilla groups by displaying all its power across the whole country. Also, the moral of the guerrilla is another weak point the regime can use to defeat the insurgency. In sum, both sides will need to find balance between the military actions and a necessary investment on logistics, propaganda and other lateral factors that will play a key role in how their strategy evolves through the years. Should no victor raise from sudden-death conditions, the historical account, as seen from those that suffered the most (the civilians) will have the final say over who obtains the victory.
