description:
2 Giugno - Per libera scelta di Popolo ("June 2, 1946 - By the Free Choice of the People") is a fast-paced and intense game for two players that retraces the campaign for the referendum in which the Italian people, at the end of World War II, were called upon to decide the institutional structure of the nation, choosing between the existing Monarchy and the Republic.
The gameplay is card-driven: in the five rounds representing the five months leading up to the elections, the two players will manipulate events and public figures in favor of their cause, mobilizing voters towards different political parties and seeking the support of these same parties.

The game where every card is random, every pack is unique, and there is only one rule.

Draw a card, if you like it, keep it; if you don’t, draw a new card.

Each round the objective changes. The first round’s objective is to have the highest card. You’ll have to open the pack to see what lies ahead in rounds 2, 3, and 4.

—description from the publisher

A Cyberpunk Solo Card Game.
In a dystopian near-future, you are a cyberpunk hacker known as a ‘Runner’. 
The evil corporation, Chisei Psitech, specialises in brain enhancements and plans to extend its corrupt hold on the city. 
To stop them, you must hack into their corporate server and thwart their schemes before their cyber-defences can track you down. 
It is a race against time, with only one winner…
You begin with a deck of basic cards. On your turn, you buy and play cards to improve your deck and to hack into the corp’s network.
The corp’s network is defended by Platform Active Defences (known as Pads). You must defeat the pads to access their server and win. 
When you run out of cards in your deck, you shuffle the discards with any cards you added to make a new deck. This way your deck gets stronger and you will be able to defeat more pads and reach the server.
Meanwhile, the corp is trying to hinder you and track you down. You are protected from the corp by links in cyberspace. If the corp can trace your links, they will capture you and you lose.

—description from the designer

description:

The main gameplay resembles Clue with the addition of witnesses that have fixed spots on the game board and each have a solution card that can be viewed when visiting that spot on the game board. There are also event cards  with facts from the actual investigation tied to different mechanics that will interfere with your investigation as well as teach you about the real investigation and the different turns it has taken. The winner is the player that can name who did it, who ordered the hit and where the killer disappeared.

A board about sailplanes and flying them cross country. Each player gets their own miniature aircraft which has specific characteristics like the real model, on which it is based.
Each round the you can ether choose to gain altitude or glide further towords your next thermal or turnpoint.
You'll have to manage your altitude and carfully plan your flight path to get to the finish in the most efficient way possible.
This game has team tactics to fly together and many different weather boards so every game feels different.

On your turn you roll two dice and advance your pawn with the sum of the dice. Winner of the game is the player who first gets through the racing track. There are also green and red spaces on the track, where you get extra turns or you have to go backwards depending of the colour.

On the rules, there are some variants, which add more dice or use subtraction of the dice rolled instead of the sum. The box contains material for six games, so it's meant for the whole school class to play.

boardgame: Wrath of the Wyvern: Sons of Rivesk
description:
In the tempestuous Umbral Highlands, monstrous Wyverns rule the skies, and humanity is on the brink of extinction. In Wrath of the Wyvern, you are the last hope, a lone hunter tasked with reclaiming a dying world, one battle at a time.

Inspired by Scottish Celtic mythology, this brutal solo TTRPG puts you in command of every decision, every battle, and every choice that will shape the future of the Highlands.

Powered by our custom-designed Fury Engine, Wrath of the Wyvern replaces dice with a Deck of Fate Cards, ensuring every check, save, and encounter is fast-paced, strategic, and cinematic.

Forge your destiny and reclaim the Umbral Highlands!

HOW IT PLAYS: 
Wrath of the Wyvern is a solo tabletop roleplaying game played on a hexcrawl map of the Umbral Highlands. As you journey across forests, mountains, boglands, and villages, you will explore locations, complete quests, encounter enemies, and uncover narrative events.

Instead of dice, the game uses a Fate Card system to resolve events, saves, encounters, and enemy activations. Combat is handled through a deck of ability cards that you build and improve over time, spending Fury Points to unleash attacks, abilities, and tactical maneuvers against enemies and legendary creatures. As you progress and complete quests you will gain new Ability Cards and weapons.

Your ultimate goal is to hunt the Sons of Rivesk (Wyverns) that terrorize the Highlands. By exploring the land, completing quests, and gathering clues, you will eventually locate a Wyvern’s lair and face it in a Boss Battle showdown.

FEATURES:
-A Solo Journey
This game invites you to reclaim the Highlands solo! No need for a group, GM, or even dice; only you, your instincts, and our Fate Card system.

-Hunt Wyverns
Track, outfox, and battle legendary Wyverns in multi-phase boss battles using a tactical battlemap.

-Shape Destiny
80+ branching quests and narrative events inspired by Scottish legends & folklore. Every decision carves your legend.
   
-A Brutal Land 
Navigate hex-based survival, faction wars, and dynamic encounters in the grim, ever-changing Highlands.

-Forge your Hero
Master unique abilities, recruit allies, craft legendary weapons, and gain Arcane Powers.

-Rebuild Crag Keep
Restore the Order of Skyfire, upgrade your fortress, and develop weapons, armor, and defenses for the coming battles.

-Fight the Darkness
Face countless monstrous foes, from twisted Hags to colossal Wyverns. Each enemy adapts, and every battle is a test of wit and will.

—description from the designer

Players reverse bones on the drum to make all of them upper, being the first. Bi-dice (also called "bones") are the game special pieces. They are thrown and come to rest with equal probability in one of two states.

At the beginning the drum is empty. Each player has their own set of 8 bones on the ground. Pick up the bone to take the first turn. If you are holding a bone in your hand, place it in the upper position on any empty space. Otherwise, remove any of your bones from the drum, reverse all adjacent bones, move the bone to any empty space, placing it in the upper position, and then reverse all adjacent bones. If all of your bones already on the drum are upper, then pick a new bone from the ground and hold it for your next move.

In random mode (Wild Bones) whenever you place a bone during your turn, roll it first and place it as it lands.

Oyno Puzzles: Shuffle the bones and place 8 wild bones randomly on the spaces of the drum. Ensure that at least one bone is upper and leave the Center empty. To make a move select any upper bone on the drum and place it in the Center. Reverse all bones adjacent to the space you just took the bone from. Then move the bone from the Center to any empty space, placing it as an upper bone, and reverse all bones adjacent to that destination space. Continue making moves until all bones are upper. For the next level of difficult1y, add one more bone and start over.

—description from the designer

boardgame: Was ist Was: Entdecke Die Welt Quiz-Spiel
description:
What is what - discover the world
What does the sand look on Lanzarote? Who invented cotton candy? These and countless other questions from five categories, children aged 8 and over follow together in the cooperative board game. Animals and plants, history, technology, science and smart minds: the cube decides which theme area comes first. Question answered correctly? The decoding card helps to find the right solutions and information. The toy figure can continue to hike. And if the question is too difficult? Then different joker chips help. The ideal birthday gift.

—description from the publisher (translated)

Wie sieht der Sand auf Lanzarote aus? Wer hat Zuckerwatte erfunden? Diesen und unzähligen weiteren Fragen aus fünf Kategorien gehen Kinder ab 8 Jahren gemeinsam im kooperativen Brettspiel nach. Tiere und Pflanzen, Geschichte, Technik, Wissenschaft und kluge Köpfe: der Würfel entscheidet, welcher Themenbereich zuerst drankommt. Frage richtig beantwortet? Die Decodierkarte hilft, die passenden Lösungen und Infos zu finden. Die Spielfigur darf weiterwandern. Und wenn die Frage doch zu schwierig ist? Dann helfen unterschiedliche Joker-Chips. Ein tolles Quiz und Familienspiel mit Lerneffekt für Groß und Klein! Das ideale Geburtstagsgeschenk.

—description from the publisher

Marin ja Mikon liikennepeli is roll and move racing game with two different boards Mikon mikrorata and Marin ja Mikon liikennepeli. There are four slightly different rule sets for Mikon autorata and that game is meant for learning the symbols in the main game Marin ja Mikon liikennepeli.

Mikon mikrorata (Mikko's Go-kart race) is very basic roll and move game, where the winner is always the player who get's to the end of the track first. First set of rules is just roll and move, nothing more. Second set introduces the markings on the board which are * (reroll), 0 (lose your turn) and then plus or minus signs (1 to 5) to go forward or backward. Third set gives you direct conflict with other players (If you end your move on other players pawn they loose the next turn.). Fourth set gives you right to go either forward or backward with your dice roll.

In the main game (Mari's and Mikko's traffic game) you are walking through the city (with die rolls) and winner is the first player to get to other side of the city. There are multiple routes on the board and on the routes there are red squares. When you end your movement in the red square, you pick up card with road sign or some general advice how to behave in the city traffic. In addition there are same markings as on Mikon mikrorata on the cards and you must obey the symbols.

Lombard’s Labyrinth is a dungeon-crawling board game where you and other Champions descend into a twisting maze of monsters, loot, and peril. Fight terrifying creatures, collect powerful items, and race to be the first to defeat the final boss lurking at the heart of the Labyrinth.

How to Play: Players start in the top left corner and take turns drawing tiles from a bag and placing them adjacent to the room they just exited, gradually building the labyrinth as they explore. Movement is determined by each champion’s Speed stat, which also decides combat order when facing monsters.

Each playable champion has a set number of stat points divided between Attack, Health, and Speed, along with two unique abilities that shape their playstyle.

As you venture deeper into the dungeon, you may uncover a variety of tile types:

Monster Tiles – Defeat monsters to earn rewards. The rarer the monster, the better your odds of receiving powerful items.

Item Tiles – The discovering player immediately gets one free item.

Shop Tiles – Roll for a chance to acquire a random item, or a chance that one of yours gets taken.

Secret Passages – Travel quickly across the board between two of these tiles.

And many more tiles can be discovered within the labyrinth.

To win, players must make their way through the labyrinth, reach the final boss room in the bottom right corner, and defeat the boss.

Each boss has its own unique stats and abilities, just like the champions. However, every boss also possesses a special ability. These abilities affect the rest of the game. After the first player fails to defeat it, this new rule is permanently added for the remainder of the game, and each boss has their own unique rules, increasing the challenge for everyone.

Combat in the game is designed to be simple and fast-paced.

First, compare Speed stats to determine who attacks first. If there’s a tie, monsters and bosses always attack first.

When attacking, roll a D6:

1–3 or 6 – Add the number rolled to your Attack stat, plus any bonuses from swords or items. This total is the damage dealt to that enemy's health stat.

4 or 5 – You miss completely, dealing 0 damage on that roll.

This system creates tense and often hilarious moments. It’s entirely possible for both you and a monster to miss repeatedly—sometimes for five turns straight—building suspense with every roll before someone finally lands a decisive hit.

Lombard’s Labyrinth is designed to keep players at a disadvantage early on. This makes every item feel impactful, allowing for rapid scaling once you gain momentum. Finding the item you’ve been searching for is both powerful and deeply rewarding; while not common, it is possible to win the game without any items at all.

—description from the designer

boardgame: Dominion of Cavaliers and Roundheads: Fight Battles of the English Civil War Solo Game
description:
This is the Dominion of Cavaliers and Roundheads: Fight Battles of the English Civil War Solo Game. 
This standalone set of rules is built on the Dominion of the Spear solo game system with appropriate rule changes for the English Civil War. 
These are very very simple rules with armies of 3-7 units and a solo battle can be fought in 10-15 minutes. 
An example play through is included. 
There is a Quick Reference Sheet on the back cover. 
Use paper and pen (or model figures of any scale) as there is no measuring (grid used). 
Uses 2 six sided dice.

28 historical battles are included ready to play, each with a brief background, army lists, deployment, and brief account of what happened historically. The battles are:

1. Battle of Newburn 25th August 1640
2. Battle of Liscarroll (Ireland) 3rd September 1642                          
3. Battle of Powick Bridge 23rd September 1642                                                
4. Battle of Edgehill 23rd October 1642
5. Battle of Brentford 12th November 1642
6. Battle of Braddock Down 19th January 1643                                         
7. Battle of Hopton Heath 19th March 1643                                               
8. Battle of Ripple Field 13th April 1643
9. Battle of Stratton 16th May 1643
10. Battle of Adwalton Moor 30th June 1643                                                
11. Battle of Lansdown Hill 5th July 1643
12. Battle of Roundway Down 13th July 1643                                         
13. First Battle of Newbury 20th September 1643                                 
14. Battle of Winceby 11th October 1643
15. Battle of Nantwich 25th January 1644
16. Battle of Newark 21st March 1644
17. Battle of Cheriton 29th March 1644
18. Battle of Cropredy Bridge 29th June 1644                                         
19. Battle of Marston Moor 2nd July 1644
20. Battle of Lostwithiel 31st August-2nd September 1644                
21. Second Battle of Newbury 27th October 1644                               
22. Battle of Inverlochy 2nd February 1645                                             
23. Battle of Naseby 14th June 1645
24. Battle of Langport 10th July 1645
25. Battle of Stow-on-the-Wold 21st March 1646                                   
26. Battle of Rathmines 2nd August 1649
27. Battle of Dunbar 3rd September 1650
28. Battle of Worcester 3rd September 1651

Although the English Civil War is included in the Renaissance ruleset Dominion of Pike and Shot, this is a dedicated set of rules just for the English Civil War, and uses additions to the Dominion system released since Dominion of Pike and Shot was released a year earlier.

This is easily read on a tablet or mobile phone and can be printed as an A5 booklet - click booklet (and fit to page in printing options depending the program used). 
48 A5 pages.

—description from the publisher

WordLab is a creative word puzzle in which players fill a grid with words that match a changing set of ingredients. Using the provided formula, players search their vocabulary for the best possible words to place on the grid.

Points are earned for clever word choices, rare letters, longer words, and completing full rows. Every decision matters: do you aim for a long word, use a tricky letter for extra points, or complete a row to secure a bonus?

Note: WordLab uses a configurable puzzle generator that allows players to create an endless variety of new challenges.

—description from the designer

A push your luck game where you collect and eat biscuits. There’s a pile of tasty looking cards on the table. But hidden among the biscuits are some miscellaneous objects which have fallen into the tin…

Turn over cards to reveal either a biscuit or a non-biscuit. Biscuits might be chocolate, round, rectangular, or sandwich — or many of these together. Push your luck to gather more biscuits, but if you reveal a Lego or a leaf, your turn is over.

Each turn you’ll also turn over an EAT card, which tells you which type of biscuits you can eat. So will you try to collect chocolate biscuits or round biscuits?

Designed in collaboration with the Year 6 class from Wilson Primary School.

In Venice, rumors are spreading that the Doge is ill... With his iron grip fading, “La Serenissima” descends into a ruthless battle of influence, dirty schemes and vicious crimes !
Two dangerous figures are employing specialized agents (“Masks”) to fight each other and seize power... Playing as one of those figures, you must choose which strategy to use, when to bluff, and above all, how to guess and counter your opponent's “Masks”!

Gameplay:
L'ère des Masques (Maskera) is a strategic duel of bluffing and timing, where every decision and every card can turn the game around!
1. Draw (2 cards) OR play a card face down to try to gain the upper hand.
2. Your opponent can take a guess at what it is: if they fail, reveal the card, add it to your collection and use its power.
-> Four cards of the same color and/or one card of each color? Victory is yours!

----- FR

Alors que le Doge se meurt, Venise est en proie à une bataille d'influence et de coups bas !
Dans l'ombre, deux figures s'affrontent et utilisent leurs "Masques" (des agents : Assassin, Espion, Noble...) pour prendre le pouvoir !

Gameplay :
L’Ère des Masques est un duel de bluff et de stratégie où chaque décision compte !
- Jouez une carte (de couleur) pour tenter de prendre le dessus ; 
- Devinez celle de votre adversaire pour en contrer les effets.
-> 4 cartes de la même couleur et/ou 1 carte de chaque ? C'est la victoire !

Whacktown throws you into a neon-soaked city of scheming gangs and fast, punchy chaos. Build your criminal gang, seize control of locations across a randomly generated city grid, and settle scores with baseball bats, Hustle-cards, and brutal timing. The game leans into darkly comic gangster vibes — think chaotic, short rounds filled with memorable, often absurd stories.

Play moves fast and is easy to pick up: each round you Move (walk your gang and trigger the target location’s effect), Hustle (play as many Hustle cards as you like, but each one costs you other cards from your hand), then Chill (draw cards and pass the turn). 
Locations are access-controlled — some require money, information, reputation or health to enter — so positioning and timing matter. 
Damage effects usually hit *all* your rivals, armor acts as extra hit points that can be damaged and later repaired, and sudden reversals are common: if you slack off, someone might literally drop a bomb in your living room. The core mechanics emphasize access control, hand-management tradeoffs, and explosive “every-player-for-themselves” confrontations.

You win by becoming the most feared boss in Whacktown: either eliminate every other player or be the first to hit 10 Reputation. If you like quick, confrontational filler games with tactical depth, player interaction, and a heavy dose of gangster humor (and you don’t mind getting whacked a few times), Whacktown is the perfect late-night brawl.

This is a unique bluffing/gambling game in which the dealer squares off against all the other players, in a contest of wits around the content of just one hand!

Powderkeg is a bluffing card game for the Island Deck.  The Island Deck has six suits with nine cards each. The number cards are Ace (one) through seven, and the face cards are Queen and King. 
It’s unusual because the dealer plays the only hand!  
In Powderkeg, a hand is “dead” if it contains a number card and a face card of the same suit. Players wager and guess whether, and when, the dealer’s hand will die.
This is a gambling game, so each hand is an independent game. The object is simply to finish with more money than you started with.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Legendary: A Marvel Deck Building Game – Stan Lee Bystander Promo
description:
In 2015, packs of Marvel 3D trading cards contained playable cards for Legendary. All but one card, a Stan Lee Bystander, were reprinted in the 2019 expansion Dimensions. This makes Stan Lee the only gameplay unique card from Marvel 3D that cannot be obtained in another printing of the game.

A Bystander promo card featuring Stan Lee and the rules text:

"When you rescue this Bystander, say a hero name. Then reveal the top three cards of your deck. Put one of those cards with that exact Hero name into your hand. Put the rest back in any order."

The full list of Marvel 3D Legendary Cards is this:
Complete list of Legendary playable cards in the Marvel 3D trading cards:

Bulldozer Driver (special bystander, 1 card)
Double Agent of S.H.I.E.L.D. (special bystander, 1 card)
Fortune Teller (special bystander, 1 card)
Photographer (special bystander, 1 card)
Stan Lee (special bystander, 1 card)

Black Widow (same 14 cards as the base set, but with alternative artwork)
Deadpool (same 14 cards as the base set, but with alternative artwork)
Hulk (same 14 cards as the base set, but with alternative artwork)

Circus of Crime (new henchmen group, need 10 copies but all are identical)
Spider-Slayers (new henchmen group, need 10 copies but all are identical)

Howard The Duck (new hero, 14 cards total: 5 commons "Traveling Companion"; 5 commons "Rebel Without a Cause"; 3 uncommons "Right Place, Wrong Time"; 1 rare "Interplanetary Visitor"

Man-Thing (new hero, 14 cards total: 5 commons "Form from Ooze", 5 commons "Burn the Fearful", 3 uncommons "Travel the Nexus of Realities", 1 rare "Eternity of Solitude"

The Artwork for Stan Lee is by community member: Jalt

Today is casting day at Glitterati Palace! In this legendary place, drag lovers, influencers and celebrities gather together. The staff is nervously scurrying around... they all want to know what's going to happen today! Who will be the new superstar performing there every night? Contestants from all over the country have brought their best drag to show off for a hungry and demanding audience.

Whoever gathers enough talent to dazzle the audience and celebrities can make it - it's a battle for fame!

Federa is a drawless annihilation game for two players: Black and White. It is played on the intersections (points) of an odd-sized, initially empty square grid (board). Players have access to a sufficient supply of pieces in two shapes (disks and tiles) and two colors (black and white).

				
				
					DefinitionsA group is a piece along with all identical pieces one can reach from it through a series of steps onto orthogonally adjacent, identical pieces.

A group is bounded if there are no empty points orthogonally adjacent to it.

A piece covets an orthogonally adjacent group if it likes the color and shape of the pieces in the group. Black pieces like pieces of the other shape, black and white alike; white pieces like black pieces of their own shape and white pieces of the other shape.

				
				
					PlayOn your turn, place a disk or tile of your color on an empty point or convert a bounded group to the color and shape of the majority of pieces coveting it, provided that those coveting pieces are of your color. If there is a tie, only the owner of the group can convert it.

Passing is not allowed, but, if you have no legal moves available, your turn is skipped.

You win if the board is full of pieces of your color.

To make the game fair, White will have the option, on their first turn only, to swap sides with Black instead of making a regular move.

-description from designer

Amicitia is a finite territory game for two players: Black and White. It is played on the intersections (points) of an odd-sized, initially empty square grid (board). Players have access to a sufficient supply of pieces of two types (disks and tiles) and two colors (black and white).

A group is a piece along with all identical pieces one can reach from it through a series of steps onto orthogonally adjacent, identical pieces. A group is bounded if there are no empty points orthogonally adjacent to it.

On your turn, place a disk or tile of your color on an empty point or replace a bounded enemy group with a friendly one of the same type, provided that the majority of pieces of the other type orthogonally adjacent to it are of your color. Passing is not allowed, but, if you have no legal moves available, your turn is skipped.

When neither player can move, whoever has more pieces on the board wins.

To make the game fair, White will have the option, on their first turn only, to swap sides with Black instead of making a regular move.

-description from designer

Fano is a high-stakes strategy card game where you act as a conduit for history's greatest thinkers. Their ideas fight through you. Their legacies shape the board. Every action is permanent, every mistake compounds. Victory isn't about power. It's about proof.

Fano is a non‑collectible card game that plays like a TCG with chess‑level depth. Each player duels using one suit of a standard deck. Interactions are inspired by octonion multiplication: combat cards, values 1–7, form a seven‑way rock‑paper‑scissors system and can be used to attack, combine, and recruit other combat cards. You advance by conjuring prestige cards, values 8-10, through combining values on the field to create their sum in your hand. Collect all three to promote from Jack to Queen. Promote three times to win the duel.

—description from the designer

boardgame: Something Wild!: Five Nights At Freddy's – Security Breach
description:
Something Wild! Five Nights At Freddy's: Security Breach is a fast-paced, light card game for the whole family! Featuring an exclusive collectible Rockstar Freddy POP! figure, as well as your favorite characters from the Five Nights At Freddy's video games.

Combine Character Cards in sets and runs to score points, just like in Rummy. Use Power Cards to add a fun twist to the classic card gameplay.

Combine multiple Something Wild games to add more character cards, more Pop! figures, and more powers to your game!
family: Video Game Theme: Five Nights at Freddy's

description:
Zones is a tactical strategy game for 2–4 players built around perspective and positioning on a shared 8×8 grid board divided into nine zones. Players place pieces to form patterns within zones while anticipating how the board’s orientation will change from one turn to the next.

Each round, players are dealt a hand of coloured cards. On their turn, a player may spend cards to take one of three actions: place a piece into a zone, rotate the board to change the orientation of the grid, or remove an opponent’s piece from a nearby zone if certain adjacency and connection conditions are met. Because the cost to place or remove pieces depends on the rows and columns facing each player, rotating the board can dramatically change which moves are efficient or even possible.

At the end of each round, the board rotates again and dice determine which colored zones may award bonus cards for specific piece patterns. These bonuses can provide additional resources in the next round, encouraging players to build formations while disrupting their opponents’ plans.

A zone becomes locked once it is completely filled by one team’s pieces. The game ends immediately when a player or team fills two zones, securing control of them and winning the game.

-description from publisher

Talons & Treasure is played over 9 rounds. In each round, players will simultaneously play a single card onto a single location and capture treasure equal to the number they played. You begin the game with all 9 cards in your hand. Each round you will use one and discard it. Each of your cards will be used a single time each game.

At the end of the 9th round, the game ends, and players add up the value of their treasure.  The player with the most
points wins the game.

In Evens Up, each turn you draw a card from the deck and play it on one of four stacks following a strict ascending sequence, wrapping from King to Ace. Jokers act as placeholders and can be played on any unlocked stack, while one stack may be locked to preserve an even card. The challenge is to manage your cards and memory to ensure that, by the end of the deck, all four stacks display an even-valued card.

—description from the designer
The Explorer expansion adds an extra two colours. Upgrade your game to an epic 4-player showdown with Lunar an d Nova fleets, plus a carry bar to take your battles on the go.
Crash into the battlefield with the Nebula fleet, which comes in a mini Shape Invaders box!. This is a Kickstarter-exclusive expansion for Shape Invaders that allows to play with up to 5 players.

boardgame: The Awkward Storyteller: Episode 1 – Expansion Pack
description:
NEW STORIES & MORE FUN: Contains 100 new cards! Episode 1 contains 35 new Story Cards and 65 new word Question Cards.

CHALLENGE YOUR CREATIVITY: With bonus cards to add to the base game, you'll have endless hours of added pure fun!

BASE GAME REQUIRED: This is not a stand-alone game. You must have The Awkward Storyteller main game in order to have all components necessary for play.

STRAIGHTFORWARD PARTY GAME: For 4 to 11 players ages 16 and up. The format is certain to get participants smiling, interacting and thinking up original twists and plot lines for their story!

PERFECT FOR FAMILIES, TEENS, AND ADULTS: Perfect for a family get-together like Thanksgiving, Christmas, holidays, camping, parties, or simply have some quality and fun time with friends. A Premium quality, perfect gift!

—description from the publisher

This goodies pack adds a few additional classified documents to the game. As usual, players must cross out as many lines as possible on their card while leaving just enough information for their teammates to guess the secret document.

Shuffle these cards with the base game to add variety to your missions.

Promo pack for Opération Zèbre.

This goodies pack adds a few additional classified documents to the game. As usual, players must cross out as many lines as possible on their card while leaving just enough information for their teammates to guess the secret document.

Shuffle these cards with the base game to add variety to your missions.

It is a new tile laying game with a pirate theme where every player has 30 “Jumping Pirates Tiles” to play on the central board to generate points.
The central board starts with 25 tiles. The player has two possibles choices to play.

—description from the designer

A resource management, worker placement and order fulfilment game set in a pixel-art universe for 1 player or 2 players in a co-operative or competitive modes.

Choose a crew, select a ship, take your workerbots and plan your galactic strategy. Fly workerbots out to planets to create the resources you need to fulfil orders before the timers run out back on the home planet.  Process harvested items on your home farm.

Planets have representatives you can build relationships with to gain in-game buffs, but watch your back as the event driven system will see pirates try to capture them and take over the planets you need - increasing their influence, which is a lose condition of the game.

Fly daring rescue missions, fight off killbots and keep the population of your home planet satisfied, because if you lose too many orders you'll lose your galaxy farmer licence.

-description from designer

SWAT: First Response is a solo theme-heavy reprogrammable card tactics management puzzle game, with memory elements.

Move around a 10 room criminal hideout, using tactics cards that are revealed each turn to 'MOVE', 'PEEK', 'SEARCH' or 'SHOOT'.

Every turn, the player reveals the left most facedown tactics card. After the first round, the player now has the ability to reorganise the cards to meet an ever changing strategy.

This play continues until three different strength criminals have been taken down and two hostages have been found and rescued.

The player needs to make sure they have the right amount of movement, to do the right action at the right time. Accidentally shooting a hostage will see you busted on the spot!

-description from designer

As a new courier, it's your job to race around a city collecting packages and returning them to the depot to score points.

Only problem is, there's up to three other players doing the exact same thing in this 2 to 4 player madcap game!

Players secretly choose a numbered card from their hand and play it simultaneously, with the highest number going first. The cards have additional powers on them too

After all of the players have played their turn, the player who had the highest number then takes over the ROGUE courier in a take-that game of package stealing!

Players can fulfill secret missions to add points to their score at the end of the game.  The winner is the one with the most points (and probably now the least friends)!

-description from designer

Super tiny giant battles is a quick and easy rules light rank and flank wargame.

Inside this 24 page A6 rulebook you'll find the basic rules needed to build an army and get into a game of tabletop slaughter. 
Simple but full of tactical depth, you'll have to manage initiative and plan carefully to get the best of your opponents forces.

Also included are a host of optional rules that allow you to up the fantasy, with rules for powerful heroes that can bestow bonuses on your army, Warbeasts capable of trampling through whole armies, and territory based campaign rules allowing you to lead your super tiny giant army on a crusade of conquest.

Each copy includes an A6 rulebook, a template sheet, and 4 super tiny dice.

boardgame: Realm Tactics: A Modular Fantasy Wargame
description:
Realm Tactics is a comprehensive tabletop wargaming system designed for players who crave ultimate agency over their forces. Set during the volatile Arcane Age, this system balances deep strategic customization with a fluid, modern engine that ensures no two battles are ever the same.

Total Creative Freedom
The heart of the experience is the ability to build a truly unique fighting force from the ground up.

     Custom Unit Design: Move beyond rigid army lists; determine the strengths, weaknesses, and specialized training of every squad in your command.
     Dynamic Leadership: Every army is defined by its Hero, a central figure whose presence on the field dictates your tactical approach.
     Mystical Augmentation: Equip your units with rare artifacts that can shift the momentum of an entire engagement.


A Battlefield of Constant Engagement
Experience a system where every move requires a counter-move, keeping both commanders locked in a battle of wits.

     Reactive Activation: The system uses a back-and-forth flow that eliminates long wait times and forces you to adapt to your opponent's maneuvers in real-time.
     The Flow of Magic: Harness a volatile arcane system where power fluctuates as the battle progresses, allowing for well-timed spells—and the potential for your opponent to shut them down.
     Environmental Impact: Utilize the terrain and manage the psychological state of your warriors as the pressure of combat mounts.


Play Your Way: Solo or Narrative
Whether you are a lone wolf or a campaign veteran, Realm Tactics provides the tools to tell your story.

     Automated Opposition: The robust solo engine provides a challenging, logic-driven opponent that responds to your tactics without the need for a second player.
     Persistent Campaigns: Lead your warband through a series of interconnected battles where every loss carries weight and every victory brings your troops closer to veteran status.
     Strategic Logistics: Manage your treasury and resources between engagements to keep your war effort alive.


Includes:

     Core Rulebook: The foundational laws of battle and army construction.
     Solo Expansion (App+Rules): Dedicated rules for solitaire play and AI behaviors.
     Campaign Supplement (Rules): Rules for progression, injuries, and narrative tracks.
     The Realm Forge (App): Access to the digital unit-builder to save and print your custom creations.
     Faction Unit Rosters: 5 distinct unit roster JSONs (Human Kingdoms, Elven Colonies, Dwarven Holds, Unliving Hordes and Savage Tribes) to get started playing right away. Simply import, print and play!

RAH RACE is an impression-based party card game.

The game can be played in three ways:

1. Impressions Race – The player whose turn it is reads an impression prompt from one of their cards. All other players race to perform the impression. The reader acts as judge and awards the point to the player who performed it first. The player with the most points once all cards have been played wins.

2. Best Impression – The player whose turn it is reads an impression prompt from one of their cards. The other players take turns performing the impression. The reader acts as judge and awards the point to the best performance. The player with the most points once all cards have been played wins.

3. Laugh You Lose – The player whose turn it is reads an impression prompt and selects one player to perform it. All players must try not to laugh. Any player who laughs is eliminated. The last remaining player wins.

RAH RACE features quick rounds, simple rules, and lots of laughter.

-description from designer

boardgame: The Momentous Issue of War: American Civil War Square Grid Wargames Rules
description:
The Momentous Issue of War is a 8x8 grid-based tabletop game loosely based on the American Civil War, and that takes about an hour to play. The game is easy to learn and has a great amount of tactical depth, high-stakes decisions and dramatic narrative beats for players to enjoy.

Visually inspired by the traditional Kriegsspiel games, players use blocks to represent units with individual abilities, each conferring varying tactical elements to the gameplay. Different types of terrain encourage a strategic approach and the game includes varied elements, from brutal action to meticulous planning phases.

A unique activation system called the Tide of Battle enables players to feel the ebbs and flows of battle.

As players lose units they roll fewer and fewer activation dice making it harder for them to retain control over the battlefield.

We decided to go with an 8x8 grid for the board and for all gameplay measurements to be orthogonal (in straight lines). This means that evaluating distances for movements and for ranges of fire is much easier, making the game quicker to learn and snappier to play.

Units in the game are based on the type of unit one might find on an American Civil War battlefield: infantry, cavalry and artillery, and lastly the commander and his retinue. Each unit has different strengths and weaknesses.

Armies can be picked in secret and revealed to the other player simultaneously, creating an extremely fun way of starting a battle.

Blue (Union) and grey (Rebel) units are provided in the book and players can stick them onto any kind of block they wish. For playtesting we used simple wooden blocks we bought at the craft shop.

Building an army is inexpensive and players will field ten units per game. So this game is very portable and quick to get going.

Recommended deployment for a starter game.

     INFANTRY: Troops on foot fighting with rifles. High health points but slow moving and great firing at close range. 
     CAVALRY: Fast moving and have almost as much firepower as infantry but are more vulnerable with less health. Can disengage from close combat without a penalty.
     ARTILLERY: Artillery units have low health but are deadly when given a good shot. They have excellent range and benefit from abilities such as being able to fire over obstructions from height, and their cannon balls sometimes bounce dangerously. 
     COMMANDERS: Have the same strengths and vulnerabilities as cavalry units, but also confer special abilities to other units, such as bolstering the front line and supporting units that retreat.


We include rules for a varied mix of terrain types which one might have found on the battlefields of the American Civil War. 
The terrain effects in the game are chiefly: providing cover, obstructing line of sight and rough ground which obstructs movement.
By mixing these effects differently for the terrain types, the game provides surprisingly deep tactical opportunities on the battlefield, and ensures players can satisfy desires for both strategic gameplay elements and a pleasing aesthetic.
We include: forests, fields, hills, fences or walls, buildings, roads, rivers and bridges.
Maps are created with terrain placement using the traditional "I place a piece, you place a piece" method or by using our fantastic map generator.
The map generator has been meticulously created to provide a mix of terrain types with a semi-random placement, and avoids players being biased toward making their part of the map more favourable. Detailed and structured with steps, it's been an favourite part of the game to our playtesters. It appears that people just love the play element of building a map and watching the battlefield evolve and come to life.

The amount of text might seem daunting, but it takes you step by step, a lot of it is example text, and it's the most revised part of the book.
The game is a "I go, you go" system. At the start of each turn, players roll for the Tide of Battle, determining simultaneously how many units they may use that turn and which player goes first. One player then uses their whole army followed by the other player doing the same.
The Tide of Battle is one of the dramatic highlights of every turn. (And it's just fun to shout "The tiiiiide of battllleee!".
To conduct the Tide of Battle both players roll a die for each unit they have left on the board, count their activated dice, and the player who scores the highest amount of activations goes first this turn. 
Next, units are activated , which is a dramatic experience of choosing the units you think might be best used in this turn; with limited choices, every activation becomes a nail-biting affair as you bet on the outcome of the coming turn.
As the game wears on and units are lost, activations are fewer and tensions rise. Finally, if both players draw, you reroll, which adds to the tension and drama of this game phase.

Players roll the Tide of Battle and tensions build!

Each turn is broken into three simple phases:

     The Tide of Battle
     Player One activations
     Player Two activations


In a player's turn their units may either move, charge or disengage, followed by either firing or close combat. If you can break the enemy's sprit in combat and force them to flee you might get the opportunity to pursue them across the battlefield. 
Broken units will gradually flee toward your own board edge unless you rally them. However, doing so means using less of your ready units, so you'll have to make decisions between advancing your men offensively or keeping them together defensively, both tactics are viable options and usually players fall into one style of play very quickly.

Movement is simple: each unit has a few ways to move, and the distance moved determines what kind of attacks it may conduct.
In the movement phase units can:

     Move a number of squares depending on their unit type. 
     Walk into combat.
     Charge into combat.
     Disengage from combat.


Combat is brutal and effective and every single combat in the game has the potential to break the enemy. No unit is safe in this game, not even in turn one!

In the combat phase units can: 

     Conduct close combat. 
     Fire their guns. 
     Potentially break the enemy. 
     Pursue a broken enemy across the battlefield. 


In the final stage of a player's turn they can conduct rallies (bring their units back into play), followed by broken units continuing to retreat toward your board edge.

Commanders boost the moral of nearby troops, allowing for attack rolls to be rerolled, and make rallying fleeing troops easier.
However, just as the commander bolsters nearby troops, so to does his death affect them. Lose your commander, and every nearby unit will have to roll to stand their ground or flee!

The game ends after ten turns, plenty of time for a brutal clash of armies.

At the end, victory points are tallied as follows:

     5 points for every enemy unit you've destroyed.
     3 points for every enemy unit currently routed.
     1 point for every point of damage caused to a surviving enemy unit.


Don't be deceived by the appearance of victory! Your army with no casualties is no good to you if the majority of it flees the battlefield in face of a stalwart enemy!

As a company, our chief game design goals are realism, ease and fun. Games ought to be about having fun, and we aim to maximise the fun elements by making our games easy to play.

When it comes to realism we do our best to sprinkle historical nods and interesting ideas in all of our games.

Some of our other favourite company maxims (Written on our office walls):

     Ease versus agency: This is about balancing two key feelings we want to give someone who plays our games. For example, it means giving a player the freedom to use all the coolest attacks and manoeuvres, while facilitating that with easy game mechanics. We can't always do both, but it means that every game design choice is tested based on these feelings with the aim of maximising a players fun.
     Depth ≠ Complication: We believe at Wicked Wargames that a game can have depth without being complicated. Depth is about providing a player with options and making those options meaningful. If it involves keeping a score,  having to remember something or it can't be explained in two short paragraphs, it usually doesn't survive the cut. Complicated isn't fun.
     Simplify and reduce: We want to provide a rulebook that says exactly what it needs to and no more. It means simplifying every paragraph so every player can understand it,  and then cutting every redundant word.

Do NOT Upset Dave is a party game about a grumpy cat named Dave.

A player will take the role of Dave, drawing both question cards that everyone else must provide unique answers to, and rules cards that everyone must follow while providing their answers.

While Dave will provide Dave Dimes to the player with the best response, everyone who successfully answers without Upsetting Dave by breaking the rules will also receive a Dave Dollar.

General Manager Football (GMF) is a game which allows the player to create fictional American-style professional football leagues, plays games, have playoffs, and then conduct off-season activities that may see each team improve in some areas, decline in other areas, or remain constant from last season.

Individual football games are played through the use of Scoring Cards. The player reveals a scoring card for each team per quarter, and then adds up the scores of each team, thereby determining a winner and a loser. Teams are rated for their offensive and defensive capabilities, and these ratings can either allow a team to play additional scoring cards or cancel opponents’ scoring cards. Additionally, each team is rated for their head coach. Head coaches have the ability to influence the game score through Key Coaching Moments (KCMs). Over the course of a season, great coaches will have more KCMs to use than good coaches, who will have more KCMs to use than poor coaches.

Each team is also rated for their Front Office, which represents their organizational strength, from general manager to the scouting department and other key personnel. The Front Office rating will impact the teams’ ability to improve in the offseason Draft & Free Agency period.

-description from designer

Cursebreaker: Game Overview

     A Wizard Battle game that takes place in the Shattered Lands of Relicblade.
     1-4 players. 2'x2' play area.
     Strategic magic system that rewards cleverness.
     Exciting dice mechanic for casting spells and stealing your opponent's dice to grow more powerful.
     Uses Relicblade miniatures in a completely new rule-set with a completely new gameplay experience. Play more games with your existing collection!


Cursebreaker is an all new, stand alone game set in the world of Relicblade. Players enter the Shattered Lands as powerful magicians in search of greatness. As you cross the threshold into those cursed lands, the laws of reality are warped beyond recognition. A completely new rule-set governs reality as you and your rivals contend with feral magic.

Players enter the Shattered Lands as a learned magician with mastery over the eight mighty books of magic lore. Equipped with advanced learning and arcane will, magicians use a pool of power dice to interact with the surging whims of magic. The results of their dice allow them to cast powerful spells.

Players use the power dice on their lore books to cast powerful spells and summon deadly creatures from beyond the veil. Magicians must master the fickle flow of magic to acquire even greater power or be stripped of their power by hungry rivals.

Games of Cursebreaker are ultimately a contest of wit and will between competing magicians and the cursed lands themselves. In the end, only one battle wizard will emerge victorious.

Cursebreaker is a strategic game of magical mastery. 
Players cannot simply kick down the door and smash their way to victory. They must apply their unmatched wizardly intellect to outplay their opponents. At the start of each round, magicians roll dice to discover exactly which spells are at their disposal. Then during their activation, they spend those dice to cast spells that deal direct damage, lay sinister traps, and summon deadly entities.

Cursebreaker is less of a magical shootout, and more like a deadly arcane puzzle. Instead of attempting to cast spells and rolling to see if it was successful. Players know what spells are available and the spells are guaranteed to have the desired effect. With guaranteed spell effects and predictable outcomes, players must rely on their intelligence and foresight instead of looking to luck for an advantage. A skilled magician will see what tools are at their disposal, then lay a perfect path to victory.

Terrible curses ravage the land, and each scenario presents brutal challenges that the magicians must overcome. Cursed objects are repositories of feral magic. Be it the tomb of a hateful crusader or the severed head of a powerful necromancer, cursed objects channel feral magic to cast spells and summon evil creatures. As cursebreakers, the players strive to strip the magic from these evil objects and claim the rewards trapped within.

Cursebreaker features a seamless campaign system that adds depth and continuity to your arcane adventures. As players travel through the Shattered Lands, they will gain new insights into their own power, claim potent magical treasures, and gather brave champions to their cause. Cursebreaker runs wonderfully with two, three, and four players in the same scenario. Campaigns can be easily coordinated for game groups as players race to complete difficult Campaign Achievements and claim ultimate victory across eleven games.

Cursebreaker includes a solo campaign for magicians forced to walk the path of solitude. The forbidden Lore of Shadows has attached itself to the solo magician like a parasite. The doomed magician does not enter the Shattered Lands with lofty dreams of power or mastery. They are driven by the consuming need to break the curse upon themselves, or at least die where the shadow will be lost among the countless other deadly curses of the wastes. Enjoy strategic wizard adventures on your own time without relying on a gaming group's schedule.

Both Relicblade and Cursebreaker use the same miniatures! 
Cursebreaker is a spin-off game set in the world of Relicblade. It uses all of the same miniatures, but it is a completely new ruleset that delivers a completely new play experience.

Above the quiet glow of distant stars drift the broken hulks of forgotten industry. Structures hang in orbit, hollowed out by time, radiation, and the promise of wealth. Inside, crews of Boilers climb through rusted corridors and rotting decks.

Kosmika is a fast, brutal skirmish game of salvage and gritty void combat. Build a crew of three hardened Boilers and dive into high-risk missions against another player, or team up to battle the Hostiles guarding your prize.

The game is played on a 16x16 multi-level Structure. Descend from Topside into The Mids and finally into The Sink, extracting the most valuable Salvage before the rising Danger and the void itself closes in.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Fighting Fantasy Quest: The Warlock of Firetop Mountain –  Lost Levels Expansion
description:
The Lost Levels present a new entry area with new challenges for you to face as well as a complete new branch of the dungeon including new monsters from Gremlins to Trolls, boss fights with an angry giant, dungeon puzzles and a wealth of new items for hours of new adventure.

Not ready to leave Firetop Mountain behind? The Lost Levels Expansion adds up to 5 brand-new dungeon levels packed with over 100+ new cards, fresh encounters, and even deadlier challenges.

boardgame: Fighting Fantasy Quest: The Warlock of Firetop Mountain – Heroes of Legend Expansion
description:
The Sentinel can work miracles, manuplating dice rolls to snatch victory from the jaws of defeat or fill up your Armor Points, helping your front-row fighters tank the monstrous adversaries' unrelenting attacks.

The Dwarf Champion can navigate secret underground passages, appearing anywhere in the dungeon, though his short legs make it more fatiguing to move around normally. His attack can be bolstered by giving up Armor Points, making him the most powerful attacker in the game...for a price.

Both come with their very own starter equipment and a number of new items to add to your treasure deck as well as their own miniatures and hero board.

Adds in two new heroes: the Dwarf Champion and the Holy Sentinel. Each comes with their own character board, miniature, and starter item cards.

With the Pulse expansion for Harmonies, your landscape becomes more dynamic. It introduces additional animal cards with a variety of requirements, which will test your spatial organization.
The familiar mechanics are still present, but the decisions become much more demanding.

At the heart of the expansion are new three-color patterns, requiring more planning and anticipation in placement. Players who take on these new challenges will have additional scoring opportunities — but they need to think even more carefully about how to integrate each element into their landscape.

The beautifully illustrated Animal cards fit seamlessly into the base game while providing a new tactical boost.

—description from the publisher

Dystopian Wars is a tabletop wargame of naval battles that uses highly detailed miniatures to represent huge engines of destruction on, above and below the high seas. Set in an alternate late-nineteenth century called the Dystopian Age, super-science fuelled nations clash over resources and power.

Pick your faction and fight for dominance over the seas and skies with incredible machines of war powered by extraordinary technology - dread marvels of the Dystopian Age.

boardgame: My Salary Was Low, My Students Were Loud, and Now I've Been Summoned to Another World Where I Must Win Ritual Battles by Rearranging Ancient Norse Runes
description:
You have been summoned and put directly to work. You were given five sacred runes and a hand of Transposition Spells, and the gods are watching.

Each player holds Transposition Cards, you will use these cards to try to match the Ritual Incantation Cards displayed in the centre. The first player to claim five Ritual Incantations ends the game. Most Favour (points) wins.

—description from the designer

Град на Лъжите is a competitive party card game of bluffing, deduction, and sabotage set in a noir city where evidence disappears and rumors become weapons. Players build their own case by playing evidence for Truth points, while using action and deception cards to disrupt opponents and protect themselves.

On your turn, you draw and play a card: secure evidence scores immediately, double evidence scores more, and hidden evidence is placed face-down to be revealed later. When hidden evidence is revealed, it can increase your Truth - or backfire as a fake - adding risk and tension to every "safe" play.

Players can interrogate, reveal, swap, steal, block, set traps, confiscate, and forge evidence to change what is on the table. The game ends when a player reaches the required Truth total, successfully frames an opponent with enough fake evidence, achieves enough successful sabotages, or becomes the last player with cards.

—description from the designer

Score the most points by building the best hand. But here’s the twist: everyone starts with face-down cards and must slot new cards into their hand in ascending order, revealing their strategy one card at a time.

—description from the publisher

Dribble’n’Dice is a tactical football board game for two players.

Each coach controls a team of eleven players and tries to outplay the opponent through positioning, timing and smart decisions.

Players move across the pitch, pass, tackle and shoot, while dice and action cards create tension and risk.

The game focuses on space control, tactical choices and momentum.

You can find the Pre-Projekt on Gamefound.

After ten long years of war against the Trojans, the victorious Odysseus hopes at last to return home and reunite with his loved ones in Odysseus.

But the gods have decided otherwise.

On his journey back to Ithaca, a thousand trials await him: sirens, cyclopes, angry gods, and enchanting goddesses... With the help of Athena, guide and protect Odysseus through the obstacles. Each trial is a challenge, each victory a step closer to home.

Your choices will determine his fate.

Become the hero of the most legendary epic of all time.

—description from the publisher

Dive into this sliding puzzle challenge!
Fuji and the Great Wave - two forces of nature clash in an epic showdown! Slide any tile, move your pawns, and be the first to place all your pawns on your home tiles. Who will slide to victory?


     Move your pawns onto their matching colored tiles.
     On your turn, take two actions: move one of your pawns and slide any tile.
     The first player to move all of their pawns onto their home tiles wins!


—description from the publisher

Grab your water bottles, we're going on a hike! But this won't be a walk in the park. We'll only make it to the top if we work together.

Fernweh is a cooperative shedding game. Each hike is a mini-campaign divided into multiple stages. To clear a stage, the players have to overcome obstacles by playing cards and emptying their hands. The more obstacles you clear, the farther along you'll get to move.

Obstacles change the rules of the game or fill up your hands. If you ever fail to clear all obstacles before your deck of cards runs out, you'll lose valuable daylight. If you run out of daylight, you have to start all over again. So be careful!

But each time you clear a stage, you unlock new cards and add them to the deck.

—description from the publisher

In this clever card game you try to attract the most birds, because throughout the game and at the end you will score majorities!

Each round you can either take cards from the display or play those cards to get a bird.

Watch out for your opponents since they can quickly take away a majority, causing you get less points.

—description from the publisher

Mahabharat: War of Thrones is a strategic area-control board game inspired by the epic battle of Kurukshetra from the Indian Mahabharata. Players take the role of rival commanders competing to expand their kingdoms, gather resources, and prepare their forces for the final war.

Throughout the game players conquer Rajyas (kingdom territories), deploy soldiers, and build alliances or betray opponents to gain strategic advantage. Resource management and careful positioning are crucial, as players must decide when to expand their territories and when to strengthen their armies.

As the conflict escalates, the battle eventually shifts to Kurukshetra, where players engage in direct confrontations to determine the fate of the war. Tactical decisions, calculated risks, and timely use of special powers can change the balance of power.

The player who successfully dominates the battlefield and outmaneuvers their rivals emerges victorious.

—description from the publisher

Ramayan: Ek Veergatha is an adventure board game inspired by the epic journey of Lord Ram in the Ramayana. Players travel through different stages of Ram’s life, gathering powers, overcoming challenges, and preparing for the final battle against Raavan.

During the game, players collect Vardaan cards, gather Raavan heads, and gain the support of key allies such as Lakshman, Sita, and Hanuman. Strategic decisions and dice rolls determine how players progress through the journey and overcome obstacles along the way.

The characters in the game are represented by hand-painted wooden peg dolls inspired by the traditional Channapatna craft of India.

The game culminates in the final confrontation with Raavan, where the player who best prepares through the journey and overcomes the final challenge emerges victorious.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Zephyr of the Zirconium Zebras: A Narrative Campaign of Linked Mini-adventures
description:
Can You Hear the Wind of Change? Is it the zephyr of heroism that inspires your characters to journey across the world, or the crass lure of wealth? Zephyr of the Zirconium Zebras is a campaign of 20 short linked missions with several optional scenarios and an open ending. Will you save the Withering Warlord’s orc hordes from genocide or cleanse the Dorantian empire from their filth? Find a killer during a fancy party — Exorcize a ghost in a forlorn castle — Journey to the sun to find a magic flower — Retrieve a cursed book from a spire — Pressure a decadent noble into marriage ... and maybe steal from his exotic collection — Execute embarrassing witnesses — Clear up a mine of refugees for exploitation — Retrieve a reveler from moral dissolution — Free a gnome city from star invaders — Escort high priests to a religious conclave — Debate theology at a holy symposium — Assassinate rival clergy without getting caught — Summon a demon with word games — Reclaim crates from a business rival — Recover treasure from a non-Euclidean dungeon — Partake in diplomatic treachery — Delight in the joy of orc genocide — Escort refugees across a sea — Defend a fortress against legions This book has everything you need for an epic campaign, at any level.

—description from the publisher

Revisit the '80s, '90s and '00s in this nostalgic MTV trivia game! Answer trivia questions about general music knowledge, music videos and iconic MTV sessions to win pieces of the MTV logo. The first player to collect all 4 pieces and complete their logo is the winner!

18Wisconsin is based on 18Milwaukee published by the TraXX Magazin as a variant of 1846 Race to the West. In order to play 18Milwaukee game components of 1846 are necessary.
18Wisconsin uses the same map but the basic rules derived from 18Rhl-Rhineland. 18Wisconsin is focusing on the beer transport by railway from the Wisconsin breweries, concentrated around the area of Milwaukee. There are several deviations due to the functionality of the breweries and some unique features. The games is suitable for 2 to 5 players.

Fishbol is a 2-4 player light abstract game where players race to collect their favorite Philippine street food snacks. Place, flip, and move colorful food tokens on a 5×5 hex board to match patterns on your score plate and trigger fun sauce tile effects. With modular powers and  double-sided tokens mechanic, every game is a tense, strategic, and delicious challenge.

Gameplay Overview

On your turn, place a food token on the board, stir the pot by flipping or moving tokens, and draw new ingredients to refill your hand. Match patterns on your score plate to collect tokens and activate fun sauce tile effects that let you move, swap, flip, or even steal tokens—adding layers of strategy and surprise!

The first player to score 3 tokens on their score plate wins

boardgame: Here to Slay DUNGEONS: Molten | Overgrown
description:
Gather your party for a strategic role-playing adventure for 2–4 players. In this cooperative dungeon crawl adventure, you’ll explore dangerous terrains, roll dice to slay Monsters, and level up as you hunt for the Dungeon Boss.

Customize your Hero with synergistic Abilities and looted Items to cover your party’s weaknesses. Every choice matters, because the Dungeon fights back with obstacles, dangers, and swarms of foes. Work together to bring down the Dungeon Boss before your whole party falls, or your legend ends before it’s ever told...

This base game features Party Leaders from the original Here to Slay game as playable characters, along with some familiar Monsters from the 2020 release.

Exploration and InvestigationIn The Unseen World, you play a paranormal investigator traveling across Japan, visiting various towns, researching haunted locations, and trying to collect evidence of the supernatural. You’ll have to properly manage your resources and time while also trying not to succumb to fear during your investigations. Level up, gear up, and build your team of investigators!

				
				
					Card-driven GameplayThe game’s main mechanism uses a deck of standard playing cards split into two decks. Your deck, the Action deck, uses the 2, 3, and 4 of each suit plus one Ace at the beginning (you can earn more Aces as you level). You’ll use a hand of 4 cards against the Challenge deck, made of the 6, 7, and 8 of each suit with one 5.

A Check is made by playing a card, applying modifiers based on the situation, then optionally making a Push by adding the top card of the Action deck to your total. Meet or exceed the Challenge card to succeed.

				
				
					Endless Towns and StoriesTowns, locations, NPCs, and haunted backstories are generated using a series of tables, allowing for a near-endless variety of horror adventures. You’ll acquire various items and recruit team members from the NPCs you meet along the way during the game. Each campaign will have its own evolving story.

—description from the publisher

Sword and Board is a miniature agnostic tabletop medieval/fantasy skirmish melee game where teams of up to 5 miniatures battle in a last man standing team melee battle on an 8/11 inch board. 
The average game takes 30 minutes to play. 
We recommend using 28-32mm scale miniatures.

This PDF includes the following:

     Components recommended for setting up the board (ex. rulers, dice, 28-32mm miniatures)
     Instructions for assembling your team of fighters
     Turn sequence rules
     Roster sheets for recording team abilities/stats


Turn Sequence

     Initiative: The player with the highest roll gets to go first.


Activated warriors may perform the following actions, there is no particular action order required:

     Movement
     Combat
         Attack Rolls
         Saving Rolls
    


—description from the publisher

In this escape game, the whole family embarks on a captivating adventure in Rome! The breathtaking metropolis hides mysterious puzzles that must be solved to survive this enigmatic adventure.

Many secrets await discovery – requiring logical thinking, mathematical reasoning, and keen observation skills from every player! Only by working together as a team can you escape the enigmatic city, provided all the puzzles are solved correctly.

—description from the publisher (translated)

In diesem Escape Game begibt sich die ganze Familie auf ein fesselndes Abenteuer in Rom! Die atemberaubende Metropole verbirgt geheimnisvolle Rätsel, die gelöst werden müssen, um das mysteriöse Abenteuer zu bestehen.

Die vielen Geheimnisse warten darauf gelüftet zu werden – dafür sind logische Fähigkeiten, mathematisches Denken und die Beobachtungsgabe jedes Spielers gefragt! Nur als Team könnt ihr aus der rätselhaften Stadt entkommen, sofern alle Rätsel richtig gelöst werden.

boardgame: Rotation Rumble: A Pack of Indies
description:
In Rotation Rumble you take over the role of a guild coach, that hires and commands mercenaries in the weekly rumble fights for money, cheesecake and glory. Chose wisely which fighters, challenges and inventory you combine to form the best possible pack of reckless rumblers.

Rotation Rumble is played with a pre-constructed deck of squared cards, which have 4 different actions on each side (in rare cases there are just 2) which can be activated by rotating them. This allows for endless combinations, possibilities and threats.

The first player to 12 Victory points wins.

This deck was part of the initial sets Kickstarter and offered as a stretch goal.

In this deck, your goal is to discard your Fighters, profit from doing so, and use your Captain's ability to rotate your crew, buff them, gain VP, and build satisfying chains of effects.

Decklist:
- Mr. Bonez
- Reets
- Sink Scepter
- Passion Panda
- Fin
- Art of War
- Architecht
- Antidote
- Torchtot
- Bond Broken
- Arthon

Welcome to the next evolution of tabletop gaming. STCG (Smart Trading Card Game) is not just another board; it’s a dynamic platform where the physical thrill of card collection meets the immersive power of digital technology. At its heart lies a simple mission: to remove every barrier between you and pure strategic mastery.

Forget dice, tokens, and manual scorekeeping. Our patented smart board features an integrated NFC reader, a crisp segmented display, and responsive light and sound systems. When you play a card from the Ragnarok set, the board instantly recognizes it. Life totals update automatically. Thematic light pulses sweep across the surface. Strategic triggers and lane captures are signaled with distinct audio cues. The tedious math is handled seamlessly, leaving your mind free for what truly matters: the battle of wits.

Ragnarok, our inaugural game, is a deep and tactical area-control battle. Command gods, heroes, and mythical beasts in a clash over three decisive lanes. Build your deck from over a hundred unique characters and spells, exploring archetypes like Enhancement, Destruction, Fortification, and Lifedrain. Each card’s abilities can combo with others, creating endless strategic possibilities. The 5x5 ability matrix on every card provides clear, intuitive rules for placement and effect, ensuring depth without complexity.

But STCG: Ragnarok is only the beginning. This board is an open platform. The same hardware that brings the Norse apocalypse to your table today will, through seamless updates, host new worlds and stories tomorrow. We are already partnering with major IPs to expand this ecosystem, transforming a single board into your gateway to countless adventures.

This is more than a game. It’s an invitation to experience the tangible future of play. Join us as we build it.

—description from the publisher

Shoots is a trick-taking game similar to Spades with a Rock, Paper, Scissors theme.  Players hold 3 cards in their hand at all times, and each player takes a turn playing a one card aiming to win the book (trick).  After playing a card, players draw to replenish their hand.  Cards played must beat the current winner by either being a higher rank of the same suit (rock, paper, or scissors), or by being a card of a suit that trumps the current suit (i.e. paper beats rock, scissors beats paper, and rock beats scissors).  The first person or team to reach 175 points wins.

54 cards featuring legendary icons from movies, celebrities, and world-renowned artists from pop culture to spice up your Drag Cards deck.

You play number cards and try to place the value of the card played into your grid in a way that creates as many ascending sequences as possible. Whoever does this most skillfully and also earns bonus points from special scoring will score the most points and win.

—description from the publisher

The sun rises over the tulip fields in the valley, where tall windmills stand against the horizon and ships wait to carry your blooms across the sea. In Windmill Valley Duel, two rival horticulturists compete for prestige, wealth, and victory by cultivating the finest tulips, investing in powerful enhancements, and claiming the most strategic ports in this vibrant and picturesque land. In this valley, you are not alone. Your windmill’s gears interlock with your opponent’s, forcing every decision you make to send ripples through the land while setting your rival’s plans in motion. Will you offer them an advantage to gain the edge you need, or will you wait for the perfect move? 
Plant your tulips with care. Build your windmills with purpose. Navigate the seas for trade. In a world where beauty and timing reign supreme, only the best horticulturist blooms victorious!

—description from the publisher
