description:
480 crazy cards to liven up your evenings and reveal your friends' worst secrets. The player with the lowest IQ is designated “Queen of Juduku”. She draws the first card and asks the question to a player who has 8 seconds to answer it. If the answers are accepted by the Queen of Juduku then the player who answered scores 1 point. The winner in turn becomes the Queen of Juduku. It’s up to her to ask the next player a question. The first player to reach 7 points will be declared the winner. Juduku "Girl'z Night" is the first edition of Juduku written by girls for girls. The game has 480 crazy cards including 120 special cards divided into 4 categories.

—description from the publisher

JUDUKU Pride Edition is THE game that spices up your evenings with hilarious questions to answer in less than 8 seconds. Created in collaboration with stubborn, the most popular lgbtqia+ media in France!

180 cards on LGBT topics with various themes. Several categories including "Queer Mimes", "Karaoke", and "Quizz" cards on LGBTQIA+ community icons! Perfect for extending your evenings to the end of the night.

SIMPLE & EFFECTIVE RULES: 

     A player draws a card and asks his/her neighbor the question
     He/she must then answer it in less than 8 seconds (no time to think!)
     If the answers are validated, the player scores 1 point. The first 7-point player is the winner!


PERFECT FOR PARTIES: Atmosphere your evenings, weekends, birthdays with a hilarious board game that will set the mood for sure (and that will reveal little secrets...​).
A very own game: cards made in France and eco-friendly: eco-friendly paper and a % of the profits are donated to charitable associations

—description from the publisher

Tip Up! is a fast and opportunistic dice-placement game set in a lively tavern. Players take on the role of servers trying to serve the best tables and collect as many tips as possible.

Each player has a unique set of five dice (d4, d6, d8, d10 and d12). On their turn, players may reroll any number of their dice, then must place one die on the table matching its value. Tables grant tips and special effects that can help manipulate dice values, disrupt opponents, or create new opportunities.

However, tables have limited space, and the dice values you roll may not always match the tables you want. When a die cannot be placed, it may be sent to the Brawl area, where the player with the most dice at the end of the round wins the accumulated tips.

After two rounds, the player with the most tips wins the game.

House of Gods: Chowka Bara is a strategy race game inspired by the traditional Indian game Chowka Bara, reimagined with characters from Hindu mythology. Players represent divine families such as Shiva, Vishnu, Brahma, and Ram, guiding their members across the board to return home first.

The game preserves the traditional cowrie shell movement system while introducing thematic elements such as Blessing Points, Curse Points, and Chakra Points, adding new layers of strategy and player interaction.

A distinctive feature of the game is the use of hand-painted Channapatna wooden dolls as play pieces. Crafted using the traditional lacquerware art form from Karnataka, these dolls bring a vibrant cultural aesthetic to the gameplay experience.

House of Gods blends the charm of a centuries-old Indian race game with mythology-inspired storytelling, handcrafted components, and modern strategic twists.

—description from the publisher

Become the Master of the Elements in this fun and strategic battle game for 2 to 4 players. Summon your Fire, Air, Earth, and Water Elementals to build your strategy and counter your rivals. Tame powerful Elemental Creatures endowed with extraordinary abilities to gain the upper hand. Manipulate the Ether at your own risk, and rise as the supreme mage by evolving your four Elementals to their ultimate forms.

How to play?

- Set Up the Game
Place the Elemental Creature tiles in the center of the table, along with the shuffled Time Distortion cards. Place your Level 4 and Level 5 Elemental cards into your main discard pile, then shuffle the remaining deck. Draw 5 cards. The game is ready to begin.

- Play Your Elementals
On your turn, you may play as many Elementals as you wish into the “Elemental stacks” in your play area, provided each card is the next weakest Elemental in that stack (starting with the Level 0 Elemental Egg, then the Level 1 Elemental, and so on). Cards are placed slightly offset from the previous ones in the stack. The topmost visible card of each stack is considered “free.”

- Resolve Their Effect
Each time you play an Elemental card into your Play Area, you may immediately resolve the effect associated with its element:

🔥 Fire Elemental — Blaze
Blaze allows you to destroy one opponent’s Free Elemental (the top visible card of one of their Elemental Stacks).
💧 Water Elemental — Cascade
Cascade allows you to counter an opponent’s Blaze.
These are the only cards that may be played outside of your own turn.
🌿 Earth Elemental — Rooting
Rooting allows you to consolidate all cards in one of your Elemental Stacks into a single pile, protecting it from future opponent Blaze effects.
💨 Air Elemental — Tornado
Tornado allows you to discard as many cards as you wish into your discard piles, then draw the same number of cards from your deck.

- Release the Elemental Creatures
Some Elemental cards display the Elemental Creature symbol. Whenever one of these cards is played into a Play Area, the corresponding mage releases the Elemental Creature matching that card’s element and level. Each of the eight Elemental Creatures has a unique ability that may be used once per game. However, be careful: The mage who releases an Elemental Creature must immediately give the corresponding Elemental Creature token (and therefore its power) to one of their opponents.

- Resolve Time Distortions
Each mage has four Ether Elementals, which act as Joker cards. They may replace certain Elemental cards but do not trigger that Elemental’s effect. Whenever an Ether Elemental is played, the player immediately draws one Time Distortion Card and resolves its effect right away.

- Discard Your Remaining Cards
At the end of your turn, you may either keep your remaining cards in hand for your next turn or discard as many as you wish. If you choose to discard, you have two options:
• Quick Discard Pile — This pile will be reshuffled and reused first when your Deck runs out.
• Main Discard Pile — This pile will only be reshuffled after the Quick Discard Pile has been completely reclaimed and emptied.
You then draw cards until you have 5 Elemental cards in hand again. Play then passes to the next mage.

The first mage to control all four Ultimate Elementals in their play area wins the game!

—description from the designer

family: History: War of the Austrian Succession

description:
A Japanese poem-matching card game in the form of a karuta.

The goal of the game is to collect as many cards as the player can, by matching segments of poems.

The first hundred reading cards (yomifuda) each contain a full poem, while the second hundred playing cards (torifuda) each contain the final phrase of the poem. To play, one player chooses a reading card and begins reading the poem, while the other players search through the face-up, spread-out playing cards to find the matching final verse of the poem; the matching reading cards and player cards also have unique artwork that spans across the pair of cards. Players take turns being the reader until all reading cards have been read, and the player with the most correct poem matches wins.

The game is based on the historical Japanese poetry collection 'One Hundred Poets, One Poem Each' (Hyakunin Isshu).

boardgame: Macià 1926: The Uprising That Never Was
description:
In this solo 'What if' historical game, you take the role of Francesc Macià, leading a small band of Catalan insurgents across the Pyrenees to spark a revolution against the Spanish dictatorship of Primo de Rivera in 1926.

Capture towns, build prestige among the population, and persuade the powerful CNT trade union to launch a general strike before government forces arrive. Move quickly, avoid civilian casualties, and seize Olot to proclaim the Catalan Republic. You can also take leverage of TNT caches and Italian anarchists. But time is short: once the Spanish army enters the region, the rebellion is crushed.

Each turn, the player advances their columns towards objectives, with the main city of Olot as a final objective. If local Spanish forces are present in the same place as a column, a battle ensues and the player can gain or loss prestige and recruits.


En aquest joc històric i hipotètic en solitari, que recrea els "Fets de Prats de Molló", assumeixes el paper de Francesc Macià i organitzes una petita partida d'insurgents catalans per tal de travessar els Pirineus i provocar un aixecament contra la dictadura de Primo de Rivera el 1926. El joc planteja la situació hipotètica en què Macià i els seus col·laboradors no són capturats per les autoritats franceses.

Captura poblacions, guanya prestigi entre la població i convenç el poderós sindicat CNT perquè convoqui una vaga general abans que arribin les forces governamentals. Mou-te amb rapidesa, evita les baixes civils i pren Olot per proclamar la República Catalana. Per fer-ho tens l'ajuda d'alguns anarquistes italians i pots aprofitar partides de TNT amagades al bosc. Però no tens tot el temps del món: un cop l'exèrcit espanyol entri a la regió, la rebel·lió serà esclafada irremeiablement.

A cada torn, el jugador fa avançar les seves columnes cap als objectius, amb la ciutat d'Olot com a fita final. Si hi ha forces locals espanyoles locals al mateix lloc que una columna, s'entaula un combat i el jugador pot guanyar o perdre prestigi i homes.

A Deadzone was declared immediately – located near the GCPS’ galactic shipping lanes, the Omega system has the potential to become a dangerous beachhead for the Plague to spread through the rest of the GCPS. description:
You and your friends are teenage witches living your best lives in the Willowspur, a magical land at the nexus of all realities in which there are wicked witches. Unlike your traditional wicked mothers, you and your crew are a jaunty and good-natured bunch, high-spirited and thick as thieves. And right now, you’re royally pissed at your moms.

Literally.

For some reason, your mothers got together and turned Prince Casper’s brother into a frog. Normally, you wouldn’t care that much, but you have a long history with Casper and he’s saved you and your gang from yourselves more times over the years than you’ll ever admit. So when he showed up at your clubhouse and begged you to save his brother, you just couldn’t say no, right?

Luckily, your mothers are selfish and petty and they don’t work well together, so their curse was poorly woven and is quite unstable.

Now, you have three months until your mothers can stabilize and make permanent their curse. Three short months to increase your thus far squandered abilities and become strong enough to thwart your moms and restore Murray to his human form.

Yeah... that Murray.

The stronzo.

I mean, seriously...?

FluentSea is a word-building, reef-restoration tile placement game where every word you make literally brings a bleached coral reef back to life.

You build the best word you can each round, and the longer the word, the more marine species you get to place, like octopus, coral, clownfish, and more.

Each species scores differently based on real behaviors, so clever wordplay and smart ecology both matter. Heal the reef, build the ecosystem, and score the most points to win.

Detonation is a sci-fi strategy card game set in an intergalactic conflict between the oppressive Neo-Imperialists and the rebellious Faithful Radicals fighting for liberation.

Gameplay:
Players command unique characters across customizable terrain, utilizing tactical tokens to boost attack, defense. Carefully maneuvering characters and leveraging their abilities is key to outsmarting opponents and controlling the battlefield in this thrilling tactical showdown.

How You Win:

A team wins when all opposing characters have been eliminated from the battlefield.

Faithful Radicals win by eliminating all Neo-Imperialist characters, allowing them to seize the base and its weapons to fuel the revolution.

Neo-Imperialists win by eliminating all Faithful Radical characters, successfully defending the base from the attack.

If playing the two-part scenario, characters that survive Part 1 continue into Part 2, carrying over any damage and tokens. The game ends immediately once one team has no surviving characters.

—description from the publisher

Intelligence suggests that a new organization calling itself “Ouroboros” is responsible. Each investigation presents players with physical clues such as fingerprints, footprints, and coded messages. By examining evidence, discussing theories, and combining information, players attempt to reconstruct events and identify the culprit.

boardgame: Карманный детектив: Тайны улиц Вены (Pocket Detective: Secrets of Vienna Streets)
description:
Карманный детектив: Тайны улиц Вены ("Pocket Detective: Secrets of Vienna Streets") is the fifth box in the series.
You're known as a brilliant Vienna thief, but your real goal is finding answers about your family in India. You bribed the police to let you go, promising to find stolen manuscripts and prove your innocence in just days. Fail, and you'll take the fall for someone else's crime—time to play detective once more.

In the Pocket Detective game series, players take up investigations of different tangled cases and mysterious stories. Players are gathering clues, interrogating suspects, performing arrests and matching pieces of information to successfully solve the case.

During each turn, a player chooses one of the cards available this turn. These cards can represent a Clue, a Location, Witness Interrogation, Special Action, Arrest of a Suspect etc. Using the cards, which were received during investigation, a player discovers what actually happened. The objective is to solve a case in a minimum number of turns.

🔫 BULLET FOR A STRANGER:

Deadwater is a trap, and the Grim Gang just sprung it. They’ve lined the Main Street, waiting
for you to blink. You’ve got one revolver, an ammo box at your hip, and a split second to react.

This isn't a fair fight; it's a chain reaction. You can only take the shot if it flows from the last

boardgame: Bulldozer: It's Time to Play Dirty
description:
Bulldozer is a light strategy tactile tactical board game for 2 players.

During your turn, roll the dice and use your trusty bulldozers to move rocks and launch dynamite. Be the first to clear your side of rocks and win! Clear your rocks by collecting them in your rock pit. Collect 6 and earn another bulldozer or dynamite. Or 'play dirty' by knocking your rocks onto your opponent's side, creating more work for them. Be careful where you leave your bulldozer, as things tend to escalate quickly with dynamite lying around!

A gorgeous 45-minute 2-player tabletop game featuring unique physical movement through sliding and pushing!

—description from the publisher

The year is 1776, and the first shots of the American Revolutionary War have thundered across the World. 
Supplies are scarce, and armies are fragile, and now every decision could mean survival—or destruction. 
Now, it's your turn to FORAGE AHEAD through history.

In Forage Ahead players lead squads of American Revolutionary War soldiers on quick daring foraging missions across a continent torn apart by war. 
Each miniature represents a unique soldier with specialized training, unique backgrounds, and skills. 
Navigating terrain while scavenging for; food, arms, and vital supplies, all while evading hostile foraging parties.

—description from the publisher

Loosely inspired on SPI’s The Conquerors, First Man in Rome is an operational simulation covering the civil war between Caesar and the Optimates, initially led by Pompey the Great and later by Scipio, Cato, and the sons of Pompey.

The basic rules are the same used by the Charles Roberts Award winner The Fate of All. The final goal is to give a realistic representation of ancient warfare, without godly interventions, auguries, and Homeric duels. Moving and feeding an army of 50,000 men will require planning, and in the end players will find that the problems faced are similar to those encountered during the Napoleonic era or the American Civil War.

The three maps cover the whole Mediterranean, from the Pillars of Hercules to Parthia, and from Britannia to Aegyptus. The scale is 60 km per hex with monthly turns.

Battles can be fought using Strategic Combat, still realistic but quicker, or to their full extent using the Tactical Rules and Map – probably the best choice for a decisive battle.

And never forget, this is a civil war where political connections and money are of the utmost importance. Client kings and free cities must be persuaded to support your faction, veteran legionaries must be convinced to enlist again under the promise of money or loot, the Roman plebs must be fed, enemy kingdoms and tribes must be kept under control... And no matter what, the unexpected always happens, and everybody could abandon you should the tide turn.

All the primary classical accounts, together with a range of modern military studies, were consulted during development. In particular, De Bello Civili by Julius Caesar has been treated as the primary ancient source, since it recounts the events from direct experience — albeit through a perspective that is, at times, unmistakably partisan.

Game Highlights

    60 km per hex, one month per turn
    Units representing various formations, from Legionary Cohorts to Elephants
    Order of battle and commanders based on primary sources and the most recent historical research
    Realistic supply problems with realistic and possibly violent solutions
    Cavalry Reconnaissance, Foraging and Raiding
    Reaction Movement, not so easy retreats and catastrophic routs
    Attrition and Army Morale, your first problems in the morning
    Naval Warfare
    Client States, Free Cities, enemy Kingdoms, hostile barbarians
    Clementia, Revolts, Treachery and worse


Scenarios

    SENATUS CONSULTUM ULTIMUM - The Rubicon and the Italian Campaign, 49 BCE
    VOLUNTAS ET FORTUNA - The First Spanish Campaign, 49 BCE
    AD COLLISIONEM - The Clash between Caesar and Pompey in Macedonia, 48 BCE
    NILUS LETALIS - The Alexandrian War and Pharnaces, 48 - 47 BCE
    DE BELLO CIVILI - The Roman Civil War, 49 - 45 BCE


—description from the publisher

Polypods are creatures of the snub-square grid.  They are composed of connected pieces of 2 types: core and feet.

Feet are the extremities; that is they are either:
1. Those pieces with only one friendly neighbor, or
2. Those with exactly 2 friendly neighbors that together fill the points of a small triangle of the grid.

Core are the other pieces that connect all the feet together. Cores are always toxic to the feet of adjacent polypods.

The game of Polypods is about growing and protecting polypods of your color so that you have the most polypod cores when the game ends.

Life and Death: 
A polypod lives when it has at least as many feet as cores. 
It dies when it does not.

When a piece is added to a polypod, any adjacent feet that are no longer at the extremities, immediately convert to core.

In like manner, when a polypod loses one or more feet though contact with the core of another polypod, any cores that thus become exposed as extremeties, immediately revert to being feet.

Play:
The Green player starts by placing a piece onto the empty board. Play alternates. On your turn you

1. Place a new piece on any empty space on the board, where it will not cause any of your own pieces to be removed.
2. Update your polypods. 
3. If any new cores in your polypod are next to the feet of any other polypods, those feet replaced with 'remains' markers that restrict future play at those locations.
4. Every opponent's polypod that lost any feet is updated.
5. If the updated polypods are no longer alive, they are entirely removed from the board.

Play proceeds until both players pass consecutively. Then the game is scored by counting the number of cores of each color.

Ties are broken by counting the number of empty locations that are only adjacent to one's own color to see who has more.

An additional rule is only relevant for play with more than 2 players:
New polypods must not be started next to the feet of more than one color of polypod.

Welcome, dungeon raiders!
You are about to enter the Dungeon of Doom. Sharpen your darts and prepare to face the terrifying monsters lurking within its shadowy halls!

Dart Quest – The Dungeon of Doom is the world’s first Throw & Write game: a print‑and‑play adventure in which all you need are some darts and a target (a great DIY target template is available—you’ll only need a piece of cardboard!).
Throughout your quest, you will encounter 8 different monsters, each with unique abilities that will challenge you as your experience grows. To aid you on your journey, you can collect 8 exclusive items and 2 legendary items, perfect for helping you overcome the monsters… and outsmart your rival, who is just as eager to strike down the Eye of Carnage, the fearsome boss of this dungeon, before you do.

Your goal
Be the one to deliver the final blow to the monsters of the dungeons of doom.
To play, you’ll need darts and a space to throw them.

Objective
Collect items and make damages to defeat monsters.
The player who defeats the last monster win the game.

How to Play
On your turn, you have 3 darts:

Head → gain 20 XP
Sword → gain 5 XP and block other players (they cannot collect XP on their turn)
Shield → removes the sword’s restriction
Potion → collect an item

Defeating Monsters

Each monster has an XP threshold that must be reached to defeat it.
Once exceeded, the monster is defeated and you move on to the next one.
You must defeat 3 monsters to reach the final boss.
Each monster has a unique ability that shapes your journey through the dungeon.

Ready to begin your adventure?

Grab your darts and dive into the dungeon of Dart Quest!

—description from the designer

Theme:
The ice is melting. Help the polar animals reach the safety of the ice. This game introduces younger children to the problem of climate change and its dangerous effects on the animals of the Arctic and Antarctic.

Gameplay:
Players help the animals move across the ice and try to reach safety before the ice disappears.

Combat! Blitzkrieg expands the Combat! game system with Polish, French, and early-war German troops.  Gamers can play any of these nationalities in eleven exciting historical scenarios, or they can use the random scenario generator to create infinite new scenarios.

Combat! Blitzkrieg is a stand-alone, solitaire game but it integrates with Combat! Eastern Front, Tunisia & Sicily, and Crete.  It also contains rules for competitive play, so that two gamers can play against each other.

In addition to the new nationalities, Combat! Blitzkrieg introduces four new maps, Graveyards, Fences, Flamethrowers, and Barricades.  Best of all, it includes new Armor rules to add tanks, antitank guns, and light antitank weapons to the game system.  Nearly 40 different tanks are included of all nationalities: Polish, French, British, German, Italian, Soviet, and American.

This is the biggest and most ambitious Combat! game yet.

description:
Poagon, the land, is a mythical realm reminiscent of Arthurian Britain, or the Central Europe of Grimm’s Fairy Tales, or Bunyan's Pilgrim's Progress, or Tolkien's Middle Earth. It brims with danger, challenges, and treasure.

Poagon, the game, is an exciting experience for 1-6 persons where each player takes the role of one character and tries to navigate that character to fortune, fame, and victory. In Poagon, your character will grow and develop as they gain experience and renown, as they encounter the denizens of the land, and as they find treasure. Each Poagon game is entirely different as there are limitless possibilities in the encounters and the potential gain (or loss) in the land. It is an addicting game that is to be savored repeatedly.

Poagon is a tabletop role-playing game; that is, it is an RPG that does not have a gamemaster.  You will enter the land with little in skills and possessions and will grow in attributes, skills, treasure, armor, and weapons, as you overcome obstacles and the denizens of the land.  The goal is to become the first person to unseat the Oppressor who rules Poagon.  The game is incredibly rich in detail and layering.  There are multiple denizens that can be encountered: Dragons, Trolls, Spiders, Sprites, Nomads, Soldiers, Priests, and many more.  Those encounters can lead to treasure and renown, or defeat and doom, and can have many, many consequences.

—description from the publisher

Step onto the pitch and experience the excitement of rugby like never before with our flicking game inspired by this legendary sport! Tactics, skill, and daring will be your greatest allies as you carry the ball and score a try under the pressure of a game full of twists and turns.

Fast-paced and suspenseful matches
With every flick, everything can change: will you launch a heroic break with your forwards or attempt a decisive kick to score? The intensity rises with every phase of play, offering unparalleled moments of suspense. The thrill of rugby comes to your table, with matches where every action is crucial and every try unleashes a wave of emotion.

description:
Put on your detective hat and solve the case of Emilia Berg, who was murdered on stage.

Crime scene investigation where players get to see court trial paper and evidence of the case. Going through them all they should be able to solve the case.

The Theater Murder is part of the Unsolved Mysteries series and offers a realistic experience where you and your friends are transformed into detectives. Step into the Ljusviken Theater and put on your detective hat to solve the brutal murder of the famous actress Emilia Berg, who was murdered in the middle of an ongoing performance in 1948.

The Forest is the next evolution of the classic push-your-luck card game: blending creepy atmosphere, humor, and lots, LOTS OF STRATEGY.

The players become fearless adventurers who venture into a dark and mysterious forest, inhabited by creatures and beings forgotten by time and civilization.

Draw a role card and define your starting survival kit. Then, advance deeper into the forest by drawing cards from the deck. Sometimes luck will be on your side, and you’ll find useful items or friendly creatures—use them wisely! But if that’s not the case… well, let’s just hope you don’t encounter THE GOAT. If you do, your team loses one of its three lives.

Currently designed for 4 or 6 players, but only two will survive. The pair that best combines cunning, bluffing, strategy, and teamwork will be the one to survive The Forest.

Remember: fortune favors the bold, but strategy saves the clever.

The Crimson Twins provide unique gameplay as 1 Leader that can be in two places at once. Shipwreck allows for additional strategies with the U.S.S FLAGG. Zarana and Jinx disrupt your opponents and gather intel on their plans.

Advance across the board, attacking other players, defending against their attacks, overcoming challenges and enemies, and ultimately searching and obtaining the keys to open the door on the last space. The player who finds the keys and reach to the end wins the game.

Development (How to Win): Each player rolls a die to advance across the board spaces. At the end of the turn, the effect of the space they land on is resolved. Along the way, players can collect tokens to improve their health, energy, and money. Players also obtain cards that they can save to use them when needed to develop a strategy. As players advance across the board, enemies will appear and try to stop them. Players must work together to defeat these enemies. Each defeated boss drops a special accessory, which is won by the player who defeats it. After defeating all the enemies, the players will fight each other, and the victorious one will keep all the special accessories and must carry them to advance to the final goal (where they are required) to win the game.

—description from the designer

Avanzar a través del tablero atacando a otros jugadores, defendiéndote de sus ataques, superando desafíos, enemigos y al final buscar y conseguir las llaves para abrir la puerta en la última casilla, cuyo jugador que lo consiga y llegue al final ganará el juego.

Desarrollo (Como ganar): Cada jugador lanzara un dado para avanzar a través de las casillas del tablero y al final del turno se resuelve el efecto de la casilla donde cayo. En el camino, el jugador puede hacerse de tokens para mejorar la salud, energía y dinero. También en el camino, el jugador obtendrá cartas que puede guardar para usarlas cuando mejor le convenga y formar una estrategia. A medida que avanzan en el tablero, aparecerán Enemigos que detendrán a los jugadores y juntos deberán derrotalos, cada Jefe al ser vencido soltara un accesorio especial que ganara aquel jugador que lo derrote. Después de derrotar a todos los enemigos, los jugadores lucharan entre ellos, en donde el ganador se quedara con todos los accesorios especiales y con estos, debe avanzar a la meta final (en donde estos son requeridos) y ganara el juego.

—description from the designer (Spanish)

PEEPS Take 4 is a fast-acting family face off game that blends quick thinking, playful competition, and the iconic fun of PEEPS Marshmellow candy characters. Though it's a kids games for ages 8+, this fast-paced card game is also super fun for teens and adults as it keeps everyone active from start to finish. Each round, players race to collect four matching cards. But when two players choose the same card, it becomes a mad dash to grab the exclusive Peeps Bunny plush before anyone else. The player who wins the plush gets to keep the card, adding suspense and nonstop energy to every turn.

Directions

     Shuffle the deck of 84 cards and deal 5 cards to each player. Place the remaining cards face down as a draw pile.
     The youngest player goes first and play continues clockwise.
     On your turn, play a number card from your hand to claim a Peeps card from the draw pile. If the number card matches the top card of the draw pile, it becomes a speed card game. The first player to grab the Peeps plushie wins the card and keeps it.
     If you don't have a matching number card, draw a card from the draw pile and end your turn.
     The first player to collect 4 matching Peeps cards wins the game.
     In case of a tie, the player with the most unique Peeps cards wins. If still tied, the player closest to the youngest player's age wins.


—description from the publisher

Harvest ‘54 is a cooperative card-laying game that fits in a standard poker-size tuckbox. Strategically place Terrain cards to chain resources and unlock Progress cards in this cozy pocket game. Fit 12 Progress cards onto your map to win!

Includes 30 Terrain cards, 20 Progress cards, and 4 rule cards.

The soup's on and trouble is simmering! Slap down cards that are higher or lower, and grab the spoons to save the pups! You have mo time to blink... Race to be the first to get your pups out of the soup!

—description from the publisher

THE GREATEST SPORT IS WAR: ENTER THE DEATH FIELDS ARENA

A Galaxy of Immortal Warriors
For thousands of years, advanced alien civilizations have visited Earth to steal its bravest warriors. These soldiers are taken to fight, die, and be reborn in arenas throughout the galaxy for the amusement of thousands of systems. Whether you lead the elite Les Grognards or the desperate dregs of a Cannon Fodder team, your warriors are assets in a cutthroat business where victory is the only path to prestige.

Death Fields Arena is a fast and furious 28mm sci-fi skirmish game that puts you in the role of a team owner in the galaxy’s most popular—and most brutal—entertainment circuit.

These rules are completely free to play. To get started, simply download this rulebook, grab a box of Death Fields miniatures, and prepare your team for battle.

Tactical Depth and Strategic Gameplay

     Continuous Activation: Move away from traditional round structures; players alternate activating single models in a free-flowing phase that mirrors the chaotic nature of the arena.
     The Stress System: Manage mental fatigue as a critical resource; Stress acts as a negative modifier to combat and movement, forcing you to balance aggression with tactical recovery.
     Lethal Combat Mechanics: Utilize a streamlined d10 system where low stats are generally better, featuring nuanced rules for Shield Saves, Armour Piercing, and Knock Backs.
     Dynamic Terrain Interaction: Master a battlefield where elevation provides crucial force multipliers and every obstacle can be utilized as a Defensive Position to block or deflect incoming fire.
     Competitive Match Play: Engage in high-stakes scenarios designed to test your tactical limits, from the shifting objectives of "Tick, Tick... Boom!" to the claustrophobic tension of "Last Man Standing".


—description from the publisher

Exo Tomb is a solo science fiction tabletop game in which players explore the cryptic ruins of an ancient alien civilization. Taking on the role of a Tomb Warrior, you venture deep into long-abandoned structures in search of powerful relics left behind by a lost species.

Each expedition is played on a modular map as the Tomb Warrior explores rooms, encounters hostile biomechanical constructs, and uncovers alien technology. Combat is fast and tactical, relying on dice rolls and positioning while balancing risk and survival during the descent into the tomb.

The objective is to survive the expedition and recover valuable relics from the ruins. Players must decide how far to push their luck, as the deeper areas of the tomb become increasingly dangerous.

Hot or Cold is a cooperative party word game about interpreting clues and thinking in opposites. One player knows the secret word, while the other players work together to discover it using a series of “hot” or “cold” hints based on contrasting word pairs.

At the start of each round, a grid of possible words is placed on the table, with one of them secretly selected as the target word. The clue giver reveals hints over the course of the game, each presented as an opposite pair (for example: sweet vs. sour or hot vs. cold). For each pair, the clue giver indicates which side better describes the secret word.

Using these clues, the team discusses and eliminates words that no longer fit. After every round, at least one word is removed from the grid. The goal is to narrow the possibilities until only the correct word remains. However, if the group accidentally eliminates the secret word, the game is immediately lost.

Kikeriki! is a lighthearted dice game in which players imitate animal sounds. As players move along the track, landing on animal spaces requires them to loudly mimic the corresponding animal noise. Failing to do so correctly results in a penalty. Special spaces may send players back, force them to wait, or require additional penalties. The first player to reach the lion at the end of the track wins the game.

boardgame: King of Fighters: The Orochi Saga
description:
King of Fighters: The Orochi Saga is a card battle game that drops players into the iconic Orochi storyline, centered on KOF ’97 and ’98. Once you’ve got the rules down, grab a deck featuring your favorite fighters—Kyo Kusanagi, Iori Yagami, Mai Shiranui, King, Terry Bogard, Leona, and more—collect their character cards, and let the best player win. Each deck goes beyond the KOF name, capturing what makes every fighter shine with tactics, combos, special and super moves, and authentic martial arts. The result: distinct playstyles and mechanics in each deck that feel true to each character!

With 4 modes of play you can play 1v1 Arcade Mode, Solo Mode vs the Boss, 2-Player Co-Op "Final Showdown", and an epic 2v1 "Ultimate Showdown" as well!

—description from the publisher
A print-and-play file, created as  a Christmas gift by Pink Troubadour, which allows you to play with different starting resources and which makes the beginning of the game asymmetric.

This is an educational game for pre-school children where they learn the numbers from 1 - 10 in various forms. The 36 cards contain digits, dices showing different numbers, sets of toys representing numbers and fingers creating numbers.

All the cards are shuffled and one card is put face up on the table. The remaining cards distributed between the children. Every child tries to put a card next to the one already on the table. It can either be the predecessor, the successor or another representation of the same number. If the child can't put any card, it's the next player's turn.

The child who is the first one to get rod off all their cards wins the game.

Bleib locker (Stay calm!) is a city tour. The players move four pawnss in their colour over the plan.

At their turn, the player rolls the dice and moves one of their pawns accordingly. They have to role a 6, in order for a new pawn to leave the "start house". 
The difference to a traditional pachisi game are the action cards. If a pawn reaches a field that is occupied by an opponent's pawn, the opponent draws a card from the action card pile and moves the pawn accordingly. The opponent does not need to go back to their start house.

If the action card requires the opponent to move to a field that is also occupied, the pawn already on the field has to move back into their start house (even it's not an opponent's but the player's pawn).

The first player to reach the home base with all four pawns wins.

This is an educational game for children to learn sets, percentages and fractions. The 36 cards contain percentages, fractions, and images showing numbers.

All the cards are shuffled and one card is put face up on the table. The remaining cards distributed between the children. Every child tries to put a card next to the one already on the table. It can either be the predecessor, the successor or another representation of the same set / fraction. If the child can't put any card, it's the next player's turn.

The child who is the first one to get rod off all their cards wins the game.

boardgame: Zombicide: Mission C48 – Hungry, Thirsty and Dangerous
description:
Designed by players for the Kyoko mission contest, and later published by the original studio, C missions are supplementary missions released online for print and play.

C48 is a medium difficulty exploration mission for Prison Outbreak.
The county jail is the last place around where you could find food. Everyone knows what hungry beasts like you are capable of to enjoy bacon, heh? Everyone, but zombies. Show them!

Designed by Stéphane Blanc-Santelli

boardgame: Zombicide: Mission C49 – Shopping For A New Home
description:
Designed by players for the Kyoko mission contest, and later published by the original studio, C missions are supplementary missions released online for print and play.

C49 is a Hard difficulty exploration mission for Season 1 & Toxic City Mall.
Your scout noticed an almost intact Mall to explore. You could even use it as a shelter for a few days! But first, it needs to be secured. Quick, you're sooo hungry!

Designed by Scott LeBlanc

The players try to save the puppies from Cruella de Ville.

The board is 3D construction. At their turn, the player draws from the pile of cards and moves their puppy accordingly. If the card shows Cruella de Ville, they move Cruella's car to the next section. All puppies in this section must hide until the next round.

If the player moves their puppy, they carry out the action indicated by the respective field. Whenever a puppy passes Cruella de Ville's house, they can rescue a puppy from the house by the help of house cards.

The player who rescues the last puppy from Cruella's house wins.

A new day begins and the player have one day  to complete their tasks: brushing their teeth at 8.00 am, catching the school bus at 9.00 am etc. The clock ticks.

The players move their character across the board. A color wheel determines the next field they move their character to. Only if they reach the section of their task at the correct time, they complete the task.

For each task completed in time, the players win a clock. The player who wins the must clocks wins the game.

A game that could only have been invented in 2020 and 2021: Players try to throw their cards onto a string from a distance of about 1.50 meters.
Each player receives his cards. The oldest player places the cord in the middle of the table. The game is played in clockwise direction. Each turn one card is thrown. The player should only through their cards either on the cord or on their own cards.  
The player who is the first one to place all his cards correctly on the cord wins.

5-Minute One Piece is a real-time co-operative game where players assemble a pirate crew and race to finish an island adventure before time runs out. Inspired by the Netflix live-action adaptation of One Piece, 5-Minute One Piece is a reimplementation of 5-Minute Dungeon: players work together to match symbols with cards in their hand like in 5-Minute Dungeon, but have the additional challenge of matching sequence by slotting cards onto the Going Merry game board.

In detail: The group chooses one of five Adventure Decks to face together, then each player picks a Straw Hat character card (Luffy, Zoro, Nami, Usopp, Sanji, Chopper) and their unique deck for themselves. Once the timer starts, players reveal the top card from the Adventure Deck which may show an enemy, event or ally. Defeating enemies (which may have their own passive abilities) is accomplished by clearing Challenge Cards, which are all 4-symbol sequences. Players play cards in real-time onto the Going Merry game board until the sequence of cards played match the sequence of symbols on the Challenge Card. Once enough Challenge Cards have been matched in this way, the Adventure Card is complete and can be replaced with the next one.

5-Minute One Piece is designed to be playable for all ages: while players have a variety of character abilities and special symbol card actions to get out of tight spots and succeed on high difficulty modes, teams of newcomers can still win using just the symbol-matching gameplay if they prefer. Higher difficulty modes add cumulative time pressure and incentive to utilize special abilities for players seeking a challenge.

—description from the designer

This game contains 4 story-based games. The players use the tip toi pen to play their way through the stories and complete mini-challenges. For each mini-challenge completed they receive points.

The player with the most points win.

The games requires a tip toi pen.

The game includes a cake, a mask, a chin rest, a knob, the base, and a dice. You can play the game with as many friends as you like. Before starting the game, press down on the arm and apply cream. Turn the knob (counterclockwise) several times to load. Then roll the dice.

Hold your face to the mask and press the button as many times as the number on the dice shows. Then it's the other player's turn. The first player to get cream on their face loses.

A globe-trotting game of travel trivia. 
Answer your way around the world in this travel-tastic trivia game. For bonus points, test your geographical knowledge by locating your answers on the travel map.

—description from the publisher

Small trivia game with 80 geography questions. Players gain points for answering questions correctly either with or without the multiple choice answers, and then bonus points for locating the answer in the right grid square on a world map.

If questions are answered incorrectly other players can buzz in (no buzzer is provided).

—user summary
This promotional card adds a new drink inspired by a traditional Brazilian beverage. The promo card is treated as a standard order and follows the normal rules of the game.

boardgame: Marvel: Crisis Protocol – Iron Lad, Iron Monger, Kang the Conqueror & Rescue Character Pack
description:
This character pack brings four new characters to Marvel: Crisis Protocol. Finally making his grand entrance into the game, the iconic Marvel villain Kang the Conqueror arrives from the 31st century. Boasting a brand-new Leadership for the Cabal Affiliation and loaded with future tech, Kang is more than a match for a team of Avengers. The young Avenger and someday Kang Iron Lad also arrives to bolster the Young Avengers Affiliation, while Rescue brings her Stark-built suit to lend plenty of medical aid and support to her fellow Avengers. Finally, Iron Monger brings heavy metal power to the Criminal Syndicate making this pack a fantastic addition for anyone who picked up the Earth’s Mightiest Core Set (CP143).

Introduces the fan-favorite villain Kang, who brings a new Leadership for Cabal squads.
Iron Monger adds durability and firepower at 3.
Threat to Criminal Syndicate Rescue is sure to be a highly valued support character thanks to her healing abilities.
Iron Lad adds some time-traveling tech to both the Young Avengers and is a solid splash character for other squads looking to harness the power of rerolls.

boardgame: Marvel: Crisis Protocol – Adam Warlock, Moondragon & Quasar Character Pack
description: This pack introduces three new cosmic heroes from the Marvel Universe to Marvel: Crisis Protocol. The perfect being Adam Warlock brings his Leadership to the team, ready to lead the fight against cosmic forces. Moondragon and Quasar add potent new powers and abilities to the fan-favorite Guardians of the Galaxy Affiliation. Moondragon brings her mystic powers, adding a new dimension to the Guardians of the Galaxy, while Quasar is a powerful brawler who uses the powers of the Quantum Bands to empower her attacks.

boardgame: Marvel: Crisis Protocol – Valkyrie and Elendil & Warriors Three Character Pack
description: This character pack for Marvel: Crisis Protocol brings two new Asgardian characters to the game for the first time and is an ideal follow up for players looking to add some Asgardian reinforcements to CP91 Loki, Thor, Mighty Thor & Lady Sif or CP198 Asgardian Affiliation Packs. Valkyrie, or Valk, brings mounted speed and power and is an ideal pairing alongside the original Valkyrie from CP198. The Warriors Three bring a toolbox of abilities, representing the martial prowess and unique character of each individual hero to make up the group.

boardgame: Marvel: Crisis Protocol – Blue Marvel & Spectrum Character Pack
description:
This pack brings two new characters to Marvel: Crisis Protocol, the powerhouse Blue Marvel and the leader of the Mighty Avengers Spectrum to the game. Spectrum adds a new Affiliation, the Mighty Avengers to the game with a new leadership that allows members of her squad to be able to transfer some of the Power they gain to their allies.

The Mighty Avengers is an exciting mixture of characters from the Marvel: Crisis Protocol catalogue, making it extremely compelling for veteran players with large collections. Blue Marvel adds a new 5 Threat to both Defenders and the Mighty Avengers Affiliations. Highly efficient with his actions, he serves as a potent tank and damage dealer who can attack from both long and short range.

INTRODUCE THE MIGHTY AVENGERS: Lead a new era of heroism with Spectrum, who brings the Mighty Avengers Affiliation and a unique leadership ability that lets her team share Power to support allies.
TWO NEW HEROES JOIN THE GAME: Add Blue Marvel and Spectrum to your Marvel: Crisis Protocol roster—two powerhouse characters that expand both team options and tactical depth.
STRATEGIC & VERSATILE GAMEPLAY: Blue Marvel adds a 5-Threat powerhouse to the Defenders and Mighty Avengers, capable of absorbing hits and dealing heavy damage from both long and short range.
COMPLETE CHARACTER EXPANSION: Includes 2 finely detailed miniatures with bases, 2 character stat cards, 5 Team Tactic Cards, and 1 insert, delivering everything you need to field these heroes in your next mission.
OFFICIAL MARVEL MINIATURES GAME EXPANSION: Produced by Atomic Mass Games, fully compatible with all Marvel: Crisis Protocol Core Sets and expansions for seamless gameplay and collection integration.

You must collect colored balls from the board with a 3-tubes magazine (or 4-tubes in solo play). 
You make points with specific color combinations or avoiding two balls of the same color in one tube.

Explorers: Jet-Setters Quiz is a trivia card game featuring 200 questions about travel, geography, and world cultures. Players test their knowledge individually or in teams, answering questions from the deck and scoring points for correct answers. Simple to play and easy to pick up, it’s a quick way for travel enthusiasts and trivia fans to challenge themselves and learn fun facts about the world.

In this fast-paced card game, you have to think quickly!
Whoever can match an animal to its correct habitat slaps the corresponding cards to win them.

When it's your turn, quickly turn over the top card of your pile and place it face up in the centre.
(Do not pile the played cards directly on top of each other; all cards must always be clearly visible and reachable by everyone.)
After playing a card, everyone looks simultaneously to see if they can find a matching pair in the centre:
Is there an animal and its correct habitat on the table?
Then quickly slap the 2 matching cards! 2 cards match if the animal on one card matches the habitat on the other card.

End of the game
The game ends as soon as no one can place any more cards in the centre. Whoever collects the most cards wins the game!

—description from the publisher

Verkehrte Welt: Ein Gorilla auf der Eisscholle? Ein Pinguin in der Wüste? Diese Tiere haben sich wohl mächtig verirrt! Wer am schnellsten die richtigen Verbindungen herstellt, räumt die entsprechenden Karten ab! Doch aufgepasst: wer die Spezialkarten clever kombiniert, kann direkt alle ausliegenden Karten gewinnen!

Spielidee
In diesem rasanten Kartenspiel muss schnell geschaltet werden! Wer ein Tier und den dazu passenden Lebensraum miteinander in Verbindung bringen kann, schlägt auf die beteiligten Karten, um sie zu gewinnen. Besonderen Pfiff bringen die Spezialkarten, bei der man die ganze Auslage gewinnen kann!

Spielende
Das Spiel endet, sobald niemand mehr Karten in die Mitte legen kann. (Diejenigen unter euch, die keine Karten mehr zum Aufdecken haben, werden übersprungen. Alle dürfen aber weiterhin auf passende Karten schlagen!) Wer die meisten Karten sammeln konnte, gewinnt das Spiel!

—description from the publisher (German)

The sheep are eager to get into their pen! In this cheerful board game Baaa-tle, children guide their flock step by step to the drinking trough and then to the flower meadow. But beware: along the way, the sheep pile on top of each other and block the path. Only smart moves and a bit of dice luck will bring you closer to victory. Roll the dice, carefully choose which of your sheep to move and free trapped animals if necessary. As soon as 3 of your sheep reach the flower meadow, you win the game!

boardgame: Two Years After: The Equal Rights Amendment 1921-1979
description:
Two Years After is a two-player card game simulating the sociocultural debates and political struggle to ratify the Equal Rights Amendment (ERA) to US Constitution, which would legally establish that “Equality of rights under the law shall not be denied or abridged by the United States or by any State on account of sex.” The ERA reads that, upon approval, “This amendment shall take effect two years after the date of ratification.” Throughout the game, the two players compete to influence cultural ideas about gender roles, sway women’s groups, political parties, and labor unions in order to push Congress to ratify or stop the Amendment.

The game begins with the initial ERA proposal, which was put forth by the National Woman’s Party in 1921 and subsequently introduced to Congress in 1923 with a language penned by Alice Paul and Crystal Eastman. Throughout the twentieth century, the ERA was met with controversy: different women’s groups debated and disagreed about whether it would make womens lives better; political support shifted between Democrats and Republicans; and labor unions and national associations actively lobbied for and against the proposal. By 1972, the ERA passed in Congress, with the support of President Nixon. As the Amendment was sent to the different states for ratification, Phyllis Schlafly created the “STOP ERA” campaign, organizing rallies and making multiple media appearances in defense of traditional gender roles. By the 1979 deadline for the states’s ratification of the ERA, the Amendment had not received the necessary state support. Despite deadline extensions, it remains unratified.

In Two Years After, one player defends the Equal Rights Amendment and the other seeks to stop it from ratification. Each round, players draw action and leader cards in six areas of contention to sway the cultural and political aspects of the debate, from philosophical debates about gender roles to congressional discussions and the growing influence of media. The actions a player can take represent ways to gain support through rallies, public debates, political connections, endorsements by labor unions, and seeking the disputed support of women’s and feminist associations. A round-track marker brings attention (and bonuses) to issues that emerged at different points of the century, from the rise in women labor during WWII to the fears of women conscription during the Vietnam War.

—description from the designer
language_dependency: 4: Extensive use of text - massive conversion needed to be playable

description:
Your cadre of 2-6 professional thieves has stolen the library of Count Vanderhorde, the world’s greatest collection of strange and marvelous treasures from throughout history. Now it’s time for the part you didn’t plan: divvying up the goods.

In Post-Heist, players pass a shared hand of cards around the table, taking one for themselves each turn, with the goal of collecting a pile worth the most gold by the end of the game. A wide variety of magical effects allow players to steal, play extra cards, and open up other synergies and routes to victory. Treasures can be protected by playing them face-down, or by stashing three treasures of the same type. There are also traps throughout the deck, which can be used to interrupt opponents' turns and derail their plans.

There are also 12 curses shuffled into the deck, which subtract from a player's score. Additionally, once there are twice as many curses in play as there are players, the group must flee from Vanderhorde's dark magic, and the game ends, keeping it a mystery which turn will be the last until the moment it comes.

—description from the publisher

Set during the final years of the Eastern Han dynasty, The War of Red Cliff (火烧赤壁) is an operational-level historical wargame about the campaigns surrounding the famous Battle of Red Cliffs. Players command the armies of Three Kingdoms warlords as they maneuver across southern China and fight battles on land and water. The game focuses on three key elements of ancient warfare—morale, supply, and generals—which influence every decision on the battlefield. Managing these elements well is essential to lead your armies to victory.

Flip Tricks with Jack’s Mom is a 3-player trick-taking card game that plays in about 15–20 minutes.

Players compete across several rounds to win tricks and score points by carefully choosing when to play their strongest cards and when to hold back. Each trick requires players to follow suit when possible, forcing difficult decisions about timing, hand management, and reading opponents’ intentions.

Because only three players are involved, every card played has a significant impact on the round, creating a tense back-and-forth where players must constantly adapt their strategy. Winning the right tricks at the right time is critical to building the highest score and claiming victory.

Originally created as part of an ongoing joke among friends, Flip Tricks with Jack’s Mom developed into a fully playable trick-taking game that blends familiar mechanics with a humorous theme.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Legends of the South: The Awakening of the Spirits
description:
Legends of the South: The Awakening of the Spirits is a competitive deck-building game for 2–4 players set in a dark-fantasy world inspired by myths and ancestral stories from the southern lands.

Each player chooses one of four unique factions and builds their deck over the course of the game, using allies, rituals, and strategic decisions to gain advantage and pursue victory. Every faction offers its own identity and playstyle, creating a distinct experience from one match to the next.

Runes and spiritual dice add tension and unpredictability to the game. Players must adapt their plans, manage their resources, and respond to shifting opportunities as ancient forces awaken across the board.

Victory comes through careful deck-building, tactical play, and mastering the strengths of your faction better than your opponents.

—description from the publisher
Two new Merchandise tiles, available in the first edition of the game.

Discover new dinosaurs, pterosaurs, eggs, and mor Butterfly Effects in this expansion, Where youl´ll find new ways to survive Extinction.

—description from the designer

Explora el Mundo Perdido a vista de pájaro y descubre nuevos dinosaurios, pterosaurios, huevos y más Efectos Mariposa en esta expansión, donde encontrarás otras formas de sobrevivir a la Extinción.

—description from the designer (Spanish)

New to the world of Alwyd?

These free Core Rules are your enlistment papers; everything you need to command a squad of Rhyflers and take your first steps into the Long War. 
Focused on fast-paced infantry skirmishes, this version captures the heart of Clash of Rhyfles without the smell of a cavalry charge or the noise of armored support.

Many of Alwyd’s greatest war stories began the same way: a muddy field, a loaded rhyfle, and orders to advance.

When you're ready to call in heavier tools of war, the full rules await!

—description from the publisher

This classic Chilean tabletop experience centers on the darkest, cruelest, and most uncensored black humor. It positions itself as a bold social critique, daring to expose the most pathetic, absurd, intense, and wicked aspects of Chilean society. Designed strictly for adults who believe humor should be personal and filter-free, the game targets those who want to laugh "like villains" with friends, creating new inside jokes that last for years. Due to its transgressive nature, it features a heavy warning: it is not for the easily offended, minors, or those who believe certain topics should remain "untouchable."

The themes covered are wide-ranging and provocative, including politics, sex, pop culture, death, and perversion. It leans heavily into Chilean slang  and general stupidity, aiming to provide a hilarious experience for friends while being "terrifying" to more conservative relatives.

Gameplay and Winning
The gameplay follows a simple, fast-paced "fill-in-the-blank" mechanic. During each round, players must complete a sentence by playing their most "out of line" or inappropriate Black Card to match the White Card currently in play. Players read their "sick" combinations aloud, and the goal is to create the most shocking or hilarious association possible to win the round.

To win a round, a player must convince the "Dealer" that their combination is the funniest of the lot. The winner of the round "earns the cash" (se gana las lucas), establishing themselves as the person with the most twisted sense of humor at the table. The game officially ends when a player runs out of "lucas." At this point, the player with the most "lucas" is declared the ultimate winner and gets to choose a "penance" or forfeit that the losing player must perform.

description:
Alykinder is Dead
Now the Long War enters a new and even more uncertain phase. From the rubble of the Great Wall of Coftyr and the Principality of Gwynt the Crymuster seeks to invade Toulmore and its elite Crusader forces. From the highlands of Fidwog to the shores of Lake Morandi the Fraedarl Overseas Division continue their dogged resistance to Easky’s mechanized divisions. The Western Arnyarans have finally established the nation of Aber, but the Creevish Trench Watch are determined to throw them back as invaders. And everywhere common quar show uncommon valor in the face of battle. Who will you lead in this...
 Clash of Rhyfles
Enter Alwyd, a world at war for centuries. Utilizing an inventive double-blind activation system and intuitive rules, you can recreate the battles of your Ancestors in quick paced skirmishes that reward fair play as much as sound tactics. Gather your squad of Rhyflers from the faction of your choice, grab your friends, and make your mark on The Long War!

—description from the publisher

Divine Descent is a high-stakes, narrative-driven tactical skirmish game for 1–3 players. You command a strike team of three powerful celestial warriors—Angels of Might, Grace, and Zeal—sent from the heavens to purge a demonic infestation and seal the Abyssal breach.

This product is designed for the modern wargamer, combining the tactile satisfaction of tabletop miniatures with a Narrative Companion App that tracks your campaign, manages character progression, and handles the "bookkeeping" so you can focus on the battle.

The Experience

     Tactical Depth: Use a unique D10 Dice Pool System where the margin of success determines the lethality of your strikes.
     Narrative Campaign: Fight through a continuous 12-scenario branching story.
     Strategic Choices: Your decisions between battles—whether to secure a perimeter or launch a surprise assault—directly impact the rewards you earn and the challenges you face.
     Zero-Friction Progression: No more messy character sheets; use the included Companion App to track XP, manage HP, and trigger powerful "Ascension" promotions as your Angels evolve into Tier 3 Seraphs and Cherubs.
     Brutal Enemies: Face a diverse Demonic Horde, from skittering Imps and soaring Gargoyles to the reality-shattering Abyssal Worm.


What’s Included?
This digital package provides everything you need to transform your 3x3' table into a battlefield:

     The Core Rulebook: A print-ready guide detailing the D10 system, alternating activations, winged movement, and terrain interactions.
     The Narrative Companion App: A standalone, browser-based application (no installation required). It features:
         Automated Campaign Tracking: Saves your progress across all 12 scenarios.
         Digital Dice Roller: Integrated combat calculator vs. enemy Defense.
         Party Management: Real-time tracking of HP, XP, and Level-Ups.
         Inventory System: Manage consumables like Martyr’s Tears and Holy Grenades.
         Save/Load Functionality: Export your campaign state to a JSON file to continue your war across different devices.
    


Coming Soon: The War Continues
The battle for Earth is only the beginning. Future standalone expansions are currently in development to extend the Divine Descent universe:

     New Narrative Arcs: Continue the crusade against the Abyss with all-new campaign chapters.
     Expanded Bestiary: Face deadlier threats beyond the initial vanguard.
     New Heavenly Ranks: Discover further paths of Ascension for your strike team.


Technical Specifications

     Players: 1–3 (Solo or Co-op).
     Playtime: 45–60 minutes per scenario.
     Space Required: 3x3' Table.
     Required Materials: 3 Angel Miniatures, 10–15 Demon Miniatures/Tokens, a pool of D10s, and any smartphone, tablet, or PC to run the Companion App.


—description from the publisher

ACT THAT! Fun Family Party Travel Card Game Of Charades

Brainteasers "ACT THAT!" is a fun, travel-friendly family charades card game featuring 50 cards (movies, books and TV shows) and instructions. Designed for acting out, this compact, fast-paced party game is perfect for on-the-go entertainment.

PŘELET is a game simulating a competition soaring task with simple roll for movement mechanics. Players compete to reach the finish first while navigating the typical challenges of a sailplane pilot: gaining altitude to cross mountains, accumulating bugs on the wings, landing out, etc.

Headlines Game is a tabloid-tactic game to test your editorial creativity and strike up fun at any get-together!

Contents: 50 cards housed in a small matchbox-style box+ 1 decision token.

Target Audience: Recommended for ages 17 and up.

Purpose: Perfect for dinner parties, icebreakers, or travel.

This game is part of a series of Matchbox Trivia games .
(Marks and Spencer)

This game is playable with a standard deck of cards.

Story: The Frog King is planning his retirement. Unsure which of his 2 children should replace him, he creates a competition. Over 4 rounds, the potential heirs must use strategy and tactics to outscore their sibling. The winner will take the throne.

Overview: Leapfrog is a trick-taking game for 2 players. Each player plays 2 cards to each trick, following suit if possible. Each time a player wins a trick, they’ll move clockwise 1 space onto the next lily pad. If the next lily pad is occupied, they’ll leapfrog over them to the next available space. Each lily pad has a value on it, and players score that many points at the end of the hand.

Each player has access to a couple powers that can advance the neutral King Frog token onto the next lily pad, or have the weakest suit in the hierarchy leap to become the strongest suit.

The highest card in the strongest suit will win the trick…usually. If there are 1 or more cards in the same suit & in sequence to the would-be trick winner, the lowest card will leapfrog to victory. For example, if a 1, 7, 3, and 8 of clubs are played, the 7 is in sequence to the 8 and the 7 wins the trick. If that 1 & 3 were actually 5 & 6, the 5 would leapfrog the 6-7-8 to win the trick.

—description from the designer

SANTORO London action game - Gorjuss

Players race to empty their hands and catch opposing players with cards left in theirs, which score points. In turns, players attempt to play a card by matching its color or number to the topmost card on the discard pile. If unable to play, players draw a card from the draw pile, and if still unable to play, they pass their turn. Wild and special cards spice things up a bit.

46 cards _ it is like UNO game, but smaller and so easier also for very young people

Stable Genius: The Game is loud, ruthless, and completely unhinged — a savage parody of the wild, headline-dominating chaos we’re all living through. Fortunes explode. Alliances collapse. One spin can make you a legend — or wreck you in spectacular fashion.

It’s strategy wrapped in satire.
It’s absurd. It’s exaggerated.
And somehow… it feels a little too familiar.

Stack outrageous amounts of cash. Make reckless moves. Watch loyalties shift without warning. Every turn mirrors the over-the-top drama of modern life — but this time, you’re the one in control.

If your group loves bold risks, sharp humor, and laughing at the madness of it all, this is the game that turns today’s chaos into pure entertainment.

But fair warning: once it hits the table, it takes over the night.

—description from the publisher

Jelly is a fast, colourful push-your-luck card game filled with charming jelly characters and shifting team dynamics. Each round, players roll dice simultaneously to try to collect Jelly cards worth points. They can keep rolling to improve their outcome — but if they roll doubles, they Burst and collect nothing for that round. Every turn is a balance between securing points or risking it all for something better.

As the game unfolds, players gain and shift between teams using Team Bean cards, creating an ongoing tension between personal scoring and collective success. Only players on the team with the highest combined points are eligible to win, so it’s not enough to simply gather points — you must also watch how your team is performing. Do you help strengthen your current team, focus purely on your own total, or swap sides if momentum shifts?

Many cards also feature one-time actions that can be triggered for a tactical effect — but using the action means sacrificing the card and its points. Jelly Burst cards add further interaction, giving players chances to lower opponents’ odds and disrupt their game. When the game ends, the team with the highest total points determines eligibility, and the player on that team with the most Jelly points wins.

—description from the designer

Visiting the Terran Exchange will maximize your profits at the Orbital Market, but beware - whoever controls the volatile markets at the Exchange can make offers that others find impossible to refuse.

The Terran Exchange was a new faction introduced for Alien Frontiers Factions in 2017. It is already included in Factions Definitive Edition and Alien Frontiers Edition X, but it was not in earlier editions.

—description from the publisher

Deli Dash is a card game where rival sandwich makers hustle to earn the most money by the end of the lunch rush! Setup includes: each player gets 5 Bread Cards to start their sandwiches, Sandwich Cards are arranged in the middle and show which sandwiches can be built with which ingredients and in what order, each player is dealt a hand of 6 cards from the main deck, 3 cards form the Trash Pile, 4 cards form the Pantry, and the rest form the Discard Pile.

Players take turns performing max 3 actions: play an ingredient card type, an event card (help yourself or hinder your opponents), rearrange your sandwich, take a card from the Pantry, finish a sandwich, or move ingredients between your sandwiches. Some event cards are also free actions! Then you discard as many cards as you'd like and draw back to 6 cards from the Draw Pile.

The first player to 5 sandwiches triggers the end of the game - everyone with turns left gets to finish their final 3 actions, then everyone counts up how much money they made. The winner is named employee of the month!

—description from the designer

Welcome, dungeon raiders!
You are about to enter the Cursed Forest. Sharpen your darts and steel your nerves, for dreadful creatures lurk in the twisted shadows of its haunted depths.

Dart Quest – The Cursed Forest is the world’s first Throw & Write game: a print‑and‑play adventure in which all you need are some darts and a target (a great DIY target template is available—you’ll only need a piece of cardboard!).
Throughout your quest, you will encounter 8 different monsters, each with unique abilities that will challenge you as your experience grows. To aid you on your journey, you can collect 8 exclusive items and 2 legendary items, perfect for helping you overcome the monsters… and outsmart your rival, who is just as eager to strike down the three‑headed hydra, the fearsome boss of this dungeon, before you do.

Your goal
Be the one to deliver the final blow to the monsters of the dungeons of doom.
To play, you’ll need darts and a space to throw them.

Objective
Collect items and make damages to defeat monsters.
The player who defeats the last monster win the game.

How to Play
On your turn, you have 3 darts:

Head → gain 20 XP
Sword → gain 5 XP and block other players (they cannot collect XP on their turn)
Shield → removes the sword’s restriction
Potion → collect an item

Defeating Monsters

Each monster has an XP threshold that must be reached to defeat it.
Once exceeded, the monster is defeated and you move on to the next one.
You must defeat 3 monsters to reach the final boss.
Each monster has a unique ability that shapes your journey through the dungeon.

Ready to begin your adventure?

Grab your darts and dive into the dungeon of Dart Quest!

—description from the designer

Welcome, dungeon raiders!
You are about to enter the Catacombs of Rot. Sharpen your darts and steel your resolve, for horrifying creatures lurk in the decaying shadows of its forsaken halls!

Dart Quest – The Catacombs of Rot is the world’s first Throw & Write game: a print‑and‑play adventure in which all you need are some darts and a target (a great DIY target template is available—you’ll only need a piece of cardboard!).
Throughout your quest, you will encounter 8 different monsters, each with unique abilities that will challenge you as your experience grows. To aid you on your journey, you can collect 8 exclusive items and 2 legendary items, perfect for helping you overcome the monsters… and outsmart your rival, who is just as eager to strike down the necromencer, the fearsome boss of this dungeon, before you do.

Your goal
Be the one to deliver the final blow to the monsters of the dungeons of doom.
To play, you’ll need darts and a space to throw them.

Objective
Collect items and make damages to defeat monsters.
The player who defeats the last monster win the game.

How to Play
On your turn, you have 3 darts:

Head → gain 20 XP
Sword → gain 5 XP and block other players (they cannot collect XP on their turn)
Shield → removes the sword’s restriction
Potion → collect an item

Defeating Monsters

Each monster has an XP threshold that must be reached to defeat it.
Once exceeded, the monster is defeated and you move on to the next one.
You must defeat 3 monsters to reach the final boss.
Each monster has a unique ability that shapes your journey through the dungeon.

Ready to begin your adventure?

Grab your darts and dive into the dungeon of Dart Quest!

—description from the designer

The Monster realm is at war. The different regions are vying for control of the Totems of The Super Weird World of Monsters.
 In Monsters at War players will take on the role of one of the great Leaders of the Super Weird World of Monsters and vie for control of the Totems of Power. The first player to gain 4 totems (Four player) wins the game.

Monsters at War is a card game for 2-4 players, aged 13 up. A game of Monsters at War will last anywhere from 45 minutes to 1 hour.

—description from the designer

The Idea Stormers is a creative ideation and pitching game where players transform random concepts into innovative solutions.

In each round, players receive a combination of Concept, Quality and Action cards that must be connected into a new idea. The challenge is to quickly brainstorm, combine words, and craft a compelling solution to the problem presented in the round. Players then present their ideas in short pitches to the group, explaining the concept, its value, and why it stands out.

After all pitches are delivered, the players or a designated judge evaluate the ideas based on creativity, originality, usefulness, or impact. The idea with the strongest pitch wins the game.

The game encourages rapid thinking, creative connections, storytelling, and persuasive communication. While the mechanics are simple, the results are often surprising, funny, and insightful as players turn unexpected combinations into imaginative solutions.

The Idea Stormers is designed for students, entrepreneurs, innovators, startup teams, and creative thinkers who want to practice ideation, collaboration, and pitching skills in a playful environment.

Winning the Game

The player who delivers the best idea and pitch wins the game.
Points are awarded by the judge or by group voting. After a predetermined number of rounds, the player with the most points is declared the ultimate Idea Stormer.

—description from the designer

The Silk Road is a versatile expansion set for all Mega Empires games that centers on the legendary Silk Road, which linked the Far East with the Middle East and the Western world. It includes additional maps that create geographical connections between The Far East - North, The Far East - South, and The East. It adds 2 new empires, Zhangzhung and Tibet, along with rules and components for various alternative game setups:


     Combination of The Far East - North and The Far East - South with 11 players.
     Combination of The East, The Far East - North, and The Far East - South with 12 to 20 players.
     Combination of The West, The East, The Far East - North, and The Far East - South with 21 to 30 players.
     Alternative setups for the combination of The Far East - North and The Far East - South with 6-11 players.
     Extending The East to 10 or 11 players.
     Extending the combination of The West and The East to 19 or 20 players.


—description from the designers

Designed by players for the Kyoko mission contest, and later published by the original studio, C missions are supplementary missions released online for print and play.

C50 is a Hard difficulty exploration mission for Prison Outbreak. Dog Companions. Zombie Dogs.

Kim, from Prison Outbreak's team, gave you the location of her old crimeboss' lock-ups. Money won't do you any good, but the best weapons in town will make your day!

Designed by Caroline & Gareth Brough

boardgame: Dirty Filth Bikepacking: The Expansion Pack
description: A 30 card expansion pack with new resources, characters, stories and disasters of all kinds.

Spell words with cards to empty your hand: cook up stronger combinations to claim VPs, make longer ones to stir things up, or strategically pass to get a bonus. So, can you scoop a word before it sinks?

The game is played in 3 rounds. Players take turns playing and claiming letter combinations. Each combination must be a word of 3+ letters and stronger than the previous one played. The earlier you run out of cards in your hand each round, the more Victory Points (VP) you score for your claimed
words. The player with the most VP after three rounds wins.

—description from the publisher

You are A.D.A. - an Automated Deflection Agent - and it is your job to protect the server from hostile intrusions. You’ve detected a hacking attempt and must prevent the attacks, isolate the vulnerability, and identify a solution.

A.D.A. is a solo dice placement puzzle which plays in about 10 minutes. Each turn you will roll a pool of dice, manipulating and placing them to defend against hacking attempts. Resolve each hacking card before it bypasses your defenses or eventually enough security vulnerabilities will be exploited to compromise your server.

All you need to play are 2 pages of printed components, 6 dice, and about a dozen tokens of any type or color (although cubes work great).

—description from the designer

Your final exam as an Alchemist-in-training is here. Can you skillfully utilize your tools to decode the Aether Matrix before time runs out?

D6 Alchemist is a solo dice-manipulation puzzle where you’ll use your unique Alchemical skills and tools to clear the Aether Matrix over five rounds.
