 ☆ Tableau Building: Develop your Palace starting from Foundation Cards and expanding through Tier 1, Tier 2, and Tier 3 buildings.
 ☆ Strategic Interaction: Use card effects/abilities to gain advantages and outmaneuver your opponents.

Building Your Palace
The construction begins with your Foundation Cards. To add more structures to your Palace, you must spend the required Pixel Bricks (PIXL), Steel Beams (BEAM), and Aether Gems (AETH) resources to play Tier 1, Tier 2, and Tier 3 buildings into your tableau.

Tier 3 buildings represent the pinnacle of your architectural achievements; these complex structures require Aether Gems (AETH), alongside Pixel Bricks (PIXL) and occasionally Steel Beams (BEAM), to complete.

Play Modes
 ☆ Competitive: The core game experience for 2–5 architects.
 ☆ Duel of Architects: A specific variant for 2 players that provides an alternate gameplay experience.
 ☆ Solo Mode: A dedicated variant for architects looking to test their skills alone.

In Mafia di Mafia, you will have to form a mafia based on a secret objective. There will be unexpected actions that cause the members of your clan to change quickly. You will have to negotiate with your opponents for their mobsters and ultimately become a mafia boss before they do.
Players will draw an objective card that will tell them what they need to do to win the game. Once they know their goal, they will place three gangster cards, and the first player will draw an action card and do what it says. Once they have performed their action, it will be the negotiation turn, where they can exchange one of their gangsters for another opponent's, as long as both agree.
When this phase is over, they move on to the next turn, which will be the same, and so on until one of the players achieves their goal.

•••

En Mafia di Mafia tendrás que formar una mafia en base a un objetivo secreto, habrán acciones inesperadas que hagan que los miembros de tu clan cambien rápidamente, tendrás que negociar con tus oponentes por sus mafiosos y en definitiva, convertirte en un capo de la mafia antes que ellos.
Los jugadores robarán una carta de objetivo que les dirá lo que tienen que hacer para ganar la partida. Una vez sepan su meta, colocarán 3 cartas de mafioso y el primer jugador robará una carta de acción, haciendo lo que esta le indique. Una vez haya realizado su acción, pasará al turno de negociación, donde podrá intercambiar uno de sus mafiosos por el de otro oponente, siempre que ambos estén de acuerdo.
Cuando termine esta fase, pasará al siguiente turno que será igual, y así sucesivamente hasta que uno de los jugadores logre su objetivo.

WITH MAGNETS THAT SNAP AND KINGDOMS THAT RISE,
BLOCKYMS CROWNS THE BOLD AND THE WISE.

Enter the world of Blockyms, a high-quality magnetic board game featuring hand-drawn art and a dual-sided board. Build, play, and explore four games in one beautifully crafted set where strategy, creativity, and imagination come together. Play the original strategy game Blockingdom, go high in Blockyms To The Moon, or enjoy classic favorites like Chess and Checkers using the same magnetic board and cubes.

Each game offers a different challenge, from deep strategy to quick tactical battles.

				
				
					BlockingdomBlockingdom is a strategy board game played with magnetic cubes. You start as a Nomad and aim to rise through Peasant, Knight, Noble, and finally Royalty. Every turn brings danger or reward.
Along the way you may dodge pirates, sharks, and volcanoes, trade in the market, take chances with Ghost Ships, hope to meet a wizard, land on Blockhenge, and collect gold from chests and Treasure Ships.

				
				
					Hold the LandYou begin with a Tent, then upgrade to a Farmhouse, Tower, Manor, and finally a Castle. Each upgrade strengthens your position and helps you advance in rank. Poor decisions or bad luck can push your land back into Ruins and set you behind.

				
				
					Rule the SeaYou start with a Raft, then upgrade to a Boat, Longship, Merchant Ship, and finally a Capital Ship. The sea can bring pirates, sharks, volcanoes, and losses, but also Treasure Ships, Ghost Ships, and gold. One wrong move can leave you with Wreckage instead of a fleet.

				
				
					Blockyms To The MoonBlockyms To The Moon is a fast-paced, family-friendly board game inspired by classic snakes and ladders, reimagined with space-age twists and playful strategy.

Launch upward on rockets, crash back on asteroids, and warp through wormholes as luck and timing decide your path. Race through space and be the first to reach the moon.

				
				
					ChessThe classic strategy game reimagined in blocks with beautiful hand-painted artworks.

				
				
					CheckersThe classic, easy-to-learn strategy game now brought to life in the magnetic Blockyms format.

In Theo’s Soccer Game, the entire city is buzzing with excitement: today is the day of the big tournament final, and Theo the Raven is the referee. But before he can blow the whistle, the animal players still need to take their positions on the field. Working together, players try to bring all animals into the stadium before Theo starts the match.

The gameplay is simple and cooperative. On your turn, you roll the soccer die to see which animal you may move onto the field. Colored soccer balls allow you to place an animal of the matching color, while the silver ball acts as a wild choice. However, the die may also show Theo the Raven—and each time that happens, he advances one step closer to the kickoff.

Your shared goal is to place all animals onto the field before Theo completes his four steps and blows the whistle.

The fast pace and friendly teamwork make it ideal for young children taking their first steps into cooperative play.

PAUSO is a Board Building & Spatial Strategy Card Game.

A moshpit for minds where two to six players collaborate & compete to cause chaos on the board.

Make the moshpit truly your own by using 36 modular board cards to shape the board into endless forms, then cause chaos by pushing your 72 player cards across it to push players, trigger special effects and score points.

—description from the designer

The Advanced Game System V is an EXPANSION for the famous Board Game: HeroQuest. It is compatible with the core box (new and old edition).

With the Advanced Game System, you can choose a "Class" and "Level Up" your Hero. You can also purchase "Advanced Equipment" during the game.

This deck allows you to play with the following expansions (new and old editions):

"Wizards of Morcar"

With Uncharted Dungeons 2 you can play HeroQuest solo or in full cooperative mode without needing a Game Master or the official app. Each door you open will reveal a randomly generated encounter, and the Monsters will act using an artificial intelligence system. No dungeon will ever be the same. This deck comes with a mini campaign of 6 Quests but you can create your own. This deck is also fully compatible with all "Advanced Game System" decks, which can be seamlessly integrated.

Compete with your friends by building structures and hiring influencers, planning carefully the best travel destinations.

In Wonderlands player will play cards from the main deck which mainly consist of attractions, all of which have a theme (color) and an activity (symbol), together with a bonus feature which could be a star or one of 4 different services. The goal is to attract tourists, tokens that are placed at the start of the year on top of 3 out of 8 randomly selected theme cards which will dictate what each tourist class will be interested to visit in the Summer.

Each game lasts 4 years, each divided in 4 seasons. Starting in Fall each player will play a card from his hand facedown, in a free spot or on top of an existing Wonderlands. When all players have made their selection cards are revealed and stacked in front of them. Crucially, it is only possible to add to an existing stack if the new attraction shares either the theme or activity with a card already played. Shards can be used to play events that will change trends and grant additional effect based on theme /activity match in the host locations. At the beginning of Summer, which is the scoring season, player will have the option to assign influencers to their Wonderlands for powerful bonuses.

During each scoring phase, in Summer, players will compete to attract tourists by matching the current trends and offering the most stars. The process is repeated for each tourist until all will have selected their destination. Then, players will earn shards for each tourist on their Wonderlands, based on tourist class (vip, premier and economy), plus additional shard for each unique service on the host location and possibly influencer effects. Awards will be granted to the most successful player each year.

After 4 years, during endgame scoring, players will count bonuses from awards and influencers and add up all the shards they have accumulated, the player  with the most shards will win.

-description from designer

boardgame: Light Speed: Arena – Tablescope Sponsored Asteroid
description:
Promo Sponsored Asteroid contained in spielbox magazin 1/2026 that brings a new Asteroid with special abilities to the game:

Tablescope - Play your Space

"Spaceships in sight of Tablescope's eye and positioned at least 3 asteroids away gain +1 damage to all their lasers. Other spaceships and asteroids can break the line of sight."

Adult fans of UNO card games will love this honest take on the classic game. It plays like regular UNO matching color and number, but if a Truth Card is played, the player must decide if they are willing to risk telling the truth versus the penalty of drawing cards. There are four levels of truth: juicy, shameful, dirty, and dark! A chance roll of the die determines which truth players must tell. A card with a “reverse” icon on it forces the player who played the Truth Card to tell the truth! But the party doesn't end there, these cards are durable, waterproof plastic cards to make them completely spill-proof and comes with a handy clip to keep them together! Makes a great gift for fans and game lovers ages 18 years old and up.

—description from the publisher
family: Versions & Editions: Adult Versions of Family-Friendly Games

description:
The game of creating friends out of enemies & enemies out of friends. If you like games, drinking and sabotaging your friends, this game is for you! Drinks with Frenemies is a card game that brings the worst out of you and rewards you for it!

It's an entertaining party game that will keep you on your toes as you adjust to the ever-changing rules. It's a drinking game that's not family friendly. Meaning you can play with any type of beverage you prefer. In fact, we've made gameplay rules for those who wish not to drink at all. From water to moonshine, we think there's no perfect way to ruin... uh... enhance your relationships!

It's a great icebreaker game that gets you moving, thinking and laughing. We've designed it to be instantly playable and incredibly enjoyable with rules that are simple to follow and cards that are easy to understand. It's just as much fun to watch others play as it is to play the game yourself. It's a great addition to any game night, pre-game or party and makes for a great gift for birthdays, graduations and holidays!

—description from the publisher

In Nemesis, you desperately want to reach Earth in one piece, but it won’t be easy. As you explore the ship and evade the intruders, you may encounter crewmates willing to betray you. Roll dice to move throughout the ship. Discover critical functions like generators, engines, and escape pods. The longer you take, the more noise you’ll make. Rooms start inactive at the beginning of the game, so you’ll need to get the ship up and running again to have any chance of escaping. Get the generators online and engines running, and prep the escape pods! The ship is full of useful items. Salvage what military or technical tools you can and utilize them wisely. Every player has their own objectives. Their choices might even require you not to make it off the ship alive. Focus on your survival and make sure you’re in one of those escape pods!

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Earthborne Rangers: Shadow of the Storm Expansion
description:
Shadow of the Storm is the 2nd in a series of products known as Valley Expansions, which expand the Valley by adding locations and paths to explore, people to meet, missions to accomplish, and rewards to unlock. Notably, each Valley Expansion also includes a new campaign guide and map, which create a “mini-campaign” that can be played before, during, or after the main story.

—description from the publisher

In Tainted Grail, you will choose your path as four enemy types confront you, each one with a different level of difficulty and different rewards for taking them down. Shift between foes to build a well-rounded hero for a final fight against the boss. Combos in combat are critical as the dial severely limits the dice you can apply to enemies, but you can get around those restrictions by playing combat cards to unlock more options. The deck-building and combat card combinations change your strategy each time you engage an enemy! If you don’t want to face a certain enemy or are scouting for a different one further down in its deck, cast it aside into the Menhir Stack at the end of an engagement. After eight turns, you will face off against an extremely difficult boss, who will bring one or more Menhir Stack minions along for the fight. If you defeat it, you’ve won the game!

—description from the publisher

A small expansion for Sacra Maioritas, consisting of a new Character card: The Camerlengo.
Remove a card at random from the base game and replace it with the Camerlengo.  Setup the game as usual.  If the Camerlengo would appear in the rondel, replace him with another card and put the Camerlengo back in the deck (the card needs to go into a player's hand).
When played, you choose the faction this card belongs to.  Gain bonusses as per faction and if the Camerlengo is part of the winning faction, the player who played the card will for the rest of the round decide where to put the seal marker!

In Etherfields, Your hero will move through an ever-shifting dreamscape map on each of their turns, making unique connections and spending resources to trigger special actions in a variety of evocative rooms. Cards will flip during gameplay and will remain that way through future sessions, creating a deck that is unique to you and the sum of your decisions throughout every game you’ve played. Expand your awareness, deploy your cunning, and unleash your wrath. Influence cards shape what’s possible in the dream. Spending these resources will augment your pool of dice and keep you moving through the dream. Players are free to use actions whenever they wish, leading to some of the most creative play in the 20 Strong universe. Work over time to uncover the deck’s secrets, finding and defeating every hidden boss Etherfields has to offer.

—description from the publisher

A small expansion that adds a new starting card: Scherpenheuvel.
This card adds another challenge to the game, as with Scherpenheuvel, you are required to visit the sanctuaries in a fixed order.  Additional bonuses/blessings are awarded along the way.

Once upon a time there was a lively and colorful village in the heart of the forest, with all kinds of different creatures and animals. Until one day the village and all its inhabitants were cursed and almost everyone was petrified into a statue and time froze. The village was wiped off the map and could not be reached by anyone.

A small group of heroes, who escaped the curse, joined forces to free the rest of the village from the curse. But the village had changed - it seems to be changing. Not what it seems as this group of heroes try to free their friends...

Gravleville is a 1-4 player board game where players store resources, move through a always-changing world and race for the most points.

In this game, you choose your own hero, your own route, and try to liberate the most villagers together – or alone. Your turn consists of two actions: moving – and playing a bonus card or bonus action. Movement, by rolling the dice, takes you to unique locations with advantages and resources. You want to be ahead of your opponent rushing to rescue the villagers.  Bonus cards move the world, leading you along new—and faster?—routes to the villagers you need to rescue. Sometimes you need to use a bonus card to put your opponent on a different path. Bonus actions grant you unique advantages to stay ahead of your opponents - more movement, more resources or even the ability to steal a card from an opponent.

The game ends when all villagers are saved - did you save the villagers worth the most points? Is luck on your side, or will you be able to influence the world around you to your advantage?

—description from the designer

This Fable Fury booster set was originally part of the Kickstarter content and later made available separately. It includes three packs of 12 cards each:

1 Giants Cave Booster Pack
1 Dragons Lair Booster Pack
1 Orc Encampment Booster Pack

Long ago in the age of the Ancient Greeks, tales of the great beast called the Minotaur instilled fear in the hearts of the people. Often, it was rumored, people were thrown into the maze of the Minotaur for sport or punishment. If they survived they were hailed as heroes and pardoned of their crimes. If they failed it was certain they would never be seen again.

Maze of the Minotaur is a competitive, family weight for 3-8 players, set in the mythology of ancient Greece. Cast into the maze, it is up to you to decide if you will work together to find the stairway to freedom, or cast aside your fellow citizens and try to reap the rewards to gain victory for yourself!

Set Up

Create the maze by placing maze tiles face down in a 5 x 5 grid. The Compass Tile goes in the center spot, and the Stairway Exit gets shuffled with 3 random tiles and those 4 are placed in the corners.

Separate and shuffle the decks of cards.

Each player chooses 1 of the 8 characters to play, and receives 9 circle tokens to be place on their 4 Life slots and 4 Luck slots, and one to track their leaves (in game currency).

All players place their player pawn in the center of the maze, on the compass tile.

Roll the die 2 times. Count up along the edge of the maze a number of tiles equal to the first roll, and a number across equal to the second roll. Place the Minotaur pawn on that tile for its starting location.

Each player rolls the die, and the highest roll get the First Player Token for the first turn.

Luck

Luck can be used during the game to:

Find a secret passage through a wall

Lose a Luck Point instead of a Life Point

Power some characters special power

To find a passage or avoid a wound a player rolls the die against their current Luck. Roll equal to or lower is a success. This reduces their current Luck by 1 point.

Turns

Each turn is broken into two phases: the move phase, and the Fate phase.

Movement

During the move phase each player moves their pawn to an adjacent space in the maze. Flipping over any face down tiles they move onto.

If they end the move in an area with a Leaf, they record a Leaf on their character board. Leaves can be spent on Items, Victory Points, or Extra Movement during the game.

Pawns may not move through walls, off the edge of the maze, or through a Gate (unless they possess a Key).

Fate Phase

During the Fate Phase players will play a Fate card.

The first player draws Fate Cards equal to the number of players plus one. They then selesct one card to play immediately. The remainder are passed to their left, and each player selects a Fate card to play, passing the remaining cards to the left. The First player will receive the final Fate card and must play that one as well.

Fate Cards

Fate Cards can reward or penalize players, provide them with Items or Victory Points, or Move the Minotaur around the Maze.

The Minotaur

The Minotaur is always in the Maze. Playing a Movement Fate card moves the minotaur in a pattern through the maze in the specified direction.

Anytime the Minotaur moves through or stops on a tile with a player pawn that player is forfeit a life point. They may roll against their current Luck value to try and lose one luck point instead of one life point.

If a player loses all their Life Points they are out of the game.

Discarding a Shield protects a player from taking a wound.

Discarding a Sword gains a player a Minotaur Wound equal to 3 Victory Points.

End Game

Once the stairs have been revealed the final countdown starts, and player have 10 turns to exit the maze.

If a player does not exit the maze beforethe timer runs out they are trapped, and cannot win the game.

Players recieve a number of Laurel Cards (value 0-4 random VPs) upon exiting the maze.

First player receives 3 Laurel cards

Second player receives 2 Laurel cards

All other player receive 1 Laurel card

Winning

All players count up Victory Points from remaining Life and Luck, Items they have in their possession, and the value of any Laurel cards they hold.

—description from the designer

Crystallo is a solo puzzle/abstract card game with a light fantasy theme. Explore the cavern lair of the wicked Black Dragon by placing cards, free six magical creatures by creating crystal sets, and, if you should succeed with that task, trap the dragon in his own cave. Collect treasure along the way, and you may emerge a wealthy champion!

Crystallo is played with a deck of 54 illustrated cards and 18 gems, and runs about 20-30 minutes.

Crystallo: Untold Fortune turns up the tension and adds new push-your-luck decisions to the first phase of the game. Freeing a creature now carries the risk of collapsing a section of the cavern, but might also reveal a useful tool. Collect gems to trade for treasure, buy a shovel to dig up cards from your cavern, or peek at the future with a crystal ball. Play on Timid, Adventurous, or Valiant mode— if you dare!

Crystallo: Den of Dragons adds a new layer of puzzle-solving adventure to the second (dragon) phase of Crystallo. In addition to capturing the Black Dragon, players can choose an additional dragon to do battle with (Mystic, Armored, or Golden.) Polyomino treasure tiles must be placed on top of and within the confines of the dragon puzzle, adding another dimension to the challenge. Defeat both dragons and earn impressive bonuses to your score; fail at either one, and both escape!

Pikes and Pamphlets is a fast-play tabletop ruleset that recreates battles of the 16th–17th century pike-and-shot era (Italian Wars, Thirty Years’ War, English Civil War).

The system emphasizes simple mechanics, flexible armies, and quick battles that still capture the tactical feel of early-modern warfare. 
Armies can be fought with 2-3mm armies on 1-inch square bases or larger scale on 2-inch square bases.  
Tabletop can typically be played in a 2'x2' space or larger for even bigger engagements.

Sequence of Play
Roll for iniative every turn. 
Iniative player follows the sequence:

     Reinforce 
     Rally 
     Shooting  
     Move
     Melee


then opponent.

boardgame: Fleet Command: Hevelius – Vignette 3: Cataclysm Vault
description:
A treasure lost for millennia lies adrift, its resting place guarded by a jealous star. 
Two Pact Traders - powerful individuals whose ambitions far outweigh their caution - seek to claim it for themselves. 
Assume command of a fleet in a 5-part narrative campaign where victory and defeat have the power to shape a galaxy.

This 2-player campaign can also be used by event organizers to run multiple tables. Each 750-point mission can be run in 45 minutes to an hour.

 Fleet Command: Hevelius – Vignette 3: Cataclysm Vault comes with:

     A short primer on the Cataclysm Vault and the two Fleet Commanders vying to make it their own
     A five-mission narrative campaign, with mission introductions letting you jump straight into battle while still knowing what you are fighting for
     A new terrain type (Hostile Terrain) for more dynamic battles

Pomorskaya Lotsia ("Pomeranian Map") is a racing board game in which players control ships going to catch fish in the Barents Sea.

The essence of the game: each participant follows the water routes indicated on the navigation charts, catches fish, tries to avoid storms and delivers the catch to the seaports of Arkhangelsk and Onega, trying to sell it at a better price.

The playing board consists of catch, storm, and port square cards laid out in a circle. Players take turns playing navigation cards, which move their ship tokens around the cards clockwise. A player who leaves a catch card on his turn can take it as a catch. The size of the hold is limited. If a player gets caught in a storm, he must discard one catch card. The sale price in ports may vary.

Поморская лоция — настольная игра-гонка, в которой игроки управляют судном, отправившимся за уловом в Баренцево море.

Суть игры: каждый участник следует водными маршрутами, указанными на картах мореходной лоции, ловит рыбу, стремится избежать шторма и доставляет улов в морские порты — Архангельск и Онегу, стараясь продать его по более выгодной цене.

boardgame: 千年丝路II：阿拉伯商人 (Silk Road II: Arab Merchant)
description: In 千年丝路II：阿拉伯商人 (Silk Road II: Arab Merchant), players become merchants starting from humble beginnings along the legendary Silk Road. Traveling between distant cities, players trade goods, bargain with Arab merchants, and deal with dangers such as bandits. Through clever trading and careful planning, merchants attempt to build wealth while navigating the opportunities and risks of ancient long-distance commerce.

boardgame: 千年丝路：纵横东南亚 (Silk Road: Across the Southeast Asia)
description: 千年丝路：纵横东南亚 (Silk Road: Across the Southeast Asia) is a trade and transportation board game inspired by the China–Laos Railway and the modern Belt and Road Initiative. Players take the role of ambitious international trade magnates transporting goods across Southeast Asia. Using the railway network as their backbone, they travel between China, Laos, Thailand, Vietnam, Myanmar, and other partner regions, overcoming route challenges and building profitable trade networks.

Card League Baseball is a tabletop sports game themed around the excitement of baseball and the fun of collecting trading cards. Designed for kids ages 6 and up, the game brings the feel of a real baseball game to the table using player cards, dice, and a field mat. Players build their teams, set their lineups, and take turns rolling the dice to create the action, making every inning fast-paced and unpredictable. The game was created to be easy to learn, screen-free, and enjoyable for both kids and families who love baseball.

Gameplay begins by setting up the baseball field mat, choosing player cards, and writing each team’s lineup on the dry-erase lineup boards. Players can use the cards included with the game or swap in their own favorite baseball cards to make each game unique. On each turn, the player rolls the baseball-themed die to determine the result of the play, such as hits, outs, or advancing runners. The scoreboard is used to keep track of runs, innings, and outs, just like in a real baseball game.

The game is designed to be simple enough for younger players while still feeling like real baseball. Because the action is based on dice rolls, no special skills are required, and every player has a chance to make a big play. This keeps the game moving quickly and allows for lots of excitement, surprises, and close scores.

To win the game, players try to score more runs than their opponent by the end of the final inning. Just like in baseball, the team with the highest score at the end of the game is the winner. Players can play a short game for quick fun or extend the innings for a longer match, making Card League Baseball easy to play again and again.

—description from the designer

Let the lanterns light your path, the drums guide your steps, and the fireworks crown your night in this lively game for 2-6 players. You'll wander between charming stalls playing fast-paced mini-games like:


     Shateki, 射的 (Shooting Gallery)  
     Kingyo-sukui, 金魚すくい (Goldfish Scooping)
     Yo-Yo Tsuri, ヨーヨー釣り (Water Balloon Catch)
     Omen-ya san, お面屋さん (Mask Vendor)


As you explore the festival and wander from game to game, you will collect colorful prizes to charm the villagers. Win their favor, and they may join you for the grand finale.

—description from the publisher

Tokens & Towers is a strategic race game for four players inspired by the classic card-driven board game Tokken (also known as Tock or Jokers & Pegs). Players move their pieces around a circular track using a shared deck of cards, attempting to guide their pieces safely into their home tower before their opponents do. While the core gameplay remains familiar to fans of traditional Tokken, Tokens & Towers introduces new systems that add meaningful strategic choices without losing the accessibility of the original game.

In the base game, players use cards to move their pieces along the track, block opponents, and send rival pieces back to the start. As in Tokken, teamwork and timing can be crucial, especially when coordinating moves with a partner. However, the game expands on this foundation with additional layers that give players more control over their strategy.

One of these layers is a card bidding system. During a bidding phase, several cards are revealed and players compete for the ones they want most. By committing resources, players can influence which cards they gain access to, adding an element of planning and psychological interaction before the race even continues.

The second major system is tower building. Each player has a personal tower board where special tiles can be placed during the game. Completing rows, columns, or the central diagonal unlocks different bonuses and abilities. These improvements allow players to shape their own strategy, whether by gaining additional resources, manipulating cards, or protecting their pieces.

The result is a game that blends the familiar flow of a classic race game with modern board-game elements such as engine building and player asymmetry. Tokens & Towers aims to be accessible to new players while offering additional strategic depth for experienced gamers and longtime Tokken fans alike.

—description from the designer

boardgame: Tails of Adventure: The Curse of Count Catula
description:
In Tails of Adventure: The Curse of Count Catula, one player takes the role of a Dread Vampire Lord, commanding dangerous monsters, scheming against the heroes, and unfolding the story as up to 4 heroic players embark on a perilous journey to save their beloved kitty homeland. The heroes work together to  cooperatively explore dungeons, defeat monsters, and uncover loot!

The Heroes:
The Knight - A master swordsman who unleashes devastating counterattacks.
The Rogue - A sneaky expert who exterminates his foes whilst they're distracted.
The Mage - An explosive spellcaster who vaporizes multiple targets at once.
The Priestess - A powerful miracle-worker who boosts the team to their maximum potential.

After each game heroes can be enhanced and customized by purchasing improved equipment and learning powerful new skills.

The lightweight rules and simple 3D punchboard terrain make this dungeon crawl board game very approachable. Expansions are also available.

—description from the publisher

Oh Mon Dieu ("Oh My God") is a party game where the Greek gods are now slightly unhinged influencers, all vying for the adoration of their followers!

Build your Pantheon, rack up some Swag, and don't let the other players steal the show. Your social legend starts here!

The rules are super simple, and laughter is guaranteed! From the very first game, you can have fun with kids, family, friends, and even colleagues. But be warned, there's no mercy: anything goes to shine on Olympus 2.0!

Frango Frito is a dynamic and fun card game where players compete to be the most efficient chicken fryer. With a kitchen full of pots and chickens, it's time to start frying — but don't forget to clean everything up before the end of the game or you'll lose points! Players draft cards from an open display to collect sets of chickens and cooking equipment. The player who best manages their kitchen efficiency and scores the most points wins the game.

Fake People is a cooperative guessing game about creating characters and shamelessly judging them.

At the start of the game, each player is given two Superlative Cards with several options(ex: best dressed, class clown, most likely to be a serial killer).

After choosing their superlatives, players are given First Name, Last Name, and Trait Cards. These also have several options. Players are tasked with choosing a name and character traits that will get the other players to successfully deduce which superlatives are theirs.

The character creation portion of the game is performed simultaneously. The guessing portion of the game is done one player at a time. Player earn points as a team by correctly guessing each others superlatives.

—description from the publisher

Dominó Dungeon is a fast-paced, fun, and competitive game where each player controls an Adventurer who faces the dangers of Dungeons to earn money and double-cross the other members of their party. Players use domino-style tiles to navigate grid-based dungeon maps, rolling dice to resolve encounters and managing their hand of cards. Each adventurer has unique abilities, and the game features scenario-based missions that keep the action fresh. With action points to spend each turn, players must decide when to explore, when to fight, and when to betray their companions. The player who earns the most gold wins.

boardgame: Vingadores Era de Ultron: Controle de Invasão
description: In Avengers Age of Ultron: Invasion Control, players must unite the forces of each Avenger to fight back against the Ultron invasion. This is a cooperative team-based game where players work together using programmed movement on a modular hexagonal board. Each Avenger has unique abilities that players must leverage strategically. Players roll dice to resolve combat encounters and move point-to-point across the board to intercept Ultron forces. The team must coordinate their actions to control key areas and prevent the invasion from spreading. The players win by successfully repelling the Ultron invasion before time runs out.

An eccentric millionaire randomly selected six strangers and informed his loyal lawyer, Mr. Smith, that only one of them will inherit his entire fortune. The first of the six people to find the lawyer somewhere in the city will become the heir to his wealth. But don't waste time — someone else might beat you to it, and Mr. Smith can change his location at any moment. To win, you must be the first to find Mr. Smith, who is hidden among 40 possible locations. The twist of this game is that Mr. Smith moves unexpectedly! Use your deduction skills, memory, and risky plays to track him down before your opponents do.

boardgame: Jogo do Eclesiástico: Fuga do Pesadelo
description:
Jogo do Eclesiástico: Fuga do Pesadelo is a cooperative card game where players are about to enter a supernatural adventure that can be compared to a massive nightmare. You and your friends will dive into three levels of cards that function as portals. Your objective is to wake up from this nightmare, which closely resembles the spiritual battles we face in our daily lives, often without being fully aware of them.

Players work together using card-driven combat and deck building mechanics to overcome challenges at each portal level. On their turn, players play cards from their hand, roll dice to resolve encounters, and move through the grid-based game board. The game features programmed movement, where players must plan their actions in advance.

There are two ways to play: competitively (one against another) or cooperatively with all players working together as a team. Gather your friends and win this battle!

For 2 to 4 players, ages 8 and up. Average playtime: 40 minutes.

In Demonic Deli up to four chefs will race to create sandwiches and feed strange customers to earn points. To do so they will utilize ingredients that chain together for combo-turns, disruptive attacks, and the power of the Deli Demon.

Chefs will build sandwiches of ingredient cards, there are four types, cheese, protein, veggie, and condiment. Ingredients can play more ingredients of different types, creating a chain reaction.

There are also special powerful ingredient cards that have been infused with the power of the demon, these are called demonic cards. Demonic cards summon the demon to possess a customer and prevent them from being fed by any of the players.

Customers want sandwiches with specific types of ingredients on them. Feeding them a sandwich with the correct ingredients will score you points. The first player to get to 10 points wins the game, and the favor of the Deli Demon.

The Escalation of Violence is a supplement for the Mars: Code Aurora miniatures game.

In the near future, humanity, on the brink of suffocation, flees Earth, soon to be depleted, to take refuge on Mars and start over. But not everything goes as planned: a technical incident during flight affects one of the ships, causing thousands of deaths; the loss of the largest supplier of equipment for atmospheric domes during landing; social tensions related to the resulting confinement...

Humanity's new beginning is not what they had hoped for. To compensate for the lack of space and material shortages, mining and construction activities take on an extremely important role in the daily life of Colony-1. These are heavy, dangerous jobs, carried out in unknown territory... and they exacerbate the already existing tensions between human and hybrid workers. And for good reason: hybrids are increasingly taking precedence over their human counterparts, as they can be exploited shamelessly and paid a pittance without causing any public outcry. The situation quickly becomes explosive...

In this book, you will discover the events that led to the Ascreus Mons revolt, in a tense social context. The situation gradually deteriorated until the private militia Empire intervened, sent to stop the strike and get the miners back to work as quickly as possible.

We offer five interconnected narrative scenarios to replay the key moments of this historic event. Whether you want to restore order in the name of E.U.R.O.P.A. or try to free the hybrids from oppression, the future is in your hands. It is of course possible to play the scenarios using other factions to simulate other conflicts of your choice.

VS Aetheros is an expansion to Aetherium, making the base game possible to play solo.

On your turn you play as you do in the base game. On Aetheros turn, you will follow the steps on her mat:

Randomly grab a dice from the bag, roll it, place the element pawn in the corresponding action space, execute the actions based off the number rolled.

This expansion brings four varying difficulties.

—description from the publisher

Mars: Code Aurora is a skirmish game that allows you to recycle any type of SF miniatures.

The standard format is 25 points, which generally represents about ten miniatures per side. Here we offer a module adapted to lists reduced to 15 points. Your squads will therefore be smaller, with an average of six or seven miniatures.

But this game mode is not just about fielding fewer combatants, so we are proposing the following changes to make games even more immediate and fast-paced, while maintaining a good balance.

This format thus features more intense and close-quarters combat. Set-up time is also shorter!

boardgame: Fleet Command: Hevelius – Vignette 1: The Maganec Incident
description:
The Maganec Resource Network is the focal point for a budding civil war, with separatist and loyalist alike recruiting mercenaries to fight in their name. You can experience this conflict firsthand through a five-game branching campaign. Assume command of your fleet in high stakes scenarios where your decisions, victories, and defeats can shape the future of the Hevelien Expansion Zone.

This 2-player campaign can also be used by event organizers to run multiple tables. 
1,500 point games can be played in 1-1.5 hours each.

Fleet Command: Hevelius – Vignette 1: The Maganec Incident comes with:

     A short primer on the civil war threatening to spill over into an already tenuous region of space
     A five-mission, branching narrative that evolves based on who is winning at what stage of the campaign
     A short introduction for each of the eight total missions in the campaign, letting you get straight to battle while still knowing what you are fighting for
     Story hooks for bringing in any of the six factions in Fleet Command Hevelius onto the tabletop


Note: this product is designed to be used with Fleet Command Hevelius - Core Rules

boardgame: Fleet Command: Hevelius – Vignette 2: The Old Ways
description:
A Hegemon is dead. It falls to you to choose a successor. Across a three-mission campaign, assume command of your fleet and demonstrate your worthiness to lead a community across the stars.

This 2-player campaign can also be used by event organizers to run multiple tables. Games of 1,000 points can be played in 1-1.5 hours each.

Fleet Command: Hevelius – Vignette 2: The Old Ways comes with:

     A short primer on succession within the Tribes of the Zemin Exodus
     A 3-mission campaign narrative which judges your right to lead
     New terrain types (No Fly Zone and Safe Harbor) to support more dynamic battles


Note: this product is designed to be used with Fleet Command Hevelius - Core Rules

Hopping – Your strategic adventure through the fascinating world of stadiums!
This unique game is all about skillfully collecting cards and strategically laying them out to visit as many places as possible during an exciting trip around the world. But the real challenge lies in the uncertainty: no one knows exactly when the decisive scoring will take place by drawing a matchday card! Will your strategy lead to success, or will the game surprise you with an unexpected twist? Take on the challenge and find out which tactic works best!

—description from the designer

All aboard! Manage the chaos of the bus station. And the demands of your dear passengers.

GAME DESCRIPTION
Four colors, four destinations. On your turn, dock a bus by taking it from the garage or your hand: it becomes active. Board passengers of the same color/destination, first those in the front row of the waiting room, then, if possible, those you have in your hand.
The challenge is to have the right destinations and to make all 10 passenger personalities work together. Combine their powers as best you can for a quick and smooth boarding: bring the Lovers together, fit the Surfer in despite his board, obey the General without upsetting too many people, calm the Farmer's pig, charm the Star with her personal destination, and so on. Be patient.
Also, be careful: a bus only leaves if it's full… And its passengers only count for the player who finishes filling it! It's all about timing.
The player who has transported the most passengers will be the winner.

—description from the designer

On embarque ! Gérez le désordre de la gare des bus. Et les exigences de vos chers passagers.

DESCRIPTION DU JEU
Quatre couleurs, quatre destinations. A votre tour, mettez à quai un bus en le sortant du garage ou de votre main : il devient actif. Faites-y entrer les passagers de même couleur/destination, d’abord ceux en première ligne de la salle d’attente, puis si possible, ceux que vous avez en main. 
Le problème est que vous devez avoir les bonnes destinations, et faire cohabiter les 10 personnalités de passagers. Combinez au mieux leurs pouvoirs pour un embarquement rapide et serein : réunissez les Amoureux, casez le Surfeur malgré sa planche, obéissez au Général sans faire trop de mécontents, calmez le cochon du Fermier, cajolez la Star avec sa destination personnelle, etc, etc. Soyez patient.
Attention aussi : un bus ne part que s’il est plein… Et ses passagers ne comptent que pour le joueur qui finit de le compléter ! Question de timing.
Le joueur qui aura transporté le plus de passagers sera le vainqueur.

—description from the designer (French)

Rice, houses, pineapples
make your island a paradise!

GAME DESCRIPTION

Moluku is a trick-taking game unlike any other.

At the end of each trick, players each take
one card from the trick and place it on their island (place it
in front of them). The player who played the highest
card chooses first, and so on.

At the end of the round, when all the tricks have been
played, each player scores the points for their island.
Each player then takes back the cards from
their island: they will only use these cards for the
next round! The game is played over 3 rounds.

So what to do? Focus on the high cards, which
will win tricks in the next round, or the
lower cards—rice, pineapples, fish, and houses—whose
quantity and combinations are worth points immediately?

Masks, Volcanoes, and even the Fisherman
can also increase your score. And the Chief
or the Chameleon will save you from trouble.
It's up to you to find the right balance to bring happiness to your island!

—description from the designer

Riz, maison, ananas
faites de votre ile un Paradis !

DESCRIPTION DU JEU

Moluku est un jeu de plis pas comme les autres.
A la fin de chaque pli, les joueurs ramassent chacun
une seule carte du pli pour l'installer dans leur île (la
poser devant eux). Le joueur qui a joué la plus forte
carte choisit en premier, et ainsi de suite.
Et à la fin de la manche, quand tous les plis ont été
joués, chaque joueur marque les points de son île.
Chaque joueur reprend alors en main les cartes de
son île: c'est uniquement avec elles qu'il va jouer la
manche suivante ! La partie se joue en 3 manches.
Alors que faire ? Privilégier les cartes fortes, qui
gagneront des plis dans la manche suivante, ou les
cartes faibles, riz, ananas, poissons et maisons, dont
la quantité et les combinaisons valent tout de suite des
points?
Les Masques, les Volcans ou encore le Pêcheur
peuvent aussi augmenter votre score. Et le Chef
ou le Caméléon vous sauvent des mauvais plis.
A vous de trouver le juste équilibre pour faire le
bonheur de votre île !

—description from the designer (French)

On your turn, place a card on one of the two piles. Play several bonus actions in a row! Mean: Slow down your opponents by changing the colour or direction of the cards! Get rid of all your cards and end the round when you think you have the fewest points... But be careful – you don't know the value of some of the cards! Will you take the plunge or play it safe?

—description from the publisher

Einfach: In deinem Zug legst du eine Karte auf einen der 2 Stapel. Schlau: Spiele mehrere Bonusaktionen direkt hintereinander! Gemein: Bremse deine Gegner aus, indem du die Farbe oder Richtung der Karten änderst! So geht’s: Werde alle deine Karten los und beende die Runde, wenn du glaubst, dass du die wenigsten Punkte hast... Aber Vorsicht – du kennst den Wert mancher Karten nicht! Wagst du den „Sprung“ oder spielst du auf sicher?

—description from the publisher (German)

boardgame: Zombicide: Mission C51 – Raid the Police Station
description:
Designed by players for the Kyoko mission contest, and later published by the original studio, C missions are supplementary missions released online for print and play.

C51 is a medium difficulty exploration mission for Season 1. Toxic City Mall. Prison Outbreak. Zombie Dogs.

This town has nothing more to offer. It's time to leave. Roads aren't safe, you'd better equip with reliable stuff before taking this trip. Cars, guns, supplies... what about raiding a police station?

Designed by Aaron Lococo

Right then, adventurer! Tired of epic campaigns that fizzle out before you even find a decent tavern? TerraClash is the legendary quest you can actually finish in a single, glorious night.

It’s a tale of cards, dice, and uneasy alliances for 1-6 players. Dive in solo, team up with your mates, or have a proper free-for-all where backstabbing is practically encouraged.

There is also a long campaign option for those who love an epic adventure, with downloadable character sheets and rule customisations to suit.

In a nutshell, TerraClash is a "micro-campaign" that blends classic role-playing with fast-paced, competitive dice-rolling and deckbuilding action. The goal? Be the first hero to gather 8 Terra Coins, defeat the final Champion, and claim eternal glory!

—description from the designer

Keep your eyes on the fries! Join a flock of greedy seagulls in a beak-to-beak showdown to snag the tastiest beach treats. Play cards to your stash for big points, or GIVE them to opponents to trigger special actions. Swap, steal, and ruffle rivals' stashes—then pull off your secret 'End Gull' strategy to become the ultimate Beach Bandit!

Topa Ou Não Topa is a Brazilian board game inspired by the TV show of the same name, which is itself based on the international game show Deal Or No Deal. Published by Elka with graphic design by Eduardo Baptista, the game challenges players to win a fictional R$ 1,000,000.00 (one million reais) in a manner similar to the TV game show.

Players must use their luck and negotiation skills to decide whether to accept or reject offers, pushing their luck to try to walk away with the biggest prize possible. Just like in the TV show, each decision could bring you closer to the jackpot or leave you with nothing!

Perfect for first-time players! Jogo das Formas was specially designed for children aged 3+ to help in the learning process and develop brilliant minds. Super SIMPLE: place a card matching the color or shape of the card on the table and you're done! The first player to finish wins! It helps develop perception, differentiate similar and different stimuli, stimulates verbal and non-verbal language, and also trains alternating attention. Easy to play with quick rounds, it also teaches children that winning and losing is part of the fun.

In Ghouls, Ghosts and Teenagers, you take on the role of two teenagers from a mystery-hunting group. A monster is hiding in an abandoned house and needs to be captured, and who better than a bunch of nosy kids to get the job done? What they didn't expect is that the monster is already among them. The goal of the teenagers is to find clues, discover who the monster is, and capture it. The monster, on the other hand, wants to pick them off one by one.

On Halloween night, creatures roam the streets in search of candy, but only the most powerful will prevail. In this card game, your goal is to use your candy to win over scary monsters that will bring you more candy and more monsters. It works like an auction where players place secret bids on the monstrosities they desire. The highest bids win the creature. Once won, it becomes part of your deck, making it more powerful. However, for a card to enter your deck, another must leave. Be careful with your choices, as the sacrificed card will count toward your final score. Good girls don't play tricks, but what about you? Do you want to be a good girl or a MONSTRUOSA?

boardgame: Roll Camera!: Disaster Movie Story Pack
description:
The fifth (and final?) Story Pack for the award-winning cooperative filmmaking game Roll Camera! is a thematic tie-in with Almighty: Disaster Movie!

Includes 5x of a disaster movie.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Odkrywcy Nocnego Nieba: Satelity i Pulsary
description:
Satelity i Pulsary ("Satellites and Pulsars") is an expansion for Odkrywcy Nocnego Nieba (Night Sky Explorers), released worldwide as Star Explorer.

You continue your observations of the night sky. Even after many hours spent gazing at the glowing objects, you still can't get enough! You want more, and your patience is finally rewarded – among the stars, you begin to notice other objects and celestial bodies: satellites and pulsars! Additionally, from time to time you decide to take on extra challenges that change as the night progresses.

This expansion box contains three separate modules that can be combined with the base game, with each other, and with other expansions. These are:


     Satellites – introducing satellites that provide extra points for elements within constellations.



     Pulsars – featuring new stars that score based on connections and for crossing sector borders.



     Variable Condition cards – allowing you to earn additional points in every round formeeting special requirements.


Components:

     5 new night sky maps (double-sided)  
     5 scoring tiles (double-sided)
     5 white markers
     7 variable condition cards  


—description from the publisher

You're a System Administrator for the Extraction Directorate, managing resource acquisition from six Concessions spread across a distant star system. The Terran Consolidation issues three escalating quotas over 15 operational cycles. Miss a quota and you earn a Censure. Three Censures and you're reassigned to waste processing. There are no second chances.

Each turn, you roll 2d6 for Events that help or hinder your operation—equipment failures, budget cuts, windfall discoveries, or catastrophic system failures. You then allocate three Directives to Survey undiscovered Concessions, Activate production sites, Upgrade infrastructure, or manipulate your political standing with the Directorate. Only Concessions you activate this turn produce resources, forcing constant triage between building your engine and meeting immediate needs.

The game uses Phased Event Tables that evolve as you progress: early turns reward exploration with bonuses and discoveries, mid-game turns pressure your infrastructure with inspections and breakdowns, and late-game turns throw Catastrophes that can destroy entire Concessions. Each Concession has unique abilities—some produce bonus resources when paired together, others resist destruction, and one generates the Political Favor you need to manipulate dice rolls or prevent disasters.

You win by delivering all three Mandates on Turns 5, 10, and 15 without accumulating three Censures. Success means promotion. Failure means the Directorate finds other work for you.

—description from the designer

Wetworks comes from the Russian KGB slang term “mokroye delo” or wet dealings. An indication that in this line of work you’re likely to get your hands dirty.

The Wetworks card game is a competitive game of covert operations and assassination. A shadow organization is offering up big money for the elimination of some top targets around the globe!

Manage your hand of 7 cards. Collect matching card sets with stealth and secrecy. Choose your moment with care. Move the target out of the sights of your competitors and into your hands. Play your cards right and take out the targets for high rewards, or go for the ultimate score as the double agent!

Wetworks offers a unique mix of tension, luck, and skill for 2-6 players.

Theme

In Wetworks players take on the role of competing assassins trying to eliminate the fleeing enemy targets. The artwork reflects this theme, but is handled a bit lightheartedly to convey the idea of the theme, but making it palatable for most players of varying ages. Even with these considerations Wetworks presents a fun often tense play experience combining a good amount of strategy with the luck of the draw aspect of card games like Poker, Set Back and Rummy.

The game boards are broken down into six zones. Each zone has a unique icon and color associated with it, and has three areas of value 4, 5 or 6.Target markers start on the Entrance space on the left end of the game board, and move toward the exit space on the right when a matching movement card is played.

Set Up

Choose one side of a board to play on.

Shuffle the deck and place it near the board.

Assign the two targets a random colored base and place them in the (E) entrance space.

Deal seven cards to each player.

Game play

On your turn you may:

Play a combination of cards to eliminate a Target from their current Area.

Play a Target Defense card to increase the difficulty of one area.

Playe a Target Movement card to move the matching Target one space forward on their matching colored track.

Discard a card and draw a new card.

Removing a Target

Play Ops cards equaling the value of the Area a Target currently occupies to remove it, and gain points equal to the Zone reward.

Ops cards can be combined with Bonus Cards to manipulate the use of the Ops cards in removing a Target from the board.

When a Target is removed thet player gains the reward listed for the Zone.

Defense Cards

Defense cards increase the difficulty of the matching Area, increasing the point value of the Areas in that Zone by 1 Ops points. This can keep your opponents from eliminating a Target.

Target Movement Cards

Movement cards advance the matching Target one space along their matching colored track.

Discarding

A normal discard is one card from your hand of 7 cards. After a Target is eliminated you may discard a Movement or Defense card that matches the eliminated Target as a second discarded card.

Wetworks is a quick playing game. Rounds typically last 20 minutes or less, and a game of four rounds can play out in under an hour.

—description from the publisher

A great battle rages..... 
And the tale will be told in the sky!

Carrion Call Pocket Edition is all the excitement of Carrion Call, in an easy to carry size. Carrion Call is a light deck building game that tells the tale of a battle between two opposing armies. The battle rages on the ground, illustrated by Carrion Call’s card play. As casualties mount the crows and vultures begin to appear in the sky above, which acts as Carrion Call’s score tracking mechanism. You must balance the damage you lay upon your opponent against managing your own casualties to keep the Flock away from your front. Over four rounds you will use a unique card matching mechanism, and powerful bonus cards to try and overpower your opponent. After four rounds the player with the least number of points over their front is the winner!

Set up

Divide the cards into the blue deck (blue outline on the face), the orange deck (orange outline on the face of the card), and the bonus deck (red outline on the face of the card). Shuffle the three decks. You will take either the blue or orange deck and give the other to your opponent. Place the bonus deck face down on the play area.

Place the board between you and your opponent with a short edge facing you and your opponent.

Place the flock tokens near the board.

Game play

You will start with a deck of 10 cards identical to your opponent's. You and your opponent both draw a random flock token to determine who plays first, higher number wins.

On your turn you will play up to 3 cards from your hand. You may play more than one card if you can make a combo from matching cards. To match cards look at the shape and number of bird silhouettes on the long edge of the card. If you have a match, you may play those cards together.

The cards come in two types. Offensive cards have black silhouettes in the top area of the card. Defensive cards have gray silhouettes in the top area. Offensive cards will allow you to place matching tokens from the flock into the flight path heading away from you (see arrows on the board). Defensive cards will allow you to move matching flock tokens from above your front into the flight path heading away from you (again, see the arrows on the board). The tokens must match the bird, or groups of birds, pictured on the card. You may not add tokens together to get the number of birds.

Take turns playing cards from your hand and moving or placing tokens, until one player has drawn their last card. This signals the end of the round. Each player get to play one more card, then the round is over.

Count up all the points from tokens over your front. Crows are face value (1, 2, or 3 points) and vultures are 5. The player with the least amount of points will draw cards from the bonus deck.

If you have the lowest total points, draw 3 cards and look at them. Bonus cards have either gray, red, or a combination of both on the face. The gray function the same as the defensive cards, move those matching tokens from over your front into the flight path. The red birds allow you to move the matching tokens from over your front over to your opponents front, skipping the flight path. Choose one of the cards to add to your deck for the next round, and pass the other two to your opponent. They choose one of the two remaining cards, and discard the third face down. You are now ready to play the next round.

Play continues for a total of 4 rounds, adding a bonus card to your deck after rounds 1, 2, and 3. At the end of the fourth round tokens are counted again and the player with the lowest total is the winner.

—description from the publisher

Tension Junior is a fast-paced team trivia game designed for younger players. Teams compete to name as many correct answers as possible within a one-minute time limit based on a specific topic printed on a card. Topics are tailored for children and may include themes such as animals, foods, fictional characters, or everyday objects.

The challenge is that only ten predetermined answers count as correct, and these answers are known to the game moderator reading the card. Teams must quickly suggest answers while the timer runs, hoping their guesses match the approved list. Each correct answer advances the team’s marker along the board.

Special board spaces introduce additional twists to the gameplay, adding variety and strategic moments to the otherwise quick rounds. The first team to reach the finish space wins the game. Tension Junior emphasizes quick thinking, teamwork, and general knowledge in a format suitable for families and younger players.

This educational board game was first presented at the 2017 Seoul Queer Culture Festival as part of the Transgender Queer Visibility Project organized by the collective Do Learn Do Run(두런두런). The game is designed to provide players with an experiential understanding of the social challenges faced by transgender and gender-queer individuals, while encouraging discussion about solidarity and collective action.

Goal and Win Condition

Happiness tokens function as both the game's score and in-game currency.

Players aim to accumulate the highest number of Happiness tokens while completing one full circuit of the board.

A player is eliminated if their number of Happiness tokens reaches zero.

The player with the most Happiness tokens at the end of the game wins.

Rules Overview

All players are assigned transgender character cards. These cards contain information about the character's gender identity and dysphoria.
Players roll a four-sided die numbered 1–4.
On a roll of 1–3, the player moves forward that number of spaces.
On a roll of 4, the player may choose to move 1–3 spaces.

Players receive a base amount of Happiness tokens at the starting space.
If a player has at least the minimum required number of tokens to undertake medical transition, they may spend the specified cost to carry out a transition step.
Each space on the board represents situations or statements that transgender individuals may encounter in society.
Landing on spaces containing discriminatory or hateful remarks causes players to lose Happiness tokens.
Landing on spaces depicting gender-affirming situations grants Happiness tokens.
When landing on a "Passing Die" space, the player rolls a die to determine the passing criteria applied for that turn.
If the criteria are met, the player gains points; if not, they lose points.
On legal gender recognition spaces, penalties apply only if the player has not obtained their desired legal gender status.
As more medical transition steps are completed, the cost of legal gender recognition decreases.
Additionally, as more players undergo transition, both the cost of medical transition and the minimum token requirement are reduced.

You now stand in the hallowed grounds of the Taskmaster House. The entrance has locked firmly shut behind you. A series of puzzles blocks your path to freedom.

To escape, you must complete the tasks, construct the Taskmaster House, and follow the clues. The Taskmaster’s Assistant, Little Alex Horne, has provided helpful hints should you need them. Nothing is included by accident. All the information is in the box.

Designed for solo or group play, Taskmaster Escape Room is ideal for fans of the show and escape room enthusiasts alike. It combines escape room mechanics and structured challenges in a focused problem-solving experience.

Read carefully. Observe everything. Your time starts now.

​Step into the enchanting world of Zanziar with A Seance in Eight, the thrilling card-shedding game for 3 to 7 players. Take on the role of a mysticism driven elf, racing to outwit your opponents and connect with mysterious spirits from the past. Play cards of any suit as long as they outrank the previous play to score points, and keep the momentum going. If you miss your chance to score, use a 'Change the Subject' cards to reset the board or risk suffering psychic feedback in the form of negative points. Every decision brings you closer to victory—or a haunting setback!

Designed for card-shedding and trick-taking aficionados, A Seance in Eight is the perfect quick, light filler that fits any game night. With adjustable gameplay for 3 to 7 players, simply remove higher ranks for smaller groups and enjoy a consistently fast-paced experience. Its compact, travel-ready box means you can bring the excitement anywhere, and its engaging gameplay shines at every player count. Bring your friends together and let the spirits set the pace!

Gameplay
Play cards to follow a question thread (Suit)trying to top your fellows with better questions (higher Ranks) to score Mystic points. But beware, sometimes the spirits get annoyed and you will suffer psychic backlash.

The game uses octagonal ring (octoring), that will have put cards around so each suit is represented at the start of the game. Players get cards (the amount is based on the player count). Then they start playing the tricks.

The dealer plays a card and the other players follow in clockwise order. They can not skip, but they can choose:


     Play a face up card of rank 1-9 to empty place in octoring or beat a matching suit in octoring. The replaced card will score 2 mystery points to you.
     Play a face up 'change the subject' card to vacant a slot in the octoring. No points are gained or lost.
     Play a card face down to replace any card in the octoring. The card you replaced will decrease your mystic points by its value.


Players play until the players are out of cards. You calculate the points for the round and start a new round. Typically you play as many rounds as there are players, but you can choose other number.

Scoring:

     Each player totals up the Mystic Points for the cards in their pile. 
     Each face down card scores+2.
     Each face up card scores the Rank value in negative.

Rats Extend, the official expansion that brings even more chaos, speed, and laughs to the Pat4 universe! Here, new colorful rats and the mysterious gray rats enter the board to create surprising combinations and unpredictable plays. This expansion was made to make the game even more dynamic, strategic, and fun — but remember: to play, you need the original Pat4. Get ready to chase the best three-of-a-kind… before the rats escape.

—description from the publisher

You can play two different characters in this heroic fantasy adventure gamebook set in the world of Austerion.

Explore an open world freely on the regional map (with a “choose-your-own-adventure” style mechanism), collect treasure cards, recruit sailors, and find the right equipment.

No dice rolls in this gamebook! Your destiny is shaped by your decisions and your preparation (equipment).

Once you have acquired your ship, the adventure moves to the high seas. Study the map and chart your course through maneuvers and battles to reach the pirates of Blood Island!

Bears. Bees. Battling? Players try to outwit and outflank their opponent to get the most honey. What makes it work are the elegant placement and scoring rules that require both players to think carefully about their own strategy… and their opponent’s.

—description from the publisher

With the re-election of President Ronald Reagan in November of 1984, the Soviet Union realizes that they cannot compete with the military spending of the United States. They end their adventure in Afghanistan and turn their eyes westward. They start by sending covert operatives via commercial airlines throughout the United States to prep for their imminent invasion through Canada and down into the American heartland. Along with their Cuban allies who invade northward through Mexico, the communists link up and control the area between the Rockies and the Mississippi, from border to border.

Into this abyss, Lieutenant James Clark, United States Army, 7th Special Forces Group, is sent along with his teammates: Sergeant Hill, Corporal Jackson, and Specialist Rivera. Their mission: to disrupt the Russian forces occupying the Midwest town of Springfield and to recruit and train any civilians acting as partisans in the neighboring foothills.

You will command the Green Berets and any partisans that join them in this new game from designer Raymond Fiore. Fight as guerillas in the farmlands and backstreets of small-town middle-America. Ambush Russian supply convoys; free hostages from the drive-in theater turned concentration camp; take over the radio station and broadcast a message of hope to the American residents, all of this and much more. Can you lead your men through some of the toughest missions ever carried out on American soil? Find out in Operation Red Dawn!

—description from the publisher

In Cadê o Cadu? the challenge is to be the first person to uncover a precise piece of information: the location of Cadu in the "Wonderland" theme park.

Use Intelligence Cards to gather clues and sharpen your reasoning to reach the most accurate conclusions before your opponents. Roll the die, perform the indicated action, gather information, and take notes to deduce where Cadu is hiding!

If you have a guess, reveal Cadu's location and silently check the answer on the back of the round's Challenge Card. If you're wrong, you're out and must keep the answer secret. If you're right, you win! The game stimulates logical reasoning, concentration, and attention to detail.

With 118 Intelligence Cards, it offers great replayability and intriguing clue combinations!

Bibliodeck is a game for 2 to 6 players that combines strategy, fun, and learning.

The goal is to conquer the most libraries by forming sets composed of a classic author and two of their works. In the end, whoever controls the most libraries wins.

Easy to learn and engaging to play, Bibliodeck is perfect both for literature lovers and for those just beginning to explore this universe. Games last 20 to 30 minutes, depending on the number of participants.

Long ago the Gems of Power were wielded by Heroes and Wizards. Commanding and combining them to subdue the powers of evil, and drive them from the land. Over time the heroes vanished and the Wizards aged, and retired. The evil returned, hiding in the dark depths and causing havoc among the lands above.

The Gems have been found again... it is time to purge the evil once again WITH MORE HEROES!!

This Dungeon2(squared) Expansion contain 4 new characters for the Dungeon2(squared) base game.

The Phantom Knight has the ability to *Phase*, and meet the Safe Passage challenge using only the 3 gem card, however when taking this option no other Rewards can be claimed that round.

The Monk has 3 empty crystals ready to be filled with purple, orange, green or white, by combining them with mixed gems of those colors.

The Assassin may use their 3 gem card as 3 blue or 3 yellow gems toward their set of challenges, or as three green gems toward a reward from another character's set of challenges.

The Alchemist has a 3 gem card with one of each red, yellow, and blue gems. Two of these gems may be combined toward an Alchemist's challenge, or the 3 gem card may be used to discard a 2 gem Treasure card to draw a Magic Item card.

In Dungeon2 (squared) take on the role of one of four characters and face the challenges of a randomly created dungeon.

Brave heroes prepare yourselves for  adventure.....

Base game play description:

Playing 4 cards from your 6 card character deck provides you with a number of primary colored gems. These gems may then be mixed to produce a number of secondary color gems which are used to succeed at the challenges of the dungeon.


     Meet the "Safe Passage" challenge of a room to safely pass through.
     Meet the "Treasure" challenge of a room to collect a Treasure Card to add to your Character Deck. Increasing your, or another player's, resource pool.
     Meet the "Magic Item" challenge of a room to gain a Magic Item that may provide you more gems or heal your wounds.
     Meet the "Gold" challenge of a room to collect Gold, which counts as victory points toward being crowned Champion of the Dungeon!


Each original character deck has one card with three gems which may be used for its face value, or for its Special Power which may effect either yourself or another character.


     Warrior: Draws an extra card or can "pull" another character along to the next room.
     Cleric: Use your protection spell to stave off damage for yourself, or another player.
     Thief: Steal the top treasure card from the deck to add to your, or another player's, character deck.
     Mage: Reduce the Challenge for another player by 2.


—description from the publisher

Pulse is a game about investigation through time, created for the Game Chef competition in May 2013. After winning the Brazilian stage, the game underwent some light revisions to improve the text and gameplay, making it clearer to learn and play. In September 2013, it was announced as the World Champion of the competition among over 160 games.

Just like a detective at a crime scene, you investigate a scene by suggesting hypotheses to determine what probably happened beforehand. Players will draw pulses from each event, going back in time to question their origins in the past, and new events will emerge using the intersections between each pulse to raise new possibilities.

For 3 to 5 players, with an average duration of an hour and a half per game, which can be extended if players wish to delve deeper into the story they have created.

Created by illustrator and caricaturist Emilio Damiani Jr, Quadrilha is a modern and appealing territory conquest game that demands concentration and keen attention. It stimulates observation and reflexes in an intelligent and dynamic way.

Players compete to control territories on the board by recreating a specific formation pattern. At the start of the game, players define a central reference model that represents the structure they must reproduce.

The goal is to occupy territories in a way that exactly matches this predefined formation using your own pieces. The first player to successfully reproduce the required pattern wins the game.

A fast-paced game where you must stay alert to your opponents' moves to win, while also encouraging social interaction. It requires strategic and mathematical reasoning, and having a broad, spatial, and accurate vision is essential.

Restore the glory of a bygone age and celebrate the 10th Anniversary of Unearth with this all-new edition. This edition has modernized graphic design, new expansion content, and updated gameplay!

Unearth is a bend-your-luck game of dice placement and set collection. Designed by Jason Harner and Matthew Ransom, it plays in under an hour with 2-4 players. Each player leads a tribe of Delvers, represented by five dice (3 six-sided, 1 four-sided, and 1 eight-sided). Players take turns rolling and placing dice in an attempt to claim Ruins. High rolls help players claim Ruins, while low rolls help players collect Stones. This opens two paths to victory: claiming sets of Ruins or using Stones to build Wonders. Delver cards help you affect your dice rolls or dice in play, and Wonders can grant abilities that impact the late game.

The 10th Anniversary Edition contains The Lost Tribe expansion and a new expansion titled Buried Lords. The Lost Tribe contains new cards and tokens, a solo campaign Against The Darkness, and a fifth player expansion. With Unearth: Buried Lords, dig up monuments to those once worshipped by your ancestors. Unlock your lost culture with cards that grant bonuses throughout the game, and play Songs on Ruins to change the shape of the world.

—description from the publisher
family: Versions & Editions: Big Box Versions of Individual Games

description:
From the explosion of the Night Sun, the Saurian Tribes were born — dinosaurs that evolved in an incredible way and transformed into wise and powerful civilizations. Driven by the desire for power of their sages and leaders, these tribes went to war over the fragments of the star that remained, which possess incredible powers. Lead one of these tribes in search of the knowledge that will determine which tribe will become the great civilization that will dominate all others!

In the game, players control dinosaur tribes competing for dominance across a divided territory. The main goal of the game is to complete three spells. To do this, players must collect territory cards obtained by controlling regions on the board. Once a player gathers the required combination of cards and performs three spells, they win the game.

Welcome to the war for the fragments, welcome to Dino Wars!

boardgame: Pokémon TCG: Mega Evolution – Phantom Flames Elite Trainer Box
description:
Pokémon Trading Card Game: Mega Evolution—Phantasmal Flames Elite Trainer Box is a premium accessory and booster collection for the Pokémon Trading Card Game. The product is designed for players and collectors interested in cards from the Phantasmal Flames expansion of the Mega Evolution series.

The box includes multiple booster packs from the expansion along with a full-art foil promotional card featuring Charcadet. In addition to collectible cards, the set provides gameplay accessories such as card sleeves, dice used for tracking damage counters, Energy cards, and markers required for standard Pokémon TCG play.

A player’s guide to the Phantasmal Flames expansion is included to introduce the mechanics and notable cards from the set. The contents are stored in a themed collector’s box with dividers, allowing players to organize cards and accessories. The product functions as a supplement for the Pokémon Trading Card Game rather than a standalone game.

—description from the publisher

Collect the magical items before everyone else to win! Help Doctor Strange find the Magical Items he needs to defend the planet!
