Academic Marathon is a playful board game that simulates the adventures of student life, from lectures and colloquiums to the awarding of diplomas. Players will find 66 challenging boxes, reflecting the real life of a student, from everyday worries to unexpected challenges. It is a cooperative board game where you and your friends work together to complete your studies as smoothly as possible and get the highest evaluation.
Work with other players to reveal map spaces, overcome challenges, and reach the “Graduation Ceremony” space to complete your studies before the time runs out.

Luau Rummy is a fun, fast family card game designed so everyone can actually play together — including young kids.

It’s a simplified rummy-style game where players collect sets of supplies, gourmet items, building materials, piggies, and party animals to score points and throw the ultimate luau. Rounds are quick (just 5–10 minutes), so you can play one game or keep the fun going for hours.

Gameplay: Help Big Bad Wolf prepare a luau party by collecting supplies, gourmet items, building materials, piggies, and party animals to complete melds.

Perfect for:


     Families with young children (ages 5+)
     Mixed-age game nights
     Anyone who wants real strategy without complicated rules


Key Features


     2–4 players (use two decks for 3–4 players or teams)
     5–10 minute rounds
     Easy to learn, with enough depth to stay interesting for adults
     Beautiful, colorful illustrations
     High quality, durable linen card stock
     Simple enough for little hands, fun for the whole family


Whether you’re looking for a cozy evening game at home or a quick, engaging activity during gatherings, Luau Rummy delivers laughter, friendly competition, and real family bonding — without anyone feeling left out or overwhelmed.

Game Instructions: Click here

App: Download the iPad App or play in your Web Browser.

—description from the publisher

The pages of Scripture come alive. Patriarchs, kings, prophets, and priests — all call for your attention. But time runs short, and only the wisest will prevail.

In Bibliostador, you are a scholar of the ancient world. Gather legendary figures, visit holy places, and invoke events that changed the course of history. Build a tableau of cards that tells the greatest narrative ever known.

But you are not alone. Other scholars seek the same treasures. Choose wisely from the Market, play your cards strategically, and fulfill the ancient prophecies before your rivals. Every choice matters — every card is a piece of the divine puzzle.

Who will be the ultimate Bibliostador?

—description from the designer

Five players work to solve (or cover up their involvement in) the murder of a young girl at a prestigious London drama school. Each player is given their own specific character and personality, and must work with the information they are given to achieve their goals. Also features the cast of the Ave Mujica band from Bushiroad's BanG Dream! franchise.

Caos, velocidad y los productos que no conoce ni su madre

Un nuevo supermercado cutrón ha llegado al barrio y ¡al fin alguien tiene todos vuestros productos NISU de confianza!
Al fondo a la derecha encontraréis almóndigas, marracones, dilo hental, servilleyas… y muchos más.

Descartaros de vuestras cartas con productos de dudosa procedencia será vuestra misión pero… 
¿Seréis capaces de hacerlo sin cortocircuitar?

Of Wheat & Wonders puts you in charge of building the mightiest empire across a variety of ever-changing scenarios.

Gather resources, construct villages and cities, and unlock powerful developments and discoveries... all while facing devastating disasters during your 20-year reign.
Will your legacy endure for centuries?

Of Wheat & Wonders is a DPnP (Dynamic Print & Play) game, distributed through a digital configurator that lets you generate and randomize scenarios—creating virtually unlimited maps, civilization sheets, buildings, and more.
No two reigns are ever the same.

Additionally, you can design your own scenarios and share their seeds with the pagepunk community, challenging other players to overcome your unique creations.

—description from the designer

The front is shifting. Behind enemy lines, soldiers cut off from command face a fate worse than defeat — capture, interrogation, and silence. These are the Lost Ranks: four Jacks separated from their armies, unable to fight their way back alone.

A lone Spy moves through the chaos. Getting your operative home is a coup. Losing them to the enemy is a disaster.

Lost Ranks is a team trick-taking game built on the tactical foundation of Spades and Whist. Two armies contest five high-value targets across a series of engagements — rescuing their own soldiers, seizing the enemy's, and maintaining the force strength to outlast the opposition. Every trick is a battlefield decision. Every captured Jack is a life that hangs in the balance.

Coordinate with your teammate. Control the trump. Bring your soldiers home.

—description from the publisher

Designed by players for the Kyoko mission contest, and later published by the original studio, C missions are supplementary missions released online for print and play.

C52 is a hard difficulty exploration mission for Toxic City Mall. Prison Outbreak.

Before the invasion, one of your teammate had a spy contact nicknamed Skeleton Key, the kind holding a licence to kill with cool toys. You spotted his base, but need Skeleton Key's biometrics to get in. The catch? He became a zombie!

Designed by Aaron Lococo

Designed by players for the Kyoko mission contest, and later published by the original studio, C missions are supplementary missions released online for print and play.

C53 is a hard difficulty exploration mission for Season 1.

Eddie, a lone survivor, got his new shelter next to your own. Unfortunately, your new neighbor is insane and targets everything in sight. You have to sort him out before he attracts a lethal zombie wave!

Designed by Mark Broadbridge

boardgame: Heroscape: Utgar's Rage – Heroes of Barrenspur Army Expansion
description:
Contents:

4 UNIQUE HERO miniatures: Khosumet the Darklord, Krug, Me-Burq-Sa, Sir Denrick

2x Sand Tiles (2-hex)

"The battles are getting tougher. New heroes are needed to lead the armies to victory. Utgar has summoned a Darklord, a Beast and a Warlord to help lead his troops. Jandar has summoned the champion Sir Denrick for support. These unique heroes bring special powers and abilities to your armies."

Khosumet strengthens Anubian Wolves and other units.

Krug hits harder the more wounded he is.

Me-Burq-Sa brings speed and range to the Marro armies.

Sir Denrick excels at slaying giants!

Additional photos and forum posts can be found on the now deprecated entry, Heroscape Expansion Set: Utgar's Rage .

boardgame: The Spanish Passion: A Wargamer's Guide to The Spanish Civil War 1936-1939
description:
The Spanish Civil War is often reduced to a simple ideological struggle between socialism and fascism, but such a view fails to capture the depth and complexity of a conflict that tore a nation apart. Spain in the 1930s was a society in flux—a country where political idealism clashed with traditional religious values, where ancient regional identities vied with modernising forces, and where ordinary people were swept into a whirlwind of violence, upheaval, and transformation. With the rise of the Republican government, these tensions ignited into a brutal civil war—one that would become a harbinger of the wider global conflict to come.

For military historians and wargamers alike, the Spanish Civil War offers an extraordinarily rich and layered field of study. It was one of the first truly modern conflicts, serving as a proving ground for new technologies and tactical doctrines. Aerial bombing of civilian targets, combined arms operations involving tanks and mechanised infantry, rapid strategic redeployments such as the airlift of the Army of Africa, and innovative urban defensive tactics—as seen in the Republican stand around the University of Madrid—foreshadowed the strategic dynamics of the Second World War. At the same time, remnants of the First World War endured: trench lines, static positions, and prolonged sieges all featured prominently, underlining the hybrid nature of the fighting.

The war also drew in powerful international players. Germany’s Condor Legion, Mussolini’s Italian forces, and Soviet advisors and materiel flooded into Spain, turning it into a global stage for ideological and military confrontation. This external support brought with it a dramatic diversification in uniforms, equipment, and weaponry, transforming the appearance of both Nationalist and Republican forces over time. The evolution of these forces is charted in detail within this volume, which includes an invaluable guide to the varied armaments, formations, and battlefield styles that characterised the war.

Beyond its historical significance, the Spanish Civil War has long fascinated the wargaming community. Its combination of large-scale set-piece battles and smaller skirmishes lends itself naturally to tabletop recreation. Many of the war’s engagements are ideal for one-session scenarios, offering clear objectives and manageable force sizes for players. Whether you are looking to game the sweeping offensives of the Ebro, the desperate defence of Madrid, or the dynamic clashes in Aragon or the Basque Country, this guide provides a wealth of inspiration, supported by scenarios, troop breakdowns, and practical advice.

Miniature manufacturers have increasingly recognised the period’s popularity, producing a wide array of figures, terrain, and vehicles to support both 28mm and smaller-scale games. Enthusiasts will also find an abundance of historical source material—much of it newly accessible in English as well as Spanish—to deepen their understanding and enrich their gameplay.

The Spanish Passion is both a historical introduction and a practical resource: a gateway into a conflict of enormous human, political, and military consequence, and a toolkit for those eager to explore its battles on the tabletop. Whether you are a seasoned gamer, a newcomer to interwar wargaming, or a student of modern military history, this book offers everything you need to bring the Spanish Civil War to life.

—description from the publisher

A Blossoming strategy game.

Pressed is a 2-5 player set collection, drafting card game where you play as competing botanical artists, planting, picking, and pressing flowers to craft a beautiful collection of flowers by artist Beth Sobel.

Place a flower into the flower bed and score that flower. Each flower scores differently and it scores usually based on like flowers in the same row and column.

Once everyone has placed 4 flowers into the flower bed, you will draft a column of cards. Four of these cards will be pressed to be scored at the end of the game and the rest of discarded.

Next round, a new flower bed is made and new hand of cards dealt. After three rounds you will score your pressed flowers: place each flower type together and score it.

Rival butchers compete to achieve the leanest cuts of meat by winning as few tricks as possible—ideally just one.

A twist on a trick-avoidance game, players gain blood when they win tricks, which can be spent to trim the value of their cards in future tricks and avoid extra wins. Each player can use their cleaver to split a trick into two separate tricks, creating new tactical choices. At the end of each round, the player who wins the fewest tricks (winning zero tricks does not qualify) is the round winner, with blood used as a tiebreaker if needed. The first player to win three rounds wins the game.

—description from the designer

Sunlit's Eulogy is a highly thematic, strategy board game for 3-6 players. It takes place in the moments following the death of the First Prophet & the turmoil that erupts. You play as one of the key disciples, trying to sway the faith towards your sect through clever strategizing & diplomacy, while ultimately trying to strengthen the faith for millennia to come... or to collapse it entirely.

At its heart, the game is an exploration of mourning & loss. Not only of death, but of the loss of faith, the loss of kinship & trust. It is a game that strives to create memorable social puzzles built on paper thin agreements, & conflict that is often forgiven but rarely forgotten.

The Orphanage was only the beginning. Now, the brave must venture into the dark.

Hidden Realms: A Monstrous Expedition is a standalone sequel to the hit "no-turns" card game Teddies vs Monsters. While the first game saw the Teddies defending the safety of the Orphanage, Hidden Realms takes the fight to the enemy. Realizing that the monsters will never stop coming unless their source is understood, a brave group of Teddy Scouts has decided to step through the dimensional rifts to explore the chaotic realms beyond.

Gameplay Overview

In this tile-placement, exploration, and set-collection game, 2 to 4 players (with a planned expansion for up to 6) take on the roles of elite Teddy Scouts. As you move through the shifting dimensions, you will physically build the map of the "Hidden Realms" by placing tiles, uncovering strange landmarks, and encountering the bizarre creatures that dwell within.

The game is semi-cooperative: while all Teddies must work together to survive the dangers of the expedition and manage the threats of the realms, only one Scout will gain enough renown to be named the Lead Explorer.

Your Mission: The Scout’s Bestiary

The heart of the game is your Scout’s Bestiary. Players must gather vital information, monster tracks, and rare essence samples to complete their research. Through clever set collection and strategic exploration, you will document the weaknesses and origins of the monsters, providing the Guild with the knowledge needed to end the nightmare once and for all.

—description from the publisher

You wake up. Your throat is dry. Your head is throbbing.

Hello?

Hi!

Please don't freak out.

But I'm afraid...

YOU'VE DIED.

DUST is a handcrafted journey through purgatory that transforms early adventure video games into a card based experience. On your own or with a friend, explore the vast world of the afterlife. DUST has no rulebook: the moment you open the box, the game has begun. Learn its mechanics as you play, whether from strange creatures, lost souls, or hand-drawn notes. Conserve scarce resources as you flip through difficult decisions—you get the feeling you're being watched. Gameplay splits across three acts, giving natural intermissions to your adventure and places to return to if you want to unlock unique interactions, locations, and endings. This is our most inventive and alluring title yet, one where mechanics and narrative turn the experience on its head again and again.

The time has come to meet your fate. Are you ready for the world of DUST?

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Heroscape: Malliddon's Prophecy – Heroes of Bleakwoode Army Expansion
description:
Malliddon's Prophecy added 4 expansion packs to the game of Heroscape. This is one of them.

Contents:
5 Miniatures: 
Taelord the Kyrie Warrior
Kelda the Kyrie Warrior
Venoc Warlord
Marcus Decimus Gallus
Tornak
1 Glyph:
Glyph of Lodin
Terrain pieces:
1x Grass Tile (2-hex)
1x Rock Tile (1-hex)
1x Sand Tile (1-hex)

Despite only saying "Flying" on his Army Card, Taelord the Kyrie  Warrior actually has "Stealth Flying" which allows him to fly without taking Leaving Engagement Attacks. The wording of his FLYING is different than all others.
 "The battle is getting tougher. New heroes are needed to lead the armies to victory. Jandar, Ullar, Einar, and Utgar are seeking reinforcements. These unique heroes bring special powers and abilities to your army. Taelord's and Kelda's ability to fly enable them to move over obstacles such as water, ruins, and their enemies. The Venoc Warlord can slither through water without stopping, while Marcus and Tornak add strength to their comrades. The Glyph of Lodin increases the luck of your army."

Additional photos and forum posts can be found on the now deprecated entry, Heroscape Expansion Set: Malliddon's Prophecy .

Kubble is a word game that plays like manipulation rummy, and has several features to make it family-friendly: 
- Each player has their own set of letter tiles and letter rack in their own color. 
- There is a skill balancing system that lets beginners play with experts.

The game starts with everyone drawing 14 letters from their own bag. In their first turn a player must first lay down a word using 6+ letters from their rack, and after that and in later turns they may make words of 5+ letters, using a combination of letters from their rack and from words on the table, as long as those words are left as valid words of 4+ letters.  If a player cannot make a new word on their turn, they draw a tile from their bag and pass.  A round ends when a player empties their rack; each player adds the point values of all the letters of their color on the table, and subtracts 1 point per letter in their rack.  The first player to 150 points wins.

The skill balancing mechanic sets minimum limits for the length of words: for their first word played in the round, for their subsequent new words in the round, and for the words on the table that have had letters taken from them.  The levels are:


     [Level name]: [min length first word] / [min length later new words] / [min length changed table words]



     Junior: 4 / 3 / 2 
     Beginner: 5 / 4 / 3
     Regular: 6 / 5 / 4 (as described above)
     Advanced: 7 / 6 / 5

boardgame: Heroes of Barcadia: Spring Booster Pack
description:
Breathe new life into your dungeon with the Heroes of Barcadia Spring Booster Pack! This vibrant seasonal expansion introduces a fresh room type... The Ally, along with a garden’s worth of new Power‑Ups, house rules, and room tiles designed to shake up your next quest.

Add 5 Power‑Ups, 5 House Rule Cards, and 5 Room Tiles to your game for a delightfully unpredictable seasonal twist. After all, Spring is the perfect time to freshen up your dungeon crawl with a little extra color (and a few extra sips).

Product Specifications:

Includes:
- 5 Waterproof Power-Up Cards

ALL cards in this game are made of PVC and are 100% waterproof

Includes Heroes of Barcadia Spring Booster Pack ONLY — Full game NOT included

—description from the publisher

Word Golf uses a clever mechanic to make a word game play like golf.  There are 9 or 18 holes (or rounds). For each hole, each player picks 5 of the 90 tiles, and then draws random tiles to bring the total held to 11, 12, or 13, depending on the hole. Everyone then has 2 minutes to spell words using all their letters if possible.  A player scores 1 stroke per word, plus 1 per letter not used -- up to a maximum of 6 strokes per hole -- so the object is to spell the fewest, longest words using the available letters. The winner is the player with the lowest total score after all the holes are played.

Rule of 7 puts players in the role of street gang upstarts building a crew of their own.
Each round, players simultaneously play and reveal two action cards. Each card targets one of two specific gangs which you can attack, or defend if they are in your neighborhood. Card values determine initiative order, and every successful attack or defense earns you a new gang member. First player to recruit 7 members wins.

Players play in partnership with a standard deck of cards.

Suits rank in a cycle ...SCDHSCDHS... for trump purposes. If two or three consecutive suits are played to a trick, the relative highest-ranked suit acts as trumps. If all four suits are played, then all suits are trumps, with ties going to the last one played.

Sides score points by winning tricks that contain combinations of cards similar to those in Cribbage: 15s, 31s, flushes, pairs, prials, double-prials, runs, running flushes, and the Jack of the 'seasonal suit', Jack Havel.

The side reaching the target score first wins a game, with a rubber going to side reaching a specified number of games.

—description from the designer

Every four years, the students of Lund throw a carnival and now you have the chance to help build the finest carnival train while visiting some of the most famous places along the train’s route through the city.

Alea Ad Astra - Celestialspelet is a roll-and-write game for any number of players. Each round, a chosen player rolls the three dice and then all players use the results in the way they wish on their game form.

The game continues until someone has reached at least the sum 70 on the carnival train or 25 game rounds have been completed. The player who has collected the highest amount of stars from the different city locations is the winner!

boardgame: Martinstaat 1744: A detailed wargame campaign set in a fictitious world
description:
A completely imaginary campaign, set in an imaginary world, fought by two imaginary countries over the territory of a third...

Sounds crazy? But not for wargamers, who have been undertaking such hobby projects for decades. Some players prefer the outright fantasy of pseudo-medieval worlds such as those described by J R R Tolkien, or made famous by games such as Warhammer or Dungeons and Dragons. But others, coming from the historical side of the hobby, have been well served by what is known as 'Ruritanian' literature such as the Prisoner of Zenda, where the action plays out in a world that looks suspiciously like our own, but with a few kinks.

It is in this genre that Henry Hyde has become well-known in the wargaming world, picking up the threads of such greats in the early hobby such as Charles Grant and Brigadier Peter Young, who fought countless battles and campaigns set in their own fictitious settings as, indeed, did wargaming pioneer H G Wells. Nowadays, wargamers have coined the term "imagi-nations" for such forays into not-quite-history, and hundreds of gamers around the world now collect armies that bear a suspicious resemblance to those of Prussia, Austria, France, Britain or the patchwork of Germanic micro-states during the eighteenth century and play games ranging from small skirmishes to huge, pitched battles and manoeuvre their armies across maps that are entirely made up by the players.

It is in this context that Martinstaat 1744 is firmly rooted. Back in the early 1990s, Henry and his chum Guy Hancock, both history graduates, leading the imaginary armies of Prunkland and Faltenland, pitched their wits against one another in a classic horse-and-musket era campaign, invading the territory of neutral Martinstaat in an attempt to outflank each other's northern border and grab natural resources and taxes to swell their own state coffers. This was part of what became known as The Wars of the Faltenian Succession.

This scenario, in itself, is not unique in wargaming—but what set this campaign apart was Henry's meticulous record-keeping in the pre-digital era, with page after page of beautiful, neat, handwritten notes and maps showing move-by-move precisely where the opposing forces were located. It was after posting a few pages of these notes online that a clamour grew amongst fans for him to turn these intricate records into a book—and here it is, including the full campaign rules used for that season.

In addition, Henry did some wonderful watercolour renditions of some of the troops, at the time when he was actually designing the uniforms for both armies (no surprise that he became a graphic designer and illustrator soon afterwards) and in creating this extraordinary volume, he has added more than 30 new watercolour illustrations to show the uniforms of Prunkland's 'expeditionary force' plus a few extras. Moreover, he has added maps with overlays to help clarify the week-by-campaign-week moves of the two sides, indicating where major events took place, so that the pages of coordinates make perfect sense, like a stop-frame animation.

Alongside the campaign logs, you will also find the handwritten pages of Henry's own notes, some of them pondering his strategic and tactical options, some of them offering less than flattering opinions about the enemy troops! These notes have been transcribed so that those who may not be able to read what is sometimes slightly scruffier penmanship can, nevertheless, follow the story blow-by-blow. Sadly, Guy never kept such detailed records, but we're quite sure that the sheer depth of Henry's documentation makes up for it!

To round out the design and what will no doubt be, for some of you, an introduction to the very notion of 'imagi-nations', you will also find a large number of sumptuous colour photographs of games in progress set in various eras, ranging from ancient times (Henry is collecting the ferocious—well, sometimes— army of Byzarbia) right through to the equivalent of early WWII, when what has then become Grossprunkland is still managing to antagonise its neighbours with its bellicose tendencies!

In short, this book is unique in the world of wargaming. There is nothing else like it, and outside the world of fantasy and sci-fi gaming that tends to be dominated by 'big brands' of the hobby, Henry admits that this is a highly unusual project. It is hard to categorize and needs an audience with an open mind that will 'get' not only the pseudo-historical context and the degree of storytelling that involves, but also the author's vision of a world that mirrors our own, but with a tweak. His world-building has already spread through the eons of imagined history and extends thousands of miles around a fictitious planet. Where might it go next?

We welcome you, therefore, to take the plunge into one man's extraordinary creative endeavour. Find joy in a truly self-published project that has been written, designed and illustrated entirely by one man for the enjoyment not only of hobby connoisseurs, but of anyone who likes the thrill of a military adventure in the era of infantry, cavalry and artillery, when bright uniforms, tricorne hats and braving shot and shell on the battlefield was the nature of war. 
Welcome, indeed, to Martinstaat!

—description from the publisher

You have been studying the dark arts of poison for many years under the tutelage of the masters of the Poisoners Guild. Now it is time for your final exam. You and your fellow initiates are led into the inner sanctum of the Guild and a cup is placed in front of each of you. In order for you to pass the exam and be admitted to the Guild . . . one of your fellow initiates can't survive!

The Poisoners Guild is a 2-4 player card game where your goal is to outsmart and outlast your fellow players in a high-stakes test of wits and nerve.  There are four deadly poisons at the table ... and you are only immune to one of them!  Plan your moves carefully - Should you check your cup?  Pour something sinister in your neighbor's cup?  Switch the cups on the table?  Or should you create a brand new deadly poison for later in the game?  And when it comes time to drink, can you still remember what is where, or who is immune to what?  At the end of the game, will you be walking out the door?

—description from the publisher

Primalgard is a fast-paced, two-player trading card game set across four distinct biomes - Desert, Ocean, Sky, and Jungle — each home to powerful Guardians, ancient Watchers, and a cast of Denizens caught between worlds.

The goal is to reach Ten Hundred population points before your opponent. Players build their board using Guardians for protection and Denizens to generate population, all while reading their opponent's moves to predict attacks and disrupt their strategy.

What sets Primalgard apart: there are no turns. Players simultaneously reveal their cards and all effects resolve together - creating a tense, reactive gameplay loop where every decision carries risk.

Your dance troupe continues their journey, refining technique, deepening their understanding of the music, and discovering new ways to express themselves within the flow of the dance. As your academy grows, the rhythms become more intricate, the timing more precise, and the choices more meaningful.

Do you have what it takes to master the dance in Raas: Advanced Academy?

This expansion introduces a collection of modular additions that reflect this growth. These modules explore advanced musical interpretation, refined timing, sustained momentum, and the distinct philosophies of individual dance academies while preserving the graceful rhythm and intuitive flow of Raas: A Dance of Love.

—description from the publisher

Migoyugo is a two-player abstract strategy game played on an 8x8 board. No dice, no cards, no luck - just you and your opponent. The rules take about a minute to learn, but every time you think you've got it figured out, there's a deeper level of strategy to uncover.

Players take turns placing Migos (temporary pieces) on any open square. When you place a piece that completes a line of exactly four of your color (horizontal, vertical, or diagonal), that piece becomes a Yugo, a permanent piece that can never be removed, but every Migo in that line disappears.

Get four of your Yugos in an unbroken row and you win instantly. If neither player achieves this and the board fills up (or no legal moves remain), the player with the most Yugos wins. Equal Yugos means a draw.

Every piece you place could be setting up your next Yugo, blocking your opponent, or holding your whole position together - but forming a Yugo might remove the very piece that was keeping you safe. Do you push for the instant win and risk leaving yourself exposed, or play it steady and grind out a win on Yugo count? Every move is a balancing act between attack and defense, and the best moves do both at once. One moment you're in complete control, but it only takes one misplaced piece and your opponent won't hesitate to deliver the fatal blow.

—description from the designer

Build first, then puzzle: Ubongo, all new! In this innovative version, one to four players build their own playing area at the start of each round. Sometimes it’s long and narrow, sometimes winding, or even surprisingly shaped—always new, always different. Then the area is filled with the tiles as quickly as possible. Hmm, maybe it needs to be rearranged? A perfect mix of easy gameplay and tricky challenges thanks to different difficulty levels. Who will collect the most gems in this fun family game for ages eight and up and be the first to shout “Ubongo!”?

—description from the publisher

Furzipups, the little rumbling dragon from Colorful Dragon Valley, brings tons of fun to the game with his loud fart clouds. Together with his friends Hicksi, the brave chicken, Rüdiger Rülps-Rüssel, and the fiery dragon girl Lulu, players engage in a wild race. They move their pieces forward or backward with the die—and the special sound button adds extra excitement: It plays one of the familiar Furzipups fart sounds and determines which piece gets to move next. This keeps the game full of surprises. For two to four players ages four and up, this cheerful dice game is a blast with the beloved characters and the unique sound button.

—description from the designer

A tactical card game of sequences, sets, and timing — played in a circle.

Learn how to play:
You can find the game rules in the Files section (English and Dutch).

SEP: Circle of Sevens is a tactical two-player card game played in a shared circle.

Create combinations of matching or consecutive cards to earn tokens.

Combinations can be formed across the entire circle — not just adjacent cards.

Action cards allow you to disrupt the circle and create new opportunities.

The first player to collect all seven tokens wins instantly — SEP!

How it works:

[*]Place number cards into a shared circular layout
[*]Create sets (same value) or sequences (same suit)
[*]Earn tokens for each combination you create or extend
[*]Use action cards to remove cards and change the game state

Why SEP is unique:

[*]A shared circular play area instead of rows or grids
[*]Combinations can span the entire circle
[*]Constant interaction between players
[*]Simple rules with tactical depth

SEP combines tactical positioning, timing, and disruption in a compact and highly replayable format.

boardgame: Zombicide: Mission C61 – Take The Hospital
description:
Designed by players for the Kyoko mission contest, and later published by the original studio, C missions are supplementary missions released online for print and play.

C61 is a hard difficulty exploration mission for Rue Morgue.
What about turning a hospital, one of the most dangerous places, into your new haven? What an outpost it would be... Let's do it!

Designed by Adam Decker

boardgame: Zombicide: Mission C62 – Turn Off Those Lights!
description:
Designed by players for the Kyoko mission contest, and later published by the original studio, C missions are supplementary missions released online for print and play.

C62 is an easy difficulty exploration mission for Rue Morgue.
Someone - or something - turned the remaining lights on in the vicinity. Your haven is now a zombie hotspot. Turn them off and secure the area!

Designed by Alvaro Royo Andreu

Designed by players for the Kyoko mission contest, and later published by the original studio, C missions are supplementary missions released online for print and play.

C63 is a medium difficulty exploration mission for Prison Outbreak.
A clever neighbor turned his trailer into a food store, locked with a barcode tatooed in his hand. Now, he's a zombie. He won't need the food, will he? And his hand?

Designed by Christophe Müller de Schongor

Designed by players for the Kyoko mission contest, and later published by the original studio, C missions are supplementary missions released online for print and play.

C64 is a hard difficulty exploration mission for Prison Outbreak.
You may have found the perfect haven. Unfortunately, three Abominations guard your beloved castle. Time for a siege, the three kings must fall!

Designed by Fábio M. Roncaratti

boardgame: Zombicide: Mission C65 – Between The Hammer And The Anvil
description:
Designed by players for the Kyoko mission contest, and later published by the original studio, C missions are supplementary missions released online for print and play.

C65 is a medium difficulty exploration mission for  Season 1.
You could use this secured precinct to scout the city from a safe outpost. First, you have to clean it from all zombies and close the gates!

Designed by Stanislas Gayot

boardgame: Zombicide: Mission A28 – The Vault of Hell
description:
Designed by the original studio, A missions are supplementary missions released online for print and play.

A28 is a hard difficulty exploration mission for Prison Outbreak.
Finding a fallout shelter in the heart of the city was an unexpected surprise. And it’s huge! Before turning it into your next secret underground base (mwahaha), you first need to clean it of all the zombies trapped inside... and fix the entrance, too, because you’re now part of the inmates.

boardgame: Zombicide: Mission A29 – A Game Called Survival
description:
Designed by the original studio, A missions are supplementary missions released online for print and play.

A29 is a hard difficulty exploration mission for Season 1.
Your team is on the road to the next destination as you notice a small town bording the highway. It’s time for a pit stop! You have 60 minutes to grab everything you can and move on. No time to waste, let’s go!

Designed by the original studio, A missions are supplementary missions released online for print and play.

A30 is a medium difficulty exploration mission for Season 1.
Fat Joe Burger used to be the place to enjoy delicious meals enhanced with Joe’s “special ingredient”. It turns out zombies are attracted to it, and mutate into toxic monsters when eating it. We have to stop it before it spreads like a plague!

boardgame: Zombicide: Mission A31 – Just a Nightmare
description:
Designed by the original studio, A missions are supplementary missions released online for print and play.

A31 is a hard difficulty exploration mission for Prison Outbreak.
The same nightmare falls on you every night. You and your friends are trapped in a huge prison. There is no escape, and zombies are everywhere. Oh, wait. You’re survivors, THEY are trapped here with you!

boardgame: Zombicide: Mission A32 – Go to Bed
description:
Designed by the original studio, A missions are supplementary missions released online for print and play.

A32 is a hard difficulty exploration mission for Rue Morgue.
You may have the guns, but the zombies don’t need to sleep! You’re out to find army folding army beds in a haunted refugee camp, and get them back to your shelter. Be careful! Real monsters are hiding under your bed!

boardgame: Zombicide: Mission A33 – Drive By Bombing
description:
Designed by the original studio, A missions are supplementary missions released online for print and play.

A33 very is a hard difficulty exploration mission for Season 1.
The quickest way to secure a zombie-filled area may be using makeshift explosives. However, no plan go exactly as planned... Zombies are coming too soon, too fast. You'll have to be quick before getting overwhelmed!

Designed by the original studio, A missions are supplementary missions released online for print and play.

A34 is a medium difficulty exploration mission for Season 1.
The zombie infection always finds a way. This time, our foes are getting predator instincts and lay ambushes to anyone cruising nearby. We have to find their nest and kill them all before they grow into a full zombie species!

boardgame: Zombicide: Mission A35 – “When Santa Came to Town”
description:
Designed by the original studio, A missions are supplementary missions released online for print and play.

A35 is a hard difficulty exploration mission for Season 1.
You better watch out, you better not cry! In this mission, you need to find the True Meaning of Christmas and stop the Santabomination, who keeps spawning zombie elves. But who knows, you may find some Christmas Gifts along the way!

boardgame: Zombicide: Mission A36 – This is Our Ground
description:
Designed by the original studio, A missions are supplementary missions released online for print and play.

A28 is a  mission for Rue Morgue.
Every survivor needs a home base if they want to have a chance to live. How dare they try to take yours! You will kill them all if that’s what is takes – zombies and survivors alike!

Designed by the original studio, A missions are supplementary missions released online for print and play.

A37 is a medium difficulty exploration mission for Season 1.
We thought the Abominations were the worst the virus could do. Apparently we were wrong. A new Mutated creature was spotted, it killed one of our own with a single blow, and all efforts to eliminate it so far were futile. It’s time to try a different approach!

boardgame: Zombicide: Mission A38 – Get Off My Lawn
description:
Designed by the original studio, A missions are supplementary missions released online for print and play.

A38 is a medium difficulty exploration mission for Season 1. Rue Morgue.
We still have to eat in a post-apocalyptic world. On top of the locusts, slugs and rats, we have to deal with a different kind of pest: the undead. Weed killer might not cut it on these guys.

boardgame: Zombicide: Mission A39 – Dude, Where's Our Keys?
description:
Designed by the original studio, A missions are supplementary missions released online for print and play.

A39 is a hard difficulty exploration mission for Rue Morgue.
GET TO THE CHOPPER! Wait, who has the keys? No, I don't have the keys, I thought you did. YOU DON'T HAVE THE KEYS EITHER?

Designed by the original studio, A missions are supplementary missions released online for print and play.

A40 is a medium difficulty exploration mission for Rue Morgue.
We found a good spot to settle, but these pesky zombies who were camping when they were infected keep showing up. We'll have to clean up the area. Good thing we have this fire... gadget... thingy. And it runs on zombies. So guess who's becoming fuel for it!

boardgame: Zombicide: Mission A41 – Loot and Rescue
description:
Designed by the original studio, A missions are supplementary missions released online for print and play.

A41 is a mission for Season 1.
So we have two goals: get back our stolen food and find our friends, who were captured by this other group before they sturned into zombies. If we can find them, they can fight alongside us!

boardgame: Zombicide: Mission A42 – A Strange Call: Bob's Campaign, Part 1
description:
Designed by the original studio, A missions are supplementary missions released online for print and play.

A42 is a mediumdifficulty exploration mission for  Season 1.
Bob's Campaign, part 1! While looting a miraculously intact convenience store, you hear a strange noise... A phone was ringing. “... I’m Bob... at the Power Stat... everything... the transformer will blow up... die... everyone... trapped... ” Bob is weak and needs rescuing!

boardgame: Zombicide: Mission A43 – A Survivor of His World: Bob's Campaign, Part 2
description:
Designed by the original studio, A missions are supplementary missions released online for print and play.

A43 is a hard difficulty exploration mission for Season 1.
Bob's Campaign, part 2! Bob is with you, but he asks your help to rescue his wife and daughter who are trapped in a nearby building. Well, since you shut down the transformer in part 1, that means you have to do that in the dark.

boardgame: Zombicide: Mission A44 – Time to Hit the Road: Bob's Campaign, Part 3
description:
Designed by the original studio, A missions are supplementary missions released online for print and play.

A44 is a very hard difficulty exploration mission for Season 1.
Bob's Campaign, part 3! Ok, Things are looking good. We rescued Bob and his family. Now we just need to get back to base. The bad part? We are now surrounded by zombies. Even worse? Bob is one of them. Yep, he died. Sorry Bob!

boardgame: Zombicide: Mission A45 – The Wild Ride
description:
Designed by the original studio, A missions are supplementary missions released online for print and play.

A45 is a medium difficulty exploration mission for Season 1.
This house seemed like a great hiding spot to get some rest. But the alarm system of the car parked right in front of it decided to randomly go off during the night and now it is attracting all sorts of zombies! If we can find the keys to the car, maybe we can escape in it - if the zombies don't destroy it first.

Pyroclasm is a 6-card micro-expansion to indie lane battler Muster: Raise the Banners that provides added variety and tactical options via brand new mechanics, while still sticking to the familiar fast-paced gameplay of the base game.

A mysterious Pyroclasm has blighted Muster’s idyllic landscape, with blazing lava and freezing blizzards wreaking havoc on these enchanted realms.

Accompanying this unseasonable surprise are mischief-making Rainbow Imps… who appear rather impervious to this wild weather!

Although whether the Imps are here to help or hinder the increasingly perilous proceedings very much remains to be seen...

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Zombicide: Mission A46 – On the Fly
description:
Designed by the original studio, A missions are supplementary missions released online for print and play.

A46 is a medium difficulty exploration mission for Rue Morgue.
We’ve picked up a faint distress signal from a nearby Hospital. This old hunk of junk helicopter has kept us going, let’s see if she can fly one more mission.

Designed by the original studio, A missions are supplementary missions released online for print and play.

A47 is a medium difficulty exploration mission for Season 1.
Merry Christmas, survivor! This may be the apocalypse, but we still want to celebrate! We are lighting the abandoned Christmas tree downtown. It will be dark, it will be dangerous, but it will be worth it.

boardgame: Zombicide: Mission A48 – Trick or Threat
description:
Designed by the original studio, A missions are supplementary missions released online for print and play.

A48 is a medium difficulty exploration mission for Season 1.
Just because it's the apocalypse, doesn't mean we can't celebrate Halloween! But where's the candy? And most importantly, where are the kids? Oh no, Trick or Treat just became Trick or Threat!

boardgame: Zombicide: Mission A49 – The Taxi Driver: A Taxi Saga, Part 1
description:
Designed by the original studio, A missions are supplementary missions released online for print and play.

A49 is a medium difficulty exploration mission for Prison Outbreak.
During a basketball game, we got surrounded by zombies. We need to survive long enough for our taxi driver friend to come back, but that's going to be a challenge!

High and Mighty is a 6-card micro-expansion to indie lane battler Muster: Raise the Banners that provides added variety and tactical options via brand new mechanics, while still sticking to the familiar fast-paced gameplay of the base game.

The realms of Muster have been blessed by a visit from the High and Mighty.

Introducing the Highborn, whose mere arrival serves to fortify their many devoted followers... alas, a usurper has descended from on high, for there can only be one truly blessed Banner!

But fear not, for the mighty Rainbow Saint is accepting of all, benevolently bestowing strength to those imbued with Rainbow Magic!

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Zombicide: Mission A50 – A Little Help From my Friends: A Taxi Saga, Part 2
description:
Designed by the original studio, A missions are supplementary missions released online for print and play.

A50 is a medium difficulty exploration mission for Prison Outbreak.
A Taxi Saga, Part 2! The taxi driver rescued us but now he wants us to help rescue his friends too. They are all trying to survive inside their own cars. Watch out, zombies, because we are fast, furious and coming for you!

boardgame: Zombicide: Mission A52 – Worst Case Scenario
description:
Designed by the original studio, A missions are supplementary missions released online for print and play.

A52 is a medium difficulty exploration mission for Toxic City Mall. Rue Morgue.
Hope for the best, plan for the worst, they say. Well, who could have planned for a battle against the undead in the middle of an earthquake? Could be the plot of a B movie but it's our life now, apparently.

boardgame: Zombicide: Western Mission E1 – The Bank Heist
description:
Hard

On our very first extra scenario for Zombicide: Undead or Alive, two outlaws were looking to make an easy cash and went to small village of Clay Town. Little did they know the new plague would arrive along with them!

boardgame: Zombicide: Western Mission E2 – The Legend of the Sunrise Riders: Part 1
description:
Western Mission

Hard
Material needed: Undead or Alive. Running Wild.
A mini campaign in the West! We are riders, and this damn apocalypse wiped out almost every horse in the Wild West, but there are rumors that say they saw a horse walking around here…

Connie & Brian Overtager Amager ("Connie and Brian conquers Amager") is based on their original comic book series. Play one of four factions with unique characters, running and effecting public life on the Danish island of Amager:

An area-control game in which local government controls welfare, the private sector grows commerce, the police upholds the law, the criminals do who knows what and ordinary Amagericans are happily unaware.

Win hearts and minds or just by the numbers. Is day-to-day Amager life really like fighting a war?

—description from the designer

boardgame: Zombicide: Western Mission E3 – The Legend of the Sunrise Riders: Part 2
description:
Western Mission

Hard
In part 2 of our mini campaign, we’ll find out if the Sunrise Riders will be able to find their saddles. If they can find it and survive until sunrise, they’ll get the new folks to the big town to catch the last train out of this hell.

boardgame: Zombicide: Western Mission E4 – The Legend of the Sunrise Riders: Part 3
description:
Western Mission

Hard
In part 3 of our mini campaign, the Sunrise Riders finally found their saddles and arrived in the big town. But there are more people than there are horses, so they will need to find a way to stop the train and save all the new folks. The grand finale is here!

boardgame: Zombicide: Western Mission E5 – Stray Dogs
description:
Western Mission

Hard

In this special scenario, teamwork is at its finest, and each team member will have a role to play as they rescue the local sheriff and his dog!

boardgame: Zombicide: Western Mission E7 – Delivery Warranty
description:
Western Mission

Hard

In this special Christmas scenario an earthshattering explosion buried all the Christmas presents. Well, no child is going to wake up on Christmas morning without their gift. Let’s hurry!

boardgame: Zombicide: Western Mission E8 – Bring Out Your Dead
description:
Western Mission

Hard

A mysterious affliction sweeps through the quaint town, turning it into a real-life thriller by claiming the lives of the townsfolk. The mayor's quick fix of dumping the corpses into a cart takes a turn when one of them decides it's not ready for its final resting place... We must get rid of that cart

boardgame: Zombicide: Western Mission E6 – The Big BOOM!
description:
Western Mission

Hard

Gather ’round, folks! The greatest fireworks show is about to begin! Bring friends, lovers, even enemies... you won’t want to miss it! A quick fix on the old Gatling Gun, a prayer from Father Horse, grab a drink, take a seat, and await the big BOOM!

boardgame: Zombicide: Western Mission E9 – The Holy Fountain
description:
Western Mission

Medium
Join the hunt for the legendary sacred spring up north, grab a bucket, and let’s wash these undead varmints with holy water out for good!

A fast, strategic bidding game where only unique bids win promotions.

Climb the corporate ladder by managing your time, outbidding rivals, and securing key promotions.

Each round, players secretly bid hours — but only unique bids succeed.  
Tie with another player and your effort is wasted… but careful timing can turn failure into opportunity.

Push for promotion, recover at the right moment, and outmanoeuvre your opponents to reach the top floor.

Why It’s Different

• Unique bidding system — only one player can win each round  
• Duplicate bids create tension and unexpected rewards  
• Simple rules with strong player interaction  
• Tactical timing: spend hours or recover them  
• Escalating pressure as players approach the top floors

How It Plays

Each round:

• Reveal a Promotion Card  
• Players secretly choose a card  
• Pay hours equal to the bid  
• Resolve from lowest to highest  
• The highest unique bid earns the promotion

Players must balance pushing forward with recovering resources using Clock Off.

Winning the Game

When a player reaches the top floors, the final round is triggered.

The player on the highest floor becomes the Chief Executive.

Key Rule

Only the highest unique bid wins the promotion.  
Duplicate bids do not win.  
If there is no unique bid, no one is promoted.

Downloads

• Rulebook available in the Files section

Hydroplane boat racing game for 2 to 12 players.  Game play is roll and move with action cards being drawn when you land on an action space on the track.  Actions range from "hit a swell in the water" "smooth section of water" "missed a bouy".  IE penalty or bonus.  To win you play thru heats and mains to determine a winner based on laps run, nitrous used, with points awarded based on finishing position.

Find pairs of cards that are either the same or mirror images of each other!

Hemiga simulates visual pattern recognition by activating your powers of observation. The player who collects the most pairs wins - or enjoy it alone as a meditative experience.

—description from the back of the box

The Boxing Kraken is a conflict simulation designed for 2 to 4 players, based on the underground kraken fighting culture of Victorian England.

In it, players take on the role of octopus trainers who, together with their eight-armed champions, develop a strategy to secure victory in an action-packed three-round battle.

The game involves both tactics and luck, the occasional surprise, and, last but not least, a dash of treachery.

Welcome to the world of octopus wrestling

—description from the back of the box (translated)

Big Apple is a reimagination of the original Big Apple Game launched by RoseBud Art Company in the 1930s.

This new version of the game features the same apple spinner with a revised board of outcomes, new apple puzzles to complete, added gameplay strategy, and a rotten apple.

In the primary version of the game, players will take turns spinning the apple, collecting tokens, and spending tokens to randomly draw apple pieces from the bag.

The first player to complete their 6 piece apple-shaped puzzle wins, however, players must complete their puzzle before the rotten apple is completed or everyone loses!

There are alternate versions for solo and cooperative modes, as well as a beginner/casual mode for new players and kids.

Phantom Epoch: Exiles of Volan is an expansion for Phantom Epoch. In a new story campaign, players uncover the lost history of the Volanen and help them reclaim their fractured home world.

The expansion adds a new campaign map, along with new adversaries and additional upgrades for the cooperative tactical combat system of the base game.

It can be played as a standalone campaign or integrated with the base campaign.

Word Slam! is a rummy-type word game with a large deck -- 232 letter cards in 4 suits, and 62 special cards.  In a round, each player is dealt 12 cards. In their turn a player first either draws 2 cards from the deck, or discards 2 cards and then draws 4 from the deck, or draws any card from the discard row (which they must use to make a new word) along with all discards following it.  Than they may do all the following as much as they wish: play words from their hand, add letters from their hand to words on the table to make new words, and play special cards; and they then end their turn by discarding a card.  When a player empties their hand the round ends and players score the cards in front of them.  The first player to 500 points wins the game.

The special cards are: 

     Wild: Stands for any letter.  It cannot be the first letter of a word, and only one Wild per word is allowed. 
     2X, 3X: Doubles or triples the value of the word it is played on. Only one is allowed per word. 
     Word Slam!: An attempt to cancel a played word. The Slam player and the word owner each draw a card, and the one with the higher-valued draw wins. If the word owner wins, they score the word immediately; either way the word is removed from play. 
     Steal: Lets a player steal a played word from another player, unless the owner can guess the color (suit) of another card played by the stealer. 
     Shield: Played on a word (only if it matches the first letter's color), and protects it from being Slammed, Stolen, or added to.  The dealer may always ignore Shield cards. 
     Switch: Force another player to swap hands with you. 


When a word is scored, its value is the sum of the values of its letter cards, doubled if they are all the same color, with added bonuses (for 3 or more of the same letter, beginning with the "Hot Letter" (the last card dealt), having 8+ letters, etc.), and finally multiplied by 2X or 3X if present.  Letters still in a player's tray at the end of a round are subtracted from their score if their color matches that of a card drawn from the deck by the player who went out.

boardgame: Obscurids: Cryptids vs Myths vs Monsters
description:
Obscurids is a free-for-all card game where cryptids, tall tales, and urban legends come to life. It captures the feel of kitchen-table TCG battles in one awesome, easy-to-play package. In each game, players choose an Encounter to face, which changes how victory is achieved and adds unique mechanics that make every match feel different. The core set includes the Jersey Devil Encounter, where players must race to bring down the monster.

The core set also includes 4 ready-to-play decks, each built around a distinct domain and its signature Legend: The Wilds featuring Bigfoot, The Depths with the Kraken, Frontier with Pecos Bill, and The Streets with the Rat King. Each deck offers its own playstyle and flavor, from raw power to trickery and control.

For players who want to dig deeper, cards can be mixed across domains to build unique custom decks, opening up even more strategies and combinations.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Star Fleet Battles: Orion Master Starship Book
description:
Cadets, your ship is escorting a freighter around the Federation-Tholian border. A ship starts to decloak. How do you react? Anyone other than Cadet Granger? Very well, Cadet Granger.

Sir, it could easily be a pirate ship from the Pharaoh or Capital cartels, more likely to be the Pharaoh since their territory overlaps with the Romulans.

Quite correct, Cadet Granger. The rest of you, study up on the Orion Pirates by reading the Orion Master Starship Book. Dismissed!

The tenth volume in the long-anticipated Master Starship Book series is now available. It includes every Orion ship in Star Fleet Battles, including X-ships and general units that are specific to the Orion pirate cartels. There is also information about the Orion pirate activities. As a bonus, there are four new escort ship descriptions.

Every ship is illustrated. All updates, corrections, and errata have been added. The ships are in order (regardless of the product they came from) with the enhanced format listing detailed information in a consistent manner.

This book contains the descriptions of the ships, but no SSDs.

—description from the publisher

Legends of Mana Island is a relaxing roll & write board game for 1-2 player.

On the sacred shores of Mana Island, ancient creatures await their master. To claim your place among the legends, you must hatch these mythical creatures and harness their elemental essence. But you’re not alone. Summon powerful Familiars to protect your eggs from sneaky Goblins and rise as a legendary wizard!

In Legends of Mana Island, players take on the role of a young wizard to score the most Mana Points. Over 12 rounds, you'll use dice to summon Familiars, hatch eggs into Legendary Creatures and defend against sneaky Goblins. At the game’s end, the player with the most Mana Points becomes the greatest wizard!

—description from the publisher

A game that exercises memory and fine motor coordination, simple and fun to play, and an excellent tool for psychologists and educators.

Option 1: Insert 24 wooden pins into the board randomly by color. Roll the die and try to find a pin of the matching color in one chance. If correct, keep the pin and play again. If wrong, pass to the next player. The player with the most pins wins.

Option 2: Place 24 pins randomly on the central spaces of the board. Each turn, a player removes 2 pins. If they match in color, keep them and play again. If they do not match, return them to the same holes. The player with the most pairs wins.

Civil Wars has more rules for Federation & Empire. Read this book to discover rules for civil wars and for the WYN Cluster. There are also rules for playing during the Middle Years Era. And finally, there are Ship Information Tables for the WYN Cluster.

Civil wars have been occurring in this world for over 4,000 years, as more than one faction has fought for control of the government and the soul of their nations. Furthermore, civil wars have been a part of the Star Fleet Universe almost since the start, with notes mentioning Romulan, Lyran, and Kzinti civil wars over the two centuries of history.

There are many rules for the WYN Cluster because that territory is unique, and we have never needed specific rules for it until now. The WYN are just ... weird. Their entire territory spans 50 parsecs, about 10% of the neutral hex they are in. That tiny distance compared to any other capital hex creates strange effects as fighters (for example) don’t have far to go to get from one planet to another.

Want to fight civil wars or attack the WYN Cluster? 
There are five scenarios for you to use. 
Into the Whirlpool is the invasion of the WYN Cluster by the Klingons, Kzintis, or Lyrans. 
Mist: Uneasy Lies the Head is one of the wars between the Lyrans, as is Hailfire. The Romulan Civil War starts in Y185 between the Romulan Republic and Imperial Romulans. 
Reignstorm: The Usurper War of Return (also known as the Second Kzinti Civil War) is the center of the last scenario.

You will need the Federation & Empire 2010 Rulebook and various countersheets to use this expansion.

—description from the publisher
language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups

description: Who can find the favorite characters from Maurício de Souza's universe? In this game, children draw one of 100 illustrated cards and try to find the matching character as quickly as possible. Horácio, Mônica, Jotalhão, and the whole gang are hidden across two game boards: one with an easy level and one with a harder level. A fun spotting game that develops observation skills and quick reflexes.

In the vast desert, you encounter four like-minded martial arts peers at an inn. After several rounds of drinks, you propose: 'In one’s lifetime, if we do not pursue the pinnacle of martial arts, isn’t it a wasted life? Today, six major martial arts sects are the masters of our world; let’s challenge them and see how strong we truly are.' Excited, your companions eagerly join, and thus begins the legendary quest to become the Kung-Fu Master.

Kung-Fu Master (功夫之王) is a 2–4 player American-style adventure game using simultaneous selection as its core mechanism. Players accompany four martial arts masters to challenge other sects, gain prestige, and compete against rival masters. Success depends on strategically using your masters’ unique skills and choosing the right opponents and sects. The game is played over four rounds, where players secretly select masters to challenge locations, defeat sect protectors, and earn prestige tokens. After four rounds, the player with the most points wins. This game is part of the first edition of the 9 Cards series.
publishers: 九张牌玩具公司(9 Cards Toy Company)

description:
In A Roda do Tarot ("The Tarot Wheel"), each player takes on the role of The Fool and traverses The Fool's Journey, composed of the 22 major arcana of the tarot, experiencing the joys and difficulties inherent to every path. Along the way, players reflect on life, share ideas and experiences with friends, and exercise free will through the choices presented throughout the game.

The game ends when a player fills every position on their personal spread. They then interpret the full tarot reading as the answer to their original question. The player who completes their spread first wins, though the real reward lies in the insights gained along the way.

boardgame: Fields of Fate: Solo-Play System for Tabletop Battles – Fantasy Edition
description:
A miniature wargaming system that lets your tabletop armies fight tense battles even when no opponent is available.

Command armies. Tell stories. Fight cinematic battles on your own terms. In your own time.

Fields of Fate: Fantasy uses dynamic activation tables tailored to specific factions to create an unpredictable enemy force that responds to the flow of battle. Each turn evolves naturally as units advance, hold their ground, or launch sudden assaults according to the chaos of the battlefield.

Bring your favourite fantasy miniatures to the table and watch the battle unfold anytime.

Features

     Designed for solo miniature wargaming
     Dynamic activation system that drives enemy behaviour
     Compatible with any fantasy miniature collection, including those commonly used in large tabletop fantasy battle games.
     Battles typically playable in 1–2 hours
     Supports forces from small skirmishes to large tabletop engagements
     Also compatible with cooperative play
     Ideal for narrative campaigns and arcade mode


Prepare your forces.
Take command.
Test your fate.

—description from the publisher

Can Can Matemático is a dynamic card game with easy rules where the winner can change right up to the last play!

Your goal is to get rid of all the cards in your hand. To discard, your card must have the same number or color as the card on the table. There are also special cards you can use to disrupt other players. Down to just one card? Watch out — the player to your left might make you draw four cards, change the play order, or skip your turn! A game for the whole family to enjoy together. Think fast, discard wisely, and good luck!

Welcome to the Dungeons of Sempreterna, your compendium of weekly dungeons and adventures where you will explore temples, ruins, caves, and various environments while facing all kinds of enemies.

The game features a simple 3d6 rolling system for resource generation (called Elemental Runes). You must build a resource reserve and then use the runes from your reserve to move through the dungeon sheets. Heroes will face dangerous creatures and traps along the way.

This is a solo print-and-play dungeon crawler delivered as a monthly subscription by Nat20 Games, featuring paper-and-pencil mechanics and grid-based movement.

Alakazam: Battle of the Mages features 4 game modes, all using memory mechanics. In TriCombo mode, players must form combinations of 3 cards, earning extra points by matching cards with their character's color. In Duel and Battle modes, players face off trying to form a trail of lightning bolts in their color before their opponent. In the 2-player Battle mode, an opponent advances automatically with each mistake the mages make. A fast and fun memory card game with a magical theme.

In the ancient lands of Huaxia, two tribes face off in a fierce rivalry. You take on the role of a tribal leader, guiding your people to compete with another player for control of the land’s resources, develop your tribe, and ultimately become a symbol of Chinese civilization.

华夏中原 (Hua Xia) is a 2-player Euro-style game that centers on auctions. In each round, players use expansion points during the Expansion Phase to compete for resources, and then utilize those resources in the Production Phase to develop agriculture, military strength, or perform divine rituals. Players can achieve victory either by gaining military or divine supremacy or, after five rounds, by scoring the highest total through overall development.

This game is part of the first edition of the 9 Cards series.
publishers: 九张牌玩具公司(9 Cards Toy Company)

description:
The game where you have fun letting your voice loose!

The goal is to be the first to reach the final space on the board. The DJ announces a keyword and players must sing at least 6 words from any song containing that keyword to earn the right to roll the die and advance on the board. If nobody can sing a song, the first player to hum any tune advances.

With different card types including challenges, duets, and special actions, this party game brings music and laughter to your table. Great for groups of all ages who love to sing!

Cybervamp CCG is a gothic-cyberpunk collectible card game for 2–4 players set in a city of eternal night, where old world European vampire aristocracy collides with neon-soaked futuristic technology — blood feuds fought with both ancient sorcery and bleeding-edge cybernetics.

Choose from four factions — each with a distinct playstyle:

Red — aggressive and relentless, built to overwhelm before your opponent stabilizes
White — control, life-gain, and ancient relics that grind the game in your favor
Purple — psychic subterfuge, card draw, and manipulation that wins through information
Black — necromantic swarms and reanimation that flood the board and never stay dead

What makes it different:
Cybervamp uses a dual deck system — a 40-card main deck paired with a 20-card land deck — so resource management is a strategic layer, not an afterthought. The Exhaust/Overexhaust mechanic creates meaningful risk decisions every turn.
Most importantly: Bleed — Cybervamp's signature mechanic. Damage doesn't always hit immediately. Bleed stacks and resolves at the end of your opponent's turn, creating a ticking clock that rewards aggression, punishes slow play, and makes every combat decision feel genuinely dangerous.

—description from the designer

9 Cards: Season 1 (九张牌：第一季) is the first edition of 9 Cards (九张牌), an initiative that gives designers the opportunity to create a full gaming experience using only nine cards.

The 9 games are:


     S1E1 曲水流觞 (The Winding Stream)
     S1E2 三国箭神 (Archers of the Three Kingdoms)
     S1E3 龙生九子 (The Nine Sons of The Dragon)
     S1E4 合体机甲 (Combine! Robot)
     S1E5 逆转文明 (Reverse Civilization)
     S1E6 矩阵帝国 (The Matrix Empire)
     S1E7 密室脱困 (Escape Room Game)
     S1E8 功夫之王 (Kung Fu Master)
     S1E9 华夏中原 (Hua Xia)


Small accessories may be needed, but not included in the box.
publishers: 九张牌玩具公司(9 Cards Toy Company)

description:
Jab. Uppercut. Fireball!
Deck-building X Arcade Fighter.

Start with a deck of D-pad directions and basic buttons  and buy stronger attacks. Fight, bluff, and COMBO your way to victory.

A tactical deck-building game for 1–4 players.

Based on 2D fighters like Marvel Vs. Capcom 2 and X-Men Vs. Street fighter.

—description from the publisher

The players take turns to roll and move. On some spaces events hinder or benefit the players. The first to reach space 100 wins the Game.

—user summary

Cleariosity has a deck of 60 transparent plastic cards. Each card has 3 letters on it, and each letter is in one of the 9 positions of a 3x3 grid. (When multiple cards are stacked, the grid is apparent.) At the start of the game, the cards are dealt out evenly among the players; any leftover cards are left face-up in the middle of the table.  Each player keeps their cards in a face-down draw pile.

To start, all players simultaneously place the top card from their draw pile face up in the middle of the table, all facing the same direction.  Players then look to find cards that will spell a 3-letter word if they are stacked together. When they see one they say the word and point to the cards, which are then stacked to see if the caller is correct. An acceptable word: 

     Is a valid word; proper names, abbreviations, and acronyms are not allowed (unless the players agree otherwise). 
     Is spelled in a straight line, horizontally (left to right only), vertically (top to bottom only), or diagonally (left to right only). 
     Has none of its letters covering or covered by another letter. (Letters not used in that word can cover other letters.)


If the word is correct, the declarer collects the cards making the word and keeps them in a separate winnings pile; if not, the cards are separated and that player may not guess again until after another player guesses.  Play then continues with the remaining cards. If no one can see another word, all players add the top card of their draw piles. Play continues until all cards have been played and no one can find any other words.  The player who has collected the most cards wins the game.

Fight for the crown in this fast-paced, challenging gateway game packed with player interaction, dicey decisions and big swings! Set in a fantasy animal realm left leaderless after the king's sudden death, players take on the roles of rival adventurers racing to claim the title of new ruler of the land.


     Roll and select dice to determine your actions.
     Move strategically across the hexagon board that’s set up differently every game.
     Play cards to amplify your attacks, manipulate dice and perform powerful combos.
     Use the terrain to your advantage to gain combat bonuses, teleport through gates and even double your points.
     Whack your opponents to steal their points!


Victory goes to the first player to reach 20 points. But with Whacks flying from all directions, holding onto your lead is easier said than done. In Whack, fortune favors the cunning, and the crown changes hands in the blink of an eye.

—description from the designer

Cleariosity: 5-Letter Frenzy is a larger version of the original Cleariosity. It also has a deck of 60 transparent plastic cards, but the cards are larger, having 5 letters on them instead of 3, and each letter is in one of the 25 positions of a 5x5 grid instead of 9 positions in a 3x3 grid. (The cards have lines on them to make the grid, and the letters' positions in it, easy to see. )

At the start of the game, the cards are dealt out evenly among the players; any leftover cards are left face-up in the middle of the table.  Each player keeps their cards in a face-down draw pile.

To start, all players simultaneously place the top card from their draw pile face up in the middle of the table, all facing the same direction.  Players then look to find cards that will spell a word of 3 or more letters if they are stacked together. When they see one they say the word and point to the cards, which are then stacked to see if the caller is correct. An acceptable word: 

     Is a valid word; proper names, abbreviations, and acronyms are not allowed (unless the players agree otherwise). 
     Is spelled in a straight line, horizontally (left to right only), vertically (top to bottom only), or diagonally (left to right only). 
     Has none of its letters covering or covered by another letter. (Letters not used in that word can cover other letters.)


If the word is correct, the declarer collects the cards making the word and keeps them in a separate winnings pile; if not, the cards are separated and that player may not guess again until after another player guesses.  Play then continues with the remaining cards. If no one can see another word, all players add the top card of their draw piles to the table spread. Play continues until all cards have been played and no one can find any other words.  The player who has collected the most cards wins the game.

In Startup Empire, players take on the role of ambitious CEOs determined to build a billion-dollar startup empire. Compete across 6 industry sectors — Tech & Software, Finance & Crypto, Health & Biotech, Green & Energy, Media & Entertainment, and Real Estate & Construction — each with its own risk and reward profile.

On each turn, players roll two dice: a Classic Die for movement and a Business Die that triggers economic events affecting all players. Land on different spaces to acquire startup cards, attract investors from the VC Pool, innovate with powerful upgrades, face market crises, or unlock rare Empire Bonus cards.

The key strategic mechanic is the merger system: combine two startups in the same sector to form a Group with a dramatically higher valuation multiplier (4.8x vs 3x for individual startups). Players must carefully balance cash flow against long-term valuation growth, negotiate deals with rivals, and time their mergers for maximum impact.

The game features 90 cards across 5 types (StartUp, Investor, Innovation, Market & Crisis, and Empire Bonus), immunity totems for crisis protection, a debt system with double repayment penalties, and multiple game modes including a quick "Startup Sprint" variant.

The winner is not the player with the most cash — it is the player with the highest total valuation, calculated from startup revenues, merger bonuses, remaining cash, and active card effects, minus any unpaid debts.

—description from the designer

Draw a card from the deck and choose one of the three noises listed to try to imitate. You can make as many sounds as you like but cannot say words or make gestures, and can switch to another noise if no one can guess. Each card has 1 point, 2 point and 3 point noises. The game ends when the 15 card deck is depleted and the winner is the person with the most points.

boardgame: Zombicide: SciFi Mission M1 – Bot Mission
description:
Medium

The current situation is dire. The survivors are locked inside the base with no oxygen tanks, and the mold is already spreading. But they do have access to the bot, and if they can get to some oxygen, maybe they can escape in time! If they survive that long…

boardgame: Zombicide: SciFi Mission M2 – Ill Tidings
description:
SciFi Mission

Security in our facility has been breached. We have to collect our research and get out of here. Time is short – they are coming, we have no oxygen and on top of that, we are not sure where we stored the papers with our findings. Things are not looking good…

boardgame: Zombicide: SciFi Mission M3 – Super Soldier Training
description:
SciFi Mission

Normally, we avoid needless bloodshed: after all, the more Xenos we kill, the more they come. But not this time. This is Super Soldier Training!

boardgame: Zombicide: SciFi Mission M4 – The Swift Retreat!
description:
SciFi Mission

The Xenos have found you and you need to escape the premise. But not so fast, you can't leave those prototype weapons and that mining intel behind! First: weapons. Then: data. Finally: get the hell out of here.

boardgame: Zombicide: SciFi Mission M5 – Abandoned Ship
description:
SciFi Mission

Medium

We need to evacuate the wounded and noncombatants, and if we can get that spaceship to work, it might be enough to do just that. But we are not leaving yet - not until the Xenos' nest is destroyed as well!

boardgame: Zombicide: SciFi Mission M6 – Blind Law
description:
SciFi Mission

Hard
We thought we were stuck, but a falling star (a ship!) granted us a wish. The survivors of that falling ship should be our way out of here. Hey, it's not kidnapping if the ransom is our own survival!

boardgame: Zombicide: SciFi Mission M7 – Door Delivery
description:
SciFi Mission

Medium

Our supply crates are here. But how to retrieve them outside before the Xenos get to them first?

boardgame: Zombicide: SciFi Mission M8 – Locked Out
description:
SciFi Mission

Medium

Zombicide: Invader Mission M8 – Locked Out is a challenging scenario requiring survivors to rescue scientists trapped behind special doors by using damage 2+ weapons to breach them, while navigating dark zones. The main goals are to find all objectives (including scientists) and escape, with the added difficulty of managing Xeno spawns in dark corridors.

Oh, great. We're locked out. The doors that lead to the safe zone can only be opened by a modified bot with the encryption codes. But there are just so many of them!

boardgame: Zombicide: SciFi Mission M9 – Broken Generators
description:
SciFi Mission

Easy

There antimatter generators sure are great... except when they aren't. They got out of alignment and now we need to fix them, but boy, do they make a lot of noise!

boardgame: Zombicide: SciFi Mission M10 – Air Tight
description:
SciFi Mission
Medium
If we can access our ship, we can call in an orbital strike and destroy all the xenos in the area! The computers are in different rooms, so we will need to snoop around a little to input the safety codes to get the shields down. Let's go!

boardgame: Zombicide: SciFi Mission M11 – Rescue the Scientists
description:
