SciFi Mission
Medium
When this lab went under attack of the Xenos, the scientists decided to lock themselves in and wait for them to leave. We'll, they're not leaving, and now the scientists are trapped. Maybe not their smartest move.

boardgame: Zombicide: SciFi Mission M12 – The Purge
description:
SciFi Mission
Very Hard
An Abomination of incredible size passed through here, leaving a trail of destruction. We have no way of defeating if, but this facility may still be salvagable... We must act quickly or the mold is going to take over everything!

boardgame: Zombicide: Invader – Gaming Night #1: Deep Breath
description:
Deep Breath is a three-Missions campaign designed to let you play Zombicide: Invader all night long. A Zombicide: Invader Core Game box is needed to enjoy it. Just play the Missions in order!

There is something wrong with the ventilation system and over the course of three campaigns you will have to fix it, before the whole base turns into a suffocating oven!

boardgame: Zombicide: SciFi Mission M13 – The Cleansing
description:
SciFi Mission
Easy
Material needed: Zombicide Invader.
This outpost sure is loaded... Both with useful material and with mold! We need this back at the base, so it's time for a deep cleaning!

boardgame: Zombicide: SciFi Mission M14 – The Teleporter
description:
SciFi Mission
Hard
We've heard about a teleporter that could get us off PK-L7 for good! But we have to get there first. Can't be much worse than where we are right?

boardgame: Zombicide: SciFi Mission M15 – Baraka, We Need You
description:
SciFi Mission
Medium
The only way for us civilians to survive this Xeno swarm is evacuate the area. But only military personnel can open the bunker doors. We need to find Baraka so she can guide us out!

boardgame: Zombicide: SciFi Mission M16 – Gas Leak!
description:
SciFi Mission
Hard
Instead of killing the Xenos one by one, our new plan is to blast them all at once! This mission is all about timing: all oxygen tanks must be activated at once, and then we have to leave as soon as possible. Are you up to the challenge?

SciFi Mission
Medium
The bot machine and the sentry gun sure help us a lot... except when they’re hacked and become hostile to humans by mistake. Now, on top of rescuing these engineers, we need to return these machines to normal, or they’ll kill us before the Xenos even get a chance!

boardgame: Zombicide: SciFi Mission M18 – Nice Guys Finish Last
description:
SciFi Mission

Medium

Hold them off! In this special scenario, Survivors may only escape after the Xeno Deck has run out of cards. And you have to protect the crew members while at it!Good guys finish last? Not on our watch!

boardgame: Zombicide: SciFi Mission M19 – Get Them Out Alive
description:
SciFi Mission
Hard
Two of our friends got locked in a room whilst out on recon. We need to break them out, but only the Sentry gun can destroy this door. Also, we might as well complete the recon. Lots to do, let’s get to it!

boardgame: Zombicide: SciFi Mission M20 – Corridor of Doom
description:
SciFi Mission

Hard
Xenos attacked the intergalactic meatball factory and are now swarming its corridors in search of juicy meatballs. Which includes us! We must use the bot to blast our way through the Corridor of Doom!

boardgame: Zombicide: SciFi Mission M21 – Enough is Enough
description:
SciFi Mission

Hard
We need to test our new Xeno Cannon. To make sure it’s working, we need to dispose of all Xenos coming through the airlock. The Mission ends when all Xenos are out of the game. This is a real Xeno-cide!

boardgame: Zombicide: SciFi Mission M22 – Magnus' Odyssey
description:
SciFi Mission

Medium
A bridge taken by Xenos, a locked exit to the escape pods, locked triggers and an injured and lost teammate. Explore some new mechanics in this adventure!

boardgame: Zombicide: SciFi Mission M23 – Fired eggs with xeno beef
description:
SciFi Mission

Hard
We discovered an old military base where the Xenos built nests. It’s easter all over again: time for an egg hunt! (But careful, the eggs explode)

boardgame: Zombicide: SciFi Mission M24 – Baraka's Odyssey
description:
SciFi Mission

A special scenario for Baraka that offers 2 game modes: easy and hard. Help is coming, so all you need to do is survive until it does! Good luck with that, though... these mold patched are full of infected Xenos.

boardgame: Zombicide: SciFi Mission M25 – Prison Infestation
description:
SciFi Mission

Hard
We equipped these mines with A LOT of safety doors, but we didn’t take these damn Drillers into account and they are now coming from everywhere! Our own ultra-safe facilities have basically become a trap for ourselves… We’ll need teamwork to deactivate switch after switch and escape!

boardgame: Zombicide: Invader – Gaming Night #2: The Xenoleaks Saga
description:
SciFi Mission
Hard

The XenoLeaks Saga is a three-part campaign designed to let players play 3 consecutive Missions of Zombicide: Invader. A Zombicide: Invader Core Game box is needed to enjoy it. Select 6 Survivors (including Mitsuki) and use them to play the Missions in order.

Mitsuki has uncovered a massive, evil scheme involving a large corporation, and now it’s up to you to invade their facilities and blow everything up!

boardgame: Zombicide: SciFi Mission M26 – It's Acid
description:
SciFi Mission

Hard
We found a mutating breed of Driller Xenos that has acidic blood. We downloaded all the research results about them onto a bot and need to escape, taking care to not get the bot dissolved – or worse, us!

boardgame: Zombicide: SciFi Mission M28 – Calling Technical Support
description:
SciFi Mission
Medium

We’re not one to turn tail and run, but we have no choice! We’re stuck in a decrepit old facility, and to escape we have to cross some dangerous external areas, but the oxygen rooms aren’t working! All we have are the tanks!

boardgame: Zombicide: SciFi Mission M27 – Cole's Odyssey
description:
SciFi Mission
Hard
In this new solo scenario, Cole decides to answer a call for help from an old friend: to enter his old, abandoned robot factory and retrieve three of his most important original robots. However, it will not be a simple task.

boardgame: Zombicide: SciFi Mission M29 – A New Danger
description:
SciFi Mission
Medium
Recent research indicates that Xenos are mutating, and the larger species, which we call Abominations, are getting faster and faster. However, its evolution seems to be hampering its ability to spread the mold. Our mission is to enter a ship wreckage, retrieve the samples and of course, survive these mutated Abominations.

boardgame: Zombicide: SciFi Mission M30 – Keep Drillin'
description:
SciFi Mission

Hard
We raided this old power plant to get a new bot and 2 prototype weapons, but drillers just keep pouring out of that hole in the center. These narrow hallways are about to get real busy!

boardgame: Zombicide: SciFi Mission M31 – Hack and Run!
description:
SciFi Mission

Very Hard
As if being overrun by Xenos wasn't bad enough, they've short-circuited the entire base. Our BOT, holding valuable data on these creatures, is out of control. We need a plan to retrieve it and evacuate before the base collapses. Mitsuki, jack in! What’s our move?

boardgame: Rat Race: From Zero to CEO
description:
Rat Race: From Zero to CEO is a fast, chaotic party board game where players take on the role of tie-wearing office rats competing to become the company’s next CEO. Through sabotage, dirty tricks, and unpredictable office meetings, players must outplay their coworkers and climb the corporate ladder before everyone else.

At the heart of the game are quick, dynamic confrontations driven by cards and Rock-Paper-Scissors duels. Players can disrupt each other’s plans, change the flow of the game, and create constant moments of tension, revenge, and laughter. The rules are easy to learn, but every match is packed with interaction and shifting momentum.

The goal is simple: survive the chaos, outsmart your rivals, and reach the top. Designed for short, highly interactive sessions, Rat Race is built to be fast to set up, quick to play, and fun to bring back to the table again and again.

—description from the designer

boardgame: In Wald und Feld: Ein Spiel zum Naturjahr
description:
There are four large landscape panels (mountains, forest, field/meadow, reed bed/pond) with matching animal and plant pictures for insertion.

Depending on your skill you can play different levels.

For beginners: 
a) Guess animals and plants!
The game master shows an animal or plant card. The first child to name it wins the card. The child with most cards at the end wins.

b) Where do animals live?
Every player gets a landscape panel. The game master draws one of the animal or plant cards and shows it. The player with the landscape that fits the animal has to demand the card. If he is right, he gets it, otherwise he has to give it back. The player that first finishes his 8 cards wins the game.

For advanced players:
a) Where does the animal live?
It is played like the beginners "guess the animals" but this time the player needs to name the animal/plant and the  landscape.
b) It is played like the beginners "Where do animals live" but every wrong answer deducts a point.

For experts:
Every player chooses a landscape panel. The game master shuffles all cards and chooses one. The player with the fitting landscape now demands the card, but only gets it if she knows the name of the animal, if it fits to her landscape panel and if she can tell a scientific explanation like "The viperine snake is harmless and non-venomous."

For mathematicians:
This time it is not played with the cards but with the puzzle parts that are punched out of the landscape panels. Every player has his own landscape panel. The game master gets all the puzzle pieces. Every puzzle piece has got a mathematical calculation on it. Now the game master chooses a puzzle part and reads the mathimatical calculation. The player that can tell the answer and finds the number that fits on his landscape panel wins the puzzle part. Winner is who finishes his landscape first.

A dystopian food-production boardgame for 2 to 4 players aged 10 and up by Néstor Romeral Andrés.

INTRODUCTION

People is a tribute to the 1973 movie Soylent Green. Please watch the movie before reading this description and the rulebook – they contain spoilers.

Watch it. Now. Okay...

In People, you run a licensed food company that produces ‘human-based’ edibles. Build factories on strategic locations to maximize your profit. You can also bribe authorities to install roadblocks or to cause mass displacements between districts by deploying Riot Control units.

—description from the designer

Grimmhain – Werewolf Reckoning is a complete reimagining of the classic social deduction game Werewolf. Players are divided into villagers and werewolves. The werewolves secretly eliminate villagers each night while the villagers attempt to identify and eliminate the werewolves during the day through discussion and voting.
What sets Grimmhain apart is its 50+ brand new roles – each with unique abilities that create entirely new strategies and interactions. A dedicated game master app handles the night order, player seating, timers, music and role reveals automatically, allowing the game master to focus entirely on atmosphere and storytelling.
Every role comes with a hand-illustrated original card. The game supports 5–25 players and is designed for both casual groups and serious game masters who want to run unforgettable rounds.

—description from the designer

La Placita is a promotional card for the board game Ruler, available as a ShikWorks exclusive.

This promo introduces a temporary global effect during a round. When active, it boosts the scoring potential of Paraphernalia cards, encouraging players to time their sets for maximum impact.

Card Effect:
"All PARAPHERNALIA cards played during this ROUND add +5 points to the original value."

The Almanac  is an expansion for Beasts and Diplomacy that greatly enhances the solo experience, by adding rule bending challenges and a captivating story. Immerse yourself in the intriguing world of Aterra's history and learn about the empires, their characters, and the relationships among them over the century that preceded the last Solstice Festival.

There are 18 chapters in The Almanac. Each chapter contains a piece of the story's puzzle, followed by a unique challenge with unique rules, which will put your skills to the test. Complete the challenge to unlock more of the story and proceed ever deeper in Aterra's past. Rumour has it that once you complete all 18 chapters there is a final challenge awaiting you.

A great evil is about to plunge Aterra into eternal darkness... Will you be able to stop it?

—description from the publisher

Pokyčių Maršrutas ("Route of Changes") is an educational game for youth about sustainable mobility and transport emissions, allowing you to dive into Vilnius city adventures where every trip has a cost... or a benefit!

Embark on a journey through popular Vilnius youth spots, plan your routes, choose sustainable or convenient modes of transport, and observe how your decisions and those of other players change the city's future. The city is alive—new laws, natural disasters, and unexpected inventions are constantly appearing in it.

Plan your movement in the city wisely, track your health status, and visit as many places as possible while trying to keep the city as clean as possible—only then will you become the winner of this game!

App Wars is a competitive card game about BUILDING APPS, STEALING USERS, AND SURVIVING CHAOS. It is a fast-paced card game inspired by the brutal competition of the tech world.

In App Wars, you compete to build the largest user base using strategic battle and situation cards to outsmart opponents. Collect users, swipe competitors’ assets, and battle your way to victory. The goal is to acquire 3 million users (normal game) or 5 million users (extended game) to win. The game is broken down into sprints (yes, like in Agile). Each sprint ends when a player acquires 1 million or more users or once all cards in the draw deck have been played. A minimum of 3 players is required.

—description from the designer

boardgame: The Last Kingdom: Destiny is All
description:
In the days before England, the island was split into four Saxon kingdoms. Peace was held between them only due to a common enemy, the Great Heathen Army. The invading Danes raid and march across the kingdoms taking land and silver. Will the Danes conquer kingdom after kingdom, or will the Saxons be able to defend their homes? Caught in the middle of this war is the mighty warrior, Uhtred. Born a Saxon and raised a Dane, Uhtred’s allegiance is ever shifting. Only destiny can say what the outcome will be. Destiny is all!

The Last Kingdom: Destiny is All is a card game for two players. One player is the invading Danish forces, trying to conquer all four kingdoms, and the other player is the defending Saxon armies, trying to repel the invaders.

Each round the Danes will try to Raid a Location, which could give them extra forces in the upcoming Battle. Whether they succeed or fail, the Danes and Saxons will then fight a Battle at a Location. They’ll take turns playing Leaders, Units, and Tactics from their hand which increase their Battle Total and try to influence Uhtred over to their side. Finally, a Destiny card will be revealed which may alter the outcome of the battle.

How to win
The Danes must conquer the Saxon Kingdoms! Win a Battle in each of the four different Kingdoms to control the whole island!

The Saxons must repel the Invaders! Win Battles at 10 Defense Points worth of valuable Locations from the invaders!

At Bible Clash, the gospel is woven into the gameplay itself. Every matchup forces players to think: What made Peter faithful? Why was John the Baptist courageous? How did Mary demonstrate obedience? The six categories—Faith, Wisdom, Courage, Obedience, Service, and Strategy—aren't random stats. They're the Christ-like virtues Scripture calls us to pursue.
Bible Clash doesn't just teach Bible facts. It sparks real conversations about biblical character—and shapes how we think about following Jesus. This is discipleship disguised as fun.

"And He went up on the mountain and called to Him those He Himself wanted." — Mark 3:13

WHAT'S IN THE BOX: 

     32 Character Cards (split 16 per player) 
     Each card features a Gospel character rated across 6 biblical categories: Faith, Wisdom, Courage, Obedience, Service, Strategy • Rules Cards


HOW TO PLAY: 

     Shuffle the deck and deal 16 cards to each player. Hold your deck face-down in your hand so only the top card is visible to you.
     The starting player (youngest player) looks at the top card of his deck, chooses one category, and announces it. For example: "Peter... Faith 8!" 
     The other player looks at the top card of his deck and reads the score for the same category. For example: “Pilate: Faith 3.”
     The higher score wins! The winner takes both cards and places them at the bottom of his deck.
     The winner of the round chooses the category in the next round. The same category cannot be chosen twice in a row.
     The first player to win all the cards wins the game! 


Extra Rules: 
Tie Breaker: If scores are tied, put both cards on the table and compare the same category of the next card. Winner takes all 4 cards (or 6…)!
Time Limit (Optional): If a game lasts longer than 15 minutes, start a 3-minute timer. When time runs out, the player with the most cards wins.

—description from the designer

boardgame: Fury of the Northman: Warfare in the Viking Age 763-1066 AD
description:
Welcome to Fury of the Northman which is a game of Viking warfare using the Men of Bronze engine from the Osprey Wargaming Series 24 (OWG 24) written by Eric Farrington. This is a stand-alone game that uses the same ideas and mechanics, but ported and modified to the Viking Age!

These rules are scale and model agnostic and do not have any specific basing requirements. 
The rules are designed for unit-vs-unit combat. 
Combat is resolved via opposed dice pools of D6 and the use of Ferocity Points to trigger special rules, initiative, formation changes, etc. 
There is no figure removal and can accommodate games of 4 units up to 10 units or more per side.

To capture the feeling of Viking Age combat and the many cultures of the historical period, unit types have been abstracted and special rules applied to capture the unique fighting styles of the time. The Lines-of-Battle in this book are all new and unique to the period and cover Viking raids, to Norse kingdoms, European Kingdoms, Iberia, and Muslim forces.

I hope you enjoy your battles in the Viking Age using these rules.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Marvel: Crisis Protocol Alliances – Night of the Goblin
description:
Marvel: Crisis Protocol Alliances - Night of the Goblin is a cooperative adventure game for up to four players. Players work together to thwart each of the Green Goblin’s sinister plots throughout the city, but stopping this nefarious villain won’t be easy. Players will have to fight their way through hordes of minions including the numerous Goblin Army goons, robotic Spider-Slayers, and deadly War Goblins.

As players defeat enemies and complete each encounter, they will earn valuable experience, allowing them to level up and customize their hero with new and more powerful abilities. Abilities they will certainly need in order to triumph in their ultimate showdown with the Green Goblin himself!

—description from the publisher

boardgame: The Ionian Revolt: A supplement to Men of Bronze
description:
Welcome to The Ionian Revolt which is a supplement to Men of Bronze from the Osprey Wargaming Series 24 (OWG 24) written by Eric Farrington.  
This is not a stand alone game.  
To make use of these Lines of Battle lists, scenarios, and campaign you will need a copy of the Men of Bronze rules to take full advantage of these rules.

The Ionian Revolt is a supplement intended to flesh out and add detail for the Ionian Revolt. 
This period has been made popular by the Histories of Herodotus. He is the primary source for this period of history and the battles fought. Frequently, the details are left scattered, or with few details.
Instead, we must build on what is written, the archeology, and good old fashion supposition to fill in some of the gaps.

This particular period is of interest to me as a gamer for a few reasons. 
One, the details are sparse so there is room for us to play the types of games we wish to play. 
Second, this was a full-scale naval and land based campaign which gives it greater scope to explore. 
Finally, understanding this conflict is like holding up a mirror to the Greek-Persian Wars that came after. In Ionian, the Persians faced and defeated Greeks. In the Greek-Persian Wars, the opposite happened. The differences in outcome are interesting.

—description from the designer

boardgame: Homer's Heroes: Bronze Age Bad Boys
description:
Homer's Heroes: Bronze Age Bad Boys is a tabletop wargame of mythical Greek combat from the Homeric Age.

Each player has a Hero and his loyal band of followers as the embark on a quest to grow their legends.  
The game can be played stand alone, but includes a comprehensive campaign system, Solo, and Co-op options.

These rules are scale and model agnostic and utilize d6 dice pools looking for a Target Number with opposed successes.  
The focus is on Model-vs.Model combat between heroes and their soldiers.

Can you become one of Homer's Heroes? 

Turn Sequence:

     Determine Initiative
     Perform Activations
     End Turn  


—description from the publisher

CL4SH: Elemental Duel is a fast-paced card game for 2–6 players where strategy meets chaos. Players use elemental cards—Fire, Water, Air, and Earth—to outplay opponents and be the first to empty their hand. Special action cards create dynamic twists, making every round competitive, unpredictable, and highly replayable.

Each turn, players must play a card that defeats the previously played element using the game’s elemental dominance system. If they can’t, they must draw a card—bringing them further from victory.

The goal is simple: be the first to empty your hand.

What makes CL4SH unique is its layered interaction. Special action cards allow players to:

     Challenge opponents in head-to-head duels
     Reverse the flow of the game
     Force opponents to draw cards
     Disrupt momentum at critical moments


These effects create constant tension, strategy, and unpredictable swings.

How Gameplay Flows

     Players are dealt a starting hand
     A card is flipped to begin the play pile
     On your turn:
         Play a card that beats the current element, OR
         Draw a card if you cannot play
         Special cards can override normal play and shift control
    


Winning the Game

The first player to successfully play all of their cards wins the round.

—description from the designer

boardgame: Heirs of Empire: Wars of the Diadochi
description:
Heirs to Empire: Wars of the Diadochi is a scale and model agnostic mass battle game set during the wars to divide Alexander's empire between his successors.  
It is a period of roughly 40 years of conflict.  
It features big battles, small skirmishes, political intrigue, alliances, betrayal, and everything that can make a historical wargame great!

Heirs to Empire uses an expanded system made popular in Osprey's Men of Bronze in the Osprey Wargaming Series.  Therefore, it is scale and model agnostic.  
The mechanics have been tweaked to help reflect the large scale battles common in this period.  
It evolves to allow players to use armies that fit the scale of combat in the Diadochi Wars.  
In addition, it has an expanded campaign system and a streamlined set of Scenarios.  
Finally, it establishes the unique armies and units of Diadochi armies.

Enjoy your games battling rival Diadochi!

—description from the publisher
family: History: Wars of the Diadochi and Syrian Wars

description:
Quillén: Bosque Esclerófilo is a deck-building and dice-management game for 2 to 4 players who take on the role of conservationists restoring a degraded Chilean Mediterranean-climate ecosystem. Through the restoration of native flora, fauna, and funga, players work to create a resilient environment capable of providing vital ecosystem services and managing the essential cycle of water.

During the game, players play hands of five cards to launch and manage colored dice representing different environmental factors. These dice are used as resources to purchase Restoration cards (which upgrade the player's deck and improve future actions) or Scoring cards. Strategy involves balancing dice rolls, mitigating the effects of "Drought" cards, and managing a limited individual reserve to optimize resources.

The winner is the player who earns the most Victory Points (PV) by the end of the round in which someone reaches 15 points. Ties are broken by the number of high-level Restoration cards in a player's possession, rewarding those who built the most complex and healthy ecosystems.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: King of the Dice: Dragon's Fury
description:
Five additional master dragons for King of the Dice.
Whenever one of the master dragons are placed at the discard pile, they eat-up all character cards.

Gather your animal friends and set sail around the globe in search of fame, fortune, and pretty flowers in this adorable game for two to five players.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Revolt!: A supplement for Wars of the Republic
description:
Welcome to Revolt! which is a supplement to Wars of the Republic from the Osprey Wargaming Series 29 (OWG 29) written by Eric Farrington. 
This is not a stand alone game. 
To make use of these scenarios and Lines of Battle lists you will need a copy of the Wars of the Republic rules to take full advantage of these rules.

Wars of the Republic was intended to be a broad tool box that allowed players to use the rules for a variety of conflicts during the time of the Roman Republic. Of course, that covers a lot of time, territory and styles of fighting. Therefore, it is inevitably broad. In fact, most of the historical detail is left to the wargamer themselves to gather from their own sources.

Revolt! Is a supplement intended to flesh out and add detail for the 3rd Servile War. This has been a popular conflict due to the modern media portrayals of the main protagonist; Spartacus! The man and his cause managed to capture the imagination. The rules for Wars of the Republic do cover fielding various armies in the 3rd Servile War but they do not dig very deep into the conflict or how to play it on the table. This supplement intends to fill that void with a much more detailed look at the war so you can play it out on your own tabletops.

—description from the publisher

Does the card unsettle small children? Unimportant! Does it bear the slightest relation to the holiday it purports to celebrate? Entirely beside the point! It's time to submit your cards to the Editor-in-Chief and compete against your rivals to become the next rising star of the Sowersby Greeting Card Company.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: The Corinthian War: Wargaming the Corinthian War 395 BCE - 387 BCE
description:
Welcome to The Corinthian War which is a supplement to Men of Bronze from the Osprey Wargaming Series written by Eric Farrington. 
This is not a stand alone game. 
To make use of these scenarios and Lines of Battle lists you will need a copy of Men of Bronze rules to take full advantage of these rules.

—description from the publisher

In the year 2100, Earth’s climate has deteriorated beyond repair. Humanity launches seed ships into space, scattering human DNA across the cosmos. Generations later, the first new humans grow up far from their origin, gazing at the stars in search of answers about their existence. Each observer sees the universe from a different perspective, but by sharing information, they can piece together the greater truth hidden among the stars.

Counting Stars (目里星辰) is a puzzle deduction game for 3–8 players, centered on asymmetric information and line of sight. The standard rules are designed for 3–4 players, while a simplified mode allows play with up to 8.

Players each hold cards representing celestial bodies, seeing only one side while others see the opposite. By asking questions about the number of observed celestial objects and interpreting the answers, players gradually reconstruct hidden information.


     Each player holds 1–2 cards, seeing only their own side.
     Other players can see the opposite side of your cards.
     Players ask questions about visible celestial bodies to gather information.
     The game includes six different questioning rules, encouraging varied logical approaches.
     Players use deduction to determine the hidden information on the table.


This game is part of the second edition of the 9 Cards series.
publishers: 九张牌玩具公司(9 Cards Toy Company)

boardgame: In Strife and Conflict: Assyrian Warfare from 1273 to 671 BCE
description:
In Strife and Conflict is a scale and model agnostic battle game set during the reign of the Assyrian Kings from 1273 to 671 BCE. This work features the Assyrians and their enemies of the Bronze Age and early Iron Age.

In Strife and Conflict uses an expanded system made popular in Osprey's Men of Bronze in the Osprey Wargaming Series. Therefore, it is scale and model agnostic. The mechanics have been tweaked to help reflect the large scale chariot battles common in this period. In addition, it has an expanded campaign system and a streamlined set of Scenarios. Finally, it establishes the unique armies and units of late Bronze and early Iron Age chariot armies.

Enjoy your games battling rival Kings!

—description from the publisher
family: History: Wars in the Ancient Near East

description:
Ring Tile is a tile game played with identical square tiles whose two sides have different colors (one side is black, other side is white). The goal is to form a ring (loop) or a territory (friendly tiles surrounding empty or partially occupied area) with your tiles while keeping your opponent from doing the same.
 Players take turns. Black moves first and places a tile on any flat surface (no board) and black side up. Then White moves and places second tile, white side up touching either corner-to-corner or edge-to-edge (or both) to another tile. An edge of the newly placed tile must be unoccupied by opponent’s tile (or it may not be placed in a position where all four edges would be occupied by opponent’s tiles). After that play alternates.

Tile placement is legal if newly placed tile shows moving player’s color, touches another tile, and if an edge of the tile is unoccupied by opponent’s tile which would disconnect it from open area. A tile’s connection to open area can either be direct (one edge is literally unoccupied) or indirect (the tile touches edge-to-edge to another friendly tile or group of them that has direct connection to open area).

Capture

If player plays edge-to-edge to all edges of opponent’s tile (or a group) you capture that tile by removing it from the tile field and win the game. For capture the newly placed capturing tile must always have an edge unoccupied by unfriendly tile (before, during, and after the capture). In other words, player may not play a tile into an illegal area in the hopes of capturing and opening an unoccupied edge for the capturing tile. 
       
First player to make a territory (by surrounding an open area and/or by capture) with his tiles wins the game.

—description from the designer

boardgame: Odin's Ravens: Viking Age Bad Boys
description:
Odin's Ravens: Viking Age Bad Boys is a tabletop wargame of Viking combat from the Norse Sagas.  
Each player has a Hero and his loyal band of followers as the embark on a quest to grow their legends.  
The game can be played stand alone, but includes a comprehensive campaign system, Solo, and Co-op options.

These rules are scale and model agnostic and utilize d6 dice pools looking for a Target Number with opposed successes.  The focus is on Model-vs.Model combat between heroes and their soldiers.

Can you become one of Odin's Ravens?

—description from the designer

boardgame: Vantage Point (fan expansion for Android: Netrunner)
description:
Vantage Point is the first standalone rotating set, and is not a part of a larger cycle. It was designed from the ground up to fill in some of the gaps left by the large rotation that accompanied the release of Elevation, injecting both variety and powerful effects back into the card pool. It features four new identity cards (for the Shaper, Criminal, NBN, and Jinteki factions), and support cards for returning mechanics such as stealth and bad publicity.

System Gateway, Elevation and Vantage Point together constitute the core card pool that forms the basis for Null Signal Games Standard and Startup game formats, and is the ideal foundation for all levels of Netrunner play.

Vantage Point explores several of the locales featured in our previous sets, and checks in on some familiar characters from them. Most prominently, we revisit the Lunar surface and marvel at the starlit domes of the hitherto-unexplored city of Méliès, while deep beneath them Corps hide some of their deepest secrets.

boardgame: Combat!: Starring Vic Morrow – World War II on TV Skirmish Game
description:
In these pages you will find a game that allows you to play out your favorite Television and Movie adventures of World War II.
You will be the director setting up scenes and playing out the events of the plot. Your direction will lead your band of plucky TV heroes across France and towards Berlin. 
Can your show earn enough ratings to stay on the air until VE day? 
Will your stars make it to the end of the Season?

The core game play mechanics revolve around using the actions of your Stars and Co-Stars to achieve results on the TV screen by rolling over target #s on a standard d6 dice. 
Stars will have more actions and screentime than lowly extras!
Plus, their exploits can earn you ratings to increase your shows production values as the Season progresses.

This is not World War II skirmish gaming, this is World War II skirmish gaming on your TV! 
Settle in with your TV dinner, and get ready to play  Combat! Starring Vic Morrow .

—description from the publisher
family: Players: Wargames with Rules Supporting Only Two Players

boardgame: Castles in the Sky: A Wargame of Flying Battleships – Addendum
description:
Castles in the Sky-Addendum is a supplement to Osprey Games' Castles in the Sky from the Osprey Wargaming Series 30 (OWG 30) written by Eric Farrington.

This is not a stand alone game.

To make use of these rules and ships you will need a copy of the Castles in the Sky rules to take full advantage of this PDF.

—description from the publisher

A competitive 2-5 player deck building card game where you control dinosaurs, outplay rivals and score big!

Rawr is a deck-building game in which alien factions mind control dinosaurs to win the war on prehistoric Earth. Your dinosaurs remain in play over multiple turns, eating prey or scoring points thanks to their ongoing presence.

Keep your dinosaurs alive from turn to turn to benefit from their start of turn ⌛effect! But fear not, once eliminated they are discarded, which means you will be able to put them back in play.

During setup, choose one of the 5 collections to customize your experience. Shuffle it with the base cards to create the central card river, from which players may obtain new cards. A lot of 5 primitive cards are always present every game, regardless of chosen collection.

The game ends when a player has reached or surpassed the target score. Everyone plays the same number of turns, then we tally the score and the highest scoring alien faction wins!

—description from the publisher

A tiny 1-5 minute wargame for 1 or more players. 
Skuffle Wammer is fast but fun. 
Play at your desk, at the kitchen table, while you wait for coffee, or practically anywhere. 
Grab a bunch of toy soldiers, some dice, and play.

The rules are designed to promote hard choices, action economy, and a ticking clock. 
You can use any miniatures you want, and the play area is only 20x20 cm (about the size of a sheet of printer paper). 
Play solo or recruit a friend for a quick taste of war gaming.

In short, if you play Skuffle Wammer to ensure you get your quota of gaming in no matter where you are or how short on time you might be!

Gameplay:
On a miniature's turn, it may move or attack, but never both.

Win:
The miniatures left standing after all dice have been removed win the skuffle.

—description from the publisher

In this game, players participate in auctions as art collectors, competing to either complete their art collection as quickly as possible or amass a fortune through buying and selling paintings.
Designed for 2 to 6 players, each game lasts about 20 minutes, making it easy to pick up and play. The game features authentic bluffing mechanics that create compelling dilemmas, striking a balance that ensures enjoyment for both beginners and seasoned players alike.
Thanks to a system with no elimination, there is zero downtime—a common issue in bluffing games—and players can aim for a comeback right up until the very end, delivering a nail-biting gaming experience.

—description from the publisher (translated)

You are a forest kami, ancient, powerful, and a friend and mentor to the foxes of the forest. Help your foxes improve their skills, play tricks on rival foxes and villagers, and earn the nine tails they need to ascend and become kami themselves.

Each fox begins with one tail. If a fox gains a total of nine tails, it ascends and becomes a kami. The first player to have helped three foxes ascend wins the game.

—description from the publisher

In Green City, players act as urban planners building a sustainable transit network to connect Riders to their destinations before the supply of tiles runs out. You will score points for every successful Walking, Biking, Bus, or Tram connection you make. Longer Tram/Bus route scores more points, while Bike/Walking Path is best in medium/short lengths.

The game is played in clockwise turns. The game ends when the Cloth Bag is empty, then each player places their last tile on hand.
On your turn, perform the following 3 steps in order:

     Place a Path Tile
     Send Riders
     Draw a New Tile



As you lay down tiles and paths, you immediately dispatch your Riders along these new routes. You may activate bonus tiles to gain more points for routing through specific points on the map. 
  
Try to send as many Riders as you can to avoid significant penalties for leaving them stranded at the end. The player with the most Victory Points at the end of the game wins.

boardgame: Unlock!: Short Adventures – La Voie du Sabre
description:
Unlock! Short adventures is a series of escape adventures for up to six players. With 30 to 45 minutes on the clock, players work through a deck of 30 cards as a team, searching for clues, combining objects, and solving puzzles. The free Unlock! companion app runs the timer while also providing clues, offering hints, and confirming successes. Once the team has reached a solution and entered the correct code into the app, they will escape and win the game!

With this adventure, take on a journey in medieval Japan and play as a samurai woman from the Kamakura era.

Difficulty: 2/3
family: History: Kamakura Period to Muromachi Period

description:
In Lucha Furia, each team of luchadores fights to be the last one standing on the ring!

- On your turn, send a new luchador into the ring, push others into position, trigger wild ring events.

- Think ahead, trap your opponents, and throw a chair at just the right moment to seize victory.

- Beware: if three of your luchadores line up, you're out!

A fast, tactical, and explosive game set in the high-flying world of Mexican lucha libre.

—description from the publisher

This expansion adds four new Faction decks that give more variety. 
 A band of Sky Pirates, Disgraced Royalty, a Mindless Cult, and an enclave of Freedom Fighters.

Red Hegemony, a band of Sky Pirates and Rogues who live on the cloudbases of the acid wastes - a loose collection of slavers and criminals who have escaped justice in other states of the world. - This faction is for players who love to hit fast and hard with small disposable units.

Cult of Apsynthos. The Cult is a group who have taken to living in the areas of the Fungal Wastes that Windwalkers fear to tread, due to the presence of toxic psychoactive xenofungus that can easily infect humans. - This faction is for players who love to create chaos and believers in victory at any cost.

Coriolan Exiles. These exiles have been forced to find a new way of live, melting down golden thrones to make bullets. Merchant caravans of exiles roam the steppe, trading between the cities. - This faction is for players who like to negotiate and players who like to bide their time.

The Dronestate is an independent enclave of Drones who have escaped from the stratified societies of Apsyntion. Drones here speak of freedom and revolution. They wage war to liberate their brethren, using datahacks, subterfuge, assasssination, and sacrifice to achieve their goals. This faction is for players who want to create efficient economies and commit sabotage.

Bote "20ml" Shikworks (Shikworks "20ml" Jar) is a promotional Permanent card for the urban card game Ruler. Unlike disposable items, this card remains in the player's play area, providing a persistent strategic advantage throughout the game.

This promo allows players to store up to 2 Material cards of the corresponding colors (Brown and Yellow). This storage capability is crucial for managing hand size and protecting essential resources needed for future high-prestige combinations.

As part of the official SW series, this card adds a layer of long-term planning to the core set collection mechanics. This card is a ShikWorks exclusivity.

This powerful storage solution allows players to keep up to 3 Material cards on it. Additionally, it offers a tactical advantage: you may discard 1 Material to swap it for the next card in the Dispensary. During a round, it can also be used as a Tray +3.

This card is part of the SW promotional series and is a ShikWorks exclusivity.

boardgame: Ruler: La Chusta Grow Promo Card
description:
La Chusta Grow is a promotional "Tienda" (Shop) card for the urban card game Ruler.

This promo card represents a real-world location in Móstoles, Madrid. During the round it is active, players can play up to 2 Materials and all Paraphernalia cards receive a +3 score bonus. Additionally, it offers a powerful one-time ability: you can discard (X) Paraphernalia and dispense (X) without losing your turn.

This card is an exclusivity of La Chusta GrowShop.

Cultura Cannábica is a promotional "Tienda" (Shop) card for the urban card game Ruler, representing the iconic location at Plaza Cascorro, 1 (Madrid).

During the ROUND this card is active, it provides the following bonuses:

Permanent Paraphernalia adds +3 points.

Disposable Paraphernalia adds +5 points.

Additionally, it features a powerful one-time ability: you can DISCARD (X) Paraphernalia and DISPENSE (X) without losing your turn. This card is an exclusivity of Cultura Cannábica.

boardgame: Ruler: Hueva Doble Cero Promo Card
description:
Hueva "Doble Cero" is a promotional Material card for the urban card game Ruler.

This Brown Material card provides a base value of +5 prestige points. However, it features a powerful synergy: if the "EL BANQUITO" event is currently active, the card's value increases by an additional +5, for a total of +10 points.

This promo is a Generación X exclusivity, specifically distributed during the InterOcio board game fair in Madrid.

One Fowl Poop is a funny, fast-paced family card game full of risky decisions, silly strategy, and constant interaction. Players compete to build the best nest by collecting egg cards, while doing their best to avoid picking up poop tokens. Every turn is a push-your-luck challenge as you balance reward against risk.

Easy to learn and quick to play, One Fowl Poop is ideal for family game nights, parties, and casual get-togethers. Action cards, moving poop, and shifting nest conditions ensure no two games feel the same. With plenty of laughs, groans, and friendly rivalry, it’s a brilliant choice for kids, teens, and adults alike.

—description from the publisher

The excitement of this yomi-style game comes from predicting your opponent's move and countering it.

Battle across two Wars with identical decks. Take turns to attack and defend. Play two cards each turn, one face-up and one face-down.

Choose carefully, as your face-down card has a hidden tactic that affects it's ally when revealed. Did we mention each Toad comes with a unique hidden tactic?! After each battle is resolved, capture the defeated as hostages. When your deck runs out, it's the end of the War.

The extra wrinkle comes from leaving out a casualty and swapping decks at the end of the first War:

Will you take out a high casualty, leave your opponent with a weak deck and go for the revenge win? Or try to dominate the first war and eek out a victory by forcing a stalemate in the second?

Features: 

     plenty of bluffing 
     a smidge of card counting, and
     hand-drawn Japanese wood-block inspired Samurai Toads


—description from the designer

boardgame: Craps!: Three dice and a deck of cards
description:
Craps! is a dice and card game inspired by the classic casino table game. Using a standard deck of cards, players flip one card at a time to set the point number, then roll three dice trying to hit it.

Game Rules:

     Players take turns rolling all three dice, aiming to hit the point number on the face-up card. Any combination of dice is valid (i.e., a point number can be scored using just one die or by adding the values of two or three dice). All three dice are rolled every turn.



     When the number on the card is hit, the roller wins the card and scores 1 point. Flip over a new card to establish a new point number.



     If a roller scores a point or hits a hardway, they keep the dice and roll again.



     The first player to 7 points wins the round and craps out!


Point Numbers:

     Cards 2–10: Use the number on the card
     Face cards: Each roller picks a number between 10–18
     Ace: The first roller picks 1 or 11
     Joker: The first roller picks any number


Hardways (Special Rolls):

     Any three of a kind
     Any straight


Bonus Points (Special Rolls Worth 1 Point):

     All 1s or all 6s
     A straight or three of a kind while hitting the point number (the roller scores 2 points)

The Dutch bees need your help! Their habitat is shrinking, and now wasps are starting to bother them as well. This must be stopped. Surround the larvae with honey to protect them from the wasps so they can no longer be stolen!

In Honey Wall, you try to score points by surrounding bee larvae with honey. By rolling dice, you can draw larvae or honey on your game board. If you roll a wasp, you may steal larvae from your opponent that are not surrounded by honey and score points with them at the end of the game. Large groups of larvae are worth more points, but they are also more vulnerable to wasps from your opponent. The player who has scored the most points with larvae at the end of the game wins!

Each player receives their own sheet with a game board. On this board, players draw their own area with larvae and honey. A turn consists of four phases: a dice phase, a honey and larvae phase, a wasp phase, and a flower phase. When it is a player’s turn, they begin by rolling 2 or 3 dice (depending on which have been unlocked by the player). The player may reroll one or more dice once before moving on to the next phase.
The second phase is the honey and larvae phase. In this phase, all dice showing a honey or larvae symbol are resolved. The active player colors in spaces on their board where possible to place honey and larvae.

After this comes the wasp phase. During the wasp phase, the player may try to steal larvae from other players. If a larva is not fully surrounded by honey, it is vulnerable to wasps and can be stolen. A player who rolls one or more wasps may place a wasp symbol on other players’ boards to indicate that a larva has been stolen. At the end of the game, stolen larvae give points to the player who stole them.

In the final phase, the flower phase, the active player checks whether they have completed a flower on their board. If so, they may move up one step on the tree at the bottom of the board to unlock bonuses. After this phase, the next player takes their turn, and play continues until an end condition is met.

The game ends immediately when a player has either two areas with seven or more enclosed larvae each, or five areas with fewer than seven enclosed larvae each. The player who has collected the most points from (stolen) larvae wins the game!

—description from the publisher

You hear the enemy approaching through the smoke. You blow on your match and check it's length. you bring the heavy matchlock stock to your shoulder and lay the long barrel on it's rest. You are ready to kill, for you are a musketeer of the English civil war.

Come out ye Cuckold! is a miniature wargame focusing on skirmishes in the English civil war. 
It's a fast paced, narrative driven wargame for 28mm figures. 
The idea is for quick, easy games that still offer depth and narrative moments. 
It works on a dice activation intiative system. 
All dice rolls use standard six-sided dice. 
The figure count is small, one side fielding about 6 to 10 models. 
The table size can scale to the number of figures you are playing with. Figures have no facing. 
Figures are equipped as you see them (WYSIWYG). 
Combat is deadly. A man is just a man; there are no superheroes here.

What's in the book

     In this book you'll find full rules for playing out skirmishes between historical factions.
     Rules for foot, cavalry and even cannon. 
     There are rules for choosing a faction and creating a force, as well as adding traits and equipment to enhance each models character.
     Rules for narrative actions during the game.
     Examples of play to help first time players.
     There is a full campaign system to play through and watch your men gain experience and injuries as you take territory from the enemy.
     A chronology of the first English civil war. 
     listed in the back is a QRS, roster sheet and a list of historically appropriate insults that are used in the game.


—description from the designer
language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups
family: Players: Wargames with Rules Supporting Only Two Players

description:
Champions of Word Wrestling is a word making and manipulation game with a theme of sumo wrestling. The art on the cards shows sumo wrestlers in various positions, and each player on their turn must change the current word into a new word using one or two actions, or they are out of the game, similar to a wrestler being pushed out of the ring.

The game's deck has 124 cards: 96 cards with one letter, 25 with two letters, one "any vowel" card and two wild cards. At the start of the game each player is dealt a hand of 7 cards and the top of the draw pile is placed on the table as the starter card. The first player who can make that letter into a word starts the game, and turns continue clockwise around the table.

On a turn, a player must use one or two moves (actions) to change the current word on the table into a new word, one not used in the game before.  The allowed actions are: 

     ADD: Play a card at the beginning, end, or anywhere in the middle of the word, without changing the order of the cards. 
     REPLACE: Play a card on top of an existing card. 
     SHUFFLE: Rearrange the order of the cards. 
     THROW: Remove a stack of cards, and discard a card from the hand.  


After making the new word, the player draws cards to replace those used for the Add and Replace actions. (The Throw action reduces a player's hand size for the rest of the game.) If a player cannot think of a way to make a new word using two moves, they are out of the game, and the last player left is the winner.

The designers of Champions of Word Wrestling include a speech language pathologist, and their intent was to make a game that was fun and engaging, as well as educational.  Their page wordwrestling.com/research has pointers to many research papers that demonstrate the cognitive, social, and emotional benefits of word games.

boardgame: Chapters: A Libraries and Archives Game
description:
The year is 2025. It has been 30 years since the National Library Board (NLB) was formed, serving both readers young and old, and those on a knowledge and information quest.

Come journey with us and discover the vast range of NLB's features over the years.

Chapters: A Libraries and Archives Game is the nostalgic tableau building and set collection game of living libraries and archives for 2 to 4 players where you build your own library and invite Friends to win as many Hearts as you can.

Build Features in your library to gain Categories!
Friends can only be invited if your library meets their Category requirements.
Each Friend has a unique effect! Get the best combination of Friends and Features to win the most Hearts!

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Neopets Battledome Trading Card Game: Champions of Meridell Starter Deck
description:
This deck introduces Petpets, Petpet Battles, new mechanics with the Hero-Villain cards, and new Neopets such as Draik, Korbat and Skeith!
Get your hands on exclusive content and the Rainbow Paint Brush, allowing all players to upgrade their collection with cards from the Battle for and Champions of Meridell storylines as well as reprints of fan favorite cards.
Upgrade your set with the Champions of Meridell Booster Packs to theme out your dec

What If...WARS is a quick under 90 minute print n play area control and unit resource managed combat strategy game for 1 to 2 players. (solo play mode included) It offers a Legacy Light options to track battle wins on each side changing the landscape of the war.  Set in chronological order of scenarios (battles) with the first four (4) included in the base game and an additional 12 to 16 more to follow based on historical encounters in World War II. Just one 6-sided die and a pencil needed with very little cutouts and relatively light on printer ink use.  Enjoy!

—description from the designer
Special 20-cards deck to play Genpei solo. It was one of the stretch goals during Kickstarter campaign.

boardgame: Just One: New Version – BGG Promo Pack
description: A pack of five cards featuring words chosen via a BGG Contest run in 2025. This promo pack features the updated layout and artwork from the new version of Just One that premiered in 2025.

Special 13-cards deck contains hidden single-use bonuses for players. Each player is dealt 2 cards at the start of the game and can use them at any time during their turn. It was one of the stretch goals during Kickstarter campaign.

In Blooming Bouquets, players are florists building beautiful bouquets for their customers by rolling flower dice to collect flowers from the market for their bouquets.

On their turn, players will roll 5 dice up to three times.  The first roll is all 5 dice and the subsequent rolls are up to the player based on the available flowers in the market and open flowers on the players' order board.  Players will collect flowers from the flower market in attempt to complete entire bouquet orders.

Bouquet orders contain 3, 4, or 5 flowers.  Daisies, daffodils, and zinnias are COMMON flowers; roses and lillies are RARE.  COMMMON flowers are easier to roll and appear more in the market, but score less victory points in the end.

The game ends once a player successfully completes 3 bouquets - the florist who scores the most points wins the game!

There are variations of the game for both solo and cooperative mode.

—description from the publisher

LETTERTRAY is an expansion to WORDMINGLE, consisting of two things:

1. Trays to hold all the letter tiles.  There are two stacking trays with spaces or slots to hold all the tiles. They are nicely designed to hold all the letters in alphabetical order, and to be easily removeable from the box. There is also a smaller tray to hold the two blank tiles, which goes below the other trays and takes up all the space not taken by the colored tile holders. (When ordering the expansion, you can specify that that small tray be personalized.) The site wordmingle.app/ltsetup has video instructions for how to use the trays.

2. A more challenging mode of WORDMINGLE, which gives you 12 letters to separate into two words, rather than 10.  The site wordmingle.app/ltchallenge is where you find those challenges. Like the original wordmingle.app, the new page has options DAILY for the daily puzzle, RANDOM for a random puzzle, and GUIDE for the rules.  When the new site gives you the 12 letters, it also gives 3 hints, showing which word (white or black) they belong to, always including one in one color and two in the other.  Even with the hints, the 12-letter mode of WORDMINGLE gives players an extra challenge in their play.

An old children's show has begun resurfacing in the dark corners of the internet. At first glance, it seems innocent, but those who watch it vanish or die under mysterious circumstances. Hundreds of rumors are circulating online: a cursed series, a dangerous cult, a supernatural creature...

As investigators, your mission is to decipher the hidden messages, uncover who is behind these, and stop the sinister deaths before it's too late. Each clue brings you closer to the truth... but also to danger.

Can you solve the mystery and make it out alive?

The game is an alternate reality game: an interactive and immersive experience that blends elements of the real world with fiction. The player must investigate real websites and platforms in order to progress through the chapters of the story. The game box contains a lot of paper puzzles with their respective instructions and letters to help you solve the chapters. The game also contains other kinds of physical objects and puzzles related to them.

—description from the publisher

Sleddy The Last Episode is an immersive analog horror escape game. You will investigate an old lost VHS series, and it will be like facing a real creepypasta.

This is a standalone game, but it is a parallel story to another of our games: Sleddy Lost Tapes. They can be played separately, but playing both enriches the plot.

The game is an alternate reality game: an interactive and immersive experience that blends elements of the real world with fiction. The player must investigate real websites and platforms in order to progress through the chapters of the story. The game box contains a lot of paper puzzles with their respective instructions and letters to help you solve the chapters. The game also contains other kinds of physical objects and puzzles related to them.

—description from the publisher

Words Built Backwards is a game for 2 players, but more players may be added by using more than one set.  Each player gets 95 6-mm letter cubes in a draw-string bag -- black cubes with white letters for one player, and white cubes with black letters for the other. The cubes are the type of square beads used in making bracelets with words along them, with the same letter on all sides of the cube.  They also each get a blue staging area strip with spaces for 8 cubes, and a red rack for spelling words, with 5 rows of 8 spaces for cubes.

There are no turns: Each player draws 8 cubes from their bag and places them on the staging area, and starts spelling words with them.  Letters are placed on any row of the red rack, going from right to left, meaning that words are spelled backwards.  Empty spaces on the staging area are filled from the bag.  It is allowed to place letters on the red rack that do not spell valid words, allowing one to bring more letters out of the bag, but such "words" are not scored.

The game can end one of several ways: 

     First to Finish: When one player is content with what they have on their rack, they say "Time", and the other player stops, too. 
     Not Timed: Both players play at their own rate, and each ends when they want. 
     Timed: A timer is set to any time (a timer is provided but with no battery), and when the time expires, both players stop. 


Each valid word on a player's rack earns one point per letter cube in it.  The player with the highest total score wins.

In every round of This is OUTchu! players will be presented with a topic card containing two questions. One question will ask for three answers, these are the SAFE responses that can be chosen in the next phase. However the other question will get only one answer that is written on the dangerous OUT card.

Once are answers are written, players line them up in random order in front of them and then take turns to flip over the cards of other players, doing their best to determine which ones are SAFE while actively avoiding any OUT cards.

For example, a topic card may come out with these two questions:

SAFE: List animals you like.
OUT: What kind of animal would you compare yourself to?

Another player may then have the answers: Sloth, Cat, Capibara and Panda written on the cards in front of them. Your challenge is to deduct which answers are in response to the SAFE question and flip those cards over while trying to avoid flipping over the one card that is a response to the OUT question.

You may think you know your friends pretty well but don't be surprised when you confidently flip over their card only to see it has "OUTchu!" written on the back!

boardgame: 愛人に私の財産の半分を遺贈する (Aijinni watashinozaisanno hanbunwo izousuru)
description:
Become a spouse, mistress, or other character and enjoy a cutthroat battle for the inheritance against the other heirs!
You can role-play as a “sly vixen” or a “thieving cat,” or you can go all out to win!
Created by a practicing attorney! Since it complies with Japan’s current Civil Code, you’ll also gain a solid foundation of real-world knowledge about inheritance!

•••

配偶者や愛人などになって他の相続人候補たちと骨肉の遺産争いを楽しもう！
「女狐！」「泥棒猫！」なんてロールプレイしてもよし、ゴリゴリに勝ちにいってもよし！
現役弁護士が制作！日本の現行民法に準拠しているので、本当の遺産相続の基礎知識もつきます！

Subways, crowded with passengers in everyday life, are in fact one of the most financially challenging transportation systems to operate. High construction costs, expensive maintenance, and fluctuating passenger demand all contribute to unstable revenue and persistent losses. As a skilled subway planner, your task is to help the company reduce its losses. Start by cutting construction costs—among multiple possible routes, find the one that best matches passenger demand while being the easiest to build. If you work quickly and efficiently, your salary might even increase.

Metro Planning (地铁规划) is a math-focused racing card game for 2–6 players. Players take on the role of subway line designers, competing to identify the route with the lowest construction difficulty among three different options. In each round, the fastest player to correctly determine the optimal route earns higher points.

—description from the publisher.
publishers: 九张牌玩具公司(9 Cards Toy Company)

description:
あふはあふかは ("Ahuha Ahukaha") is a card game for three players in which players become guardians of the stars and aim to match cards using a batting-style mechanic.

Each round, players select one card from their hand, place it face-down on the table, and reveal them all at once. The order of play is determined by the numbers on the cards, and on your turn, you resolve the card’s effect and perform the “Star Matching” process.
Card effects include searching for specific stars or passing cards to the player next to you. The key to this game lies in using these effects to read each other’s intentions. However, the “Mazukohorikeru” effect—used to pass a card to the player next to you—requires careful timing: on the first use, the card is simply placed face-down between you and your neighbor, and it only reaches their hand the next time the same effect is used.
Matching is determined by comparing attributes such as the size and color of the stars. If you play cards haphazardly, matches will rarely form, leading to disappointment. It’s important to value every encounter in each round.
The 7th round, Tanabata, is the final round and introduces special rules. The 5th round is the point where you make your final strategic decision, and in rounds 5, 6, and 7, you’ll assess whether your strategy has been successful. A dramatic comeback is possible in the 7th round, so you can’t let your guard down until the very end.

One series consists of 7 rounds in which you use all 21 Star Cards. In the cooperative game, the three players are evaluated based on their combined score for that single series. In the competitive game, the first player to win two series is the winner.
You can enjoy the fast-paced feel of playing just one series in the cooperative game, as well as the tension of trying to outwit each other while gauging everyone’s intentions in the competitive game. Alternatively, if you don’t achieve your target score in the cooperative game, you can keep trying until all three players are satisfied.

Another fun aspect of the game is imagining the stories behind the stars based on the waka poems written on each star card!

Since ancient times, conflicts between people have often stemmed from misunderstandings. Fortunately, the Attitude Terminal helps resolve this! This device records the thought process in your mind and generates a unique mental model just for you. Not only does it help you better understand yourself, but it also matches you with others who share similar viewpoints.

Attitude Terminal (态度终端) is a social party game for 3–6 players centered around a betting mechanism. Each round, the current player silently answers three different questions from a card in a chosen domain, while other players try to guess their answers. The goal is to gain points by correctly predicting others’ responses and to deepen mutual understanding among players.


     The current player rolls a die to select a question on the card (red side: everyday questions; blue side: sharper questions).
     Cards are arranged into a 3-layer pyramid. The player answers the top card, then continues down the left or right branch depending on their choice.
     Other players secretly place bets on which of four zones the answer will land in using dice.
     After answering, the current player explains their reasoning. Players reveal their bets and score points: Bets on zones 1 or 4 win a large block, zones 2 or 3 win a small block (Small block = 2 points, Large block = 5 points).
     Turns rotate clockwise; rounds continue until each player has had the designated number of turns (3–4 players: 3 turns each; 5–6 players: 2 turns each).
     The player with the highest score at the end wins.
publishers: 九张牌玩具公司(9 Cards Toy Company)

description:
In the harsh jungle, only the cleverest predators survive. Strength alone won't make you dominant—memory and strategic thinking are just as important. Anticipate your enemies, exploit their weaknesses, and avoid falling to the bottom of the food chain. One misstep, and you may become prey in this unforgiving ecosystem.

Beasts in Mist (斗兽迷踪) is a 2-player board game centered on memory and tactical placement. Players take turns performing one of four possible actions to manipulate the board, hide information, or challenge their opponent’s recall. Points are earned by successfully tracking or capturing animals, with the first to 3 points winning—but mistakes can result in immediate defeat.


     Placement: Draw a card and place it in a 3×3 grid, flipping adjacent animals face down.
     Activation: Flip a face-up animal to trigger its movement effect.
     Tracking: Declare a hidden animal to test your opponent’s memory; points or victory may result.
     Predation: Attempt to 'eat' another animal according to the food chain; success earns points, failure may lose the game.
     Food Chain: Mouse → Elephant → Lion → Tiger → Leopard → Wolf → Dog → Cat → Mouse.
publishers: 九张牌玩具公司(9 Cards Toy Company)

description:
"A wallet-sized roguelike of stamina, souls, and cycles."

Lore

In a desolate world of fading light, you must undergo the Rules of the Rite. As a Cursed One, you descend into the Chasm to battle hollowed sentries and scavenge souls from the ash. Every death is merely the start of a new cycle; you must burn your gathered souls at the bonfire to transcend your failing flesh and eventually face the Boss hidden within the fog.

Game Details

Number of Players: 1 (Solo Roguelike)
    Genre: Dark Fantasy / Micro-RPG
    Play Time: Quick sessions / Infinite cycles
    Main Mechanics: Dice-based Combat, Resource Management (HP/Stamina/Souls), and Permanent Character Progression

Core Mechanics

The Chasm Table: Use a 1d6 to determine your encounter, ranging from deadly Ambushes and Sentry battles to finding restorative Relics and Bonfires.
    Stamina-Based Combat: Manage your limited Stamina (ST) to perform actions: Strike to deal damage, Dodge to evade certain death, or Recover to regain energy while leaving yourself completely vulnerable to enemy hits.
    Soul Ascension: Defeat enemies to claim their souls. Spend 5 Souls at a Bonfire to permanently increase your Maximum HP or Maximum Stamina.
    Death and Cycles: At 0 HP, you "Wither." You lose all current Souls and must restart the cycle from the beginning, carrying only your permanent upgrades forward.
    The Final Trial: Navigate the fog to face the Boss, a formidable foe with 15 HP and 4 Damage, requiring perfect tactical execution to defeat.

Requirements

Dice: One six-sided die.
    The Card: A single printed "wallet-sized" game card (cut and folded as instructed).
    Tracking: A way to track Vitality (10 HP), Stamina (3 ST), and Souls (0 SO) along the card's margin.

The Go Words deck has two colored suits, pink and dark gray, and each suit has 27 cards, one for each letter of the alphabet, and a blank (wild) card. Each card has a point value between 1 and 10 depending on the letter's frequency, the blank being worth 0. Each card also has a line drawing, typically of something starting with its letter (e.g. Apple, Balloon, Carrot, Dog, etc.)

Go Words is not a single game, but a set that can be used for many games.  The deck does not come with any rules, to encourage the user to make up their own games. The game's product page (peak-ed.com/product-page/go-words) says "You can play so many games, including Word Hold-Em, Word Scramble, Cross Words, Word Spit, adult drinking games, and many many more! ... and if all else fails these can be used as flashcards for little ones." And the rules page (peak-ed.com/go-words-rules) does have a few videos showing possible games to play with the set:


     Go Fish with Letters.  The play in the video is actually a rummy game, with players drawing and discarding until they can spell a word of 3-5 letters.  Go Fish could also be played with this set, of course.
     Crosswords. Two players each start with one color of cards, and on their turn they play cards to build and extend a Scrabble-like crossword. 
     Wordfinder. A moderate number of letters are formed into a grid, and players write down all the words they can see, similar to Boggle.
Best Friends Forever is a VORA micro-expansion that introduces the first cube with a dual mechanic. Featuring two dogs, each side of the cube generates different dynamics and scores differently.

Subtitled "The New Cross Word Card Game", L-E-X-ington is another game modeled after Lexicon and Kan-U-Go, that appeared the late 1930s.

Its deck has 50 cards including 2 Jokers, the rest having a letter of the alphabet and a value between 2 and 10.  The players are dealt 7 cards (if 6+ players use 2 decks). The first player places a word of 2-4 letters on the table, and each player in turn adds a word to the growing crossword. If unable to make a word, the player takes the top card of the draw deck or discard pile, and passes.  When one player plays empties their hand, each other player adds the total points of cards in their hand to their total.  Once a person's score is 100 or higher, the player with the lowest score wins the game.

boardgame: No risc, no fun: win, lose or pass
description:
Who generates the most money after 10 rounds?

Each player receives coins worth 30 at the start.
- The previously designated card dealer pays 3 coins into the pot that is in the middle.
Procedure: - Players look at their cards face down and can then decide whether they want to play this round or pass, i.e.,
drop out for this round. If they wish to play, they can exchange up to three cards from the draw pile in turn. The dealer handles
the exchange of cards from the draw pile. Play always proceeds clockwise.
 The player to the left of the dealer comes out and plays 3 of his 9 hand cards as the 1st trick.
He can choose what to play, either regular cards or trump cards. The total value of the cards counts, and possibly the number
of trump cards he plays. Following suit is not required.
The player with the highest total value or the most trumps in the trick wins. In case of a tie, the total value always decides.
If this is also equal, the players draw a tie card from the draw pile until a decision is reached. The player with the highest
tie card then wins the trick.

—description from the designer

boardgame: Hidden Games Tatort: In den Fängen des Bösen
description:
English description (via deepl):
A new and very complex special case for Inspector Hahnke.

A brutal kidnapping has the entire nation in a state of fear and panic. When the kidnapper goes so far as to mock the police during a radio broadcast, one thing is clear: this is a case for Inspector Hahnke. A weeks-long manhunt for the perpetrator begins, and you’ll be there live.

Embark on a thrilling quest for the truth.

A mystery game or Advent calendar in 24 episodes
Investigate daily or all at once
A dangerous kidnapping and numerous tasks await you
Analyze a variety of evidence with Inspector Hahnke
Long playtime of approx. 4–5 hours
Can you catch Mr. X?

—description from the publisher (translated)

Ein neuer und sehr umfangreicher Spezialfall für Kommissar Hahnke.

Eine brutale Entführung versetzt die gesamte Nation in Angst und Schrecken. Als der Entführer während einer Radiosendung auch noch die Polizei verhöhnt, ist klar: Das ist ein Fall für Kommissar Hahnke. Eine wochenlange Jagd nach dem Täter beginnt und ihr seid live dabei.

Begebt euch auf eine spannende Jagd nach der Wahrheit.

Ein Krimispiel oder Adventskalender in 24 Episoden
Ermittelt täglich oder am Stück
Euch erwarten eine gefährliche Entführung und zahlreiche Aufgaben
Analysiert mit Kommissar Hahnke eine Vielzahl von Beweismitteln
Lange Spieldauer von ca. 4-5 Stunden
Könnt ihr Mr. X schnappen?

—description from the publisher

A party game where you answer “Yes” or ‘No’ to a question and guess the total number of “Yes” answers from all players!
If your guess is equal to or less than the actual number, you score points! If you guess exactly right, you get bonus points! If you guess more than the actual number, you “bust” and don’t score any points!
The game ends after 6 rounds! The player with the most points at that point wins!

boardgame: アル×ナイドッチ？ ラブ＆ハード(Arunai docchi?: LOVE&HARD)
description:
An expansion pack to enhance your enjoyment of “Al × Naidocchi?”, which includes a total of 100 questions on romance and risqué topics!
This expansion features a unique “Secret Mode”: since the designs on the back of the YES/NO cards are identical, you can collect both the cards you played and the ones you didn’t after answering, so no one will know who played which card!
You can feel free to answer honestly, even to difficult questions like, “Actually, there’s someone here I’m interested in.”
This game gets even more exciting when played over drinks!

    You cannot play without the standard edition.

boardgame: 失われた記憶と私と巡り廻る夜の歌(Escalogue: Ushinawareta Kioku)
description:
It was one night, in a certain place, in a certain world.
In a quiet corner of the town, a girl named Lila woke up.
But she had no idea why she was there, or who she was.
That’s right. Lila had lost her memory.

All she had with her was a single, old trunk.

And inside it were seven strange, plate-like items.

Eventually, Lila realized that messages from “someone” were appearing on those items.

The messages were all meant to help Lila, who felt lonely and vulnerable all by herself.

You’ve probably figured it out by now, haven’t you?

That “someone” sending the messages is you.

However, the time you and Rila have to communicate is very limited.

And when the time limit is up, the story returns to the “beginning”—the moment Rila wakes up on the street corner.
Who is Rila? What secrets are hidden in this town?
Please search for the answers in this story that unfolds over the course of a single night.

boardgame: シャーロック・ホームズの追悼 (Escalogue: Sherlock Holmes)
description:
April 1902. Watson, who had established a practice on Queen Anne Street, visited Holmes at Baker Street.
At the time, Holmes was engrossed in experiments using a state-of-the-art typewriter that had reportedly been released in the United States the previous year. The typewriter was electric, and a power cable ran up to a light socket in the ceiling.
Eventually, while conducting his experiments, Holmes began talking about a strange newspaper advertisement.

The advertisement, published in The Times, consisted of “coded text,” and the name of the person placing it was Dr. Kyle Robinson, a renowned medical doctor.
From that day on, Dr. Robinson’s newspaper advertisements continued to appear daily, each with a different “code.”
While working as a doctor, Watson tried to decipher the codes published in the newspaper, but he simply could not do it.

Then, seven days later—when eight of these “coded newspaper advertisements” had accumulated—Watson discovered a startling entry in the classifieds section of the same newspaper.

Today marked exactly ten days since Dr. Robinson’s death, and a memorial service (a ceremony to commemorate the deceased) was to be held. In other words, the newspaper advertisements in question had been placed by the deceased.
Piqued by curiosity, Watson headed back to Baker Street, but Holmes was not there.
Instead, a handwritten note from Holmes had been left on the table.

This is a draft-and-civilization-rise-and-fall card game where players control seven nations and vie for supremacy across five eras.

The goal of the game is to win as many of the 10 naval and land battles laid out in the center as possible to collect points from the supremacy cards!

So how do you compete for them?
You’ll form teams using the game’s main feature—civilization cards—and battle it out!
Players select cards from their hand using a booster draft-style system to build a collection of 7 civilizations.
