Once the draft is over, the battle begins! Well, all you do is place one card face-down for each era and then reveal them (except for the first era, where you place three face-down).

Each civilization has naval and land power, and the player with the highest total value across their three cards claims naval and land supremacy for that era. However...
Civilizations have two ways to boost their power!


     Card Effects: Civilizations come with various abilities, and some can dramatically boost your land power by meeting certain conditions or matching icons.
      Regional Bonuses: Since this is a war, locals naturally have the advantage. Every time a battlefield matches one of the five regional tags—“Nordic,” “Europe,” “Mediterranean,” “Middle East,” or “Asia”—your combat strength increases further. Some civilizations even span three regions as global empires...!


Think strategically about combining abilities, focus your power on either sea or land, and seize supremacy!

Rules for a historical tabletop wargame set exclusively during the American Civil War, designed for 28 mm miniatures (but also adaptable to other scales). The army profiles for the Union and Confederate forces play differently. The gameplay is shaped by the individual characteristics of generals, officers, and units. The book includes rules, profiles, an index, scenarios, and a quick reference. The rulebook is professionally laid out and includes images, illustrations, and graphics.

The Epic Challenge enriches the Core set’s gameplay in components and game mechanics. As new iconic heroes, Seoni, the sorcerer, and Fumbus, the alchemist, together with their new spell, feat, and item cards, introduce new mechanics and new possible strategies for the players. Moreover, five new monsters (Black Dragon, Goblin Pyro, Gargoyle, Nightmare, Pharoah Mummy) increase the variety of the game and add new monsters’ special abilities. Furthermore, the Trap tiles introduce an entirely new game mechanic. More accurately, they substitute some Arena tiles and teach a new way to score points. As soon as you reach them, firstly, you roll the summoning die to check if the trap is active. Then you draw a trap card. Such cards are divided into four decks, reflecting the four rounds of the game. They pose you a challenge based on your ability modifiers. If you succeed, you score glory points; otherwise, you get misfortune points.

boardgame: Pathfinder Arena: Monsters of the Arena
description: The Core Monster Miniature Box features high detail miniatures of the most iconic monsters of Pathfinder. The creatures summoned in the Arena never leave their spot, but the labyrinth shape is dynamic, and players can change it by moving the board’s tiles. Players can then use monsters to attack other heroes, leaving only one winner. Since this box includes 14 unique monster plastic figures, it is intended to catch Pathfinder Arena’s players and the Pathfinder roleplay games community, and, more in general, every miniature collector.

Astatos is a team-based card game for 2-8 people where players act as crown mages for either the divine god, Makari-hari or the devil Mukaki.  The goal of Makari-hari's team is to activate 5 seal cards and seal away Mukaki while Mukaki's team tries to corrupt the opposing team onto their side.  The teams try to achieve their asymmetrical goals over a series of trials where energy is used to recruit heroes with unique special abilities.

Players draw energy cards during each trial which act as player health and currency to activate seals and recruit heroes.  The players and hero cards on each team swap frequently due to different card effects, so having too much energy may lead to teams fighting over players more than heroes.

The complete edition includes expansions 1-4.

The Academy is just two steps away... until the world shifts.

In WizoCat, you are an apprentice mage facing the ultimate final exam. To graduate, you must cast 6 spells and return to the Academy. But beware: in this chaotic realm, the ground itself can deform at any moment, thwarting even the best-laid plans.

WizoCat is a dynamic spatial puzzle disguised as a competitive race. Featuring a modular board of shifting islands, the game creates a permanent illusion of proximity: your goal is always within sight, but every rotation and slide of the tiles redefines the geometry of the possible.


     Hack the World: Use your magic to deform the map, try to control the instability of the magical realm. A clever shift can open a shortcut for you or leave your rivals stranded on a drifting island.
     Cast & Craft: Gather resources across floating islands to power your magic and create powerful scrolls.
     Tactical Race: Manage your movements and resources to outsmart your rivals in a race against time and chaos. It’s not just about speed; it's about solving the shifting puzzle better than the others.
     Easy to Learn, Hard to Master: Simple rules let you start playing in minutes, but the shifting environment demands constant adaptability and tactical mastery.
     High Interaction, Low Frustration: Outsmart your opponents by rewriting the map. The competition is fierce, but the victory belongs to the most agile mind.
     Language Dependency: No necessary in-game text.


Will you be the first accomplished mage, or will you remain a mere apprentice?

—description from the designer

Auspice is an expansion for Brute Fort released as a stretch goal for the original game's Kickstarter campaign. It consists of 5 new fort cards and introduces a new WILD resolve type. This expansion also released with new deck-building guidelines and an optional set of rules for "unlocking" expansion cards.

A postcard sized wargame set in the Italian campaign of world war one.

In 1915 Italy entered the first world war to fight Austria- Hungry over control of Dalmatia. They thought that it would be easy to push to Trieste. They learned quickly what every other country was learning,  that war in the 20th century was slow and horrible. 
To Trieste! is set in one of those early battles where Italy sent waves of men against Austrian fortifications.

Can you break their defenses as Italy? 
Or hold the line as Austria?

—description from the publisher

A Postcard sized wargame for 2 people.

Hannibal spent a dozen years menacing Rome, now he’s been recalled by the hated carthage oligarchs. 
Now, in the north african desert, he has to face his equal.
Scipio has followed him across the sea, and is heading to a faceoff in the desert.

—description from the publisher
family: Players: Wargames with Rules Supporting Only Two Players

description:
A post card sized wargame for 2 people.

The Australians are on their heels. 
The Japanese have moved fast though the islands of Southeast Asia, and have now landed on New Guinea.  
If they get to the other side, only the Torres strait separates them from Queensland. 
The local Aussie units are underpowered, lacking artillery and machine guns. 
The general's thought that no one could take them through the narrow jungle tracks, but the Japanese proved them wrong. 
Now, the Aussies are put in the worst position an army can be in, retreat.

If they can safely fall back, they may just hold them back.

—description from the publisher

A Postcard sized wargame for 2 people.

In the near future, Earth demands Martian obedience. 
After Martians rebelled against indentured servitude, their techno-anarchist revolution stopped shipments to earth orbit. But the Martians have to move fast. The window for Hohmann transfers is open, so Earth can afford to send the big ships to Mars. 
The Martians have retrofitted their small transport ships, called proas, for war. These small ships have to stop the Earth capital ship from reaching Mars, or it will start orbital bombardment.

—description from the publisher

Echonautz is an immersive strategy game set in an evolving universe where reality ends and discovery begins. Players take on the roles of elite explorers and strategists tasked with navigating a world filled with shifting challenges, hidden secrets, and unexpected twists. The theme centers on the "Echo"—a ripple effect where every decision made by a player resonates through the environment, altering the course of the journey and ensuring that no two game sessions are ever the same.

The gameplay revolves around a dynamic blend of resource management, tactical movement, and narrative-driven exploration. Players must choose their paths carefully, balancing the risk of diving into the unknown against the necessity of securing vital information and assets. As the game unfolds, the board and the challenges within it adapt based on player actions, forcing participants to constantly re-evaluate their strategies and cooperate or compete to master the ever-changing landscape.

To win Echonautz, players must successfully navigate the "Core Mysteries" of the session while outmaneuvering the unpredictable threats of the Echo. Victory is achieved by the first player (or team) to complete their specific objectives and secure their legacy within the universe before the environment reaches its critical threshold. It is a race against both time and the choices of your rivals, where the most adaptable mind ultimately claims the throne of the hero.

—description from the designer

boardgame: Call Triple A: A Gaslands Scenario
description:
A scenario for Gaslands Refuelled set in a Wintry Wonderland featuring the most dreaded of all winter predators: the Slam-Boni!

—description from the publisher

Dice Clash Arenas is an expansion for Dice Clash that adds exciting new content and fresh new mechanics!

Arenas introduce a fun tactical wrinkle and alternate win condition to this dice placement battler.  There are 6 total Arenas (1 on each side of the 3 cards).  At the start of each round, one is revealed and the Bonus Die indicated is placed in the center spot. Players' pawns start at the ends.

Advancing: Each Arena has a Challenge Number on it (die icon).  After placing a die, if its face value meets the Challenge Number, you advance your pawn one space forward. If the space you move into has a modifier icon, you perform it immediately.  These typically affect dice in your pool.   If the opponent is in the space you're moving into, push them back.  If this pushes them off the edge, you win!

Bonus Dice: The first player to get to the center space gains the Bonus Die there for the remainder of the game, and replaces one of the dice in their pool with it.  There are 3 types of Bonus Dice:

Green - Defense Die: This die always parries attacks of 6 or higher, regardless of its defense value.
Yellow- Focus Die:  This die can block attacks of equal value.  You may choose whether it is affected by modifiers, powers, or dice effects.
Red - Special Die:. Adds a 2nd Special Die to your die pool, allowing you to activate your special a 2nd time in a round.

Kuaket and Jane VIII from Super Snipers have joined the battle!

Kuaket has a unique Special Die power.  Instead of an immediate effect, all dice placed adjacent to her Special Die gain a modifier.  On her Basic Side, they gain +1 Attack.  On her Adrenaline Side, they gain a +/- 1 face value modifier.

When Jane VIII plays her Special Die on her Basic side, she places a marker on a space on the opponent's Battle Grid, removing its modifier for the remainder of the round.  When on her Adrenaline side, she is able to stack a die on top of her Special Die and choose a modifier for it.  This not only gives flexibility, it keeps other spaces open.

Puteri is the new Automa Opponent.  Based on a princess from Malaysian myth, she is going to give you a challenge!  On her Basic side, she will change all of your black dice to whatever number her Special is.  On the Adrenaline side, she will remove all the black dice from your pool matching her Special.

—description from the publisher

Mint-Tin Monster Mash-Up: Arenas adds a ton of new content to Mint-Tin Monster Mash-Up!

12 new Cryptid Monsters: New Monsters with new powers!

2 new Mad Scientists: Dr. Evolve can re-make her monster mid-battle, while Dr. Time can pull off big swings in the Boost Phase by predicting his opponent's signal.

New Automa:  Dr. Fission is able to resurrect his creatures once per battle!  Good luck!

Updated Dr. Chaos: The most playtesting you'll ever get is after your game releases!  It's become apparent that Dr. Chaos was a little underpowered.  This new version corrects that and is meant to replace your current Dr. Chaos.

10 Arena Cards: These mini cards have fronts and backs like the monsters do and combine to create unique conditions every round!

Arenas:
Each round, the top card of the Arena stack is flipped and added to the right side to create a new Arena combo.  If players meet the "If" condition, they place their Arena Tracker on the Arena.  They can spend it that round to activate the Arena's "then" effect.

—description from the publisher

Sustainable Swisspearl is a four-player game, where the players assume the roles of a Swisspearl production unit management team. Working together, you'll drive innovation in your unit toward a more responsible and competitive future - strengthening resilience to crises, delivering solutions to exceed evolving customer expectations and staying ahead of regulatory demands.

—description from the publisher

In Eden, players become gardeners competing to cultivate the most flourishing garden. By rolling dice and strategically placing floral tiles, you will grow vibrant arrangements of Seeds, Leaves, Vines, and Flowers to score points and create beautiful combinations.

But nature is never predictable. As the garden moves through Spring, Summer, Autumn, and Winter, each season brings new opportunities and challenges. Dice rolls may bless your garden with growth—or allow rival gardeners to influence the fate of your blooms.

Adapt to the seasons, plan your garden wisely, and cultivate the most beautiful plot in paradise. Gardeners with the most points at the end of the 4 seasons wins.

—description from the designer

Card Tenho is a card game that condenses the entire strategic experience of mahjong into a single game. Players draw two cards and decide whether or not to exchange one of them to form a hand, then choose to reveal their hands (or fold) all at once to exchange points... and repeat this process.

The cards are numbered according to the likelihood of forming a hand, and symbols indicate whether a combination is valid or not, making it easy to understand even for those unfamiliar with mahjong. Of course, hands like Yaku-man and Tenhou exist, but the probability of them appearing is low. However, as the game progresses and the card pool shrinks, that probability increases.

In the new edition, the hand combination symbols—which were not fully accurate in the previous version—have been aligned as closely as possible with those of traditional Mahjong. Additionally, the inclusion of two “Lucky Draw” cards increases the likelihood of Yaku-man (while also causing competition), and the ‘Tenhou’ card now prohibits the use of specific numbers, adding a touch of romance. “Card Tenhou” is a casual card game themed around Mahjong that combines luck and strategic maneuvering.

Here’s how to play: 1) Pick a card at random from the box. 2) Look at the number written on the top card in the box. 3) The person who picked the card tells a story based on the topic corresponding to that number. It’s okay if the story doesn’t have a punchline. When the story is over, everyone should say, “That was a great story!” This will lighten the mood and get the conversation flowing.

A chaotic guessing game where 200 painfully good puns are hiding in plain sight. Your job? Crack the pun before anyone else! Each card gives you a clue (how many words are in the answer) - then it's a brain race as everyone stares down the card, blurting out guesses, and ugly-laughing as they try to crack the picture first! The first player to yell it right wins the card, and the first to collect 5 cards wins the game!

—description from the publisher

Below the falls is a "sometimes cooperative" game of hidden roles, strategy, deduction and betrayal for 2-4 players.

A powerful friend is missing and you must explore the underground in mob-ruled Mantis Falls to find her. You are told that other allies of the resistance will join you for the search. Together you must get her to safety without compromising your own. But are you all who you claim to be?

By the deal of hidden roles, each game could have only allies, who only win if one of you finds and delivers your missing ally to the safehouse. But there could secretly be an assassin hidden among you whose aim is to find her AND kill one of you.

Below the Falls is an infinitely replayable game of deep tactical card-play, hand-building and coordinated effort. It is a thematic journey in which cooperation and communication is unrestricted and key to success, but all players constantly tangle with reasons to lie and reasons to distrust.

In your search for the missing, you are faced with what else you may lose below the falls.

Below the Falls is a standalone sequel to Mantis Falls.

—description from the publisher

In Azorean Happy Cows, you take on the role of a farmer whose goal is to have the best-kept Azorean farm. Thanks to the efforts of dedicated farmers, these cows have developed superpowers, becoming Happy Cows — but beware: everyone wants one… and they won’t hesitate to do whatever it takes to get them!

Azorean Happy Cows is a take-that game where you must feed your cows while defending against attacks from the fearsome GodzFilas. Whenever needed, activate the superpowers of your Happy Cows and throw poops at your opponents.

Well-fed, clean cows are Happy Cows!

—description from the publisher

Azores — a place where the weather changes at every moment; sun, clouds, and rain dance side by side, creating some of the most beautiful rainbows in the world.

In Azorean Rainbow, your goal is to be the first to collect the 7 differently colored Rainbow Cards in each round. To win Rainbow Cards, you must use your personal cards to gather a Cloud, a Drop, and a Sun on each Rainbow Card — the three elements of a rainbow.

Azorean Rainbow is a real-time, fast-reaction game of matching symbols. Let the sky inspire you… and react at the right moment.

—description from the publisher

In Azorean Settlers, you take on the role of a Captain-donatary, entrusted by the Crown with the noble task of leading settlers to the nine islands of these remote seas.

Across nine expeditions, whether by Royal Charter, Corsair Raid, or Adventurous Impulse, you will strive to establish your presence throughout the archipelago.

Azorean Settlers is a trick-taking, bidding, and area control game. Place the Betting Marker on the Island you wish to settle. Choose the type of settlement, Royal Charter or Corsair Raid. Adjust your bid using Mysteries and use Boats to settle new islands.

Master the tricks, claim the Azores and leave your mark on History.

—description from the publisher

The great armies converge... to have a parade!
In Pageantry, you are a Gnome directing creatures from the armies of six fantastical kingdoms to create the biggest displays in a parade. Draft cards and align neighboring kingdoms to make your displays more impressive than your opponents and get farther ahead in the parade.

GAME OVERVIEW:
The annual pageantry parade is here! As a Gnome you’ll put together squads of creatures to get farthest ahead in the parade.

The game takes place over several rounds (typically 3-4) with each round having 8 turns. On each round you get 2 face up cards (1 to begin each squad) and 8 face down cards as your hand. Each turn you’ll draft a creature from your hand to one of your squads, pass the hand to the player on your left, then update your kingdom scores. Once all cards are placed for the round, you’ll score parade progress based on the amount of competition and where you rank in each kingdom. Then it's time to score your style points!

First player to reach the finish line triggers the end of game scoring, after which whoever is in the front of the parade wins.

—description from the designer

Bored is a card game to use when travelling and is useful with children in order to score points based on surroundings observed or basic general knowledge.

There’s lots of small games that can be as long as you want them to be and scoring you can do wooly if need be. Suitable for 4 upwards and aimed thoroughly at adults interacting with their kids.

Cave people on a mission to explore new regions, sow the land, and restore the population of our old friends - dinosaurs!

Dino Farmers is a solo or cooperative Print-and-Play game where you manage a prehistoric village. Grow rare plants, raise dinosaurs, and send adventurers into the wild to spread these species across new lands. Combine different maps and villages, develop your settlement, and build a thriving prehistoric ecosystem.

Allocate two dice each turn — one for village tasks and one for exploration. Overcome challenges like resource shortages, overpopulation, unexpected events, and even tsunamis. All in a light roll-and-write format suitable for players of all ages.

—description from the designer

Play 18 holes with dice. As in classic golf, the winner is the one who completes the holes with the fewest shots.
The board features 18 holes from some of the world's most famous courses: Merion (USA), Carnoustie (Scotland), Royal Antwerp (B), Royal Liverpool (GB), Sotogrande (E), Oakland Hills (USA), Pebble Beach (USA), Royal Melbourne (AUS), Murfield (Scotland), Olgiata (I), Hirono (JAP), and more.
For each hole, choose the path you want to take. High risk if you want to try for a birdie or eagle, low risk for a par.

A 2-player board game that was manufactured and released exclusively in Greece in the early 1970s as a gift for consumers who purchased the product under the name Klinex bleach.

The game consists of a board, a ball and a special dice. Each game has a predetermined duration of around 15 minutes. The board, which depicts the playing field, consists of straight, dashed and dotted lines in black and yellow on which the ball moves.

At the start of the game, the ball is placed in the center. The movement of the ball is done by using the special dice, each time on the lines on the field with the color of the player whose turn it is. Each player in his turn continues to roll the dice and move the ball until he reaches a point where he cannot pass it to his teammate but must pass it to an opponent, at which point it is time for the other player to play. The game continues in the same way until one of the two players scores. Then a winner is declared and a new game begins.

Vedema is a strategy board game inspired by the olive harvest and olive oil production in Crete, where players take the role of olive growers trying to make the most of a year of abundant yield. By claiming fields, harvesting olives, and managing production, players turn their work into valuable oil and victory points.

Over four rounds, players place workers in personal, public, and cooperative fields, as well as in key action spaces on the main board. They must balance timing, resource management, card play, and special abilities while dealing with different field types, oil qualities, merchant demands, and tactical opportunities.

As fields are completed, players collect oil, wood, pomace, symbols, and other rewards, while also using merchant cards, Vedema cards, objectives, and tools to strengthen their position. The player with the most victory points at the end of the game wins.

In A·NI·MA·LES Sabana, you venture into the savanna to discover and capture animals by correctly forming their names using syllable cards. It’s a fast-paced and highly accessible game where you’ll need to stay alert and react quickly.
In each turn, cards are revealed and you must find a way to complete the name of an animal before anyone else. If you succeed, you keep that animal. It may seem easy… but you’ll need memory, mental agility, and a bit of speed to get ahead of the other players.
Games are quick, dynamic, and full of fun moments. It’s also an ideal game for the classroom and for early readers, as it helps reinforce syllabic awareness and vocabulary in a playful way.
You win if you manage to collect more animals than anyone else, proving you have the sharpest eye… and the fastest mind in the savanna.

—description from the designer

Rumini Rummy is a family card game based on classic rummy-style gameplay, set in the world of the Rumini stories. Players aim to be the first to get rid of all the cards in their hand by forming valid combinations and placing them on the table.

During the game, players collect sets of cards either by matching numbers of different colors or forming sequences of consecutive numbers in the same color. Once a player has placed a valid combination, they may also manipulate existing sets on the table by adding or rearranging cards, creating additional tactical possibilities.

The game includes multiple variants, such as a simplified introductory version, a more interactive “pirate rummy” mode where players can reorganize table cards, and an additional trick-taking style variant. Across all versions, the objective remains to efficiently manage cards and be the first to empty your hand.

Kvízkaland ("Quiz Adventure") is a fast-paced trivia game in which players compete by answering questions from a wide variety of topics. Each round, a player receives a question and attempts to answer correctly, relying on general knowledge and quick thinking to succeed.

Correct answers allow players to advance in the game, typically moving forward toward a shared goal or end condition. The gameplay is simple and accessible, but success depends on a combination of knowledge, speed, and consistency across different categories. The large pool of questions ensures high replayability, as players are unlikely to encounter the same content frequently.

The game can be played individually or in teams, making it suitable for both family settings and social gatherings. The objective is to outperform opponents by answering more questions correctly and reaching the end condition first.

Kukucskáló kuflik ("Peekaboo Kuflik") is a simple observation and creativity game designed for young children. Players use illustrated cards with cut-out shapes and try to find objects in their surroundings that visually complete the missing parts of the images. The game encourages movement, as players are not restricted to the table and may search both indoors and outdoors.

During play, a player rolls a die to determine which type of card to draw, then attempts to match the card’s cut-out area with a suitable object or pattern. Other variants introduce time pressure with a sand timer or allow players to compete for reward tokens based on how quickly they find a match.

The game focuses on visual perception, imagination, and interaction with the environment rather than structured board play. It is particularly suited for children who benefit from active, exploratory gameplay and short play sessions.

Project BORBA is a deep, tactical duel for two players that combines the elegance of chess with a medieval vassal setting. Players command units of warriors on a thematic map featuring mountains, forests, and fortresses.
The game uses point-to-point movement, where every maneuver is critical and there is no room for luck. With different starting positions and strategic terrain, each session offers a unique tactical challenge. The goal is to outsmart your opponent and secure your influence over the border lands.

—description from the designer

THERE IS UNREST IN THE KINGDOMS!

Outwit the rival realms and lead your team to victory in this challenging game where you must win a majority of the tricks to score points. Not sure if you can prevail? Try rolling the fate die for a chance to improve your lot...but beware: war or famine could break out at any moment!

—description from the publisher

Aye Matey! Welcome to BelloLudi Pirates! A skirmish game by BelloLudi. 
Nice that you have taken an interest in wargaming. 
These rules have been developed for people who have no previous experience in wargaming, yet like to play games. 
From page 23 on, we included some extra, or advanced, rules. You will not be needing them at first, but we can imagine that you would like some variations as your experience grows. Start with that page when you have some battles under your belt.

The game is designed as a multiplayer game but will work just as fine with only two players or even solo. The same goes for the number of miniatures involved. It is possible to start with only 13-odd miniatures and a ship per side, but that can easily be expanded. Hopefully, playing the game will encourage you to create your own armies. Similarly, the distances are given in inches for use with 28mm scale soldiers. Feel free to adapt to centimetres should you not have the space or when you use a smaller scale of miniatures.

This ruleset simulates battles in the Golden Age of Piracy between 1650 to roughly 1730. Much valuable cargo was being shipped from all over the world to Europe over vast ocean areas. Pirates often received their training in the service of European navies, but were discarded just as easily as they were hired. Corrupt and ineffective government in European overseas colonies created enough space for pirate communities. Colonial powers at the time constantly fought with pirates and engaged in several notable battles as they tried to stem the Pirate scourge.

At sea, the enemy was whittled down by the concentrated fire of infantry and cannons. Once this was accomplished, the crew could deliver the coup de grâce and capture the enemy vessel and more important, their cargo! Tactics were therefore based on teamwork between the crews and their vessels, on fire and movement. Every unit would have its own specific task and if they executed their tasks in unison, success was often achieved. The aim in this game, is not to annihilate the enemy’s units to the last man, but to inflict enough damage that morale is reduced and that the unit will become shaken or will run away in panic and consequently will be removed from the tabletop. By a clever combination of movement, shooting and dashing boarding actions, this can be achieved. The tactics are based on teamwork. Not only within a group, but also between groups and as such, the whole army.

Your role will be that of a commandeur of a small pirate gang or soldiers representing a government. Should any disagreement arise concerning the rules, we suggest you to roll a dice. The one who rolls the highest number has it right, for now.

So onwards, to glory! And, of course: have fun!

—description from the publisher
language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups

description: This is a special McDonalds Edition for 2-4 players only of the famous Phase 10 game. It contains only 68 cards.

Momojan is a card game that combines elements of Mahjong and Hanafuda and that challenges the two players to collect sets. The more sets you play, the more your hand is revealed — and the more your sets overlap, the more points you'll score if you win.

The opponent can steal your point sticks, however, and whoever holds the most point sticks at game's end wins.

Players take on the role of casino builders, competing to create the most impressive and valuable casino floor. Using a shared set of cards, each turn moves quickly—draw, play, and expand. Cards are placed to form connected areas, and the way those spaces link together can make or break a strategy.

Because all players draw from the same pool, no two games play out the same. Plans can come together quickly or be disrupted just as fast, keeping everyone involved and adjusting on the fly.

By the end of the game, the player who has built the strongest casino—through smart placement, clever connections, and well-timed decisions—wins.

—description from the publisher

Hero of Rome: Usurper is a free Low-Ink PnP, lore-heavy, high-agency political simulator, grand strategy, and historical roguelite set in the treacherous aftermath of the Great Fire of Rome. It blends tight resource management, push-your-luck dice resolution, and tableau-building into a ruthless race against time.

You play as a wealthy Equestrian from the frontiers, one of the feared and powerful Rhenani. Your goal? Use every means possible to secure the access, power and influence that will let you replace Nero with your Iberian Candidate... before his patience runs out.

You will actively manoeuvre through Rome's interconnected network of Client-Patron relationships. Choose your intrigue targets carefully: from lowly clients to the wives and daughters of the most powerful Romans. You will need to spend your vast (but limited) treasury to bribe, intimidate, negotiate, romance, marry, gamble, organise private banquets and even send your targets across the River Styx.

But beware: every client disappearance or failed romance triggers an often bloody chain of revenge from their Patrons. A successful intrigue lets you acquire the access and support of the powerful. A failed one drains your treasury or your priceless network of allies, and irritates the Gods. And every single intrigue moves you 2 months closer to the unavoidable deadline set by your co-conspirator.

---

The free PnP game has few AI generated illustrations - mainly of 3 Gods. The final game will have human-designed illustrations.
The UI, mechanics and lore are original and created by the designer.

—description from the designer

This is a game for 2 people who know each other very well. One player shuffles the cards and takes the first card. He reads the card to the other player who guesses what this player might answer. If the answer is correct, the player gets a point. 
The player who is the first one to have 5 points wins the game.

boardgame: DreamWorks: Drachen zähmen leicht gemacht – Happy Meal Edition
description:
All cards are shuffled and distributed to all players. The youngest player starts and places the top card from his deck face up on the table. The next player puts his top card next to it. This continues, until both cards match. The player who is the first one to shout "Match" gets all cards on the table. 
The game continues until one player has all cards.
family: Movies: How to Train Your Dragon

description:
Who’s that at the window? Is it the hungry monkey, the drumming sloth or the waving wallaby? Be the first to guess your friend’s mystery animal!

A classic and fun game for two players, featuring a unique double-sided game board with flip-around windows.

Mindful of the environment, the packaging is made using 50% recycled materials and printed with vegetable inks on FSC paper. Perfect for ages 4-8.

Features a unique double-sided plastic-free game board with flip-around windows.

boardgame: Arkham Horror: The Card Game – Tommy Muldoon Investigator Deck
description:
No longer a rookie, Tommy Muldoon is here to put his experience as a police officer to good use on the streets of Arkham. Wielding firearms and focus in equal measure, Tommy is ready to protect the innocent and fend off foes.

This Investigator Deck for Arkham Horror: The Card Game comes with a pre-built, ready-to-play Guardian deck, allowing you to jump into a campaign from the moment you open the box. Also included are additional cards for upgrading the deck during a campaign.

—description from the publisher
family: Game: Arkham Horror – The Card Game
Series: Arkham Horror Files (Fantasy Flight Games)

boardgame: Arkham Horror: The Card Game – Carolyn Fern Investigator Deck
description:
As the enigmatic secrets of the Mythos begin to unfold, it takes a keen mind like Carolyn Fern’s to help piece everything together. With her intellect and deep understanding of the human mind, Carolyn can heal horror from her fellow investigators while cracking mysteries of the unknown.

This Investigator Deck for Arkham Horror: The Card Game comes with a pre-built, ready-to-play Seeker deck, allowing you to jump into a campaign from the moment you open the box. Also included are additional cards for upgrading the deck during a campaign.

—description from the publisher
family: Game: Arkham Horror – The Card Game
Series: Arkham Horror Files (Fantasy Flight Games)

boardgame: Arkham Horror: The Card Game – André Patel Investigator Deck
description:
Introducing André Patel, the latest and greatest star of the silver screen! With a smile and a wink, André steals the show with his extra actions and versatility, ensuring that every scene is a blockbuster performance.

This Investigator Deck for Arkham Horror: The Card Game comes with a pre-built, ready-to-play Rogue deck, allowing you to jump into a campaign from the moment you open the box. Also included are additional cards for upgrading the deck during a campaign.

—description from the publisher
family: Game: Arkham Horror – The Card Game
Series: Arkham Horror Files (Fantasy Flight Games)

boardgame: Blitzkrieg in the West 1940: 10 Scenarios for Wargamers – Arras
description:
These 10 wargame scenarios feature the battles around the city of Arras from 21 to 24 May.

Arras was to become one of the most famous actions for the BEF, and particularly for the Royal Tank Regiment, in the Campaign in France and Flanders in 1940. 4th and 7th RTR with their Matilda tanks lead a sortie around the western side of Arras that was initially successful as it slammed into the flank of Rommel's 7th Panzer Division. The attack soon flagged as the Germans reacted swiftly, and a painful withdrawal ensued. After the tanks withdrew there were further desperate attacks to defend the town and its flanks before a final withdrawal on 24 May.

This pack includes scenarios that break the tank attack into its component parts from the initial advance to the withdrawal. It then carries the story past that with two battles on the flank, and a final scenario that covers 150th Brigade's withdrawal. Three of the scenarios are specifically designed so that they can be played solitaire.

These scenarios have detailed maps and organisations for battles and are designed for wargamers who enjoy their history as much as their gaming. All scenarios include the actual map from 1940, accurate forces and real battles. These are highly detailed and well researched games.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Blitzkrieg in the West 1940: 10 Scenarios for Wargamers – Gembloux
description:
These 10 wargame scenarios feature the battles in the Gembloux Gap on the plains of Belgium.

This gap represented the fastest way for the Germans to move through Belgium after crossing the Meuse north of Liege and so the French moved their Cavalry Corps forward to fight a covering force action to buy time to establish a strong defensive position on an alignment through Gembloux.  These troops were the best in the French Army, well trained and well equipped. and with high morale.

Confronting them was the XVI Panzer Korps of 3rd and 4th Panzer Division. These were two of the largest panzer divisions in terms of tank numbers, but their light companies were still equipped with Pz I and Pz II, putting them at a technical disadvantage against the French armour. Their superb training and experience redressed the balance somewhat, and made the actions in the gap a very even contest. This also makes them great for wargaming.

These scenarios cover both the cavalry actions in front of Gembloux and and also the tough infantry fights on the Gembloux Line.

These scenarios have detailed maps and organisations for battles and are designed for wargamers who enjoy their history as much as their gaming. All scenarios include the actual map from 1940, accurate forces and real battles. These are highly detailed and well researched games.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Guiné-Bissau: Luta pela Libertação/Guinea-Bissau – Struggle for Liberation
description:
Guiné-Bissau: Luta pela Libertação is a free two-player game simulating the Guinea-Bissau War of Independence (1963–1974), one of Portugal’s three simultaneous colonial conflicts in Africa. One player controls the Portuguese Government forces attempting to maintain colonial control, while the other controls PAIGC (Partido Africano da Independência da Guiné e Cabo Verde), the revolutionary movement led by Amílcar Cabral seeking independence.

This game uses an adaptation of GMT Games’ COIN (COunterINsurgency) series framework as created for two players. Players familiar with Colonial Twilight or The British Way games will recognize the core mechanics.

Note: This game is a non-commercial fan project created for personal use. It is not affiliated with or endorsed by GMT Games. The COIN system mechanics are used with appreciation for GMT Games and the original designers.

boardgame: Arkham Horror: The Card Game – Marie Lambeau Investigator Deck
description:
When spirits are low, Marie Lambeau strides onto the stage to lift them back up with an enchanting song. Hard times call for harder resolve, and Marie uses the gifts of her family’s dark legacy to turn her pain into literal power.

This Investigator Deck for Arkham Horror: The Card Game comes with a pre-built, ready-to-play Mystic deck, allowing you to jump into a campaign from the moment you open the box. Also included are additional cards for upgrading the deck during a campaign.

—description from the publisher
family: Game: Arkham Horror – The Card Game
Series: Arkham Horror Files (Fantasy Flight Games)

description:
La Bataille de la Marne, 1914 [The Battle of the Marne, 1914] plunges you into one of the decisive campaigns of the First World War — at the exact moment when everything could still change.

Building on the acclaimed “Les Campagnes de la République” series, this game shifts to an operational scale where every decision matters. With one day per turn and a wide battlefield stretching over 250 kilometers, you take command at a level where coordination, timing, and momentum outweigh tactical detail.
Command your forces as structured Combat Forces representing divisions, corps, and armies—but beware: their true composition is hidden. Fog of war is not a gimmick here—it’s the heart of the system. You will rarely know your opponent’s real strength until it’s too late… unless your cavalry manages to uncover it.
Every activation is a dilemma. With a limited pool of action markers, you must choose: strike hard in one sector or maintain flexibility across the front. Initiative shifts dynamically, recreating the historical turning point as the French gradually regain control of the battle.

Combat is fast and decisive, driven by combined arms and a powerful fatigue system. Your troops don’t just take losses—they wear down. Push them too far, and exhausted formations will falter at the critical moment.

Logistics are not an afterthought—they are the backbone of your strategy. Maintain your lines of communication, protect your supply routes, and use engineers to disrupt your enemy. A well-timed disruption can decide the campaign.

Historical events add tension and unpredictability: intelligence breakthroughs, command failures, logistical setbacks—and even the legendary Taxis of the Marne. Each can tip the balance… if you seize the opportunity.

In just ten turns, you will experience a full campaign arc where every choice counts. This is a tense, fast-paced operational game—easy to learn, rich in decisions, and deeply replayable.

A game of maneuver, uncertainty, and timing—almost like chess… but where the pieces refuse to reveal their true strength.

Key Features
Operational-level command with meaningful strategic choices
Hidden unit structures and realistic fog of war
Dynamic activation system and shifting initiative
Innovative fatigue mechanics affecting combat effectiveness
Fully integrated logistics and engineering actions
Historically grounded random events

Game Information
Complexity: 5/10
Solo suitability: 1/10
Playing time: 2–4 hours
Time scale: 1 turn = 1 day
Unit scale: Division / Brigade / Artillery groupings
Map scale: ~20 km per area

—description from the designer
language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups
family: Players: Wargames with Rules Supporting Only Two Players
Series: Les Campagnes de la République (Vae Victis)

boardgame: Arkham Horror: The Card Game – Miguel de la Cruz Investigator Deck
description:
The woods near Arkham have more than just wildlife lurking about, as Miguel de la Cruz has learned firsthand. Still, even horrific monstrosities can fall prey to a hunter’s traps, and Miguel’s traps are second to none, letting him track and ensnare his enemies with ease.

This Investigator Deck for Arkham Horror: The Card Game comes with a pre-built, ready-to-play Survivor deck, allowing you to jump into a campaign from the moment you open the box. Also included are additional cards for upgrading the deck during a campaign.

—description from the publisher
family: Game: Arkham Horror – The Card Game
Series: Arkham Horror Files (Fantasy Flight Games)

description:
Station Decimus is a competitive push-your-luck card game for 2-6 players. Players send crew members to explore a derelict space station, The Decimus, competing to gather the most valuable loot while avoiding lethal encounters.

Each round, players choose their initiative order — going first for the best choice of loot but facing the greatest danger, or hanging back for safety while others claim the rewards. An encounter card is revealed each turn, stacking the odds of something deadly happening, making every step deeper into the station more dangerous than the last.

Players who push too far face won't just lose a crew member, they'll lose ALL the loot they collected that mission. But play too cautiously and your opponents walk away with everything.

Four crew types add strategic variety: the Minion, the Enforcer, the Scientist and the Tracker. Hidden survival cards, set collection scoring, and the ability to spend your hard earned loot to take actions instead of scoring them, create a game where every decision matters and every mission tells a different story.

Over four missions, players must balance risk, greed, information, and timing to emerge with the most valuable loot.

—description from the publisher

Join the iconic characters from Kjell Aukrust’s beloved Pinchcliffe (Flåklypa) universe in an epic race that spans from the streets of Paris to the majestic Pyramids!

In Flåklypa: Fra Paris til Pyramidene, players take control of famous characters like Solan, Ludvig, and Ellen Busk, each racing to be the first to cross the finish line. This family-friendly racing game combines luck with tactical card play. On your turn, you will roll the dice to advance your vehicle along the winding track, but the road is full of surprises.
Using "Event Cards" (Hendelseskort), players can gain powerful boosts, navigate obstacles, or even play "dirty tricks" to slow down their opponents. Whether you are pushing others off the track or finding a clever shortcut, every move counts in this high-speed adventure.

Amp up the karnage with the Brutality expansion! Brutalities are powerful kards unique to each Kombatant that start in your discard pile.
Features 7 additional Kombatants and their Brutalities and 27 additional kards for the main deck.

—description from the publisher

In a whimsical fantasy world where even pigeons dream of fries, you lead a quirky squad of domesticated pigeons on a daring dungeon expedition.

In Team Rogue-Like (肉鸽小队), players command a team of heroes in a roguelike-inspired adventure, battling ever-changing enemy squads in pursuit of the ultimate prize: fries hidden deep within the dungeon. Each run presents new challenges, enemy combinations, and tactical puzzles, requiring clever planning and adaptability to overcome overwhelming odds.

—description from the publisher

This game is part of the second edition of the 9 Cards series.
publishers: 九张牌玩具公司(9 Cards Toy Company)

description:
Time for a team-up! Partner with an ally in the 2v2 Deadly Alliance Expansion! Pass cards, form unique strategies, and work together to smash the enemy team! Discover unique synergies with 10 new 2v2 Kombatants, all with teamwork KOMBO abilities. Play powerful new Fatalities and Main Deck kards that can both help your ally and beat up the opposing! Each turn, you choose a member of your team to be on the defensive.
-Includes 10 Kombatants, 51 new kards and 2 taunt tokens.

—description from the publisher

Summon 21 classic Mortal Kombat Kameo characters from across the franchise's history! Each card delivers iconic moves, tactical support, and MK nostalgia.
At the start of a fight, choose a Kameo to fight by your side! Kameos have special abilities that can be used once per turn to power up your deck or dish out the hurt!

—description from the publisher

Players are dealt a three-card hand and play cards over successive rounds, with a Roshambo-like mechanism: Poo scares Fingers away, Fingers tear Paper, and Paper wipes Poo away. When the deck is exhausted, the person who has collected the most cards wins.

The animal deck is laid out in a 9x6 grid and each player is given a pattern card. Players then take turns flipping over or swapping face-up cards until someone reconstructs their secret pattern, thus becoming the winner.

Players pick which developer they want to play (Burnit, Sparks, Flameon, or Enfuego) and take turns drawing one card and playing it on the table, forming a grid of connected houses. When a Fire Starter card is drawn, it burns the number of adjacent houses indicated on the card. The goal is to be the owner of the most real estate when the deck is exhausted.

You and a friend are contestants on a reality show! You’ve been stranded on a tiny tropical island, which is connected to six others by rickety bridges that have yet to be located. The first team to reach the designated beacon wins a million bucks! How do you know which beacon to go to? Just look at the sail on the ship currently docked. Better get there quick before a different ship comes in!

—description from the publisher

Town Craft is a card-drafting, deck-building, resource-collecting game in which players gather materials, manage their hands, and build their towns one structure pair at a time. At the beginning of each turn, players collect resources and use action cards to strengthen their plans, create new opportunities, and guide the growth of their settlement.

Each pair of structures you build lays the foundation for a more prosperous town, but success requires more than simply expanding. Careful hand management, wise timing, and thoughtful card play are essential, especially as neighboring towns can influence the resources you gain and the structures you are able to complete.

With every turn, your town has the chance to grow from modest beginnings into a thriving community. Plan carefully, adapt to changing conditions, and build with purpose as you craft the most successful town of all. First player to complete 5 different structures ends the game and the most victory points wins.

—description from the designer

Recreate the feeling of a massive TCG with a single, 30-card box!

Two yokai summoners duel for supremacy over the spirit land. Both players are armed with identical 15-card decks...meaning victory isn't about who has the rarest/strongest cards, but who can plan ahead better.

You can use up to 2 actions on your turn:

Summon & Cast: Bring powerful Yokai to the field or unleash devastating spells.

Evoke & Release: Attach Spirit Souls to boost your Yokai’s power, or "Release" them to trigger game-changing abilities.

Instead of casting a spell directly, you can set it as a secret trap to ambush your opponent mid-attack!

With identical decks, the game becomes a high-stakes psychological battle. If you know exactly what’s in your opponent’s hand, can you outmaneuver them before they do the same to you?

Skip the Salad! is a trick-taking card game for 3-8 players where the goal is inverted — you are trying to LOSE the right tricks, not win them.

Each round features a different penalty vegetable that you must avoid collecting. Players take turns playing cards to win or lose tricks strategically, keeping track of which ingredient is dangerous that round.

Extra topping cards — including Rotten Tomato, can be added to increase chaos and strategic depth. The player with the lowest score at the end wins.

—description from the publisher

In Abraca Who?, players are guests at a haunted house party who have been cursed by a ghost, causing them to lose their sense of identity. To lift the curse, you must navigate the chaos and collect mysterious stones while trying to figure out who you are.

On your turn, you either Investigate by naming your hidden card to trigger powerful abilities or Duel an opponent to deal damage based on your card's power. Since you can only see everyone’s card but your own, you must use deduction and the growing pool of revealed cards to stay alive.

The game ends instantly when a player collects 7 mysterious stones, earned by winning duels, successfully investigating, or being the last survivor of a round.

—description from the publisher

Jusayo is a fast-paced hand management and deduction game where the goal is to avoid playing your own cards by forcing others to play theirs. Players move a meeple across the colour board, and on their turn, they must either play a card matching the current colour, request a card from an opponent ("Jusayo"), or call someone out ("Bet you can’t"). Strategic depth comes from Double Colour Cards, which act as two colours and immediately shift the meeple to a new space, potentially disrupting the next player's plans.

The tension lies in the risk of elimination: if you request a card and the target cannot play, or if you call someone out and they can play, you are knocked out of the round. Conversely, being called out when you have no playable cards also results in elimination. The round continues until only one player remains, who then flips their victory card to the "flower" side. The first person to survive two rounds wins the game.

—description from the publisher

Welcome to The Quest of Dunyazad, where Dunyazad and her sister, Shahrazad, need your help creating new stories each night.

In this fast-paced, cooperative memory game set in the world of the Arabian Nights , players take on the roles of Dunyazad and her friends to find story elements. Their goal is to help Shahrazad craft a new story for the King before the sun sets each day. The game is played over four 'days,' with each round lasting a frantic 90 seconds.

During a round, players draw tiles from five face-down stacks to match five revealed story cards in order from left to right. While Djinn tiles provide wild-card flexibility, Guard and Hypnosis tiles act as traps that stall the team and drain the clock. Memory is key, as players must remember the locations of specific tiles across multiple rounds to succeed.

If the timer runs out with tiles missing, the King's Mood Tokens are flipped to their 'disappointed' side. If all tokens are flipped, the King becomes furious and the players lose the game. If the players manage to finish the fourth day with at least one King’s Mood Token remaining on its 'happy' side, they win the game.

To complement the standard adventure, 12 specialized scenarios (variants) guarantee high-stakes variety, offering modular challenges that adapt to your team's skill level.

—description from the publisher

Take on the role of an ambitious new hire and climb the ranks and seven floors of The Company, a board game combining classic mechanics — movement, resource management, and competition, with meaningful RPG elements.

Each turn, players will move around the board, attempting to win the influence of Staff Members and Floor Bosses alike — through actions such as Romance, Sabotage and Bribe, skills they get to invest in on their ID card as the game progresses, similar to tabletop RPGs.       
               
As players acquire influence and earn promotions, they will gain salaries, purchase items to help in winning over Staff and Bosses, team up against other players by completing Hustles, and unlock new floors — bringing them one step closer to the C.E.O.

—description from the publisher

Pido la Palabra is a party question-based game designed to encourage communication and connection between players through the sharing of personal experiences, opinions, and perspectives.

In each round, players respond to open-ended prompts that invite them to take the floor and express themselves. As the game progresses, it creates a balance between lighthearted fun and introspection, leading to both relaxed and meaningful moments.

The game includes a dynamic where players recognize the participant who engages the most with the experience—whether through openness, creativity, or willingness to share. In this way, a “winner” emerges, defined by their level of participation and the connections they help generate during the game.

Ideal as a filler or icebreaker, it adapts to different groups and settings, promoting fluid, genuine, and engaging interaction.

Yogui Juego is a light party game of movement and body awareness in which players perform and hold different yoga-inspired poses.

Throughout the game, players draw cards that indicate poses to imitate, with varying levels of difficulty. Each card includes guidance to help with execution, allowing both children and adults to participate without prior experience.

As the game progresses, players must correctly hold or perform the required poses. Those who fail to do so are eliminated from the round or do not advance, while successful players continue.

The objective is simple: be the player who completes the most poses correctly (or the last one remaining, depending on the chosen mode) and become the winner.

Ideal as a filler or icebreaker, Yogui combines movement, focus, and fun in an accessible, dynamic experience adaptable to different groups.

In Tacklebox, card prompts shift the nature of reality around you. Keeping each prompt to yourself, any time you draw a card you're tasked with roleplaying its change in circumstances to your fishing partner. Prompts can either be kept a secret for the full game, strategically played to guide roleplaying, or revealed when guessed to earn points. Simply choose your rules, grab your cards, and cast your line into the unknown!

boardgame: Zombicide: Fantasy Quest B61 – The Lost Guardians of Neva
description: The grip of the siege on Wintergrad is tightening. For centuries, the Neva Palace has had the best trained guards in the region, but we haven't heard from them in a long time. We need to organize a caravan to try to find the Neva guardians. Once they are reunited, they will summon their ancient power!

boardgame: Zombicide: Modern Mission WM42 – Mutant Zombies Must Fall
description:
Hard

Radiation hotspots are mutating zombies: now they’re faster, fiercer, and way more dangerous! Create Rad-Clean foam, neutralize the sources, and save the neighborhood before the mutants take over!

How good of a dancer are you? How bad is your road rage? What did your report card look like as a kid?

In Cross the Line, you’ll answer boldly honest, occasionally awkward, and always entertaining questions by secretly choosing your answer on a sliding scale.

Think they know you? Think again. If they can cross your line, they score. The bolder they bet, the more they stand to gain—or lose.

Whether you're calling out your roommate’s pizza preferences or discovering your cousin’s karaoke confidence, Cross the Line is the ultimate game for getting to know each other - and laugh while doing it!

—description from the publisher

4BEE is a family board game played using 4-sided dice, bee-themed game pieces, and a portable game board. Players roll dice and use their BEE, QUEEN and STINGER game pieces to form a 4-piece SQUARE, LINE, or STACK to win the game.

—description from the publisher
Ten New Cards to expand the laughs on your game. And also a blank Card for you create your own rule!

After a period of major internal unrest, the nation is contested by four rival factions, each determined to seize control and be recognized as the sole legitimate government.
You will lead one of these factions and must guide it toward international recognition.

But don’t be fooled: military strength alone won’t be enough. You will need to weave skillful diplomatic relations, build a solid economy, and maintain strong popular support.
Will you be able to assert your Status in the complex global geopolitical arena?

—description from the designer

Awakened in the middle of night by a surprise breakout, they managed to escape amidst all the chaos. But then, we lost sight of each other. The Locusts can’t be far!

A standalone mission for Washington Z. C.! The capital has fallen and one of our Secret Service contacts has notified us that there are two important agents somewhere in the White House. They need to be moved to the presidential bunker for protection as soon as possible.

boardgame: Zombicide: Modern Mission WM27 – The Dead Elite Squad
description:
Modern Mission
R.Sonnesso – T.Von Glehn

16 June 2023
Very Hard
6+ survivors
120 minutes
Material needed: 2nd Edition.
A standalone scenario for Fort Hendrix! A member of the Elite Squad that was in charge of Dr. Linda Kirkland’s security has taken refuge in one of these newly-zombified sniper squad’s barracks. The morning light will give us an advantage to exfiltrate her. To the rescue!

boardgame: Zombicide: Modern Mission WM29 – The Santa HELLpers
description:
Modern Mission
R.Sonnesso – T.Von Glehn

23 December 2022
Hard
6+ survivors
45 minutes
Material needed: 2nd Edition.
You better watch out, better not cry, this is a special Christmas scenario! Living in a zombie apocalypse sure is tough, so this year the survivors will step in as Santa’s little HELLpers.

Please knock twice before entering. 
Pass by the table where a card game is in full swing under gentle lighting, and grab yourself a warm cup of coffee. Then come meet me on the balcony where palm trees sway.
If you haven’t lingered too long on the lower floor, would you join me upstairs? Follow the spiral staircase all the way to the rooftop.
This is a house of cards. It’s time to build it—one story at a time, each floor filled with stories of its own, stacked by your own hands.

Many Stories Mansion is a game that blends ladder climbing with polyomino-style puzzles. Each player receives 15 cards from five different colors—there are no numbers—and the order of cards in hand may not be rearranged.

On your turn, you must play a combination that beats the previous one. There are only two types of combinations: all cards of the same color or all cards of different colors. The cards you play are placed into your personal area, stacking like blocks to gradually form a tall and vibrant house.

The higher you build your mansion, and the more polyomino patterns you complete, the more points you score. The game ends when two players empty their hands, so you’ll need to carefully balance between constructing an efficient house and shedding your cards quickly.

—description from the designer

R.Sonnesso – T.Von Glehn

2 December 2022
Hard
6+ survivors
45 minutes

Things are oddly quiet here, but quiet places mean more supplies for us. But there’s a horde of zombies waiting for us, and we need to be as quiet as possible to get out!

Based on an Mission by Lliane Louet, this is a very different scenario for Zombicide: 2nd Edition. Enjoy!

boardgame: Zombicide: Modern Mission WM18 – Wanda, An Origin Story
description:
Modern Mission
Guillotine Games

6 May 2022
Hard
2+ survivors
