In Dragon Overload, you must outmaneuver your rival to control five unique landscapes. Each territory is tied to a specific dragon suit with power levels from 1 to 9.

With only three slots available per player and landscape, every decision is high-stakes. Each time you deploy a dragon, you are forced to gift a card from the breeding zone to your opponent. While you can use deception by playing a limited number of cards face down, the ultimate challenge is maintaining order. If your dragons' power is so overwhelming that it doubles your opponent's, they will descend into a chaotic "overload", costing you the very land you sought to conquer.

After only 15 minutes, the player who claims the most territories (or all of them), wins.

Dragon Overload is part of Lookout GreenLine, manufactured 100% eco-friendly. '

—description from the publisher

Visit your favorite places in and around Stars Hollow in MONOPOLY: Gilmore Girls edition! Grab your favorite custom token from among the Dragonfly Room Key, Luke’s Baseball Hat, School Newspaper, Picnic Basket, Cup of Coffee, or First Snowflake. Travel around town to buy iconic properties like Kim’s Antiques, Doose’s Market, and the Independence Inn. “Where You Lead” and “I Will Follow” cards will have you reliving some of your favorite moments. It is a celebration of the people and places in Stars Hollow every time you pass GO!

—description from the publisher

Step into the dynamic world of mutants and heroes in MONOPOLY: X-Men 97! Buy , sell, and trade to build your ultimate mutant team with Cable, Wolverine, Jean Gray , Magneto, and Morph. Featuring 6 custom sculpted tokens – Cyclops’ Lense, Gambit’ s Cards, Magneto’ s Helmet, Storm’ s Bolt, Wolverine’ s Mask, and Rogues’ Gloves! Chance and Community Chest (Renamed Human and Prime Sentinels) cards unleash unexpected twists, from gaining new powers to facing off against classic villains.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Gévaudan, sur les traces de la Bête: Le Navigateur
description:
New playable character for the game.
Special ability: Once per game, on your turn, you can travel between squares where the river passes without having to spend an action.
Promo available in Plato magazine 183

Expand your Floe experience with Life Along Floe! Featuring many new Critters to help and ally with, Landmarks to discover, Treasures and Items to empower you and Hidden Passages to brave through. The Iceberg Sea awaits!

—description from the publisher

boardgame: God, Hitler and Gandhi Walk Into a Bar
description:
A fun game to debate ideas, some trivial and others more controversial. Embody characters who can defend ideas that are opposite to yours or attack ideas that you even agree with. Step into someone else’s shoes, argue your heart out, and make God laugh, cry, or applaud!

Goal of the Game:
Be the first team to win five debates by convincing a schyzophrenic God with your arguments, wit, or speed.
Each player will separate chocolates from a sheet to create the best tray. They'll make all sorts of cutouts on the dies and then pass it on to the next player in the round to earn the most points.

Another day, another leader! Save democracy, claim victory, trust nobody!

Your mandatorily beloved Leader is dead. Poisoned, stabbed, shot, pushed out of a window, and run over by a truck, probably because they were so beloved. The politicians are in shambles, the military are plotting, the people are on the verge of revolution, and you, the small council of the Leader's most trusted advisors, must scramble to try and put some order to the chaos and elect a new Leader (preferably the person that most benefits your personal agenda).

Loyalty is a social deduction game where rather than working in hidden teams the players have secret connections to each other, meaning that whilst you might know whose team you want to be on you don’t know what their objective is. Players may be rooting for other players success or attempting to bring about their doom but the objective is to elect a new Leader by a majority vote, so much of the game is the players figuring out who to elect that benefits the largest number of them, or lying about it to try and get the outcome that suits their agenda alone.

The Loyalty deck is made up of “Loyal”, “Betray”, and “Manipulate” cards that link your victory to another players at the table, either trying to help, kill, or trick them. In addition there are a number of “Unique” cards that give players a special victory condition or ability.

There are two ways to win the game, either become elected Leader or fulfil at least one of your Loyalty cards, but neither of these is a guaranteed success. Regardless of other motivations players all know that their best bet (or at least simplest option) for winning the game is being elected the new Leader, however, only one of them can be elected, and that victory is disturbingly easy for another player to tamper with, so most will have to rely on their Loyalty cards.

In short a players Loyalty cards allow the player a victory condition usually tied to another player, either by being Loyal to someone and winning if they are winning, Manipulating a player and stealing victory from them if they are elected Leader, or Betraying someone in the hopes of seeing that player executed. Unique loyalties each have their own effect as listed on the card itself but all players should be familiar with which Unique cards could be in the deck.

The “Winning/Losing” card is used to keep track of whether or not you are winning or losing (surprise!). You should flip it any time this status changes, and it may change a lot.

As previously stated but worth repeating, being elected Leader or being Executed negates your loyalty cards, they can no longer be claimed unless the card says otherwise.

—description from the designer

This is a warm and welcoming village where kind-hearted residents have taken in refugees fleeing disaster. Unbeknownst to them, amidst the growing crowd and unfamiliar faces, two werewolves have slipped in, disguising themselves as humans. As day and night alternate, the werewolves seek to kill and replace the villagers, while the humans must uncover and execute these hidden threats. A deadly struggle between life and death is about to unfold.

The Village of Werewolves is a 2-player “Ameritrash”-style board game centered around a voting mechanism. The two players take on asymmetric roles: the Wolf Leader and the Investigator, each with different actions and victory conditions. The Wolf Leader aims to eliminate enough villagers under the cover of night so that werewolves can take their place within the village. The Investigator must analyze information from the dead and use daytime voting to identify and execute the hidden werewolves among the population.

—description from the publisher

This game is part of the second edition of the 9 Cards series.
publishers: 九张牌玩具公司(9 Cards Toy Company)

description:
May, 1942. Only five minutes earlier, the Kido Butai launched an overwhelming attack on Pearl Harbor. The Japanese sunk four battleships, damaged four, destroyed 188 aircraft, killed 2000 sailors and a hundred Marines. Not to mention the numerous other ships and aircraft damaged.

Since then, the Empire of the Rising Sun has expanded almost without resistance. Now, intelligence intercepts, signal a looming Japanese invasion of Port Morseby.

The only two aircraft carriers available to halt the Japanese invasion fleet are the Lexington and the Yorktown. Will they be enough to stem the Japanese onslaught?

"Battle of the Coral Sea" is tactical board game designed for solitaire or two-person play. Each piece represents a ship, aircraft, or a flight of aircraft.

—description from the back of the box

In the vast ocean lie countless treasures—but also deadly dangers. Vortices scatter the seas, constantly altering ship routes. Luckily, you possess a magical compass inherited from your grandfather, allowing you to control these vortices and guide your ship toward the legendary Atlantis. Command your pirate vessel, navigate shifting sea lanes, and race to claim the greatest treasures. The first to gather them all will become the most feared pirate king of the seas. —description from the publisher.

In The Vortex Line (漩涡航路), players take on the roles of rival pirates navigating a dynamic sea route shaped by rotating tiles. By manipulating vortices and choosing optimal paths, players gather resources, collect treasures, and outmaneuver opponents.


     Players take turns rotating route tiles and moving ships toward Atlantis
     Movement is restricted to forward progression along connected locations
     Manage resources (food, coins, cannons) to enhance movement and actions
     Engage in raids when ships meet on the same route tile
     Collect treasures through exploration and treasure maps
     First player to collect 6 treasures wins


Solo Mode:

     Play against an automa opponent, Blackbeard, with unique behavior rules
     Adjustable difficulty based on Blackbeard’s starting resources


This game is part of the second edition of the 9 Cards series.
publishers: 九张牌玩具公司(9 Cards Toy Company)

description:
June 25, 1950. Korean People's Army (KPA) troops pour across the 38th parallel. Soviet-built tanks lead the assault, supported with heavy weapons and equipment. Well-trained and organized, the KPA moves with a purpose.

The Army of the Republic of Korea (ROK) on the other hand, lacks heavy weapons and tanks. In contrast, it is not well organized. Previously, fearing the ROK would attack North Korea, the US deliberately refused South Korea's request for heavy weapons.

However, as KPA forces easily roll south, Truman is in a bind. There is no US defense policy that includes South Korea, but the invasion could threaten Japan, and Japan is critical to the stability of the free world.

Truman has no choice, but to intervene.

—description from the back of the box

Spend your last day in Guangdong enjoying a delightful dim sum feast with a friend. The restaurant is crowded, and ordering becomes a competition. With a limited budget, try to eat as much as you can of your favorite dishes, and even pack leftovers to take home. The taste of each dish represents a memory of the city—a memory you wish to treasure.

Cantonese Dim Sum (早茶大王) is a 2-player puzzle-style game. The main mechanism is tile covering. Each round, players simultaneously choose actions:

     Serve a dim sum dish by placing it on the table
     Place a 'like' token on a dish they admire


Players aim to maximize points by strategically covering dishes they want while avoiding those they don’t, all within budget constraints.
publishers: 九张牌玩具公司(9 Cards Toy Company)

description:
S&C is a fast-paced party card game for 3–8 players that blends strategy, psychology, and controlled chaos into a single, unpredictable social experience.

Each round, players draw cards that trigger actions — forcing alliances, betrayals, callouts, and bluffs. The mechanics are simple enough to explain in under two minutes, but the social dynamics ensure that no two sessions ever unfold the same way.

S&C rewards players who can read the room as well as the cards in front of them. Form alliances strategically. Break them at the perfect moment. Outplay everyone — or stir just enough chaos to survive.

There is no winning on autopilot. The deck evolves. The table evolves. And the player who adapts fastest wins.

Designed for game nights, parties, and any social setting where bold personalities meet competitive energy.

—description from the publisher

In this game you place colored dominos to create and race across a park and reach the ice cream truck first.

At your turn either :

     pick a dominos in one of the two pile 
     place one with a matching path color then move your character to the next space of the same color of your placed dominos


The ice cream truck is placed on the path when one of the drawn is emptied. The first one to reach it wins.

What is different or new in Volume 2: Masquerade?

In Masquerade: Secrets & Scoundrels, Mr. Peter Applesby returns from Volume 1 as a self-made man! With his fortune comes a desperate need for a woman to hold its reins at his side as a new bride.

Mr. Applesby---a formerly poor stable boy turned royal horse breeder---announces his new wealth in the city by throwing a lavish Masquerade Ball, where he intends on announcing his betrothal... but to whom?

Gather secrets & influence to help you acquire the perfect costume for the ball---for the best-dressed players have a distinct advantage at winning the treasured proposal at midnight when the masks are removed and the ladies' faces are revealed to the bachelor!

How does this work in relation to the base game?
From Volume 1, you will use the Gameboard, Tokens, Shillings, Pawns, Dice and Ball Scenario Number Cards. However, all story elements are replaced by new cards, booklets, and more!

boardgame: Dinosaur Rush: To eat, to be eaten or to feed
description:
Who will generate the most points after card pile is empty.

You will place your cards in three splits on your playmat.
The three dice will give you advice were to place your cards.

A Heroclix Classics Mega Battle Pack, a complete game in every pack, with everything two players need to play.

Iron Man and his allies have joined together to fight the forces of evil, and they want your help!

Character figures include The Ironmonger, Iron Man, Black Widow, Nick Fury, Crimson Dynamo, and a second Iron Man in different armor.

boardgame: HeroClix: Fantastic Four vs. Dr. Doom
description:
A HeroClix Classics Battle Pack.

Help Mr. fantastic and invisible woman defeat, Dr. Doom and his plans to rule the world. But beware the power of the world’s most ruthless, super villain.

boardgame: HeroClix: Fantastic Four vs. Mole Man
description: From his underground empire, Moleman is devising a plan to destroy every major city in the world. You must brave the darkness of the underground and help human torch and thing defeat more man before it’s too late.

Set in a grunge meets cyberpunk world, you are a Crime Boss who controls Faction territories represented by polyominos placed over the city. Over the course of five rounds, each player will buy territory, place lieutenants into strategic positions, and attempt to outwit your opponents. At the end of each round, one of the four city sectors is raided. If unprotected, the territory's value is reduced. Each round your empire expands, and the more of the city you control the more money you collect. Money talks, and in Crime Guys, whoever has the most at the end wins the game.

During the siege of Brest, the coastal batteries on the Crozon peninsula, on the southern shore of the harbor, were hindering the advance of the US Army. 
It was therefore crucial to silence them. 
To achieve this, the barrier closing off the peninsula had to be breached: the Ménez-Hom peak, rising to 330 meters. 
This mission was entrusted to the FFI-FTP battalions of central Finistère. 
It took three weeks, from August 12th to September 1st, 1944, and the reinforcement of a US cavalry brigade to finally defeat the German, Russian, and Caucasian defenders.


     Complexity : 4/10
     Duration : 2 to 3 hours
     Turn = three days
     Zone = 1 to 1,5 km
     Infantry Units = a company of tens to hundreds of men 


—description from the publisher
language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups
family: History: Resistance during WW2 in Europe
Players: Wargames with Rules Supporting Only Two Players

description: The game deck contains 100 cards of crowdfunded products. Among them are exciting solutions, strange experiments and downright amazing ideas. In the game, you will have to guess related to these products.

One grid. Two players and two ways to win:

- Match 4 of your pieces in a row.
- Capture 4 cells of same color on the board.

In Quatrix, every move is a calculated risk. On your turn, you’ll place an X or □ piece, but here’s the twist: your opponent’s last move dictates your next. Will you adapt, outmaneuver, or fall into their trap ?

Play baseball like never before, on your table.

PLAKKS Baseball transforms the classic game into a fast, physical tabletop experience. Pitch, swing, and run the bases using real skill, precision, and timing.

Every play feels alive: strikes, fly balls, outs, and home runs happen in seconds.

Built for quick matches and endless rematches, it’s baseball reimagined for any space, any moment, and any generation.

—description from the publisher

Recipe Rumble is a family friendly, easy to learn and fun to play game for all ages. It's a fast-paced game where everyone trades their cards at the same time. Be the first player to collect all of the ingredients on your recipe card to win.

Recipe Rumble - Fully Loaded expands on the original game by adding 6 additional recipes and their ingredients, for an increased player count and more variety from game to game.


Recipe Rumble est un jeu familial, facile à apprendre et amusant pour tous les âges. C'est un jeu rapide où tous les joueurs échangent leurs cartes simultanément. Soyez le premier à rassembler tous les ingrédients de votre carte recette pour gagner.

Recipe Rumble - Fully Loaded enrichit le jeu original avec 6 recettes supplémentaires et leurs ingrédients, pour un plus grand nombre de joueurs et une variété accrue à chaque partie.


—description from the designer

Naturkabalen is a cooperative family card game where players work together to build and balance five different habitats.

Through simple choices and small challenges, players must carefully decide how to grow nature while overcoming obstacles that can slow progress. Each decision matters, and teamwork is essential to succeed.

Can you make the right choices and restore nature before the deck runs out?

—description from the publisher

Learn about the life cycle of the mighty Brazil Nut Tree as you build your 3D tree while fighting against natural and human hazards to survive and thrive in the Amazon Rainforest!

There are 3 levels of gameplay, with your nemesis, the parasitic strangler fig, becoming more and more of a threat in the intermediate and advanced levels.

This is both a competitive and cooperative game where players aim to be the most thriving tree but also can help their fellow trees to become together a thriving section of the Amazon!

—description from the designer

In This year nobody dies you play as teenagers in an urban fantasy world inspired by classics like E.T, Stranger things, Cobra Kai and Buffy, If you manage to survive for the whole school year you win, but will you be able to face all the strange dangers while making friends in clubs, money in your students Job and facing family drama? The game is based on a huge cooperative adventure book that you will have to read in rythm with your school calendar. It bring out a nervous narrative experience where every choices is capital...

You start with a calendar, a job and a club.

Job let you program work days in the calendar to earn money. Money let you buy stuff.
Club let you program club days to make friends. Friends can help you when needed.

There is a first story entry already written on your calendar (it is the main adventure). Your club and job will each give another story entry (they are your personal stories)

Then you start reading the entries. You make choices. You program them in your calendar for the following weeks.
All the stories move forward in parallel. Of course it is cooperative so you can help your friends on their stories, Coordinate to deal with the main adventure and judge each others choices while you listen to what is happening.
You can explain the rules while playing, start instantly with the stories. Most of the entries are kept short to keep a steady rhythm but of course when the emotional situation requires it, some passages will be longer (and pretty outrageous too)

Throughout the game you will receive stuff in the form of cards. These will represent important allies, tools, weapons, accomplishments and traumas you collect along the way. Stuff. It gives your character a sense of growth. And, In the rogue lite tradition. They will unlock new stories and bonuses for your next games.

New main adventures, new clubs and jobs to start the games with. Better equipment but mostly completely different stories.

—description from the designer

boardgame: Atomic Twisted Tabletop: Mars Attacks Board Game
description:
Atomic Twisted Tabletop: Mars Attacks Board Game is a fast-paced, modular board game where players compete to collect Power Cores and return them to Base Camp while navigating a dynamically built map.

Each turn, players roll dice to move their Hero and strategically control enemy Combatants to block opponents, trigger battles, and create chaos.

The Mars Attacks module introduces Martian units, flying saucer movement boosts, and invasion-themed gameplay layered onto a universal system designed for crossover play with other yet to be released licensed properties.

No two games are the same thanks to tile-based board building, action cards, and unpredictable player interaction.

—description from the designer

Take on the role of Archéonauts, a brotherhood of explorers sent on a mission into an Irkalla: labyrinthine catacombs, remnants of a forgotten past.
Your mission: recover the three Zarkrys, artifacts of immeasurable value.
However, every minute spent in these catacombs exposes you to irradiance.
Find the exit before it slowly transforms you into Nam’tars — the same nightmarish creatures that hunt you.

—description from the publisher

Incarnez des Archéonautes, une confrérie d’explorateurs envoyés en mission dans une Irkalla : des catacombes labyrinthiques, vestiges d’un passé oublié.
Votre mission : retrouver les trois Zarkrys, des artefacts d’une valeur inestimable.
Cependant chaque minute passée dans ces catacombes vous expose à l'irradiance.
Trouvez la sortie avant que celle-ci ne vous transforme lentement en Nam’tars, ces mêmes créatures cauchemardesques qui vous traquent.

—description from the publisher (French)

boardgame: Wrath of the Wyvern: Isle of Skye
description:
The Isle of Skye offers a new region to explore, featuring a unique campaign, new enemies, hex types, and more!
It is the home of the fairy glens and pools, inhabited by the Fae, who are the guardians of the island’s magic.

The Skye Clan gained control of the island´s other clans through a pact with the fairies. Their leader, Leod Skye, was given a magical sword to be returned when he came to power, but he refused. The home of the Fae begins to be consumed by corruption, and they turn violent as the sword maintains the balance of the Stoirme wellspring beneath the island.

It will be up to you to decide the fate of the Isle of Skye: return the sword to the Fae and rid them of the corruption, or succumb to the ambitious plans of the Leod Skye.

Every step deepens your legend. Will you side with mortals, fairies, or neither? Will you restore what has been broken, or claim it all for yourself?

What´s Included
Below is a list of what is included in this expansion:

     A new Hex map: The Isle of Skye.
     A new campaign: The Sword of Skye.
     30+ new Narrative Events and Quests.
     New enemies: Skye Fighters, Corrupted Fae, etc.
     3 new tunes for your clarsach.
     A new ally.
     and more!


A Living Isle
Explore Skye across new hex types: Ghost Villages, Fairy Glens, Cliffs, and Moors. Each terrain offers new actions and event tables.

New Quests & Narrative Events
Test your honor and courage in branching quests and events. Listen to the story of people whose villages have been destroyed and Fae whose fairy glens have been consumed by the corruption of the Stoirme.

Legendary Relics
Uncover Skye’s treasures, including the Fairy Flag of Dunvegan, the rune stones of the Cuillins, and the Sword of Skye.

New Foes
Within Skye lie deadly new foes that will test your combat skills from the Bodach that haunts ruined homes to the skilled swordsmen of Clan Skye. Each enemy shall stand in your way in your mission to return peace to the Isle.

The Fate of Skye
Death and destruction have brought the Isle to its direst state. Its fate must be sealed once and for all. Will the Sword of Skye be returned to the Fae to bring nature back into balance, or will they be slaughtered, so that Leod Skye may reign freely on the island? The choice is yours. The effects of your actions shall have consequences in the Isle forever.

—description from the designer

Beasts & Bones brings new foes, treasure and more to your base game of We're Sinking.

Sailing into uncharted waters while low on crew, what could go wrong? When monsters rise from the dark depths and things get dicey, you'll have to summon a literal skeleton crew to your ranks to try and stay afloat.

The skeleton crew provides new ways to play. Including a solo and 2 player mode. Have 3 or 4 players but want more chaos? Add the Skeli crew to bring your crew up to 5 or 6. But be warned! These skelis have a mind of their own and just like their greedy living crew, they have their own selfish wants.

Beasts & Bones will also have LOTS of new enemies to fight against. Exploring all new mechanics and devastating ways to sink.

—description from the publisher

Curate a biodiverse tableau in just 15 minutes.
From mosses that cling to the forest floor, to towering canopies that sustain fauna in the rainforest, our planet is full of natural wonders that have evolved over millennia. In Earth Express, you’ll cultivate your own vibrant ecosystem in minutes.

Draft cards to build a 3x3 tableau of Flora and Terrain, creating a self-sustaining habitat by triggering the coloured abilities in your tableau. Generate Sprouts, stack Growth, and curate your hand by spending Seeds to create the perfect habitat for fauna; each with a unique scoring objective.

Engaging, strategic, and easy-to-learn, Earth: Express is a standalone game for 1-8 players.


     Simultaneous Play


Players resolve their turns at the same time, watching their tableau grow over the course of 5 rounds.


     Personal & Shared Goals


Fauna objectives reward you for building habitats to accommodate animals such as hummingbirds, leopards, and echidnas.

Optional Ecosystem objectives have scaling rewards according to how well you’ve met their conditions.


     Positive Player Interaction


Certain cards grant benefits for the cards in your neighbours’ tableau, while others generate extra resources for other players when their abilities are triggered.

—description from the publisher

In Behind the Lens, you become a nature photographer documenting our planet. With turns taken simultaneously, you’ll adjust your camera settings using a sliding tile mechanism to capture striking images of flora, fauna, and stunning landscapes.

Every photo expands your portfolio and sharpens your skills. Add tiles to your journal, increasing the victory points you earn when you capture photos with the same settings in the future. Earn additional VP by completing Assignments, public objectives that challenge you to capture photos under specific conditions.

Tactile and incredibly puzzly, Behind the Lens is a standalone game for 2-4 players.


     Multi-Use Cards


The cards you draft are used to charge your camera, gain new tiles, and take bonus actions.


     Slide Puzzle Mechanism


Your camera is represented by a grid of sliding tiles. Manipulate them using battery charges to align the settings and snap a photo.


     Personal & Shared Goals


Compete to satisfy public Assignments earning more VP the earlier you complete the objective.

At the end of the game, earn additional VP from private Awards that scale according to how well you’ve met their conditions.

—description from the publisher

Yandere Girlfriends: Imouto, Osananajimi, Senpai is a two-player lane battler card game. Each player plays as an alternate timeline of Makoto, a poor guy that three obsessive girls have claimed as their own. Each girl controls a zone of his life (Home, School, Town).
 Players take turns playing cards in those zones, trying to win control of at least two out of three. Cards played face-up contribute their Devotion value and fire an ability; cards played face-down are worth a flat 2 and hide your intentions. Zone placement matters: matching zones give full value, adjacent zones halve it, and playing across the board drops it to nothing.

Rounds end when both hands are empty, or someone concedes early to limit the damage. Control two zones, and you score 5 points, but sweep all three, and you lose 3 instead, so try to win just enough without winning too much. The first to 12 points across multiple rounds wins the match.

An Advanced Mode shuffles the zone layout each round, adds bonus cards, and high-cost Devotion 7–8 cards. The core game stays tight: read the board, bluff when it counts, and don't let your opponent outmaneuver you.

—description from the designer

Use your tractor beams to grab alien animals and fill your corrals with abundance or variety. But be careful of pesky space cats wreaking havoc on your plans!

Each turn, you will choose one of your available tractor beam shape to collect a set of wildlife cards from a central grid. Each card collected must be flipped face-down to start a new corral or placed in an existing corral with other animals of the same type.

When the deck runs out, score each corral for abundance (quantity of cards) or variety (quantity of colors). Be careful! Adjacent space cat cards will always come along for the ride and the player with the most cats at the end of the game will lose points.

—description from the publisher

In Tricky Traps, it's easy to score points. The real challenge is to hold onto them!

The game plays over 3 rounds. Each round, you’ll draft 9 cards (3 at a time), then play them out in tricks. Every card has a value, and the highest card wins the trick.

But setting traps is the real game:
The winner takes all the cards won from the trick and adds them to their personal scoring zone, grouped by trap "type". Only your lowest cards score, and consecutive runs cancel each other out. Every trick is a delicate balance of risk VS reward.

You’ll collect plenty of points… just don’t expect to keep them all.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Batman: Gotham City Chronicles – Absolute Batman Expansion
description:
Batman: Gotham City Chronicles is an asymmetric miniatures board game for 2 to 4 players, where Heroes and Villains clash in handcrafted scenarios across Gotham City. One or more players take on the role of the Dark Knight and his allies, while another one controls the Villains.

Key Features of this fourth Season:
Absolute Universe Setting.
Discover new takes on iconic characters and missions set in the critically acclaimed Absolute Batman by writer Scott Snyder and artist Nick Dragotta.
True Vertical Gameplay. Fight across rooftops, streets, and interiors with multi-level maps that add height and depth to every decision.
Multiple Game Modes. Play solo, cooperatively (with an automated Villain system), or in competitive asymmetric skirmishes.
Includes reprints of the base game and previous expansions.
This new season offers the most advanced and immersive Batman: Gotham City Chronicles experience to date.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Company of Heroes: Настольная игра (Russian edition)
description:
Company of Heroes, the World War 2 board wargame, is based on the video game from Relic/Sega. It closely captures the choices, tactics and mission strategies of the videogame. In teams, or as individuals, players will maneuver units and battle for control points to collect manpower, munitions and fuel. Players spend these resources to construct new buildings, purchase units and upgrade abilities, eventually unlocking the powerful end-game units of their nation.

This version of the Company of Heroes base game was specifically designed for the Russian market and contains the factions of Wehrmacht and Soviet.
family: Players: Games with expansions that add solo play
Players: Games with expansions that change player count

description: Zahlen Rock-N-Roll is a dynamic game to help learning multiplication. The game consists of a board with numbers from 1 to 100, 2 D10 dice, one minute sand timer and 20 pawns per player. When it's your turn, you'll have one minute to roll the dice. After every roll you'll multiply the dice values and place your pawn on the applicable spot on the board. If the spot is occupied, remove the pawn there - even your own - and place it back in the owner's reserve. The goal is to be the first player to get rid of all their pawns.

Adds 3 more unique characters to the Amoebunnies base game.

Bruno - Amoebugs have invaded the Rainbow Mists and have acquired a taste for jelly! Help Bruno defeat them and keep the amoebunnies safe.

Jin - A lightning bug friend of his has run out of charge. Aid Jin in gathering energy to infuse into his friend to get him flitting around again.

Melaina - She has split into four copies of herself to form a band. Help her put out posters to attract other bunnies to attend and start the show!

—description from the publisher

Face the raging waves of the Cyclades!
Typhon, ancestor of beasts, and the Nereids, mortal nymphs with enchanting voices, emerge from the ocean depths to conquer the cities. Standing against their fury are mythical champions, including the swift-footed Achilles and the indomitable Heracles.

Face the turbulent waters of the Cyclades!

—description from the publisher

Magyar Szókártyák (Hungarian Word Cards) is a deck of Hungarian alphabet word-building cards produced by Piatnik in the 1940s.  The game is played with the 80 cards featuring the letters of the Hungarian alphabet.

Players: 
The game is suitable for 2 or more players.

Dealing: 
Shuffle the cards thoroughly. Then, deal a set number of cards to each player, commonly 7 cards, but this can be adjusted to make the game longer or shorter.

Gameplay:
The first player starts by laying down a word formed from their hand of cards. The word must be a valid Hungarian word. From then on, players can add letters to the beginning or end of existing words, or they can form entirely new words. The "AZ" card acts as a wild card and can represent any letter the player chooses. At the end of the turn, the player draws cards to fill their hand back to its original size.

Scoring:
The scoring system can be determined by the players. For example: Each letter can be worth 1 point. Longer words can earn bonus points. Using all the cards in your hand at once can earn a significant bonus.

Challenging: 
If a player believes that a word formed by another player is not a valid Hungarian word, they can challenge it. If the challenge is successful, the challenged player must take back their cards.

Winning: 
The game continues until all players have used all their cards, or until no more words can be formed. The player with the highest score at the end of the game wins.

—user summary

"In 19th-century Sicily, wealth is built on sulfur and salt, but a legacy is forged in the salons of the nobility."

In 1840 Age Of Lions'', players take on the roles of ambitious entrepreneurs during the Golden Age of the Florio dynasty. Your goal is to transform a modest commercial operation into a global industrial empire, eventually ascending to the highest ranks of Sicilian aristocracy.

The Gameplay Challenge: The 28-Move Puzzle
Time is your most unforgiving resource. With a strict limit of only 28 actions per game, every decision is a high-stakes calculation. You must perfectly synchronize your economic growth with your social ascent. A massive fortune is useless if you lack the prestige to enter the royal courts, and prestige is impossible to maintain without a thriving industrial engine.

The Economic Engine: From Soil to Sea
Manage a deep, interconnected supply chain based on historical Sicilian industries:

Extract: Mine Sulfur (the "yellow gold"), harvest Grapes, produce Salt, and catch Fresh Tuna.

Transform: Use innovative synergies to increase value. Use sulfur as a catalyst to quadruple grape yields, or combine salt and tuna to produce the legendary Florio canned preserves.

Export: Dominate the Port of Palermo. Load your fleet of steamships—the Vincenzo, Ignazio, and Marsala—to fulfill demands in international markets like London, Marseille, and Barcelona.

The Social Leap: Royal Marriages
Wealth is merely a tool. Invest your profits into Palaces and Luxuries to climb the Prestige Track. Only when your reputation is high enough can you meet the demanding requirements for a Royal Marriage. Securing a noble spouse grants powerful unique dowries and the Aristocracy Points (PA) needed to win the game.

Key Features:
Tight Optimization: A "Heavy Euro" experience where every action matters. No room for waste in the 28-move countdown.

Digitally Balanced: Developed using a "Digital Twin" logic engine to ensure perfect mechanical harmony and multiple viable strategic paths.

Historical Immersion: Experience the grit and glamour of the Belle Époque through stunning period-accurate illustrations and thematic mechanics.

Dynamic Markets: A shifting port economy requires players to adapt their shipping strategy as destinations and cargo slots change organically.

—description from the designer

Cat Tree: Time to Climb! is a light tile-laying strategy game about cats climbing a cat tree. Players use cards to build a communal cat tree, then try to get their cats to the highest perches, moving other players' cats out of the way, and stealing their perches. At the end of the game, only cats in perches score, and the higher the level of the perch, the more points the cats are worth.

GOAT is a bluffing and betting game where players form contracts with demons and wager on the most valuable of 3 suits each round . Each player will have a slightly different set of concealed information, so they must carefully observe the bets of other players and make a calculated risk to come out ahead.

Players start with 4 chips of varying denominations which they may exchange to obtain a contract with one of game's demons, who will provide chips with unique powers in return, though they'll also risk the player's life total should they lose the wager.

Each player will draw a tile from a bag with one of the 3 suits on one side, and a value of 1, 2, or 4 on the other. The players choose one side to keep secret, then all players reveal the reverse side at once, adding another dealer tile that no player knows the reverse of.

One at a time, players place all of their chips face down distributed among the suits as they wish, then reveal them one at a time in the same order, resolving any demon powers that activate. The players who bet the most on the top suit gain a point, and those who bet the least lose a life point. The game ends when anyone scores 3 points or loses all of their 7 life points.

In A Life 2-4 players play through a lifetime. From birth to old age, players choose which experiences their characters will have. In doing so, they collect resources such as knowledge, experience, dreams, and money, which they need to develop experiences. In addition, the experiences determine the inner values of the characters, which determine which resources each player receives victory points for at the end of their life. So it is not the richest, smartest, or most successful who wins, but the one who has lived their life best according to their values.

—description from the publisher

Camuflarte is a party game where players participate in an unusual art contest where cunning is rewarded over artistic technique or beauty.

The rules are simple: each participant chooses an icon, draws it on their whiteboard, and then tries to camouflage it by adding more lines and drawings around it. The main objective is to score the most points by successfully hiding your own icon while simultaneously finding the icons that the other players have hidden on their respective whiteboards.

Only those who master the true art of camouflaging—and observing—will claim victory in this fast-paced and chaotic drawing game.

—description from the designer

Palabria is a game of linguistic ingenuity where you will transform an initial word into many more. Sometimes you will lose letters along the way, other times you will manage to keep them; it will all depend on your creativity. The more ingenious and strategic your words are, the more points you will earn.

The game is played over three rounds. The objective is to find the best words to overcome specific challenges and achieve the highest score after completing the three rounds. It features simultaneous play where players engage in an ingenious race to create words , moving to the final phase of the round without waiting for the rest of the participants.

—description from the designer

​Chronicles of Khyronia is a strategy and adventure board game created by Juan Salvador Bella. Set in a savage continent recently opened to maritime routes, players arrive as adventurers with nothing but determination and a few coins, seeking to forge their legend through sweat and blood.  
​The game focuses on survival, resource management, and the tactical development of the land.

​Key Gameplay Elements
​Objective: Players compete to accumulate the highest number of Victory Points (VP).  
​The Seven Kingdoms: The board features seven distinct regions: Aethoria, Nerath, Veridia, Eldrador, Tharagon, Kyrios, and Elyria.  
​The Path of Effort: To gain riches, players must manually dig in mines, chop wood in deep forests, or quarry stone from steep cliffs.  
​Settlement Growth: Players use their gathered resources (wood, stone, and gems) to transform humble villages into grand Cities.  
​The Dice Tower Mechanics: A unique "Tower Phase" uses a mini-map where dice results are influenced by the player's current location. Landing in your current kingdom grants bonuses like extra Action Points (AP) or Impetus for combat.
  
​Character Classes
​Players choose from four distinct archetypes, each with unique stats and abilities:  
​Warrior: High health and combat prowess.  
​Geologist: Specialist in mining gems and stones.  
​Ranger: Expert in logging and resource substitution.  
​Rogue: Master of trade discounts and resource infiltration. 
 ​Challenges and Threats
​The world of Khyronia is alive and dangerous:
​Combat: Players face off against wolves, orcs, bandits, and legendary enemies using a system of contested rolls (Attack vs. Defense).  
​Missions: Adventurers can accept quests in major cities, ranging from dismantling cults to exploring ancient temples for Legendary Points.  
​Global Events: Every three rounds, global events like "Commercial Booms" or "Bandits on the Roads" change the rules for everyone.

—description from the publisher

El tablero de Crónicas de Khyronia es el centro neurálgico donde se desarrolla la conquista y exploración de los siete reinos. Está diseñado con una estética de mapa de fantasía clásico y cuenta con áreas específicas para organizar todos los componentes del juego.

RESUMEN Y PREPARACIÓN

     Objetivo: Acumular la mayor cantidad de Puntos de Victoria (PV).
     Inicio: Cada jugador elige un personaje, recibe 5 monedas y se coloca en una de las Capitales de los seis reinos periféricos, Veridia con su capital Zamoria queda exenta. Cada jugador toma el peón del color del reino que ha elegido comenzar o haya decidido representar y lo colocará en la Tabla de Puntos de Victoria. Este peón servirá para contabilizar los puntos de victoia que vaya ganando el jugador a lo largo de la partida
     Duración: Se controla con el marcador de soles en la parte superior del tablero:


o2 Jugadores: 15 Rondas.
o3 Jugadores: 12 Rondas.
o4 Jugadores: 10 Rondas.

Además del tablero, los componentes visuales refuerzan las mecánicas:

     Tokens de Recurso: Incluyen imágenes de la cantera (+1 piedra), la mina (+1 gema) y el bosque (+1 madera) para indicar qué se extrae en ese nodo. Una vez colocados en el tablero están a disposición de cualquier jugador, con el límite de que solo pueden estar extrayendo recursos dos (2) entes, es decir, dos personajes, un personaje y un trabajador contratado o dos trabajadores (del mismo o diferentes jugadores)
     Tokens de Asentamiento: Muestran un Pueblo que puede ser mejorado a Ciudad entregando el coste de 4 maderas, 4 piedras y 4 gemas de una sola vez por un jugador, no es acumulativo. Del mismo modo que con el token de recursos, estos también quedan a disposición de todos los jugadores, pudiéndose hacer las mismas acciones que se hacen en las capitales de los reinos (curar y compra-venta de equipos). Una vez que se mejore el pueblo en ciudad, se podrán realizar las mismas acciones que se realizan en Zamoria (curar, compra-venta de equipos, contratar trabajadores, realizar una misión y preguntar por un rumor)
     Tokens de Enemigo: Incluyen la imagen del enemigo con sus valores estadísticos, como el Orco (con 5 de vida y un d8 de ataque/defensa, +0 en ataque, +1 en defensa). Una vez vencidos se le da la vuelta mostrando que se ha tomado la correspondiente recompensa.
     Misiones: Se pueden obtener en Zamoria o en los pueblos mejorados en Ciudades, como la Cueva de la Bestia, que detalla el objetivo, la tirada de dados necesaria (1d8 + habilidad) y las recompensas por éxito. Se colocan en la zona de la ficha de personaje de cada jugador


Este diseño permite que los jugadores identifiquen rápidamente su ubicación y las oportunidades de expansión en el mundo de Khyronia

—description from the publisher (Spanish)

SimblyCity is 2-4 player board game where players navigate to destinations on the simplified map of a city. The game is currently only for the city of Mumbai.

Players are commuters in the city using public transport to get to a common destination. Choose between buses, taxis, rickshaws, trains, metro, monorail and even ferries. Whatever is available at your position, and whatever it takes to get you to the destination before the others.

The game reimagines money & travel fares in a simpler form called Simbles. Players compete to reach a common Destination, getting Simbles from the bank when they win a race or finish runners up.

The player with the most Simbles at the end wins!

—description from the designer

boardgame: The Lord of the Rings: The King's Gambit
description:
Restoration Games and Space Cowboys are partnering to revive a beloved classic board game, The Queen’s Gambit – this time set in the world of The Lord of the Rings.

Like its predecessor, The Lord of the Rings: The King’s Gambit features a sprawling, epic battle across multiple fields of play. The Riders of Rohan clash against hordes of Orcs on Pelennor Fields. The brave Gondorians fend off the forces of Shadow in Minas Tirith. Aragorn, Gimli, and Legolas maneuver their way through Haradrim on the Great Port at Pelargir to join the fight. And Frodo and Sam navigate the perilous journey through Mordor to the fires of Mount Doom.

Game design is led by Rob Daviau, the legendary designer and co-founder of Restoration Games. Packed with detailed figures, thrilling decisions, and countless ways to win, this game delivers the ultimate experience for fans of The Lord of the Rings and board gaming alike.

Based on Disney's animation movie "Hercules" which was released in 1997, this board game was released the same year from Milton Bradley company.

The game can be played by 2 or more players and the youngest player goes first. All players move the same pawn (Hercules) and the winner is the first player to move Hercules to the platform where the Hades pawn is and save Megara. To reach the Hades platform, Hercules must first overcome the following challenges:


     the journey through the arena, where he must defeat the 7 mythical monsters in the arena
     the crossing of the river by passing or fighting the Titans of Hades
     the final showdown with Hades to save Megara
Small Danish quiz game. 60 questions about celebrations, occasions, holidays etc. Answer correctly, get points. Most points win.
Small Danish quiz game. 60 questions about general knowledge. Answer correctly and get a point.
Small Danish quiz game. 60 questions about fun and interesting quotes. Answer correctly and get a point.
Small Danish quiz game. 60 questions about sports. Answer correctly and get a point.
Small Danish quiz game. 60 questions about travel and geography. Answer correctly and get a point.
Small Danish quiz game. 60 questions about cuisine. Answer correctly and get a point.
Small Danish quiz game. 60 questions about history. Answer correctly and get a point.
Small Danish quiz game. 60 questions about Christmas traditions, songs, food etc. Answer correctly and get a point.

boardgame: Nova Lectio: Status – Promo Cards: "Fondazione di Novalandia" and "Alleanza con la Bielorussia"

     "Alleanza con la Bielorussia" can give you wild resources or nothing at all.
     "Fondazione di Novalandia" will reward players with an income token.

Possiboard is a fast-paced dice game where players race to reach the finish line. Before the game begins, players create their own track from start to finish using playing cards. These cards are placed face down, so no one knows what surprises lie ahead.

Each card reveals a different color or action—ranging from simple effects like “move forward five spaces” to unexpected twists that can completely change the course of the game.

While movement is determined by the roll of the dice, players still have meaningful choices to make. Strategic decisions and player interaction play a key role, as participants can influence the game and hinder each other’s progress.

The result is a surprising, interactive, and highly entertaining experience—perfect for playing with friends and family.

—description from the designer

Snarf is the sweetest board game around! Compete for the title of the best sweet shop in town, but beware of the pig, who might surprise you and mess up the game! Play strategically, stock the shops before your opponents, and use special cards to steer the pig’s moves. The first to get rid of all their cards wins!

—description from the publisher

Fun facts about everything Danish

Vikings, pastries and much, much more!

Test your knowledge of all things Danish. Find out how LEGO got its name, the right way to enjoy koldskål and what goes on in Cold Hawaii.

Ideal for anyone with an interest in Danish life and culture – tourists, students, expats, business connections, family and friends.

60 questions in 6 categories

Æ Ø Å WTF? Language

SKÅL OG VELBEKOMME Food & drink

GREAT DANES Famous people

ONCE UPON A TIME History

EARTH, WIND & ISLANDS Geography

SERIOUS BUSINESS Danish companies

Each card has a ‘TELL ME MORE’ section with extra facts and fun bonus info.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Heroes of Barcadia: Blotto's Bazaar Spring Promo Tile
description:
This promotional Heroes of Barcadia Blotto's Spring Bazaar Room Tile is available as a Gift With Purchase when you buy the 2026 Spring Booster Pack. Not available for individual sale. While supplies last.

A player entering the special Blotto's Bazaar room tile may discard 1 Loot card to shuffle up to 3 of their Power-Up cards into the deck and draw the same number of Power-Ups.

1918: Spring Offensive brings the climatic battles of the final year of World War I to your tabletop, putting you in command of a platoon or company of soldiers seeking to break the deadlock on the Western Front. Including the core and advanced rules, the book covers the opening Spring Offensive by the German Army on the Western Front, allowing you to command either the hard-fought British and French defenders, the fearsome German Stormtroopers or the newly arrived, and comparatively fresh, U.S. Forces.

This book features:

     The Core and Advanced Rules for 1918, including rules for infantry, cavalry, artillery and vehicles. 
     Eight varied scenarios.
     Force lists for the British Empire, French Republic, Imperial Germany and the United States


WHAT IS 1918?
1918 is an alternating-activation historical tabletop wargame where players refight the battles of the last year of the First World War using miniatures. A typical game uses around 30-60 miniatures on each side and lasts 2-3 hours. The game focuses on combined arms (i.e. infantry, cavalry, tanks, artillery and aircraft working together) and the command challenges faced by junior officers leading men in the first truly industrial war.

     Alternating activations - 1918 uses alternating unit activation (rather than IGOUGO). This means decisions on when to activate units matter and both players are engaged at all times.
     Realistic suppression - 1918 has a simple but effective pinning and morale system with four statuses (Under Fire, Pinned, Suppressed, Wavering) which allows players to utilise period accurate 'small unit' fire and manoeuvre tactics. 
     Dynamic leadership - The positioning and use of Leaders in 1918 is key. Senior Leaders, your officers and sergeants, issue orders to nearby units, such as Advance, Fire, At the Double or Stand To, and provide re-rolls for Morale and Recovery Tests. Senior Leaders can also trigger special actions that disrupt the normal activation sequence/game state. 
     Tailored playstyles - Before battle begins, players can alter how their forces will fight through selecting a Doctrine (Lightning Assault, Grand Offensive or Tactical Defence). Each Doctrine provides a unique set of advantages, grounded in history, that affect force selection and how a force fights.
     Impactful command - When using the Advanced Rules, players can spend Command Dice to use Gambits off their Dugout Board. Which Gambits are available on the Dugout Board are determined by their choice of Nation and Doctrine. Gambits present players with additional historically-plausible tactical options and decisions that disrupt the normal Core Rules gameplay.  
     Varied, historically plausible forces - The force selection rules give players a huge range of options to build colourful and interesting forces for Germany, Britain, France and the United States on the Western Front, within a well-researched historical framework. The book includes full rules for vehicles and artillery (including off-table artillery batteries) and planned future supplements will include full rules for aircraft, more nations, more fronts and much more. 


—description from the publisher

boardgame: Neopets Battledome Trading Card Game: Illusen Structure Deck
description:
Prepare for Battle with Jhudora’s Bluff & Illusen's Glade Structure Decks, where the magic of your favorite virtual pets comes to life on your tabletop!
These decks provide players an advanced way to play with a pre-constructed deck centered around the iconic faeries, Jhudora & Illusen.
Players will compete against their opponent to complete quests to gain powerful effect and additional actions during their turn with a all foil deck of cards.
Gather your Neopets, challenge your opponents, and let the BATTLE begin!

boardgame: Neopets Battledome Trading Card Game: Jhudora Structure Deck
description:
Prepare for Battle with Jhudora’s Bluff & Illusen's Glade Structure Decks, where the magic of your favorite virtual pets comes to life on your tabletop!

boardgame: Neopets Battledome Trading Card Game: War for the Obelisk
description:
War is upon us! The Neopets Battledome Trading Card Game War for the Obelisk set introduces over 200 new cards.
Bringing new faction cards into the Battledome, players can align their Neopet with 6 warring factions from the War for the Obelisk plot line. These factions are The Sway, The Brute Squad, The Thieves Guild, The Order of the Red Erisim, The Seekers and The Awakened – all vying for the power of coveted and mysterious Obelisk!
Collect the parody album cover art cards featuring these factions. TWO new Paint Brushes- Shadow and Relic, allow players to customize their pets and enhance their abilities.
Petpets return to the Battledome, with a new exclusive Petpet for players to include in their deck. Find these cards and more in booster packs to add to the collection and enhance players deck with the magic and wonder from Neopets.

boardgame: Neopets Battledome Trading Card Game: The Void Within
description:
A dark and mysterious force threatens to unravel Neopia. Embark on a journey of redemption, friendship, and power of unity in the face of darkness in The Void Within. The set features cards from the NEW plot from Neopets and including key Hero, Villains and Faeries important to The Void Within storyline. Embrace the power of Legendary Void characters like the Nyx, Juni and the mysterious Void entities, removing color from Neopia.
This set features new Void Rares, Paint Brushes for players can collect cards and build a deck with powerful Neopets and more! Petpets return to the Battledome, including a new exclusive Petpet that can be redeemed as one of the items with Grundo Warehouse code cards on Neopets.com. All cards feature Custom Art from fan favorite artists such as Anthony Conley, Krista Staggs, Shamine Athena King and many more!
Players will immerse themselves in thrilling fast-paced gameplay of Neopets Battledome Trading Card Game by combining strategy, endless possibilities, and the beloved characters from Neopia. Gather Neopets, challenge opponents, and let the adventure begin! Use new booster packs to add to players collection and enhance gameplay.

Playing the role of pirates out for riches or glory, knock out all the other pirates to win the game, or dock at your Home port with three treasures in your hold. Start the game by drawing ten cards as desired from two decks, one tweaked for combat and the other for exploration. Take three lives and choose your Home port. If you lose all your lives, you're knocked out of the game.

Set sail, rolling dice each turn to determine how far your voyage can take you. Choose your heading carefully to line up for warfare, or to continue your voyage to your destination on your next turn.

Dock at port to draw/filter cards, dredge up sunken treasure with the right mix of luck and timing, or aim your cannons at your foes to take them out of the game and plunder their riches. Beware, however, as your target might well fire back if they survive your assault. Watch out for your opponents' plans as you race Home for victory.

Unleash the Kraken on the board, a threat that each player gets to move at the end of their turn, or turn the tide by becoming the sole controller of the beast. It'll stay in play until it lands on an occupied space, dragging the ship to the depths with it.

—description from the publisher

Eight Elephants is a real-time solitaire (or two-player) matching game. The game is played in 2-10 minutes, depending on the difficulty level chosen. The goal is to match and collect all eight sets of elephants before the timer runs out. Rocks stand in your way, but are easily avoided once revealed. Cobras are dangerous and cranky when disturbed. Monkeys are mischievous in their movements, swapping positions with other cards when revealed, and thwarting your attempts to remember the locations of the elephants.

—description from the designer

Super Senha is a cooperative and competitive game where players try to guess as many words as possible before the hourglass runs out.

Each round, a pair card is drawn, showing which player will be the narrator (giving clues to help their partner guess words) and which will be the receiver (the one guessing). The narrator chooses a difficulty level (Easy, Medium, or Hard) and flips the hourglass. They have a list of words and must give clues to help the receiver guess each one. For each correct guess, both the narrator and receiver earn victory points that vary based on the chosen difficulty level. The card is kept by the narrator at the end of the round. The game ends when all pair cards have been drawn (for 5 or 6 players) or when all pair cards have been drawn twice (for 3 or 4 players).

At the end, players count points from their collected cards (1 point per Easy card, 2 points per Medium card, and 3 points per Hard card). The player with the most victory points wins.

DoUndo is a two-player abstract strategy game where every move reveals,and conceals intention.

Easy to learn. Difficult to master. Impossible to ignore.

Players place, move, and stack cards across three overlapping layers, shaping patterns that can be built, blocked, or redirected. Every action leaves a trace—and every position can be interpreted.

Victory is never immediate. Completing a row is only the beginning—it must survive one additional turn before it can be claimed, giving your opponent a final chance to disrupt it.

Face Off is the game’s defining moment. At any time, a player may risk everything to read their opponent—attempting to identify matching symbols in their hidden hand. Be right, and you win instantly. Be wrong, and the game ends just as quickly.

With minimal rules and complete freedom of movement each turn, DoUndo becomes a continuous contest of positioning, timing, and perception.

There is no randomness to rely on.
No speed to hide behind.

Only decisions—and the mind sitting across from you.

The goal is to carry your money from home to the Safe, located on the other side of the city — but be careful, as all of Duckburg's thieves have their eyes on you! There are several paths to choose from: some are longer and safer, others are shorter but riskier. Along the way, you may gain or lose money as you advance. The goal is to reach the Safe with more money than the other players. Choose the best path and good luck! A game for the whole family to enjoy!

Cachola is a memory game featuring 42 wooden tokens, six colorful boxes, and a colored die. Each token displays an illustration on a colored background, and tokens are paired by matching background colors with related images — such as a mouse and a piece of cheese, or a birthday cake and a gift.

The game offers two modes of play. In the classic mode, all tokens are shuffled and placed face-down on the table. Players take turns flipping two tokens, trying to find a matching pair that shares the same background color and a logical connection between their illustrations. A successful match earns the pair and another turn. In the box mode, one token is hidden under each colored box and players must memorize which figure is where. On their turn, a player rolls the die, names the figure they believe is hidden under the matching colored box, and then reveals it. A correct guess earns the token and a new one is placed underneath for everyone to memorize. In both modes, the game ends when all tokens have been claimed, and the player with the most wins.

Piper’s Call is a play-at-home escape game that tells a dark folk tale about children who vanished after following a tune only they could hear. Players are tasked with rescuing them by speaking with townspeople who last saw them and solving the puzzles left along their trail.

Using the physical game components alongside a companion web app accessible by phone, tablet, or computer, players progress through a series of music-themed puzzles. No prior musical knowledge is required to play.

—description from the publisher
family: Folk Tales & Fairy Tales: The Pied Piper of Hamelin

description:
A game where you have fun singing your heart out!

The goal is to be the first to reach the final space on the board. Players write up to 8 keywords on a notepad. The youngest player starts as the DJ, announcing one of their keywords and starting a 15-second timer.

The first player to sing at least 6 words from any song containing the keyword earns the right to roll the die and advance on the board. If nobody can sing a song, the DJ must prove one exists for that keyword.

Léxis is a card-based word game collection for 2 to 6 players, featuring five distinct game modes designed to challenge literacy and cognitive skills. In Visual Stop, a letter card is drawn and all players race to write down as many words as possible starting with that letter before time runs out. Caça-Palavras has players arranging letter cards on the table and forming words by following a direct sequence without skipping any letters. In Mestre-Palavras, everyone tries to build the most words using all the face-up letters in the center, scoring extra for unique answers no one else found. Pensamento Coletivo flips the script — players write words associated with a central image, but only score when other players made the same connection. Finally, in Doutor Letras, each player holds ten cards and must form and rearrange words of at least four letters on the table, with the first player to use all ten cards winning. Each mode offers a quick, accessible challenge suited for families and word game enthusiasts alike.

A clever game for kids! Each player receives a base with colored buttons. A card is flipped and players must move the buttons on their bases to match the pattern shown on the card. The first to complete the arrangement rings the bell. If the pattern is correct, they win the card and the round. The player with the most cards at the end wins.

Two difficulty levels with different numbers of pieces keep the challenge fresh. It also features multiplayer elimination mode and a solo mode with a timer. Stimulates concentration, observation, coordination, imagination, visual cognition, judgment, and logical reasoning.

Meeple Ataca is a card-based monster battling game where players compete to defeat the most meeple monsters and score the highest points. Each meeple card features a monster illustration, hit points (HP), a reward value, and colored damage icons along its edges.

On their turn, a player picks one of the face-up meeple cards from the center of the table and places it orthogonally adjacent to a card already in their personal territory. Cards cannot be rotated or overlapped unless a special effect allows it. Damage is generated through connections between cards — when at least two matching damage icons on adjacent card edges are linked, the damage activates and is compared against a meeple's HP. If the total connected damage meets or exceeds its HP, the meeple is defeated. Different edge colors trigger special end-game effects: red connections can unleash massive area damage, orange pairs boost existing attacks, and other colors interact with specific monster types.

The game ends when there are no more cards to replenish the central row. Players then tally the reward points from all defeated meeples, apply color bonus effects, and the player with the highest total wins.

boardgame: Cooper's Hill Cheese Rolling: The Most Dangerous Race In The World
description:
Cooper’s Hill Cheese Rolling: The Most Dangerous Race In The World is a fast-paced party game inspired by one of the world’s wildest traditions. Players race downhill by rolling meeples upright as quickly as possible while battling through injuries, scenarios, and mini games that make each round more chaotic than the last.

Each round begins with the cheese die rolling down the hill to determine how many meeples must be rolled. Players compete simultaneously, trying to land their meeples on their feet and grab the cheese first to advance.

The first player to win four rounds and reach the bottom of the hill claims the cheese and wins the race.

—description from the publisher

Plot is a fast-paced two-player card game of bluffing and deduction set in a medieval court. Players place hidden characters, challenge opponents, and outmaneuver each other to form a row of three of their cards. Simple rules makes this an accessible game for players of all ages, while tense decisions, and clever mind games make every duel quick and highly replayable.

Do you want a small, very portable game to play while travelling, on the bus, in a bar or cafe? Plot can be played in 30 seconds, but is meant to be enjoyed as a series of matches where you quickly begin to understand how your opponent strategizes, and how you can trick them next time!

—description from the publisher

Caravela is a resource management card game about the Spice Trade during the Age of Discovery! Players compete to fill up different types of ships with valuable resources in order to fulfill different contracts in order to obtain victory points. The players with the most victory points in contracts at game´s end are victorious!

Pirates, Cranes, and special contract types add twists and turns to the games and reward more experienced players and add replayability. The game also comes with an A3 poster describing the different classes of ships represented in the game and their historical inspiration!

Accessible and dynamic, with a strong historical foundation, this game combines adventure, resource management, and direct player interaction for a fun 15-40 minute game session!

—description from the publisher

Players enter their spaceships from the edge of a squared map that features a central sun and 12 planets, some of which have asteroids around them. The players move according to the roll of a single six-sided die either to maneuver next to planets, which they can capture only on the roll of "1", or to land on another player's piece to bounce them back off the board. Winner is the first to capture 4, 5 or 7 planets, depending on the number of players.

Vengeance is a dark, witchcraft-themed card game for 1–4 players in which you embody a powerful Witch bound to a fearsome Demon. The creature has already been summoned, and now the ritual has changed: the race is no longer to call it forth, but to control it and bend its wrath toward your own retribution.

Each Witch pursues five personal objectives (Vengeances and Gates), all visible on the table, while players secretly influence the Demon’s movement by playing Ritual Objects (Herbs, Minerals, Potions). The majority of objects played determines the Demon’s next step across a circle of 8 Locations, potentially activating objectives, triggering Gate effects, and shifting the balance of power.

Players may also unleash Spells, use Artifacts, and manipulate timing to disrupt their opponents’ plans. But power comes at a price: every fulfilled objective strengthens the Demon, breaking its chains. If the Demon is unleashed before a Witch completes her path, everyone loses.

Fast, tense, and highly tactical, Vengeance is a shared-control majority game of manipulation, prediction, and calculated risk—where only one Witch will claim victory… if the world survives the ritual.

—description from the designer

Čiurlionio iššūkis turns art into playful challenges full of guessing, acting, and unexpected laughs. Interpret iconic paintings in your own unique way and race your friends to figure them out—every round becomes a fresh burst of creativity where imagination matters just as much as winning.

Featuring 80 of M. K. Čiurlionis artworks and 3 unique ways to play, it’s a fun and imaginative way to connect with one of the most iconic Lithuania’s artists.

—description from the publisher

Unleash your imagination in a fast-paced game where creativity is your greatest weapon. Invent completely new words and dare your opponents to trust your thinking – or risk losing their points.

Enter a world of fantasy by creating clues with made-up words to describe the various characters in the game. Other players will have to decide if they think they have your clue right and risk losing their points. The trickier the clue, the more points you earn. However, if nobody understands it, you will lose points yourself. This colourful game is perfect for families, groups of friends and parties!

—description from the publisher

The sunny edition of the cult game STADT LAND VOLLPFOSTEN. The ultimate game with 26 creative and fun categories – each player receives one page of the A4 pad and places it in front of them. A time limit is set for each round (we recommend 2 minutes). The innovative letter strip helps to determine the letter. Then the aim is to write down all the terms as quickly as possible. Eight terms are sought per round.

—description from the publisher

Die sonnige Edition des Kultspiels STADT LAND VOLLPFOSTEN. Der ultimative Spielblock mit 26 kreativen und lustigen Kategorien – damit ist Malle nicht nur einmal im Jahr! Jeder Spieler erhält eine Seite des DIN-A4 Blockes und legt diese vor sich hin. Es wird eine Zeit pro Durchgang ausgemacht (wir empfehlen 2 Minuten als Begrenzung). Die innovative Buchstabenleiste hilft bei der Ermittlung des Buchstabens. Dann gilt es so schnell wie möglich alle Begriffe einzutragen. Pro Runde sind 8 Begriffe gesucht.

—description from the publisher (German)

The heart-warming edition of the cult game STADT LAND VOLLPFOSTEN. The ultimate game pad featuring 26 creative and fun categories for hours of fun with children, parents, and grandparents. Each player is given one page of the A4 pad and places it in front of them. A time limit is set for each round (we recommend 2 minutes). The innovative letter strip helps to determine the letter. Then the aim is to write down all the terms as quickly as possible. Eight terms are sought per round.

—description from the publisher

Die warmherzige Edition des Kultspiels STADT LAND VOLLPFOSTEN. Der ultimative Spielblock mit 26 kreativen und lustigen Kategorien für gemeinsame Stunden mit Kindern, Eltern, Oma und Opa. Jeder Spieler erhält eine Seite des DIN-A4 Blockes und legt diese vor sich hin. Es wird eine Zeit pro Durchgang ausgemacht (wir empfehlen 2 Minuten als Begrenzung). Die innovative Buchstabenleiste hilft bei der Ermittlung des Buchstabens. Dann gilt es so schnell wie möglich alle Begriffe einzutragen. Pro Runde sind 8 Begriffe gesucht.

—description from the publisher (German)

A lion, a genie and a Penguin go to a fortune Teller. The lion asks the others "How many Horror movies do you know?" - "I know three." mumbled the penguin. "Five!" countered the lion. "THRITEEN!!!" shouted the genie in a booming voice. The goat behind the crystal grinned and said " I see it differently... but good luck"

You might not be any smarter now, but at least you know what it´s all about! Pick a topic, place your bets - but don´t go too far, or you´ll have to deliver!

Only those with some energy left at the end will win!

This time: with questions for adults!

—description from the publisher

Ein Löwe, ein Flaschengeist & ein Pinguin gehen zu einer Wahrsagerin. Da fragt der Löwe die anderen: "Wie viele Horrorfilme kennt ihr?" - "Ich kenne 3.", möpte der Pinguin. "Fünf!" konterte der Löwe. "DREIZEHN!!!" rief der Flaschengeist mit kräftiger Stimme.

Die Ziege hinter der Glaskugel griste: "Das sehe ich anders, aber viel Erfolg!!"

Jetzt seid ihr zwar auch nicht schlauer, doch nun wisst ihr immerhin worum es geht! Wählt ein Thema, macht eure Einsätze, doch geht nicht zu weit... sonst müsst ihr abliefern! Nur wer am ende noch Energie übrig hat, gewinnt!

Dieses mal: mit Fragen für Erwachsene!

—description from the publisher (German)

Rescate en Sherwood ("​Rescue in Sherwood") is a cooperative family board game designed for 2 to 4 players, ages 5 and up, with an approximate duration of 45 minutes.

​Story and Objective
​The Sheriff of Nottingham has kidnapped Lady Marian and locked her in a cell deep within Sherwood Forest. Players take on the roles of Robin Hood and his friends (Little John, Friar Tuck, and Azeem Bashir) with the goal of finding their lost weapons, obtaining the Master Key, and defeating the Sheriff to rescue her.

​Main Mechanics
​Cooperation: Players win or lose as a team. The game is lost if any hero runs out of life points or if it becomes impossible to complete the rescue.
​Exploration: The board consists of 16 forest tiles arranged in a 4x4 grid. Players must move along the paths and uncover "enigma tokens" to find items, food, or allies while avoiding the guards.
​Actions per Turn: Each player has 2 actions per turn, which can include moving (up to 5 spaces), flipping a token, using the hawk to explore, trading items, or attacking the Sheriff.
​Combat: Six-sided dice are used to resolve encounters with guards and the Sheriff. Heroes can improve their attack by finding weapons or with the help of Villager archers.
​Inventory Management: Each character has a player sheet with a health track and space for two items in their inventory.

—description from the publisher

Rescate en Sherwood, es un juego de 2 a 4 jugadores, cooperativo, de trabajo en equipo, con una duración de unos 45 minutos y a partir de 5 años de edad.
El objetivo es buscar a través del bosque el equipo necesario para derrotar al Sheriff de Nottingham y encontrar la Llave Maestra para poder abrir la celda de Lady Marian. Solo derrotándolo y encontrando la llave sin perder la vida de ninguno de nuestros héroes se conseguirá el objetivo y ganar el juego.

Rescate en Sherwood, se juega a lo largo de turnos, que transcurren de la siguiente forma:

Turno Jugador inicial:
El jugador elegido empezará tomando a su Héroe de Sherwood e inicialmente, podrá ejecutar hasta 2 acciones por turno, de las que podrá elegir:

     Moverse hasta 5 casillas
     Levantar un token.
     Utilizar el Halcón
     Intercambiar o dar un objeto a un compañero que esté junto a él
     Atacar, pero solo al Sheriff de Nottingham


Una vez haya finalizado sus 2 acciones le tocará el turno al jugador de su izquierda y así sucesivamente. Estas acciones vienen explicadas detenidamente en el apartado 6.3.

Ganar y Perder:
Rescate en Sherwood, es un juego cooperativo, de manera que los jugadores ganan o pierden como un equipo. La partida se gana rescatando a Lady Marian, y se pierde cuando resulta imposible cumplirlo o un personaje se queda fuera de combate tras perder todos sus puntos de vida.

—description from the publisher (Spanish)
family: Folk Tales & Fairy Tales: Robin Hood

description:
A lion, a genie and a penguin go to a fortune teller. The lion asks the others "How many Horror movies do you know?" - "I know three." mumbled the penguin. "Five!" countered the lion. "THRITEEN!!!" shouted the genie in a booming voice. The goat behind the crystal grinned and said " I see it differently... but good luck"

You might not be any smarter now, but at least you know what it´s all about! Pick a topic, place your bets - but don´t go too far, or you´ll have to deliver!

Only those with some energy left at the end will win!

—description from the publisher

Ein Löwe, ein Flaschengeist & ein Pinguin gehen zu einer Wahrsagerin. Da fragt der Löwe die anderen: "Wie viele Horrorfilme kennt ihr?" - "Ich kenne 3.", möpte der Pinguin. "Fünf!" konterte der Löwe. "DREIZEHN!!!" rief der Flaschengeist mit kräftiger Stimme.

Die Ziege hinter der Glaskugel griste: "Das sehe ich anders, aber viel Erfolg!!"

Jetzt seid ihr zwar auch nicht schlauer, doch nun wisst ihr immerhin worum es geht! Wählt ein Thema, macht eure Einsätze, doch geht nicht zu weit... sonst müsst ihr abliefern! Nur wer am ende noch Energie übrig hat, gewinnt!

Players bet on their knowledge on different questions. But dont bet to high!

—description from the publisher (German)

The Sakura Dice: Bonsai expansion introduces new challenges while preserving the clean and elegant core of the original game.

In this expansion, players cultivate a small bonsai tree while engaging with new elements and rules that deepen the gameplay.
