The focus shifts to carefully shaping the tree: players must grow branches in a way that maximizes blooming flowers within a limited space, while also managing the swarming of bees.

Bees are no longer fixed on the board—players must create suitable habitats for them. Additionally, hollows formed in the trunk of the bonsai become an important factor, as their value at the end of the game depends on how they were developed.

The pagoda appears as a new gameplay element in this expansion, introducing difficult decisions especially for passive players and opening up fresh tactical possibilities.

Several systems have been reworked:

the Geisha and Daimyo tracks have been renewed
the Samurai Yin–Yang tracks gain new functions
bonus actions have been removed and replaced by penalty points

Sakura Dice: Bonsai offers a more complex and strategic experience while staying true to the clarity and design philosophy of the base game.

—description from the publisher

A number of combinations of object and location cards are laid out on the table, and now the aim is to guess which (most likely crazy or strange) combination of cards the player has chosen from among the possible combinations on the table. The other players try to narrow down the choice by taking turns to ask quirky questions from a pile of pre-printed question cards they hold in their hands. With these questions, each player attempts to work out which combination might be the one chosen. This can be difficult, and initially it is often a matter of ruling out some of the other combinations.

When one of the players has gathered enough information to make a guess at the combination of cards, that player makes a seal sound and is thus granted the right to guess.

If the guess is correct, the player wins a point, whilst an incorrect guess disqualifies the player from winning the round.

Once the combination has been guessed, a new round begins with a player selecting a combination from the table, and the game continues in this way until a player has guessed 5 correct card combinations and thus scored 5 points.

—description from the publisher

The game is designed for 2 players, although it can be played with up to 6, as it features a face-off between 3 herbivorous dinosaurs and 3 carnivores. The herbivores start at the lake and must reach the forest, while the carnivores start in the forest with the goal of "snacking" on the herbivores. The first player or team to either save or eat 3 dinosaurs wins the game.
​Gameplay & Mechanics

​To move, attack, or trigger each dinosaur’s unique ability, players use cards.

     Starting Hand: You begin with 5 cards.
     ​Actions: On your turn, you can play as many cards as you see fit.
     ​Drawing: At the start of your subsequent turns, you draw only one card.
     Hand Limit: The maximum hand size is 7 cards.
     Trade-in: You can exchange 4 cards of the same type for any action you need.
     1 vs 1 Mode (2 players): You can use your cards on any of your dinosaurs currently in play; you aren't restricted to using them on just one.
     ​Combat: Attacks are resolved using dice.


​
Scoring & Respawning
​Once a player reaches their goal with a dinosaur—either by escaping through the forest or by eating an herbivore—a new one is drawn from the corresponding deck. There must always be 3 carnivores and 3 herbivores on the board. The player keeps the card of the defeated or saved herbivore as a trophy or score point.

—description from the publisher

Juego para 2 jugadores, aunque se pueden jugar de 2 a 6, ya que se enfrentarán 3 dinosaurios herbívoros contra 3 carnívoros. Los primeros comenzarán en la zona del lago y deben llegar hasta el bosque y los carnívoros empezarán en el bosque y se tienen que merendar a los herbívoros. Ganará el primer jugador o equipo que logre salvar o comerse a 3 dinosaurios.

Para lograrlo se utilizan cartas, las cuales se usarán para avanzar, atacar o realizar la habilidad propia de cada dinosaurio. Se comienza con 5 cartas y podrás usar todas las que veas oportunas en tu turno y al comienzo de los siguientes turnos se robará una sola carta de este tipo, se puede tener un máximo de 7, pudiendo cambiar 4 de un mismo tipo por la acción que necesites. En el modo de juego de 1 vs 1 (2 jugadores), podré usar las cartas con cualquier dinosaurio que esté en juego, no estaré obligado a usarlas con un mismo dinosaurio. Para los ataques se usarán dados.

Una vez un jugador alcance su meta con un dinosaurio, ya sea escapando por el bosque o merendándose a un herbívoro, aparecerá uno nuevo del mazo correspondiente, habiendo siempre en el tablero 3 carnívoros y 3 herbívoros, quedándose el jugador la carta del herbívoro vencido o salvado como premio o puntuación.

De momento, y por causas de logística (miniaturas de plástico del Toy'r'us), tenemos más herbívoros que carnívoros, por lo tanto el dinosaurio herbívoro que salga de la partida ya sea de un modo u otro ya no volverá, aparecerá otro del mazo de su tipo. En el caso de que muera un carnívoro, este no puntúa, pero su carta se barajará con la del resto de este tipo pudiendo volver a salir al tablero de juego.

—description from the publisher (Spanish)

boardgame: Apollo Mine: Join the Race for Space
description:
Apollo Mine: Join the Race for Space  is a print and play solo drawing game where you'll draw your lunar mine, uncover resources from the moon, fight enemies, overcome challenges, and survive as long as you can in the dangerous environment.

Gameplay takes place in three turns - Your Turn, Resource and Combat Rolls, and the Persistent Turn.

On your turn, you'll decide where to build hallways, ladders, and rooms by drawing your mine on the moonscape. You'll also claim benefits from the rooms you build in your mine. How you build your mine is completely up to you! The decisions you make impact how to survive enemies and other dangers.

Next, you'll make a Resource Roll and Combat Roll to determine what you've found, and how you get attacked. Your lunar base will also be hit by radiation!

Finally, some enemies offer greater challenges. These will occur during the Persistent Turn.

Gameplay follows this turn sequence - Your Turn, Combat and Resource Rolls, and Persistent Events - until your mine achieves a victory condition, or your mine runs out of personnel.

Whether you win or lose, you'll end the game with a high score, that can be compared to other Apollo Mine games you play!

What's In Apollo Mine: Join the Race for Space

     Draw your mine your way – in Apollo Mine, you have the freedom to draw your mine exactly the way you want. The game allows you to explore different strategies of building a mine – perhaps digging wide, avoiding more dangerous enemies but staying exposed to radiation – or perhaps digging deep, braving the dangers of the moon but collecting more resources and avoiding radiation!



     Resource and Combat Lists – four different resource and combat lists to roll on, depending on the current depth of your mine.



     Radiation – this is a new mechanic that threatens the personnel in your Apollo Mine! You can protect against it by building specialized rooms – or by digging deeper into the surface of the moon!



     Sealing Rooms – have a pesky yet dangerous enemy lurking in the depths of your Apollo Mine? There is a new, space-based strategy to use. You can seal your rooms, depriving the enemy of oxygen. This defeats the enemy, at the cost of the rooms you seal.



     Hallway/Ladder Collapse – this new rule gives limitations on the length of a hallway or ladder, before it collapses.



     15 New Rooms – Included with Apollo Mine are 15 new rooms, including some twists on concepts from Dwarf Mine.



     4 Base Level Lists – Similar to Dwarf Mine, Apollo Mine comes with 4 Level Lists to use based on how deep your mine goes – each with fun theming for mining in space! These include new enemies, new discoveries, and new persistent threats!



     6 Expansions – There are 6 built-in expansions that will be included with the base game!



     3 Maps – Apollo Mine comes with 3 maps to choose from and play!


—description from the designer

A strategic engine-building board game where you step into the role of a pioneering park director. Over three in-game years, you'll unearth fossils, incubate and hatch long-lost species, and build a thriving attraction filled with prehistoric wonders.

Your choices shape your park. Co-operative tasks, hidden personal objectives and shared competitive milestones keep every game fun, dynamic, and full of surprises.

—description from the publisher

ORAL is an uncensored party game for adults that puts 3 to 8 participants in 200 extreme situations and embarrassing decision-making challenges. In each round, one player reads a scenario aloud and secretly chooses one of four possible answers (A, B, C, or D). The rest of the group then tries to guess which option the player picked, based on how well they know them. Once everyone reveals their answer cards, the active player moves forward one space plus one extra space for each correct guess, while every player who guessed correctly also advances one space. If nobody guesses right, no one moves. The game continues with the next player, and the first to reach the finish wins — although victory ultimately depends on how well the other players can read you.

With Hydro, you will have to work as a team to take on this challenging project, where everyone will be part of the same team in this exciting assembly, with an interactive and fully dynamic board. Each game brings a new project, a new challenge, and a new thrill. An intelligent game for the whole family. Shuffle the board and have fun!

In Adivinhação you need to represent objects through mime, drawings, sounds, and even touch. The other players must guess what you are trying to represent. Can you imitate the sound of a faucet, demonstrate a cake through mime, draw things that resemble a clown, etc.? Adivinhação, besides being a creativity test, is incredible fun for you and your friends!

A Halloween-themed game that challenges kids to earn candy or play tricks as they traverse the double-sided board.

To escape the haunted house, each player must complete challenges: sing “Happy Birthday” meowing like a cat, act like a ghost, tickle the player next to you, and many more.

The first player to complete this spooky journey and make it out of the haunted house wins!

A website-exclusive collaboration promo card "Penguin" included in Spur Note.

Card Description
If you are last position on the turn order track gain the following effect:


     2 VP
     Gain 1 Guest card from 1-4 of the Guest Area

In this game, we will discover who will become the champion of Big Brother Brasil.

The game starts with participants receiving followers based on each chosen personality. Going through various challenges and events, players will gain or lose followers trying to reach the top 3 finalists.

The champion will be the player who becomes the finalist unanimously or when the last player is eliminated.

Catacombs of Kural is a solo/coop adventure module for Relicblade.

This adventure directly relates to the events of the Storms of Kural2-player box set.

This 61-page book includes rules for playing solo/coop games alongside a specific player-versus-environment solo/coop adventure.

—description from the publisher

The Unsung Hero of the Dazzling Circus is...the Lighting!
You are Gaffer the Lighting Technician!  Skillfully shine the spotlight on the performers as they do their acts!

Follow the cue sheet and place and overlap your spotlight cards in any direction still within the arena to attempt to accurately shine a spotlight only on the correct performers.

The game comes with 4 modes : 
In Small Theatre spotlight as many performers as possible.
In the Grand Show you now need to work together to avoid shining the spotlight on the wrong performers.
Once you've mastered the others you can go on the World Tour, in which you recruit new performers to aim for the Grand Finale! But it's on the world stage so you must stick to the timeline and spotlight accurately.
And the final mode is Solo Mode, where you can aim for personal goals in either the Small Theatre or work on your own in the World Tour.

Use your problem solving skills to find the best combination of spotlight cards and focus the light on the main performers!

—description from the publisher

Two Pants is a set of three two-suited trick-taking games where players draft their hand and try to complete goals.

In Two Pants (the basic game), players try to take the majority of tricks in one suit. If two players succeed at that, they win, and the other player loses. If, however, one player captures the majority in both suits, the other players win.

Reversible Two Pants has the same goal, but the cards have the other suit on their opposite side, and players can earn gems, which they can spend to flip their cards. They thus control the suits in their hands.

Totally Not a Compantendium Game is similar to Reversible Two Pants, but players draft asymmetric goal cards when they draft their hands, and the player(s) completing the most goal cards is the winner. It is NOT a compendium game, despite one aspiring to be one.

The first expansion pack for the Chinese version of Firefight.
Includes new unit counters of 1980s era, such as Soviet's T-72A and T-80U, US M1 Abrams and M2 Bradley.
Also includes relevant charts for these new units, 6 new scenarios which includes the usage of these units, and 1 pad of planning map for the A+B map of Firefight.

Menú del Día is a solo or two-player cooperative game where you must survive the rush of the service and help your restaurant progress day after day.

The game features a short campaign where new scenarios and game modes are gradually unlocked.

In each round, both problems and customers appear in equal measure, increasing the challenge. During your turn, you can serve customers, collect payments, solve issues, load the dishwasher, make coffee, and retrieve ingredients—all in order to complete the objective of each scenario.

—description from the designer

Looking for a card game that brings laughter, excitement, and a dash of friendly competition to your family nights? Look no further than Match & Toss! This fast-paced, hilarious card game is designed for 2-6 players, ages 3 and up, and guarantees 15-30 minutes of pure fun and engagement.

The Mission:
Be the first to toss all your Image Cards by matching them with their related Text Cards. Roll the dice, pick cards, and race to make the funniest matches.

What’s in the Box?
60 Text Cards: Each card features a funny phrase that will have everyone laughing.
60 Image Cards: Hilarious illustrations that match the Text Cards.
1 Joker Card: A wildcard that adds a twist to the game.
1 Dice: Roll to determine your action, pick cards, skip a turn, or toss cards to slow down your opponents.
Pick & Toss Boxes: The game box doubles as a card holder and tossing area, making setup a breeze.

Why You’ll Love It:
Quick & Easy to Learn: Perfect for players of all ages, with simple rules that get the game started in minutes.
Endless Laughter: With quirky phrases like "A monster brushing his teeth" and "A pizza running a marathon," the combinations are as funny as they are unexpected.
Family Bonding: Whether you’re playing with kids, teens, or adults, Match & Toss is a fantastic way to bring everyone together for some quality family time.

Perfect for:
Family Game Time: A great way to unplug and enjoy some screen-free fun.
Parties & Gatherings: Compact and easy to carry, it’s the perfect game to bring to any social event.
Gifts: Looking for a unique gift? Match & Toss is a hit for birthdays, holidays, or just because!

Educational & Developmental Benefits:
Match & Toss isn’t just fun—it’s also a great way to help kids (and adults!) develop important skills:

Concentration: Players must focus to match the Text Cards with the correct Image Cards.
Matching & Memory Skills: The game encourages players to remember which cards they’ve seen and make quick connections.
Reading Practice: The Text Cards provide a fun way for young readers to practice their reading skills.
Strategic Thinking: Deciding what card to toss to slow down opponents adds a layer of strategy.
Social Skills: Taking turns, following rules, and engaging in friendly competition help build social interaction skills.

—description from the publisher

Eternal Sevens is a modern reinterpretation of the traditional shedding game Sevens (Fan-Tan).

On each turn, players have only two choices: play a card or pass. Passing is limited, and if a player exceeds this limit and cannot play a card, they are eliminated from the game. As a result, players must constantly decide whether to play cards to empty their hand or deliberately hold back to force opponents into elimination. This core structure is based on the traditional Japanese rules of Sevens.

In this game, passing is not just a way to avoid playing, but an action that can trigger effects. In addition, playing cards with the numbers 2 or 9 can activate special effects that influence yourself or all players.

There are two paths to victory: either discard all cards from your hand or earn stars. Stars are awarded to players who complete a horizontal sequence. Because of this, choosing not to play and waiting for the right moment can become a powerful strategy.

The game includes both a Casual Mode and a Gamer Mode. In Casual Mode, players can experience a streamlined, modern version of Sevens. In Gamer Mode, after receiving their hands, players choose a character. Each character changes the effects of pass actions, as well as how victory points and stars are earned, creating different strategic approaches while keeping the victory conditions the same.

The combination of hand management, character abilities, and player decisions creates a dynamic and evolving strategic experience.

—description from the designer

Players take on the roles of young heroes who refuse to remain passive in the face of the German occupation. Together, they organize the Resistance in their village by carrying out secret missions while avoiding enemy patrols. To win cooperatively, just complete the scenario objectives before been caught by the soldiers or before tension become too important.

Suitable for ages 8 and up, the game combines strategy, teamwork, and immersion in a powerful historical setting. A smart family game that conveys strong values and emotions.

Tis game is inspired by the bestselling comic book series, with over 1 million copies sold. It is an immersive, family-friendly board game with 5 progressive and replayable scenarios.
family: History: Resistance during WW2 in Europe

description: A memory and calculation game. You get to open two cards from the grid and try to add or substract in order to accomplish the commanment number. You have to be careful with the special cards that can make you lose your turn or change the board, but if you're lucky, you can get to spy the kitchen.

boardgame: A Mad Endeavor: The Fight for Stony Point, July 16, 1779
description:
The British outpost at Stony Point along the Hudson River prevented any movement by the American along this crucial waterway. It also allowed the British nearly unfettered transit that could cut off the New England colonies from the rest of United States. Such a position had to be taken and reduced. To that end, General Washington ordered General “Mad” Anthony Wayne to assemble a strike force to take Stoney Point by assault. What ensued was one of the most daring assaults in the Revolutionary War.

Can you as the British player hold off the Rebel’s attack at Stony Point? Will you as the Americans take the Loyalist stronghold to help secure New England’s position in the revolution? Learn and enjoy!

—description from the publisher

Os Cavaleiros do Zodíaco is a race and battle board game for 2 to 5 players, based on the classic Saint Seiya anime. Each player takes on the role of a Bronze Knight — Seiya, Hyoga, Shun, Shiryu, or Ikki — on a quest to reach the Sanctuary and defeat the evil Master Ares so that the goddess Athena may reclaim her rightful place.

Players start with five lives and roll a special die to move their heroes along individual paths that cross and diverge across the board. Spaces may instruct players to advance, go back, gain a life, or draw a luck card. When a hero lands on an enemy space, combat begins using Attack and Defense spinners — the attacker must meet or exceed the enemy's required strength within a limited number of attempts dictated by the enemy's token. Failure costs the hero one life, forcing them to retry next round. Losing all lives eliminates the player. As heroes approach the Sanctuary, enemies grow tougher, but so does the heroes' inner Cosmo. The first player to reach the Sanctuary and defeat the Master of Evil earns the Sagittarius Armor and wins the game.

In this game, players must navigate through the dark labyrinth of Gotham City and find the characters who inhabit the city. On a board with movable and fixed tiles, players must shift the movable tiles to find the characters on their cards. The first player to find all their characters and return to their starting position on the board wins the game!

Each player chooses a color of blocks, receiving the cubes and cards for that color. Each player flips a card and grabs the cubes to try to build the face shown on the card. The first player to complete their face must ring the bell. All other players must stop immediately. The card and the build are checked — if correct, the player who rang the bell wins the round and keeps the card. If the build is wrong, that player sits out the next round. All players return their cards to the bottom of the pile and start the next round. The first player to collect 12 cards wins. It is also possible to play solo using a timer! This game stimulates concentration, memory, coordination, and agility!

Batalha Flip Cup: Challenge and Beat Your Friends! Ready for a battle among friends? This is the game that will separate the true champions from the mere amateurs. Batalha Flip Cup is here to test your ability to provoke and draw laughs from your opponents. Gather your friends, sharpen your skills, and get ready for the most intense competition you've ever experienced.

Embates da Física (Physics Clashes) is a competitive hidden identity game for 5 to 8 players. One group of players represents Einstein while another group SECRETLY represents Newton. Einstein's team wins if they manage to correctly answer 4 questions, while Newton's team wins if 4 questions are answered incorrectly. During the game, players are free to talk and discuss whatever they want. Social interaction, logical deduction, and bluffing are essential to victory in this game, especially for the Newtons!

Cubo das Emoções (Emotions Cube) is a children's game designed to promote coordination and stimulate sensory development through emotions. It teaches children to correctly recognize shapes and colors while practicing thinking skills. Each player chooses a color and takes the corresponding colored squares (4 cubes). During the game, players must match the blocks in their hands to the shape shown on the game card as quickly as possible. Suitable for 1-4 players. How to play: Shuffle all game cards to form the card pile. Each child chooses a color and takes the matching colored squares. Race to match your blocks to the pattern on the revealed game card!

Safari Zap is a card game about capturing the best adventures in the African savanna! Using a bit of memory and strategy, earn the most likes in the WhatsApp group and win the game. You and your friends will have fun taking photos and selfies of wild animals by collecting cards of the same animal to score points. You earn extra points by photographing animals against breathtaking landscapes! Safari Zap is perfect for those who enjoy quick games with simple gameplay. An accessible game for the whole family.

Shatterfields brings the spirit of classic early‑2000s RTS games to the tabletop, transforming the thrill of building a base from nothing, racing up your tech tree, and unleashing the perfect counter‑force into a physical, highly tactical experience.
Set in a collapsing near‑future where corporations wage brutal micro‑wars over dwindling resources, every match begins with the same setup and evolves into constantly varying battles for dominance, shaped entirely by your strategic choices.

As your forces expand, the battlefield becomes a sharp contest of timing, positioning, and composition, where every clash matters and the right unit can overturn overwhelming odds. Reactive combat keeps all players engaged, streamlined line of sight keeps the action fast, and four distinct maps offer varied tactical challenges.

—description from the publisher

The Necromancer is Dead! Brave Villagers it is time to enter the Tomb, and relieve the blight upon the land. The Tomb is filled with the lingering evil of the Necromancer. Minions roam the rooms, waiting to take you health or corrupt your soul. Loot from a hundred years litters the place. Weapons & Upgrades can help your chances, Treasure & Artifacts hold the way to victory, and a chance of falling victim to a Cursed Item, Keys may hold your chance of a way out.

Beware, for the Necromancer lays in his sarcauphogus, but his influence still fills the halls of the Tomb. Travel deep enough, and you may pay the price.

DarkQuest is a fun, dexterity driven, co-op dungeon crawl for 2-5 players that takes 60-90 minutes.

Rescue as many monks as you can from the demon infested monastery. A Run consists of several consecutive encounters and may be ended after any of them. If the player’s paladin character dies, all rescued monks are lost as well.

From two standard decks of playing cards, select the 40 cards indicated on the reference sheet; these form the game deck.

The game uses five suits:

     hearts: the paladin’s combat cards
     diamonds, clubs, spades: the demons’ combat cards
     jokers: the monks


Gameplay Overview

Encounter Setup

     Shuffle well
     Draw 4 cards


Turn

     Discard X cards
     Draw up to 8 cards
     Play X cards


End of Encounter

     Gain 2 or 3 items


Suggested background music: the original Diablo (1996) soundtrack.

Part of the Demon War series, alongside A mágus küldetése (The Quest of the Mage) and The Sorceress and the Demons.

—description from the designer

boardgame: Dominion of the Spear: Revised Expansion II – Fight 36 Battles of the Ancient World
description:
This is the second expansion for the Dominion of the Spear solo wargames rules and contains 36 more ancient and medieval historical battles with each having a very brief background, army lists, suggested historical deployment, and very brief description of what happened in the actual battle and the consequences. 
Needs the revised Dominion of the Spear rules. 
Designed to be played solo - refight the battles and see if history changes! 
Then try different deployments for the armies.

The battles are:

     Uruk 2334 BC, 
     Mount Uaush (Waush) 714 BC, 
     Ulai River (Til-Tuba) 653 BC, 
     Pelusium 525 BC, 
     Thermopylae 480 BC, 
     Delium 424 BC, 
     Mantinea 418 BC, 
     Leuctra 371 BC, 
     Chaeronea 338 BC, 
     Granicus 334 BC, 
     Issus 333 BC, 
     Hydaspes 326 BC, 
     Paraitakene 317 BC, 
     Gaza 312 BC, 
     Sentinum 295 BC, 
     Heraclea 280 BC, 
     Battle of the Elephants 275 BC, 
     Bagradas River (Tunis) 255 BC, 
     Trebia 218 BC, 
     Zama 202 BC, 
     Pharsalus 48 BC, 
     Zela 47 BC, 
     Watling Street 61 AD, 
     Tapae 101 AD, 
     Tricamerum 533 AD, 
     Talas 751 AD, 
     Stamford Bridge 1066 AD, 
     Ager Sanguinis 1119 AD, 
     Didgori 1121 AD, 
     Montgisard 1177 AD, 
     Bouvines 1214 AD, 
     Indus 1221 AD, 
     Stirling Bridge 1297 AD, 
     Halmyros 1311 AD, 
     Poitiers 1356 AD, 
     Ankara 1402 AD.


—description from the publisher

boardgame: Legends of the Ancient World: On the Savage Plain – Volume 1
description:
As far back as time itself, the plain has been your home. Home, but a hostile home at best. Cold wind tears across a desolate expanse, where few animals roam, and only  sparse and thorny weeds grow.

The few reservoirs of life are the scattered thermal lakes that dot the expanse. But even these are dangerous. Though the  lakes bring life, they also bring predators - and in these conditions, the predators are brutal.

Ang is the master of this land. Ang's great great grossfather, Tor, drove back the northern tribes that invaded our land. Though defeated, the northern tribes still pose a real and present threat.

But Tor led our people through a violent campaign of killing and extermination. Though centuries ago, Tor's descendants roam this land without equal. There are threats though, and that is why, as a guardian of the land, you patrol the realm.

Now a new problem lies before you. One that will change the fate of the tribes and will spell salvation or ruin for your people.

—description from the back of the box
family: Series: Legends of the Ancient World (Dark City Games)

description:
Abyssalia: Life in the Dark is a strategic board game by Antropoceno Games that casts players as scientific institutes exploring the deep ocean.

A competitive experience that feels inherently collaborative. Players map habitats, research diverse species, and launch legal challenges to shut down harmful mining operations.

Success requires managing a complex pipeline of scientific evidence and political lobbying to improve the odds of winning high-stakes judicial dice rolls.

The game features a wide array of authentic marine biology, illustrated through detailed artwork of creatures like the Vampire Squid and Greenland Shark.

Players can pursue multiple victory paths, including territorial expansion, species protection, or ecological restoration after mines cease their extractive operations.

The game's mechanics mirror real-world conservation dilemmas, forcing players to balance limited resources with the urgent need to preserve fragile ecosystems.

—description from the publisher

Weasel Wars is a tactical adventure and strategy board game set in the living, ever-changing forest of Evermoss.

Lead your clan through a vast woodland filled with opportunity and danger. Explore a large, fixed board shaped by dynamic, interchangeable settlements, gather resources, discover powerful artifacts and confront ruthless raiders and rival players.

Upgrade your fortress, develop your hero and adapt your strategy as the world evolves around you.

But Evermoss is not just a setting, it is alive. The forest reacts to your actions, shifting the balance of power and striking back when least expected. Every decision can trigger new threats, opportunities or consequences, making each game unpredictable and unique.

With asymmetric heroes, multiple paths to victory and a strong focus on player freedom, Weasel Wars blends exploration, area control, resource management and tactical combat into a rich and replayable experience.

Easy to learn yet deep in strategy.
No two journeys through Evermoss are ever the same.

—description from the publisher

Cardúmenes is a tactical underwater combat game for two players where rival seahorses clash for ultimate control of the ocean. In this deep-sea confrontation, strategic positioning, energy management, and tactical foresight will define the victor.

Your mission is to deploy your units, breach enemy lines, and capture the opponent's Seahorse, dragging them back to your base to claim victory.

Key Gameplay Elements:

Energy Management: Roll the dice each turn to generate energy, the lifeblood of your strategy.

Strategic Choices: Every decision counts. Spend your energy to move your troops, launch devastating attacks, or trigger unique special abilities.

The Market: Invest your hard-earned energy in the market to unlock powerful benefits and synergies. Master the market to outsmart your opponent and dominate the currents.

—description from the publisher

Lithic is a 2–4 player abstract strategy game where players take turns placing stones on 1 of the 36 intersections of the board, competing to form the largest connected group. Each placement is critical as it expands your territory and strengthens your network, but every move also shapes the possibilities for everyone else.

Instead of placing a stone, you may set one aside to perform a powerful tactical swap: exchange one of your stones with an adjacent connecting stone and anchor them in place by flipping them over, preventing further swaps. This creates permanent structures that can secure key connections, disrupt opponents, or lock in scoring advantages. When all stones have been played or set aside, the player with the largest connected group wins, with ties broken by comparing next-largest groups until a winner emerges — or the game ends in a hard-fought draw.

—description from the publisher

High in the Andes Mountains, survival is quiet and unforgiving. You are a chinchilla alone in the cold, gathering stones and foraging food to strengthen your burrow against the dangers. Survive long enough to build shelter, find friends, avoid predators, and weather the unknown.

This game is single player only, and you need to choose which survival tactic is most critical. Will you fortify your burrow among the rocky terrain, build your food stash, or take a risk out into the wilds to hopefully find friends and not foes?

—description from the publisher

The first board game set in the fantasy world of Tormenta.

Expedição Escarlate is a cooperative dungeon crawler for 1–4 players set in a world threatened by the relentless invasion of the Torment.

Players take the role of members of the Scarlet Expedition, a group of adventurers sent into dangerous and unstable dungeons corrupted by chaotic forces.

Will the Scarlet Expedition hold the line against the Storm, or will it consume everything?

—description from the publisher

Lion Rampant: Viking Blood Feud is an expansion for Lion Rampant Medieval Wargaming Rules: Second Edition. 
It brings to tabletop life the dramatic and evocative blood feuds of the Icelandic sagas, and enables you to create your own saga.
Set in the Viking Age, the Icelandic sagas offer unique glimpses into the everyday lives of relatively ordinary but larger than life characters, who experienced twists of fate, conflicts, and heroic as well as petty deeds, all set in a stunning subarctic landscape on the edge of the habitable world.

Blood feuds play centre stage in many sagas. Their violent clashes provide dramatic plot twists, but they are also like thunderclaps in  quarrels in which surrounding Icelandic society is spectator and judge. Battlefield victories are often stages of wider games of honour, glory, and social standing.

The expansion provides historical lore on Viking Age Iceland and its sagas, and then brings them to your tabletop with suggestions on how to create your warband and bring to life your characters; optional rules and terrain ideas; five standalone scenarios; and a campaign system that fleshes out the origins of the feud, determines how scenarios affect your characters, and uses feud actions that determine the direction of the campaign and add complications or temporary advantages.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Infinity REM Racers: The Card Game
description:
Infinity REM Racers The Card Game is a fun game of racing Remotes, AI-enhanced robots, piloted at a distance by daring Hacker-Pilots. Play your cards right, confound your rivals, and adjust your speed to enjoy the craziest adventure in the Human Sphere.

Content:

97 cards
1 rulebook

Twitterperlen Nicht Lachen Challenge is set in a social gathering where players engage with internet humor drawn from viral jokes and posts.
The objective is to maintain a straight face while others attempt to provoke laughter through increasingly absurd or constrained performances.
Gameplay revolves around reading or performing joke cards, often modified by additional challenge conditions that alter delivery style or behavior.
Players who laugh or react lose the round, making emotional control the central mechanic driving Competition.

Vonyuchiy nosok is designed as a lighthearted party game for families and friends. The game follows the classic Old Maid mechanic but with a humorous Russian twist—players match pairs of socks while trying to avoid being left with the dreaded Stinky Sock card. The game features 54 cards including special action cards with whimsical effects like "Laundry," "Grandma," "Dog," and "Birthday" that add unexpected turns to gameplay. Designed for 3-6 players aged 5 and up, with games lasting 15-20 minutes, it emphasizes quick learning, laughter, and simple fun. The compact packaging makes it portable for travel, and the matching sock cards can double as a memory game.

Micro-expansion that consists of one character card and one attribute card.

It was given for free during the Kickstarter-campaign if you subscribed to the AEG-newsletter.

The character has the name 'Game Designer'. It will give you 10 money, 1 influence of your choice, 2 cards and an extra turn, but you have to stun the card afterwards.

The attribute has the name 'board gaming' and will give you 1 VP for each card you still have on hand at the end of the game.

Empire of the Steppes is a sandbox, CDG, Block/Counter hybrid Wargame where three factions, Mongol Empire, Ayyubid/Mamluk Caliphate and the Christian Franks vie for supremacy through strategy, warfare and shrewd diplomacy.

Empire of the Steppes can last up to twelve turns.  Each turn consists of three phases. Starting with the investment phase, where players receive and spend their production on purchasing new units, upgrading existing units, purchasing cards and tactics.  The strategy phase, where players can play multi use cards to play events, develop technology and invest in diplomacy. Finally there is the action phase where players can use cards to move their armies, siege castles, raise provincial units and engage in battles which are resolved through dice.

Victory can be achieved in three ways.   Economic Supremacy, your victory points equal 25 at the end of any turn. Victory can be achieved through conquering a enemy capital of which each player has one.  If none of these conditions are met at the end of turn 12, the victory is determined by who has the highest victory points.

A bilingual Atlantic-Canada themed boardgame by New Brunswick school teacher Joan Green, where pawns move across the board passing through the four Atlantic provinces while trying to attend at local attractions and festivals.

Can you survive the night?
Without becoming monster fodder?

Casper has made a bet with Niki: 24 hours in the haunted house. It should be easy enough… but it’s teeming with monsters! As night falls, Niki wakes up. There are sounds in the darkness. Shadows are moving. Doors are creaking. And… shortly after, they arrive. The monsters. And there are many of them.
Can you survive?

You have two ways to win:

     Eliminate all the monsters in the house!
     Find and kill the 3 terrifying bosses!


But be careful! You only have 24 hours. If you run out of monster cards, Niki will run away—and then Casper will have won the bet!

Are you ready to fight the monsters?

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Artful: The card game about collecting real art
description:
Artful is a card game about collecting, curating, and trading artworks by iconic and breakthrough artists. Build the most prestigious collection and lock it down before your rivals steal away your most valuable pieces.

01 | Bid for Artworks - Compete in auctions to acquire new pieces for your collection. Every round matters, and even the most modest opening bid has a chance.

02 | Read the Market -News Cards impact your artworks in different ways. Most will drive up their value. But beware, some stories may call your collection's authenticity into question…

03 | Trade and Deceive - Swap artworks with other players, whether they like it or not. Bluff, flatter, and misdirect to rid yourself of dubious pieces and bag a masterpiece or two.

04 | Secure Your Collection - Lock away your strongest pieces to protect them from other players. But be careful, because once in your vault, their value can no longer increase.

05 | Build the Most Valuable Collection - When the game ends, the true value of each painting is revealed. Did you invest with your head or your heart?

—description from the designer

Good help is hard to find! It’s the annual July 4th celebration in Cape Cozy! The festivities are underway, and everyone is looking forward to tonight’s Fireworks Show. But…the Fireworks are all out of order! The contractor really messed things up. Everyone knows that a good Display starts slow and ends with a showstopping blaze of glory. You recruit a friend to help you get the job done in time. Together, your task is to get the Fireworks back into the correct order (from 1 to 25). Unfortunately, the Display is rather large… and your walkie talkies won’t transmit anything but static! Communication will be (nearly) impossible. You’ll have to divide and conquer!  Work quickly and keep an eye on the Fireworks that your friend has already checked on. Once you think that you’ve got the Fireworks back in the correct order, use the Match and the Fuse to set off the Display and light up the night sky. If the Fireworks Show goes off without a hitch (the cards are in ascending numerical order); you win. If everyone can tell the difference between a professional display and your shenanigans (the cards are out of order); then you lose! Good luck and watch out for the rockets’ red glare!

Fireworks for the 4th is a real-time, cooperative game for two players. The game is played in 2-10 minutes, depending on the Difficulty Level chosen. The Goal is to get the Fireworks Cards in the Display (the 5x5 grid of mortar tubes shown on the Display Board) into ascending numerical order from left-to-right and top-to-bottom (from the top-left corner to the bottom-right corner of the 5x5 Display) AND set off the Fireworks Show before the Timer runs out.

Includes Solo Mode!

—description from the designer

Liberty, DEATH, or TAXES is a bluffing 72-card party game with a patriotic theme. Designed to commemorate America’s 250th birthday, the game is a tongue-in-cheek celebration of American humor.

Every player gets a DEATH card and 5 additional cards at game start. Then the fun begins!

On your turn, will you Pay Taxes? Or Go to Work. Perhaps you'll Declare Your Liberty. But if you do, and you don't have a Liberty card to back up your declaration, then be prepared for another player to call your bluff! And, as a last resort, Death awaits all but the last player standing!

—description from the designer

9 Cards: Season 2 (九张牌：第二季) is the second edition of 9 Cards (九张牌), an initiative that gives designers the opportunity to create a full gaming experience using only nine cards.

The 9 games are:


     S1E1 目里星辰 (Counting Stars)
     S2E2 地铁规划 (Metro Planning)
     S2E3 态度终端 (Attitude Terminal)
     S2E4 斗兽迷踪 (Beasts in Mist)
     S2E5 量子分离 (Quantum Separation), out of scope for BGG
     S2E6 早茶大王 (Cantonese Dim Sum)
     S2E7 漩涡航路 (The Vortex Line)
     S2E8 狼人村落 (The Village of Werewolves)
     S2E9 肉鸽小队 (Team Rogue-Like)


Small accessories may be needed, but not included in the box.
publishers: 九张牌玩具公司(9 Cards Toy Company)

description:
President Lincoln is on the train to Gettysburg when the conductor’s kitty knocks his speech on the floor! Now, you (the player) have to get the Gettysburg Address back in proper order before the train stops, the game ends, and President Lincoln must give his speech! Good luck!

Lincoln’s Cat is a 36-card in-hand solitaire game featuring the Gettysburg Address. Lincoln’s Cat is played over the course of 8 Rounds. Your goal is to get the Gettysburg Address back in order (from 1-35) before the end of the 8th Round.

—description from the designer

Pumpkin Spice & Everything Nice invites you to relax, unplug, and unwind by enjoying all of the coziness of an Autumn weekend getaway. Walk through the woods while sipping a Pumpkin Spice Latte. Appreciate the Fall Foliage. Stumble across a Sleeping Fox. There’s plenty to enjoy indoors as well. Warm up with a nice Mug of Coffee. Or enjoy a steaming Hot Cup of Tea. There’s scrumptious Pumpkin Roll, fresh from the oven. Smell the Pumpkin Spice in the air. And don’t forget to pet Lola the Cuddly B&B Cat!

In Pumpkin Spice & Everything Nice, you are enjoying an Autumn weekend getaway at the Cape Cozy Bed & Breakfast, the home of the original Pumpkin Spice Latte! During the game, the Cards that you play represent your Weekend, which will include both Outdoor and Indoor Experiences. Each Card has an Outdoor Half and an Indoor Half. 

     On the Outdoor Half of each Card, you will find a mix of Outdoor Experience Icons on top of a background of fallen leaves: Maple Leaf, Sleeping Fox, Pumpkin Spice Latte (PSL), Pumpkin, and Oak Leaf. 
     On the Indoor Half of each Card, you will find a mix of Indoor Experience Icons on top of the Bed & Breakfast’s wooden table (woodgrain background): Hot Cup of Tea (with Cookie), Lola the Cuddly B&B Cat, Mug of Coffee, Pumpkin Roll, and Pumpkin Spice Blend.


Each Card contains a unique combination of Experience Icons. Your goal is to craft the ideal Autumn Weekend, balancing your Outdoor and Indoor Experiences.

To play: Draw and Play the topmost card from the Deck to your play area to Start your Weekend. Then Draw 2 more Cards. Play 1 of these Cards adjacent to each side of the Start Card. You choose which Card goes where. Remember these Cards go next to the Start Card but do not overlap it! Then Draw 2 additional Cards to form your Starting Hand. From this point forwards you will Play 1 Card from your Hand to your Weekend. Each new Card must be played overlapping (at least) one half of an existing Card (or Cards) in your Weekend. You may Play an Outdoor Half over an Indoor Half, an Outdoor Half over an Outdoor Half, an Indoor Half over an Outdoor Half or an Indoor Half over an Indoor Half. You can overlap sections of multiple Cards. You can even overlap an Entire Card! Once you Play a Card, immediately Draw a replacement Card to replenish your Hand. And that’s it! Keep Playing and Drawing Cards until you have played all 18 Cards. Once you Play the 18th Card, your long weekend is over and it’s time to add up your score! At the end of your stay at the Cape Cozy Bed & Breakfast, count the visible Outdoor and Indoor Experience Icons in your Weekend:

     If the separate totals of the Outdoor and Indoor Experience Icons are within 0-3 Icons of each other: You had the ultimate cozy getaway and score both categories! 
     If the separate totals are farther apart, you still had a pretty great Weekend. Score your highest Experience Icon total (Outdoor or Indoor, not both).


—description from the designer
family: Contests: Button Shy Game Design Contests and Challenges

description:
The United Cards of America is a 99-card family game of set collection and shenanigans in honor of America’s 250th birthday. Collect States and complete Regions to earn the most points. Use Actions creatively and watch out for the Government Shutdown!

Playing the game:
On your Turn, you must do 1 and only 1 of the following:

     Play a State Card: Play 1 State Card to your Play Area.
     Play an Action: Play an Action Card from your Hand. Do whatever the Action Card says, then place it face-up on the top of the Action Pile. 
     Take a Card: Take the topmost Card of the State Discard Pile into your Hand. You can only Take from the State Discard Pile you cannot Take from the Action Pile!
     Draw 2 Cards- Keep 1, Discard 1: Draw 2 Cards from the Draw Deck. Place 1 in your Hand (Keep 1) and Discard the other (Discard 1). 


Once you have completed your Turn, gameplay passes clockwise and the next player takes their Turn. 
Play continues until the Draw Deck runs out and the Game Ends. The player with the highest score is the winner!

—description from the designer

Emotional Dice (情绪骰子) is a party game for 3–8 players centered on acting and emotional expression. Players are given scenario prompts and must perform a combination of three randomly assigned emotions, blending them together in their performance. Who will prove to be the master of expression, and who will excel at reading others?

—description from the publisher
publishers: 九张牌玩具公司(9 Cards Toy Company)

description:
The Abyss is a mix of a tempo and combo card game for 2 to 4 players set in the depths of the ocean where every draw is a gamble. On your turn, grab a card from the discard pile or push your luck by drawing two from the deck and keeping one. The only cards that score are the ones safely in your hand, so every combo you play is a sacrifice of points.
Rounds end when a player holding 25 or more points calls "Stop" or only one player is left standing. First to 70+ total points wins the game.

Combos (2 cards of the same type):

     Eyes cards let you look at the cards in the deck 
     Hooks cards force other players (or yourself) to take a risk and draw a card
     Swords cards let you steal cards from another player or gift them one of your Panic cards. 


The real tension comes from Panic cards, dangerous cards that land face-up in front of you the moment they're drawn. Collect three and you're instantly eliminated from the round, scoring 0. There is no way to get rid of them except by sacrificing 15 points' worth of cards from your hand or using a Sword to gift one to an opponent.

So every decision is a trade-off: hoard points and stay quiet or burn points to play combos and mess with your opponents?

—description from the designer

Politike: Manage a country, survive the scandals.

Draw cards, uncover the bad news—and make the right decision?

Politike is a card game where you take on the role of a Head of State, tasked with staying in power for as long as possible while navigating absurd crises, improbable scandals, and diplomatic dilemmas.

Please note: this game is 100% digital—a print-and-play edition.

No board, no dice: just cards, decisions, and—above all—heavy consequences for the game's three pillars: your Popularity, Stability, and Democracy points. Lose them, and you lose the game.

A strategic, satirical, and delightfully wicked party game... yet one that is fully sourced. Every card contains a kernel of truth—whether in the situation described or the solutions offered. Can you figure out where I got the idea?

In this game, you draw various cards, each presenting a political problem that you must resolve. Depending on your choices, you will either gain or lose points.

—Is that it?
—Yes, that’s it! Your mission: maintain Democracy, Popularity, and Stability... without triggering a war, an economic crisis, or an international scandal.
Will you be a visionary strategist, a ruthless opportunist, or simply the one who gets thrown off the train?

Special cards are included to spice up the game; you can choose an initial political context that penalizes your Democracy, Stability, and Popularity (DPS) scores. You can also experiment with different democratic systems through specific mandates (British-style, German-style, etc.), which adds an extra layer of difficulty.

Remember: freedom of speech only wears out when you don't use it.

—description from the designer

Universal Hockey is a fast-paced rule set for getting detailed, minute-by-minute hockey games. This game gives you flexibility as a coach to make strategic decisions behind the bench and let your lines do their work on the ice!

To play, you will need 3 six-sided dice for each player, a pencil, 2 teams and a table/score sheet. A token to show puck possession is helpful but not entirely necessary.

Threemo Robo is an original twist on the classic memory game for the whole family. There are no identical cards at all! Instead, the game offers four difficulty levels, so every player can choose what suits them best.

In this fun board game, your goal is to collect as many sets of “mom, dad, and baby” from families of hardworking robots as possible. Note that each family is grouped not by color, but by shape and function. On their turn, players can reveal 3 to 6 cards, allowing both kids and adults to play at full capacity and still have a fair chance to win.

Threemo Robo helps develop memory, attention, and logical thinking, making it perfect for children aged 3+ and the whole family. All illustrations are created with care and inspiration, and the bright, sturdy box makes it a great gift.

The fourth book in the series of Choose or Lose Adventures. This book also comes with an exclusive card.

Crypts of the Contrarian Cadavers is an old fashioned death dungeon that’s trying to kill you. A cramped, clammy crypt stuffed with necro-nasties in the Lowlands of the Würstreich. You are pushed through a series of deadly situations. Crypts of the Contrarian Cadavers comes with an as yet undisclosed exclusive card for use with DUNGEON DEGENERATES.

Welcome to the wonderful world of unicorns!
Make your fellow unicorns happy to become the best of them all!

Distribute your Gift cards to match your opponents' Taste tiles.
Use your unicorn powers to increase your score.
Move your opponents along the Happiness Track, and aim for the best spaces to collect the most stars!

In this game of strategy and anticipation, for 2 to 5 players, you play as multi-talented musical unicorns whose sole purpose is... to make others happy! But beware: a gift isn't given just any old way... or at any old time!

Distribute gifts wisely, respect the favorite tastes of your opponents (and friends!), advance along the Happiness Track, and become the friendliest unicorn of all!

—description from the publisher

The game is played on a 5x5 spaces board with 25 double sided pieces. The first player takes 13 playing pieces and 2 flip gems and the second player takes 12 playing pieces and 3 flip gems. There are no pieces on the board to start.

On their turn, the player must place a playing piece showing their color on the board on any open space.  Other than being unoccupied, there are no limitations on which space can be used. When placing a piece "sandwiches" an opponent's piece(s) vertically, horizontally or diagonally,  the player flips all sandwiched pieces to their own color.

During their turn, a player can use one of their flip gems instead of placing a piece.   This enables them to turn over any one opponent piece on the board.  If flipping a piece using the gem creates a sandwich of the opponents other piece(s), the player flips all of the sandwiched pieces to their color. Once a flip gem has been played it cannot be used for the remainder of the game.

When both players have finished all of their turns, the game is over. The player who has the most pieces of their color on the board wins the game.

A board game which was produced in Greece in the late 1960s or early 1970s by ERKAP toy company.

It can be played by unlimited number of persons. A leader is chosen who indicates the turn of each player. The leader puts the left plug in the socket which corresponds to one question which is to be told to the players. The first player is to give the correct answer in 30 seconds. In case that he is not able to answer the second player is to do etc.

All answers must be registered. The right answer will be given automatically when the right plug will be put in the socket and in the meantime a lamp lights.

Those who answered correct will get +1 and the others will get -1.
 The winner is the player who will have most points in the end and in case of more that one person, the winner is the player who has answered the questions in the shortest time.

—user summary

Kickstarter super exclusive expansion that contains 1 card and a miniature of the giant wolf from Norse mythology.
This creature can be tamed by a player who is ready to sacrifice cards from his hand. But you can't be a host of the wild wolf for a long period.  
Fenrir is very powerful and can devour any warrior from the opponent's team. After that it is transmitted to his new host.

Flag Rascals is a head to head capture the flag themed strategy board game. Nostalgic, zany, and pure chaos! Build a team of Rascals, hide your flag, and capture your opponent's flag before they capture yours!

Begin the game by drafting your team of Rascals from the 1- available Rascals. Each Rascal has two abilities: their normal ability and their powerful team captain ability, which is reserved for the first Rascal you draft. Then, you will hide your flag tile along with any traps or boosts facedown on your side of the board.

Use your Rascals' unique abilities to move their tokens to explore and reveal your opponent's tiles to find their flag! Once you have found their flag, you'll have to bring it back to the creek in the middle to win the game, using the custom dodge die to avoid being tagged and sent back to your picnic bench.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Scholars of the South Tigris: Promo Starting Translators
description:
A set of 11 promo cards that alternatively replace the original starting translators in Scholars. They provide new retire abilities, some of which utilise the expansion mechanisms from Body of Books.

—description from the publisher
family: Series: The South Tigris Trilogy (Garphill Games)

boardgame: Ezra and Nehemiah: Altar Card Promo
description:
Two new double-sided Altar Cards, providing four fresh scenarios for the judgment of the Prophets.

—description from the publisher

The Great African Migration is underway, and everyone from elephants and snakes to giraffes and gazelle are on the move. In Tembo Tiles, players simultaneously flip hexagon tiles and add them to a shared, ever-growing herd spreading out from a central watering hole.
Each player is assigned an animal and a color. On each flip, a tile must be placed adjacent to a matching color or animal already in the herd. Players build chains strategically while looking for opportunities to break up opponents' progress. Crocodile tiles are placed on top of existing tiles to disrupt chains, and lion tiles act as wilds. With no turn order, everyone plays at once, creating a fast and chaotic scramble. When all 216 tiles have been flipped and placed, the player with the largest connected chains wins.

—description from the publisher

The forest is stripped bare, and rivers have been polluted.  Humans continue to hunt creatures, and ravage the lands.  Now they wield weapons powerful enough to bring down dragons.  In the forest a wounded dragon appears.  Protect the dragon and the forest so they may recover.

Circlora is a cooperative tower defense game, where players take on the role of forest spirits to protect a wounded dragon from humans and help it escape.

Players take turns to select one of the 3 Action Circles to activate the next action on rondel in order to summon and move the golems.  Plan together and move the golems into spaces with humans to defend the area and deal damage. Charge up the dragon's energy, and use your special abilities to hold back the humans.  Use the "Dragon Power" action to gradually heal the dragon.

If you can raise the Dragon's Power to 12 before the humans reach the dragon, you will be victorious in helping the dragon escape.

The second expansion pack for Firefight (Kilovolt Design Chinese version). Includes counters for East Germany and West Germany forces, such as East Germany T-72M and West Germany Leopard I and II.
Also includes a new map (Map C) that can be linked with other maps, and 3 scenarios to put the new counters into action.

Leaning Tower of Pizza is a fast-paced, "deliciously" tense dexterity game where players compete to build the world’s tallest pizza tower! Inspired by the iconic "pizza saver" (the little white tripod found in pizza boxes), this game tests your steady hands and spatial awareness in a race against gravity.

Gameplay Overview
The rules are as simple as ordering a pie, but the execution requires nerves of steel. Players take turns stacking large, mouth-watering pizza slices one by one.

The Catch: You don’t just stack the slices flat—you must use the pizza savers to support and elevate each new layer.

The Challenge: As the tower grows, it becomes increasingly unstable. Every slice added changes the center of gravity, making the "Leaning Tower" more precarious with every move.

Victory & Defeat
There is no room for mistakes in this kitchen!

The Loser: The player who causes the pizza tower to topple immediately loses the game.

The Winner: The player who successfully placed their slice right before the collapse is crowned the master pizza architect!

Inside the Box: 8 Pizza slices, Pizza Savers

Splish, splosh, truck wash, have you seen? Now this truck is nice and clean!
Land on a colour to clean a matching truck in this fun, first game.  Clean all your trucks to win this game.

When it comes to fresh fish, all peace and quiet ends. On the nearby river, two rival animal clans clash - seasoned in every trick in the book and not above using claws, beaks, wings, or any other cunning maneuver. Because only those who claim the fish will secure dominance over the best fishing spot!

On your turn you either play 1 card or draw 2 cards from your deck.

Once you've caught eight fish, you win!

Zombie Overlord is a competitive deck-building card game where players take on the role of powerful undead overlords in a ruined, post-apocalyptic world. Instead of surviving the outbreak, players embrace it, luring desperate survivors from the wasteland and transforming them into loyal minions. Each survivor is drawn by specific resources such as ammo, fuel, food, or medicine, shaping the strategy and identity of every player’s growing horde.

Throughout the game, players build and refine their decks by acquiring trap cards, deploying actions, and managing resources to attract and convert survivors. Once captured, survivors become permanent minions that strengthen a player’s engine and contribute to future turns. Traps can be used not only to lure survivors more effectively, but also to generate supply, disrupt opponents, and provide defensive or offensive advantages. Players must balance efficiency, timing, and interaction as they adapt to an evolving board state.

Direct player interaction is a constant presence, with attacks, defenses, and strategic interference playing a key role in the game’s flow. Players can use their minions and abilities to weaken opponents, bypass defenses, or protect their own position. The combination of deck-building and player-versus-player combat creates a dynamic experience where no two games unfold the same way.

The objective of the game is to reduce your opponents’ life totals to zero while maintaining your own. Each player begins with a set amount of life, and through careful planning, resource management, and tactical aggression, one overlord will rise above the rest to dominate the wasteland.

—description from the designer

You're a skeleton, but you've fallen apart! To put yourself back together, you'll have to drink your opponent's Mojo: the delicious liquid leftovers of their soul.

Headway is a strategic, free-form card-overlapping game for 2, 4, or 6 skeletons. Head to head or in teams, you'll shift your bones around your opponents to get their Mojo before they get yours. Use each bone's unique abilities, such as arms that can throw your other bones, legs that can walk around together, and a skull that can roll tricky distances to outsmart your opponents.

Headway has no board or grid! Cards are placed and oriented freely on the table, but are limited by overlapping your other bones.

The first player or team to drink an opponent's Mojo wins.

—description from the publisher

In hydroelectric competition, authority is built through vision and results.

Barrage: Earned Authority marks a new chapter in the Hydroelectric war. 
After years of competition, some of the most interesting ideas didn't come from the design table alone, they came from the players themselves.
Through a community design challenge, a large number of players submitted their concepts for new Executive Officers.

Sixteen designs made it through playtesting and community review, then went through refinement by Simone Luciani and Tommaso Battista. 
The winning designs now joins the game as a full-fledged addition.

This expansion introduces 16 new Executive Officers, each built around a different approach to water management and energy production. 
The community-designed entries will open new strategic options that will push you to reconsider how you handle dam construction, resource management, and power generation.

These are new XOs with fresh perspectives on rivers and water control, shaped by players who know the game inside and out.

—description from the publisher

All Mine is a mining claim (trick-taking) game in which players predict their hauls of each of the four treasures (gold, gems, mushrooms, and dragon eggs). However, instead of predicting the number of hauls they'll take, they must state the treasures they think they'll receive the most and least of. Since all cards display their treasure type on the back, players have more information than usual.

During each claim (hand), you must play the same treasure type if possible, even with the special trump cards. This requirement makes it difficult to predict which hauls you will bring in. In the end, the player who comes closest to their predictions wins.

Don’t get too greedy as you playfully place your bets in this game guaranteed to please card game enthusiasts and families alike.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Star Trek: Deep Space Nine – Holosuite Homicide
description:
Officially licensed murder mystery game set in the Star Trek world of Deep Space Nine as you try to clear Quark’s name and find clues, confirm alibis, and solve the mystery of the Holosuite Homicide.

STARDATE 49519 // You are a junior investigator with Starfleet Command, called in by Quark, the owner of Quark's Bar, Grill, Games, and Holosuite Arcades on the Promenade of Deep Space Nine. The assignment: Prove Quark's holosuite didn't malfunction and clear his good name. If you prove foul play was involved, what consequences will that have for Constable Odo, Captain Benjamin Sisko, and the entire Deep Space Nine community?

Holosuite Homicide includes:

     Starfleet Command Investigations badge – 2.5" diameter metal pin with dual rubber clutch
     Poster Map with Deep Space Nine on one side and the station's Promenade on the other
     High-quality, immersive handouts and webpages filled with case notes and clues used to solve the objective in each of three game phases
     Starfleet Command Investigations notepad – Ideal for taking notes during the game and long after


—description from the publisher
family: Series: Star Trek Mystery & Escape Room Games (Beadle & Grimm’s)

boardgame: Star Trek: Lower Decks – Chaos Protocol
description:
Officially licensed escape room game set in the Star Trek world of Lower Decks as they uncover a strange and dangerous mystery deep within their starship.

Climb aboard the USS Cerritos for a wild and hilarious escape room experience. Lower Decks: Chaos Protocol is a tabletop escape room game for any number of players. You play the Lower Decks team as they discover a powerful, dormant Federation security system – and then unleash it upon themselves. Defeat a series of clever and increasingly insane puzzles to free Boimler, Rutherford, Mariner, and Tendi from a trap of their own making.

Chaos Protocol includes:

     Cerritos Crew badge
     Full color poster map of the Cerritos
     Ten stages of progressively difficult escape room puzzles
     Narrative elements that make you part of a new Lower Decks story, featuring all of your favorite characters from the show


—description from the publisher
family: Series: Star Trek Mystery & Escape Room Games (Beadle & Grimm’s)

description:
You are the founder of an infant tropical Island Nation that you will build into a great civilization!  Your tasks will be to select the buildings you will construct, manage resources, fight off or convert intruders, and keep the highest surplus of goods to score points! 
Each player, on his turn, will choose one of 5 actions by moving his/her player token to an action space on the main board.  If a player chooses an action another player has chosen on his/her most recent turn, that other player gains a resource card. These resource cards are stacked in face-up piles to the side of the main board.  If a player chooses an action that contains no other player's token, the current player gains an advancement on a bonus track on his/her own player board.
Resource cards are used to facilitate 4 of the 5 actions which are building structures, fending off barbarians, converting barbarians, and exploring.  The other action of gaining cards also accompanies gaining points for the 5th action.
Resources are managed on individual player boards moving up or down sliding tracks based on civilization structures on that player's island. Each structure requires resources to maintain and grants resources produced.
Stacks of structures available to build are tiles stacked to the side of the main board.

—description from the publisher

The Iceberg Sea roars as the Kraken surfaces, the Mandrake folk run in terror as an aberrant monster stalks the Bog, colossal cracks wound the land as the Crystal Tyrant and his army unburrow to conquer once more and the skies divide as Zephyra, the winged judge of all Critters, descends.

The Monsters Unleashed expansion introduces 4 new big threats to Floe each bringing along new and unique experiences. But new dangers await below
the earth as well!

New Heroes, Familiars, Items, Treasures and a host of amazing characters await to join your epic adventures.
In this time of uncertainty, brave Heroes are needed more than ever if the Floe Village is to survive. Will you heed the call?

—description from the publisher

You are a detective trying to determine if super beings in your city are good or evil.  You will surveil them in the act of either criminal activity or helping others.  By this you will know if they are villains or heroes. Your goal is to arrest the villains and hire the heroes. By doing this you will gain points and the player with the most points is the winner.
Tokens are drawn from a bag each round and placed on the main board in 6 locations.  Each token shows a number of resources and number of actions.  For four of these spaces the resources gained depend on the space from which the tokens where taken. They are public support, police co-operation, technology, and money.  These resources allow a player to gain surveillance items and allow them to view super beings activities. The other two spaces allow players to build up their containment facility (for when a villain is arrested) and their team morale (for when a hero is recruited).
The activities are cards placed on a grid of the main board.  If the player views an activity (card) from both the row and column of the card, he/she can keep it secret from the other players.  If the activity is detected from just the row or the column, the player must make it publicly known for all players to see. Each activity also shows a level of severity or generosity depending on if the action is evil or good. By these numbers a player knows how strong his/her detention facility must be to hold an evil being, or how strong his team morale must be to accept a new super hero.

—description from the publisher

You have been alerted to a zombie outbreak and must stop the zombie hoard.  Among the zombies are some well known undead beings that are definitely the hardest to kill.  You will see how much glory and notoriety you can gain as you defeat the hoard or if you are overrun by them.
This is a completely different game than the original Famous Zombies also published by Joe Magic Games.
Each player chooses a main action that aligns with a bonus action grid on the main board.  The two main actions are to kill a zombie or zombies or to scout for resources.  Additional actions may then be purchased by spending supplies. As actions are chosen, zombies and resources are placed on the main board on the map section.  These resources are used to gain special advantage cards, in improve a player's actions (as tracked on the player's individual board) or to gain points as tracked on the main board.  Players gain the ability to use stronger weapons, better methods of travel, and other play advantages like change turn order.
When famous zombies are killed, like zombie Bigfoot, or zombie Robin Hood the player gains bonus points, but the famous zombies are harder to kill in their native terrain than ordinary, every-day zombies.

—description from the publisher

Promo Event card for Snow Planner created in promotion with 14games' game Spur Note.

Penguin: 
If you are the last position on the turn order track, gain the following effect.

boardgame: Snow Planner: Visitor Center Promo Card
description:
Promo Event card for Snow Planner created in promotion with Arclight Games' game Snow Colony.

Visitor Center:
After looking at the next round card, return it face down on top of the deck.

The Kingdom lies teeming with monsters. Goblins guard every village, and animated skeletons and beasts roam the country side. The Fortresses and Mage Towers have hired Elite Knights and summoned  ghastly creatures to isolate themselves, while at the center of it all, the Dark Knights exert their dominion from the Castles. You are a Glyph Knight, a wandering hero who must travel the lands, recruit allies, and master ancient skills to take back the Kingdom.

Glyph Knight is an 18-card game played solely in hand. Your hand of cards acts as both your "map" and your source of actions. Each turn, you’ll plot your journey through the land, generating glyphs to defeat enemies, acquire new skills, recruit allies, and ultimately conquer the Castle to win the game.

Your cards represent both a location, an enemy, and the paths you take to get there. The paths generate the glyphs needed to power your actions, whether that be combat, or diplomacy to try and train more allies. At the end of each journey is your destination, which is the location you interact with, if you can defeat the monster guarding it. When you leave, if you've upgraded the location by training allies there, or learned skills, you get to take them with you to help in the next conflict. But upgrading locations come at a cost, since doing so also empowers the enemies at that location. Glyph Knight is a game of delicate balancing and path optimization, while also introducing skill and ally ability combos to help you overcome the challenges it presents you.

—description from the designer

Agent Black is a social deduction party game for 2 to 6 players.

In this game, players take on the roles of secret agents, each receiving three cards: a Costume, a Place, and an Item. Together, these three cards form your secret identity for the round.

The objective is simple: track down and expose all spies while keeping your own secret identity hidden until the very end.

Will you be the one to survive the mission and earn the prestigious title of "Agent Black"?

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Bloody Badajoz: The Citadel Night Assault
description: Bloody Badajoz - The Third Siege of Badajoz (Mar-Apr 1812) was one of the bloodiest in the Napoleonic Wars. Some 4,800 Allied soldiers (under Wellington) were killed or wounded in just a few hours of intense fighting on April 6th while storming the breaches of the citadel at Badajoz. In the aftermath, the victorious and blood-crazed troops ran amok inflicting additional terrors on the surviving defenders and civilians.

Welcome to Yangste Incident a solitaire naval wargame that places you in command of HMS Amethyst during her time trapped on the Yangtse River and her escape to Shanghai in 1949.

In this solitaire game, you assume the role of the commanding officer of HMS Amethyst. You will lead the ship’s company as you recover from the initial damage and grounding, attempt repairs and other preparations for a breakout, and then command the ship as she makes her escape to Shanghai and safety.

The game comprises three distinct phases.


     In Phase 1 the effects of the initial Chinese bombardment are resolved, establishing the starting conditions for the rest of the game.



     Phase 2 sees you preparing the ship for breakout, perhaps recovering injured crew or receiving replacements thanks to the RAF, repairing damaged weapons and the ship’s engines, obtaining extra fuel from reserves at Nanking,



     Phase 3 is the critical phase of the game, where you attempt to break out to Shanghai and safety, running the Chinese batteries and forts, and negotiating a treacherous boom across the river. Victory is by no means assured as the batteries pose a real threat, and Amathyst may succumb to gunfire and grounding damage.


—description from the publisher

boardgame: あてっこどうぶつ (Guess the Animal)
description:
Use two "item cards" to communicate your "animal card" to other players.

Choose cards that seem to relate to the animal or scenery depicted on your "animal card."

If you successfully communicate, both you and the person who guessed correctly earn points. The player with the most points at the end wins.

How to Play:

     Each player is assigned an "animal card" as their theme.
     Choose 2 from the 15 "item cards" on the table that seem to relate to your "animal card."
     Each player tries to guess the other players' "animal cards." If correct, both you and the person who was guessed earn points! The player with the most points at the end of the game wins.


—description from the publisher

Surrounded! is a solitaire, print and play modern tank wargame.

As a commander of a Ukrainian T-64BV, its up to you to defend your outpost until reinforcements arrive. 
You're not alone. 
Ukrainian Infantry and Engineer units will help you defend your position against an onslaught of Russian mechanized infantry and armor units. 
Name your crew, manage your tank and available units. 
Are you up to the task?

—description from the publisher

boardgame: 絵くそ・シスト ψ (Horrible Artist Exorcist)
description:
The active player spins a roulette to determine what cards are drawn – an animal card, and a number of transmutation cards.

They then draw on the dry-erase board with his eyes closed, the transmutated animal.

Other players look at the picture drawn, and guesses what cards were drawn.

Set contents: 
Devil card – 20 cards
spell cards – 20 cards
Sheet – 1
Board – 1 
roulette – 1
pen – 1

Can’t Win is a trick-taking game where you must guess exactly how many times you’ll win, embellished with a twist at the start of each round: You also have to choose a constraint for yourself that limits which cards you are able to win tricks with. The constraint you choose can work for you—or against you—in matching the number of tricks you actually win to the number you bid you would win. At the end of every round, you’ll record the points you earn for your wins and matching bids.
