Your final score is calculated at the end of the game by multiplying your total wins with the points you score by bidding correctly.

—description from the publisher

Iron Quest: The First Ember is a Viking adventure roll-and-write game for 1-4 player. To expand the kingdom's reach and secure its legacy, the King has commanded his bravest captains to sail into the North Sea. In Iron Quest: The First Ember, players take on the role  of a Viking captain, navigating these uncharted waters to settle new lands and protect your people from the shadows of the north.

In Iron Quest: The First Ember, players compete to become the most glorious Captain by fulfilling the King’s Orders and earning the most Glory Points (GP). Over 15 rounds, players will recruit Crew, establish Settlements, defeat Raiders and light the Ancient Beacons. At the end of the game, the player with the most Glory Points wins the game.

Will you be crowned a Sea King or lost in the deep?

—description from the publisher

Sushi Go! Party Palooza is a fast-paced card drafting and set collection game for 2–8 players. Over three rounds, players pick and pass hands of cards, selecting sushi dishes to build the most delicious (and high-scoring) meal. Different combinations score in unique ways — stack tempura, collect sashimi sets, dip nigiri in wasabi, and save room for dessert!

With a customizable menu of over 20 sushi types, each game offers fresh strategies and endless replayability. It's easy to learn, quick to play, and absolutely soy much fun!

Compatible with Sushi Go Party!

boardgame: At the Gates of Loyang: Second Edition
description:
The classic economic strategy game from acclaimed designer Uwe Rosenberg, At the Gates of Loyang, is back with a brand new second edition. This game rewards careful planning, clever trading, and tactical use of resources.

China, 2000 years ago. Loyang (Luòyáng) capital of the Han Dynasty, flourishes as one of the great centers of ancient civilization. The growing city hungers for fresh food, and local farmers rise to the challenge - cultivating wheat, pumpkins, turnips, cabbages, beans, and leeks in the fertile fields surrounding the city walls. After each harvest, they gather at the gates of Loyang to sell, trade, and expand their farms, vying to become the most prosperous farmer.

This second edition includes an all new cooperative mode by Ralph Bruhn and ode. In the cooperative mode you are asked to provide the City Festival with vegetables, and to fulfill special requests for certain customers. You are confident you can deliver vegetables, serve customers, collect donations . . . and still assure your own prosperity. But can you keep your promises? Work together as a team to try to manage all the appointed tasks.

Operation Mars is a tabletop wargame that captures both mobile defense and frontline breakthroughs. You take on the role of the commander directing this fierce battle, leading your forces to victory.
This is an intense, high-stakes tug-of-war between attackers and defenders. You must not only withstand the enemy’s assaults but also preserve your strength to hold hard-won positions.
Every unit under your command is vital, and you must make the most of their capabilities. Only the most skillful commander will prevail—do not let any chance to destroy the enemy slip away!

—description from the designer

One Piece Legendary is a deckbulding game for 1 to 5 players, based on concept of Legendary, but with a campaingn mode through of the story of Eiichiro Oda since Romance Down up to the final of this series (Not yet published).

This game have new innovations like Shops and free game mode, besides include a "What if" mode to the campaign mode.

Defeat the Enemies Leader and find the One Piece.

•••

One Piece Legendary es un juego deckbulding de 1 a 5 jugadores, basado en el concepto de Legendary pero donde se ha generado una campaña continuada que aspira a recrear la serie de Eiichiro Oda desde sus inicios hasta el último de sus arcos aún no publicado.

El juego además posee mecánicas innovadoras como los barcos y un modo de juego libre, además de poder jugar la campaña con un modo "What if".

Derrota al Líder Enemigo para ganar y encontrar el One Piece.

Battle of Dice is a fast-paced dice game for 2–4 players.

Players compete to capture areas on the board by rolling dice and making strategic decisions. Each turn involves choosing between safe plays and risky moves.

The game features simple rules, short playtime, and dynamic interactions between players.

Suitable for both casual and competitive play.

—description from the publisher

Octa – The Game of 8 is a tactical card game for two players, played with 2 standard decks of cards.

Players build runs of ascending cards in the same suit, aiming to create an “Octa” — a run of eight or more cards — while also reaching a total of at least 28 cards in runs on the table. Focusing on a single long run is not enough; players must balance expansion and timing to win.

The game features a shared pool of cards and a set of action cards (Jacks, Queens, and Kings) that introduce interaction and disruption. These action cards are not always available: they only trigger when the two visible action cards match in suit. This creates moments of tension, as players anticipate whether an action will occur or not.

Octa combines planning and uncertainty. Players can influence the game state, but never fully control it. The result is a dynamic back-and-forth, where early advantages do not guarantee victory and comeback opportunities remain throughout the game.

Because Octa uses a standard deck, it is easy to set up and play anywhere, while still offering a surprising level of depth.

Savannah animal tiles laying game on 4 playground tiles.

Whoever strategically eats the most animals and generates the most points.

Preparation:
The starting player is the one who roars like a lion first, otherwise the older player.
The game board is placed between the players with both sides facing out. The corresponding water sources are placed to the right and left of the game board.
Each player receives a different color of animal tiles, and the starting player places their first tile.

Gameplay:
The player whose turn it is either places a tile from their supply onto the game board or he moves one of his already placed tiles on the game board according to the rules.
The opponent's eaten tiles are claimed by the player and used for the final scoring.

End of game:
The game ends immediately if one player has no more tiles or if the other player has eaten all of their opponent's green tiles.
The game can also end if the opponent's gorilla is immobilized, meaning it can no longer move in any direction. The gorilla must, of course, have been placed on the game board. In this case, scoring proceeds normally, or in the event of a tie, the player who immobilized the gorilla wins.

Twelve animal families are waiting for their happy ending. They’ve lost sight of each other and want to be reunited. Depending on the game variant, the memory tiles are shuffled well and placed face down in a grid. In the easier version, the starting tiles of each animal family – the ones showing exactly one animal – are sorted out beforehand and distributed to the players. This way, everyone already knows at the beginning which animals they are looking for.

Now the game begins: The youngest player starts and flips over any tile. If it shows an animal that belongs to one of their families, and it also has the correct number of birds, fish, and other creatures on it – increasing from one to four – they may place it on the corresponding stack and immediately take another turn. If that’s not the case, the tile is turned face down again and the next player continues. Whoever completes all their animal families first wins and ends the game.

In the more challenging variant, the animal families are not assigned. The goal is again to find all four tiles of a species in the correct order and stack them. Whoever finds the fourth and final animal may take the entire family and scores a point. Once all families have been reunited, the player with the most stacks in front of them wins.

—description from the designer

Dyrefighten ("Animal Fight") is a fun children's game that only takes 2 minutes to learn and can therefore be played by the whole family. Before the game starts, each participant is given 7 tokens, which the task is simply to get rid of. It sounds simple and easy - but be careful that your opponents have not suddenly blocked your opportunities to get rid of them.

On a players turn they roll the 8-sided die. If the die come up with an animal, they must place one of their tokens on that animal. If there is already a token on the animal, they must instead take that token instead. If the die comes up with the logo, they must place one of their tokens on the logo (there can be any number of tokens on the logo).

First player to get rid of their tokens wins the game.

Little Dot Garden is a peaceful place where nature lives in balance. But something has gone wrong, and it’s up to you to restore it.

In this tile-laying game, you will build your own garden by placing tiles and helping it grow back to life. Along the way, you’ll also decide the prices of flowers on the market, shaping how your garden develops and how you score points.

Work alongside the tiny guardian, the Golden Beetle, to bring harmony back and turn your garden into a beautiful, thriving place again.

Pažink Rietavo kraštą is an engaging and educational board game designed for the whole family. It invites players of all ages to embark on an exciting journey through one of the most culturally rich corners of Lithuania - the Rietavas region.

As you travel across the board, you will test your knowledge and learn new facts about the Rietavas region's history, culture and notable personalities. In addition, players can enjoy an entertaining building search activity on the game board, spotting and identifying different landmarks along the way.

Through interactive gameplay, players are encouraged to think, learn, and discover together. Whether you are answering questions, completing challenges, or searching for buildings, every step offers new knowledge and engaging experiences.

Sūrio kelias is a board game reflecting the century-long history of the company AB "Rokiškio sūris". This lively strategy board game introduces the stages of cheese production and allows players to feel like true professionals in the dairy processing industry-developing their own factory, expanding export markets, and modernizing processes.

—description from the publisher

The Ties That Bind Us is a fully narrated, cooperative electronic horror game for 1–3 players. There are no cards, dice, or moving pieces. The entire game runs through a custom-built circuit board. LEDs track every player and every creature in real time, and audio chips narrate every moment of the experience through the voice of Selah Wren, a soul bound to the attic who sees everything...

Three characters wake in a four-level house with 41 rooms and no memory of who they are or where they are. Eleanor the Caretaker, Grant the Soldier, Julian the Artist are the stars of this game. The doors are sealed. The windows won't break. Outside is nothing but grey. Something moves through the walls. It remembers them.

Players explore, scavenge for weapons and items, manage noise levels, and survive three distinct threats: the Shape That Remembers (relentless and unkillable), the Husks (what rises when a player dies), and the Wailing (what rises when a player loses their mind). Each player tracks three resources: HP, Sanity, and Sin. All three can end you. Sin is especially dangerous. Using darkness to fight is effective, but every use grows your sin, and if it climbs too high, the house simply absorbs you.
There is no healing. Combat is a last resort. Stealth is survival. Multiple endings, good, bad, and secret, are determined by what you remember, what you chose, and the final judgment of Selah Wren in the Altar of Judgment.

—description from the designer

Habitopia: Insects is an 8-card expansion for Habitopia featuring 8 insect cards.

When you play, you can replace the "C"-cards from the base game with these new "F"-cards, introducing new scoring opportunities to further modify the gameplay.

This game is the third installment in a trilogy supplement to be played using the counters from La Bataille d’Eggmühl 1809 as published by Marshal Enterprises in 2024.
The package includes:


     A new map, supplemental 
     28-page special rules
     information about formations and special situations


Other information associated with the Thann 1809 campaign is located on the Marshal Enterprises website.

Per the designers:

The Thann 1809 game recreates the April 19, 1809 battle during which Austrian Archduke Charles attempts to isolate and destroy Marshal Davout’s III Corps before Napoleon arrives, along with substantial reinforcements, at the Bavarian front just two days prior to the Battle of Eggmühl.  Tabbed as a meeting engagement battle, both the French and a greatly strengthened Austrian Armee fought an intense all-day battle only ended by a torrential rainstorm.

The size of the Thann map is 24 inches high and 36 inches wide...Thann 1809 can be played by itself or together with La Bataille de Dünzing 1809, another of the trilogy downloads.  Thann 1809 and Dünzling 1809, along with Rohr 1809, make up the pre-Eggmühl 1809 trilogy.

In Mota Duel, two growers go head-to-head with their buds in a showdown to see who has the finest harvest.

Throughout the match, you’ll have to decide where to play your Bud cards, how to leverage support between columns, and when to trigger special powers. Only the player who best optimizes their plants' yield to secure the top prizes will emerge victorious, racking up more victory points than their rival.

boardgame: Arcs: Lost Vaults & Fated Leaders Expansion
description:
The Founders left us bereft and alone. Why do they speak only in poetry and paradox? They knew so much, and we know so little. Would they be proud of their children? Ashamed? Perhaps they never cared. Perhaps the torch we carry was never meant to be lit.

The Lost Vaults & Fated Leaders expansion introduces a wealth of new content to the base game of Arcs.

Discover a bevy of new leaders, including the new fated leaders. Each fated leader comes with special pieces that alter core rules of the game, like a distilled version of their campaign counterpart. As God’s Hand, add a whole new suit of action cards, the faithful. Or, as the Firebrand, offer players a new way of seizing the initiative. The fated leaders are fully compatible with the existing pool of leaders and well-suited to competitive or casual play.

Through the expanded setup, you can now generate a unique map each time you play, including a totally new feature: the Lost Vaults. Floating silent for generations, these vast edifices can be awakened to gain tactical advantages. Use the Bastion to defend a critical system, travel along the Stone Road to previously inaccessible planets, or explore the Ruins to find catastrophically powerful artifacts.

These advantages may come in handy with the arrival of a fifth player to the Reach. To help you navigate the froth and chaos of five-player games, we're even adding a new type of action card. Finally, you'll find a whole new court deck of never-before-seen cards, dramatically changing the strategies available to you.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Arcs: Beyond the Reach Campaign Expansion
description:
Beyond the Reach introduces strangers from beyond normal space and time. Gather your forces for a full-scale invasion with the Invader, or slip into broken worlds to search for lost artifacts as the Scavenger. Some Fates will also use a new mechanism: the secret agenda. These agendas will determine how their Fate develops. Is the stranger from another world the true Successor, as they claim, or are they just a Pretender looking to take advantage of chaos in the Reach?

You must own the Blighted Reach Expansion to use this.

—description from the publisher

Party & Protest is a worker placement game about the 1970's Black Power Movement. Players play different factions including the Saints, the Press, the Militants, the Pretty People and the Visionaries working to get the city working for all the people and trying to secure the best legacy.

Party & Protest is a 2 to 5 player semi-coop where players play against COINTELPRO and each other. It's for players 14 and up and takes 60-90 minutes.

—description from the publisher

Halls of Power dramatically widens Arcs’s political game. Manipulate the power brokers and revolutionaries with the cunning Socialite, or reimagine the Empire's political system with the Reformer. Players will find new tools of negotiation and points of leverage.

You must own the Blighted Reach Expansion to use this.

—description from the publisher

Demon's Manor introduces a new mechanic: dungeon crawling with a twenty-nine card Dungeon deck to create a variable map that will lead to five boss fights. A ten-card story deck the provides a round-long effect, and seven Undead denizens that are continually resurrected by their masters. For solo play one begins with the Relic card that has an ongoing bonus.

Midsummer is a competitive card game about crafting flower crowns for iconic characters from classic Swedish Midsummer songs. These characters include: the baker, the chimney sweeper, the three old ladies, the little frogs and pigs, the three musicians from Skaraborg, Andersson, Petterson, Lundström, and even “you.”

The goal of the game is to claim these characters by crafting their flower crowns.

The characters each request a specific combination of flowers gathered from the meadow. There are always five characters and five flowers in the play area.

Players take turns interacting with the communal meadow, which contains five flower cards. On your turn, you may play flowers into the meadow (or sometimes pick from the meadow) to work toward completing one of the characters’ requested crowns. If, at the end of your turn, all required flowers for a character are present in the meadow, you may claim that character for points.

A more precise arrangement earns extra points. If the flowers in the meadow match the exact pattern shown on a character card, you may, in addition to claiming that character card, also tuck a flower card under your pillow according to Midsummer tradition. (The legend says that by tucking seven unique flowers under your pillow on Midsummer night, you will dream of your future spouse.)

The game ends when the flower deck is depleted. Players then total the points from the characters they claimed and the number of unique flowers under their pillow. The player with the highest score wins.

Midsummer is the second game in the Swedish Holidays series; the first game celebrates the festive holiday “Lucia.”

—description from the publisher

The apothecary is filled with the aromas of herbs and bubbling bottles.

Outside, people are lined up seeking relief, and the apothecary needs someone to mix the right ingredients without wasting time.

Observe, combine, and prepare the precise remedies: the first to use all of their ingredients and empty their hand will be the one to keep the city safe.

Oona's Garden provides a second dungeon crawl campaign, with a twenty-nine card Dungeon deck to create variable maps that end in five boss fights. A ten-card Story deck that provides a round-long effect, and a seven-card Curse deck triggered by dungeon cards. For solo play one gets a Relic card that has an ongoing bonus.

Ludus Fatidicus is a two-player strategy and bidding game played with the full tarot deck. Players compete over the Minor Arcana cards to score points while managing limited information and anticipating their opponent’s moves. Major Arcana cards provide unique powers that can alter bids, predictions, or scoring conditions. The game combines tactical decision-making with elements of deduction and hand management in short, tense duels.

—description from the designer

El Chou is a dynamic party game inspired by the variety and talent show television formats. Players must compete in a series of eclectic challenges designed to test their creativity, performance skills, and quick thinking. The game features multiple categories of trials, including mimicry (pantomime), singing, general knowledge trivia, and physical dexterity tests.

As players move across the board, they must successfully complete these diverse assignments to earn points and advance. The game emphasizes social interaction and humor, aiming to recreate the high-energy atmosphere of a live TV broadcast. The winner is the player who best handles the variety of challenges and reaches the end of the show as the top entertainer.

boardgame: The Great Sea: Alternate Combat Cards
description:
Originally released through the 2026 Kickstarter campaign for The Great Sea.

Included are 32 Combat Cards (8 per player). With them come rules for how to use the new cards, including ways to combine them with those from the base game.

These were originally released during the 2026 Kickstarter for Towers of Sifnos.

Included are 12 new cards which get shuffled into the main draw pile. Unlike most cards that stack above action slots, these are placed below specific buildings. Once those buildings have been filled with dice, these new cards provide their strong benefits.

Agents & Advisors adds exciting new ways to build and defend your empire, with 16 Agents and 20 Advisors ready to do your bidding for a price, as well as several brand new Ventures (5), Favours (7) and Skeletons (6).

Agents add powerful one off actions and Advisors provide either passive benefits or additional actions whilst you employ them.

The expansion is a seamless addition and can even be added in a first playthrough.

—description from the publisher

Squelze is a "flicking" game that combines precision with strategy. 
Played on a tabletop, this team game involves flicking wooden pucks as close as possible to a central metal peg. 
While its mechanics might bring to mind a sort of tabletop pétanque, Squelze is actually much more than that: a single rule changes everything! 
If a puck falls off the table, it is eliminated—not just for the current round, but for the next one as well! 
The result? With every turn, you must choose between positioning your own puck or attempting to knock your opponent off the table. The game pieces are designed to allow for everything from magnificent glides to spectacular misses, from sneaky low blows to, above all, great victories.

The icing on the cake is that Squelze is accessible to players of all ages (6 and up), making it your new favorite game for any occasion: family gatherings, game nights with friends, or even team-building events!

Squelze is a team-based game played around a table. Players from both teams are seated in alternating positions around the table. You can play in teams of 2, 3, or even 4 people.

The player starting the game uses a flick of the finger to place their puck as close as possible to the central marker. The player to their left—a member of the opposing team—then has two choices: attempt to place their own puck even better (closer to the central marker, in a more sheltered position, etc.) or try to knock the opposing puck away or off the table. Indeed, if a puck falls off the table, it is set aside and no longer counts for the current round... nor for the following one! This constitutes a significant disadvantage for that player's team. It is a short-lived handicap, however, as out-of-play pucks are reintroduced into the game after one round. But be careful: an offensive strategy carries risks... are you sure your aim is precise enough?

Players continue taking turns in this manner until everyone has played all of their pucks. The round then concludes, and the team with the puck closest to the central marker scores points equal to the number of their pucks positioned closer to the marker than the opposing team's closest puck (much like in pétanque or palet breton).

The next round begins. Each player retrieves the pucks they still have on the table (those that did not fall off during the previous round). Starting with Round 3, any pucks that fell off during the round *prior* to the one just completed are also reintroduced into the game—potentially helping to level the playing field!

A game of Squelze is played to 15 points; the first team to reach this total wins the match! However, you are free to set a different target score for shorter—or longer—games!

Fight Me, Bro! is a quirky, trick-taking game for 2-4 players. Six original characters who have the superpower of creating multiples of themselves are going head to head, in an epic street brawl, and you're along for the ride.

During play, your goal is to collect cards in an effort to make your play into an upcoming trick the strongest possible, when it is ultimately triggered by a "Fight Me, Bro!" card. The more multiples of a character you have, and the greater the power rating of those cards, the better chance you have to take the trick, and get ahead. Be the first to win 3 tricks, and you win the rumble!

boardgame: Unstable Explosives: Fire Power Party Expansion
description: This twelve-card expansion adds more bombs, each with a unique effect, such as the Fireworks that allows you to shuffle one of your stacks and distribute it to other players, or the Dud to cancel out a Detonate card.

Brain Golfing is a pocket-sized pen-and-paper strategy game. The format is a small Wire-O-bound DIN A7 booklet. No dice required, the game uses a fixed number strip, giving the player full information to plan their path across each hole.

Each booklet contains 90 individually designed holes across five courses. The playing field is a hex grid map with terrain types including water, sand, trees, hills, wind fields, and portals, each with distinct mechanical effects. The player has access to five power-ups that can change the dice or alter the path making the game even more engaging.

The goal is simple: move the ball to the hole in as few strokes as possible and beat the target score. Brain Golfing supports solo play as well as head-to-head and group modes, all within a single shared booklet.

—description from the designer

Assemble! is a fast-paced, team-building card game where players take on the role of a strategist assembling the ultimate team of heroes inspired by classic archetypes across fandom. From fearless leaders to brilliant tacticians, heavy hitters, and wildcards, each hero brings a distinct role to your team. The fun comes from combining these familiar archetypes in new and unexpected ways.

On your turn, you’ll draft, play, and position cards to build your team while watching what your opponents are assembling. Each hero you add can unlock new abilities or enhance others, creating a web of interactions that grows as the game progresses.

The core of the game is synergy. Some archetypes naturally complement each other, forming powerful combinations, while others disrupt opposing teams or create unexpected advantages. Success comes from recognizing these patterns and adapting your strategy as the table evolves.

To win, you’ll need to assemble the most effective team by the end of the game. Whether that means maximizing powerful combinations, completing key archetype groupings, or outmaneuvering your opponents at the right moment, the player who builds the strongest team wins.

—description from the publisher

JumbleJack is a high-energy deduction party game wrapped in a cursed pirate ritual. Each player is a member of a villainous pirate crew with a hidden identity determined at the start of the game. Every crew member has a unique combination of Hat, Hook, Cape, and Sword. The goal is to dress the JumbleJack to match the player it is handed to, and hope the curse doesn’t land on you.

The player holding the JumbleJack Doll is safe from accusation. Each round, players debate, accuse, and read each other while tracking who has what. When the table is ready, everyone chants “JUM… BLE… JACK!” and points to decide who will face the ritual.

The chosen player receives the Doll, and the current holder may add or remove one accessory before passing it along. The target must reveal just enough information to survive, but cannot lie about what is shown. Bluffing, memory, and timing all shape the outcome.

If the Doll matches a player’s secret identity exactly, they are cursed and lose, while everyone else survives. To win, avoid being the match. If the game reaches its final ritual, everything comes down to one last call where one player survives… or everyone else does.

—description from the publisher

Flip a coin to determine turn order or do a puck drop, and then the winning player starts by flicking the puck (or ball) with your little hockey stick to try and score a goal. The opponent flicks from where the puck was left, and so on until one of the two opponents scores a goal. When a goal is scored, the opponent who has conceded a goal has to remove one of their players and kick off from the point in front of their goal. The player who has managed to leave the opponent without any players wins. Players are also goalkeepers, attempting to stop shots with their stick. The final shot must also be a rebound.

Word Wipe is a fast, cooperative, real-time word association game where players work together to clear a growing spread of cards before time runs out. Each table card represents a noun concept, and players must think creatively to connect them through a single shared idea.

At any moment, a player can attempt a “word wipe” by choosing a secret word that links their hidden card with one or more cards on the table. They reveal their card, group the cards they are connecting with their clue, and flip a sand timer while the rest of the players race to guess the exact secret word. You can give clues by arranging or tapping cards, or by tapping a Hint Card to show if your word is a being, place, or thing, but you cannot speak.

The gameplay is chaotic, collaborative, and driven by quick thinking. Players are constantly scanning the table, forming connections, and deciding when to take their shot. Successful guesses remove cards from play, while missed attempts add more cards, increasing the pressure for the whole group.

To win, your team must clear all the table cards before time runs out. Every correct guess brings you closer, but every mistake makes the puzzle harder. Stay sharp, think creatively, and wipe the board before time runs out.

—description from the publisher

As the waters warm, the Flat and Round Crabs of Purple Palm Island prepare to make their way inland to their customary stomping grounds. As is the tradition, the crabs will fight for space on the island and the species that claims more territory wins bragging rights! (And more coconuts)

Crabbage is a two-player area majority game that utilizes cribbage as its engine of play. Each player will represent one of the two crab species: Round or Flat. Over six deals of Cribbage, players will move, deploy, push, and dominate the limited space on the island.

Those familiar with cribbage will be delighted to know the card play of Crabbage is identical to the timeless classic. Each deal consists of Play, Hand, and Crib scoring. Crabbage is different in that points earned are used to control areas on the map, leading to interesting choices with each hand and in every situation.

—description from the designer

No No Puppy! is a bluffing card game where adorable puppies create lighthearted chaos. Players take turns playing cards face down onto shared stacks and declaring their values, which may or may not be truthful. At any moment, another player can call “No No Puppy!” to challenge a bluff—correct calls earn points, while failed ones reward the active player. As the stacks grow, players try to keep the numbers in order, though bluffing is always allowed. Points come from successful bluffs, well-timed calls, and building up your own stack, and the player with the most points at the end wins.

—description from the publisher

Exploration on contains 160 cards, counts as 1 expansion for up to 2 kingdoms (since there is so much to explore, 2 different kingdoms may use the same expansion set of cards. This is not required, just a possibility). It will offer almost endless exploration, even if you take it as your first expansion, and it is the richest of all expansions if you want more cards and grow your kingdom size. Note, though, that it is quite expensive in terms of resources needed to do well.

Nile 2500 BC is a family-oriented strategic archaeological race where 2–4 players compete to explore ancient pyramids along the Egyptian river. Starting from the Nile Delta, explorers must first secure a permit at the Cairo Embassy and manage a hidden hand of 7 resource tokens (Torches and Dynamite) to overcome the specific difficulty levels of each tomb.

Gameplay balances risk and resource management. Movement is dictated by die rolls, modified by the river's current: traveling North grants a +1 bonus, while heading South imposes a -1 penalty. Players must navigate these waters carefully to avoid crocodiles and hippos that destroy equipment, while deciding whether to spend time in Cairo perfecting their supplies or rushing to claim smaller pyramids before their rivals.

To explore a pyramid, players combine their Torches, Dynamite, and a die roll to beat the pyramid's level. Success grants a high-value Insignia Card, but a tie results in losing a resource, and a failure forces a total disaster—losing all tokens and retreating to Cairo to resupply. An exact roll is not required to land on a pyramid, allowing for faster tactical strikes.

The game ends once all pyramids on the board have been explored. Players tally the points on their collected Insignia Cards, and the archaeologist with the highest total prestige score is declared the winner. Balancing speed with preparation is the key to mastering the Nile.

—description from the designer

Among 12 is a social deduction game set among a crowd of 5000, miraculously fed from a boy’s lunch, and leaving twelve baskets behind. Now, the mission is clear - do not waste what remains, and share the miracle. But not everyone agrees. Some seek to silence the message and keep the truth from spreading.

Each player is secretly assigned one of three roles. Some work to ensure the baskets are shared, carefully observing and reasoning through others’ actions. Others aim to create confusion, disrupt trust, and waste the baskets without being revealed. A few thrive in uncertainty, benefiting when balance is maintained. Your identity is hidden, but your choices and how you justify them, shape how others see you.

The game unfolds over multiple rounds. In each round, players build trust by sharing bread, influence decisions, and take part in missions where baskets are filled in secret. Bread and Rat cards determine whether a basket is shared or wasted, tugging the truth to either side. But outcomes alone don’t reveal who is responsible. Players must observe behavior, question motives, and interpret subtle clues to understand who is working toward which goal.

The game ends when 12 baskets have been used. If enough baskets are shared, Disciples win. If too many are wasted, Pharisees win. If the result is balanced, Citizens win. To succeed, you must not only play your role well, but also understand others and decide who to trust before it’s too late.

—description from the designer

This stand-alone expansion takes Hitster to a whole new level. Players can now choose their favorites from three legendary music eras: vinyl classics (before 1985), cassette and CD hits (1986–2005), and streaming songs (2006–2025). The first player to lay 10 cards in the correct order wins the title of “HITSTER Champion.”

You are in charge of a travel agency in the Entremonde land. To attract tourists and outshine the competition, you will build unusual mansions by placing your cards and cleverly arranging the rooms!
Each turn, choose cards for you and your neighbors, place them in your manor, linked to the main entrance by stairs and ladders.
But keep in mind the curious demands of the architect, the decorator, and the mystery guests to ensure your visitors leave positive reviews on Tok Tok Tok and Instacrame. Be careful, as negative reviews will damage your reputation.

—description from the publisher
A quick and easy card game for all ages. Search the mine for your color and shape. Find carts to help you traverse the mine quicker. Be the first to find all your gems to win!

KAPOW! is a rummy-style game, with a twist. A player's hand starts with a 4 x 3 matrix of face-down cards. Each group of three cards is referred to as a triad. Players seek to build runs and sets by selecting cards from a draw or discard pile. The twist: the deck includes KAPOW wild cards, that can be moved from triad to triad, as needed. The deck also includes Power Cards, that can be used to raise or lower the value of face up cards as needed to complete a run or set. Completed triads are discarded. After 10 rounds, the player with the lowest score wins.

Lucky Undies is the family card game where strategy, sabotage, and dirty undies collide!

Lucky Undies is a fast-paced shedding card game for 2–5 players where everyone races to get rid of all their cards first.

To play, each player is dealt 21 cards (using one deck for 2 players or both decks shuffled together for 3–5). Without looking, everyone sets out 4 face-down Undies in a row, creates a Personal Draw Pile of 10 cards, and looks at the remaining 7 cards in hand. Players then choose 4 of those hand cards to become their Covers, which sit face-up on top of the Undies. This leaves everyone with exactly 3 cards in hand to start.

On your turn you must play one or more matching cards from your hand that equal or beat the top card on the central Discard Pile. If you can’t play, you pick up the entire Discard Pile and add it to your hand (beginners can optionally take just the top 4 cards), then immediately play any card to start a new pile. After playing, you always draw from your Personal Draw Pile until you have 3 cards in hand again. Once your hand and draw pile are both empty, you start playing your Covers, and finally flip your hidden Undies one at a time, hoping they’re lucky enough to match or beat the current top card.

Four powerful Wild cards add the perfect dose of mischief and strategy: RESET lets the next player play anything while keeping the pile in play, CLEAR removes the entire Discard Pile so the next player can play freely, BRING A FRIEND adds an extra card of any value, and ATTACK forces another player to take the whole pile (they can defend with their own Attack or a Clear). If four or more identical cards are played in a row, the Discard Pile is removed from the game entirely. The first player to shed every last card; hand, draw pile, Covers, and all their Undies, is crowned the Lucky Undies Champion!

—description from the publisher

Welcome to the world's hottest game! 
Aim for 100 degrees – the first to get there wins and becomes the undisputed champion of the sauna!

How to play:

In the middle of the table, a sauna board is placed, along with the deck of cards and five dice that have been thrown at the beginning of the round. At the start of the game, all players get six cards each and their own sauna thermometer.

To win the game, a player must reach 100 degrees Celsius on their sauna thermometer. To raise the temperature of the sauna, the sauna owner must match the cards in their hand to the dice on the game board.

All five cards must have the same symbol as the dice.

When the sauna owner has five correct cards, they shout out loud: “ONE, TWO, THREE, SAUNA!” The round then ends immediately. The winner of the round adds 20 degrees to their thermometer, and the next round starts.
language_dependency: 2: Some necessary text - easily memorized or small crib sheet

description:
An alien armada surrounds a peaceful explorer class spaceship in deep space. Can its crew render repairs while under phaser fire and complete their escape?

Captain Down is a 1 to 4 player cooperative game with solo play. Playing as a member of the crew, will you be able to work together and save your ship from destruction?

Your explorer class spaceship has four main system rooms connected by multiple corridors to traverse. The immediate mission is to charge the power, phasers, shields and atmosphere gauges to maximum. Aiding the crew are the ship's sensors, teleportation jumps, oxygen deployment, power transfers, medical facilities and their own unique training.

Phaser fire however is randomly damaging areas of the ship causing a temporary loss of vital systems. Do you attempt the mission steadily or take strategic risks to ensure your timely escape?

—description from the designer

boardgame: Blood Bowl (Third Season Edition): Spike! Journal #21
description:
High Elves are a truly fearsome force on the pitch – discipline, flawless passing, and resilience set them apart. Every throw and play is a masterclass in teamwork and finesse. They hold their ground and strike with deadly accuracy, blending strength and skill like no other team.

Guest editor Alandrian the Exiled, Ex-prince of Caledor, takes us on a journey to Ulthuan in this Spike! Journal with an emphasis on the Caledor Dragons. This exciting issue will help you get the most out of your team, and includes rules for themed pitches and balls, the Spectacle of the Flame Eternal, coaching tips, and entertaining editorials.

Inside this 32-page softcover magazine, you'll find:
– Princes of the Pitch: History and background on High Elf teams, a breakdown of the players' positions, and a High Elf teams roster
– Famous Teams: With rules for fielding the Caledor Dragons in games of Blood Bowl, and the history and highlights of other well-known teams
– The Spectacle of the Flame Eternal: Rules for running an alternate league with a High Elf theme
– New Rules: Special rules for running games on an Elven Opulence pitch, and High Elf balls
– Dirt from the Dugout: Mindy Piewhistle's regular gossip column investigates rumours that the Everqueen’s own clothing line might not be as ‘Forest Friendly’ as it claims
– Chat with the Rat: Hackspit 'The Scribbler' Quillchewer's regular article includes an interview with Elontir Bloomtide
– Enjoying the Finer Things in Life: Tips and guides for coaching the High Elves to victory
– Cabalvision Presents: A Brawl at Sea: A spotlight article on a famous game, and rules to help you recreate it
– Special Rules and Achievements: Six new Achievements and three Special Rules
– Ask Lord Borak: The legendary Chaos player offers his unique perspective on all things Blood Bowl
– Coffin Corner: A memorial for those who gave their lives doing what they loved best

boardgame: Blood Bowl: Third Season Edition – High Elf Team Reference Card Pack
description:
High Elf teams are filled with stellar athletes, from the lowliest linemen to the famed Dragon Princes. They play with an effortless grace that makes the game look easy, and they're surprisingly tough, covered in flexible scale armour.

This set of 26 cards will help you keep track of your High Elf team in games of Blood Bowl. There are individual cards for your players, skill reference cards, and special rules cards for High Elf balls and pitches. They are an ideal way to easily check key information in the middle of a match while the crowd cheers and crunching blocks fly. There are also 16 blank Player reference cards, allowing you to track the development of your team during league play. These reference cards are only available while stocks last.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Blood Bowl: Third Season Edition – High Elf Pitch: Double-sided Pitch and Dugouts
description:
The High Elves are known for their love of expensive things. Their Blood Bowl stadia are no different, with nobles eagerly erecting marble edifices in an attempt to outdo their neighbours. No expense is spared – concession stands stock the finest fast foods, tuxedo-sporting familiars deliver drink orders directly to the stands, and Cabalvision screens showcase action replays of the best passes.

This set contains a double-sided, fold-out card Blood Bowl pitch, depicting the home stadium of a High Elf team. One side features beautiful marble flooring, polished to perfection, while the other shows perfectly manicured grass.

You'll also find a pair of matching double-sided Dugouts to complete the theme. Each Dugout features score, turn, and Re-roll trackers, plus Reserves, Knocked-out, and Casualty boxes. This pitch and Dugouts set is only available while stocks last.

The pitch measures 906mm by 538mm when fully opened, with reinforced folds, while the two Dugouts each measure 302mm by 135mm.

Rules for using each side of the pitch in your games of Blood Bowl are included – you can also find these rules in Spike! Journal: Issue 21 or in the High Elf Team Reference Card Pack, both of which are available separately.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Blood Bowl: Third Season Edition – High Elf Blood Bowl Team: The Caledor Dragons
description:
Smack bang in the middle of the Great Ocean sits Ulthuan, the picturesque ancestral land of Elves everywhere. Known for their high spires, high fashion, and high opinions of themselves, the High Elves love to try to enforce a measure of elegance on Blood Bowl games, darting to and fro as they toss the ball around – after all, they can't be expected to actually physically interact with the unwashed hordes that are regular Blood Bowl players!

The Epitome of Grace and Skill

High Elves are a refined and deadly hybrid team, blending martial strength with unmatched passing precision. Their armour gives them staying power few agility teams can match, while their Thrower opens up the field with pinpoint strikes at just the right moment. With disciplined positioning and careful protection of their elite players, High Elves can pick apart cages, outmanoeuvre bash teams, and score with effortless grace. Coached well, they don't just win, they do it in style, as only true sons of Ulthuan can!

The Pinnacle of Passing

This multipart plastic kit builds a High Elf team for use in games of Blood Bowl. White Lions are ready to pounce on their prey, taking on heavy hitters, while Phoenix Warrior Throwers and Dragon Princes are nimble and athletic. High Elf Linemen boast some of the best stats of any team. They are ideal for any coach who likes an agile and graceful playstyle.

—description from the publisher

Return to the cozy town of River Valley! But this time, dig a little deeper. Glass is so last year.

Now, it's all about the rare, radiant gemstones under our idyllic little town of River Valley. Miners, craftspeople, and artisans of all kinds have converged to make and sell the most beautiful jewelry in the land.

On your turn, roll dice to gather gems. Use your unique movement rules and player powers to explore the mine. Carefully lay tiles to improve your potential spoils. Finally, use your gems to craft exquisite jewelry to build a powerful scoring engine!

While River Valley Jewelcraft is a return to the setting and soul of River Valley Glassworks, it's a completely brand new game with completely unique gameplay and components.

—description from the publisher

You'll cooperatively use logic to compare, contrast, and categorize Things—just like in the first game (Things in Rings). But, instead of a Venn Diagram, the player playing as the Knower comes up with the rules of a cascading flowchart.

As more and more Things get added, groups split with new rules.

While the flowchart grows, you'll surprise yourself with how well you can follow the logic (or, laugh at yourselves at how lost you truly are).

—description from the publisher

A dynamic game of observation and quick decision-making where every second counts. Form pairs or trios of correct situations to save yourself each round… if you fail, you’ll lose a life.

—description from the publisher (translated)

Un juego dinámico de observación y decisiones rápidas donde cada segundo cuenta. Forma pares o tríos de situaciones correctas para salvarte en cada ronda… si fallas, perderás una vida.

—description from the publisher

A flatpack furniture-building experience that will actually make you feel smart. Sometimes cozy, sometimes crushing (just like real-life furniture assembly).

On your turn, you'll draft colorful, interlocking furniture tiles. If you complete a furniture blueprint with these tiles, you'll place it in your home. Mind the special scoring rules!

You can probably guess: you won't be able to fit everything easily. But you can try.

—description from the publisher

Until It Flips! is a lighthearted dexterity game set around a chaotic version of Noah’s Ark, where animals from different groups must be carefully placed onto an unstable structure. Each animal belongs to a color group that determines how it can be positioned: some can be stacked freely or placed on elevated areas, while others must remain on the base level. Every placement affects the balance of the ark, creating constant tension as the structure becomes increasingly unstable.

Players take turns adding animals to the ark, either freely or guided by dice and card mechanics depending on the chosen game mode. In some modes, players must also remove animals based on color interactions, creating dynamic shifts in the structure and forcing players to constantly reassess risk. Precision and careful planning are essential, as even a small misplacement can destabilize everything.

If two or more animals fall, the ark is considered to have flipped, triggering the loss condition or ending the round depending on the mode. If only one animal falls, it is repositioned according to the specific rules of the current mode, often allowing strategic recovery or even new opportunities.

Players either win collectively by successfully placing all animals and keeping the ark stable, or compete across rounds to score the most points by stacking and controlling animals. Victory depends on maintaining balance, managing risk, and making the most of each carefully calculated move.

The game is played over 4 rounds, each consisting of 3 turns.
At the start of each turn, players choose one of their three 5‑card rows.

There are two types of cards: spell and demon attack.
Demon attack cards cause demons to strike the Sorceress.
Spell cards allow players to cast attacking or defensive spells.

Each spell card is divided into two halves:

     one half shows a Form,
     the other half shows an Element.


Casting a spell requires 1 Form and 1 or 2 identical Elements.
For attacking spells, the Form determines the direction between the two targets, while choosing the appropriate Element is important because demons have different levels of elemental defense.

There are three defensive spells, depending on the Element used:
Healing, Teleport, and Frost Armor.

Part of the Demon War series, alongside A mágus küldetése (The Quest of the Mage) and The Possessed Monastery.

boardgame: Waste of Parts: Remnants of the Fallen Expansion
description:
Remnants of the Fallen Expansion was an add-on for the Waste of Parts Gamefound campaign, and also as a promotional insert for pledgers of the Vestige campaign, which ran at the same time.
It adds sixteen cards to the Salvage deck: two room upgrades, Azell Robotics Bay x3 and Azell Auto-Processor x2; two gear cards, Azell 5V x2 and Azell RF-1KT x2; two armor cards, Vitessence Injector x2 and Azell Type-9 x2; and a weapon, Azell MK25 x3.
It also contains a flavor text card explaining how an unmarked crate was found containing "waste protocols," referring to the Waste of Parts game.

boardgame: Legend of the Galactic Heroes: The Amritsar Campaign
description:
The Amritsar campaign is a doujin (fan made) scenario kit for use with the Tsukuda Legend of the Galactic Heroes game system. The kit consists of a rulebook and printed counter sheets which will need assembly.  Ownership of one of the four Tsukuda games is required.

The kit includes 6 smaller scenarios which can be played alone or combined as a campaign for the larger Amritsar scenario

Alviss
Bilrost
Dverger
Jafnhar
Lugen
Wansteit
Amritsar
family: Setting: Legend of the Galactic Heroes

boardgame: Throne Tactics: A Modular Historical Wargame
description:
Throne Tactics'' is a high-stakes, miniatures-agnostic tabletop wargame designed to bring sweeping maneuvers and brutal clashes to your tabletop. 
Whether you command a raiding party of the Dark Ages, a disciplined Roman Legion, a thundering Macedonian Phalanx, or a desperate Crusader host, Throne Tactics provides a streamlined, tactical engine that rewards positioning, morale management, and decisive leadership.

The Core Experience
Built on an Alternating Unit Activation system, the game eliminates the "wait-and-see" of traditional turn-based games. Every decision matters as you and your opponent trade blows, reacting in real-time to shifting front lines and sudden charges.

     Miniatures Agnostic: Use any historical or fantasy miniatures in your collection. The game supports individual model basing or "multi-basing" (diorama-style movement trays) without changing the core mechanics.
     Leadership Matters: Your Commander isn't just a stat-block; they are the "beating heart" of the army, providing an Aura of Command that keeps nearby units from breaking under pressure.
     Morale & Break Tests: Units don't just fight to the last man—they break. Managing the Broken status and successfully Rallying your troops is the difference between a tactical withdrawal and a total rout.
     Advanced Regimental Facings: For players seeking deeper simulation, optional rules for Flanking and Rear Attacks reward clever maneuvering with significant combat bonuses.
     The Armory: Customize your warbands with unique War Gear like War Banners, Pavise Shields, or Master-Forged Weapons—but choose wisely, as army-wide limits apply.


Solo & Campaign Play
Throne Tactics includes a robust Solo Automata Engine, allowing you to fight against a "Decisive AI" that follows a unified doctrine based on unit roles.

     Grand Campaigns: Embark on a 10-battle narrative journey. Manage your Treasury, track Experience Points (XP) for veteran units, and deal with the aftermath of Battle Scars and injuries.
     Historical Tracks: Structure your games around themed campaigns like Alexander’s Conquest, Caesar in Gaul, or The Crusades, each with unique special rules and scenarios.


Digital Integration: The Forge & Automata App
Included in your purchase is access to the Throne Tactics Forge and the Automata Engine App.

     The Forge: A browser-based army builder that calculates points automatically and generates beautiful, print-ready Unit Cards.
     Automata Engine: A digital companion that walks you through AI decision trees for seamless solo play.


—description from the publisher
family: History: Wars of Alexander the Great

boardgame: Legend of the Galactic Heroes: Battles for Iserlohn
description:
The Battles of Iserlohn is a doujin (fan made) scenario kit for use with the Tsukuda Legend of the Galactic Heroes game system. The kit consists of a rulebook and printed counter sheets which will need assembly.  Ownership of one of the four Tsukuda games is required.

The kit includes 2 scenarios that deal with the battles fought for the fortress:

5th Iserlohn 
11th Iserlohn
family: Setting: Legend of the Galactic Heroes

description:
Take a risk and tease the cats hiding inside mysterious boxes! In Meme Unboxing (喵喵开箱), players push their luck by rolling dice and interacting with increasingly dangerous cats. The more you provoke them, the greater the rewards—but beware: go too far, and you’ll lose everything you’ve built as others swoop in to claim the prize.

During the game, players take turns performing actions in rounds, carefully balancing risk and reward while competing to complete boxes and score points.

This game is part of the third edition of the 9 Cards series.
publishers: 九张牌玩具公司(9 Cards Toy Company)

description:
It's 1893.  Chicago has unveiled its' first 264 ft tall Ferris Wheel at Chicago's World Fair.  There's only one problem...the carts are all in the wrong positions!  With a storm on the way and hundreds of riders in line, it's your job to rearrange the carts before the storm hits!  Are you up for the challenge?

Work individually - or together - to rearrange the colorful Ferris Wheel carts to their correct positions so fairgoers can enjoy a wonderful ride before the storm comes.  If 3 storm cards are turned from the deck before the ride is fixed, the game ends.

Carts can be moved when your meeple is on the cart with matching color and symbol to tickets in your hand.  Play the correct color and symbol tickets together to move the cart to the correct position.

The game can be played cooperatively, competitively, or solo.  In the cooperative and solo versions, players will try to fix the entire Ferris Wheel before the storm comes.  In the competitive version, players try to fix as many carts as they can.  The player with the most corrected carts (prizes are given to keep track) when the storm comes wins the game!

—description from the publisher

It's the Gearloc you never expected - because he's not a Gearloc at all! Meet Trover, the resourceful kobold who is obsessed with the heroes of Daelore and wants to be just like them. The cardboard replicas he makes of the Gearlocs are definitely not creepy... They're just loving homages!

While Trover doesn't have many skill dice of his own, what he does have is access to all of the Tyrant Dice that Too Many Bones has to offer, converting the existing die faces to brand new and crazy effects. Better yet, he can use the dice multiple times per battle, so long as you can get them back from his Heatup Track!

Japan’s Self-Defense Forces covers all ships and aircraft in Japanese service from 1955 to the current day. It is designed as a sourcebook for Harpoon, fifth edition, but it can also be used as a general reference. Organized as a collection of annexes, Annexes and B provide statistics on ships and aircraft respectively as first built, and any modifications over time, sometimes many times. Annexes C and on give detailed weapons and sensors characteristics, including guns, missiles, antisubmarine weapons, torpedoes, mines, airborne ordnance, radars, sonars, and data links, with all the information needed to use them in a Harpoon scenario.

The book also provides information on land-based surface-to-air missiles systems, coastal defense missiles and their battery organization. Supplemental annexes list ship classifications and factors used to convert weapons and sensor information into game data.

As a bonus, in addition to having information on the characteristics of Japanese ships, planes, weapons and sensors, it includes data on the how the ships are organized into flotillas and divisions, and aircraft into squadrons, and where they all are based. Another map shows Japan’s air defense regions.

—description from the publisher

Also known as Funny Board Game, it's the game where roll the dice and do what it says on the square. Maybe you'll strike a superhero pose, maybe you'll beat your chest like a gorilla, and maybe you'll ...simulate childbirth?

Clanimals is a trick-taking card game where rule-breaking creatures bend the conventions of traditional play.

Instead of suits like hearts and spades, players compete to win tricks using four animal clans: Mammals, Birds, Reptiles & Amphibians, and Ocean creatures. Each clan includes 20 unique cards, led by a dominant King of Beasts and supported by three Apex Beasts—powerful cards that can break the usual rules of trick-taking.

Clanimals is played with 3–6 players. With 4 or 6 players, players form two-person teams; with 3 or 5, everyone plays solo. Each player is dealt 13 cards, and a card is flipped to determine the trump clan. Players bid how many tricks they expect to win, aiming to hit their bid exactly while preventing their opponents from doing the same.

But in Clanimals, several unique elements require both strategy and adaptability to hit your bid. 
Trump changes every hand. 
Not all cards are dealt—so you’re never certain what’s in play. 
King of Beasts cards don’t have to follow suit, so there’s always a chance one is lurking, ready to snatch your trick. 
Apex Beasts can also be played anytime, and they carry bonus points if captured, adding another layer of risk and reward.

Tricks are won by the highest card of the clan led or the highest trump played. After each hand, players score based on how closely they matched their bid, with bonuses for precision and penalties for falling short.

For players who want more variety, Clanimals includes optional expansion sets:
Insects, which modify cards, tricks, or entire hands
Beastmasters, which grant special abilities to the rotating dealer.

The game ends when a team reaches 500 points or an individual player reaches 300.

Players familiar with trick-taking games will find the foundational mechanics of Clanimals familiar. But on that foundation, Clanimals is designed to have layers of wild, chaos-making evolutions. Making every trick a battle for survival, and every player both predator and prey.

—description from the publisher

Welcome to Vanishing Academy, the mystical school for magicians!
Beyond your studies, all students are evaluated in Limelight Royale, a magic show where different teams compete to perform the most impressive tricks. As the captain of one of these teams, which magicians will you befriend and recruit? Combine your team’s skills and unleash them at the right time to put on the greatest show! Can you outshine the opposing teams to take the Limelight?

Limelight is a trick-taking game for 3 to 5 players. Played over the course of 4 Acts, where each player drafts characters to their team and performs Tricks. Whoever has taken the most Tricks by the end of the game wins!

—description from the publisher

Tta-Tta-Tta: Hangeul Collision is a fast-paced, educational word game designed to help players master the structure of Hangeul (the Korean alphabet) through dynamic interaction. The game focuses on the intuitive combination of consonants and vowels, making it accessible for both native Korean children and language learners.

In this game, players compete to complete Hangeul syllables by "colliding" or combining different letter tiles. The gameplay emphasizes quick thinking and spatial recognition of how characters are formed. With various modes of play, it challenges players to expand their vocabulary while improving their reaction speed.

The ultimate goal is to collect the most points by forming valid syllables and words more efficiently than your opponents. It serves as an excellent tool for educators and parents to teach the fundamentals of the Korean writing system in an engaging, competitive environment.

—description from the designer

"Highrise Heist" is a tactile abstract strategy game where rival raccoons build towering skyscrapers and steal the top floors from opposing structures.

Players move entire stacks of blocks between towers, constantly choosing between expanding their own buildings and taking control of enemy high-rises. Simple rules create surprisingly deep tactical gameplay focused on positioning, timing, and spatial planning.

The goal is to complete more skyscrapers than your opponent before the game ends.

Players can take on the role of Snappy the raccoon and battle for control of the city skyline in quick but highly interactive matches suitable for both families and strategy gamers.

Kena Story is a cooperative storytelling card game designed for 2 to 6 players. The game challenges players to use their imagination and storytelling skills under time pressure.

At the start of the game, players collaboratively decide on a main character by choosing a name, profession and city. Once the timer starts, players take turns drawing cards featuring specific narrative prompts (such as ‘Add an Investigation’, ‘Add a bag of money’ or ‘Add a romantic moment’). The aim is to turn these elements into a coherent and engaging story within 90 seconds.

Science Fiction and Fantasy cards can also be used to create surreal stories. Furthermore, incorporating character cards can enhance the fun by introducing more challenging narrative styles.

—description from the designer

“Earth as we know it is threatened, but not now, in the past! Since the discovery of time travel and the translocation gene, you have been worried about the possible threats. Now those threats are being realised, and if the small team of specialists you have brought together can’t keep us safe, the world will suddenly change, and we will never realise it! This is a war, but one we never imagined, and the fight will take us throughout history, and even to alternatives we never imagined!”

The Wardens of History is a tabletop miniatures game with a small number of figures per game, that is the most miniature agnostic game you could wish for, as every battle takes place in a historical or even non-historical setting of your own choosing.

What you have here is a set of Story Arcs. Each consists of 15 games that revolve around a specific story. Your team will improve and may get additional equipment which will be needed to take on the steadily more challenging battles. The whole thing becomes a narrative that will differ from campaign to campaign, enabling you to play it through multiple times without being the same.
 The rules are not aiming to be in any way a detailed simulation of armed combat, instead they concentrate on getting into the action as quickly as possible. They have also been specifically designed to be played solo. You completely control your team, but the adversaries start as randomly moving markers until you spot them and then their reactions are driven by rolls of the dice.

All the games in the Horrendous Gaming Adventure System use the same dice mechanics, so that you don't have to learn a completely different system when wanting to switch to a different genre.

You’ll need your own figures for the Warden and their team of 10, around 12 adversaries for each period you want to use, and around 10 bystanders. A 2 foot square or 3 foot square playing area, ideally with suitable terrain for the period. Also 2D10 and 25 things you can use as markers while the adversaries and bystanders are still unknown.

—description from the publisher

A simple dexterity game for 2 players 6+

There are three frogs on a mechanical board and a “spin the wheel” wheel

Whatever colour is spun, the first to hit their coloured button will launch the frog to their side of the table and win a point

First to ten points wins!

Beautiful physical mechanics in the game board - a one piece integrated design typical of early 80s (ie well made)

Orbital is a cooperative board game for 1-4 players set aboard a failing space station. The crew must work together to repair systems, manage crises, and keep the station operational before it's too late.
What sets Orbital apart is what's hidden inside every astronaut figure — embedded electronics that communicate with the Nexus hub at the center of the board. Place your astronaut on a station tile and the hub registers it instantly. Nexus is driving events, tracking station systems, and escalating tension as the mission progresses. No scanning, no tapping, no setup. The pieces know where they stand.
The AstroLink companion app responds to crew positions in real time. The physical and digital layers are inseparable — decisions made on the board affect the app, and events triggered in the app change the board state.

—description from the publisher

Have you ever wanted to photograph a wild animal when you saw one? So, let's photograph the animals in our natural spaces and… shh! Don't make any noise, they are very timid and the picture might be ruined…
Photograph the most animals and win the game.

The guide draws the top card from the deck and looks at the pictured animal (lynx, deer, wolf, owl, or turtle).
They announce aloud the action indicated on the back of the top card of the deck. This is the action they will perform.
They give the players a few seconds to observe the animals, then ask them to close their eyes.
With the players' eyes closed, the guide performs the action on one of the tokens representing the animal on their card
As soon as a player gives a correct answer, they take the Photo card and begin creating their album by placing it in front of them. 
The Photo Safari ends when the draw pile is empty. 
The player with the most photos in their album wins the game.

SPA-¿Quién no ha visto nunca un animal salvaje y ha querido fotografiarlo? Vamos a fotografiar los animales de nuestros espacios naturales y… ¡chss! no hagamos ruido, que son muy sensibles y la imagen podría salir desenfocada, movida…
¡Sé quien fotografíe más animales y gana la partida!

—description from the designer

A game where everyone laughs and has fun, but... what if I cheat a little? In Trampos, cunning borders on cheating, so keep your eyes peeled to see who's sneaking ahead! Be the first to reach the cushion and win the game!
On your turn:
Choose one of the cards in your hand and place it face down to your right.
Everyone together, turn over the played cards.
The person who played the lowest non-repeated card can move their bear.
This way, you'll gradually get closer to the cushion, your goal! The first to reach it will be able to rest all winter!

Play by the rules we are explaining below, but remember...you're allowed to cheat!

SPA
Un juego lleno de risas en el que todo el mundo se divierte, pero… ¿y si hago una pequeña trampa? En Tramposo, el ingenio es tan importante como el engaño. 
¡Mantén los ojos bien abiertos para ver quién avanza cuando no le toca!

Zoorajidos is a tactical card game about daring animal outlaws competing for the richest hauls in the Wild West.

Each player controls a band of desert raiders — kangaroo rats, a rattlesnake, coyotes, and powerful pumas — each with a different strength. Over a series of rounds, players secretly choose which member of their gang will join the next raid.

Train wagons, stagecoaches, and other targets appear each round, offering valuable loot… or dangerous trouble. Reading your opponents, managing the strength of your gang, and choosing the right moment to strike are the keys to success.

At the end of the game, the outlaw who has collected the most valuable loot becomes the richest bandit in the West.

—description from the publisher

Haaibaaien is an interactive dice game where players take on the role of sharks hunting in their own bay. By collecting matching dice, players fill spaces in a 6×4 grid to complete rows and columns and score points.

But the ocean is unforgiving: whenever a player completes a row or column, their shark invades the bays of all opponents, forcing them to cross out that entire line. What you build can disappear at any moment.

Jokers provide flexibility, while shark fins create tension and unexpected twists. Special moments like calling out “Shark!” at the right time or completing rare combinations keep all players engaged, even outside their own turn.

When players finish 3 rows or columns, the game ends.

In Paardenkoppen, each round you receive a number of playing cards and, if you're lucky, one or more face cards (horse 13, cow 12, pig 11, and goat 10). You place chips on the different rows of animals.

If you have a face card, you try to play it during the round. If you succeed, you win the chips associated with that row of animals. Don’t have a face card? Then try to prevent face cards from being played that round, so the chips remain and you can win them in a later round.

Make sure you win the highest value of chips! Win the most chips and become a true Paardenkop!

—description from the publisher

The battle erupts on Bavianenberg! The baboons are fighting for glory, but keep their identities secret in order to drive other baboons away. The higher they climb up the mountain, the more points they are worth at the end of the game. Unmask your opponents and become the Baboon King.

In Bavianenberg, you try to move your baboons as high up the mountain as possible to score the most points. Keep your color a secret and try to figure out which colors your opponents are playing, so you can drive their baboons off the mountain.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Terraria: The Board Game – Seasonal Promo Pack
description: Christmas and Halloween-themed armor, vanity, consumable and equipment cards for Terraria: The Board Game. KS exclusive.

In 4 Seasons, you embark on a year-long campaign of Azorean Happy Cows, where each Season brings new challenges and decisions.

In Spring, abundant grass leads to a Summer of intense milk production. In Autumn, top producers join Cooperatives to face the threat that lurks in the Winter mist, where GodzFilas appear in Packs!

And amid alliances and dangers, two iconic figures emerge: the Cooperative Cow and her false friend, the ever-treacherous Crazy Cow.

The Farmer who scores the most points by selling surplus Happy Cows at the end of each Season wins the game.

—description from the designer

boardgame: Terraria: The Board Game – Developer Promo Pack
description: 11 vanity cards for Terraria: The Board Game, inspired by Re-Logic's Developer team vanity sets. KS exclusive.

The street cats are looking for a house to call home. Everyone in the neighbourhood wants to be the household with the most cats.

Use items to tempt cats into your house. Then more items to keep the cats happy, or else they will leave.
But those same items can be used to disrupt your neighbours happy home, making their cats leave back to the street. 
Then you can try again to get more cats into your home.

To win, be the first to get the winning amount of happy cats in your home.

This promotional pack contains five new location cards to add to your game of A Familiar Find. The new locations are the Chittering Portal, Leaky Tap, Rexxentrum, Stasis Bubble, and Table of Plenty. These cards add greater complexity and variety to the game, and are recommended for more experienced players.

Skorm is a medieval combat strategy board game that models ancient military tactics on a hexagonal grid. Designed to simulate historical combat formations, the game utilizes pieces representing specific battlefield units: Shieldmen, Archers, Horsemen, and a Warlord. Players command these armies across a board composed of sixty-one hexagonal tiles, aiming to execute maneuvers such as shield walls, ranged volleys, and cavalry flanking. Skorm is pure skill game with great strategic depth.

Gameplay mechanics emphasize tactical positioning and directional facing. During a turn, a player may freely rotate their Shieldmen, who are impervious to both attacks and captures from the specific direction they face. Following any rotation, a player must choose to either move, shoot, or capture with one piece. Movement capabilities vary by unit type; Shieldmen and Archers move a single space, while Horsemen and Warlords can move up to two spaces. Combat is also unit-specific: Archers can shoot targets one or two tiles away without moving and can fire over other units, while the remaining piece types eliminate enemies by capturing their occupied tile.

The central objective of the game requires players to either eliminate the opposing Warlord or to hold the central Gold tile with their warlord. Victory is achieved through one of three specific conditions. The "Eliminating" condition occurs when a player successfully captures or shoots the opponent's Warlord, which immediately ends the game. The "Conquering" condition requires a player to maneuver their Warlord onto the central Gold tile and survive the opponent's immediate next move. Finally, the "Dominating" condition provides an automatic victory if a player moves their Warlord onto the Gold tile when the opponent is reduced to four or fewer pieces.

—description from the designer

More Containers includes... more containers! Plus other brand-new modules and updated fan favorites from the classic game.

Player Loans
Each player now holds two loan cards. Any player seeking a loan must first approach fellow players instead of the bank. The lender collects interest, creating tense new negotiation opportunities.

Luxury Containers
Add 10 golden containers to the game with new scoring rules as they escalate in value. Players can trade in any two containers to upgrade into a gold container. And yes, they're metal to match the Deluxe Edition!

Trucking Expansion
Ever wonder how containers move from factories to warehouses? With trucks, of course! Each player gains a trucking board that earns $1 whenever goods are transported. These trucking companies accumulate wealth and can be auctioned off, giving their new owner both the current cash and all future shipping income.

—description from the publisher

Milimemory is a visual estimation game where players must guess the real-life size of everyday objects.
Each round, a card shows an object, and players try to reproduce its actual size using a measuring tool or by marking a length. Afterward, the real measurement is revealed and points are awarded based on accuracy.
The game combines perception, estimation, and quick thinking, making it suitable for families and educational settings.

Mother of Frankenstein is a narrative puzzle game based on the life of Mary Shelley. It is a collaborative experience featuring 12-18 hours of gameplay divided between three volumes.

In Volume 2, you'll follow Mary Godwin, Percy Shelley, and Claire Claremont to a ball at Castle Frankenstein (yes, Castle Frankenstein was a real place). You'll construct a 500-piece circular puzzle of the castle floorplan in order to work out just what happened that fateful evening. Afterwards, you'll need to solve the puzzles that alchemist Conrad Dippel set for Mary Shelley while educating her in the nascent field of "galvanism"--research that would one day inspire her famous novel.

—description from the publisher

Mother of Frankenstein is a narrative puzzle game based on the life of Mary Shelley. It is a collaborative experience featuring 12-18 hours of gameplay divided between three volumes.

In Volume 3, you'll return to Castle Frankenstein, which you'll build as a fully realized 300-piece 3D puzzle (which sits atop the 2D puzzle floorplan from Volume Two). Then you'll have to solve a set of fiendish puzzles in order to learn how to construct the elaborate origami roofs for the various castle buildings. Finally, you'll progress through a series of "advent calendar" style diary entries while searching out details in the castle, each time placing a new window into the 3D puzzle. By the time you've finished, you'll have finally learned Mary's terrible secret...and created a beautiful object you just might want to keep on your mantle forever.

—description from the publisher

The Course is Built. The Flight is Real.
