Welcome to Dice-N-Disc, the tabletop game that combines the strategy of disc golf with the thrill of dice rolling. Draft your bag from an exclusive lineup of high-performance Infinite Discs, then challenge fate on courses that change every time you play.

Do you have the nerve to throw your best disc?

How to Play

Draft your bag of four real-world discs —Putter, Mid-Range, Fairway, and Distance Driver—and use your disc's specific flight numbers (Speed, Glide, Turn, and Fade) to calculate your throw across a Modular Hex Grid.

Speed determines your total distance using dice up to a d20.
Glide dictates how far your disc flies straight as physics takes over.
Turn steers your shot into a curve, and Fade delivers the final, aggressive hook to the basket.

Challenge the Terrain

Master the wind and navigate challenging terrain. Will you risk an uphill shot that stops your flight short , or watch your drive roll off a downslope? Watch out for trees that can reduce your speed or send your disc bouncing off course!

The lowest score wins the hole. Build your bag, read the wind, and trust your roll to become the Dice-N-Disc champion!

—description from the publisher

Each round begins with the reading of a word from a card (and there are 168 double-sided cards waiting in the box!). Then, the active player sets the pre-cranked jaw in motion – and from that moment on, the clock starts ticking. While the jaw is clicking, players must produce as many correct answers as possible.

When the jaw stops, the player receives points for correct answers, moves their pawn around the board, and activates a distraction that could disrupt the next round. The game continues until someone reaches the finish line (the rest take their turn) or until a predetermined number of rounds have been played. The player with the most points wins!

The year is 1931 and the Penrose Society has discovered something the world isn't ready to know: other dimensions exist. Echoes of our own world rippling outward into the unknown. As a Steward, your job is theoretically simple: Breach the barrier into an Echo, explore what's on the other side, and extract before everything collapses around you.

Echobound Adventures is a cooperative sandbox game for 1–4 players. You play as Stewards, explorers sent into shifting environments to complete quests and uncover secrets before a looming Adversary shuts you down.

Tense, Press-your-luck Dice Play
Success depends on a unique dice system where Moxie is your most valuable resource. Use it to upgrade your own rolls while lending a hand to teammates in a pinch. You can "fail forward" and reroll for more successes, but rolling a Threat symbol speeds up the villain’s dark schemes.

The Adversary: A Shadow that Becomes a Threat
The Adversary is more than just a timer. It starts behind the scenes, enacting disruptive schemes when the threat track reaches its max. If this happens multiple times, a Crisis can be triggered, and the Adversary enters the board as a physical miniature that moves, attacks, and can even end your Expedition if they aren't defeated in time.

Risk vs. Reward
The goal is to collect as many Adventure points as you can and then extract. However, the longer you stay to hunt for better rewards, the more threat you accumulate. If the Adversary achieves their goal during a Crisis, or a Steward is incapacitated, the expedition fails and you only score half of the points you've collected.

Campaign Progression
Between games, your hard-earned points fuel a persistent Environment progression track. This unlocks new Quests or Steward skills that are unlocked permanently and can be used in future Expeditions. With a narrative Adventure Journal that reacts to your choices, every session of Echobound Adventures shapes the world around you and influences the stories yet to come.

—description from the publisher

Guided by the King’s vision, you must raise towering structures, cultivate the land, and weave terrain into harmony. The island awaits your hand, and the King awaits his masterpiece.

Royal Architect: King’s Archipelago is an expansion to the base game that introduces a new challenge: ports. During play, you’ll link islands together by establishing port connections. This expansion also adds 6 new Structure Cards, which can be combined with cards from the base game and any other expansions.

Guided by the King’s vision, you must raise towering structures, cultivate the land, and weave terrain into harmony. The island awaits your hand, and the King awaits his masterpiece.

Royal Architect: Great Blueprint is an expansion to the base game that provides a larger map, allowing for longer and more engaging gameplay. It also includes 6 new Structure Cards, which can be combined with cards from the base game and all other expansions.

Guided by the King’s vision, you must raise towering structures, cultivate the land, and weave terrain into harmony. The island awaits your hand, and the King awaits his masterpiece.

Royal Architect: New Discovery is an expansion for the base game that introduces a brand-new map to boost replayability. It also adds 6 new Structure Cards, which can be mixed and matched with cards from the base game and all other expansions.

Guided by the King’s vision, you must raise towering structures, cultivate the land, and weave terrain into harmony. The island awaits your hand, and the King awaits his masterpiece.

Royal Architect: Realms’ Outskirts is an expansion for the base game featuring a new map to explore. This map includes 6 monuments that award bonus points when their conditions are fulfilled. The expansion also introduces 6 new Structure Cards, which can be added to the cards from the base game as well as any other available expansions.

Discover Bulgaria’s striking landscapes, from the alpine peaks of the Rila Mountains to the sunlit shores of the Black Sea. Wander through historic towns, forested valleys, and mountain villages rich in tradition. This expansion for The Wayfarer includes a detailed map of Bulgaria.

Bulgaria reveals a landscape shaped by mountains, monasteries, and centuries of layered history. In the south, the jagged peaks of the Rila Mountains rise above glacial lakes and pine forests, sheltering the ornate Rila Monastery within their folds. To the southwest, the dramatic sandstone cliffs of Melnik Pyramids glow warmly in the afternoon sun. Across the heart of the country, the rolling ridges of the Balkan Mountains divide north from south, while the Valley of Roses near Kazanlak fills the air with fragrance each spring. Along the coast, the Black Sea laps against golden beaches and historic port towns like Varna and Burgas. In the capital, Sofia blends Roman ruins, Orthodox cathedrals, and lively cafés beneath the watchful slopes of Vitosha. From alpine summits to sunlit shores, Bulgaria offers a journey rich in natural beauty, folklore, and enduring tradition.

Experience Estonia’s serene beauty, from misty northern bogs to the rugged coastlines of its Baltic islands. Stroll through cobblestone towns, hidden forests, and villages where history and folklore still linger. This expansion for The Wayfarer comes with a detailed map of Estonia.

Estonia enchants with its tranquil and diverse landscapes, where nature and history intertwine. Along the northern coast, windswept cliffs and golden beaches meet the Baltic Sea, dotted with small fishing villages and lighthouses. Inland, endless forests and misty bogs stretch across the countryside, while rivers like the Emajõgi River weave through meadows and wetlands teeming with wildlife, from cranes to beavers. Charming towns such as Tallinn and Tartu showcase medieval streets, vibrant markets, and rich cultural traditions, blending centuries of history with contemporary life. Off the mainland, islands like Saaremaa and Hiiumaa offer quiet retreats, coastal forests, and windswept shores perfect for exploration. From serene wetlands to sunlit archipelagos, Estonia invites travelers to discover its hidden beauty and timeless charm.

Big Stretch is a quick, easy 2 player push your luck game where you flip over cards to try to get a later time on the clock without going back in time. Features whimsical art and fits in your pocket with only 18 cards

Journey through South Korea’s diverse landscapes, from the misty peaks of Seoraksan to the volcanic shores of Jeju Island. Explore bustling cities, tranquil riverside towns, and villages where centuries-old traditions endure. This expansion for The Wayfarer includes a detailed map of South Korea.

South Korea is a land of striking contrasts, where ancient traditions meet vibrant modern life. Towering mountains like the Seoraksan National Park cradle misty valleys and crystal-clear streams, while the volcanic landscapes of Jeju Island reveal lava tubes and rugged coastlines. Cities like Seoul pulse with energy, where bustling street markets, historic palaces, and futuristic skyscrapers exist side by side, while Busan blends vibrant port life with scenic beaches and lively festivals. Other urban centers, like Gyeongju, offer a step back in time with temples, ancient tombs, and centuries-old traditions. Rivers like the Han River wind through the country, connecting peaceful parks and cultural landmarks, while rural villages preserve traditional hanok houses and temple-studded hills echo with spiritual calm. From snow-dusted peaks to sun-kissed islands, South Korea offers a journey full of energy, natural beauty, and cultural depth.

That Cold Week is a solo, diceless campaign expansion where you follow a seven-day journal through bitter winter conditions. The first day's freeze prevents leaf growth entirely, forcing you to rely on stems alone. Throughout the week, completing three identical numbers in the daytime tracker lets you choose between bonus energy, a free leaf, or a free stem—offering flexible rewards for careful planning.

The expansion introduces cold-themed rest days, including scoring based on every "3" in your tracker and counting connected creepers. With its opening restriction and adaptable bonuses, "That Cold Week" creates a patient, deliberate solo challenge where every choice matters against the quiet bite of winter.

Add more gameplay content to your copy of Calimala Retail Edition with the Intrigue Expansion!

Originally available as part of the Deluxe Edition but later made available by itself, this mini expansion increases complexity by introducing a new "intrigue action" step, allowing players to manipulate area majorities (Emperor/Cardinal) or contribute to new banking and artwork spots for end-game scoring.

Contents:

     1 intrigue board
     3 church overlay tiles
     9 family cards
     10 faction track markers
     4 intrigue action tiles
     1 Palazzo Vecchio overlay tile
     6 banking tiles


Note: This content is already included in Calimala - Deluxe Edition. However, this pack does not contain the component upgrades that are present in Calimala - Deluxe Edition.

boardgame: Imperium Card: The Third Servile War
description:
Imperium Card: The Third Servile War is a historical strategy card game set during the Roman slave uprising led by Spartacus (73–71 BCE). Players recreate the conflict between the Roman Republic and rebel forces through a flexible, sandbox-style system focused on battle simulation and strategic command.

Unlike traditional board games, Imperium does not rely on a fixed rulebook or turn structure. Instead, players construct armies using curated sets of unit and command cards representing historical forces, then deploy them across a chosen battlefield formation. Tactical positioning, timing of engagement, and adaptive decision-making determine the flow of each encounter.

The system emphasizes player-driven interpretation of history. Engagements can follow historical outcomes or diverge into alternate scenarios, allowing for both structured recreations and open-ended strategic exploration.

Victory conditions are scenario-based and may include:

Elimination of opposing forces
Control of key battlefield positions
Fulfillment of campaign objectives inspired by historical events

Imperium Card is designed as a modular system. This set can function independently or be combined with other expansions to create larger and more complex historical simulations.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Imperium Card: Mercenaries & Veterans Booster Pack
description:
Mercenaries & Veterans – An expansion for Sumeru & Seed Imperium cards Chapter III: Age of Iron & Glory, Edition I: The Second Punic War, designed to expand the Core Set with new strategic depth and battlefield possibilities.
Includes a curated 20-card booster pack featuring elite mercenaries and seasoned veterans such as War Elephants and Triarii, alongside new characters, military units, artifacts, and commodities that deepen strategy and bring the Second Punic War to life.

—description from the publisher

How awkward are you willing to look in front of your friends to win the game? Will you pretend you are hula hooping while talking like you're drunk? Keep periodically sniffing your finger while describing the boldest thing you've ever done while you were naked? Players take turns doing something ridiculous while saying something equally absurd. But you'll only get points if your opponents think you've earned it!

Pick up a card and try to draw or act out the f*cked up word in 60 seconds Many ways to play: multiple drawers at the same time, Charades round, etc.

If no one guesses in 60 seconds, pass the card to the other team for them to try.

G’Nuzzle is the riotously fun, fast-paced puzzle game where strategy meets speed! Race your friends to drop all your pieces first — it's part game, part puzzle, all sizzle.

Each player has just one minute per turn to hustle, shuffle, and G’Nuzzle their pieces into play. Match your pieces with others or extend the puzzle board to outwit your opponents. First to finish, wins!

Cora has been so busy, she's forgotten where she built her nests! Race to help Cora retrace her steps in this interactive listen-and-seek color matching game. To play Color Scramble, spread out the colored nests in a room, listen carefully as Cora announces colors then quickly find and place Cora on the correct nests. Choose to play solo, competitively or cooperatively! At the end of the game, Cora will tell you how many colors you matched. For an additional challenge, advanced players can play Memory Scramble: As Cora says different colors in a sequence, adding one more color each turn, players must repeat the sequence, placing her on each nest in the correct order.

A classic race and chase Dinosaur board game with some rather devious strategy. This game contains 17 individual dinosaur playing pieces. Each player has 4 dinosaurs and your aim is to race your pieces around the board from the desert to home. But beware...Tyrannosaurus Rex is lurking and waiting for a juicy meal!

boardgame: Come out ye Cuckold: Commission of Array – Scenario Supplement
description:
As a man of import and a strong conviction for King or Parliament, you have a duty to the cause. You have received the Commission of Array and Parliament's Militia Ordinance. You can only heed one. For which cause do you fight?

Overview
Commisson of Array is a scenario supplement for the English Civil War skirmish wargame Come out ye Cuckold!
This is a supplement and doesn't contain the main rules! To use this book as intended a copy of Come out ye Cuckold is necessary. 
This supplement expands on the scenario ideas from the main rulebook and gives players a starting point to create their own. The scenarios can be played as one off battles or as an ongoing campaign.

What's in the book?

     21 fully fleshed out and expanded scenarios (including 4 bonus scenarios).
     21 detailed table layout diagrams.
     A wide variety of objectives. e.g. Hunting for a spy, storming a breach, collecting taxes, looting a village, a fistfight in a church and many more.
     Expanded rules to support narrative play.

September 1942. The German Sixth Army is advancing through Stalingrad. Your battalion holds five street blocks of rubble, steel, and broken concrete somewhere between the factory district and the river.

The Volga is behind you. There is nowhere left to go.

Stavka has sent orders. They make no sense.

You should follow them anyway or maybe not at all and risk a bullet in the head from the Battalion's Commissar.

Not One Step Back is a solo wargame where you command a Soviet battalion in the street-by-street defense of Stalingrad, fighting a two-front war simultaneously. One against the Germans grinding through your sectors with infantry and Panzers, and one against a centralized Soviet command structure that will shoot you for losing too much ground they ordered you to hold.

Every round the Germans come and your orders arrive regardless of what is happening at the front. Following them costs you militarily. Ignoring them costs you politically. Both tracks must stay above zero. Lose either one and the game ends immediately, the Battalion shattered, or its commander shot.

Not one step back. Not one.

boardgame: Magic: The Gathering – Teenage Mutant Ninja Turtles Turtle Team-Up
description: Splinter's students are taking on the Foot Clan, and they need your help to win! Enter Turtle Team-Up from Magic: The Gathering | Teenage Mutant Ninja Turtles, a fresh way to play Magic with your friends. Here, you're not competing against each other. You're uniting against a horde of Shredder's minions and the Turtles' many foes. This experience is designed for two to four players, with an average game length of about 45 minutes. It's a great way to play with a new player or just bring a fresh spin to your Magic nights.
family: Comic Books: Teenage Mutant Ninja Turtles

boardgame: Magic: The Gathering – Commander Deck: Teenage Mutant Ninja Turtles – Turtle Power
description:
Brand-new Commander deck. Featuring new-to-Magic cards inspired by the Turtles' journeys in the digital realm, Turtle Power! has everything a Teenage Mutant Ninja Turtles fan could want.
This deck's face commander is Leonardo, the Balance, but it wouldn't be Teenage Mutant Ninja Turtles without a tubular team-up. Partner—Character select lets you have two commanders with that ability. Select your two favorite legendary creatures with this ability and show off their combined strength.
family: Comic Books: Teenage Mutant Ninja Turtles

description:
It is time to awaken the Ancients from their hibernation! As Warden of the Grove, your duty is to perform awakening rituals on the slumbering Ancients. Each awakening ritual is uniquely attuned to a specific Ancient, drawing its power from a combination of limited Wildseeds. Once awakened, some Ancients may need to consume seeds to restore their vitality. Plan your actions wisely to ensure you have enough seeds to awaken all the Ancients and preserve the grove’s harmony under your careful stewardship.

In Awaken the Ancients, carefully manage your limited supply of seed tokens to perform unique awakening rituals attuned to each Ancient. You'll strategically move seeds Mancala style around a grid of 8 Ancients, placing them on specific Ancients to match their awakening requirements. Once an Ancient is awakened, it may consume certain seeds for nourishment, removing them from play.

The challenge lies in planning your moves to ensure all eight Ancients are awakened before you run out of seeds or it becomes impossible to complete the rituals. Manage your resources wisely and use the Keepers' abilities to control the direction of seed movement, striving to achieve victory by awakening all Ancients. After a win, you final score is equal to the number of seeds remaining in the game.

This version of the game also includes the following expansions: Blight and Bother, Starstones, and the Dream Eater.

—description from the publisher
family: Versions & Editions: Big Box Versions of Individual Games

description:
Welcome to the buzziest, hungriest challenge ever! Seven mosquito chefs are ready to cook up the most delicious dishes from around the world... and their secret ingredient is you! In this game of speed and memory, you’ll have to squash the mosquitoes before they turn you into their next meal!

Mozquitoz is a game that requires both speed and memory. It is played over a total of three rounds, each with 7 cards to win.

Place card number 1 (fuchsia) in the centre of the table and take the fuchsia die.

The player who most recently killed a mosquito rolls the die and then the game continues clockwise.

Try to be the first to put your hand on the card, but only after having done the action indicated by the die. If you win the card, you put it in front of you on the back side, and the die is placed in the dice tray inside the box with the face that just came out facing up.

Now, after closing the box so that the die just played is not visible, place card number 2 (purple) in the centre of the table and roll the purple die.

Try to be the first to do the action indicated by the die and then put your hand on the card.

If you have done the wrong action (e.g. you hit your neck instead of your forehead), you have a penalty (see page 3), otherwise you have the chance to win the card, but only if you properly repeat the sequence of actions from the previous turns. While you repeat the sequence, remembering to conclude it by tapping your hand on the card as in the example below, the other players check that it is correct by looking at all the dice that have already come up inside the box (as in the picture on the side).

Is the sequence you made correct?
YES You win the card and the die is put in the tray at the end of the sequence. All the dice are covered by the lid of the box. The card with the next score (e.g. 4) is placed in the centre of the table and the new die, of the same colour (e.g. light blue), is taken and then thrown

NO NO. Unfortunately you take a penalty (see page 3), nobody wins the card and the same die must be rolled again, without being put within the tray.

You get a penalty when you don’t properly remember the sequence of all the actions or when you do the wrong action before putting your hand on the card in the centre of the table. After taking a penalty, you can’t participate in the next roll of the die. After having skipped a game turn, you will be in the game again.

If you get a third penalty you must abandon the current round and you will return to play starting from the
next round.

When the last of the 7 cards (the red one) is won, the round ends and all players sum up the points of all the mosquito cards that they have conquered. The score of the second round is added to the score of the first round, and the same for the score of the third round.

All players begin the second and the third round without penalties. If they ended the round with a sting card, they put it back into the box. At the end of the third round, the player with the highest score is the winner.

—description from the designer

Everlore is a high-fantasy adventure board game where players explore a richly detailed world through freeform exploration, narrative-driven quests, and strategic character development.

Players begin by spawning at different locations on the board and customising their characters through a card-based system, selecting race, class, faith, and destiny, which influence their starting stats: strength, agility, perception, intelligence, endurance, nobility, and timing. The game offers flexible play sessions, with either a single 15-turn round (75 minutes), a seven-round campaign (105 turns), or multiple sessions, akin to traditional role-playing games like Dungeons & Dragons.

During their turn, players move across tiles to reach various locations such as farms, towns, forts, ruins, docks, and caves, each offering unique opportunities such as trading, battling monsters, acquiring items, or making impactful choices that influence their karma and destiny. Players can explore independently or in parties, engaging in quests, guild activities, and strategic conquests at citadels, all presented through choose-your-own-adventure style narratives. Encounters with non-player characters, event markers, and monster battles provide rewards like cards and experience points, while the outcome of these decisions shapes their journey and progress toward the endgame.

The ultimate goal is to collect all five Evergems by completing guild storylines to confront the Endgame Boss, the Enchantress. Success depends on strategic exploration, completing quests, and managing resources, with the fate of the world hanging in the balance. Failing to gather all Evergems results in the Bad Ending, while players who succeed are rewarded with epilogues detailing their ultimate fate. Everlore emphasises player choice, exploration, and narrative depth, creating a dynamic and immersive experience.

—description from the publisher

Don't Go Back There is a standalone expansion for Don't Go In There. It can be played on its own or combined with the base game. Though you were warned to never return, curiosity got the best of you and now you’ve accidentally awoken the Vampires!

Decide how risky to play as you venture deep into Dracula's castle. Do you take first choice of the items and risk attracting Vampires, or play it safe? Collect the right items and to dispel Curses and ward off the Vampires.

On your turn, choose a part of the haunted house to explore by placing a token in one of the 3 rooms. Each room has a line of 4 spaces. The deeper you go into a room, the more likely you are to get the Cursed item you desire, but be careful! The further you go, the more Vampires will stalk you.

When 3 tokens have been placed into a room, the Vampire dice are rolled and players gain Vampire tokens, but may negate Vampires if they stay toward the back of the line. Next, players will take cards from the room in order of how far each player ventured into the room.

At the end of the game. The player with the most Vampire tokens is possessed and gains extra Curses. The player with the fewest Curses wins!

Don't Go Back There features all new Cursed items, all new Rooms, and new Vampire dice and tokens. Mix these together with the base game to see if you can escape both the Vampires AND the Ghosts!

—description from the designer
Roll all 9 dice (d6). They show a complete poker deck including 2 wilds. Arrange the dice in a row and a column with 1 die connected to both. Score points according to the table. Highest score wins.

The Seagull is a trick-taker for 2-4 players inspired by Yusuke Matsumoto’s Nokosu Dice and Anton Chekhov’s The Seagull, playable with a standard deck of cards.

As in Nokosu Dice, players draft cards at the start of each round to determine trump, then play out all cards but one to determine their final bid.

The twist is that players also start with two “Guns” – a card in their hand and a card on the table – which are hidden from them at the beginning of the round.

These can be flipped face-up to a trick at any time to bypass following suit: potentially allowing a player to escape a tricky situation.

If not played to a trick, they can instead be converted to a zero bid, or used to make a blind bid for more points  – or a more catastrophic failure – at the end.

The game lasts for 3-4 rounds (depending on player count), with the highest-scoring player at the end declared the winner.

The theme is inspired both by Chekhov’s play The Seagull – and his principle that “if you introduce a gun in the opening of a play, it must go off before the finale” – and by the audacious and mean-spirited nature of gulls more generally, which players are encouraged to adopt as a guide to play.

—description from the designer

A crazy card game with exploding pigeons gets even crazier with these promo cards that can be added to the base game.

Includes a rule card, 2 Search cards, and 2 Explosion cards. The Bitka cards force all players to pass their cards to a neighbor. The Chili card eliminates a pigeon if another blue Feeding card is played on it, and the Limča card has a value of -2, but your pigeon only explodes when it has 5 different feeding colors.

Grow your calendar with smart card swaps, unlock and collect scoring conditions, and stack season symbols to power up your calendar’s total. Can you make every month count?

The game is played in rounds until at least one player runs out of cards. During the game, players will put together their own Calendars by swapping Month cards from the table with the ones in their hands. The goal is to gain the most Victory Points (VP) at the end of the game for different week and Month sequences, season symbols in your Calendar, and additional Scoring Cards you acquired.

—description from the publisher

Hill 226 is a small solitaire wargame set during an episode of the Battle of Kursk, when the Totenkopf Division, part of the II SS Panzer Corps, was tasked with capturing the town of Prokhorovka. To achieve this objective, they first had to secure Hill 226.6.
The game is played over 8 turns, representing the 8 hours available to accomplish the mission, on an 8 × 8 hex grid map.
The player assumes command of a detachment from the Totenkopf Division, which must face the Soviet 10th Tank Corps.

GAME OBJECTIVE

As an armored officer in command of a detachment from the Totenkopf Division, your mission is to capture Hill 226.6 in less than 8 hours.
To win the game, the player must end Turn 8 with at least one German unit occupying a Hill hex. Each turn represents one hour, reducing the time available to complete the mission.

—description from the designer

Leap screaming into the spirit of spooky season with Nyx: Toil and Trouble! Play a few sick riffs on your broom guitar, brew a mysterious potion, or compete in the coolest witch’s hat competition (she’ll win). Fly with her under the harvest moon, surrounded by bats and festive pumpkins.

This digital download includes:


     Supported STL file
     Supported LYS file
     Unsupported STL file
     Adventurer, Item, and Initiative Card PDFs


The digital STL file included with this purchase may be used as a replacement figure for any Nyx Adventurer you may own!

First Tracks is a fast and competitive light-strategy card game that distills the experience of resort skiing into a card game centered on timing, positioning, and tactical disruption. At its core is a shared, ordered draft queue aka the “Lift Line” that players actively manipulate to secure the most valuable Rider cards.

Played over 3 rounds (“Days”), players must balance immediate gains against long-term positioning. Each turn presents a key decision: take the best available Rider now, or reshape the Lift Line using Kicker cards to set up stronger plays, or deny opponents theirs.

With five distinct Rider classes, players must weigh raw point value against class-based synergies, timing their picks to maximize efficiency while disrupting opponents’ plans.

The Parking Lot serves as the game’s discard pile, where used cards are placed. While many cards cycle through it, certain effects may interact with or reference the Parking Lot, making it an open and relevant part of the game state.

Turn Structure:

     Optionally play a Kicker card to manipulate the Lift Line or trigger effects.
         Temporary Kickers resolve immediately and are then sent to the Parking Lot.
         Permanent Kickers remain in play and modify scoring or game conditions.
    
     Take the Rider card from the front of the Lift Line and add it to your Base (score pile).
     Draw a Kicker card.


The Lift Line is a shared, constantly shifting resource. Kicker cards enable players to reorder Riders, disrupt opponents, accelerate or stall the round, and create high-impact swings. Because players are limited to one Rider per turn, sequencing and timing are critical.

A Day ends when the Lift Line is exhausted, a collection event is triggered, or a Kicker forces an early end, often creating pivotal tempo shifts. Turn order carries over between Days, adding another layer of strategic consideration.

Scoring and Victory:

At the end of Day 3, players total the points from all Rider cards at their Base, then apply any modifiers from Kicker card effects. The player with the highest score wins.

Why it stands out:


     Shared draft queue manipulation as a core mechanic
     High player interaction with constant indirect conflict
     Tactical hand management and timing decisions
     Set collection through five Rider classes
     Persistent vs. one-time effects create layered strategy
     Fast turns with meaningful choices every round


First Tracks rewards players who can read the table, anticipate opponents, and strike at exactly the right moment.

—description from the designer

Pack of three 18-card expansions for the Doom Pilgrim board game:

Abyssus Mentis (Abyss of the Mind) introduces psychic illnesses and various sorts of spiritual darkness into the gameplay. This expansion is symbolized by a moon icon.

Beyond the Borders expansion brings meetings from beyond the borders of the known world – there, in the lands out of the reach of all the maps, strange creatures roam, and everything is possible. The symbol of this expansion shows one of such fantastic creatures.

Caves & Castles is an expansion bringing adventures in caves, towers, castle ruins, and similar areas full of danger. This expansion is symbolized by an icon of a demon, which you can easily find in such a ruin or dungeon.

—description from the publisher

This expansion for Doom Pilgrim or Doom Pilgrim Pocket brings 36 characters to play with, each with their own special abilities, and win conditions (which we call Quests). It comes in a hook box full of illustrations.

While the basic Doom Pilgrim game is about building your own fate, here you find some pre-built fates and the story you uncover while playing then finishes these fates. Into triumph, or doom.

Each card has a full drawing of the character on the front side and their story, stats, and quests on the flip side.

The cards are as big as the classic Doom Pilgrim cards, but can also be used with the Pocket version of the game, because you do not mix them with your cards. It is compatible with both versions similarly to the Black Bag expansion.

Doom Pilgrim Miserables is also compatible with Flails & Citadels and its (upcoming) Pocket version. Or you can use these stories as the basis for character building in your own dark fantasy RPG, like Mork Borg or similar.

boardgame: Lyrically Correct: A Music Trivia Game – Pop Quiz: '90s & 00s Pop Edition
description:
Don’t want to just chillax? Then get jiggy with us. Get ready to sing, rap, and mumble the words to some of the hottest songs of the 90s and 2000s. ‎ ‎ ‎ ‎ ‎ ‎ ‎ ‎ ‎ ‎ ‎ ‎ ‎ ‎ ‎ ‎ ‎ ‎ ‎ ‎ ‎ ‎ ‎ ‎ ‎ ‎ ‎ ‎ ‎

Grab two or more players and take turns answering questions about the '90s and 2000s Pop Hits. (But remember, pick those teammates wisely).

But hold up, that’s not it. Just when you think you have the right answer, a challenge card can be issued at any time to test everyone's knowledge a little more.

—description from the publisher

Reveal Blessings. Choose your path. Trust your fate.

On your turn, reveal 2 cards into the Sanctuary, then choose:


     Calmly Pray: Take 1 Blessing Card.
     Test The Fate: Draw a fortune stick to attempt taking all cards.


A favorable omen lets you claim everything. An ominous omen can wipe out your progress.

Collect Blessing Cards — Wealth, Beauty, Food, Rest, and Fame — to form sets. Complete 3 different sets, or collect 5 Fame cards, to win the game.

But beware: each completed set applies a permanent penalty, making future turns more difficult.

Play it safe and fall behind, or risk everything to seize your destiny.

—description from the designer

Trivia game based on the Sons of Anarchy TV show.

A player selects a trivia card and challenges another player by reading one of the two trivia questions on the card. If the challenged player answers incorrectly, they are knocked out. The reader can now draw another card and challenge another player. If the challenged player correctly answers the question, the reader is knocked out.

The challenged player now becomes the reader, selects a new card and challenges another player still in the round. Once all but one player remains, the remaining player wins the round and collects a picture card. Once someone has four picture cards they win the game.

There are special "Steal" cards that allow players to challenge another player with a trivia question and steal a picture card from them.

Dancing with Masks (舞池假面) is a competitive Euro-style point-scoring board game for 2–4 players, centered around a component-building mechanism. In the game, players don masks and step into a grand fairy-tale ballroom—where until the very last moment, no one knows your true identity.

—description from the publisher

This game is part of the third edition of the 9 Cards series.
publishers: 九张牌玩具公司(9 Cards Toy Company)
九张牌玩具公司(9 Cards Toy Company)

description:
Sorcerers were first introduced in the original Here to Slay Kickstarter campaign as an exclusive. As we’ve shifted away from gameplay locked behind exclusives, we decided a while ago that we wanted to incorporate Sorcerers in a new expansion. The Sorcerer cards in this expansion will be familiar to anyone who owns the original exclusive set, but each comes with newly designed artwork.

Squires are the bigger news - this is the first new class we’ve added to the game since 2022, so this is a big deal for us? Squires act as support Heroes, and the class works a little differently than the others because their Hero effects don’t require a dice roll. These resourceful crows will always look out for you if you take care of them!

Within this expansion, we also included a few extras to pair with the Here to Slay Banner Quest Expansion. Since that one was released before Sorcerers & Squires, we added Banners for each of these two classes in this expansion, along with the tokens you’ll need to pair this expansion with Banner Quest.
This ten-card expansion adds weapons to the game: three throwing stars, three bats, three brass knuckles, and a sword.

boardgame: Sea Salt & Paper: Includes Expansions Extra Salt & Extra Pepper
description:
A Walmart exclusive edition of Sea Salt & Paper which includes the first two expansions: Extra Salt and Extra Pepper.

During your turn, you assemble your hand, maybe place cards for their effect, and decide if you want to end the round. But do you think you are the one with the most points in hand?
You will have to choose: stop the round immediately or give the others an extra turn to try to extend the gap? Is it worth taking the risk?
The game ends when you reach 30/35/40 points (4/3/2 players).

The excitement of ending the round to catch your opponents off guard

The pleasure of playing your effect cards and making combos

It's a set collection card game like Rummy. The origami created especially for the game is just the illustrations on the cards.
family: Versions & Editions: Big Box Versions of Individual Games

description:
Search for clues, unmask the suspects, and catch the jewel thief before they disappear into the night! 
There's a grand masquerade ball happening in Coolsville, and everyone who's anyone will be there including the Mystery Inc. gang! But disaster strikes when the famous
Coolsville Diamond is stolen during the festivities! With everyone wearing costumes and masks, the thief could be anyone at the party. It's up to Scooby and the gang to search for clues, unmask the suspects, and catch the jewel thief before they disappear into the night.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Zombicide: Modern Mission WM45 – The Great End-of-the-world Egg Hunt
description:
Medium
This Easter, we decided to surprise the other Survivors. We scattered Easter Eggs all over the neighborhood and kicked off a full-scale egg hunt! The Zombies won’t stop harassing us, but that’s part of the challenge. Grab your partners and start searching - and remember: those colored eggs are mine! Cross that line, and we’ll have trouble!

Nem igaz! ("Not True!") is a party game centered on bluffing and deduction, where players take turns assuming the role of the deceiver. In each round, one player selects a card and answers questions about it, but is allowed to mix truthful and false statements in order to mislead the others.

The remaining players must carefully evaluate the answers, relying on memory, observation, and logical reasoning to determine which statements are true and which are not. Their goal is to correctly identify the chosen card or expose the deception.

The gameplay combines questioning, guessing, and social deduction elements, creating a fast-paced and interactive experience. The game emphasizes attention to detail and the ability to read other players, making it suitable for families and casual gatherings.

This is a small expansion of a game 'Mare Istra' adding new scenarios, new crew and new ways to play the previous game. It is a semi-cooperative horror-survival game for 1-4 players.
To win you must finish objectives of your Crisis cards before time runs out and escape via 'Columbia', your rescue ship.

boardgame: Három kis dínó és az időzű
description:
Három kis dínó és az időzű ("Three Little Dinosaurs and the Time Warp") is a cooperative children’s board game based on the world of the “Három kis dínó” stories. Players take on the roles of the three dinosaur characters and work together to restore order after time and space have become mixed up. Objects and elements from different eras appear in the wrong places, and players must return them to their correct locations.

The game is centered around a shared play area featuring multiple locations connected through a central volcano mechanism. Players move between locations, collect items, and decide collectively how to best return them to their proper time periods. Memory and observation are key, as players must remember which objects belong to which locations while planning efficient routes.

Gameplay emphasizes cooperation, communication, and storytelling. Players are encouraged to describe their actions and create a narrative as they travel through different times and places. The game combines simple rules with tactical decision-making, making it suitable for families and young players while supporting replayability through varied setups.

Generalissimo is a tactical zero-luck strategy game for two players, played on a square grid. Players command a diverse set of units, including Infantry, Cavalry, Archers, and the powerful Catapult.

The core gameplay is built on a "Zero Memorization" system. Unlike traditional abstract games, all movement rules and attack patterns are integrated into the visual design of the pieces, allowing players to focus entirely on strategy. Each unit has distinct tactical roles: the Catapult can strike targets from a distance (up to 6 squares ahead), destroying everything in its impact zone, while the Cavalry provides high-speed maneuvers.

How to win: The game is a battle of attrition. There is no king or general to protect. Victory is achieved by eliminating all of the opponent’s pieces. The last player with at least one unit remaining on the board is declared the winner. With no dice, no cards, and no hidden information, every win is a testament to the player's superior tactical positioning and foresight.

—description from the designer

boardgame: Legacy of Man: The Ancients Expansion
description:
Expand the map and excitement with this addition for Legacy of Man.

The Ancients Expansion adds the option to create alliances while bringing the total number of players to 8. Added Empires include all the same game pieces as the base game, with unique units to fit their theme.

The Ancients Expansion adds Maya, Egypt, Sparta, and Persia to the mix. The new empires have unique ability powers, and includes additional knowledge cards paired with new Objectives as well.

—description from the designer

Forge History. Command Nations. Define Your Legacy.

Throughout history, empires have risen and fallen—yet now, the past and present collide on a battlefield shaped by your hand. Will you lead your nation to greatness through strength, diplomacy, or cunning resource management? The fate of civilization is yours to decide.

Legacy of Man: Evolution is the vision that created Illustrious Games. This is a Legacy style game (what we are calling Evolution) for 2–4 players. (Expandable to 8 players with the expansion) We saw a gap in Legacy style games for 6 or more people, so we created our own.

At the start of game 1, players construct the beginning of the map tile by tile. As the campaign goes on and more tiles get introduced, you end up with a unique world. This evolving landscape ensures endless strategies and fresh challenges with each play. Every game has something new introduced or unlocked with an objective. At the end of the campaign, you end up with the Legacy of Man base board game with an additional mechanic that you can choose to play with or not. It is a fully playable stand-alone game after the campaign, and includes the regular game manual so once your campaign is over you can still have fun playing it while remembering some of the wild things your group did creating this world.

Command Mighty Nations

Step into the role of one of four powerful nations—USA, Russia, China, or Mongolia—each with its own unique units. Gather resources, and expand your empire across the ever-changing board.

Forge Your Path to Victory

Victory is not claimed by conquest alone. Players win after getting 7 stars on the star tracker. The ways to get stars include:

War: Crushing your enemies in tense, dice-driven 1v1 battles, or subsequently defending against an onslaught.

Ingenuity: Building the map, gathering resources, and strengthening your empire while others fight.

Objectives: Completing different tasks gives stars as well.

—description from the publisher

Chim Poom is a musical multi-game card deck designed to help players learn and reinforce music reading and core musicianship skills through play.

Using a deck of more than 100 cards with musical notation, Chim Poom supports more than 20 playable game modes inspired by well-known traditional and modern games, as well as original designs created specifically for music learning. These game modes focus on note reading, rhythm awareness, attention, reaction, memory, quick visual recognition, and other key musical skills.

Created by musician and teacher Borja Ocaña, the system was developed, adapted, and tested in real classroom settings. Chim Poom is intended for music education, home learning, and playful practice, and it is designed for students, teachers, families, and anyone interested in approaching music in a more active, creative, and accessible way.

—description from the designer

A sculptured, plastic game from Happy Hour Inc.  The game is a large plastic, colorful board in the shape of the clown Kokomo Jr. The game comes with 6 white "Wonder Balls" made of Dylite.  The goal is to get the balls into the openings in the face and avoiding the deduct 25 point space below the bow tie.

Kokomo Jr. is a copyright of California National Productions, Inc.

Welcome to the zany world of Looney Tunes in Hunting Wabbits! A card game where chaos reigns and cunning is king! This game plunges you into the beloved universe of Bugs Bunny and his mischievous pals, where players face-off in a madcap battle of wits and luck. In Hunting Wabbits! each player strives to outsmart their opponent by cleverly playing character cards. The heart of the game lies in predicting your opponent’s moves and strategically using your characters' unique abilities to triumph in each round. The ultimate goal? To win the most Bugs Bunny tokens, as the clever rabbit is the key to claiming victory in this wacky contest.

How to play: 
ach player takes a set of 12 Character Cards to create their draw deck. Shuffle these cards and take 3 to make your starting hand. Each turn you will choose one card from your hand and play it face down in front of you. One player will start reading off character names, and you reveal your card when their name is called. You announce the card by using their signature catch phrase!

The player who has played the highest value card each round generally wins, but every character has their own crazy abilities that will shift the game. Once you have played 10 rounds the game ends and players add up their scores. Capture the most Wabbit tokens to increase your score. The player with the highest score wins!

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Game of Thrones: Masters of Intrigue
description:
Schemes are whispered. Loyalties shift. Power is up for grabs.
In Masters of Intrigue, players take on the roles of schemers and strategists from across Westeros, using bluffing, alliances, and betrayal to rise above their rivals. In each round of this trick-taking game, you’ll play cards representing the great Houses, choosing when to support the current power—or when to backstab and seize control for yourself.
Every decision counts: who you align with, when you betray, and how well you read your opponents. Hidden agendas, shifting allegiances, and clever timing are the keys to victory. With iconic Houses like Stark, Lannister, Targaryen, and more in play, each game captures the ruthless politics of Westeros in under an hour. Quick to learn but rich in strategy, Masters of Intrigue is perfect for fans who crave sharp turns, bold plays, and unforgettable betrayals.
Play boldly, lie well, and remember—when you play the game of thrones, you win or you fall.

How to Play
Build the Realm Deck, shuffle and deal out all the cards to the players. Game of Thrones: Masters of Intrigue is played over several rounds, known as chapters. Each representing a chapter in the stories of plotting, alliance-making, and power plays among the great houses of Westeros. One player is selected to be Hand of the King and they start the chapter.

Each chapter, players secretly choose a Rival House by playing a card face-down in front of them. This marks the noble house that the player is not aligned with. Any cards collected of this house represents setbacks and betrayals their house faces from this opposing house. Rival House cards collected reduce points from their score during scoring.

In a 3 or 4 player game, play 4 chapters.
In a 5 or 6 player game, play 3 chapters.

The Hand of the King starts the first cycle of intrigue by selecting a card and placing it face up on the table. All other players must then choose if they play aligned with the lead house – by playing a card from the same house, or in rebellion – by playing a different house. Tricks are taken based on how many allied cards vs how many cards in rebellion are played, with your goal to be to collect points each cycle. Cycles of intrigue play until all cards are played, at which point scoring begins.

The player with the most points at the end of the game wins and is declared the King or Queen of the Seven Kingdoms, of the Andols, the Rhoynar and the First Men, Protector of the Realm!

—description from the publisher
family: Books: A Song of Ice and Fire

boardgame: Monopoly: Panini Prizm – FIFA World Cup
description:
Celebrate current stars, FIFA WORLD CUP legends, and memorable moments—and compete at the FIFA World Cup 2026 in the Monopoly Panini Prizm: FIFA World Cup game! In this version of the Monopoly game, select soccer stars on Panini cards to build a world-class team. Then move around the board taking control of as many FIFA World Cup historic matches as possible. Compete for points from opponents who land on those matches by comparing stats on the Panini cards and rolling special dice. To earn more points, beat opponents in Game Changer challenges and compete at the FIFA World Cup 2026! The player with the most points at the end of the game wins. Looking for fun games for Family Game Night or cool gifts for kids, teens, college students, or adults? This strategy board game for soccer fans is a great choice! For 2 to 4 players, ages 8+.

Highlights

COLLECT, TRADE, COMPETE: Go for FIFA World Cup 2026 glory in this Monopoly Panini Prizm: FIFA World Cup board game! Includes Monopoly Panini Prizm FIFA World Cup 2026 trading cards

CURRENT STARS & FIFA WORLD CUP LEGENDS: Select and trade players using the 16 Panini cards (one 8-card Starter Pack, two 4-card Prizm Packs). Look for stars like Lionel Messi, Kylian Mbappé, and Mia Hamm

EXCLUSIVE & ULTRA-RARE CARDS: Find 6 exclusive Gold Cracked Ice Monopoly Panini Prizm cards in every game box! Look for exclusive Hat Trick cards and ultra-rare White Money Blast inserts

PROPERTIES ARE LEGENDARY FIFA WORLD CUP MATCHES: Take control of historic matches on the board, then compete with opponents who land on them by rolling special dice to boost players’ attacking or defensive abilities

COMPETE AT THE FIFA WORLD CUP 2026: Players compete against opponents by revealing and comparing soccer stats on the backs of their Panini cards

GIFT FOR KIDS & COLLECTORS: This family board game for adults, teens, tweens, and kids ages 8+ makes a great birthday or holiday gift for FIFA soccer fans and soccer trading card collectors

ADD MORE SOCCER STARS: Look for Monopoly Panini Prizm: FIFA World Cup 2026 Trading Cards booster boxes for use with this game! (Sold separately. Subject to availability.)

The board shows a track with 26 spaces, each showing a letter of the alphabet. Deal each player 7 cards. Each will show a category (e.g. color, farm animal, rock star). Set the timer/pointer to 30/20/10 seconds depending on desired difficulty level. To start, flip the top card of the deck. The first person to names something in that category moves the timer/pointer to the space showing the last letter of the item named. This automatically starts the timer. Players now take turns playing a card and naming an item in that category that starts with the letter the timer is on. If they name an item, they discard the card and move the timer to the last letter of the item named, which restarts the timer. If they fail to name an item, they draw a penalty card then pick the timer up and put it back down on the same letter to restart it. The first player to get rid of all of his cards wins.

Chess & Balance fuses tactical chess with spell card magic. Cast spells. Move pieces. Chaos on the board. 
AI solved chess. It can't solve this.
Ancient chess | Modern magic | Infinite strategies


♟️ Gameplay - is a 2-player card game that fuses standard chess with elemental spell decks. Each player picks one of five elements and uses 9 unique spell cards to summon pieces, protect their King, disrupt their opponent, and reshape the game. (Advance variants uses plasma for sideboard)


⚔️ HOW A TURN WORKS
Each turn has two phases:- 🔮 Spell Phase (Optional), Play a card, cycle a card, or spend Spirits to summon new pieces onto the board.

- ♟️ Chess Phase (Mandatory), Make one standard chess move.


✦ SPIRIT is the resource that powers your deck. 
Every time your opponent plays a card, you gain Spirit equal to its cost. Higher-cost cards summon stronger pieces or trigger devastating effects.


🌟 THE FIVE ELEMENTS
- 🔥 Fire   - Aggressive removal and board disruption
- 🌊 Water  - Control, manipulation, card advantage
- 🌿 Nature - Pawn swarming, promotion, King protection
- 💨 Wind   - Positional chaos, movement manipulation
- ⚡ Plasma - Global persistent effects that reshape rules for BOTH players


🏆 OBJECTIVE Capture ALL of your opponent's Kings.


📦 WHAT'S IN THE BOX
- 🃏 36 Spell Cards (9 Fire, 9 Water, 9 Nature, 9 Wind)
- ⚡ 4 Sideboard Cards (4 Plasma x 2 players)
- ♟️ 1 standard chess set and extra chess board for the extra pieces (not included)
- 🪙 Tokens or coins for tracking Persistent Effects turns


📋 GAME DETAILS
- 👥 Players:   2
- 🎯 Age:       8+
- ⏱️ Play Time: 30-120 minutes
- 📖 Edition:   Beta Edition


🌐 LEARN MORE
- https://chessnbalance.com/
- https://www.thegamecrafter.com/games/chess-balance


BETA EDITION | JericoDP Design | AI Polish

Main component is a large plastic bird cage with a googly-eyed cat on top. Place the 9 tokens in the bird cage and close the door. Place the 10 silk feathers in the holder/hopper on top of the cage and push down the lever in back to lower the bird inside. Place the 10 numbered keys facedown. On turn, pick a key, put it in the lock on the cage, and turn it. This will cause the cat to roll its eyes and claw for the bird in the cage. If the cage door doesn't open, set the key aside and the next player goes. If the door opens, the feathers will fly in the air and the player takes a token then resets for the next round. First player to collect 3 tokens wins.

boardgame: Heart and Skull: The Core of Sepulchre
description:
Heart and Skull is the stripped-down card-based version of Sepulchre: A Grave Game.

Heart and Skull is a card-based game for 2 to 6 players that utilizes a deck that consists of three types of cards: Skeleton Cards for players to build skeletons, Decay Cards for dismantling the skeletons of opponents, and Cemetery Cards which drive the game by providing a wide range of offense and defense options along with a variety of other card-specific tactics.

The objective of Heart and Skull is to be the first to complete three seven-piece skeletons using combinations of the three types of cards in the play deck. The skeletons are represented in this version of Sepulchre in the form of transparent skeleton piece cards that are overlaid and arranged left to right in a row.

Heart and Skull takes players on a journey of all things cemetery and graveyard related, ranging from the symbolism of headstone art to the natural (and supernatural) denizens one might encounter while visiting.

—description from the designer

Each player is dealt a random character card with individual interests. The game master auctions off several trips to tourist destinations. Players may then trade their travel cards with each another. At the end of the round, each player may book a trip by playing a travel card. Points are awarded at the end for matching interests.

Explore the 4Keys dungeon.

Collect Door and Key cards to create a 4x4 grid dungeon.

Position your keys strategically to unlock rooms and earn the most Gold Coins (GO) to win the game.

Papercuts Trading is a competitive, strategy-driven card game themed around the psychology and unpredictability of financial markets. Players take on the role of traders navigating price movement, market sentiment, and unexpected events. The game blends real-world trading concepts—such as momentum, contrarian thinking, and news-driven volatility—into an accessible format that emphasizes decision-making under uncertainty rather than technical complexity.

Gameplay unfolds over a series of rounds where each player secretly commits to a market position—Buy, Sell, or Flat—based on limited information. One player acts as the Market Maker each round, revealing an Indication card that represents the current market direction or condition. After positions are revealed, outcomes are applied to each player’s score. Optional Event cards may be introduced to amplify results, simulating sudden news or external forces that reward correct positioning. Players must manage risk carefully, as losses can be mitigated with a limited-use Stop Loss card.

The core tension of the game comes from anticipating both the market and the intentions of other players. Since the Market Maker role rotates, each player alternates between influencing the market and reacting to it. This dynamic creates a layered strategy where timing, probability, and behavioral insight all play a role in success.

The objective is to grow your account by accumulating points through correct positioning and strategic risk management. Players who mismanage risk can be eliminated if their balance drops too low, while consistent performance leads to victory. The first player to reach the target profit level—or the last remaining trader—wins the game, earning the title of Top Trader.

boardgame: A Wedding to Die For: Murder Mystery Dinner Game
description:
A Wedding to Die For is a fully improvised murder mystery dinner game for 16—25 players, designed to be hosted at home without a professional cast or facilitator. The setting is a wedding reception. The groom has been murdered. Every guest at the table is a suspect.

Unlike scripted murder mystery box sets — which provide fixed dialogue and clues leading to a predetermined solution — A Wedding to Die For is built entirely around player improvisation. Each player receives a detailed character briefing before the event, arriving at the dinner table already knowing who they are, what secrets they carry, and what motives they have. There are no scripts and no lines to read aloud. The murderer knows their identity from the start and must conceal it while the other guests spend the evening interrogating, accusing and improvising their way toward the truth.

Clue cards are revealed progressively throughout the meal, deepening suspicion with each course. The evening ends with an accusation round and the unmasking of the killer.
The result sits closer to collaborative theatre than traditional board gaming — social, performance-driven, and entirely unpredictable. Every group produces a different evening. Format: Print-and-Play digital download. Print at home or at a local copy shop. No special components required.

A Wedding to Die For is part of The Murder Mystery Guide's library of 11 improvised murder mystery dinner games, spanning themes from a White Lotus-inspired island resort to an Illuminati conspiracy and a five-star hospital scandal. All titles are available as instant digital downloads worldwide.
World premiere: March 2026, Penhill Farm, Cape Town, South Africa.

Word Factory is a word-spelling game for two to four players, where players will be taking letter tiles from a central hopper board and adding them to their assembly line to try and make words.

It has been released as a print-and-play game that requires only three sheets of paper to be printed out, along with letter tiles pilfered from a popular, well-known game that rhymes with Brabble.

Each player gets a player board that shows an assembly line, and then two areas to the left and right of the board: a Fallout area, where unused or extra letters are placed which count against you, and a Word area, where the letters from spelled words are collected. At the end of the game, players simply score the number of letters in their Word area minus any letters in their Fallout area.

On a player's turn, they will select one of the five available hoppers in the central factory board, paying a letter to the board per hopper skipped. These letters will be added to their assembly line, and if there are too many letters some will be discarded to the Fallout area. Then that player must refill the empty hopper on the central board, and (optionally) play a valid word that they have built on their assembly line. Players will receive bonus letters if they build long words.

The player who operates the most productive assembly line, wins!

boardgame: The Case of the Curiously Quiet Theater
description:
As a member of the SHhhh, We're Reading Book Club in Hincksville, you've been patiently awaiting your turn to read the advance copy of the highly anticipated new Ells and Ernie mystery, when Mr. Hincks gives you the terrible news that the book was lost to the library. Never fear, though, Mr. Hincks has a solution!

Mr. Hincks has recorded an abridged audio version of the book and put together a box of peculiar reference materials in order to recreate the story for you before the book club meeting. But remember: the first rule of book club is, NO SPOILERS! Mr. Hincks will have to use a few puzzling tricks in order to recap this mystery book for you without spoiling any of the twists.

In The Case of the Curiously Quiet Theater, Ells and Ernie find themselves embroiled in a mystery amidst special effects, costumes, and shady characters. While attending the hottest play in town, Ells and Ernie discover that someone is interfering with the production in a most nefarious way. The detective duo will need to utilize all their skills to pull back the curtain and discover the truth behind the strange occurrences happening behind the scenes. Ells and Ernie are no strangers to mystery, but this time they’re up against a pool of suspects who are all classically trained actors and any lie is going to sound convincing.

Can you unlock all the audio clips before the SHhhh, We're Reading Book Club meeting? What are these mysterious markups on the reference pages?? Why is a lack of noise at a theater curious???

Answer all of these questions and more in... The Case of The Curiously Quiet Theater!

—description from the publisher

Imagine a Constitution written when the air still smelled of revolution — not just one revolution, but two. Imagine Philadelphia in 1789, after the French Revolution made the dream of universal human equality a living political force. Imagine the delegates who might have walked through those doors of Independence Hall if the moment had been seized differently — men and women, Black and white, Native and Spanish, — all of them with something to say about what a republic could truly mean. Imagine the document they might have written.

Each round, an article of the constitution is put up for consideration, which is either a Federalist, Anti-Federalist, Big State, or Small State issue. Players have a hand of three delegate cards that represent their respective states and also have a special ability. Players may use these cards' special abilities, use the cards to vote for or against the issue under consideration, or to try and claim tokens in each of the four types of issues. When a round ends, the issue either passes, or is flipped to its opposite side (Federalist/Anti or Big/Small).

Players score points for having voted with the majority, and also for having tokens matching the issue types that are voted into the constitution.

A More Perfect Union is a constitutional sandbox for 2–6 players, built around the great unresolved argument at the heart of the American founding: not merely how to structure a government, but who has the right to govern. Two complete scenarios are included in the box.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Spot The Bigfoot with Southwest Expansion Pack
description:
​The Spot the Bigfoot game with Southwest Expansion Pack adds new territory and up to 3 more players! A new Southwest board connects with the original Pacific Northwest to expand the range of exploration and gameplay. A new set of cryptids take the stage. Game mechanics change. Things get stranger still.

Spread out and expand your territory as a family of friendly Aliens - the Greys. Play zone defense as the Legendary Goatman of Lake Worth. Go on raids as the feared scourge of the Southwest - El Chupacabra! Each new cryptid has their own strategy style.

-description from designer
Make words with wooden, interlocking tiles, then roll the die to see your score.

It is a game in which seven of the Dalgona tokens with numbers from 1 to 99 are selected and put down on the personal tray first in ascending order.

1. Import tiles 
While importing dalgona tiles, take one tile from the pile of dalgona tiles. At this point, if you already have tiles on the storage tray, you can choose between a pile of tiles and a storage tray to import them. If you import tiles from the storage tray, you can only bring the top tile.

2. Tile placement
Down the tiles you just brought, so that the numbers are visible from the front of your personal board. If there is a tile that you have previously placed down, you should put down a larger number. If you cannot or do not want to put down the dalgona tiles, put them down so that the numbers are visible on one of the markings on the storage tray. If there is a blank, make sure to put them down in one of the blanks.

3. Game Ending 
If someone fills the individual board with 7 tiles in ascending order, or if they run out of dalgona tiles, the game will end immediately. The number of tiles you put down will be your score, and the person who puts down the most tiles will win. If it's a tie, the person later in the order will win.

If you're disappointed with a single round, you can enjoy it in multiple rounds. The number of dalgona tiles you put down will be the score. The person with the highest sum of points will score each round, so the person with the highest score will win.

Move the box by hand so that the moon in the box goes into the hole. The fast-seeker of all four wonderful pictures of the full moon wins.

Ready

     Remove the lid of the game box and put it down to one side. 
     Take out the smallest box in the box and put it down in the middle of the table. Then take out the box and place it on the small box as indicated. 
     Stack four boxes one by one in the same way. 
     Put two balls on the top box and prepare a second clock to time. All set to find the moon is ready! 


Play the game 

     When you are ready to start, shout 'Moon roll~' and start the timer. 
     Move the box with both hands so that the ball in the topmost box can escape through the hole. 
     When all the balls that have fallen out fall into the bottom box, the first box is organized into the bottom box. 
     Move the balls that came into the second box to the bottom box in the same way. 
     When the balls come into the smallest last box, organize them into the game box as they are. 
     When you are done organizing, shout out! "Moon roll~" 


Check the timer with the end of the game. The person who finds the moon the fastest will win.

boardgame: Cosmos so grand so small where echo's of only words only stood in voids.
description:
A card game, can you beat the stack reshuffle and hold the winning cards? What is the cosmos, what is out in space, outside of earth, yes, that place that is becoming overpopulated.

Would you chance the hand in effects that might be still or order you place, can you beat the person to think alone by reading quicker, understanding quicker how good your maths your order of play when the cosmos rules are not so fixed by human nature.

Can you collect more joules than the other person or will you find another way to win? What else might you learn along the way?

—description from the designer

In 1942 and 1943 the war in the Pacific and in Asia covered vast distances as Japanese ambitions sought to bring much of these lands under their control. In the British colonies of Malaya and Burma, the Japanese swept British and Indian forces towards the Indian frontier in what seemed like an unstoppable tide.

In the Western Pacific Ocean, the Japanese seized US possessions and dependencies, quickly overwhelming garrisons, even taking the Philippines from local and US troops. They even reached the doorstep of Australia, taking control of the Dutch East Indies and landing in Papua New Guinea and prompting the Australians to bring their forces back from the Mediterranean theatre and hastily dispatch home defence troops to Papua New Guinea.

However, as the Allies recovered from these initial setbacks they began the long and hard fought battle to turn back the Japanese tide. The British and Indians launch a number of offensives to recover Burma, while the Americans and Australians fight together to secure Papua New Guinea, while the Americans begin the fight for the Solomon Islands at Guadalcanal.

INSIDE YOU WILL FIND:

     Histories of the Pacific War in 1942 and 1943.
     Japanese Force, Formations and Units for Japanese Imperial Army Type 97 Chi-Ha, Type 89 Chi-Ro, and Type 95 Ha-Go Tank Companies, and an Infantry Company. Formations and Units for japanese Imperial Navy Type 2 Ka-Mi Tank Company and Naval Infantry Company.
     US Force, Formations and Units for US Marine Rifle Company, M3 Stuart, and M4 Sherman Marine Tank Companies. Formations and Units for US Army Rifle Company and M3 Stuart Tank Company.
     British and Indian Force, Formations and Units for a British Rifle Company, an Indian Rifle Company, Honey, and Grant/Lee Armoured Squadrons.
     Australian Force, Formations and Units for a Rifle Company, Militia Rifle Company, Commando Independent Company, and a Grant, a Stuart, and a Sentinel Armoured Squadron.
     Pacific Terrain and Mission rules.
     Inspirational colour photos
     Painting and Basing Guides.

Finally, an impersonation game that’s even better if you’re terrible at doing impressions. Inside you’ll find 500+ prompts, each featuring a well-known character, animal or object to mimic—and players compete by trying their best to imitate them. Psst: we’ve even included a pad of paper so you can add your own. Each round is judged on a randomly selected (and secret) criteria—everything from ‘most accurate’ to ‘unintentionally hilarious.’ It doesn’t matter if you’re talented or not—anyone can win when it comes to imitating a dog that’s about to throw up. Includes 300 game cards–each with a different prompt to mimic like ‘Kermit the Frog’ or ‘A Gumball Machine’.

—description from the publisher

Enter the world of Doraemon and Nobita with the Doraemon Escape Game board game. A family game where players must escape from Gian, playfully fostering skills such as decision-making and strategic thinking.

Players swing a sort of roulette that tells them how many spaces they'll move or play a card to take specific action. Gian might move instead of you.

Thanks to its reversible board, it offers double the fun with two ways to play, adapting to different tastes and occasions:

1. Escape from Gian's Recital: Players will have to be quick and agile to escape Gian and avoid attending his recital. Collect 3 coins and return home to win.

2. Marathon Game: It's marathon day, but Gian has interfered. You must flee and be the first to reach the finish line, using coins and Doraemon's gadgets to win cleverly.

Enhance your military dominance in the Strait of Gibraltar! The New Military Technologies promo pack introduces 3 brand-new tiles that seamlessly integrate with your Nucleum: Gibraltar expansion. Unlock powerful new effects for urbanizing, energizing, and endgame scoring to outsmart your rivals.

—description from the publisher

ColorBlind is a fast, tense secret-color trading card game for 3–6 players.
Everyone is secretly racing to complete a number set in their own hidden color.
Voluntary trades, forced Blind Swaps, and clever action cards create constant table-reading and surprise wins.

—description from the designer

Grid Lock is played on a 4x4x4 grid. Each player's deck will contain Heroes, Beasts, and Weapons. The Heroes can ride the Beasts and wield the Weapons. Each turn you must play a card from your hand into the row closest to you. Resources can move, attack, and use their effect once per turn. If you cannot play a card when required to you lose.

—description from the publisher
language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups

description:
Four distinct animal factions compete on a 13x13 grid with the goal of being the last one standing. The factions are primates, felines, elephantids, and rhinocerosids, each with different abilities, items, and statistics; but all with one objective: survival.

The game features systems for gathering and crafting items, feeding, and reproduction with genetic inheritance and variations. Each faction has one or more species, each with a different repertoire of abilities and statistics.

On each turn, the player controlling each faction must manage their animals, their reproduction, their active abilities, and gain control of the game's core: the tree from which all factions obtain food and items. The abilities and statistics of each species are determined by observable behaviors in real life in their natural state, but adapted to a board game to be playable.

—description from the designer

Cuatro facciones de animales bien diferenciadas compiten en un tablero de 13x13 casillas con la finalidad de ser la última que quede en pie. Las facciones son primates, felinos, elefántidos y rinoceróntidos, cada una con habilidades, objetos y estadísticas diferentes; pero todas con un fin: sobrevivir. El juego cuenta con sistemas de recolección y fabricación de objetos, alimentación y reproducción con herencia genética con variabilidades.

Cada faccción cuenta con una o varias especies, cada una con un repertorio de habilidades y estadísticas diferentes. En cada turno, el jugador que maneje cada facción tendrá que preocuparse de alimentar a sus animales, manejar su reproducción, administrar sus habilidades activas y hacerse con el control del núcleo de la partida: el árbol del que todas las facciones obtienen comida y objetos.

Las habilidades y estadísticas de cada especie están determinadas por comportamientos observables en la vida real en su estado de naturaleza, pero adaptadas a juego de mesa para ser jugables.

—description from the designer (Spanish)

boardgame: Out of the Limelights: The Battle of Upper Tajuña, March-April 1938
description:
The Battle of Upper Tajuña, March-April 1938.

With the Aragón Front breached in early March 1938 and Franco's army in their way to split the Spanish Republic in two, several attempts were made to weaken the breakthrough by launching diversionary attacks at other fronts. The only one to achieve some success was IV Corps attack at the Upper Tajuña river; but the feat sank into oblivion due to the scale of the Republican defeat at Aragón.
This game is a battalion level simulation of this bitter battle in a highly complex terrain. Rules emphasize initiative, limited logistics and time management.

—description from the designer
language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups
family: Players: Wargames with Rules Supporting Only Two Players

description:
Horse Racing (春秋赛马) is a two-player card dueling game inspired by the famous Chinese story of Tian Ji’s horse racing strategy. Set in the Spring and Autumn period, players assemble teams of legendary figures and horses, cleverly arranging their lineup to outmaneuver their opponent in a tactical series of races. With multi-use cards and evolving racefields, each decision can shift the balance of power.

This game is part of the third edition of the 9 Cards series.
publishers: 九张牌玩具公司(9 Cards Toy Company)

description:
Players take turns playing numbered bean cards and comparing them to the previous number.
If the number is higher, they can draw another card; if it's lower, they can score points from their hand.
However, running out of cards or exceeding the limit results in penalty points.

boardgame: Last Call: An Imaginary Drinking Game
description:
Heavy Hitter, Sorority Gal, and Babooshka are just some of the characters of Last Call.  They each have an objective number that represents how much alcohol they can drink in a night out without having a hangover the next day.  Think of blackjack, but instead of trying to hit "21", each player has their own unique number they are trying to hit.  Go over your "limit" and you are out.  Whoever gets closest without going over has maximized their night out and wins!
