But, unlike blackjack, you can also impact the hands of other players.  On your turn, you will draw a Draw Card which have point values ranging from one to six.  On your turn, you can also play a Strategy Card, which range in point value from negative six to positive six, either on yourself, OR on someone else, pushing them over their limit.

When you feel you are as close to your number as you will get without going over, you "stand" - you have had your Last Call.  Now you wait for everyone else to finish to see who "maximized their night out" without getting a "hangover."

No actual drinking is required for this fun game!

—description from the publisher

Cosmic Contraband is a fast-paced social bluffing game of lies, suspicion, and sneaky profits. Take on the role of a Smuggler, trying to sneak valuable cargo past inspection, or the Justicar, the self-appointed space law enforcer who decides what gets through… and what gets “confiscated” for personal gain. As a Smuggler, you’ll promise your cargo is completely legal because of course it is… but will you risk sneaking contraband through for bigger rewards? As the Justicar, your job isn’t to catch every lie—it’s to catch the biggest liars and take their goods for yourself. Bluff - Blame - Betray because in the end, the player with the most credits wins…

—description from the publisher

Adventure fantasy meets unhinged all-ages party game! Pitch wannabe heroes for hilarious quests to make your friends laugh and win!

Welcome to Dungeon Tryouts, your new favorite party game!

The premise? You run an adventuring agency where you try to find quests for wannabe adventurers.

The problem? The heroes in your roster all have "quirks" that make them hard to hire. They aren't exactly the cream of the crop–and neither are the gigs you have for them.

It's your job to pitch your hopeful heroes at quest auditions, spinning their weaknesses as strengths in the hopes of scoring them the job. In the tradition of adventure fantasy, you'll need to use your imagination.

Dungeon Tryouts is like Lord of the Rings meets Apples to Apples, or D&D meets Whose Line Is It Anyway? It packs the hilarious chaos of a roleplaying game gone off the rails into a breezy party game package that everyone can enjoy.

Battle of Sekihara (关原之战) is a 2-player card-driven board game focused on area control. Players step into the tense night when Ishida forces invaded the Tokugawa inner domain, with both sides on edge, competing to outmaneuver each other in troop deployment and strategy. With only nine castle cards forming the battlefield, every decision is critical in this compact yet strategic duel.


     Players secretly draw ambush markers tied to specific castle cards.
     Each round, players alternate selecting three different actions.
     Troops are deployed based on ambush strength and action effects.
     Majority control determines ownership of castle cards.
     Claimed castles form each player’s territory for the final phase.
     Players resolve all territory effects and compare total strength.
     Highest strength wins, with tie-breaker based on lowest base strength card.


This game is part of the third edition of the 9 Cards series.
publishers: 九张牌玩具公司(9 Cards Toy Company)

description:
Setting
A fantasy-themed trick-taking game where players form an adventuring party confronting a series of monsters.

Goal
Players must defeat monsters together by reaching the required attack strength, while competing to earn the most valuable rewards based on their contribution.

How do you play?
Each trick represents a battle against a monster with a defined attack requirement.
Players play cards to collectively meet or exceed this value. If the total is insufficient, the monster is not defeated.

If the monster is defeated, rewards are distributed based on card strength ranking:
the highest card gains the best reward, followed by lower-ranked contributions receiving lesser rewards.

boardgame: えだまめの国 (The Land of Edamame)
description:
Welcome to the Land of Edamame, where soybeans reign supreme.
In this game, you step into the boots of an edamame farmer. You roll dice to cultivate daring new varieties. The player who creates the most delicious combination will be crowned the Edamame Minister, complete with special privileges.
The match ends when a player collects three Edamame Cards, or when someone spends too recklessly and goes bankrupt.
publishers: ちょんまげ工務店 (Chonmage Game)

description:
Set on the planet Morrow, players take on the role of top executives of the Yena Federation, competing to become the next leader.

The goal is to earn the most contribution points by expanding influence, controlling territories, and completing missions.

This is a heavy strategy game combining worker placement, deck-building, and area majority. Players recruit allies, run “Shinogi” (operations), and secure key positions on the board while optimizing their engine and timing.

An educational logic and programming game for children aged 3 and up, focused on deciphering color sequences. Through trial and error, players must guess secret patterns using illustrated tiles. It encourages spatial awareness, memory, and reasoning.

How do you play?
Each player must decipher a secret color sequence created by their opponent. To do so, they will try different combinations and, using clues, gradually get closer to the correct solution. A perfect deduction game to introduce children to concepts such as pattern recognition, logic, and strategy.

Game goal
To figure out the secret color code using observation and trial and error.

Atrapa palabra is a game where the players have to finding objects starting with any of the letters available in the turn. When they find, the have to put the object around the card. Every player has to put at least one card per turn. When a player puts the fourth card around a letter and so surroundes it, he gets the card (one point)

Edo-Kyoto Relay Race 1977 is a sports game featuring a relay race [EKIDEN] from Tokyo to Kyoto.

Game A, enjoyable for beginners and families, is a game where you predict the rankings. 
A solo game is also available (30-minute playtime).

Game B features a five-section race between Tokyo Ueno and Hakone, with 6-8 universities competing. It's the only ekiden (relay race) game where you can easily record times. You can enjoy playing as a manager(coach), making changes to the entry list based on the athletes' condition and deciding when to make your move.

—description from the publisher
Players take cards in turn. The first player who gets one card of each of the 6 healthy foods wins. There are special card that remove cards from your deck, or take cards from opponents.

Rabbitopia is a competitive island-building game set on an uncharted volcanic island. You have been chosen as a prime candidate to become the next Chairman of the Kusia Trading Company, and the ultimate trial for succession has begun! Hire adventurers, gather resources, and construct facilities to transform this desolate island into a bustling, thriving town within a strict time limit.

But beware—mysterious rabbit-like monsters known as ""Rabis"" lurk in the shadows, waiting for the perfect moment to ambush your adventurers. You must adapt your strategy, hunt down the Rabis, and dedicate yourself to the island's defense.

Utilize your cunning strategy and sharp judgment to overcome countless obstacles and maximize your Contribution Points. Only the player with the greatest achievements will claim the prestigious seat of the next Chairman!

—description from the publisher

Slopes of Bordeaux is a tile‑laying puzzle based on the four‑color map problem, using distinctive pentagonal tiles.
You can score points in various ways—creating complex tile formations for high scores, taking consecutive turns, or aiming for the largest vineyard bonus—making it a highly strategic and puzzle‑like experience.

—description from the publisher
publishers: さとーふぁみりあ (Sato Familie)

description:
This is a two-player board game themed around baseball. It captures the intense psychological battle between pitcher and batter found in real baseball, allowing players to experience an immersive game enhanced by engaging mechanics and beautiful artwork.

—description from the publisher

Falsilux is a competitive game designed for two players.

As one who has reached the lighthouse, you must survive a single night together with your friend (your opponent) and the lighthouse keeper. Your only means of protection are the shard of the lens in your hand and your ability to detect inconsistencies in the words and actions of others.

Using the “Omen Cards” you receive and your conversation, you must deduce who the Mimic is, then seal its shadow within the lens shard to protect yourself. However, only one shadow can be sealed—either your friend’s or the lighthouse keeper’s.

If you are human, your objective is to correctly identify the Mimic and seal its shadow. If you are the Mimic, your goal is to prevent your friend from realizing it.

In either case, both players seek to expose each other’s lies.

—description from the publisher

• What is the setting?
A lively animal book festival called “BOOK FESTIVAL!” where players run their own bookshelf booths, displaying books and promotional POPs to attract customers.

• What is the goal?
Earn the most “Review Star” ratings from visiting customers and become the festival’s MVP booth.

• How do you play?
Players build and customize their personal bookshelf by placing book and POP cards. After upgrading, all players roll dice simultaneously to move animal customers through the bookshelf spaces (which function as a board). Customers stop, buy books based on their preferences, and award ratings. At the end, stars are tallied through a draw-based vote system to determine the MVP.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Here to Slay Dungeons:  Holographic Promo Card Pack
description:
Looking for the Holographic promo cards without the Hero Vinyl Figures? This add-on will come with all 13 Hero Holographic promo cards and cards sleeves to cover all of them. Just shuffle them in to play your Heroes with extra flair!

For the Hero vinyl figures, we took a different approach - each of these will come with a special holographic Item card designed for the vinyl figure series (which means these Item cards are not in any of the base games). We’ve seen a number of requests for extra loot in the game, and here’s your chance to add some special shiny Items to your game! Each Hero is paired with a different unique item:

Oh, and I almost forgot to mention, we did design one to go with the exclusive Rain vinyl figure as well ??

Since these items are unique to this set, we're offering Holographic Promo Card Pack as an add-on for $5 for anyone who isn’t collecting the vinyl figures (or just wants an extra set for gameplay)! Each pack will come with all 13 holographic Item promo cards (along with 13 card sleeves to protect them during your dungeon crawl adventure).

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Here to Slay Dungeons: Rain Hero & Meeple
description:
This bonus hero, Rain, is a new exclusive playable Hero that's a special callback to the original Here to Slay campaign. You can only get the Hero and meeple set during the campaign so don't miss out!

Don't miss out on the fun as we celebrate the legacy of the original Here to Slay game with this bonus set. Included are the Class card, Hero card, and Meeple for Rain the Unstable (Unicorn). Rain doesn’t have an assigned class (and can take on any two Schools of Abilities), so you can customize this character any way you want while bringing a nostalgic twist to your adventure! Plus, Rain comes with a completely unique Hero ability.


    This Hero & Meeple set will fit inside one of your Deluxe Edition base games, so you will not need to store them separately.



        All Early Followers and Former Backers will receive this as a FREE Gift that will automatically be added to pledges at the end of the campaign. If you are not an early follower or former backer, or just want an extra set, you can select this add-on.
    



            This add-on may ship inside your game box rather than in separate packaging
        
    



                A Print-and-Play version of Rain will be available before fulfillment begins to make sure all fans have access to this Hero .
            
        
    


—description from the publisher

boardgame: Birds on Birds: Duck, Duck, Squirrel Expansion
description: Hmmm … you don’t look like the others? Expand your bird stacking chaos with these dead-eyed rubber ducks and premium wooden squirrels to keep score (fits in Birds on Birds standard box).

Dog Spotting is a fun and fast series of games where the objective is simple: be the first player to spot the hidden dog in the crowd! These games are best played with 2 players, but can be enjoyed with more.

Race to spot dogs the fastest or work together to find them all!

Each game requires a set of Crowd Cards and some Dog Cards, depending on the game.

A dog will ONLY be on 1 of the 3 Crowd Cards.

They will look exactly as pictured on the Dog Card, although part of it may be hidden behind other dogs.

Have fun Spotting Dogs!

—description from the designer

A simple bidding game where all you need is your fingers! Decide the order for picking ships by the number of fingers you hold up on the count of three—but watch out! If you get too greedy, you might end up dead last. You also have a one-time-use “gun” to turn the tables in an instant! Collect the most treasure to win, but be careful: gather too many ghosts, and you’re out of the game. It’s a perfect mix of lively party fun and intense strategy. Use your luck and courage to seize victory!

—description from the publisher

Contains 25 Insect-Chips with 5 different insects on the back and 3 flowers on the other side

Setup: Flip all chips on the insect side and each player take a chip of each insect.

Gameplay: When you have the flowers in your wildflower meadow matching one of your chips you may flip this chip on its insect side. 
You now may use this insect.

Story: Robots Seeking Utopia
In a world after the extinction of humankind, autonomous robots wander the desolate land, dreaming of building their ideal city. Then, from a gear in a pile of rubble, they discover a "power to grant wishes" that even humankind hadn't noticed. "If we can create a powerful Great Clock with the power of this gear, we might be able to accelerate time and complete our ideal city…" The robots' story now begins.

Game Overview
Players draw Gear Cards from the deck and connect them in a "chain." Using the power of these gears, players can acquire Production Cards that give them an advantage in the game, and Clock Parts Cards essential for victory. Ultimately, the goal of the game is achieved by acquiring the City-Building Card.

Strategy and Features
There are many challenges to overcome. There are set rules for connecting the gears, and Calamity Cards appearing from the deck will hinder the player's progress. Deciding the order in which to acquire cards and when to use their effects is key to success.
The game consists of three difficulty levels, and the story changes as you progress. Starting with the creation of an agricultural village, it progresses to an efficient smart city. What will they ultimately desire after accelerating time in this way? The answer is revealed in the text of the final City-Building Card.

—description from the designer

In Gallery Gambit, players act as rival gallery owners competing over three rounds to curate the most prestigious art collections. The goal is to build three Valid Sets (e.g. consecutive run-in the same suit or cards of the same rank). On a turn, players can optionally discard a card before taking a core action: drafting a card from the shared Market to play into one of their three fixed-capacity Slots, playing their hidden Private collection card, or using a "Gambit" to rearrange their own cards or execute a "Sneaky Trade" with an opponent's incomplete Slot. Because a round instantly locks and ends right after one player fills all their Slots to capacity, speed is crucial; during scoring, players earn base points for completed Slots and compete in "Slot Battles" to win extra bonus points for having the superior collection, though any set containing a forgery (a Joker) automatically loses its battle.

—description from the publisher

Ki kakilt ide? ("Who Pooped Here?") is a children’s memory and matching game in which players pair animals with their droppings. Each tile features either an animal or a distinctive type of animal poop, and players must find the correct pairs by turning over tiles and remembering their positions.

The game builds on classic memory mechanics but introduces an unusual and humorous theme that engages young players. As tiles are revealed, players learn to associate animals with specific characteristics of their droppings, encouraging observation and recall.

In addition to the matching gameplay, the included booklet provides educational facts about animal droppings, offering light educational value alongside play. The game is designed for young children, with simple rules, short playtime, and scalable difficulty depending on the number of tiles used.

Frakk és a macskák ("Frakk and the Cats") is a children’s board game based on the classic Hungarian animated series featuring the dog Frakk and his ongoing rivalry with two mischievous cats. Players take on the roles of characters from the story and move them through the rooms of a household setting inspired by the original series.

During the game, players draw task cards that describe short scenarios or objectives. Using a limited number of actions each turn, they must move characters, interact with locations, and complete these objectives as efficiently as possible. Action cards can provide additional movement or special effects, adding light tactical decisions.

The goal is to complete a set number of task cards before the other players. The gameplay focuses on simple planning, movement, and objective completion, making it accessible for younger players while still offering engaging decision-making and replayability.

Schlock!B-Movie Magnate The X-pansion adds 99 unique new tiles to the board game, Schlock! B-Movie Magnate, across 3 new tile sets:
The CyberPulpNoir tile set adds globetrotting adventure, dystopian worlds and rain-soaked streets.
The Xploitation tile set catapults you into the 70’s grindhouse peak of the B-movie, featuring lunatics, murder and all things fantagoria horror!
The NSFW tile set contains movie-making for mature audiences, including cheeky adult settings and scandalous tropes.

First Down is a competitive American football strategy game where every play is a mind game. Outsmart your opponent and fight for every yard.

It is a head-to-head strategy experience focused on play-calling, anticipation, and decision-making, offering depth across a full match rather than relying on short, isolated moments.

Players take on the role of opposing teams, selecting plays simultaneously and trying to outthink each other on every down. Each decision carries weight—balancing risk, timing, and strategy is key to advancing the ball and ultimately scoring.

The game unfolds over multiple drives, requiring players to adapt their approach, manage momentum, and think several steps ahead to gain a lasting advantage.

Rather than relying on heavy randomness, the game emphasizes player interaction and psychological gameplay. Success depends on reading your opponent, adjusting your strategy over time, and executing the right play at the right moment.

Core Gameplay:

Simultaneous play selection creates constant tension
Strategic mind games on every down
Resource and risk management through cards and decisions
Structured turns with continuous player engagement

Game Experience:
Every down is a duel—but the full game is a battle of strategy and adaptation.

Will you make the safe call or go for a high-risk play?
Will your opponent predict your move or fall into your trap?

Across the full match, players must adjust their tactics, respond to changing situations, and maintain control over the flow of the game.

The game captures the strategic essence of American football while remaining accessible to players without prior knowledge of the sport.

—description from the designer

Szeretem az ovit - Elveszett játékok ("Lost Toys") is a children’s board game in which players help recover toys that were left behind in a park. Moving around the board, players search for their missing items and try to collect them as efficiently as possible before leaving the playground.

On their turns, players roll a die and move along interconnected paths, choosing directions freely to optimize their route. Along the way, they reveal and collect tokens representing lost toys, relying on memory to remember their locations and make better decisions in future turns. Special die faces may allow additional actions such as looking at hidden tokens or moving more freely.

The goal is to collect all required items and exit the board at the starting point. The game combines simple movement with memory and light planning, making it suitable for young children while encouraging observation and basic tactical thinking.

Óvodás vagyok! ("Kindergarten Day") is a children’s educational board game in which players experience the events of a typical day in kindergarten. The game takes players through different locations such as the playroom, dining area, dressing room, and playground, each associated with specific activities and tasks.

During the game, players move around the board and draw task cards related to the current location. Each card presents challenges that develop motor, logical, and verbal skills. The tasks are presented in two difficulty levels, allowing children of different ages and abilities to participate together.

The objective is to complete the route through the kindergarten day and return to the starting point. While players can compete to finish first, the primary focus of the game is on playful learning, cooperation, and skill development rather than competition.

Szabadítsd ki Ruminit! ("Free Rumini!") is an adventure and deduction board game set in the world of the Rumini stories. Players take on the roles of Rumini’s friends, who must locate and rescue him after he has been captured and hidden somewhere beneath the Pelevár bazaar. The exact location of Rumini is unknown at the start of the game and must be discovered through investigation.

During the game, players move across a network of locations, visiting shops where they can collect items and trade them for information. These clues are represented by special cards that can be checked against a hidden code system, gradually narrowing down the possible locations where Rumini might be imprisoned. Players must combine logic, memory, and risk-taking to interpret the information correctly and eliminate false leads.

The game features both competitive and cooperative modes. In the competitive version, players race to deduce Rumini’s location first, while in the cooperative mode, players work together to find him before an opposing character reaches a critical point. Additional luck cards introduce variability and tactical opportunities. The objective is to identify the correct location and successfully attempt the rescue.

Malac és Liba – Betakarítás ("Pig and Goose: Harvest") is a children’s game in which players help two animal characters collect and transport corn from the field. The objective is to load as many corn pieces as possible onto a truck while minimizing the number of pieces that fall or remain unused.

During the game, players roll a die to determine how many corn tokens they must take, then carefully place them onto a growing stack on the truck. As the load increases, the structure becomes more unstable, requiring attention and coordination when placing additional pieces. Players may also use special cards or actions to influence the process and improve their chances.

The game combines simple counting, light strategy, and dexterity elements. It encourages fine motor skills, planning, and risk assessment, making it suitable for young children and family play. The winner is the player who finishes with the fewest unused or dropped corn pieces.

Gruiiik is a fast-paced, family card game for 3–8 players. On each turn, players play cards to carefully manage the pig’s total weight, avoiding exceeding 100. The game features fun, illustrated cards and simple pictograms, making it easy for children and casual players to join in. Strategic choices and quick thinking are rewarded, while the playful theme and artwork create a lively social experience. Perfect for families and groups looking for a quick, engaging card game.

Sztárszobrász ("Master Sculptor") is a real-time party game in which players create objects, characters, or concepts using modeling clay while others attempt to guess what is being represented. Each round, a player or team draws a card and must sculpt the depicted subject before time runs out.

The game can be played individually or in teams. While one player sculpts, the others try to identify the object as quickly as possible. Correct guesses earn points, and players advance along a track or accumulate scores depending on the chosen mode. The included cards feature a wide range of challenges with varying difficulty levels, making the game accessible to both children and adults.

The gameplay emphasizes creativity, speed, and communication. Players must translate abstract ideas into physical forms using limited time and materials, often resulting in humorous interpretations. The objective is to score the most points by successfully sculpting and guessing during timed rounds.

The time has come to show your management skills and compete against managers across the city! Build your own mall and become the most profitable enterprise!

This tableau-building card game will see you build out a mall, find the best combos to earn the most amount of sales from the cards you have available, and win the game.

Every turn, play a new store from your hand, building out your mall. Calculate the sales for your stores and then draw back up to five cards. The game ends when every mall has seven stores. The player with the most money wins the game!

Set out from the hustle and bustle of the big city and see the cozy world of Everdell on your own two paws in Everdell Journeys.

In this card-drafting adventure, you'll lay down a path of cards featuring the breathtaking landmarks, riveting cities, and new friends you'll find on the road. Send your traveler along the path of cards to activate them, scoring points and gaining resources. Chart your course wisely and score powerful combos based on the cards in your path, filling your journal with memories to share by the hearth at your journey's end!

—description from the publisher

Garden of Alcinous is a 1 to 4 player tile placing strategy game inpsired by a tale from the Odyssey. Part Tetris, part Azul - Garden of Alcinous will challenge player's spatial reasoning skills as they jostle to design a garden worthy of the gods! Victory will hinge on completing daring combination moves and completing divine quests.

—description from the designer

Zip through space faster than ever before with 20 new rules for Warp 99!

There are few expected rules that tweak things, like =9 or +2, but the real fun comes from all sorts of new rules, like "Higher Even" (you can play any even number that is higher than the one shown on the discard pile) and "Not the Same as any cards in my Personal Card Row" (you can play any number or color that isn't in your Personal Card Row, so your options get smaller the longer your Personal Card Row gets!). There's a rule that let's you play the difference or sum between the two discard piles, and even one that lets you add cards to your Personal Card Row!

To play, just swap out one of the existing rule cards for one from the expansion!

Includes a reference guide for all rules.

The expansion and rules fit in the base game box!

—description from the publisher

Cryptid Smash is a fast-paced card game in which players race to be the first to play all the cards from their hand. Each card features a unique cryptid inspired by real folklore, hand-illustrated and set within the world of Kryptothera. While the objective is simple, the gameplay is highly interactive and often unpredictable.

On their turn, players must play a card that matches the colour, symbol, or effect of the top card on the playing pile, or draw if they cannot. Many cards include effects that disrupt opponents, alter turn order, or force players to draw or discard cards. These effects can be chained or combined, creating escalating interactions and sudden shifts in advantage.

In addition to standard cards, tactical and legendary cards introduce further variability. Tactical cards allow players to influence the flow of the game directly, such as choosing the next player or changing the active colour. Legendary cards trigger immediate, table-wide effects when drawn, regardless of turn order.

The game is won by the first player to play all cards from their hand, though play can continue until only one player remains. With frequent reversals, chained effects, and constant player interaction, the outcome can change rapidly until the very final turns.

—description from the publisher

Most skirmish games are about firepower. 
Deadwake is about firepower and the slow collapse of your operatives' minds under the weight of everything the battlefield is doing to them.

The Deadwake Miniatures Combat Rules is a complete, standalone skirmish wargame set in the post-apocalyptic science fiction universe of Deadwake: 96 pages of fully developed tactical rules, three faction rosters built around radically different combat philosophies, nine ready-to-run scenarios, a complete campaign system, and a force-building framework, all built around one core truth: the operative who loses their nerve first loses the battle.

This is a game of small squads, hard choices, and a battlefield that fights everyone simultaneously.

Three Ways to Play:
Play it as a pure tactical wargame, completely standalone with no RPG required. Play it as the combat resolution layer of a full Deadwake narrative campaign, where the wounds your operatives take on the tabletop carry directly into the story sessions that follow. Or play it as a hybrid experience where narrative choices create the conditions for each skirmish and the outcome of each battle rewrites the next chapter of your campaign. One book, three complete experiences, all using the same rules.

The Resonance Roll: One Mechanic, Every Action
Every action on the battlefield, from an Audit Executioner advancing through difficult terrain to a Gen-hanced Warden performing Overwatch as a free action to a CAD operative flooding an enemy's systems with a Data Spike, is resolved with a single unified dice pool mechanic. Build a pool of d6s equal to your relevant attribute, roll, and count successes. A result of 6 generates Overload, giving you a choice: take the clean result, or channel the Overload for a powerful benefit and make a secret Threadlock check to see what the psychic feedback costs your operative. The same risk-reward tension that drives the RPG lives at the heart of every exchange on the tabletop.

The Cohesion System: The War Inside the War:
Every Agent on the battlefield has two health tracks. Vigor measures physical damage. Cohesion measures psychological stability, tracked separately and fluctuating constantly as the battle grinds on. Witnessing a squadmate destroyed within six inches costs Cohesion. Failing a Threadlock check when channeling Overload costs Cohesion. Taking fire from weapons with the Terror quality costs Cohesion. When Cohesion breaks, the consequences range from a unit becoming Suppressed and losing its offensive capability, to Faltering and losing actions, to suffering a Psychological Fracture where the Facilitator may force the unit to freeze completely or attack the nearest model, friendly or enemy.

This is not flavor. It is the game's central tactical problem. The faction that ignores its operatives' psychological stability pays for it.

The Psychic Battlefield: The Environment as a Third Player
Every battlefield in Deadwake has an Anomaly Severity Index rating, from ASI 0 in a sterile corporate Clean Network to ASI 5 at the epicenter of a psychic cataclysm where reality has stopped following its own rules. In an ASI 2 zone, every Agent makes a Threadlock check at the end of each Resolution Phase. At ASI 3, all Command actions become harder as the Static interferes with coherent thought. At ASI 4, a natural roll of 1 on any die triggers an immediate result on the Resolve Malfunction Table. At ASI 5, the Facilitator introduces Mass Hallucinations, Identity Scrambles, and Sensory Deprivation effects that can turn your own operatives against each other.

Locus Points pulse with concentrated psychic energy, damaging minds but offering devastating temporary abilities to Psy-Sensitive units willing to risk the exposure. Static Storms drift across the board on random vectors. Psychic Echoes left by destroyed operatives raise the local ASI and force Threadlock checks on anything that enters their radius. The terrain does not wait patiently for you to solve it. It participates.

Three Factions, Three Completely Different Games
Consolidated Axiom Dynamics fields armored Audit Executioners as its line infantry backbone, relentless soldiers trained to trust the Logic Engine's network over their own instincts, supported by unarmed Clarity Drones that broadcast targeting solutions across the battlefield. The CAD game is one of patient network warfare: keep the Oracle alive, maintain Data-Linked Targeting synergies, and let the Optimized Firing Solution re-rolls grind the enemy into a statistical inevitability. Destroy the Oracle and the entire machine loses its eyes. CAD operatives advance without movement penalties for difficult terrain, but their total reliance on a centralized data network makes them critically vulnerable to hacking and command disruption.
The Helios Foundation wages war through biological control and surgical precision. Pheromonal Control imposes a penalty to enemy Resolve Checks whenever two or more Bio-Engineered units are in proximity, turning the mere presence of a Helios force into a psychological hazard. The Neural Calming Protocol pacifies enemies who have already taken Cohesion damage, preventing them from attacking entirely. Pruner subfaction forces gain bonus damage against any target already suffering from a negative status effect, creating a brutal loop where the first suppression leads to the next and the next. The Gen-hanced Warden is Helios's elite vanguard: genetically augmented for superior reflexes, carrying a high-damage flechette rifle and a toxin-laced blade, ignoring partial cover bonuses with Heightened Senses, and performing Overwatch as a free action.
Kenshin Industrial fields the disciplined Ashigaru Line-Holder as its line infantry: exceptional soldiers who gain bonus Resilience when they hold their ground without moving, the living embodiment of a force built to anchor a position while its elites decide the battle. That elite is the Kensai-Class Battleframe, Kenshin's masterwork walker: a machine that moves with the grace of a martial artist, carrying a long-range Amaterasu Heavy Railcannon and a master-crafted Vibro-Katana, re-rolling failed melee attack dice with Living Blade, and answering any melee attack it survives with an immediate free counter-strike via The Perfect Strike reaction. The Kenshin game rewards operatives who fight without cover, bonusing melee attacks and Dodge reactions when no terrain protection is claimed, and punishes enemies who refuse a Formal Challenge or try to disengage from one.

Legendary and Prototype Units
Each faction includes optional Legendary and Prototype units that define extreme playstyles. Exec-Inquisitor Malix carries no weapons. His power is entirely psychological and informational: Logical Deconstruction forces a penalized Resolve Check on any enemy within line of sight with the player choosing the failure result on the table; The Inevitability Model aura prevents enemies within twelve inches from re-rolling any dice; and every successful Data Spike inflicts Cohesion Damage in addition to its normal effect. He is the most dangerous operative in the game and has never fired a shot.

Subject Alpha, the First Bloom, is Helios's perfected biological specimen: an unarmed prototype with Monofilament Claws that ignore armor entirely, Uncanny Grace that allows a free Dodge reaction without spending a reaction point, and Engineered for the Kill that grants a free Move action after every melee kill, making it a thing that does not stop once it starts moving toward you.

Todoroki Akane pilots the Akuma Battleframe, a custom red walker built for velocity and aggression: Twin Oni-Claw Chain-Katanas that deal bonus damage on critical success, Demon's Speed granting a free Move after every kill, Pragmatist's Strike adding damage when attacking an already-engaged enemy, and Relentless Assault allowing two Attack actions in a single activation.

Nine Scenarios, Each a Different Problem
Asset Recovery, Hold the Line, Assassination, The Conduit, Data Breach, Biohazard Retrieval, Scavenger's Gauntlet, and The Ghost Signal each present a distinct tactical challenge with specific deployment rules, victory conditions, special rules that reshape how the board behaves, and faction hooks that connect each mission to the broader political landscape of the Wake. The Biohazard Retrieval scenario introduces a live mutation table where Organic units caught in spreading spore zones can gain permanent biological effects for the remainder of the battle, and forces with opposite objectives converging on the same three objectives simultaneously.

A Complete Campaign System
Three campaign structures accommodate every style of play. The Branching Narrative Campaign makes every battle outcome a story fork. The Escalation Campaign grows from small recruit-level skirmishes to full engagements between veteran forces and Legendary units. The Persistent Territory Control campaign plays out on a strategic map where control of each territory affects resource income and unlocks special battlefield conditions.

Campaign play includes the full Scar and Insight advancement system, downtime and readiness rules, faction-specific recovery protocols unique to CAD, Helios, and Kenshin, Reputation and Standing tracking with full Ripple Effect consequences, Ideological Pressure and Doctrine Review for operatives who act against their faction's core philosophy, Campaign Complications, Asset Management and supply scarcity rules, Commander Progression traits, Strategic Reserves and Reinforcement protocols unique to each faction, Cross-Faction Intrigue mechanics, and a Political Landscape tracker for managing the cold war between powers.

The Requisition Value System
Every unit has a calculated Requisition Value derived from its core stats, wargear, and special abilities. Agree on an RV limit before the game and build your force from there. A 100 to 150 RV game is a tight skirmish between small elite squads. A 250-plus RV engagement is a full combined-arms battle where Legendary units and subfaction upgrades operate at full scale. An advanced tournament calculation system adds range modifiers and ability complexity ratings for competitive play where every point matters.

Designed to Connect
Every stat on a Deadwake character sheet translates directly to the tactical map with no conversion required. If your group also plays the RPG, the same operative who survived a Helios interrogation scene last session is on the table this session, carrying those wounds. The story does not stop when the models come out.

96 pages. A complete skirmish wargame. Three factions with distinct tactical identities. Nine scenarios. A campaign system with genuine political consequences. And a battlefield that is already working against everyone.

—description from the publisher

The year is 2148. Earth and the rest of the solar system have been stripped of resources by mega-corporations like FaceTick and eMuk.

But there is still hope. Beyond Neptune lies the Kuiper Belt — a frozen frontier full of opportunities.

In “The Kuiper Initiative”, you take on the role of a spaceship captain exploring deep space. Discover new sectors, contact alien worlds, and upgrade your ship into a finely tuned engine of efficiency.

At the heart of the game is energy management. Each round, you must decide how to spend your limited energy: the more you invest in an action, the more powerful it becomes – but the fewer actions you can take.

Over 5 rounds, you will:

Explore space and move your ship to strategic positions. Timing is crucial – your rivals may exploit your discoveries and reach them before you.
Visit alien planets to make first contact with new civilizations. This unlocks missions and small mini-games that provide rewards and new objectives.
Deploy machines to harvest resources from the Kuiper Belt. Place them early for longer-lasting effects or wait until competition is lighter.
Upgrade your ship with technology that can bend the rules. But use your actions wisely – what good is the coolest ship if a rival’s rusty bucket reaches the best resources first?

Kuiper is a low-luck competitive strategy game that rewards planning, synergies, and smart execution. Read the game state, identify powerful combinations, and turn every unit of energy into an advantage.

Only the most efficient captain will emerge victorious.

boardgame: Die drei ???: Das kooperative Fan Quiz
description: In this cooperative quiz about „The Three Investigators“, you’ll need plenty of knowledge about the books, audio dramas and cover art of the beloved series. Each game consists of 20 questions. For every correct answer we move one step forward; for every wrong answer the opponent advances zero to four steps. In the end, we need to stay ahead to win the game together. During the game, we can also earn jokers and use them whenever we need a little help.

Overslay is a competitive medium weight strategy based card crafting game set in a distant world many years ago.

As a noble bounty hunter, you have been tasked by the king to defeat the monstrosities that roam the land.

You will need to buy weapons, armor and potions to go on journeys to rid the lands of these beasts. But beware, should you not have enough strength to defeat the monsters, you will become wounded and forced to rest, leaving your competitors to collect the bounty's and gain the kings favour.

The player with the most reputation points at the end, wins the game and is crowned hero of the land.

—description from the publisher

Get ready to challenge your mind and your luck in 3 Dragões ("3 Dragons"), a strategy game where your goal is to collect dragon parts and complete three powerful dragons before your opponents. Each dragon part played activates a special action, and you can even steal dragon parts from your opponents. Seek the perfect combination to accumulate more points and become the greatest Dragon Master!

—description from the publisher

Nevasca is a competitive card game for 2 to 4 players set in a world buried under snow, where each player must gather food, protect their cabin, and survive the arrival of a relentless winter storm. Players search for resources, recruit allies and animal companions, discover mystical artifacts hidden in the snow, and launch strategic attacks to steal resources from their opponents.

Throughout the game, players build up their cards to strengthen their shelter and endure both the dangers of nature and the actions of rival survivors. Each round brings the harsh effects of the blizzard, making careful planning and timely use of special cards essential. The first player to play 6 Food cards wins the game.

—description from the oublisher

The flames are rising in Inferno, the first expansion to Hotshots. Wildfires continue to burn across the land, putting both manmade structures and nature at risk. Your team of Hotshots has recruited new members, with new skills, and you’ll need them to contain the fire. There are reports of Civilians among the flames that need evacuating. If you can reach them in time, you can save them. New rewards are waiting for you as well as 2 new vehicles to help battle the blaze. Can you put out all the flames before too much is lost?

—description from the publisher
family: Players: Games with expansions that add solo play

description:
Swooble is a mad dash card and dice game where players go head to head in a race to match their dice rolls to the dice combinations on the card.

The first player to match their dice to the card AND slap the card wins the card. The player with the most cards wins.

Travel around the globe taking down different enemy targets, for a price….

Players control one of four different Hitmen or Hitwoman using the revolutionary Gear Card system. The rotating hexes on the Gear Cards allow different actions to chain together.

Plant bombs, poison food, or line up the perfect shot while sneaking through the cities of the world. Use the crowds to hide from the police and the secret service.

Choose from four different assassins to make the perfect hit against eight different targets. Use the money you make to outfit your assassin with top of the line weapons and tools.

The hex map changes every game. Can you take down all the targets? Choose your attack wisely!

—description from the publisher

Become the world's most renowned musician in this 1-4 player musical adventure game. Take on the role of an aspiring musician from various countries around the world and practice, perform, build your skills, and prove yourself to be the world's greatest virtuoso.

Players can choose a unique player character, as well as from up to 6 unique instruments: Violin, Piano, Cello, Trumpet, Clarinet, & Flute, each with their own unique instrument deck, story scenario cards, goal cards, and abilities. Players gain and spend action points each turn based on their upgrades & accommodations to perform various actions such as travelling to new locations or cities, practicing new pieces of music, shopping for instrument and upgrade cards, performing with orchestras, teaching lectures, competing in competitions, and more.

Musicians will earn money and renown from their exploits and must manage their finances and resources properly in order to plan efficient performance tours, secure the best venues & travel accommodations, win the most prestigious competitions, and increase their renown. Players can choose to buy upgrades from their own personal instrument decks which can increase their playing abilities and grant new skills for performances and competitions, or they can buy from a shared marketplace available to all players which contains upgrades for each musician's business management, support staff, travel plans, & well-being.

After 20 years (rounds), the players who scores the highest renown through performances, competitions, studio recordings, goal cards, and total wealth wins, and is officially the greatest Virtuoso of all time.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Super Liga: dónde tu eres el DT
description:
Become the manager and coach of your club in this soccer-themed board game of strategy and competition. Your goal is to turn your team into the most popular club in the world by winning championships and growing your fanbase. Build a competitive roster by buying and selling players, developing young prospects, and making the right decisions to strengthen your squad.

Take on rival teams in League matches where smart tactics and dice rolls decide the outcome. Choose your formations, exploit your players’ strengths, and manage every match carefully to achieve the best results. Lead your club to glory and earn the largest fanbase in the game.

Alone Against the Zone is a solo narrative survival game set in a mysterious and hostile exclusion zone inspired by Eastern European post-disaster landscapes. Blending horror, exploration, and resource management, the game casts the player as a lone stalker venturing into a dangerous territory filled with anomalies, abandoned facilities, and hidden threats.

Designed as a sandbox hexcrawl adventure, Alone Against the Zone emphasizes freeform exploration over linear storytelling. The Zone is generated and explored hex by hex, with each location presenting unique dangers, discoveries, and opportunities. Rather than following a branching narrative, players create their own stories through emergent gameplay, shaped by risk-taking, preparation, and chance.

Gameplay centers on survival and decision-making: managing health, equipment, and scarce resources while navigating hazardous environments and resolving encounters. Each expedition is unpredictable, driven by random events and discoveries that can quickly shift the situation from manageable to deadly.

A distinctive feature of the game is its legacy-style continuity. When a character dies—as they often will—the game does not end. Instead, players may continue exploring the same map with a new stalker, inheriting part of the previous character’s equipment or discoveries. This creates a persistent sense of progression and an evolving relationship with the Zone itself.

Atmospheric and unforgiving, Alone Against the Zone focuses on isolation, tension, and emergent storytelling. There are no companions—only the player, their decisions, and the ever-dangerous Zone.

Key Features:

Solo sandbox hexcrawl exploration
Persistent game world with character succession mechanics
Emergent narrative shaped by player decisions and random events
Resource management and high-risk survival gameplay
Strong thematic focus on isolation, danger, and discovery

How long can you survive—and what will you leave behind for those who follow?

—description from the publisher

Gather yer crew and ready yer hands ... it's time for Sneaky Scallywags!

This daring game of bluffin' and bold moves will have pirates young and old guessin' who's hidin' the gold. Watch close as yer rivals perform sneaky gestures like Swab the Deck and Sails Up! Only one hand holds the treasure so ye better choose wisely. Guess right, and the coin is yers! But beware ... some coins be cursed!

Can ye bluff yer way to bounty?

Finspan: Sharks & Reefs adds to the variety of the core game with a focus on sharks and fish that live among coral reefs. This expansion introduces new coral reef habitats to your ocean mat and more incredible sharks—with fearsome new abilities!

Players can now nurture colorful coral reefs in each of their ocean's three dive sites. Healthy reefs enable you to play powerful reef fish, unlock fish abilities, and score bonuses at the end of the game. Meanwhile, sharks scatter schools of young (to form even more schools elsewhere) and leave behind food scraps that any fish in your ocean can consume.

To play this expansion, you need the Finspan core game.

—description from the publisher

27 Up, 27 Down is a solo hand-management card game where you step onto the mound in pursuit of one of baseball’s rarest feats: a perfect game.

Played with a standard 52-card deck, three suits combine to form a nine-batter lineup, each hitter represented by three cards that reflect their skill and ability to adjust over nine tense innings. The final suit becomes your strategy deck—giving you the tools you need to stay in control and keep every batter off base.

With elite defense behind you and 27 outs standing between you and history, do you have the Stuff to finish the job?

boardgame: Novel Beginnings: A Quiz Deck of Opening Lines
description:
“Call me Ishmael.”
Do you recall the opening line of your favorite novel, the sentence that drew you into the story’s world so suddenly that you couldn’t stop reading? The novel beginnings in this 48-card quiz deck will test your memory of the books you’ve read and provide you with others to add to your reading list. The card fronts contain an opening line, and the card backs reveal the title and why it and its author are noteworthy. And to keep you on your literary toes, two novels’ “first lines” actually introduce their second paragraphs. Can you find them? Here’s a hint: their authors’ initials are S. A. and C. I. Oh, and we slipped in one play, but it’s widely known, and we couldn’t resist. Happy reading!

boardgame: The Heart and Brain Quiz Game
description:
Put how well you know friends and family to the test with a game from the creator of the New York Times bestselling Heart and Brain comic series, the Awkward Yeti! Guess your opponent's responses correctly and win!

Will the answer be no-nonsense, poetically deep or absolutely crazy?
This game of intuition and humor will gauge just how well you can predict your peers.
The Heart is emotionally nonsensical, the Brain is logically responsible while the Yeti is absolutely unpredictable.

Language brings the world together - well, except maybe in this case. The concept is deceptively simple: Just use the 'lettered' dice to form as many words as possible in a crossword-esque fashion. Oh, and do it as fast as humanly possible - because it's a race against the clock - and your friends' inflated egos.

The kicker? You can only use the letters that you roll. No matter how terrible they are. So good luck with efgiiimdddxvrjz. You'll need it.

A mysterious note found among your grandfather’s belongings draws you to Mirosia Airport — decades after a catastrophic earthquake reduced it to rubble. Burdened by debt and failing health, you’re chasing the prospect of unexpected wealth. But as you move through the ruined terminal, the clues scattered among the wreckage begin to tell a different story: your own family’s past is tangled up in whatever happened here. Can you force the airport to give up its secrets, or will it claim you along with everything else it has swallowed?

Gameplay focuses on investigation and deduction through a deck of cards. Players explore the abandoned airport by revealing cards, reading narrative fragments, examining atmospheric illustrations, and collecting clues that must be analyzed and connected. Escape-room-style puzzles challenge players to recognize patterns and contradictions while the full picture of what happened at Mirosia slowly comes into focus. The rules are simple and unobtrusive, keeping attention on atmosphere, reasoning, and storytelling. The cards remain intact, allowing the game to be replayed.
To win the game, players must piece together the evidence and determine what truly occurred at Mirosia Airport — and what role their own family played in it. Success depends on correctly interpreting clues, separating coincidence from conspiracy, and forming a coherent explanation that accounts for everything uncovered along the way. The game is won when the final conclusions align with the full body of evidence gathered across 72 cards.​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​

Masquerade Trick Party takes place at a masquerade ball. Behind every mask lies a secret desire—and a role to play. Dance gracefully in the ballroom, then offer a rose to the one you believe you’ve found. Tonight, your hidden feelings will decide the ending.

In Masquerade Trick Party, players receive a Role Card with two goals: a Day Mission and a Night Mission. During the Ball Phase, players play a trick-taking game for 10 tricks, managing how many tricks they win. After the final trick, players enter the Offering Phase: all players place their Rose token on the Role they believe is the target of their Night Mission. Then roles are revealed and missions are checked.

The game includes 12 roles for varied play and shifting stories from game to game. It supports 3–5 players, with additional 2-player and solo variants. Clear iconography and short playtime make it easy to teach, while the hidden-role deduction keeps every round tense until the final offering.
publishers: Mysboard Games (ミスボドゲームズ)

description:
Somewhere in the American Midwest, a derelict circus ground seems like the perfect setting for a college graduation party — unsettling enough to be fun, abandoned enough to be yours for the night. But the charred remains of Circus Inferno have a way of making visitors feel less than welcome. A fire tore through this place years ago and left nothing but wreckage and unanswered questions. Was it an accident, or did someone make sure it burned? You’re still turning that over in your mind when one of your friends vanishes without a trace. The party is over. The investigation has begun.
Gameplay focuses on investigation and deduction through a deck of cards. Players explore the ruined circus by revealing cards, reading narrative fragments, examining atmospheric illustrations, and collecting clues that must be analyzed and connected. Escape-room-style puzzles challenge players to recognize patterns and contradictions while the truth behind the fire — and the disappearance — slowly comes into focus. The rules are simple and unobtrusive, keeping attention on atmosphere, reasoning, and storytelling. The cards remain intact, allowing the game to be replayed.
To win the game, players must piece together the evidence, determine what really happened the night the circus burned, and find out what became of their missing friend. Success depends on correctly interpreting clues, separating accident from arson, and forming a coherent explanation that accounts for everything uncovered along the way. The game is won when the final conclusions align with the full body of evidence gathered across 72 cards.

The DJ Character Expansion adds 1 additional playable character to the game and comes with 3 acrylic standees, 3 character tokens, 38 game cards, 1 reference sheet, 2 turntable tiles, 4 circular vinyl disk cards, and 1 tuck box.

In Scrown, players are dealt one face down pocket card and the rest of the deck is spread out in the middle of the play area. During a 15 second timer, players may draw as many or as few cards as they like from the center, faceup and in real time. Most cards in the deck have numbers, and lower numbers are more desireable. However, there are also 6 skull cards in the deck which eliminate the player who draws them from the game. There is also one crown card which causes the player who draws it to instantly win. At the end of the timer, whoever has the lowest card wins, unless any player's pocket card (alive or dead) cancels that number, in which case the next lowest wins, and so on.

—description from the designer

QurumQ is a competitive financial life simulation board game where 2-6 players build careers, invest wisely, and navigate life events over 8-10 rounds, each representing one year.

The goal: reach $1,000,000 in total capital OR $30,000/round in passive income before anyone else.

Unlike Monopoly, your character has a real life: a career with two distinct branches (management or expert track), health that can deteriorate, skills you earn through education, and a network of connections that open doors. Unlike CashFlow, each session takes under 90 minutes.

How it plays:
Each round, you collect your salary, draw an event card (Work, Learn, Connect, or Life), and make one action: invest in stocks, buy property, start a business, get educated, or build connections. Career promotions require specific skill and connection thresholds. Macro-economic events affect the entire market, forcing everyone to adapt.

What makes it different:

     Career ladder with meaningful progression (Specialist to CEO)
     Four resource types: Money, Health, Skills, Connections
     Investment catalog with real financial logic (stocks, property, startups, businesses)
     Macro events that shift the market for everyone
     Every decision is a genuine trade-off


Two editions:

     Original Edition (14+): Competitive race to financial independence
     Family Edition (10+): Cooperative mode with simplified rules


QurumQ was designed by an HR professional with 25 years of experience building teams. The game grew out of corporate training sessions and hundreds of hours of playtesting in Wroclaw, Poland.

—description from the designer

Nippon: Genro is a major expansion for Nippon: Zaibatsu that adds a powerful new layer of interaction.

The expansion deepens what already makes the base game strong, introducing new strategic dimensions and increasing complexity through decisions. Designed to synergize with the core production → delivery arc, Genro reshapes familiar strategic patterns and can redefine your entire game plan.

Each Genro also imposes a persistent global constraint or penalty, shifting priorities and making their influence impossible to ignore. The expansion includes five Genro, and you choose two for each game, creating 10 possible combinations that dictate the flow, tension, and tempo of play. You choose them to fine-tune the experience for your group, keeping every session fresh and intentional.

While the real-world Genro were influential advisors and key figures behind Meiji-era reforms, the game uses collective, fictionalized representations of various state actors rather than specific historical figures.

—description from the publisher

A fan made card for the boardgame Terraforming Mars that was released for the Dutch Open Terraforming Mars 2023. The fan made card is named 'Floyd Continuum' to honour the efforts by Floyd, the organiser of the Dutch Open, for building a Terraforming Mars-community throughout Europe. The card was designed in cooperation with FryxGames.

The card costs 5MC to play and has a science tag. It's action reads: "Gain 3MC for each completed terraforming parameter".

Go on RASALVA! is a tile-placement game for 2 to 3 players set on Rasalva, a moon of the cursed planet Éthimor. Players take the role of alien prospectors — Cyclortes, Vinériens, or Blorcks — competing to become the Chief Administrator of this resource-rich satellite.

Each turn, players spend resources to place hexagonal tiles on the board: Mines (black border), Drills (blue border), or Greenhouses (green border). Placing a tile adjacent to your own tiles earns bonus resources; placing it adjacent to an opponent's Greenhouse forces you to pay them resources. Neutral Simple Extraction tiles (red border) can be played at no cost but block eye formation.

The key strategic move is creating an "Eye": surrounding any tile with exactly 6 non-neutral tiles places a Research Post on it — scoring points and displacing any opponent's alien there.

Turn order is dynamic: the player with the fewest resources in reserve always goes first, creating constant reversals. The game ends the moment the last Research Post tile (white border) is placed, triggering immediate final scoring.

Scoring: 1 pt per Greenhouse · 2 pts per Drill · 3 pts per Mine · 4 pts per Research Post (5 for the final one) · bonus points for resources remaining in reserve.

—description from the designer

boardgame: IMJIN WAR 1592-93: First Japanese Invasion of Korea
description:
The Imjin War relates the story of one of the toughest conflict in the end of the 16th century, depicting the invasion of the Joseon kingdom of Korea by the Japanese. The Japanese must capture possibly the whole of the peninsula from their Busan foothold in the South. The Korean player must repel the invasion with the help of China...

The Japanese have very numerous land forces at the start of the game, but their navy, although numerous, is weak and poorly led and their goal is to conquer all of Korea in order to reach – and conquer - China.
The Korean player must first hold desperately against the initial invasion and superior enemy troops, waiting for Chinese reinforcements, counting on local rebellion, and most of all his powerful navy to alter the course of events.

Seizing the main areas, winning land and naval battles, gaining allies will prove the decisive factor in winning this war.

The game events and tactical chits allow full replay ability thanks to the numerous various situations that their create on the diplomatic, military.
Scale: One combat unit counter represents 500 (samurais) to 2,000 men (soldiers and civilian servants) or cavalry on its front (full side) and half that on its back (reduced side).
One artillery/fire wagon/siege weapon counter represents about ten guns/rocket launcher/towers and half that on its back.
One naval unit counter represents 10 light ships, 5 medium ships or one heavy or super-heavy ship and half that or reduced on its back.

Turn length: One month in fair weather (March–October); two months in inclement periods (November–February).

—description from the designer
language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups
family: Players: Wargames with Rules Supporting Only Two Players

description:
On the trail of survival, dinosaurs are competing for their spot. But, it is a viscous trail, where many animals will become someone's lunch or pushed out into obscurity. This is the Jurassic Trail, a game for 1-2 players consistent of only 18 cards. Whoever pushes out, or eat more dinosaurs at the end of three rounds, will become the master of the trail, by scoring the most points.

Each turn player can play only one card from his hand (unless there is a special ability activated from a card that breaks this rule).
By playing a card, player is moving a dinosaur his full walking speed form the top, or from the bottom of the Jurassic Trail. Direction is shown by the arrow on that card's objective ribbon. If arrow goes in both directions then player can choose from which side to enter the trail.
Card moves by 'walking' over other cards considering them as spaces of a track. If it moves beyond the trail and doesn't go beyond the sixth card, it will just became a part of the trail, either on top, or on the bottom.

Village Stroll is a game for 1-2 players where you will form a row of cards representing the path traveled from your memory, trying to accomplish tasks and score as much points as possible through 3 rounds of play.

Game is played in three possible rounds. Each round first player changes. If in two first rounds one player wins by better score, third round is not played. But, if each player manages to win one round, third round is played and the first player of a third round is the one that scored less tasks in a previous round.

Each round players will do one common action – take one card from the memory row, place it in your row and then either activate the action from the card or skip it.
Round lasts until both players have 8 cards in their rows, after that each player will score their row of cards.

Build your burger.
Smash the rest.

Be the first to prepare all the burgers on the menu by stacking the ingredients in the correct order and preventing others from beating you to it: stealing, burning, or swapping ingredients is not only allowed, but essential to win!

—description from the publisher

Sé el primero en montar tus hamburguesas mientras estropeas las de todos los demás.

En Smash Burger, el objetivo es sencillo: prepara las hamburguesas lo más rápido posible, apilando los ingredientes en el orden correcto.

Pero cuidado… porque mientras tú cocinas, los demás harán todo lo posible por sabotear tus creaciones y arruinarte el menú.

—description from the publisher (Spanish)

Stapel die Zutaten deines Burgers – und smashe die Burger der anderen!

Sei die erste Person, die alle Burger auf der Speisekarte vollendet, indem du die Zutaten in der richtigen Reihenfolge stapelst – und gleichzeitig verhinderst, dass andere dir zuvorkommen.

Klauen, Verbrennen oder Tauschen von Zutaten ist nicht nur erlaubt, sondern absolut notwendig, um zu gewinnen!

—description from the publisher (German)

Proung-Nee-Ruay: The Path to the Grand Prize
Unlock the mystical power of numbers and claim your destiny! In this game, the ultimate "First Prize" lottery ticket is within your reach—if you can decode the sacred secrets and outsmart your rivals. Inspired by the vibrant culture of Thai spiritual beliefs and the thrill of the national lottery, this is where faith meets strategy.

What’s the challenge?
Six hidden digits stand between you and a life-changing fortune. As a player, your mission is to arrange your numbers to match the winning Thai lottery result. But you aren’t guessing in the dark—you’ll harness the power of Sacred Thai Spirits and divine charms to gain clues and gain an edge.

How to play?
Every turn, you must reveal a Number Card to the table. Choose wisely—once a number is revealed, it can never be taken back. As players unveil their numbers one by one, you’ll need to observe their moves, decode the hints, and anticipate the final draw.

The Grand Reveal:
Once everyone has revealed 5 cards, the moment of truth arrives! The hidden First Prize is unveiled.

Earn 1 Point for every matching digit.

Boost your Score: Match the exact position of the official Thai lottery draw to earn 6 Bonus Points.

With a total of 12 points up for grabs, will you be the one to master the miracle? In this game, anyone can become a millionaire—but only the sharpest mind fueled by faith will claim the ultimate victory.

Are you ready to see if "Tomorrow You Are Rich"?

—description from the designer

A catastrophic meteorite is racing toward Earth, tearing through time and space — and  a handful of brave explorers race to their time machine before the world collapses.
In Meteorite Rush, players become time travelers thrown into a vibrant prehistoric world, where every path is unstable and every decision matters. With the help of powerful dinosaurs and ever-shifting routes, you’ll race against the clock, adapt to constant danger, and fight to reach safety before impact changes everything.
Designed as a fast-paced family adventure, Meteorite Rush blends intuitive rules with smart, modern dice mechanics that turn every roll into a meaningful choice. As you move across branching paths, unlock special abilities, and master the rhythm of risk and reward, each game unfolds differently — creating tense races, dramatic comebacks, and unforgettable shared moments.
Whether you choose to cooperate as a team or compete for victory, Meteorite Rush delivers a flexible, replayable experience that brings together players of all ages around the table for one thrilling journey after another.

—description from the designer

A fast, tactical card game where players collect cards from the Forest Floor and build stashes to specific values. Earn Acorn Tokens during play (especially for collecting NON-Green cards) and at the end of the game the player or team with the most Acorns wins.

Each turn, you must play exactly one card, choosing one of three actions: Collect, Build a Stash, or play to the Forest Floor. Playing a green card allows you to collect cards from the Forest Floor. You may take all cards that match the green card’s number, any number of combinations that sum to that value, or a mix of both in a single play. All collected cards, along with the green card used, are placed into your Score Pile. If you cannot or choose not to collect, you may instead build a stash or discard to the Forest Floor.

You may play any card to build a stash, which is a set of cards on the table totaling a declared value. You can create or add to a stash using cards from your hand, the Forest Floor, or both, as long as you either hold a card matching the stash’s value or that value has already been established by your partner. Stashes remain in play until collected with a green card of the same value, though their value may be changed when adding cards—unless the stash is “squirrelled away.” A stash becomes fixed and cannot change value once it contains either multiple cards of the same number or multiple valid combinations equaling its total.

Before taking your main action, you may spend one Acorn token to refresh your hand by discarding any number of cards and drawing the same number, then proceeding with your turn. At the end of each hand, the dealer places one Acorn token on every card and stash left on the Forest Floor; these remain until collected and may accumulate across rounds. During play, collecting with a green card earns immediate Acorns based on the colors of non-green cards collected, with a bonus for clearing the Forest Floor. At the end of the final hand, the last player to collect also takes all remaining cards and stashes, gaining any Acorns on them. Afterward, the player or team with the most cards earns a bonus, and the winner is whoever finishes with the most Acorn tokens.

—description from the designer

Top 10 is a refreshingly different trivia card game that flips traditional quiz night on its head. In this clever party game, the goal isn't to come first - it's to strategically aim for tenth place! Players guess answers to entertaining questions spanning music, food, world geography, and pop culture, then discover how their responses rank. The closer your answer lands to the coveted tenth spot, the more points you earn without going over. This unique scoring system levels the playing field, making it perfect for mixed groups where not everyone is a trivia buff.

With quick rounds that keep the energy high and questions designed to spark conversation and laughter, Top 10 transforms game night into an engaging experience where being last is actually winning. No encyclopedic knowledge required - just bring your best guesses and prepare for unexpected results.

Scoring Example: Top 10 countries by population.
Person 1 Guess: USA
Person 2 Guess: Brazil
Person 3 Guess: Ethiopia
Person 4 Guess: Japan

Scoring: Person 1 gets 3 points as USA is 3rd highest, Person 2 gets 7 points, Person 3 gets 10 points!!! and Person 4 gets 0 points as Japan is 12th place. As you can notice, the closer to tenth place you are the more points you receive!

How to win: To win the game, the player that reaches fifty points first wins! You can also play a time variation where the person who has the most points after ten rounds wins!

—description from the designer

Wingsuit Flying (翼装飞行) is a fast-paced adventure board game for 2–4 players where competitors soar through treacherous mountain skies in wingsuits. Players must carefully manage altitude, speed, and direction, making daring sprints or cautious maneuvers to navigate hazards, rise with air currents, and collect useful items. The game ends when a player crosses the finish line, with the highest combined score of altitude and completed actions determining the winner.

This game is part of the third edition of the 9 Cards series.
publishers: 九张牌玩具公司(9 Cards Toy Company)

description:
Lairs: Kickstarter Edition includes the following items not included in retail version of this game:

- Lairs: Adventurer's Pack
Unique attribute cubes unlock powerful abilities, and you'll need them to overcome the original monsters and traps your opponent will have at their disposal. Includes four tuckboxes and one vault box adding impactful elements that introduce new concepts. Also includes six envelopes adding content for concepts you already understand.

Kickstarter exclusive goodies
- Two customized full-size Lairs pencils
- One Hungry Blob Eraser
- One Hungry Blob enameled metal pin
- Six real metal treasures chests
- Three Wooden start tiles and two wooden exit tiles
- One kickstarter-exclusive monster with two matching wooden monsters
- One kickstarter-exclusive trap with two matching cardboard trap tiles
- A kickstarter-exclusive treasure set comprised of three new treasures
- One exclusive gold-foiled Guild Membership card
- Skelepede boss monster mini-expansion

You’ve worked hard to earn your spot in the Adventurers Guild, but one last obstacle remains. You must square off with a cunning rival guild hopeful in an exam of precarious paths that will test your skills at building a devious dungeon...and exploring one. To pass, you have to complete the tricky labyrinth your opponent has created full of dead ends, traps, enchanted monsters — oh, and if you can find it, valuable treasures. You enter their dungeon with your own set of skills and over the course of this challenge, you'll grow as an adventurer. Will it be enough to map the right path, defeat the monsters in your way, and escape with the most valuable collection of treasure? Your spot in the Adventurers Guild depends on it!

Lairs is a two-player game for ages 10 and up that plays in about 35 minutes. Players begin with a set of walls, traps, treasures, and monsters, then use them to create a challenging catacomb for their opponent to explore while at the same time attempting to navigate their opponent's dungeon using guile, deduction, and a little bit of luck. The stakes are high! Only one player can make it into the Guild!

description from the publisher's website

In Hidden Heist, everyone is after the same loot. The gems are hidden, and the thieves compete to find them first. Will you manage to steal 3 gems before anyone else?
Hidden Heist is a fast-paced and competitive game where you draw cards to sort your sequence before everyone else. Each round you win gives you the chance to take a key and find out if it hides a gem. Plan carefully, use the powers to your advantage, and be the first to collect three gems to win.

Kings and Peasants is a fast-paced card game about climbing the social ladder, stepping on egos, and doing whatever feels morally acceptable for roughly twenty minutes.

Each round, players compete to get rid of all the cards in their hand as quickly as possible. The first players to empty their hands becomes the King and the Prince, while the last players become the Peasant and the Servant.

At the start of each round, cards are shuffled and dealt to all players. Before play begins, players exchange cards according to their rank - the Peasants must give their strongest cards to the Kings, while the Kings return any cards of their choice (typically their lowest, for obvious reasons). The round then starts with this imbalance already in place.

To give lower-ranked players a fighting chance, the game includes a set of ability cards. Some provide powerful, unique effects that can shift the outcome of a round, while others offer no benefit at all, life huh.

The game continues over multiple rounds, with players constantly shifting positions in the hierarchy. The objective is simple - be the fastest to get rid of your cards and secure the highest rank as often as possible.

The easiest way to win the game is to have luck on your side, if thats not an option, count cards, strategize and just do other smart things in order to not end up a peasant.

—description from the designer

Fablehaven Adventures is a cooperative board game for 1-4 players that lets you immerse yourself in the world of Fablehaven like never before. Take the role of beloved characters as you journey together through four connected campaigns, each revealing a new chapter of the story. Use an intuitive push-your-luck bag-building system to test your fate, grow stronger, and evolve over time. With hundreds of characters, creatures, and artifacts to interact with, every playthrough presents new challenges to overcome and secrets to explore.
