How to Play: Begin by selecting a campaign: Drink the Milk, Midsummer’s Eve, Petition the Fairy Queen, or The Battle for Fablehaven. Each campaign offers a unique story and challenges. Prepare your bag with Quality and Dread tokens, and place your character on the board. On your turn, draw and resolve an Event card - from helpful boons to dangerous threats, group activities, side quests, and more. Then choose to search your location or challenge a threat. Draw tokens to see if you succeed. Successful actions earn resources for the group and experience to grow your character. Work together to advance through the campaign before the Event deck runs out – or you’ll leave Fablehaven with secrets undiscovered.

Fablehaven: Woodland Battles is a fast-paced card game for 2-8 players, pitting Fairies against Imps in a battle of strategy and chaos. Work together with your team, wielding a unique deck of powerful cards to outplay your opponents and turn the tide of battle. Call upon the Fairy Queen for powerful aid, or unleash the devastating wrath of the ancient demon. With constant momentum and flexible team sizes, every game feels like a magical showdown.

How to Play: Players divide into two teams: Fairies vs Imps. Each player is dealt cards from their team deck. On your turn, you and your opponent select a card, with both cards being revealed simultaneously. You might pick a powerful card to win its eventual duel. Or you might pick a card that boosts the active duel, but will need more help to win its own in the future. Play your cards right and you’ll summon your team’s champion. At the end of every turn, the active duel is resolved. Once either team wins 10 duels, they win.

Three Kingdoms to One (纷争三国) is a competitive board game for 3–4 players set during the chaotic era of the Three Kingdoms. Players face nine cities and must discover efficient ways to activate them, develop their military strength, and expand their influence in order to unify the divided land.

Over three rounds, players take turns placing their officer dice to activate city actions, gather resources, recruit and move soldiers, learn skills, and engage in battles. Strategic positioning and timing are key, as players compete for control and scoring opportunities across the map.


     Place dice between or around city cards to trigger one or two actions
     Manage resources (money and food) to recruit, move, and develop
     Control cities through military presence and gain majority bonuses
     Engage in combat and raid opponents to gain points and disrupt plans
     Score points each round and during final scoring for cities, resources, and control


This game is part of the third edition of the 9 Cards series.
family: Theme: Romance of the Three Kingdoms
publishers: 九张牌玩具公司(9 Cards Toy Company)

description:
In Micro Hero: Achilles, the legendary Greek hero is no longer a warrior, but a demanding coach training an Olympic team. With your entire deck held in your hand, you develop your athletes, organize their progression, and optimize their positioning to build the most efficient training cycle possible.

The game is played over four Training cycles. During each cycle, you interact with a limited number of visible cards, choosing whether to assign athletes to your Support Area to generate resources, or to train them to higher levels. As athletes grow stronger, they unlock new abilities and scoring potential. Managing your resources, timing your upgrades, and controlling the order in which cards cycle through your hand are key to building a powerful and consistent system.

Unlike traditional card games, the order of your deck is central to the experience. Every action influences how your cards will reappear in future cycles, turning your deck into a structured sequence that you must carefully plan and refine. Additional objectives, called Trials, guide your strategy and reward efficient play throughout the game.

At the end of the fourth Training cycle, you score points based on your athletes’ levels, their position in the deck, and your coach’s final state. The goal is to achieve the highest level of prestige by creating the most optimized and well-organized training engine.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: EXIT: The Game – Kids: Puzzle Fun in the Sea
description:
Dive into an adventure on the ocean floor! Shelly the mussel has lost her sparkling pearls, but Sam the turtle sets off with her to find them. At the far end of the reef, curious sea creatures await, who will only hand over the pearls in exchange for solving tricky riddles. Work together to solve exciting tasks and help Sam retrieve the pearls! Six different types of puzzles, which can be combined in countless ways, await one to four children aged five and up. The game materials are reusable, so EXIT - The Game for Kids can be played again and again.

—description from the publisher (translated)

boardgame: The anarchy of Stephen and Matilda
description:
A card game driving meeples and cubes on a map of England and Wales 1135-1155.

Four  card decks cover the period of the game covering appointing earls, raiding, sieging, Church matters, rebellions annnd even a Crusade.

The card effects are reflected in control of Major Fortifications, armies that appear and disappear to fight battles of besiege locations, and "Waste" that reflects the destruction of the period "When Christ and his saints slept."

There are also historical personality cards

Victory comes from political control (Lords and Fortifications) and by having a viable heir when King Stephen dies.

—description from the publisher

The Black Fog is alive; and it is hunting us.

''For you see, "The Murk" and its reality-distorting presence will never stop chasing them. It is an encroaching wave of Shadow that corrupts everything in its path. When distant drums begin to pound, and the earth itself bleeds an oily ichor--you know The Darkness isn't far behind.

Darkwise is an apocalyptic horror skirmish game that pits warbands of bounty hunters, cyborgs, monstrous beasts, and other horrible denizens against one another to survive their world's inevitable doom.

A focused Campaign system allows warbands to persist and improve (or die) between missions. Use your influence to gather new weapons and gear; train your followers and grant them rare Traits; and roll to see if they are forever maimed by their violent struggle.

     Build a warband of followers from one of 5 unique factions, each with their own specialties, armaments, and playstyles.
     Investigate mysterious, random events on the battlefield and reveal that which was once concealed by Darkness.
     Over 50 pieces of equipment and odd artifacts to bestow upon your haggard warriors.
     Every Scenario is a race against time. The Murk creeps ever forward, and those who bask too long in its delirious Shadow become undone by it.


What You Need

     4-8 Miniatures that represent your warband
     Percentile dice or 2d10
     A 3x3’ area for 2 or 3 players; or a 4x4’ area for 4 players
     A deck of standard playing cards
     Tokens or dice to mark Action Points, Wounds, Discoveries, etc.
     Pen & paper to record your warband roster & character changes
     Appropriate tabletop terrain features for each scenario.


—description from the publisher

Tetra Tower is a fun and challenging game!
The balance between logical reasoning and motor coordination is fundamental!
The objective is to fit all the pieces onto the base (pendulum) without letting them fall, preserving balance each round.
The cards dictate the rules for placing the pieces.
The player who drops any piece or the entire base loses and is out of the game; the last remaining player is declared the winner!

—description from the publisher (translated)

Tetra Tower é um jogo divertido e desafiante!
O equilíbrio entre raciocínio lógico e coordenação motora é fundamental!
O objetivo é encaixar todas as peças na base (pêndulo) sem deixar que elas caiam, preservando o equilíbrio a cada rodada.
As cartas ditam as regras para colocação das peças.
O jogador que deixar cair alguma pela ou a base todas, perde e sai do jogo, o último jogador restante é declarado o vencedor!

—description from the publisher

In this 2-4 player battle for the bog, you’ll advance your frogs across lily pads, eating flies along the way. Complete routes, land on opposing frogs and score frog ‘formations’ for points. The player with the most points at the end of the game is the winner!

Based on BOG (Pack O Game), this board game expands the core concept in a beautiful full-size title.

—description from the publisher

Play as a heist crew of barnyard pals with asymmetric powers. Together, you’re pushing your luck trying to sneak through the county fair at midnight to rescue the Golden Goose's egg. Can you pick up enough treats to bribe Roscoe, the guard dog, into looking the other way? Or will he bark and blow your whole operation?!

In this cooperative, dice-rolling caper, your turn follows the same loop: roll all three custom dice to collect Treats (bribes for Roscoe) and Tickets (event cards that might positively or negatively change your luck) then decide to push your luck and roll again *or* scram and bank any treats you earned on your turn.

But watch out! If you roll too many "Noise" or "Notice" on the dice, Roscoe barks and you lose any treats you earned that turn.

To win, get the required number of treats for your crew into Roscoe's bowl and he'll look the other way while you steal back that egg. But if he barks too many times, you're all busted and the heist is over!

—description from the publisher

Experience the warm and gentle life of a cat. From kitten days to cozy afternoons and precious memories, "NYANSE" is a heartwarming dice-based board game from Japan. Players take on the role of a cat, navigating through various life events and creating their own unique emotional journey.

boardgame: Castles of Mad King Ludwig: Melissa's Markup Royal Decree Promo Card
description:
Originally available from Tantrum House Season 11 Kickstarter campaign.

This is a royal decree card. The Royal decree expansion is included in Castles of Mad King Ludwig: Collector's Edition or in Castles of Mad King Ludwig: Expansions.

Text says:
Other players pay you 1 coin more per room when you are the Master Builder
family: Game: Castles of Mad King Ludwig

boardgame: Castles of Mad King Ludwig: Katie's Ko-Opt Bonus Card
description:
Originally available from Tantrum House Season 11 Kickstarter campaign.

1 VP for every other player's bonus card(s).
family: Game: Castles of Mad King Ludwig

description:
The only way out of the Cursed Casino is to uncover the secrets hidden inside its six slot machines.

Strange Slot Simulator is a real-time deduction card game for 2 to 5 players. Each of the six slot machines has its own unique "Settings" — a distinct combination of eight symbol card types — and no one knows which machine has which Settings when the game begins.
Each round, players claim one machine and have four minutes to repeatedly draw cards and record what they find. Drawing bonus symbol cards (Bonus, Cherry Bonus, or Watermelon Bonus) advances your Bonus line, unlocking skills that let you draw even more cards per turn. After time is up, players analyze their notes and write down their best guess for each machine's Settings. The player who correctly identifies the most Settings across all four rounds wins.
With everyone acting simultaneously, tension runs high — rivals may grab the machine you were eyeing, and the clock is always ticking. Easy Mode eases newcomers into the deduction process, while Expert, Cooperative, and Solo modes offer fresh challenges for seasoned players.

boardgame: Die Legenden von Andor: Die Ewige Kälte – Das Licht der Dunkelklinge
description: An icy wind sweeps across the steppe. In the midst of the blizzard, an enemy from ancient times appears: the mighty ice giant. As the Eternal Frost grows ever stronger, so does his power. A second legend tells of the mysterious dark blade. This legendary sword is said to be so sharp that neither rock nor steel can withstand it. In search of clues to its final resting place, the heroes and heroines face new dangers and decisions.

Deep in the steppes, a tribe lives a peaceful life.
But among them, a young shaman seems to have forgotten everything... even her own name.

In this book, meet the members of the tribe and discover the totem animal that watches over each of them. Each spread is accompanied by an immersive soundtrack: a musical tapestry that illustrates the scene and sometimes holds the clues you need for your quest.

Observe. Listen. Decode.

Restore the scattered fragments of memory to the shaman, and reveal her forgotten destiny.

—description from the publisher

Au cœur des steppes, une tribu mène une existence paisible.
Mais parmi eux, une jeune chamane semble avoir tout oublié... jusqu’à son propre nom.

À travers ce livre, partez à la rencontre des membres de la tribu et découvrez l’animal totem qui veille sur chacun d’eux. Chaque double page s’accompagne d’une bande sonore immersive : une fresque musicale qui illustre la scène et recèle parfois les indices nécessaires à votre quête.

Observez. Écoutez. Décodez.

Redonnez à la chamane les fragments de mémoire dispersés, et révélez son destin oublié.

—description from the publisher (French)

Simple layered wheels with pictures of the sorts of things you see out of the windows of a car (cars, road signs and things of interest). Each player takes a wheel with the same picture, and one moves round the wheel clockwise and the other anti-clockwise spotting items. First back to the start is the winner.

Would you rather...? Tough questions for kids – for all fans of DIKKA 
DIKKA – the rapping rhinoceros creates wild, cheeky and funny songs for strong-willed children – and their parents too!
For all fans, the first card game is now available: Would you rather...? 
Over 60 cards with crazy questions. Handy portable format – perfect for at home and on the go 
For all fans of the popular children’s star. 
Would you rather... jump out of a cake? Or bathe in sweets? ...swing for ten hours or have a party on the underground? 
Lots of crazy questions in a handy card box in a portable format. Also perfect as a gift.

The Carpathian Dog has gone missing! Who stole the valuable sculpture? Will Justus, Peter and Bob catch the thief in time? A mysterious and dangerous investigation awaits The Three Investigators. Two to four players re-enact the film *The Three Investigators and the Carpathian Dog*. Card by card, they gather evidence, search for suspects and investigate every lead. Great fun and a case to get excited about with the three detectives and their latest cinema adventure – a must for all crime fans and budding detectives aged eight and over!

The Dinosaur Board Game is a lively race game where players, each controlling a dinosaur pawn, compete to collect six "foot" cards first. Starting on their color-matched spaces, players roll the dice, move clockwise, and draw cards based on the space they land on: "foot" cards bring them closer to victory, while "bridge" cards introduce strategic choices or delays. Hazard spaces add excitement, allowing players to use "bridge" cards to shortcut or slow opponents. The first to gather six "foot" cards wins, blending luck and light strategy in a fun, fast-paced race for all ages.

Sette Al Sette is a simple and engaging card game. The goal is to combine the cards you receive to reach a score of 7.
But beware: one wrong card can flip the game's fate in seconds.
the goal is to score 7 three times to win the game instantly or, if no score 7 three times, to pass the most rounds.

An epic campaign-based tabletop adventure with strategic card-driven mechanics, immersive scenarios and beautifully detailed miniatures!

An Aygor Campaign is played over many sessions, generally one scenario at a time. Characters work together cooperatively, traveling through Aygor, learning skills, acquiring items, defeating monsters, completing scenarios, earning XP, and leveling up. The Party wins the game by completing all scenarios. A Solo campaign works exactly the same way as a multiplayer game.

—description from the publisher
language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups

description:
Welcome to Forlazia—a world where reality has thinned, and the echoes of gods, monsters, and ancient powers shape every mountain, river, and ruin. A century and a half ago, the Veil was formed, forever altering the fabric of existence. In its wake, fragments—shards of that moment—linger across the world, distorting magic, awakening ancient forces, and giving rise to horrors long thought impossible.

The world did not break in a single instant—but it has never been the same. Kingdoms struggle to adapt to shifting truths, forbidden magic resurfaces in dangerous ways, and something ancient stirs beneath the soil. The wilds have grown more perilous, the old ruins more restless, and whispers of buried evils now carry farther than ever before.

Forlazia is a land of stark beauty and ever-present danger. Verdant forests conceal the remnants of lost civilizations, while great cities cast long shadows over catacombs teeming with the restless dead. The Inir River winds through trade towns and haunted glades, and the Dry Steppes burn beneath crimson skies. From the peaks of the Ulgar Mountains to forgotten depths buried beneath the world, every corner of the land holds secrets waiting to be uncovered—and dangers waiting to be unleashed.

You are among the few willing to rise in this age of uncertainty. Whether driven by vengeance, ambition, prophecy, or the fragile hope of salvation, your path will lead you into forgotten dungeons, across blighted wilderness, and toward the power—and peril—of the Veil’s lingering shards. The fate of Forlazia is still being written.

What role will you play in what comes next?

—description from the designer

Don't bring the canopy crashing down!

Dusk is falling in the rainforest, and the first sleepy forest dwellers are blinking from their hiding places. In Leaf It!, you must carefully feel your way through the foliage to build—and then dismantle—a wobbly primeval giant piece by piece.

Your goal is to collect as many sleepy, valuable forest creatures as possible. But beware: those who are too greedy will bring the canopy crashing down and hand valuable points to their opponents!

—description from the publisher

Letters Klacks is a fast and easy-to-learn card game inspired by the classic Mau-Mau principle. Players take turns matching cards by letter or color while learning the alphabet in a playful and intuitive way.

Each card combines a letter with a matching animal, helping children build associations and recognize letters naturally during gameplay. Special action cards add variety and excitement, keeping every round dynamic and engaging.

Designed for children aged 4 and up, Letters Klacks is perfect for families, early learners, and anyone looking for a fun introduction to the alphabet through play.

—description from the publisher

The wind sweeps across the Great Plains. The buffalo are still far away. Winter is coming.

In Tatanka, you lead one of the great peoples of the Plains through the cycle of two years of thirteen moons. 
Your decisions are guided not only by strategy, but by tradition, ceremony, and the rhythm of the land itself.

Gather resources before the cold comes. 
Strengthen the spiritual life of your tribe. 
Honor your dead. 
Use the CHEKUTNAK - ritual dice that decide not only what you do, but what you prevent others from doing.
Every moon brings new demands. Every choice shapes the legacy your tribe leaves on the Plains.

Tatanka is the result of over a decade of historical and ethnographic research — developed alongside anthropologist Alessio Martella, adopted member of the Lakota community, and photographer and journalist Alessio Vissani, author of "Unbreakable. A Lakota Story".

—description from the publisher

A fan made card for the boardgame Terraforming Mars that was released for the Dutch Open Terraforming Mars 2024. The fan made card was selected as the winner of a design competition. The card idea was submitted by Frank. The fan made card was exclusively available for participants to the Dutch Open 2024.

The card costs 20MC to play and has a building tag and a city tag. The card text reads: "Requires 4 city tiles ON MARS. Increase your MC production 3 steps. Place a city tile on top of an already placed ocean tile, following normal city placement restrictions. This tile counts as a city and an ocean. Place this tile rotated 30 degrees on the ocean, so the ocean is partly visible".

An anonymous tip reaches Inspector Hahnke: stolen goods have turned up inside the Klein-Borstelheim steelworks, and several employees have gone missing. The rest of the workforce isn’t talking. Are the perpetrators still on site — or have they already vanished into thin air?

Hidden Games Tatort: Glutspur is an immersive crime investigation game that dissolves the boundary between the game and the real world. Working as a team, players sift through physical evidence, check alibis, listen to witness statements, make actual phone calls, chat with the police, and conduct online research — all while examining real soil samples and running hands-on experiments. Every lead is a real lead. Every clue points somewhere.

No components are destroyed during play, and the case can be revisited.

Welcome to the magical and dangerous realm of Entaria, where eight clans war over a dispute whose beginning is lost to time. You are mighty champion setting out to defeat your enemies once and for all. As you arrive in the ancient forest, you realize that your enemies have settled on the same tactic- a battle to the end! But beware, as there are Werewolves and other beasts in these woods with threats of their own...

Wizerds, Warriors, & Werewolves is a fast-paced card-battling game for 2-8 players. Use your Champion’s special ability to gain an advantage. Die, and join the Ungrateful Dead, where you haunt other Champions. Survive, and become a legend in the realm of Entaria!

—description from the publisher

Supplies add powerful but limited actions for commanders to turn the tide of battle. Move all Gruntz at once, swap vehicle attachments, Paradrop Gruntz, and more!

—description from the publisher
language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups
family: Players: Wargames with Rules Supporting Only Two Players

boardgame: A Message From The Stars: Extinction Protocol
description: An expansion for the puzzle party game A Message From The Stars. This expansion adds two new game modes to the base game. Not only are the secret letters hidden, but even the rules themselves may be scrambled. can the scientists still decipher the aliens' messages?
family: Components: Dry Erase Markers / Dry Erase Surfaces
Mechanism: Give a Clue / Get a Clue
Organizations: Game Designers of North Carolina

description:
Like the classic Medici Board Game, Traders is a tile game that takes you to 15th century Florence, Italy. Players acquire the most valuable goods and optimally pack them on their ships. They simultaneously draw goods tiles, adding up to 3 tiles to their tray, which they then move to the center of the table.

After all players have added their trays to the center, the first player selects one of the trays, adding florins (coins) to the tray, as they wish, to help solidify their claim to those goods. Future players may either take a tray from the center (again placing florins to the tray to help solidify their claim) OR surpassing the number of florins on a tray that another player has already claimed, and taking that tray. The original player gets their florins back. Play continues around the table in this way until all players have claimed a tray.

Florins used to claim trays are turned into the bank and players load the goods from their claimed tray onto their ship. Ships in Traders have multiple levels - one for each day (turn) and an upper deck that can hold special goods with upper deck railings.

The above steps are repeated until all players have filled all 7 spaces on the current day's ship level.

At the end of each day, players score. First, players score based on who has the highest points of goods on the current day's level of their ship. Then, players score for each type of good anywhere on their ship. Players with the most of each type of good score the most points for that good.

At the end of three days, the player with the most florins wins.

—description from the publisher

After having the same terrible dream for several nights in a row, the Pharaoh realized it was an omen: he would be the last great Pharaoh. Seeking to perpetuate his name in history, he hired you and other renowned architects to design the Perfect Pyramid!

Now you are an architect on a mission to create the project for the grandest work in Egypt, the legacy of the last Pharaoh. Will you have the wisdom and strategy necessary to make the best project and be the one chosen by the Pharaoh?

In Perfect Pyramid, your objective is to earn the most victory points over 3 rounds by designing the Pyramid that best meet the Pharaoh’s desires. If you achieve this goal, you will be chosen to design the greatest architectural wonder of all time!

But to win, you must reach the highest total score at the end of the 3 rounds! How to do it? Simple!

PLAN: Buy cards and capture the best ones for your Pyramid.
IMPROVE: Use special actions to reorganize, renew, or advance your project!
SCORE: At the end of each round, the Pharaoh will evaluate rigorous criteria that can give you more points and guarantee your victory!

Are you ready to begin your most magnificent work?

—description from the publisher (translated)

Após ter o mesmo sonho terrível por várias noites seguidas, o faraó percebeu que aquilo era um presságio: ele seria o último grande faraó. Buscando perpetuar seu nome na história, ele contratou você e outros arquitetos renomados para projetar a Pirâmide Perfeita.

Agora você é um arquiteto com a missão de criar o projeto da mais grandiosa obra do Egito, o legado do último faraó: a Pirâmide Perfeita! Será que você terá sabedoria e estratégia necessárias para fazer o melhor projeto e ser o escolhido pelo faraó?

Em Pirâmide Perfeita, o seu objetivo é fazer o maior número de pontos de vitória durante 3 rodadas, projetando as Pirâmides que melhor atendam aos anseios do faraó. Se você atingir o objetivo, será o escolhido para projetar a maior maravilha arquitetônica de todos os tempos!

Mas, para vencer, você terá que alcançar a maior soma de pontuação ao final das 3 rodadas!
Como fazer? Simples!

PLANEJE: compre cartas e capture as melhores para a sua Pirâmide.

APRIMORE: use ações especiais para reorganizar, renovar ou avançar o seu projeto!

PONTUE: ao final de cada rodada, o faraó avaliará critérios rigorosos que poderão te dar mais pontos e garantir a sua vitória!

Preparado para começar a sua obra mais magnífica?

—description from the publisher

In Calicut, players take on the role of spice merchants, trading and collecting valuable spices in the busy markets - and black markets - of 16th century Calicut, at the height of the European/Indian Spice Trade. Each merchant (player) is competing to acquire the best sets of spices and export them to Lisbon, garnering the favor of King Manuel I of Portugal and Europe's ever-increasing demand for exotic spices.

The catch? The King's demand for spices is constantly changing.

Each card belongs to one of 5 different spices - pepper, nutmeg, ginger, cinnamon and cloves - and has a value (1-4). On your turn, you may trade spices from the market / black market, or export spices.

To trade spices from the market, discard any value from your hand, and then collect spices:

     of ANY type and with that exact value, OR
     of a SINGLE type, and equal or lower value.


Cards discarded this way are sent to the black market, which players may also choose to confiscate - collecting all spices in there for free!

To export (score) spices, look at the Demand Row. The Demand Row dictates how much of each spice are needed for export - either 2, 3 or 4 cards. The two spices with the lowest demand can't be scored at all! When a spice is exported, its demand drops to the bottom, increasing demand for the other spices. Timing in Calicut is key - you must always hold the right sets, at the right time!

Time your decisions carefully. Anticipate the demand, read the market, buy at the right moment, and score at the best opportunity to amass the most riches!

Moreover, a 7-card hand limit will force you to optimize your actions as best as possible, managing your stock and striking the best balance between spice trading, confiscating and exporting.

A round ends when a player scores their 5th set, at which point all player scores are counted. For each of the 5 spices, the player with the highest value collects its respective Demand Card for bonus points, so make sure you score higher than your opponent in as many spices as possible!

The first player to win 2 rounds is the winner and the best spice merchant in Calicut!

—description from the publisher

Purchase increasingly powerful wands using the ones in your hand.

Take advantage of their unique abilities to acquire even stronger ones or remove them to prevent your opponents from using them.

Managing your hand wisely and choosing the right moment to acquire a unique wand will be key to achieving victory.

—description from the publisher

XElement TCG is a strategic trading card game where players summon elemental creatures and evolve them through sacrifice-based mechanics. Each player manages a unique star resource system that determines how and when powerful creatures and abilities can be used.

The game focuses on battlefield control, timing, and evolution chains. Creatures can grow stronger through progression, while players must balance their resources and positioning to gain advantage.

Victory is achieved by reaching 7 points through direct attacks when controlling more creatures than the opponent. Every decision, from resource usage to evolution timing, can determine the outcome of the match.

—description from the designer

A print-and-play board game where you terraform entire planets with dice, pencils, and meaningful choices.
Hexoplanets is a compact space terraforming game you can print at home and start playing immediately. You roll dice, color hexes, and watch each planet evolve — mechanically and narratively — based on the decisions you make.

No minis. No long setup.
Just paper, dice, and a universe that reacts to you.

It’s Rotten is a clever twist on the classic falling block puzzle—reimagined as a solo puzzle experience you can take anywhere. Requires 1d6 to play.

There’s no sliding pieces into place. Once you choose a column, your piece falls straight down—forcing you to think ahead, adapt quickly, and embrace a little chaos. Some rows reward you. Others are cursed and will drag your score down if you’re not careful.

In Traveluencer, players must try to make their posts go viral and become the most popular travel influencers!
Trending topics change daily - only by staying on top of the lastst buzz can you succeed as a KOL(key opinion leader)!

Traveluencer is a trick-taking game. Players play any number of cards in an attempt to win tricks and boost engagement. They must manage their resources carefully,or they risk losing followers due to inactivity.

boardgame: Chronicles of Midgard: Campaigns and Scenarios for Midgard Heroic Battles
description:
The Chronicles of Midgard is the much anticipated expansion for Midgard Heroic Battles which takes your games into new and exciting territory, adding rules for fast-moving narrative campaigns and fifteen new dynamic and challenging scenarios. 
A simple map-based campaign system focuses on heroic deeds as two Courts of Heroes compete to gain renown and victory for their Host in a historical, legendary or fantasy setting of your choice. 
Fifteen new scenarios add multiple tactical situations to your games, including ambushes, broken terrain and attacking fortifications. 
The supplement also features From Athelney To Ethandun– a complete historically-based campaign pitting Alfred the Great against the Vikings in the spring of 878 AD. Muster Rolls provide more detailed Force Lists for both sides along with appendices designed to help you create your own.

A copy of Midgard Heroic Battles is needed to use the contents of this supplement.

The time has come to saddle your warhorse, break out the mead and make your enemies food for ravens.

—description from the publisher

A new Korea expansion pack for the game 3 Second Try.
The expansion adds 20 new cards with new challenges themed around Korea!

In 3 Seconds Try, players face either physical or brain challenges in under 3 seconds to score the most points.
The first player (called "Master") chooses a "Physical" (Dexterity) or "Brain" challenge and reveals the card.
Placers bet on how many times they think they can do the challenge in under 3 seconds, and the highest bidder gets to do the attempt.
If they can complete the challenge before the ball reaches the end of the slope they win the card. If not, the next highest bidder gets to try the challenge.

The first player to earn 2 cards wins.

Bubble and Shadow is a light strategy board game with a literary fantasy theme, adapted from the novel The moon and sixpence.

In the game, playes will alternately appear as either an emissary of the Magician or a Mortal of the earthly realm, signing pacts with each other and engaging in transactions centered around different prices.

Through mutual speculation and probing, you will approach a delicate balance-everything you possess will determine the final outcome.

Cursed Parade is a fast-paced card game where players struggle to rid themselves of cursed relics.

Each player starts with a pile of Relics cards and must play them to the center. Relics can trigger powerful effects, interrupt other play, and shift the burden between players.

But beware! Cursed relics never truly disappear. They return, they spread, and they escalate.

In the end, only one thing matters:

Don’t be the last player left holding relics.

In BOTS, two players pit their teams of four bots against each other. Players take turns placing their bot cards faceup in the 8 spaces around the Neutral Bot. Once all cards have been played, the Neutral Bot is revealed and each bot attacks in turn from lowest strength to highest. Once all of the bots have attacked or died, the player with the most survivors wins! In the case of a tie, the player with the lowest strength individual survivor wins.

—description from the designer

Bombagól is a clever, fast‑paced card game that lets you relive the thrill of the World Cup — score as many goals as you can!
A match is played between two players, but with more people you simply take turns, each stepping onto the pitch with the team they’ve chosen. The game even works great solo, though the excitement grows with every additional player. After all, the more contenders there are, the higher the stakes: who will become the world champion in the end!

boardgame: Die Legenden von Andor: Die Kreaturen des Ava
description:
A punchboard promo for Legends of Andor, part of the 2023 Fan Legend Competition. Available for download and home printing on the Andor website and produced only in a very limited quantity. It shows five new figures for Legends of Andor – The Eternal Frost: Die Mondseehexe, Der Tanghorn-Gor, der Grauskral, der Schattenhäuptling und Banator der Steppenriese.

—description from the publisher

Ein Promo Stanztableau für Die Legenden von Andor, teil des Fan-Legenden-Wettbewerb 2023. Verfügbar zum Download und Selbstdruck auf der Andor Webseite und nur in sehr limitierter Stückzahl produziert. Zeigt fünf neue Figuren für Die Legenden von Andor - Die Ewige Kälte: Die Mondseehexe, Der Tanghorn-Gor, der Grauskral, der Schattenhäuptling und Banator der Steppenriese.

—description from the publisher (German)

Heist Team: The Fortuna Diamond is a tabletop game inspired by escape rooms and heist movies.

Gather your friends, plan the heist, and execute it as you face
interactive escape room puzzles, unexpected twists, and a dramatic finale. For 1 - 6 players.

It’s like an Escape Room meets Ocean’s Eleven, enhanced with digital interaction and RPG-inspired strategy!

Smart10: Ukraine is a trivia game with a Ukrainian theme where everyone gets a chance to participate. Each of the 200 questions has 10 possible answers, and all players or teams can respond to every question, so there is no need to wait for your turn. Your goal is to give as many correct answers as possible. If your answer is correct, you earn points. If you are wrong, you are out of the round and lose the points you have just gained, so it is important to know when to answer and when not to.

The 200 questions are divided into several groups, each requiring a different approach to finding the correct answers. Recall colors, arrange items in order, count, choose between "yes" and "no", and name people and years. Each question requires not only knowledge but also decision-making. Should you take a risk or play it safe? Prove that you are a pro. Let your friends witness your triumph!

—description from the publisher (translated)

Wanted: Showdown is a fast-paced, highly customizable tabletop skirmish level wargame set in a brutal, unpredictable Wild West.

Players assemble their own posse of outlaws, lawmen, and warriors — each with unique abilities and playstyles — and battle for control through tactical movement, clever positioning, and explosive shootouts. Every decision matters, and every duel can turn the tide.

The game blends tactical planning with chaotic, high-risk moments, creating a dynamic experience where no two matches play the same.

—description from the designer
language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups
family: Players: Wargames with Rules Supporting Only Two Players

description:
It’s party time — confetti is flying, bunting is fluttering, and all the animals are celebrating. What can’t be missing? Of course: memorable photos! Help the animals take the perfect Beast Selfie!

Each round, you choose an animal and place it in an empty row of your selfie frame (face-down toward you). But space is limited, and Penguin, Crocodile & Co. want to show off their best sides. Once all five rows are filled, you flip your selfie frame and snap real photos with a camera. You score points for every fully visible favorite feature of an animal!

—description from the publisher

boardgame: 4 ILLES: Pirates i Corsaris de la Mediterrània
description:
Put yourself in the shoes of an island pirate captain! 
With the help of your crew, you will play route cards in ascending order or of the same suit to hinder the other captains. Your goal is to be the first to run out of cards to get the hidden treasure of the islet of Ses Roquetes and crown yourself King of all Pirates. Can you become the best pirate in the Mediterranean?

In addition, with the route card deck you can play any game that requires a classic deck, since it is composed of 4 suits of 12 cards each, numbered from 1 to 12.

—description from the designer

This box contains 2 mini-expansions for the core game. They introduce the missing fourth "X" - exploration - into the gameplay.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Temple of Horrors: Divine Favor Expansion
description:
The new Temple of Horrors expansion, featuring many new contents!

This set includes the 9 bosses: Medusa, Mimic, Banshee, Air Elemental, Gelatinous Cube, Bugbear, Chimera, Mind Eater, Giant Serpent, and the Dragon.

It includes a new double-sided Hour board to modify the difficulty and 7 new power cards for Heroes

The Northwest Passage. An elusive sea route along the northern coast of America that's said to link the Pacific and Atlantic oceans. Since the 15th century, expeditions were launched with the dream of discovering it, only to then vanish in the icebergs. This is a cooperative game where you aim to connect 15 cards together while meeting the necessary conditions. But if you veer off course even slighty, your voyage will end in failure. Work together and predict which cards the other players hold, make use of items, and successfully traverse the route that humanity has dreamed of!

The Frozen Passage is an exciting co-op game where players are part of an expedition team trying to navigate at sea, but the journey has become dangerous with lots of icebergs threatening to sink the ship. Players work together to successfully navigate through this dangerous journey and make it out safely. This is a board game filled with adventure and excitement, where you gradually expand and extend your path across the sea towards the goal cards. Which direction should you go? Should you use up your limited special items? Players are limited in how they can communicate, adding to the tension in this game.

Players work together to arrange three colors of cards numbered 1 to 9 in an inverted pyramid shape, starting from the bottom and aiming for the goal cards at the top. If all players manage to place all of their cards, everyone wins. However, there are specific rules for how the cards must be arranged. If you lose focus on your turn, you might steer the ship towards disaster. Since everyone’s hand is hidden, you must try to complete the path by predicting each other's cards and helping each other out!

—description from the publisher

Tiempaleza is a card game where you'll reconstruct part of Hortaleza's history, shaped by the events and people who have given this Madrid district its personality, idiosyncrasies, and stories.

The impact of Filomena and the musher who went viral, Luis Aragonés, the struggle of the residents of Portugalete, and the story of the silo in Huerta de la Salud are just some of the points covered in this game, which aims to showcase the unique stories of one of Madrid's districts, both big and small.

Each turn, select a card to put in a timeline, and check if it doesn't break the timeline's continuity. First player to get rd of all their cards, wins.

In the region of Toubama, sushi is the new craze and the most prestigious sushi bar will do anything to get fresh fish to serve. Seeing the money they could get, all the yakuza families will want to get the money by getting the precious fish.

But in the fish market, only a few fishes arrive at each batch and there isn't enough for everybody. Players in lightning rounds push to get a certain player eliminated, and whoever is the most voted is out, alongside anyone who doesn't voted for them. But players who think they will get voted can try to protect themselves and eliminate a player. But sometimes, you can all do an agreement and share the few fished that will come in this batch.

If two players are left, they can try to trust the rival family to split equal the fish, go for the sure single fish or try to get greedy.

But getting all the fish is not the best option, since some fishes have special effects.

The region of Towerbeach has nearing elections with no clear winner. You must try to get the assurance that the five main workers gilds of Towerbeach will pressure their workers into voting and doing campaign for you.

Each turn, deal six cards to every player. Players must place bets on how much of a certain guild, with the help of mercenaries they could reach. After no bid are upped, by order of biggest bet, the betting player gets all the cards of the same guild from the other players, add the betted guild and mercenary cards and try to get the remaining value of the bet in two dice rolls.

The first player to convince the five guilds is the winner.

Celebrating the anniversary of the foundation of Toubama, the two kamis that make the nigh sky try to bring joy with their stars.

Discard a card in of the three play spaces, always discarding the immediate next valued card, to make the brightest night sky.

Players score based on how many cards their rival still have in hand when they can't play a hand, but in they're too aggressive, the other kamis will think you're too harsh and give a point to the losing player.

First player to reach 6 points, wins the game.
